summary: "Check for trailing or leading white spaces before Issue import"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When importing Issues into a Project via CSV or JSON, if there is a trailing or leading white space, Jira will create a new entry for that element rather than merging with existing elements.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Export Issues in a Project in CSV.    # Add a whitespace before or after a value.    # Import the Issues into Jira.      In the particular case that spurred this Suggestion, Issues were imported that had a whitespace preceding the Version.  Jira now shows _two_ versions that have the same version number.  However, when rendering the versions on the page, the whitespace is disregarded by Java, and so the two identically-displayed versions are confusing to end users (they don't know why there are two identical versions displayed and cannot know which one to choose).  Different issues will be displayed under the two identical versions.   h3. Expected Results    Jira will disregard the extra whitespace and merge or map the values to existing values.   h3. Actual Results    Jira creates a separate entry for the value instead of merging the value into existing fields.   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Workaround    Unless the application type is specifically needed, the _""""type: com.atlassian.jira""""_ line can simply be removed the JSON used to create the link and it will render just fine. summary: "Setting up Jira returns a timezone error on SetupDatabase.jspa"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Error below is returned during the installation process:  {panel}  The server time zone value 'EDT' is unrecognized or represents more than one time zone. You must configure either the server or JDBC driver (via the 'serverTimezone' configuration property) to use a more specific time zone value if you want to utilize time zone support.  {panel}  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Install Jira using Timezone set to America/New_York, for example.  h3. Expected Results    No error during the Jira installation.  h3. Actual Results    Error below returned during the installation process:  {panel}  The server time zone value 'EDT' is unrecognized or represents more than one time zone. You must configure either the server or JDBC driver (via the 'serverTimezone' configuration property) to use a more specific time zone value if you want to utilize time zone support.  {panel}  h3. Workaround    Set timezone as UTC to bypass the timezone check and finish the setup Wizard. This is described in [this community post|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/The-server-time-zone-value-CEST-is-unrecognized-or-represents/qaq-p/1172019]."""

summary: "CustomFieldIndexerAdapter does not delegate correctly wrapped object "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Having multiple indexers with the same id registered via multiple custom fields might cause the issue with reindexing due to {{CustomFieldIndexerAdapter}} doesn't delegate correctly.    Context:   * It uses equals() and hashCode() from Object instead of delegating to the wrapped object which means that custom field indexers returned from com.atlassian.jira.issue.index.managers.FieldIndexerManagerImpl.getAllIssueIndexers() are no longer deduped.    h3. Expected Results  {{CustomFieldIndexerAdapter}} delegates correctly    h3. Actual Results  {{CustomFieldIndexerAdapter}} doesn't delegate correctly    The below exception is thrown in the Jira file during reindexing:        h3. Workaround  None"""
comments: "We are impacted by this issue and is troubling us whenever we enable script runner forcing us to rollback to Jira DC version 8.9.1.    Re-Index of the instance is completely blocked.    This issue is a showstopper for us as we  rely on SR very much in our production environments and are not able to upgrade to later version as long as this issue exists" [SEP] "Bram, please contact [Adaptavist Support|https://productsupport.adaptavist.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/2] as there is a workaround for the ScriptRunner issue you are affected by. We will be able to help you through that channel."

summary: "Adding 'Single Email Address' to the notification scheme stops 'Batching email notifications' for that specific event"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding 'Single Email Address' to the notification scheme (any event) stops 'Batching email notifications' for that specific event  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable ⚙️> System > Batching email notifications   # Configure the _Browse Projects_ [Permission Scheme|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/managing-project-permissions-776636362.html] of a project to _Group (Anyone, including non-registered and anonymous users)_.   # Set one of the events (i.e. *Issue Created*) of the [Notification Scheme|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/creating-a-notification-scheme-776636401.html] of that same project to _Single Email Address_ and enter a valid one. Save the changes.   # Create a new ticket   # None of the email recipients (reporter, groups, watchers, etc.) receive the batched email   # Only the Single Email Address gets an email with a non-batched notification template   # Other events (like Issue updated) works as expected with batched template    h3. Expected Results    All the recipients should receive batched email notifications including the event where Single Email Address is added  h3. Actual Results    All the recipients are not receiving batched email notifications for the event where is added Single Email Address    Only the Single Email Address gets an email with a non-batched notification template  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Java 11 GC logging configuration is not parseable in GCViewer"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The currently shipped GC logging configuration file (see """"set-gc-params.sh"""" & """"set-gc-params.bat"""" files ) does not allow the GC logs to be parsed in GCViewer correctly. GCViewer throws """"_Failed to recognize file format_"""" error.    This is the current configuration :    It is missing """"tags"""" and """"level""""    I have raised a ticket to GCViewer to get some insight on this here:   * [https://github.com/chewiebug/GCViewer/issues/245]    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use JAVA 11 ( AdoptOpenJDK ) with Jira. You can use the JAVA_HOME=""""path-to/adoptopenjdk-11.jdk/Contents/Home"""" to configure this on the setenv.sh file.   # Try to open the GC logs in GCViewer    h3. Expected Results    GC logs will parse correctly in GCViewer  h3. Actual Results    This error will be thrown :    !image-2020-08-10-19-43-25-865.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    Reply from [https://github.com/chewiebug/GCViewer/issues/245]  {quote}GCViewer relies for performance and complexity reasons on the presence of the """"tags"""" and """"level"""" information in the java unified logs generated by JDK 11 VMs ([https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/158]). Your logs are missing both (this part of the configuration:   -Xlog:gc*:file=/<path-to-file>:time,uptime:...).    The readme gives examples of configurations, which GCViewer at this time is able to process:    -Xlog:gc:file=""""path-to-file"""" (uses defaults)   -Xlog:gc=info:file=""""path-to-file"""":tags,uptime,level (minimum configuration needed)   -Xlog:gc*=trace:file=""""path-to-file"""":tags,time,uptime,level (maximum configuration supported, additional tags ok, but ignored; additional decorations will break parsing)   If you just add """"tags"""" and """"level"""" to your configuration, GCViewer should be able to parse your log files.    Best regards,   Jörg  {quote}  JIRA Specific workaround for Linux   # Stop Jira   # Edit the JIRA_INSTALL/bin/set-gc-params.sh file   # Modify this line :    to    (i) Adding tags & level     # Start Jira    JIRA Specific workaround for Windows   # Stop Jira   # Edit the JIRA_INSTALL/bin/set-gc-params.bat file   # Modify this line :    to    (i) Adding tags & level     # Start Jira    JIRA Specific workaround for Windows when Jira is being run as a service   * Stop service   * Edit the JIRA_INSTALL/bin/set-gc-params-service.bat file   * Modify this line :    to    (i) Adding tags & level     * *Reinstall service*   * Start service"""

summary: "Implement batch API for custom fields reindexing"
description: """h3. Problem    Further improve custom field full reindex times and performance.  h3. Solution    Batch API was introduced to fetch custom field values for a batch of issues instead of one which should improve performance during full reindex operation.              (i) This feature is only available in Jira Data Center 8.12 and later."""

summary: "collation error at startup when using Postgresql in Azure templates "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When spinning up Jira for the first time utilizing the """"quick start"""" templates in Azure the collation is set to English_United States:1252 this causes Jira to failed the startup collation test erroneously. This is the correct collation for Windows Postgres servers.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Spin up a new Jira DC instance in the Azure portal using these [instructions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/running-jira-on-an-azure-cluster-969535564.html]   # Attempt to start JIRA    h3. Expected Results  Jira health check does not give the false warning when using .1252 collations    h3. Actual Results  The collation check fails on startup   !image.png|thumbnail!   This is a false warning and can be ignored, provided the client encoding is set correctly.    h3. Suggested Solution  We should update the health check to accept the Windows equivalent of UTF-8.    h3. Notes  According to [Postgres|https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.3/static/multibyte.html]:  {quote}UTF-8 encoding can be used with any locale.  {quote}  In addition, we've reviewed this [blog post|http://www.g-loaded.eu/2011/02/27/locale-windows/], Locale in Windows, and it appears that you should be able to use English_United States.1252.    h3. Workaround  You can click ignore and continue for now, until the health check is fixed."""

summary: "As a Jira admin, we would like to be alerted that a plugin may not be downgraded after upgrade"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Some plugins updates can not be downgraded, for instance, when the plugin has internal data structure changes.  We would like to have a clear advertisement that this may happen and general steps for a rollback.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Following our documentation at [Preparing for the upgrade|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/preparing-for-the-upgrade-966063325.html#Preparingfortheupgrade-apps]   # One of the steps is to check """"Check the compatibility of apps (add-ons)""""    h3. Expected Results  * We would like to be advised to verify the downgrade steps at the plugin documentation prior do the plugin upgrade.  * The plugin could have a clear flag on the list of plugin versions on UPM that says """"we know that upgrading to this version means you won't be able to downgrade"""".     h3. Actual Results  We are not advised that this might happen.   The steps provided on [Downgrading an Add-on to an Earlier Version|https://confluence.atlassian.com/upm/downgrading-an-add-on-to-an-earlier-version-311918719.html] will not work as there were DB changes.   The Database needs also to be restored.      h3. Workaround  1. Get the XML backup and backup jira-home/shared-home (cluster).  2. Stop Jira.  3. Restore Jira home/shared home from backup (this will restore the plugin/installed-plugin)  4. Start Jira.  5. Restore the XML backup    """

When using the standart IssueLinks it always allows to link to any other Jira issue, e.g. it can result in having a BusinessEpic as child of a Story or such nonsense.    Example:    We have BusinessEpic, Epic and Story as issue types in our project and want to configure that the Epic child-of link will only allow to enter the BusinessEpic issue types - it shall be filtered when the user tries to create the link."""

summary: "When a node is recovering an index from snapshot it does not return 503"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a node recovers an index from a snapshot it does not return {{503 Maintenance}} as it does when you do a full reindex, instead it returns a 200 and it still allows user traffic.     This means that users can access a node without a working index, which results in incidents.    Jira should block access to a node that does not have a working index.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Perform and index recovery on a node  # Confirm you can still access that node while recovery is underway    h3. Expected Results  A node should block while it does not have a working index, the same as it does it you perform a full reindex.    h3. Actual Results  The node is available and users can access it, but without an index they cannot view issues, reports, etc.    h3. Workaround  # Manually remove the node from your load balancer while it is recovering the index  # Watch the logs for progress  # Once complete, manually re-add the node into the load balancer    NB: This affects all versions of Jira"""

summary: "Activity Feed Needs to show timestamps to include seconds"
description: """The activity feed today shows events updates in the following format:    05/Aug/20 11:01 AM    The preferred time stamp should include seconds like below:    05/Aug/20 11:01:22 AM    Reason: We need this functionality because we have sometimes MANY updates occurring within a minute time period like automation rules and other external integrations that are updating tickets. These updates can overlap along with real users making changes to tickets. To best determine when things happen within that minute interval we need seconds to be displayed.     """
comments: "Component should be - Issue - View."

summary: "Relocate cache replication and indexing stats to their own log"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Cache replication logging (_LocalQCacheManager_/_LocalQCacheReplicator_) is currently output to the *atlassian-jira.log*. This is undesirable as cache statistics messages are generated every minute, for every node, which overwhelms the logs, particularly when you're looking for non-cache-related events.    A similar situation maintains for index stats logging (snapshot stats, lucene-stats).    h3. Suggested Solution  Separating cache replication and index stats logging to dedicated log (e.g., *atlassian-jira-stats.log*) would greatly reduce the noise in atlassian-jira.log. Additionally, this will allow for retaining more cache stats and indexing stats logging, which is useful for examining trends over a large time frame."""
comments: "Yes, please! Any action that emits periodic messages into the main log file can potential end up hiding the important messages in there. In fact all stats could go in their own log." [SEP] "As usual, Matt is totally right; all stats should go in their own log."

summary: "Jira cannot differentiate when the two attachments with the same name are inserted in two separate comments."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira cannot differentiate when the two attachments with the same name are inserted in two separate comments using the browse option in WYSIWYG editor    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create sample JSW project  Create a JSW issue  update the issue comment with image B (image.png)  update a new comment with image C (image.png)    h3. Expected Results    Jira should be able to differentiate between two different attachments with same name when they are added in different comments    h3. Actual Results  Jira is unable differentiate between the attachments.  When the attachment is an image and another image with the same name is attached, the previous comments with inline image/attachments will be pointed to the latest updated image (including the thumbnails)    h3. Workaround    After adding the attachment in the comment and then saving it.You need to update the reference of the attachment in the comment.  """
comments: "Likewise, when downloading attachments via the API, if there are two with the same name, then the second overwrites the first and no error is thrown.    The only way to find out there was a problem is to capture attachments count first, then compare that with the number of files downloaded. "

description: """h3. Summary  According to the Supported platforms document, Jira Server 8.5 only support AdoptOpenJDK up to version 11.0.2.   - [Supported platforms|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver085/supported-platforms-981154553.html]  {quote} - We support AdoptOpenJDK up to version 11.0.2.{quote}    However, JDK 11.0.2 caused below bug.   - JRASERVER-70780: Webhooks intermittently stop working with Java 11    This bug fixed JDK 11.0.8 or JDK 13.   - [https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8241054]   - [https://hg.openjdk.java.net/jdk/jdk13/rev/5022a4915fe9]    h3. summary: "Keyboard shortcut for submitting a comment does not work correctly"
description: """-Similar to [JRASERVER-63438|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-63438]-  h1. Problem    The 'Share with Customer' button in the form to enter a comment has a keyboard shortcut for submitting the form, which on Mac+Chrome is *Ctrl+Alt+e*. With the new Visual mode editor for comments, the keyboard shortcut does nothing.    Note: -Tested on Mac using Chrome.-   Customer's Note: -This key combination does not work in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge for Mac, and we have also found it not to work in Chrome for Windows (not sure about others).-  h1. Steps to reproduce   # Create/view an issue, press the Comment button   # Enter a comment and use the keyboard shortcut to submit the form while having the Visual tab selected.    h2. Expected    A comment is added as a result of step one and another comment is added as a result of step 2.  h2. Actual    Only using the Comment button works. The shortcut does not seem to work at all (neither Alt+e for Internal comment nor Alt+s for regular comment in SD projects)  h4. Some other tests made to check:    *Visual Mode*   * CTRL+ALT+E (x)   * COMMAND+ALT+E (x)   * CTRL+ALT+S (/)    *Text Mode*   * CTRL+ALT+E (x)   * CTRL+ALT+S (/)    Notes: The above 'Visual' and 'Text' mode results were tested with Jira Software 8.5.4 and 8.11.0. The customer who noticed this problem could use Ctrl-Alt-e if he switches to """"text"""" mode instead of """"Visual""""."""

summary: "Add support to ARM architecture processors"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    According to our [Supported platforms|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html] page, at the moment, we only support Jira running on x86 hardware and 64-bit derivatives of x86 hardware.    h3. Suggested Solution    Provide a Jira distribution that officially supports ARM processors in newer Jira versions.  """

summary: "Handler create s new ticket for every response when there is no space before and after the issue key"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the subject of email id is UTF encoded then while parsing such email, the handler creates a new ticket for every response instead of adding a comment. This is because while parsing emails the normal space before and after the ticket gets dropped      No space before and after issue key in the subject creates a new ticket for every response  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # I sent an email to my mailbox and it first created a new ticket   # Copied the issue key from the new ticket created notification which is """"AT-15""""   # Sent another email to the mail server with subject as """"3A=C2=A0 AT-15 _Aw=3A=C .   # Here, in this case, Mail handler reads the subject line and recognize the issue-key and add the comment to the original ticket    # I sent another email to the mail server with subject as """"3A=C2=A0AT-15_Aw=3A=C .   # Here, in this case, Mail handler is unable to read the subject line for issue key hence created a new ticket    # This is because in the first case we added a space before and after the issue key hence mail handler could read the issue key but in the second case since there is no space added hence Mail handler is unable to read the subject line for issue key     h3. Expected Results    It should add a comment to the ticket but instead, it created a new ticket   h3. Actual Results    h3. Workaround    No workaround available     h3. Note    This issue impacts both:  - the Jira Mail Handler configured in *⚙> System > Incoming Mails*  - the Service Desk Mail Handler configured in *Project Settings > Email Requests*"""

summary: "Radio Buttons do not render when accessing Jira via IE11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Radio Button custom field options are not showing when accessing Jira via Internet Explorer 11. This issue only seems to occur when embedded search JQL is in the URL. Such as:  http://example.domain.com:8080/browse/TEST-123?jql=project%20%3D%20TEST    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Radio Button custom field and add it to relevant screens.  # Go to the Issue Navigator and switch to the list view of issues.  # When accessing any issues from the list, note that the JQL parameter is still on the URL (expected behavior).  # Try to edit the value of the field (either in-line or via the screen) and see that the radio buttons are not selectable.    (i) The issue is reproducible on _Internet Explorer only_, which is no longer supported as of 8.6.x.    h3. Expected Results  The field values are selectable and the user is able to edit it.    h3. Actual Results  The radio buttons do not show up. There are no exceptions on the browser console nor on a HAR file.    h3. Workaround  Remove the JQL parameters on the URL and reload the page."""
comments: "Please note this issue also affects Checkbox fields, in addition to radio buttons." [SEP] "Hoping to clarify that 8.5 is the version in which version this bug was introduced?  Does this mean 8.4.3 was the last version without this issue?  Thank you" [SEP] "Please note that this issue also affects version 8.4.3." [SEP] "This issue is not present in 8.3.x.  Introduced version appears to be 8.4."

summary: "Mark tabs which contain required fields"
description: """We have several tabs containing various fields for an issue type creation screen.  If we set some of the fields to required on different tabs, the user has no visual indication which tabs contain required fields and must search though all the forms to find which required field is preventing submission.    Please add some visual indication, like an asterisk or red text, to show if a tab contains required fields which prevent users from submitting changes.  """

summary: "Consider renaming ""Whitelist"" and ""Blacklist"" to ""Allowlist"" and ""Blocklist"" "
description: """h3. Problem    The use of racially charged terms like """"whitelist"""" and """"blacklist"""" connotate goodness and badness to specific colors, which can be exclusionary and offensive.    h3. Solution    Use non-exclusionary terms instead.    h3. Notes    The change has been made in our documentation but not in-product. For example, [Configuring the allowlist|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-the-allowlist-1014667309.html]."""
comments: "I fully support this 100% and as a customer was pleased to see in Jira Service Desk, the term in the email settings is ""allowlist""!    Not sure if Atlassian will ever actually reply to this, but for the reported, seems they're already moving that way.         Edit: I see now this is for server. I am referring to JSD Cloud. "

summary: "DefaultIssueIndexer warning when re-indexing empty issue list"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Frequent warnings are being logged when *DefaultIssueIndexer* is provided with an empty list of issues to reindex.    DevStatus plugin calls DefaultIssueIndexer when there are no issues to replicate, causing a high number or WARN messages.   This extra logging started after the upgrade to Jira 8.10.0 from Jira 7.6.6.    The messages are triggered under two different conditions:         *1) From DevStatus ServiceRunner:*         *2) When a user is editing an issue ISSUE-34744*   * Multiple warnings (at the use case, 271 warnings during 22 minutes) for the User request to  /browse/ISSUE-34744:          h3. Steps to Reproduce    DoS team was able to reproduce this issue by programmatically providing an empty list to *DefaultIssueIndexer*.  h3. Expected Results    No additional logging  h3. Actual Results  High number of warning messages being logged:    h3. Workaround   * Modify the {{log4j.properties}} file:   * Change the log level to ERROR:  """
comments: "Filling logs full of distracting information is a serious problem with Jira in a few areas. Sounds like this is another one. Please prioritize cleaning up log files to help admins understand when there is a real problem." [SEP] "Raised this on GHS-194110 and subsequent issue kindly created.  As reported by Matt above, it's incredibly distracting in the log file.         We're using the temporary workaround for ease at the moment, as I'd rather not lose sight of the log messages by forgetting about the workaround entry in log4j.properties.    Temporary approach:   * In Jira, go to *⚙* *> System > Logging and profiling page*   * Scroll down to *Default Loggers > Configure logging level for another package*, and add com.atlassian.jira.issue.index.DefaultIssueIndexer package with the ERROR level     "

summary: "Application access page not accessible if the number of groups exceeds 1500"

Steps to Reproduce    *Pre-requisite:* There should be more than 1500 groups in the Jira instance   # Add  1500 groups to the """"Jira Software"""" Application Access   # Click on *Cog Icon* > Applications > Application Access   # The page will keep on loading and will give multiple errors (Attached herewith)    There are no traces of error in the logs related to the behavior    Heap size : 2GB  h3. Expected Results    The page should load without any errors  h3. Actual Results    The page never loads due to which new projects cannot be configured and application access can't be granted  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

With 8.5.4 Data Center we have a dozen AD groups given access, and thousands of AD groups available for choosing, and no problem  " [SEP] "Yes, application access page become unresponsive when groups are added over 1500. We have got this issue in Jira DC version 8.2.3.     We don't have any issue with choosing option, because page itself was not loaded beyond certain number of groups.    Now, with this bug - we are totally blocked to assign license to any new group as part of new project on-boarding. Please provide patch fix at-least via REST API to solve the issue."

summary: "Changing dashboard layout truncates recent week's data in Created vs. Resolved gadget  "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In certain situations, when *Created vs Resolved Chart* gadget is included in narrower dashboard layouts or monitor/browser sizes are reduced, data points for last week """"Created Issues"""" show the week before rather than the present week.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a *Created vs Resolved Chart* in Jira Dashboard, with the following configuration:   ** Saved filter containing multiple issues (both created and resolved in the past few months).   ** Period: weekly.   ** Days Previously: 300.   ** Collection Operation: Cumulative.   ** Display the Trend of Unresolved: Yes.   ** Display Versions: None.   ** Auto Refresh box: Checked.     # Change dashboard layout to either a 3 columns or a 50% and narrower layout column layouts.   ** This behavior is also experienced when using the full-width layout, both when changing the scale of the monitor screen to a smaller one or when the browser window is not maximized.    h3. Expected Results   * Users should be able to see present week data when hovering the mouse over the last data points for *Created Issues*.    h3. Actual Results   * Instead, users see the week before data for *Created Issues*.   ** Attached screenshot shows a test conducted on the week of 25th July.   ** When clicking in the last data point, dates and issue counts are correct. It's only when hovering the mouse over where the behavior appears.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Applying a workflow on a sub-task issue type having a condition ""This transition will only execute if there are no Open Crucible reviews related to this issue"" fails and allows transitions to happen"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Applying a workflow on a sub-task issue type having a condition that shouldn't allow the transition if a crucible review associated is open fails, and allows the transition to happen.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue type of sub-task type """"*Modify Individual Module*"""" and associate this with one of the projects.   # Create a workflow with 2 statuses """"*Source Code Peer Review*"""" and """"*DV Peer Review Passed*"""" and add a transition """"*Approve Source Code Changes*"""", which takes the issue from  the ticket in state """"*Source Code Peer Review*"""" to """"*DV Peer Review Passed""""*. Add a condition to workflow """"This transition will only execute if there are no *Open* Crucible reviews related to this issue."""" (The naming conventions can be base on user choice).   # Update the workflow scheme used by this project to use this workflow for this issue type """"Modify Individual Module"""" and one of the standard issue type (say bug).   # Link both Jira 8.5.4 and FECR 4.8.0 through application links.   # Create a Jira issue of """"*Bug*"""" type and a sub-task of """"*Modify Individual Module*"""".   # Create a Crucible review and add both the issues to linked issues in Crucible review and ensure that 1 open review is highlighted in both Jira issues.   # Now execute the transitions for issue type """"*Modify Individual Module*"""" and see it complete successfully without checking any conditions.   # Try the same steps for issue type """"*Bug*"""" and see the condition is applied and transition option will not appear as long as the Crucible review is in open state.    h3. Expected Results  - As we can associate same workflows to standard and sub-task issue types, both should respect the condition """"This transition will only execute if there are no *Open* Crucible reviews related to this issue"""" evaluation before the transition.  - If this not applicable for sub-task issue types, it should be clearly documented and needs to be added as a current limitation to the the feature.    h3. Actual Results  - The sub-task issue types wont respect the workflow condition """"This transition will only execute if there are no *Open* Crucible reviews related to this issue"""" and allow the transitions to happen smoothly.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Importing XML backup in Jira 8 with Oracle 12 database fails with ORA-00955: Name is Already Used by an Existing Object"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    This issue has been noted with *ORA-00955* error in other places, but the other bugs mention Greenhopper (Software suite) specifically.  This failure is on an app table.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Take an XML backup from a site with Zephyr installed.    # Import the XML backup into a site with Oracle 12c database.      The driver does not seem to matter (version 12 vs. version 19).    h3. Expected Results    XML import completes without error.     _IF it did encounter an error_, then we also expect that dropping the table or sequence would fix it (it does not help).   h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround    _Deleting the sequence, table, or database does not help as listed in previous bugs._     Instead, you must modify the XML backup, specifically the activeobjects.xml:    The solution is to change the autoIncrement=""""true""""to """"false"""" in a single line of activeobjects.xml, for table name=""""AO_7DEABF_CUSTOM_FIELD""""   * Original line in your system:       * Changed line (solution)      Here are the steps needed for changing the file:   # Unzip the backup.zip file in a specific folder   # Edit the referred line into the table <table name=""""AO_7DEABF_CUSTOM_FIELD"""">   # Save the file   # Add the 2 files in a new zip file   # Copy this file to the recover location and proceed with the restore using Jira admin interface.    It is important that only the 2 files must be zipped, if you put only them in a folder and zip the folder, this will not work."""

summary: "Ability to disable the @mention notifications for a specific project"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently there's no built-in feature to disable @mention notifications for a specific project.  h3. Suggested Solution    As an admin, I should be able to disable/enable the @mention email notifications for any project.  h3. Why this is important    This functionality could help to prevent sensitive information to be exposed when the @mention feature is being used.  h3. Workaround    There are two things that allow @mention to work.   * The @mention uses the *Browse Users* global permission to determine whether they will be able to see the user list or not. So to prevent this you can remove all groups in the Browse Users global permission so your user would not be able to search for the user at all. The downside of doing this is you would not be able to search for the user even in any other field such as Assignee or Reporter. For more details please see [Managing global permissions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/managing-global-permissions-776636359.html].     * The comment field needs to be of *Wiki Style Renderer*. By default; fields like *Description* or *Comment* is set to Wiki Style Renderer. If you change it to Default Text Renderer in your Field Configuration; you will no longer be able to use @mention. However, you'd also not be able to styling your text in the Comment any more and you can't no longer use tags such as the code tag. For instructions please refer to [Specifying field behavior|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/specifying-field-behavior-776636443.html]."""
comments: "It would also be useful if @mentions respected the Notification Scheme of a project. Right now in 8.5.4 you can remove the Notification Scheme from a project, for example to stop a staging Jira sending confusing email when people are testing changes in it. But if someone uses @mention email is sent to the mentioned person.     *I would like the @mention code to look at the notification scheme for its issue's project and not send email if the notification scheme is empty*"

Sarah A    Atlassian | Jira Server Support"

summary: "JiraHomeHealthCheck doesn't have detailed logging for error path"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JiraHomeHealthCheck doesn't have detailed logging for error path    Code in {{com.atlassian.jira.health.checks.JiraHomeHealthCheck}} will log a message in case of happy flow:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  private final LazyReference<Either<JiraHomeStartupCheckFailure, File>> homeDirRef = new LazyReference<Either<JiraHomeStartupCheckFailure, File>>() {          @Override          protected Either<JiraHomeStartupCheckFailure, File> create() throws Exception {              final Either<JiraHomeStartupCheckFailure, File> result = getConfiguredJiraHome().flatMap(jiraHome -> validateJiraHome(jiraHome));              result.forEach(homeDir -> log.info(""""The jira.home directory '"""" + homeDir.getAbsolutePath() + """"' is validated and locked for exclusive use by this instance.""""));              return result;          }  {code}    Eg.      At the same time, there is not much logging in case of error path:      h3. Suggested Solution  Add additional logging    h3. Note  For the reference, validation checks done by JiraHomeHealthCheck:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}     // Validate the jira.home, and create the directory if required      // Note that we only save jiraHomeDirectory if everything is valid.      private Either<JiraHomeStartupCheckFailure, File> validateJiraHome(final String jiraHome) {          final File proposedJiraHome = new File(jiraHome);          return checkProposedHomeAbsolute(proposedJiraHome, jiraHome)                  .flatMap(this::checkWebappPathCollision)                  .flatMap(this::checkJiraHomeCreationIsPossible)                  .flatMap(this::createJiraHomeAndSharedHome)                  .flatMap(this::lockJiraHome);      }  {code}      h3.Workaround  None"""

description: """h3. Issue Summary  The recent changes in the batched notifications contain bug that prevents the emails containing mentions to be sent immediately. Steps to Reproduce   # Set longer interval, e.g. 10 minutes on /secure/admin/ConfigureBatching!default.jspa   # @mention somebody    h3. Expected Results  The batched email containing mentions should be sent almost immediately (approx 1 minute).  h3. Actual Results  The mention mail is sent after at most the configured interval period (at most 10 minutes).    Debug mail logging may be enabled to trace the batching mechanism in atlassian-jira-outgoing-mail.log  h3. Workaround  Set smaller interval. """

summary: "Security scanners have been recommending on having Clear-Site-Data header in response header when users log out."
description: """Security team has been reporting on security threat due to absence of Clear-Site-Data header when user logs out of Jira. Details follows:    h2. Problem Statement:    Storing plaintext sensitive data in client side local storage makes the data easily accessible by anyone who gains privileged access to the client system. This bypasses user authentication enforced by the application.    In addition to data leakage in shared client environments, such as a public computer's browser, a cross-site scripting (XSS) flaw allows attackers to easily access sensitive data.  h2. Details:    Configure your application to send the Clear-Site-Data header when the user logs out. Configure the header using the following directives:    """"cache"""": clear all cached files for this origin   """"cookies"""": clear all cookies for the domain (both HTTP and HTTPS)   """"storage"""": clear all locally stored data, including ServiceWorker registrations and appCache data   """"executionContexts"""":   """"*"""": all of the above, including additional future directives   For example, the header shown below clears cookies and Local Storage.    Clear-Site-Data: """"cookies"""", """"storage""""   The header shown below clears everything and reloads all application contexts:    Clear-Site-Data: """"*""""   The browser is not a secure storage area. Therefore, it is recommended to clear client-side application data when the user logs out."""

Suggested Solution  * Increase the cache limit  * Increase TTL      h3. Workaround   None"""

summary: "'duplicate key' SQL exceptions for Active Objects tables after XML import and switching database to PostgresSQL"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    'duplicate key' SQL exceptions for Active Objects tables after XML import and switching database to PostgresSQL  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Take an XML backup   # Switch database from Oracle (for example) to PostgresSQL   # Import XML backup (is completed successfully) but there are lot of SQL exceptions only for Active Objects tables    h3. Expected Results    JIRA would import the data with no issues at all  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file: Complete [^Stack trace]    h3. Workaround   * Search for unique Active Objects tables       * Use this KB article to fix the unique AO tables   ** [Duplicate key value errors in logs in Jira Server using PostgreSQL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/duplicate-key-value-errors-in-logs-in-jira-server-using-postgresql-958771364.html]   * Fix sequences by following a recommendation by PostgreSQL's official wiki space    ** [Fixing Sequences|http://wiki.postgresql.org/wiki/Fixing_Sequences]"""
comments: "Just had this occur with Jira version 8.7.1 + Postgres 9.6 restoring an XML backup to a replica environment using the same versions of Jira and Postgres.  Doesn't seem like switching database types is the cause here.    It broke some of our plugins catastrophically to the point where the environment was not usable as a replica.    On repeating the same restore process on another identical environment (automated deployment template) it completed successfully without any errors. "

summary: "Batched notifications tables can lead to high CPU usage on MSSQL"
description: """h4. Problem    Batched notifications execute periodical tasks (every 14 days) to clean up old events from the {{AO_733371_EVENT}} and {{AO_733371_EVENT_RECIPIENT}} tables. However, on unknown circumstances, this leads to extremely slow queries on the database if using Microsoft SQL Server. This issue seems to happen more often on older MSSQL versions such as 2012 and 2014.    The issue seems to be related to JRASERVER-65168 where it was described that single column indexes used by Jira might lead to this problem. Even though that specific bug is resolved, it wasn't created multi-column indexes for the batched notifications tables.    (i) The same problem can cause delays in batched notifications delivery.    Example query:    h4. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up Batched Notifications (enabled by default on Jira 8.0)   # Have a large number of email notifications occurring daily   # Monitor the database CPU usage with graphs    h4. Expected Results    The cleanup tasks occur without affecting the database performance, as it does on other DBMSs such as Postgres.  h4. Actual Results    The cleanup tasks take several hours to complete, with the CPU spiking to 100% usage during that period. General slowness is experienced on Jira due to this.    h4. Note    When analyzing thread dumps, we should see 2 Caesium threads running batched notification jobs:  - Thread 1    - Thread 2:       When analyzing long running queries on the DB side, we should see long queries that look like this:      h4. Workaround  No confirmed workaround available.     # Users that have upgrade to newer MSSQL versions (such as 2017) noticed that the occurrence of the problem is smaller.   # If you're using newer MSSQL versions and experiencing this problem, verify the compatibility mode set to this database. If it was upgraded from previous MSSQL versions, it might be set to mode 120/110, and there was improved query execution on MSSQL 2017 as can be verified on [this Microsoft document|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/statements/alter-database-transact-sql-compatibility-level?view=sql-server-2017#differences-between-compatibility-level-120-and-level-130]"""
comments: "This issue affects the operation of our Jira instance on MS SQL 2014" [SEP] "This affect us as well ion Jira 8.10.1 with MSSQL 2017"

summary: "Support STARTTLS without hoops"
description: """Connecting to an SMTP server that uses STARTTLS is not a strange and unusual circumstance. Simple email applications like Thunderbird handle the set up of these connections without any fuss, yet Jira Server still doesn't support it without moving to the rather fragile JNDI setup involving moving jar files (which currently seems broken BTW as the jar files in the doco do not match the reality in the application.)    I honestly don't know why you don't just handle it.    e.g. SMTP    Server: mail.aserver.com    port: 587    connection security: STARTTLS or TLS or none    Auth method: Plain text (normal password)    username: blah    password: secret    If you can handle that, you can connect to gmail too"""

summary: "Duplicate Fields Are Added To A Screen By An Known Reason"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Duplicate fields are added to a screen and it causes errors when checking the Required fields to create an issue in a project using a REST API, or when accessing Custom Fields configuration page from JIRA → Settings → Custom Fields, etc.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to JIRA Administration > Issues ># Screens   # Look for the problematic screen   # Click Configure    h3. Expected Results    The screen configuration screen should open without any error  h3. Actual Results    The exception below is thrown on the UI:   !Screenshot 2020-07-22 at 12.49.43.png!    and exception below is thrown in the {{atlassian-jira.log}} file:    h3. Workaround  # Run the query b below to find out the duplicated value:   {code}   select f.name, i.fieldidentifier, count(*)  from fieldscreen f, fieldscreenlayoutitem i, fieldscreentab t  where f.id = t.fieldscreen  and i.fieldscreentab = t.id  group by f.name, i.fieldidentifier having count(*) > 1;  {code}  If there is a duplicate, an example of a result is below:  {code}                name               | fieldidentifier | count     ---------------------------------+-----------------+-------     SSD: Scrum Default Issue Screen | assignee        |     2  {code}   # Navigate to _JIRA Administration > Issues > Screens_   # Look for the _Screen_ mentioned in the SQL query result e.g. _""""SSD: Scrum Default Issue Screen""""_   # Click _Configure_ and look for the field e.g. _""""assignee""""_   # Remove one of the fields from the screen.    (i)  If the duplicate field is not shown on the screen or if the screen cannot be opened, the solution is to delete duplicate field references directly from the database.  (!) *Please back up your database before proceeding with this workaround. Also, if possible, test the workaround on a staging environment before applying it to production.*   The steps are as follows:   # Identify and write down ID value for each duplicate field reference; screen name and field identifier need to be modified accordingly. For the current example, the ID can be identified as follows::  {code:java}  select f.name, i.id, i.fieldidentifier  from fieldscreen f, fieldscreenlayoutitem i, fieldscreentab t  where f.id = t.fieldscreen and i.fieldscreentab = t.id  and f.name like '%SSD: Scrum Default Issue Screen%'  and i.fieldidentifier='assignee';  {code}   # Delete all duplicate rows from fieldscreenlayoutitem table and retain only one row.  {code:java}  delete from fieldscreenlayoutitemwhere id = <duplicate_id>;  {code}   # Restart Jira"""

summary: "Add Issue Ids in the log for message, 'Unable to index custom date field', generated during reindexing"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    During reindexing, an 'Unable to index custom date field' message is logged when a custom date field for an issue has an invalid value, but the Id of the issue is not recorded.    Logging the Id of the affected issue when this message gets generated would help save time correcting the invalid value.  h3. Suggested Solution    Log affected Issue Ids for 'Unable to index custom date field' messages generated during reindexing operations.  h3. Workaround    If there's only a single issue with an invalid value, the problematic issue can be found by utilizing a SQL query:      !https://confluence.atlassian.com/s/en_GB/7901/af536c7c6dffcc1d697b914b797aa7f2f306b4f8/_/images/icons/emoticons/information.svg!  Replace \{customFieldId} with the Id of the custom date field logged in the WARN message       Another workaround for finding the invalid value is detailed in [Reindexing fails with Unable to index custom date field error in Jira server|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/reindexing-fails-with-unable-to-index-custom-date-field-error-in-jira-server-294488445.html]:  {quote}To find the invalid date you must run the SQL query below in your database:      !https://confluence.atlassian.com/s/en_GB/7901/af536c7c6dffcc1d697b914b797aa7f2f306b4f8/_/images/icons/emoticons/information.svg!  The <CUSTOM_FIELD_ID> in the above query can be found in the error code as part of the string: """"customfield_10267""""  {quote}"""

summary: "Upgrading Jira 8 between minor versions will always indicate that setenv.sh has customizations that are not applied"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When upgrading Jira 8 between minor versions (8.6 > 8.8 for example, or 8.10 > 8.11 as another upgrade example), after the upgrade is complete, the admin will see a warning page that explains that some configuration/settings files that contained customizations could not be copied over. This is expected for any startup/installation file where the admin might have made changes. However in this case, the [$JIRAINSTALL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/jira-application-installation-directory-938847745.html]/bin/setenv.sh file will always appear on this list, even when the admin has made no changes at all to that file.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # install Jira Software/Core 8.10.0 (other 8 versions likely affected)   # Complete the setup and make sure Jira works   # Make no changes to setenv.sh   # upgrade that existing installation directory to a newer version, such as 8.11.0   # start Jira after upgrade    h3. Expected Results    Expecting that there are no actual changes to that setenv.sh file and the initial startup would not indicate there are when you first start Jira after the upgrade.  h3. Actual Results    Admins will always see that the setenv.sh file as having had customized changes to it that have not been applied to the upgraded install    h3. Workaround    The warning page does not prevent upgrades to Jira in this case. This is just a warning message that appears to be a false positive in most cases. You can still dismiss this and complete the upgrade."""
comments: "We're impacted by this issue, and with a multi node DC cluster, you need to ignore these warnings on each node. To get around this issue for now, we've disabled the Post Upgrade Landing Page (PULP) module itself, but that means there may be warnings we should be caring about that we're not getting notified of, so would prefer a proper fix to this issue.         Looking at the contents of our /customisations/ directories, we can see we have the following tracked:    ./atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes  ./atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/jira-application.properties  ./atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/email-template-id-mappings.xml  ./atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/seraph-config.xml  ./atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties         But we also have customisations to the below files that don't seem to get tracked:    ./bin/setenv.sh (what JRASERVER-71338 is about)  ./conf/server.xml  ./atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/crowd.properties         We use Puppet to enforce configuration of all these files, so we ha

summary: "Changing User Directory in Crowd disables user in Jira"

Actual Results  Jira disables the user, striking the name and adding a [X] at the end.  Running another full synchronization in Jira reenables the user.    h3. Workaround  Run a full synchronization again"""

summary: "Batched email notifications format looks different when default language is set to French,German in v8.10"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the default language in the instance is set as French or German, the batched email notifications looks different. The bug is not reproducible in older versions.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set the default language in the instance as French (or German)   # Set up outgoing email in your instance.   # Enable batched notifications.   # Make some updates in an issue which triggers batched notification.    h3. Expected Results    The notification email should like 'There are 2 Updates' followed by the update  h3. Actual Results    The email looks like 'There is' as in the below screenshot.   !Screenshot 2020-07-21 at 6.58.24 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Download and install Jira language pack with updated/fixed translation keys.    Basically download language pack for Jira (in 8.14.0.v20200826125100 version - translations should be backwards compatible so 8.14 should work on lower versions) and install it via UPM.    Basically [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/install-a-custom-language-pack-for-jira-server-295305642.html] but without unpacking and updating translations. The lang pack to download should be named as """"jira-core-language-pack-[lang]/8.14.0.v20200826125100/""""    Eg. to fix German language download proper """"jira-core-language-pack"""" jar from [https://packages.atlassian.com/maven/com/atlassian/translations/] eg. [https://packages.atlassian.com/maven/com/atlassian/translations/jira-core-language-pack-de_DE/8.14.0.v20200826125100/] and install via UPM.  h3. Existing Workaround:    Adding the following code into an Announcement Banner on JIRA will hide all the notifications that can be closed on JIRA:        (!) *Note*: This will hide all the messages that are configured under a HTML div with the following classes and also the Announcement Banner itself:      """

summary: "Top menu dropdown does not scroll down to show all options  if the screen is small."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you have many options in the """"Issue"""" dropdown(in top menu), and if you have a small screen, some options are out of screen. However, you cannot scroll down to see these options.         It is a similar issue to JRASERVER-69709: More button dropdown not shown properly if the screen is small. (fixed in Jira Server and Data Center 8.6.0), but happened in different places.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1   Change resolution of display to a small screen mode.   # Step 2   Click Issue dropdown from top menu, some options are out of screen.    h3. Expected Results    scroll down works to show the options out of screen.  h3. Actual Results    scroll down does not work well, the options out of screen cannot be seen.  h3. Workaround    No."""

summary: "Clarify ""Restored index backup"" logging message for index copy between nodes"
description: """h3. Current State:    In Jira Data Center, the search index is replicated between nodes automatically through the shared home. This process adds logging to track the progress – for example:    When this process completes, the following message is logged:    However, the restore can fail between these two points and the initial message {{Restored index backup}} is misleading as it indicates the snapshot restore is complete at this time.  h3. Suggested Improvement:    Remove or change the message {{Restored index backup}} within the logs to indicate the restore is not complete."""

description: """h3. Problem Definition    When adding the field Log Work to a screen, the Work Description field is not displayed.     _When clicking the More -> Log Work, the work description is displayed_    !Work Description is displayed.png|thumbnail!   _After adding the Log Work field on a Screen the Work Description is not displayed:_  !Work Description is not saved.png|thumbnail!  h3. summary: "User creation & update on-the-fly with data from SAML attributes"
description: """Currently, while configuring SAML with Jira, it is required using an User Directory (internal or LDAP) to manage application access and such configuration must be done upfront, as there is no automated way to synchronize users between internal directories and the SAML identity provider (as described [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/adding-saml-integration-to-your-existing-user-management-infrastructure-861244976.html#AddingSAMLintegrationtoyourexistingusermanagementinfrastructure-Usingyourapplication'sinternaldirectory]).    The suggestion here is to create this automation, where user creation and update can be done automatically while log in Jira using SAML.  """

summary: "Upgrade the bundled version of Apache Tomcat to 8.5.57"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The recently disclosed vulnerability regarding Apache Tomcat    * [CVE-2020-13934|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13934]    affects the following versions:              Apache Tomcat 8.x from 8.5.1 to 8.5.56      Apache Tomcat 9.x from 9.0.0.M5 to 9.0.36      Apache Tomcat 10.x from 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M6    Additionally, the following disclosed vulnerability regarding Tomcat:    * [CVE-2020-13935|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13935]    affects the following versions:        Apache Tomcat 7.x from 7.0.27 to 7.0.104      Apache Tomcat 8.x from 8.5.1 to 8.5.56      Apache Tomcat 9.x from 9.0.0.M5 to 9.0.36      Apache Tomcat 10.x from 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M6    We should bundle a more recent version of Tomcat so that Jira is not affected by this in the future.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Check the CVE reports:  ** [CVE-2020-13934|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13934]  ** [CVE-2020-13935|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13935]    h3. Expected Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Actual Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Workaround    * Manually upgrade Tomcat according to our [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-upgrade-apache-tomcat-version-used-by-jira-879957866.html]."""
comments: "This is critical for us and needs to be in 8.5 the enterprise release.    Is there a plan for this?" [SEP] "There are also two other vulnerabilities that affect Tomcat used in Jira    #  *CVE-2020-11996* Affect Tomcat 8.5.0 to 8.5.55  [CVE-2020-11996|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-11996]  # *CVE-2019-12418* Affect Tomcat  8.5.0 to 8.5.47 [CVE-2019-12418|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2019-12418]    We need a long term supported version *8.5.7* ASAP    Thanks, Luca     " [SEP] "This affects Confluence 7.6 also. This vulnerability was published a month ago and has CVSS3 score of 7.5 High. Can we please get an update on when this will be fixed or some kind of document on if this affects Confluence and Jira and if so how to mitigate the issue?"

summary: "Bulk Migration during WF publish should use pagination"
description: """The issue migration done when publishing WF changes loads all affected issue data at once. On big projects this becomes a problem, because this uses a lot of heap. The migration should use pagination to avoid massive heap usage. """
comments: "Due to this, we have to make such changes 'weekend-only' to avoid impacting performance during business hours."

summary: "Vague error message: ""Unable to find the username of the principal."" - which username? Which principal?"
description: """Without warning, a JIRA v8.6.0 server fails while synchronising an LDAP directory with the following error:    -  """"Unable to find the username of the principal.""""    The error message does not tell us the username, nor does the error message tell us the principal, and so makes life unnecessarily difficult for admins.    Acceptance Criteria:  - Add the username (or the criteria being used to find the username) to the error message.  - Add the principal (or the criteria being used to find the principal) to the error message.    2020-07-17 14:12:43,132+0200 Caesium-1-3 INFO ServiceRunner     [c.a.crowd.directory.DbCachingRemoteDirectory] FULL synchronisation for directory [ 10000 ] starting  2020-07-17 14:12:43,208+0200 Caesium-1-3 ERROR ServiceRunner     [c.a.c.d.l.mapper.entity.LDAPUserAttributesMapper] The following record does not have a username: NameAwareAttribute; attributes: {sn=NameAwareAttribute; id: sn; hasValuesAsNames: false; orderMatters: false; values: [Template], nsuniqueid=NameAwareAttribute; id: nsUniqueId; hasValuesAsNames: false; orderMatters: false; values: [022efe81-76d411ea-a1b6ce9f-216b1919]}  2020-07-17 14:12:43,210+0200 Caesium-1-3 INFO ServiceRunner     [c.a.crowd.directory.DbCachingRemoteDirectory] failed synchronisation complete for directory [ 10000 ] in [ 78ms ]  2020-07-17 14:12:43,290+0200 Caesium-1-3 ERROR ServiceRunner     [c.atlassian.scheduler.JobRunnerResponse] Unable to synchronise directory  com.atlassian.crowd.exception.OperationFailedException: org.springframework.ldap.UncategorizedLdapException: Unable to find the username of the principal.    """

Expected Results  User should be able to Check/Uncheck the check box    h3. Actual Results  User cannot Check/Uncheck the check box    h3. Workaround    The only workaround is to click on text associated with the checkbox to check/uncheck     !image-2020-07-17-17-35-22-585.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "RequestCache being used in an invalid context"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There's warnings being triggered on the latest Jira versions due to the 'RequestCacheImpl' being used outside of a request thread by 'JMXInstrumentsLoggingService' and 'PlatformInstrumentsLoggingService'. This will not work in the future, so we need to fix it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira Software 8.10.0 or 8.11.0    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround    Setting the log level of the {{com.atlassian.jira.cache.request.RequestCacheImpl}} package to ERROR per the documentation [Logging and profiling|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/logging-and-profiling-938847671.html]    (i) This configuration will filter out relevant warnings as well.    h4. Temporary way to set the package to ERROR (this setting will go away after a Jira restart)    - In Jira, go to *⚙ > System > Logging and profiling page*  - Scroll down to *Default Loggers > Configure logging level for another package*, and add {{com.atlassian.jira.cache.request.RequestCacheImpl}} package with the ERROR level      h4. Permanent way to set the package to ERROR (this setting will remain after a Jira restart)    - Edit the file {{<JIRA_INSTALLATION>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties}}  - Add the 2 following lines at the end of the file and save it:    - Restart the Jira application    """
comments: "Hi it seems like minor bug as it is with logging. but its impact is very high. Please see my comments below.   # As Jira administrator managing 1000s of users 25% of my day goes through looking at logs and issues in jira. Now with so much logging it is just impossible for me to find any valid information with the sequence of event as the logs are pretty useless now.   # Ours is a pretty dynamic environment where nodes come and goes. So it is just not possible to keep modifying the logs daily.    Now unnecessary we have to put the temporary fix and when resolved again remove the fix.     " [SEP] "Duplicate of JRASERVER-71310"

Additionally, it would make sense for them to be able to post comments restricted to any role.    For example, they could direct a comment to the Developers, without being part of the Developers role.    This could even be configurable in the global options."""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    After the upgrade on windows from JSW 7.13.4 to JSW 8.11 RC01, the """"Jira failed to start"""" error appears on the screen. However no negative impact to the instance is recognised.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Using instenv:   # Deploy instenv Windows box with postrgreSQL rds, no application installed   # Once deployed, install JIRA 7.13 connect to PostgreSQL if needed but I think h2 should be good enough   # Modify xmx value to 2048mb cause it's the minimum recommended value for 8.x Jira   # Upgrade to JIRA 8.11, start Jira   # As soon JIRA starts 'starting up' after the upgrade, open JIRA in the browser and monitor the progress.    h3. A workaround will be added here when available    *Note:*    After clicking """"Take me to my Jira home"""", the instance works properly."""

summary: "Logs of usage of RequestCache without context is flooding the log files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The warning logs were added, because starting from Jira 9 it would not be possible to use RequestCache without the context - it's incorrect and slow. Context is necessary for proper creation and termination of the RequestCache. Without it, RequestCache does not behave like a cache - it will fetch data from database for every call.     It was straightforward task, but with unexpected results. It seems that in Jira DC incorrect usage of RequestCache is very common. So common, that it is flooding the log files of running instances & makes the testing very hard.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira Software 8.10.0 or 8.11.0    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround    Setting the log level of the {{com.atlassian.jira.cache.request.RequestCacheImpl}} package to ERROR per the documentation [Logging and profiling|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/logging-and-profiling-938847671.html]    (i) This configuration will filter out relevant warnings as well.  h4. Temporary way to set the package to ERROR (this setting will go away after a Jira restart)   - In Jira, go to *⚙ > System > Logging and profiling page*   - Scroll down to *Default Loggers > Configure logging level for another package*, and add {{com.atlassian.jira.cache.request.RequestCacheImpl}} package with the ERROR level    h4. Permanent way to set the package to ERROR (this setting will remain after a Jira restart)   - Edit the file {{<JIRA_INSTALLATION>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties}}   - Add the 2 following lines at the end of the file and save it:       - Restart the Jira application"""

summary: "Searching for groups with special characters ""#"" or ""&"" on ""Add users to a role"" makes search stop advancing after typing the first special character"

Issue Summary  In case of looking for groups with the special characters *&* or *#* on name by using the """"Add users to a role"""" feature, it stops working at the first special character that is typed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a group with the special characters *&* or *#* (E.g.: """"#TestingGroup 2020"""");   # Try to add that group into your project role by typing """"#TestingGroup"""".    h3. Expected Results  JIRA shows the specific JIRA group;    h3. Actual Results  JIRA shows every group with """"#"""" on its name.   !image-2020-07-15-17-11-58-162.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Unclear Captcha images on login page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On a server instance login page, some users may be presented with unclear CAPTCHA's that are unclear/impossible to decipher.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Refresh the captcha box/reload the login page    h3. Expected Results  CAPTCHA code is clear enough for a human to read    h3. Actual Results  Some codes can result in letters with significant overlap, delaying the login process    h3. Workaround    Refreshing the CAPTCHA box"""

summary: "Truncated Kanban Board column header names"
description: """The Kanban Board column header names are getting truncated when creating a board with more columns and their names are long or have the MIN/MAX constraint labels.    Need to have an option to edit this header layout which lets the header appear completely. Also, an option to move the MIN/MAX constraint labels below the name, should works.    !Screen Shot 2020-07-15 at 13.41.04.png|width=420,height=211!"""

summary: "Configurable limit for uploaded user avatar files"
description: """Currently, uploaded image files for user avatars/profile pictures are limited to 10 megapixels and automatically resized to roughly 48x48 pixels. Uploading files larger than 10 megapixels results in an error.     It'd be nice to have a configurable limit to this file upload in case users want to upload larger files for their user avatars.    """

summary: "Sending an invite type of email to a Jira's mail handler can result in an loop"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Sending an invite type of email to a Jira's mail handler configured with, for example, O365 mailbox shared via Secure POP, can make the Mail Processor go over all the messages received after the invite as they queue in the inbox every single minute, but then not being able to process them and delete them from the inbox.    As a result, once the invite messages are removed, the inbox is cleaned up but the Mail Processor starts to process a new ticket that is a repetition of previously opened tickets as many times as minutes passed from the moment the invite arrived, which can cause several issues.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure a O365 mail handler in Jira with POP   # Send an invite to this email in between normal emails that will generate tickets    h3. Expected Results     # Jira process the email or filter it out    h3. Actual Results     # Jira can't process the email and get's stuck in a loop    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:      h3. Workaround    Remove the invite mail from the inbox."""

summary: "ability to extend the period of link validity for password changes on create user notification"
description: """Dear Atlassian team,    I would like to raise the change request in Jira server, as for now when we create the user in user management via create user on our Jira server, where we click on an option """"send an invitation link"""", but the issue is that the invitation link is valid only for 24 hours and there is no way to change this default behaviour. Can it be implemented to jira, it really should make our lifes easier.     E.g. New starter start on monday and I will create his account on friday, so when user receive his invitation mail, the link inside this mail is invalid because 2 days already passed.     For your information the issue was already in solution in ticket: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-37072 and also in service desk [https://getsupport.atlassian.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/20/GHS-194001].     Thanks a lot,  Kind regards,    Adrián Bárdossy"""

summary: "vertical scroll bar is not present in the description column"
description: """Vertical scroll bar is only viewable when editing the description or when creating a ticket but on saving the description or on ticket creation, the scroll bar does not appear anymore but instead full details view of the description shows .         *Workaround:*    Currently, we can include the comments inside a code block but table or text cannot be. modified to bold or Italic inside the code block      """

summary: "Upgarding from versions below 8.0 to 8.5.x or above removes in-line editing of certain fields"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrading from any version below 8.0 to 8.5.x or above, some customers notice that the labels field becomes non-editable in-line. The field seem to be removed from the edit screen scheme and this prevent editing of labels in-line within the issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Unable to replicate the problem so far with a upgrade from 7.13.11 to 8.5.4 with sample projects and issues created, but have had sporadic cases from customers complaining about the same  h3. Expected Results    No change in behaviour of in-line edit of any of the fields in the issue or dropping of the field from edit screen scheme  h3. Actual Results    The in-line edit of fields like labels and priority are disabled.    h3. Workaround  #  Click on the Administration link.  #  Under the Fields section, click on the Field Configurations link.  #  Locate the Field Configuration that is associated with the affected project and click on the Configure link under the Operations column.  #  Locate the Label field and click on Screens.  #  Tick on the respective edit screen and the inline editing would be available again."""
comments: "I have a test Jira DC instance that was upgraded from 7.13.8 to 8.5.4 but am not able to reproduce this problem"

summary: "Allow configuration of Required Fields per screen"
description: """Allow us to say that this field is required for this screen but not this screen or work item type.  We understand that we can do this on the validation on the workflow, but that then requires configuring at each project and workflow level and cannot just be done at the enterprise level.    Example:    * On a Bug we have a field called found in.  We want to make this required on all bugs.   * On a Story this is not a field on the screen, it doesn't make sense on the story   * Both the bug and story have the same workflow as for each team because they are both backlog items.   * Each team gets to decide their workflow.     """

summary: "Add Config Option To Require Every New Custom Field to Add a Context"
description: """Would it be possible to expose a system configuration option that would enable an organization, if they choose, to enforce that any newly created custom field always be assigned a project-specific context and to automatically delete the global context?    This would never stop a custom field from being reused.  You simply add another project-specific context.    But this would stop the sprawl of custom fields into field configurations across all projects in a Jira instance, and would lessen the probability that, with the creation of each new custom field, we necessarily make Jira slower for everyone.    Because unfortunately not every organization has a dedicated Jira admin or set of admins that force the reuse of custom fields and actively prevent or discourage their creation."""

summary: "Provide a REST API for obtaining the External user ID"
description: """Currently, it is not possible to obtain the External user ID via REST API. The suggestion is to either provide a REST API that provides the information related to the external user management or to add the information like the external user ID to GET /rest/api/2/user."""

summary: "The word Issues is not translated properly in Kanban board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The word *+Issues+* is not translated properly in Kanban board    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change your user language to Deutsch (Deutschland) or German.    # Navigate to any of your Kanban board configurations.     h3. Expected Results    The word *+Issues+* is translated to *+Vorgänge+* like it is on the JIRA header.     h3. Actual Results    The word is not translated but instead, shows as Issues:    !Screenshot 2020-07-13 at 11.50.55 PM.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2020-07-13 at 11.50.40 PM.png|thumbnail!     || English || German ||   | We're only showing recently modified issues. | Es werden nur kürzlich geänderte Issues angezeigt. |   | Hide completed issues older than | Abgeschlossene Issues ausblenden, die älter sind als |     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira allows illegal edit (issue type) through /rest/api/2/issue/<ISSUE-KEY>"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira doesn't error out if we edit the issue type from REST API to a value which is not allowed. Issue with issueType=""""X"""" can be changed to issueType=""""Y"""" even though X and Y don't share the same workflow if done from REST API.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create two *issue types* (_Example:_ """"Support"""" & """"TR"""")   * Make the two issue types tied to *two different workflows*   * Create an issue of issueType=""""Support""""   * Edit the issue Type to TR from REST:     * Resulting issue will not act according to the new workflow    h3. Expected Results    Jira throws error response code as Illegal action  h3. Actual Results    Jira responds with *204* and the new issue type shows inconsistent workflow state and transition options:    !2020-07-13_16-06-17.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    NO known workaround to make jira stop this action from REST.    From customer's perspective below are the workaround options to get the affected issue keys back to working state:  * Run Integrity checker to find affected issue keys   * Use the same endpoint to set the issue type back to original issue type and then use the UI and *Move* the issue to the new issue type instead of edit   * Cloning the issue gets the current workflow status(es) and Transition(s) to show up   * Make both of the issue Types share the same workflow rendering this bug not applicable to begin with  """

summary: "While running long JQL the Jira UI is not rendering properly "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While running long JQL the Jira UI is not rendering properly  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enter the longest JQL in under issues dropdown > search for issues and click on enter    h3. Expected Results    The Jira UI should show the results with and the area where we enter JQL should expand but it's not rendering properly.      h3. Actual Results    The page will not render properly and the output will not be displayed completely      h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: """Its working fine other versions like 8.5.4"" - that astonishes me. That JQL is way more complex that I have ever used. I would be recommending my users work with a small set of queries, perhaps even a remote script to use code to decide if an issue fits in the set of wanted isues." [SEP] "I saw that text was removed, does that mean that this didn't work in 8.5.4 either?" [SEP] "Hi [~mdoar2],    We have removed the text that says ""Its working fine other versions like 8.5.4"" because it was working fine in versions till 8.9 from 8.9 seems the problem introduced, which is the reason we have removed the text that states ""Its working fine other versions like 8.5.4"" as the UI looks good previous versions! However, if you have further queries or if you've observed this behavior in other versions please feel free to raise a ticket at https://support.atlassian.com/jira-software-server/     !Screenshot 2020-07-13 at 11.44.54 AM.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2020-07-13 at 11.45.13 AM.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot

summary: "Replace all the HTTP links with HTTPS in the logo"
description: """Compliance Rules of customers require that every URL in the body is set to use """"https"""". The Jira or Confluence Systems always puts a logo with a http:// link below in the footer. This nonsecure link redirects to the secure """"HTTPS"""" link. We need to replace all the HTTP with HTTPS. Please help us to fix this as we have been notified of vulnerabilities by our security team. Request that you consider changing the http:// Link to https:// in future releases."""

summary: "Filter out special characters in Full Name field "
description: """While creating/editing Full name in User profile, special characters are getting accepted.    For example <>/""""'(){}=-\ can be used as a full name. This is causing problems for a few customers.      Request to have additional validations for the Full Name field.    """

summary: "Restrict moving Issues based on project category"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, JIRA has a dropdown list of the available projects, when moving an issue, which let the users to move issues to all the projects they want and have *move issue* permission.     h3. Suggested Solution  Allow administrators the ability to control which projects will appear by each project category, when the Jira users are moving the issues"""
