summary: "Ability to customize the Look and Feel of Agile Boards"
description: """Once there are multiple boards associated with a specific project, users may start to confuse these boards and this may lead to incorrect input being fed through these boards.    It would be great if administrators were able to customize the Look and Feel of Agile boards, to allow users to differentiate these boards."""

"SAML Single Sign on URL redirection in loop issue for non-authorized pages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    We are facing an issue when user have a bookmarked page (JIRA admin pages e.g https://<base-url>/secure/admin/ViewApplicationProperties.jspa) and when they directly try to launch the bookmarked URL we see that there is a redirection between sign-on url and Jira url. This redirection keeps going on in a loop. The only way to come out of it is by closing the browser.    Note: Disable websudo does not solve the loop.    Use Case: Admin users sometimes need to sign in with a non-admin user account for test purposes. Eventually, an admin page may be incorrectly accessed, incurring in a loop solved only when closing the browser.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * SAML enabled:   ** We used Onelogin and SSO 2.0 on Jira   ** Jira Internal Directory   ** We did create a domain on [https://www.onelogin.com/developer-signup]   ** We set a username matching Jira's username (NameID as Username)   * Bookmark an URL from the admin secure section, for instance the user management at http://<jira base URL>/secure/admin/user/UserBrowser.jspa   * Open a new incognito browser and sign in using a non-admin user   * Copy and Paste the URL bookmarked on 2     h3. Expected Results   * Permission denied error message, landing at the user dashboard page   or   * Permission denied error message, landing at the login page with the URL cleaned    h3. Actual Results   * There is a redirection between sign-on url and Jira url. This redirection keeps going on in a loop.   * We can observe the following sequence of URLs:      h3. Workaround   * Close the browser to interrupt the loop   * Edit the bookmark, removing the restricted page"""

summary: "API Documentation for 8.9.1 for auditing records should be updated."
description: """Hi Team,    This relates to the API documentation for Jira Server 8.9.1:    Currently we see the API documentation for 8.9.1 for audit records highlights about the end point: /rest/api/2/auditing/record, but when we try to retrieve the records, it throws the error shown below.    Tried with plane request and also by adding a query string.      However the rest point """"*/rest/auditing/1.0/events*"""" works well and we are able to retrieve the results. So there is a need to update the API reference documentation for 8.9.1 with latest working rest end point. summary: "Have option to change ""Reply-to"" email to another verified domain"
description: """We need to have the flexibility for configuring 'Reply-to' for Jira Service Desk notifications.    *How it works now:*    JSD sets """"Reply-to"""" as per configuration in """"Email requests' of the JSD project.  JSD sets """"From"""" as per the configuration of the Notification scheme email.    There could be situations where a customer doesn't want to configure custom email for 'Email requests' (for example, security concern), however would still like to change the """"Reply-to"""" email for the project's notifications.    *Suggestion:*    Have the option to change the """"Reply-to"""" email to match the Notification scheme email (""""From"""" email). In this case, we make sure that we use _verified domain_ of the instance."""

summary: "Allow admins to disable notifications for bulk notifications"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:  Currently, only administrators can disable notifications for changes made through bulk edits.    h3. Suggested Solution:  Add an option for admins to disable notifications on bulk changes for all operations across the system - this would cover bulk operations performed by regular users in the application, as such users can't opt-out of notifications being sent.     In the past, the {{jira.option.bulk.send.notifications}} property existed (but it seems like it never worked), which sounds like a useful property - this one was removed on through [JRASERVER-15660|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-15660].    h3. Why this is needed:  Depending on the scope of the bulk edit, hundreds of thousands of notifications might get generated by a single user. Today, Jira and the mail server are forced to take the hit if a user performs such a change. Having an option such as this one allows admins to reduce the impact of a big bulk edit without requiring temporary changes to a project's notification scheme."""
comments: "Can't project admins of all the issues that are changed also see the check box about sending or not sending email for a bulk change? Jira admins certainly can as of 8.5.4" [SEP] "Hey Matt,    The goal here is to give admins an option to disable notifications for all bulk changes, system-wide. Since regular users can't choose if notifications are sent out or not, this could be preemptively enabled to keep thousands of notifications from being triggered.    I'm going to make some changes to the wording on the description to make the suggestion more clear on this point."

However If the comment button is already at the bottom of screen, It scrolls up automatically and work as expected. We can reproduce it in almost all versions. Expected Results    Screen should scroll up automatically as we click on comment button.  h3. Actual Results    h3. Workaround    As of now we can scroll up manually"""

summary: "keyboard shortcut CMD+ENTER doesn't work anymore when creating an Epic in Issues view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    keyboard shortcut CMD+ENTER doesn't work anymore when creating an Epic in Issues view   As per [Keyboard shortcut|https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/ViewKeyboardShortcuts!default.jspa] the combination CMD+ENTER should submit a description or comment.   In previous version of Jira while creating an Epic from the Project Issues view and pressing CMD+ENTER after entering the issue summary triggered the Epic creation screen.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the project Issues section   # Clic on the bottom on *+Create* Issue   !Screenshot 2020-07-08 at 13.05.56.png|thumbnail!   # Select *Epic* as issue type and after typing the summary, press *CMD+ENTER*   !Screenshot_2020-07-08_at_13_00_16.jpg|thumbnail!   # The page refresh and nothing happen while the Epic creation screen should appear    Observe that the issue is instead created *if any other issue type* is selected.  h3. Expected Results    After a refresh the create epic screen should appear  h3. Actual Results    The page refresh and the Create Epic screen doesn't appear  h3. Workaround    Pressing the *ENTER* key instead trigger the Create Epic Screen"""

summary: "Configurable Kanban columns other than status"
description: """Hi,    This case is related with https://getsupport.atlassian.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/20/GHS-191157  The documentation on Kanban column configuration is very clear – Columns are based on global statuses, but user want to decoupling JIRA boards: columns to status 1-1 relationship.     In agile, we hope we can create any number of columns without being mapped to status, changing status when changing column is optional,  and to track which column a card is in there is a """"BOARD COLUMN"""" field distinct from the status field. It can be used to find where the work item are on the Kanban independently from the work item status and report accurately on work in process.      Hope the feature can be implemented in Jira agile board.         Thanks,    Jing"""

summary: "Ability delete Orphaned Background and Project Re-index Tasks"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Similar to these existing reports, JRASERVER-47045 and JRASERVER-66204, if a background re-index or project re-index is abruptly stopped due to the node becoming unavailable, it prevents a re-index operation to be run:   !Screen Shot 2020-07-07 at 5.40.19 PM.png|thumbnail!     A solution was implemented to unlock index jobs, but this *only works for foreground re-index*: JRASERVER-68616    h3. Suggested Solution  Improve upon the {{/rest/internal/2/reindex/unlock}} endpoint to also detect and unlock project and background re-index jobs.    """

summary: "Implement the ability to impersonate users."
description: """Jira Cloud allows administrators to impersonate users and it would be useful if this feature was available on Server.    """
comments: "There are two or three plugins that provide this feature in Server/Data Center. I agree it's very useful"

summary: "As a Jira admin, I'd like to have more monitoring tools at the Jira UI to help monitor the integration between Jira and GitHub"
description: """As a Jira admin, I'd like to be able to monitor the integration status between Jira and GitHub through the Jira UI, especially on cases where the sync process may be stuck. It is possible for the DVCS syncing process with GitHub to get stuck without any errors being thrown in the log, as the sync itself is still trying to finish. Knowing the status of syncing would make it easier to determine if there is an error and where it is"""

summary: "When bypassing Datacenter SAML SSO, Jira redirects invalid login to SSO login, Captcha not displayed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Jira Datacenter is configured with SAML SSO integration, and with _auth_fallback_ enabled as per the KB: [Bypass SAML authentication for Jira Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/bypass-saml-authentication-for-jira-data-center-869009810.html], if a user tries to login using <JIRA_BASE_URL>/login.jsp?auth_fallback and specifies a wrong credentials, instead of showing an invalid credential message and/or ask to enter Captcha for security validation, the user is taken to SSO login page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Installed Jira Datacenter, tested on Jira 8.4.1.   # Integrate Jira with a SAML SSO provider (Tested with Keycloak).   # Configure SAML as primary authentication from SAML SSO configuration page in Jira   # Create a Jira internal user and set the password.   # Try to login using the URL <JIRA_BASE_URL>/login.jsp?auth_fallback, and specify a wrong set credentials.    h3. Expected Results    Jira should display an invalid credentials message or render a Captcha if the number of failed attempts satisfy the _jira.maximum.authentication.attempts.allowed_ property.  h3. Actual Results    Jira redirects the user to the SSO login page.   The user is not informed that his credentials are wrong.   If Captcha is to be used, the Captcha screen is not presented to the user:   The below messages can be seen in the security log file:    From the UI, You can see the redirects:    !screenshot_472.png|thumbnail! !screenshot_473.png|thumbnail! !screenshot_474.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    If the user manages to remember the password in the first couple to tries they can click back on the browser and try to login again.   If Jira decides to use Captcha after multiple failed login attempts, Captcha screen is not rendered and there is no way for the user to login on his own.   The user will need to contact the Jira admin to reset the login count and the Captcha screen from Jira user management."""
comments: "It affects also Jira in older version 8.3. We reproduced this on test and production environment for Jira DC."

summary: "Unable to download the Mobile App for Android without an access to the Google Play app store"

Additionally, mobile phones of some brands (like Huawei) are blocked from using the Google Play app store.    h5.Suggested Solution  Add the Mobile App for Android to other app stores or provide an alternative download method."""

summary: "Provide usage statistics for Jira items"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, Jira does not provide any internal tool to display usage statistics from Jira items (e.g. How many times a dashboard, board or plan was accessed), so users do not have a consistent way to purge unused items on Jira.    h3. Suggested Solution    Add a section on Jira's UI to display this information, allowing users to easily find and remove unused items."""

summary: "Watcher picker is too short in width for some scenarios"

"""

summary: "As an Jira Administrator I want to test disk and DB performance"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When Jira has performance problems due to Environmental issues it's hard to collect all required data at the right timeframe.   Also, having the Application point of view is important, since it helps to isolate other factors (like permissions, QoS, DB Execution plans, )    h3. Suggested Solution  Create a testing tool embedded in the product to collect the performance stats related to Environment: Disk performance, DB latency and Query time, Network latency and throughput.      h3. Workaround   Use  [Test disk access speed for Jira server performance troubleshooting|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/test-disk-access-speed-for-jira-server-performance-troubleshooting-818577561.html]"""
comments: "And, as a Jira administrator I would like to schedule this to run as a service executing N times a day, so I can compare results over time and enhance monitoring."

summary: "Jira behaviour is inconsistent when the attachment name contains curly brackets"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira allows you to upload attachments that contain wiki markup characters such as curly brackets. However, you won't be able to embed these attachments in a comment, rather a broken thumbnail is displayed.    You can't also embed these attachments without a thumbnail and make them available to download, because this also doesn't work.    You can't edit comments which contain such formatting because it breaks the editor, for example, with the following wiki markup formatting:      Breaks the editor when trying to edit with the following error:     !Screen Shot 2020-07-02 at 13.09.21.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upload an attachment with a name such as {attachment}.jpg   # Try embedding that file as an attachment, such as a thumbnail for images or an attachment to be downloaded    h3. Expected Results    Jira behaves consistently and properly parses the file's name, escaping any wiki markup characters from it. Or, prevents the upload of those files initially with a warning about the problem.    h3. Actual Results    Jira allows you to upload the files but the editor doesn't it, so this causes frustration to the user that initially doesn't understand that the problem is due to the special characters, as there is no clear error message.    h3. Workaround    No workaround available, except from not uploading files with these characters to Jira."""

summary: "Provide a way to configure the frequency at which JQL autocomplete suggestions may submit search requests."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user writes JQL queries via Jira's Advanced Search, certain fields support autocompletion, which will suggest values as the user types.    As the user types, Jira will use a thread to perform a search against what they've typed and return possible results back to the user. In large or slow instances, and depending on the field being autocompleted, this functionality may not return quickly, and can take multiple seconds.    It is possible for the user to continue typing and end up making multiple autocompletion requests before the previous requests ever complete and return to the user. Jira will not cancel or otherwise terminate previously ongoing autocompletion threads in this case.  h3. Performance implication    The implementation of this suggestion can be used as a workaround for any potential performance bug associated with JQL suggestions REST calls.   One such example is JSWSERVER-20272 but there might be others.    Another workaround can include disabling *JQL Auto-complete* per *Administration >> System >> General Configuration*  h3. Suggested customization   * Provide a parameter to customize how much to wait for new keystrokes.   * Provide a parameter to customize the number of characters to be typed, after which the suggestions kick in. By default, we start suggesting as soon as we type."""

summary: "Database Collation health check in JIRA for MySQL should check connection string for 'connectionCollation' option at startup"
description: """Originally raised as https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATST-998    *Problem Definition*  When installing JIRA against MySQL 5.7, the MySQL server default collation must be set to utf8mb4_bin.  Otherwise, JIRA will not even start.  In Confluence, there is an option to set the connectionCollation option in the JDBC connection string so that the MySQL server default can remain something else (if already used by another application):  https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-fix-the-collation-and-character-set-of-a-mysql-database-744326173.html    Server character set considerations for Confluence  If you want to use utf8mb4, and character_set_server  is not set to utf8mb4  in the my.cnf  or my.ini  file on your MySQL Server and you can't change this (for example utf8 is required for a database used by another application) you will need to add the connectionCollation=utf8mb4_bin parameter to your connection URL in order to use utf8mb4. See the Connector/J 8.0 or Connector/J 5.1 documentation for more information.     Suggested Solution  Check the connection string for 'connectionCollation' option during JIRA startup.    Workaround   :cross_mark: ​No current workaround.  Set the MySQL server default collation"""
comments: "+1 for the same.    The workaround from ""Server character set considerations for Confluence"" is applicable to Fisheye/Crucible as well.    Would be great to have this in Jira."

summary: "Documentation Fix: Description for modifying jira.search.views.max.limit setting is incorrect"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In *Administration >> System >> General Configuration >> Advanced Settings*, the *jira.search.views.max.limit* setting contains the following description:  {quote}This number sets the absolute limit on the number of issues that can be exported from search results. Users trying to request more get a 403 (Forbidden) HTTP error code. To disable, set to '-1'. After changing it, update the value of 'jira.search.views.default.max' to a value that is lower than or equal to this one.{quote}    Per [JRASERVER-67839 : Unable to disable the maximum limit of number of exported issues from GUI|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-67839], this is no longer the case as we explicitly do not allow for disabling this setting by design.    However, the description is still outdated, as attempting to set this value to -1 will instead cause the following error which immediately contradicts the description:  {quote}Value must be at least 0{quote}    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to *Administration >> System >> General Configuration >> Advanced Settings*    h3. Expected Results    The description for *jira.search.views.max.limit* does not contain the following sentence:  {quote}To disable, set to '-1'.{quote}    h3. Actual Results    The description contains an incorrect instruction.    h3. Workaround    N/A"""
comments: "This bug report involves the same topic as [JRASERVER-67839 : Unable to disable the maximum limit of number of exported issues from GUI|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-67839], but is intended to address the incorrect description, rather than change the behavior of the setting."

summary: "Batch updates using DiagnosticPreparedStatement may increase memory pressure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    com.atlassian.jira.diagnostic.connection.DiagnosticPreparedStatement which is a PreparedStatement wrapper, maintains its own list of batch queries for reporting purposes. If the same preparedStatement object is used for multiple [executeBatch()|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/sql/Statement.html#executeBatch--] calls without calling [clearBatch()|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/sql/Statement.html#clearBatch--], this list accumulates batch queries. For very large updates which use multiple batch steps (like restoring an XML backup) this may cause memory pressure and in extreme cases \{{OutOfMemoryError}}s.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    restoring an XML backup with large AO tables and using a debugger;   # install Jira   # create a table in JIRA database to test purposes named """"AO_111111_TEST""""   # insert 6,000 rows to this test table (number must be greater than 5,000 as imported is using batches with the size of 5,000 i.e. 6,000 rows will use to batch inserts, first will insert 5,000 and second will insert remaining 1,000)   # create an XML backup (*gg* > *Backup System*)   # connect the server with a debugger and put breakpoints in method com.atlassian.dbexporter.importer.DataImporter$BatchInserter.flush()   # import the XML backup generated above (*gg* > *Restore System*)   # observe in the debugger that ps.batchQueries is not reset after the first batch is executed and size exceeds 5,000 on second batch step    h3. Expected Results    calling {{executeBatch}} shall clear the batch, also on {{DiagnosticPreparedStatement}} because underlying Statement does that automatically without requiring explicit calls to {{clearBatch()}}. Hence calling {{clearBatch()}} between batch steps does not seem to be a requirement.   Quoting [JDBC 3.0 Specification|http://java.cnam.fr/iagl/biblio/spec/jdbc-3_0-fr-spec.pdf] 15.1.2  {quote}Calling the method executeBatch closes the calling Statement object’s current   result set if one is open. The statement’s batch is reset to empty once   executeBatch returns.  {quote}  h3. Actual Results    {{executeBatch()}} does not clear the batch on {{DiagnosticPreparedStatement}}.    Even if SQL works just fine, *batchQueries* list accumulates all statements for logging purposes.    In extreme cases like an XML backup importing millions of rows per table, this may lead to an OOME. Below exception is a stack trace of an OOME resulting from above on a production system importing 9 million rows in a single table. Since we accumulate the statements, on each batch step, Jira prepares a longer string concatenating them for logging.    h3. Workaround    Contact Support"""

summary: "search-request-view plugins (implements com.atlassian.jira.plugin.searchrequestview.SearchRequestView) are not executed when search query contains some unicode characters."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    search-request-view plugins (implements {{com.atlassian.jira.plugin.searchrequestview.SearchRequestView}}) are not executed when search query contains some unicode characters.    h3. Environment  Issue appeared after Jira update to 8.5.0 -> 8.8.0, and reproducible with new *8.9.0*    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # create plugin with search-request-view module using SDK:  _atlas-create-jira-plugin-module > .. > """"17. Search Request View""""_   # package and install on Jira   # in Jira, perform search for issue, where query contains some non-ascii character. For example: *summary ~ """"Über""""*   # Execute plugin by calling option Export -> MyPlugin       h3. Expected Results  plugin writeSearchResults() method to be executed    h3. Actual Results  * error page with text: """"_HTTP Status 400 – Bad Request. Error in the JQL Query: The character 'U+009C' on line 1 at position 13 must be escaped. Use the escape '\u009c' instead. (line 1, character 13)_"""".    * None of plugin class methods is invoked, as error page seems to be rendered earlier.      h3. Note   native export functions work without issues, but they seems don't implement {{com.atlassian.jira.plugin.searchrequestview.SearchRequestView}} and called differently than user-plugins.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Allow Jira Ticket to Watch a Linked Confluence Page"
description: """To support requirements development authoring in Confluence and requirements tracking in Jira, please add the ability for a Jira ticket to watch a linked Confluence page.    Currently, a user may watch a Confluence page and receive notifications showing the delta between 2 versions of the page.  Request is for that same notification to be sent to the linked Jira record and added as a comment.  This provides users who are tracking a Jira requirements record the ability to quickly see what has changed in the linked Confluence page +from within the Jira ticket itself+.    This functionality provides a +complete requirements management solution for Atlassian+:   * Early authoring / designing of requirements in Confluence with review workflow   * Transition to Jira for requirements tracking / implementation   * Record of changes made to the linked Confluence page that can be seen in the Jira record's comments"""

summary: "Strange behavior when updating usernames in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When updating a user name in Jira such as naming a user in an LDAP as the same username as an internal user, this can cause some undesirable behavior if you choose to rename one of the users back to their previous/any other username. Similar to JRASERVER-36594.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up Jira to a connector LDAP directory.   # Create two users, test and testtester. One in the internal and the other in the LDAP directory.   # Rename the LDAP user to match the internal directory user. Jira will recognize this user as the same user since the name matches between directories.   # Rename the LDAP user back to their previous username(testtester) or any other username.    h3. Expected Results    This will update the current record inside of Jira's {{app_user}} table so it recognizes the user as its previous user.  h3. Actual Results    When you change the user back to their old username in LDAP so they don't match the internal users username, Jira creates an {{app_user}} record for a brand new user and then renames the historical record of the user name with a #1 behind it but nothing gets added to {{cwd_user}} so the relationship ends up being broken.    *Before Test*    *app_user* table:    *After Test*    *app_user* table:      h3. Notes    This causes some undesirable behavior where the old user record cannot be interacted with in the GUI if they were an assignee of an issue before the username change. Example:    !Screen Shot 2020-06-24 at 9.36.14 AM.png!    Here, we can see the broken Assignee record in the JQL results. This user can be changed as the assignee but this shows the broken relationship between what's in the GUI compared to what's in the database since the link for the user between {{app_user}} and {{cwd_user}} gets broken.    h3. Workaround    If you plan to rename an LDAP user to an existing user_name of the internal directory, the following steps can avoid this behavior:  # Disable the LDAP directory;  # Move the internal directory to the top of the list;  # Rename the internal users to something different;  # Rename the LDAP users to the target username;  # Enable the directory and move it back to the top of the list."""

summary: "Jira Server mobile app cannot connect to Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Jira Server mobile app cannot connect to Jira when the bug JRASERVER-71175 is mitigated.    JRASERVER-71175 is mitigated by either being on a version of Jira where the bug is fixed ('Fix Version/s"""") or by implementing the workaround dark feature, {{jira.redirect.anonymous.404.errors.enabled}}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira 8.9.1   # Configure the correct base URL   # Attempt to connect the Jira mobile app to it.    h3. Expected Results    The app connects uneventfully.    Testing the {{server-info}} endpoint via {{curl}} reveals:    h3. Actual Results    A message indicating """"Can't connect to your site"""" is shown on Android and """"Can't check compatibility"""" on iOS   !image-2020-06-24-18-11-30-207.png|thumbnail!    !image-2020-06-24-18-11-48-487.png|thumbnail!    Testing the {{server-info}} endpoint via {{curl}} reveals:  +Example 1: Jira 8.5.4, with dark feature {{jira.redirect.anonymous.404.errors.enabled}}+    +Example 2: Jira 8.9.1+    h3. Workaround    The issue is related to a security fix and as a temporary solution this can be disabled by   # adding the [dark feature flag|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html] {{jira.redirect.anonymous.404.errors.disabled}}, and,   # removing, if present, the [dark feature flag|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html] {{jira.redirect.anonymous.404.errors.enabled}}"""
comments: "Seems like there's a missing part in the automated testing for Jira releases around the mobile app." [SEP] "Looks like the issue is gone with the latest iOS app update.  Successfully tested with Jira App Version 1.11.0.1 on iOS and Server 8.10.0, connection is now possible." [SEP] "h2. Clients    iOS 1.11.0 has a fix    Android 0.12.1 has a fix    It helps everyone except of following cases:  h2. Basic Auth Proxy in Front of Server    Customers with basic auth proxy in front of server may still experience the issue, and using jira.redirect.anonymous.404.errors.disabled is still the only option.     "

summary: "Optimizing custom fields, it temper the default configuration scheme for custom field with global context "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    To be optimized, a custom field must have a global context and be used in *several projects only*. A good practice is for the field to be used in fewer than 10 projects. Otherwise, it's considered an actual global custom field, and you shouldn't change its configuration. So the custom field which is associated with all the issue type and all global projects having a data consistency issue but this functionality is present in Jira by design.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set a  custom field with a global context    # Go to Custom fields optimizer and Scan for the custom field to be optimized    # Once the scan is complete, look for the custom field to be optimized with a global context    # Once you select and change the context, it throws a pop, this change won't affect the values stored in your custom filled.   # Whereas it removed the Default configuration configured but If you prefer the pop message before proceeding the context, it is not throwing any warning to the user for any changes. Please refer the Kb article    # [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/optimizing-custom-fields-956713279.html]    h3. Workaround    To be optimized, a custom field must have a global context and be used in *several projects only*.    We need to make the user notified for the exact change for the global context custom field, as its result for data loss or discrepancy     We have made the screen recording for the steps replication, which is also attached in a feature request.     It is not throwing any warning to the user for any changes. Please refer the Kb article [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/optimizing-custom-fields-956713279.html]"""

summary: "As a Jira Admin, we do need that all pages provide the X-AUSERNAME header"
description: """*Problem*   As a Jira Admin, we do need that all pages provide the X-AUSERNAME header so we can always identify the user that submitted the request.    Multiple Jira pages do no provide the X-AUSERNAME header and this behavior can be observed even on older Jira versions.   We do not use the GDPR strict mode enabled.    Some pages where the x-ausername does not show:   * JSW Issues filter - [http://localhost:8080/projects/SCRUM/issues/SCRUM-1?filter=allopenissues]   * JSD Queues - [http://localhost:8080/projects/ITSD/queues/custom/65]   * JSD Issue View - [http://localhost:8080/projects/ITSD/queues/custom/65/ITSD-60]    But on most other pages (Dashboards, Addons, Admin, Issue View etc) - the x-ausername hearer shows as expected like:   * [http://localhost:8080/secure/BrowseProjects.jspa?selectedCategory=all&selectedProjectType=all]   * [http://localhost:8080/browse/SCRUM-1]   * https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70172         *Business Justification*   To effectively monitor/manage our environment, we have the logs from all our application nodes. One of the key pieces of information is the user who made the request as this helps us looks for abuse patterns, helps encourage more efficient use of the platform etc.    Affected Version: 7.0.2, 7.13.8, 8.5.4, 8.5.5"""

summary: "Webhook calls fail if destination URL contains special characters"
description: """h3. Summary    There are cases where base64 code is needed to be sent as a URL argument on the JIRA webhook so that JIRA can authentic to the recipient webhook server. Special characters on these URL arguments are not sent out properly from JIRA.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    - Create a webhook in JIRA  - Specify a URL argument containing special characters. (i.e. {{code=asdasohdoasdoasd==}})  - Trigger the event    h3. Expected Results    Special characters should be sent as part of the URL arguments.    h3. Actual Results    |Format|Webhook|URL Received|  |Plain Text|https://www.webhhook_server.com/endpoint?code=asdasohdoasdoasd==&test=test|POST /endpoint?code=asdasohdoasdoasd&test=test&user_id=admin&user_key=admin|  |URL Encoded|https://www.webhhook_server.com/endpoint?code=asdasohdoasdoasd%3D%3D|POST /endpoint?code=asdasohdoasdoasd%253D%253D&user_id=admin&user_key=admin|    h3. Notes    (!) This can happen more frequently with endpoints generated on Azure. Usually, the generated endpoint will end with double equal signs, which are removed when the webhook is fired by JIRA.     h3. Workaround    * This workaround already worked for a few customers affected by the BUG, it consists in trying to generate another *authorization key* in the Azure side making sure that it doesn't contain any special characters.     """

summary: "Scrum board showing move to done button when filtering by fix version"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Story with status """"done"""" and with all subtasks also """"done"""" is showing the """"move to done"""" button when the fix version is part of the filter used.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project area SCRUM  # Create a story and a subtask.  # Define 2 releases: release1 already released, release2 unreleased  # Create a new board """"unreleased"""" for project area SCRUM using the filter:    # Set both story and subtask to release2  # Open the default board and observe the results: Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.  # Open the board """"unreleased"""" and observe the results: Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.  # Now, change the story to release1   # Refresh the default board and observe the results: Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.  # Refresh the board """"unreleased"""" and observe the results    h3. Expected Results  Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.    h3. Actual Results  Story and subtask listed but the button """"move to done"""" is presented.    h3. Workaround  * Remove the fixversion from the filter  or  * Set fixversion to a released version, thus both story and subtask will not more be presented at the board  """

summary: "While uploading an attachment during issue creation as anonymous user throws an error saying ""You are not authorized, the session has expired or connection is lost."""

Issue Summary    While uploading an attachment during issue creation as an anonymous user throws an error saying """"You are not authorized, the session has expired or connection is lost"""" though the """"Browse Project"""", """"create issues"""" and """"create attachments"""" permissions to """"Anyone on the web"""" group was given to """"Anyone on the web"""". Expected Results    The issue should be created without any errors  h3. Actual Results    The issue creation fails with an error saying """"{color:#FF0000}You are not authorized, the session has expired or connection is lost""""{color}  h3. Workaround    As a workaround, we can add attachments using REST API as through GUI it's not working.    {quote}As the anonymous users will not have any user ID & password we tried to remove -u\{username}:\{password} and ran the command like below and it works(y)  {quote}  *Example*:    (i) For more information, please go through our documentation for [How to add an attachment to a JIRA issue using REST API|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-add-an-attachment-to-a-jira-issue-using-rest-api-699957734.html] & [Server - add attachment|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.0/#api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey}/attachments-addAttachment]."""

summary: "Validate automation rules before saving"
description: """As a Jira user writing automation rules    I want a rule validated prior to save    So I can create automation more effectively         Acceptance criteria:   * At save, the smart value syntax is validated. Errors are shown next to the rule components.   * At save, the rule components are validated for missing parts. Errors are shown next to the rule components.   * Bonus: where smart values can be entered, auto-completion is provided to reduce entry errors.   * Bonus: where null values can short-circuit smart values, warnings are shown     """

summary: "BulkEdit Progression screen showing more than the limit of 1000 issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira is respecting the limit of 1000 when using BulkEdit. Running a JQL  search on the edited issue returns 1000.   !BulkEdit2.png|thumbnail!    But during the BullEdit the progression screen is wrongly  showing more than 1000 issues in this case 1438 see attached screenshot   (i) The BulkEdit functionality is not affected but it is confusing for the users    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use BulkEdit and select 1000 issues   # Follow the BulkEdit steps   !BulkEdit1.pmg.png|thumbnail!    # The progression screen show more than 1000 issues being edited     h3. Expected Results  The BulkEdit progression screen displays  the exact number of the edited issues    h3. Actual Results  The BulkEdit progression screen display a number higher than the limit   !Bulk-Edit-progression-screen.JPG|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Support for Azure Database for MySQL"
description: """Request for Support for Azure MySQL: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/services/mysql/  We have customers who are utilising these databases type in Azure and it would be great if we extend out support for this."""

summary: "Dashboard not editable if the user does not belongs to all the groups in the Viewers field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a user is granted the Edit permission of a dashboard but is not in all of the groups added to the View permission, the user is not able to edit the dashboard.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 2 groups (GroupA and GroupB)  # Create 2 users (User1 and User2)  # Add User1 into both groups  # Add User2 into GroupB only  # Grant GroupA and GroupB as _Viewers_ in a dashboard  # Grant single user User1 and User2 as _Editors_ in the same dashboard  # Login as User1 and edit the dashboard   # In another browser, login as User2 and edit the dashboard    h3. Expected Results  Both User1 and User2 are able to edit the dashboard    h3. Actual Results  Only User1 can edit the dashboard. The Edit, Share and Delete dashboard functionality is hidden from User2    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "This affects my team. We were just acquired and some of the LDAP groups that we were using in security changed or were removed. Now the owners can't edit their dashboards and as a Jira Admin, I can't help. We had to have our Sys Admin go into the database to remove the group. The owner should always be able to edit."

summary: "Force customer notification when converting internal comment to Public"
description: """Currently Jira Service Desk and JIRA Software will not send a notification (or update) to customer when a comment on a case is converted from Internal to Public (even when """"Share with customer"""" button is clicked)    This feature request is to add an option to forcefully share a notification with customer when comment is changed from Internal to public.    *Suggestions:*   # Add """"Force customer notification"""" checkbox next to """"Share with customer"""" button on a comment or even better;  # Add additional dialog box when comment is converted from internal to public with Yes or No and checkbox """"Force notification"""";  # A global setting """"Send notification to customers when internal comment is converted to public)""""    *Workaround*  Copy your draft/internal comment into a new comment and share it with the Customer.  """

summary: "'No ObjectCodec defined for the generator' error on various functions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrading Jira from 7.x to 8.x, the error {{No ObjectCodec defined for the generator}} is thrown on various functions.    The error appears to be because Jackson is being passed a list or set object to log, and it cannot serialize that object type.  h4. More tech details    In the examples we have encountered, {{atlassian-logging}} is passing a LinkedHashSet through to Jackson, but the version that Jira uses cannot serialize that type of object and throws an error: [https://github.com/atlassian/jackson-1/blob/master/src/java/org/codehaus/jackson/impl/JsonGeneratorBase.java#L495]    In Jira 8, the version of {{atlassian-logging}} in use was downgraded, to remove the dependency on Cloud libraries.    Seems like this might be the fix for serializing lists: [https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-logging/pull-requests/54/jces-227-add-list-support-for/diff]    That fix was released in {{atlassian-logging 3.1.0}}. In Jira 8 we downgraded atlassian-logging from 3.1.4 to 2.21. Jira would previously have been able to handle list/set serializisation, but not after the downgrade.    h3. Environment  * Jira configured with Log4J Json logging format (com.atlassian.logging.log4j.layout.JsonLayout.format).     h3. Steps to Reproduce    This issue is instance specific. It occurs because people have either scripts or plugins that rely on functionality that was previously available in Jira 7, but is now gone in Jira 8, due tot the downgrade of {{atlassian-logging}}. We therefore cannot provide steps to reproduce.    I do however have an internal instance where this issue can be reproduced so please reach out to me for more info.  h3. Expected Results    Functionality that worked in previous versions continues to work after upgrade.  h3. Actual Results    Various actions can fail. This is an example stacktrace that is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround    If the stacktrace contains a reference to {{com.onresolve.scriptrunner}}, it's likely that you have a script being run by Scriptrunner that is expecting to be able to log a complex object type like a list/set. You should update or remove the script.    Otherwise, as the failure is logging related, you can tune the logging level for the class that caused the error so that the failing logline is no longer called.    Find and tune:   # Get the stack trace and search for the first instance of log4j - whatever class is before that (the line underneath) is the one that called the logging.   # Go to Admin > Logging and Profiling and set the class to a level higher than was called, ie if it called ERROR, set FATAL. You will likely need to do this from 'Configure logging level for another package.'    NB: This workaround may not work in all cases"""
comments: "This impact us greatly as we opened a bug on scriptrunner side for it.    As this is regression, please fix in the enterprise release.    Martin Poirier  Ubisoft"

summary: "Show username instead of user key in the audit logs when a user is added to a project role"
description: """h3. Description    When a user is added to a project role, when checking the Audit logs, this user is referred to by its """"user key"""" instead of its username:   !AuditLog_ProjectRole.png|thumbnail!     With the recent [GDPR changes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/gdpr-changes-in-jira-975041009.html#GDPRchangesinJira-userkeys] introduced in Jira 8.4, the user keys or newly created users have the format JIRAUSERXXXXX, which makes it impossible to know which users were added to a specific role from the Audit Logs. Instead, the Jira admin user needs to have access to the DB and query the {{app_user}} table to find the relationship between the user key and the user name.    h3. Step to reproduce    # Install Jira 8.4 or any higher version  # Create a new user  # Add this user to any project role  # Go to the page ⚙ > System > Audit Logs  # Look for the entry corresponding to the addition of the user to a project role, and verify that the user key is displayed instead of the username    h3. Suggestion    Instead of using user_key, the Audit Logs should show the username as it's more relevant to the users. Note that it's already the case when a user is added to a group (as shown in the screenshot below), so the Audit Logs should be consistent and show the username whenever some modifications is made to a user.   !AuditLog_UserManagement.png|thumbnail! """
comments: "good day!    the same case is reproduced in Server v8.8.1. also new users displayed the same way in issue history."

summary: "Eliminate the 1000 row return limit in the Jira Issues Macro"
description: """I've learned the Jira Issue macros will only return a max of 1000 rows regardless of emptying the Maximum Issues field (where it says it will return all rows if empty).  We develop Confluence dashboards that aggregate data returned in Jira Issue macros using the Pivot Table macro.  Not allowing all rows to return from queries that return more than 1000 rows results leaves Pivot Table results inaccurate.  I would like to ask for this limitation to be removed or configurable."""
comments: "+1    A 1000 issue limit is bad enough - but the UI should not imply that you can raise the limit, or ""leave empty to get all issues.""  Is there some configuration parameter that can increase this limit as there is for search results?"

summary: "Add ""View Worklog"" Permission"
description: """Hi there,    I know there is an issue that's been rejected before, but this is an important issue, especially in Europe (regarding GDPR and data sensitive companies / enterprises).    We have several customers who want to work with Tempo Timesheets, but they can't. Tempo has nice permissions to only allow Project Managers to see all team members' worklogs, but everyone else can still see the worklogs in Jira itself. Tempo claims this is a Jira Bug, because they don't change anything in the Jira configuration.    It would be great to have a separate permission in the permission schemes to allow only specific users to see the worklogs / other users worklogs.    In this case team members would still work on the same project, but don't see the other users worklogs. We have one customer in particular who is working in a SAFe environment and this missing feature is a showstopper (3k Data Center setup) at the moment!    Other teams have to be able to browse other teams issues, but they should not be able to see all the worklogs. Please add some permission to change this, because this is some kind of basic functionality if you want to scale and will be a showstopper for multiple customers.    Thanks a lot  Marten"""

summary: "Revert functionality to match user session to Reporter field when providing feedback through Issue Collector"
description: """h3. Problem definition    In attempt to mitigate the effect of implementing SameSite cookie control in Chrome browser v80, changes were done to Issue Collector in scope of this bug ticket: JRASERVER-70494.    One resulting effect of fixing the same was dropped XSRF check, which has a consequence that the session of a logged in user is not matched to the Reporter field after reporting issue via Issue Collector.    Instead, the user gets presented with a reporter email address field that will be used to match the Reporter of the issue. This makes the Issue Collector feedback a bit less efficient to collect the feedback for already logged-in users.  h3. Suggested resolution    Bring back the user-session matching functionality to the Issue Collector in Jira."""
comments: "This issue is currently set as Suggestion.  Shouldn't a product change that breaks existing functionality and is linked as ""is caused by"" be considered a Bug?" [SEP] "This is definitely a bug since previous functionality is now broken." [SEP] "I am seeing the same issue since this update and have had to use javascript on every page with an issue collector in a sort of hack-y workaround, would love to see this get picked up for a fix in the next release." [SEP] "Cory, Could you shrew your hack? That would be a {color:#de350b}*HUGE*{color} help!" Hopefully

summary: "Add a burndown that separates scope creep from initial scope"
description: """As a Scrum Master putting together data for the Sprint Review, I want to be able to show a burndown that shows the burndown of initial scope along with the burndown of scope creep, so that when my team finishes everything they committed to at the start of the Sprint and they Bring more work in, it communicates that we've not only met our Sprint Commitments but met them.    The chart then would show the idealized line and then the burndown of the work that was present at the start of the Sprint. When Items are brought into the Sprint, instead of a single burndown line going up, it branches and moves upward (this line would track the increase/completion of items outside of the scope of the Sprint commitment). If all outside of scope items are finished, the line merges with the burndown and goes away."""

summary: "Jira server log throws Security framework of XStream not initialized, XStream is probably vulnerable."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Jira server log throws """"Security framework of XStream not initialized, XStream is probably vulnerable"""" message.    h3. Suggested Solution    Set the permissions for the XStream library when it is initialized and do not rely on the defaults. An explicit call to addPermission() would no longer log the warning.    h3. Workaround    There is no workaround."""

summary: "Jira 8+ does not run plugin upgrade tasks after the first launch"
description: """h3. Summary  Jira 8+ does not run plugin upgrade tasks upon the first launch. After the upgrade, it requests immediate re-indexing, re-indexes the issues, but didn't schedules upgrades to run (in {{com.atlassian.jira.upgrade.UpgradeScheduler}}) and didn't execute the plugin upgrade tasks.   The job runs and reports to be finished but plugin upgrade tasks do not run. This will cause problems for plugins that have upgrade tasks (e.g Dev.Status).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup Jira 7.x  # Upgrade to Jira 8.x     h3. Expected Results  The job runs and executes the upgrade tasks.  * example for DVCS:      * and related logging for {{com.atlassian.jira.upgrade.UpgradeScheduler}}      h3. Actual Results  The job runs and reports to be finished, but plugin upgrade tasks do not run    * {{com.atlassian.jira.upgrade.UpgradeScheduler}} is not scheduled.      h3. Notes  * jira.upgrade.delay.minutes default to 1    h3.Workaround  * Restart Jira. Jira will run plugin upgrade tasks after restart.   * Trigger the delayed upgrade tasks through REST API: POST /rest/api/2/upgrade  ** see https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.5.5/#api/2/upgrade-runUpgradesNow"""

summary: "Concurrent invocation of updateFieldConfigScheme on FieldConfigSchemeManager may result in IssueTypeScheme and PriorityScheme to revert to default"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Concurrent invocation of {{updateFieldConfigScheme}} on {{FieldConfigSchemeManager}} may cause a race condition which may result in IssueTypeScheme and PriorityScheme to revert to the default schemes.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # create a project {{TestProject}} and assign a new IssueType and Priority scheme to this project   # create a large number of projects (>500) using shared configuration from {{TestProject}} so that we have 500+ projects sharing the same configuration. Take care to create projects in a non-concurrent fashion (i.e. one-by-one) to not trigger the race condition.   # create multiple new projects with the same shared configuration concurrently (in parallel).   # notice that IssueTypeScheme and PriorityScheme for some projects may revert.    Since this is a race condition it may be possible to not trigger the behaviour described all the time so this may need a couple of trials. For recreation purposes, we can use a load testing tool like JMeter for steps (3) and (4) above. For (3) we set threadpool size to 1 to and for step (4) we set it to 5 with no ramp-up time so that we try to create 5 projects concurrently.  h3. Expected Results    All new projects created and old projects have their schemes assigned to the schemes we've created at step (1)    h3. Actual Results    For some projects, priority and/or issuetype schemes revert to default schemes.    h3. Workaround  Performing issueTypeScheme and priorityScheme changes, or operations that may trigger such changes automatically, like creating a project using shared configuration in a non-concurrent fashion (i.e. not in parallel but one action at a time)    *Note*: this bug report looks similar to [JRASERVER-66625|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66625], however, the execution path mentioned in this ticket (see steps to reproduce on this ticket) is already fixed and does not cause this issue.     h5. Recovering the Issue Type Scheme associations  See JSWSERVER-20581 or JRASERVER-66625 for details how to recover Scheme associations"""

summary: "JQL does not filter issues properly with Due Date with time zone of Baku (+4)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JQL does not filter issues properly with Due Date with the time zone of Baku (+4)  h3. Environment    Jira 8.5.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Please create a project and add a due date field on the issue screen.   # Set the due date in a few of the issues.   # Please verify and set the default user time zone to system field   # Now especially set a due date of 31st May in of the few issues.   # Please run the following JQL query """"project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03""""   # The JQL will filter the desired results    # Now  go-> system -> advanced setting and change the time Region = Asia, Time Zone = (GMT+04:00)Baku   # The same JQL project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03  seems to be skipping May 31 results     h3. Expected Results   * Please run the following JQL query project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03   * The JQL will filter the desired results     h3. Actual Results   * The same JQL project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03  seems to be skipping May 31 results     Notes   JIRA Build: 8.5.1   JIRA Installation Type: Standalone   Java Version : 1.8.0_202 - OpenJdk  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Hi, the problem is very important. This is really stressful for our users (especially the management), because they do not see all the data in the system, so the reports also show incorrect data." [SEP] "It is not enough to have Jira-only settings to reproduce the error.    There must be some configuration:   *Server*   Region, City- Asia, Azerbaijan    Time Zone Baku (+4)    *Jira*    Region Asia   Time Zone Baku (+4)         *Note:*    If you change the configuration for Jira only the error will be reproduced as in the JRASERVER-71034 ticket.                " [SEP] "Hi, do you have a plan when the bug will be fixed?"

summary: "Add MariaDB as a supported platform for Jira"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-32347].  {panel}  JIRA does not officially document the support of Maria DB, or indicate any support for this. As some major Linux distributions are moving to use MariaDB and the functionality is similar, it would be good to provide support for this Database version.  h3. Suggested Solution    Officially support MariaDB for JIRA, including adding documentation and any other articles as required.  h3. Workaround    MySQL can be installed on RHEL, Oracle Linux, CentOS and Fedora using the MySQL Yum repository, [https://dev.mysql.com/downloads/repo/yum/]. The [Quick Guide to Using the MySQL Yum Repository|https://dev.mysql.com/doc/mysql-yum-repo-quick-guide/en/] walks through how to configure the repository and install MySQL."""

summary: "Incorrect information in our documentation article: Supported Platforms"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Supported Platforms article indicates that the only supported version for several of our latest releases incorrectly states that:    * Application server  **     We support Apache Tomcat 8.5.35.   **    We don't support deploying multiple Atlassian applications in a single Tomcat container.     Our latest releases bundle different Apache Tomcat versions such as 8.5.40, 8.5.42, and 8.5.50.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Step 1: Access the following URL:  [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html]   # Step 2: Scroll down until you reach the *Application Server* section.    h3. summary: "DeferredReplicationCachedReference 'get' operation triggers unnecessary replication events"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The {{get}} operation on a {{DeferredReplicationCachedReference}} is deferred, hence causes a new {{DeferredReplicationStrategy}} instance to be created, registered to a {{ThreadLocal}} variable and run. The execution is almost exclusively no-op, for the majority of 'get' invocations won't cause a change in the cache to be replicated, apart from the initialization of the reference on first use.    This behaviour generates unnecessary computational overhead. Caches shall be optimized for reads as much as possible; for regular 'get's (which don't generate side-effects to be replicated) we shall have a non-deferred implementation. As an example, read-only cache operations like gets are not deferred at {{DeferredReplicationCache}} instances.    In addition, in certain JVM states like defined at JRASERVER-71058, registering {{cacheReplicationStrategy}} ThreadLocal may consume very high CPU.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Reproducible always  h3. Expected Results    Read-only operations like '{{get'}} shall not trigger a replication event on {{DeferredReplicationCachedReferences}}  h3. Actual Results    {{get}} operation is deferred, hence goes through deferredReplicaction logic  h3. Notes    Example of the merged thread-dump containing {{com.atlassian.jira.cache.DeferredReplicationCachedReference.get}}    Note: 18% of snippet is actually doing {{runDeferred}}, other code is busy with *ThreadLocal* operations.      h3. Workaround  None"""

Users would like a REST API which allows them to remove all groups for a specific user.      h3. Suggested Solution    Add a new REST which allows admins to remove all groups for a specific user.      h3. Workaround     None."""

summary: "Update Outgoing Mail Server help text to suggest port 587"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The help text on the """"Add SMTP Mail Server"""" page is confusing for some users. The help text suggests port 465 for SMTPS, while the majority of mail providers at this time are using 587 with STARTTLS for outgoing mail. Port 465 should not be used in most contexts - reference: [https://www.mailgun.com/blog/which-smtp-port-understanding-ports-25-465-587/]  h3. Quick fix    Change the *SMTP Port* help text _from_    to    h3. Longer fix    Change the layout of the page to suggest STARTTLS as the secure option to use instead of SMTPS. Potentially add this as an option in the Protocol dropdown (which would remove the TLS checkbox)"""

summary: "REST API: Return parent reference in API Issue results"
description: """Issue results contain a good deal of contextual references: linked issues, sub-tasks, project, etc. However they do not contain a reference to the parent issue.    [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v2/#api-rest-api-2-issue-issueIdOrKey-get]    This omission makes it difficult to construct tree-like views on issue queries. The backing data structure clearly maintains this information, because issue queries can filter by parent ID. However, it is not possible to disambiguate the parents of multiple issues returned by the same JQL query.    The message boards are littered with requests for how to obtain the parent of an issue, with the answer invariably being run a secondary query. The limitation can be seen in Jira's own UI as the issue view slowly builds the branch structure, one parent query at a time. All of these use cases would be made much simpler and reduce API spam by adding a reference to the parent issue."""

summary: "Error when Installing Jira service using service.bat "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When installing Jira service using *Service.bat* shipped along with Jira 8.9 .zip archive for windows, there is an error witnessed stating below:    The error is observed when executing service.bat after renaming *tomcat8.exe.x64* to *tomcat8.exe*, so service.bat was looking for the .x64 file, which didn't exist.   When running service.bat without renaming, the command runs fine, and windows then throws the error on trying to open an .x64 file (and thus, the service isn't actually installed).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # *Required* 64-bit (Processor/Architecture) environment to reproduce the behavior   # Download .zip archive for v8.9   ** [https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/downloads/binary/atlassian-jira-software-8.9.0.zip]   # Extract the archive   # Navigate to /bin directory and execute the service.bat install jira   # An error is witnessed:  {panel:title=Error Witnessed after executing Service.bat|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#000000|titleBGColor=#adcdff|bgColor=#d1e3ff}  """"C:\atlassian\jira-software-8.9.0\bin\Tomcat8.exe.x64""""' is not recognized as an internal or external command,   operable program or batch file.   Failed installing 'JIRASW8_9_0' service  {panel}     # Error is witnessed as .x64 is not recognized executable, there are restrictions in some environment to allow only certain executables like .exe to run    h3. Expected Results   * There should be some message to inform users that there are restriction due to which error is witnessed, so that next steps could be taken accordingly and service can be installed    h3. Actual Results   * There is an error observed and services could not be installed    h3. Workaround    Remove below lines from service.bat:    Rename Tomcat8.exe.x64 to Tomcat8 and execute service.bat"""

summary: "Executing indexers for custom fields being applicable only"
description: """h3. Problem    Whenever an issue is being indexed, Jira retrieves all registered custom field indexers and calls their addIndex() method regardless of whether the custom field is applicable *(visible and in the context)* for the issue or not. This generates additional overhead and affects indexing time.    *Note:* The custom field is considered as visible when it is not hidden in Field Configuration menu. Screens are not used to calculate fields visibility - the field may be not assigned to any screen, but still be considered as visible.  h3. Solution    To mitigate it we switched *all custom* *fields* to be indexed only when they are applicable for the particular issue.      (i) This feature is only available in Jira Data Center 8.10 and later.  See also: [Optimizing custom fields|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/optimizing-custom-fields-1005343684.html]"""

summary: "Executing indexers for custom fields having value only"
description: """h3. Problem    Whenever an issue is being indexed, Jira retrieves all registered custom field indexers and calls their addIndex() method regardless of whether the custom field has any value or not. This generates additional overhead and affects indexing time.  h3. Solution    To mitigate it we switched *Jira built-in custom fields* to be indexed only when they have values assigned for a particular issue. This is done by checking the values in the {{customfieldvalue}} table.      (i) This feature is only available in Jira Data Center 8.10 and later.  See also: [Optimizing custom fields|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/optimizing-custom-fields-1005343684.html]"""

summary: "Removing redundant data from Lucene index"
description: """h3. Problem    JQL supports sorting by the custom field values with the 'ORDER BY' clause:     {{project = MyProject ORDER BY MyCustomField}}    For this to work, Jira built-in custom fields store sorted_cf_name in the Lucene document. Whenever there is a custom field value assigned to an issue, it is stored in the index. However, when no value exists for a custom field, the sorting marker is stored instead (Double.MAX_VALUE, Long.MAX_VALUE or \ufffd depending on the custom field's type).  h3. Solution    As the comparators used by Jira built-in custom fields are capable of sort null values correctly, so we decided not to store markers for null values in the Lucene index any more and rely on comparators to sort null values.    (i) This feature is only available in Jira Data Center 8.10 and later.  See also: [Optimizing custom fields|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/optimizing-custom-fields-1005343684.html]"""
comments: "Does this change apply to Jira Data Center only, or Jira Server as well? In Jira Software 8.10.x release notes, it is listed in the [Optimized Custom Fields|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-10-x-release-notes-1004948108.html#JiraSoftware8.10.xreleasenotes-customOptimizedcustomfields] section that is tagged with ""DATA CENTER"" only." [SEP] "[~kalle.niemitalo]  Thanks for checking.  This is DC only change. I will update the ticket to reflect that. "

summary: "Add possibility of adjusting stale nodes retention period"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    In Jira 8.10, automatic stale nodes clean-up solution was presented - JRASERVER-42916.   In some scenarios two days time period to move node from _Active, no heartbeat_ state to the _Offline_ state is too long. This problem becomes more significant in the case of managing the cluster deployed with Ansible, AWS/K8s auto-scaling etc.   h3. Solution     Add system property to change the default retention period for stale nodes - """"_jira.not.alive.active.nodes.retention.period.in.hours_""""  h3. Example    *Current flow:*   *{color:#00875a}ACTIVE{color}* → <_5 minutes> →_ _{color:#ffab00}*NO HEARTBEAT*{color} →_ <+_*2 days*>_+ → *{color:#c1c7d0}OFFLINE{color}* → <_2 days>_ → *{color:#de350b}GONE{color}*    *Flow with new system property:*    When you will add to your JVM flags on startup this value:   _-Djira.not.alive.active.nodes.retention.period.in.hours=3_   Then the flow should looks like:   {color:#00875a}*ACTIVE*{color} → <_5 minutes> →_ _{color:#ffab00}*NO HEARTBEAT*{color} →_ <+*3*+ _+*hours*+>_ → {color:#c1c7d0}*OFFLINE*{color} → <_2 days>_ → {color:#de350b}*GONE*{color}    {color:#172b4d}{color:#de350b}{color}{color}{color:#de350b}{color:#172b4d}Node will be moved to _Offline_ state after 3 hours instead of 2 days. {color}{color}"""

summary: "Users synced from an LDAP directory can't be added to a Jira local group from the Jira UI under some conditions"
description: """h3. Summary    Users synced from an LDAP directory can't be added to a Jira local group from the Jira UI if the 2 following conditions are met:  - this group exists in both the Jira internal directory and in the LDAP server  - the LDAP directory is configured in Jira as *Read Only, with Local Groups*    h3. Steps to Reproduce    - Configure a new LDAP directory in Jira via *⚙ > User Management > User directories*  - On the LDAP server side, create:  -- a new group that already exists in Jira. For example """"jira-administrators""""  -- a new user. For example """"sa"""". Make sure that this user is not part of the group """"jira-administrators"""" on the LDAP server side  - Sync the LDAP directory with Jira via *⚙ > User Management > User directories*  - Go to *⚙ > User Management > Users*, and verify that the user was synced in Jira:   !Screenshot1.png|thumbnail!   - Go to the page *⚙ > User Management > Groups*  - Click on the *Edit members* link next to the group """"jira-administrators""""  - Try to add the LDAP user """"sa"""" to the group """"jira-administrators""""    h3. Expected Results    This user should be added to the group, since:  - the LDAP directory is configured in Jira as *Read Only, with Local Groups*  - the group """"jira-administrators"""" exists in Jira's internal directory    h3. Actual Results    The following error is thrown in the logs:       !Screenshot2.png|thumbnail!     When running the following SQL query, we can see that the group 'jira-administrators' exist twice in the cwd_group table, once in the internal directory (id = 1), and one in the LDAP directory (id = 10100).       (!) Note that the {{local}} column is set to 1 for the group coming from the LDAP directory (id=10100). Normally, local = 1 should mean that this group membership should be managed locally in Jira (since the LDAP group is configured as *Read Only, with Local Groups*). However, this setting seems to be ignored and instead Jira tries to add this user to the group 'jira-administrators' directly on the LDAP server. This operation fails, since Jira does not have the write permission in the LDAP directory.    h3. Other symptom    When trying to add the user to the group 'jira-administrators' from the page *⚙ > User Management > Users* instead, nothing happens:  - no error is thrown in the UI  - the group is not added to the user    h3. Workaround    h4. Workaround 1    The workaround consists in temporarily configuring the LDAP directory as *Read/Write* in Jira, and then add the user to the group 'jira-administators'.  Note that:  (!) With such configuration, when the user is added to the group 'jira-administators' from the Jira UI, this user *will also be added to this group on the LDAP server side*  (!) If you set the LDAP directory back to *Read Only, with Local Groups*, and then try to remove the user from the group 'jira-administators' from the page *⚙ > User Management > Groups*, you'll get the error below. Basically, you will need to set this LDAP directory back to Read/Write, whenever you want to update the members of the group 'jira-administators'.      h4. Workaround 2    - Remove the group 'jira-administators' from the LDAP server. This way, the members of this group will be entirely managed locally on the Jira side.  - Sync the LDAP directory in Jira via *⚙ > User Management > User directories*  - Add the user to the group 'jira-administrators' from the Jira UI"""

summary: "Batched Notification emails have an empty content when triggered by a custom event using the Issue created template"
description: """h3. Summary    Batched Notification emails have an empty content when triggered by a custom event using the *Issue created* template.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Go to *⚙ > System > Batching email notifications* and enable batched notifications   - Go to *⚙ > System > Events*   -- Create a custom event using the *Issue Created* event as a template   !Screenshot1.png|thumbnail!   - Edit any workflow as follows:   -- Click on the transition triggered when an issue is created   -- Click on the *Post functions* link from this transition   -- Change the event fired in the post functions to the custom event   !Screenshot2.png|thumbnail!   -- Publish the new workflow   - Open a new ticket using this workflow   - Wait for the batched notifications to be received   - Check the content of the batched notification email    h3. Expected Results    The batched notification contains:   - an sentence saying """"This issue has been CREATED""""   - the details about this tickets fields (Summary, Issue Type, Priority, etc...)   !Screenshot3.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results    The batched notification is almost empty. It contains the issue key and summary and a link to it, but it's missing the rest of the information:   !Screenshot4.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround   - Fire the default *Issue Created* event during the *Create* transition   - Or disable the batched notifications feature"""
comments: "Considering that batched email notifications were announced as one of the highlights of version 8.0 and were even made the default since 8.4, the workaround is rather disappointing. Some of our workflows rely on group notifications, and this can only be achieved with custom events:    https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/send-notifications-based-on-issue-type-for-a-project-in-jira-server-678559814.html    Please make sure to attach a limitation notice to this article, that the solution doesn't work with batched notifications." [SEP] "@Hi Tobias, I correctly understood, that issue can be solved with forced re-indexing in post-function?"

summary: "Testing connection with server authenticated with OAuth2 in add/update server page while having errors in the form will disable Test Connection button after one trial"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Test connection button becomes disabled once there is an error in the form.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open Jira with OAuth2 integration     # Go to Incoming Mail page (type . ~~ and then Incoming Mail)     # Click Add mail server (or Edit on existing one)     # Select OAuth2 integration as Authentication method and perform Authorization      # Fill the form in a way there will be error in it (e. g. leave Name field empty)     # Click Test Connection button    h3. Expected Results    Test fails and messages about error in the form is displayed. Test Connection button is still enabled.  h3. Actual Results    Test fails and messages about error in the form is displayed. *Test Connection button becomes disabled.*  h3. Workaround    To have Test Connection button enabled again one must perform Authorization procedure once more."""

description: """We have users that would like to filter/report on tickets and include the last 'x' public comments.  These views would then be used in discussions with external customers regarding their tickets."""

It would be a godsend to have a way to enable this besides yet another scripted custom field (which will have to be maintained through every Jira upgrade, in perpetuity).    I believe this improvement would strongly enhance teams' organizational connectivity and ability to collaborate other teams.   # Scenario one:   ## Team A wants to collect feedback | has a kudos project for teams or individuals, and wants input from outside their team.   ## Each feedback item | kudo is a Jira issue.    ## They want to display the currently active issues on a Confluence page where everyone can see this activity.    ## They want to be able to display the most recent comment on each issue, as a way to inform others and to encourage participation.   # Scenario two:   ## Team A supports Teams B and C, e.g. for deployments, license agreements, or customer configuration activities.   ## Team A also does other work, so not all tickets relate to external team requests.

Removing the Team custom field, and preventing the duplicate custom field name will avoid this problem."""

summary: "When an agent transitions an issue to a status they do not have permission to, they are stuck on a loading page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the admin creates a transition which limits the visibility of an issue, the agents getting stuck on a loading screen. When the agent then refreshes the page, the are presented with a permissions error (as expected).    This was reported via the Atlassian community: [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Service-Desk-questions/How-to-redirect-agent-to-custom-screen-after-transition/qaq-p/1335476]  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an agent who does not have access to view issues when they are in a particular status   # Create a transition which transitions issues to the status above which the agent cannot view   # As the agent transition the issue to this status   # Observe the page stuck loading (should not happen)   # Refresh and observe the permissions error (expected)    h3. Expected Results    Agent is redirect to the portal home page, or allow the transition to define a redirect page to land on.  h3. Actual Results    The agent is stuck on a loading page.  h3. Workaround    Refresh and then redirect back to the portal"""
