description: """Ein """"[~username]"""" wird in Überschriften nicht gerendert und bleibt ein """"[~username]""""!"""

summary: "Document Tomcat versions for different Jira releases"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, information about which Tomcat version is included with each Jira release is not easily available. Such information is useful when   planning a Jira upgrade, for example, to get around Tomcat vulnerabilities on specific versions.  h3. Suggested Solution    A document that is similar to [Bundled Tomcat and Java versions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/bundled-tomcat-and-java-versions-1005786018.html] which currently available for Confluence.  h3. Workaround    [Finding the Bundled Tomcat Version Per JIRA Release|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/finding-the-bundled-tomcat-version-per-jira-release-779291457.html]   A current possible workaround would be to download the archive release of a Jira version and check which Tomcat version is included, but as this can be time-consuming when checking different versions, making this information more readily available would be an improvement."""
comments: "[https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/JIRACORE/Bundled+Tomcat+and+Java+versions]" [SEP] "Thanks, I've wanted this for years!" [SEP] "Though I would be astonished if the Tomcat or bundled JRE changed in a patch release!   Well, looking at 7.13.2 it did, so good to know"

summary: "Allow permissions groups to only allow certain users to applinks "
description: """currently, if I create an app link from:   JIRA > Bitbucket let's say, all users if they have developer permissions, will see the following if there is dev panel info:   !image-2020-06-04-13-18-36-978.png|thumbnail!   Now, if I have multiple git based apps linked, I may be challenged for authentication to view the dev panel info, _even_ if I don't have permissions to view the repo on the Bitbucket side. And even worse, if the user has never logged into this Bitbucket instance but is being auto-added to the application default group on log in, this can consume a license.   Proposed Fix:   Allow the ability to define permissible groups on the app link itself, therefor only users that actually have permissions on the back end repos will be challenged for auth, saving time, money and frustration. """

summary: "Few external links are being lost while editing comments in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Few external links are being lost while editing comments in Jira    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create 2 external links in issue comments in Jira in both visual and text mode.   # Add the comment.   # click on edit comment and try to add few more links in both text and visual mode, some links will go missing    Attached the video of the issue replication    h3. Expected Results   All links should be visible and customers should be able to open    h3. Actual Results  Some links go missing or lost while editing comment     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Adding share permission to multiple entities(group/users) using rest API fails with error 400(bad request)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Adding share permission to multiple entities(group/users) using rest API as explained in the [documentation|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.1/#api/2/filter-addSharePermission], fails with an error.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Login to Jira and create filter.  * Make sure you have two groups with names """"jira-software-users"""", """"jira-administrators"""" present for purpose of this test.  * Hit the endpoint below using any tool(postman or browser extension):  {code:java}  http://domain_url:8080/rest/api/2/filter/<ID_OF_FILTER>/permission/  {code}  With following body:  {code:java}  [  {    """"type"""": """"group"""",    """"groupname"""": """"jira-software-users"""",    """"view"""": true,    """"edit"""": false  },  {          """"type"""": """"group"""",          """"groupname"""": """"jira-administrators"""",          """"view"""": true,          """"edit"""": false      }  ]  {code}      h3. Expected Results  It should add the permissions to mentioned groups.    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown:      (i) If we pass just one entity(group/user) it works.    h3. Workaround  None  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Date Picker in Business Projects is displayed in English while using Jira in Turkish, displaying Date format error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Although Jira is in the Turkish language, Date Picker is displayed in English. When creating an issue and picking a date (e.g. Due Date), Jira shows the following error:   !Jira error.png!    This problem was just found in Business Projects.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change the language of your profile to Turkish   # Create a business project   # Have a field that uses the Date Picker on the create issue screen, e.g., Due Date   # Pick a date and try to create the issue    h3. Expected Results    When choosing a Date, the months and days should be in Turkish.  h3. Actual Results    Days and months are displayed in English, even though Jira is in Turkish. It displays an error saying the date format is incorrect.    h3. Workaround    Users can manually change the language of the month, eg. 3/Jun/20 into 3/Haz/20    OR    Use Jira in English instead of Turkish."""

summary: "Two dimensional filter statistics gadget showing wrong values for 'None' on X axix"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The two dimensional statistics gadget shows wrong value for 'None' when there is multi-select-list custom field in the axis. Also, redirects to a wrong filter when clicked on the value.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create custom field with multiple choices (eg. custom field named Browsers, can have internet browser as options - IE, Edge, Firefox, Chrome etc.)  2. Create a simple filter like """"project = TEST and issuetype = Bug""""  Add Two dimensional filter statistics gadget to your dashboard specifying the filter from Step 1  3. Set XAxis = some multi-select-list custom fields  4. Set YAxis = epic link  5. Save the filter.    h3. Expected Results  The 'None' value will show the empty fields and clicking on the value will take to the JQL correctly.  h3. Actual Results  The 'None' values are shown wrong data and when clicking on the value, the previously clicked filter result is displayed.  h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "As a Developer, we would like that the DVCS plugin was also open-source, as Jira"
description: """We would like to have access to the DVCS plugin source code for our own research, as we do have for other Atlassian products as Jira.  The plugin used to be public but it is not anymore. We can find an old version still available at https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/jira-dvcs-connector/src/master/."""

summary: "Removing OAuth2 integration used by incoming mail server integration makes Jira return 500 error for every page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Problem related to new method of authentication of incoming mail servers.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create new OAuth 2.0 integration in Jira (Administration > System OAuth 2.0)   # Create new incoming mail server integration (Administration > Incoming mail > Add new Mail server)   # Select integration from Step 1 as authorization method in new mail server integration.   # Remove OAuth 2.0 integration from Step 1.   # Restart Jira or wait for cache invalidation (around 30 minutes). Removing an another mail server configuration also triggers cache invalidation    h3. Expected Results    Jira work without problem.  h3. Actual Results    Every page in Jira returns 500 errors due to cache creation problems  h3. Workaround    Remove from database table `MAILSERVER` entry for mail server associated with removed OAuth2 integration"""

summary: "Support HEIC file format attachment"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When attaching HEIC file into Jira, it will be attached without Thumbnails. When clicking on the file, it will download the file rather than show a preview. There are also cases where clicking the file would prompt the preview window displaying an error message.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow Jira to support HEIC file format attachment so that it will be treated just like other image formats (e.g.: PNG, JPG)    Alternatively, Jira could also automatically convert the attached HEIC file into the supported format.    h3. Workaround   Manually convert the HEIC format to the supported format before attaching"""

summary: "Unable to change dashboard/filter ownership if shared to public but Public Sharing is turned off"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a dashboard/filter is shared to Public(""""Anyone on the web""""), but _Sharing with anyone_   _on the web_ is turned +OFF+, when attempting to +Change Owner+, the admin receives the following error:  {panel:bgColor=#f8d7da}  (x) Dashboards and filters in this Jira instance cannot be shared with anyone on the web anymore. If you want to modify this item, you must change the Shares so that it is not shared with anyone on the web.  {panel}  It may be impossible to change the ownership of that filter/dashboard if the filter/dashboard is owned by someone else (potentially who is no longer contactable), and the admin does not have +Edit+ permissions, because the admin is not able to edit existing permissions unless they are the owner, or have (EDIT) permissions (JRASERVER-41269)    Users fixing filters/dashboards that need to be remediated is harder due to JRASERVER-68523  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *ON*   # Create a dashboard. You are the owner of this dashboard.   # Share the dashboard to Anyone on the web (VIEW)   # Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *OFF*   # Jira Admin -> System -> Shared Dashboards   # Find your dashboard and click *Change Owner*   # Attempt to change the owner to someone else   ## (!) The error is shown    h3. Expected Results    The dashboard ownership is changed. The admin can then remove the _Anyone on the web_ share setting.  h3. Actual Results    The admin receives the error above and is unable to remove the share.  h3. Workaround   # Temporarily enable public sharing via Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *OFF*   # Remove the _Anyone on the web_ share from the affected filter(s)/dashboard(s)   # Disable public sharing via Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *OFF*"""
comments: "I was not able to reproduce this bug in Jira 7.13.9. [~mwalas].  Also, https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-68523 was not reproducible on 7.13.9 for ""Public sharing"" permission either.     could it be due to https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-18076 ?" [SEP] "I can reproduce it in Jira Server 8.5.5"

summary: "Misleading error message when linking issue without application access"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When trying to link issue between instance A and B where user doesnt have application access in instance B, it will throw:  {quote}  The Jira server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation.  {quote}  This is misleading for system admin. No error is thrown in the log either.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create application link between instance A and instance B.  # Grant user X the application access of instance A, but not instance B.  # Link issue in instance A with issue in instance B.    h3. Expected Results  Error message will be thrown stating user doesnt have application access in another instance.    h3. Actual Results  It throws the following error as soon as user tries to type the issue key:  {quote}  The Jira server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation.  {quote}  without any error in the log either.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Issue collector visual bug after form submission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Normally, the issue collectors would return a feedback to the user after the form was submitted.    Currently, it is failing to render a message. Instead, the form just goes blank, and there is an error message in the browser console:   !Screen Shot 2020-06-01 at 13.22.53.png|thumbnail!  h3. Expected Results    The issue is created, and there is a success message after the form submission.  h3. Actual Results    The issue is created, but the form just goes blank after the form submission.    The following error is displayed n the bowser console:    h3. Workaround    Use different browsers(Firefox, Safari,etc) other than Chrome.    The issue seems to be fixed in Chrome 83.0.4103.97."""

summary: "As a user, I'd like to be able to identify archived issues in issue or epic links"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When an issue is archived e.g. an Epic. There is currently no indication on linked or child issues, that the linked issue has been archived. So owners of linked issues who need to update their links don't have a way of identifying that they are still linked to an archived (potentially obsolete) issue, and may not know to make necessary changes e.g. re-link to a more current issue.         h3. Suggested Solution  Add a form of visual marker (e.g. an automatic *Archived* label) on Epic or other Issue links, if the linked issue happens to be archived.     """

summary: "Jira startup in Azure - on Server reboot"
description: """When customers use our Azure/AWS templates to spin up a Data Center environment in Azure/AWS, there is not feature to restart Jira application on server reboot. Customers would like to have this feature in order to use auto scaling features in cloud infrastructure.    Currently this is not supported and customers need to configure the service start-up configurations manually. Hence this feature request. Based on feasibility, please help customers with this new feature.  """

summary: "Add true Typeahead support to system fields"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, Jira does not support true Typeahead searches when adding fix versions, components, labels, and other fields as such. As an example, if a version named {{1.12.13/charlie}} exists in the project, users will not be able to add it to the Fix Version/s field by simply typing """"Charlie"""" - for the user to find this version, it will need to type {{1.12.13/}} before they are able to find the {{charlie}} version.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add true Typeahead support to the Jira system fields.    h3. Why this is important  This provides a better user experience where they are able to filter and search labels/components/versions using any section of the string."""

summary: "Page alignment and status change issue after editing Issue list through filters/JQL query"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Focus is shifting to top of the page when issues are edited from the list after getting the list through filters/JQL  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to any existing filter or write a new JQL query to get a list of issues.   # Scroll down to the latter half of the page, go to “...” > Edit > (Any changes in the Issues’ Edit Screen) > click Update. Now you see the control/focus is taken to the top of the page. You have to scroll down to see the same issue.   # Scroll down to the later half of the page, go to “...” > Edit > Change the status of the Issue (InProgress/Done/any other). The control/focus is taken to the top of the page. When you scroll down to the issue where status was changed, it is not reflected. The status change will be reflected after ‘refreshing’ the page. (Refresh and scroll down where the issue was listed)    h3. Expected Results   * The focus of the page should remain on the Issue which was edited.   * If Issues’ status is changed, it should be reflected right away, and the focus of the page should stay on the row where the issue was edited.    h3. Actual Results   * After editing the issue, the focus of the page is shifted and taken to the top of the page. Need to scroll down to the original issue’s row.   * After changing the status of the issue, change is not reflected right away. We need to refresh the page and scroll back to the row in which issue was present    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "System restore functionality skips ActiveObjects data import if a single XML file is specified"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  One can specify a single XML file containing entities data when using the *Restore system* funcionality. When this is done, Jira will only import entities and skip importing ActiveObjects entirely.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Generate an XML backup by navigating to *Cog Icon > System > Backup system.*  # Place the generated zip file inside {{JIRA_HOME/import}} and unzip it in order to expose the files {{entities.xml}} and {{activeobjects.xml}}.  # Navigate to *Cog Icon > System > Restore system* and fill in the value *{{entities.xml}}* for the field *File name*.  # Click *Restore*.    h3. Expected Results  Jira should only accept a zip file containing both {{entities.xml}} and {{activeobjects.xml}}, aborting the process if a different file type is specified.    h3. Actual Results  Jira will only import the entities in the provided file, and nothing else. ActiveObjects import will be completely skipped.    This will cause all information stored in AO tables to not be imported. Such data includes:  * Agile boards.  * Sprints.  * Issue ranking data.  * Webhooks.  * Most data used by Atlassian's or third-party apps.    No errors or exceptions regarding partial data being imported are logged.    h3. Workaround  Make sure to specify a zip file containing both {{entities.xml}} and {{activeobjects.xml}}.  """
comments: "Hi all,     I transitioned the initial bug report to a suggestion since the described behaviour is the expected one according to the current documentation [https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/ADMINJIRASERVER/Backing+up+data]    Cheers,    Jira Server Bugfix team"

summary: "sprint health gadget"" shows data from old sprint"

Steps to Reproduce  # I created a sprint test3 and start working on it.  # After that, I create a sprint health gadget in my dashboard and selected the sprint test3.  # After completing/closing that sprint, I created another sprint named sprint 4 which is active  # After that, I checked the dashboard (sprint health gadget) and it was still showing sprint 3 rather than sprint 4.  # The refreshing browser did not help as well.      h3. summary: "Use Project Category to associate boards to projects"
description: """When a board is built it will not associate with a project it is pulling from when the query is using category the way it will with using the project field.    e.g.    team = 100 and project in (A, B, C)  – this filter can be used to build a board and will associate with each project.         team = 100 and category = D  – if the projects A, B, C have been added to the project category D and return the exact same results, this filter will NOT connect to the associated projects.     Being that category is associated with projects it should associate the boards with projects in the same manner.  In the first example I have to go to every board and update it if want to add a project to it and keep them associated.  With the second I merely have to update the project to the appropriate category."""
comments: "This is an excellent suggestion!    I second the motion; Project Category is the answer to so many scaling issues in Jira, and I wish it could be used in this way and in many other contexts (custom field config, permissions, built in reports, etc.)."

summary: "Pasting into prefotmatted box removes line breaks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When copying some texts into a preformatted box, in some situations it removes the line breaks.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    I found 2 distinct situations in which the line breaks vanish in the preformatted box:   # HTML content. That means when you copy some content and it goes as HTML content to the clipboard. It happens on Word, Visual Studio, Web browsers... anything that keeps the formatting over the clipboard.   # When there is *more than one line break with carriage return in a row*, it removes all of them instead of at least leaving one behind. This applies to Windows notepad, putty, and so on.  On Mac, for example, it's common to have only line breaks without the carriage returns, and it removes _only_ the several in a row, leaving one behind.    h3. Expected Results    In all cases, the text paste should behave in the same way.  h3. Actual Results    Depending on the text line breaks form, it behaves differently.    For example, let's take the text:    If it has only line feeds, and it's copied to the preformatted box, we get the expected result:    If it uses line feed+ carriage return, we get an extra line break removed:    If copied from Visual Studio Code, or any browser that does it in HTML in the clipboard:    h3. Workaround    To use the """"code"""" format box, or paste into the text mode."""

summary: "Support unified login and switching between multiple instances in the mobile app"
description: """An instance switcher or UI nav to switch between authenticated/app linked instance would be great…if most companies are using SAM/SSO for login, it would be ideal if the admin could configure or use application links to specify all the available instances that can be logged into and the user can choose from a dropdown list and then proceed to their IDP to authenticate as needed."""

summary: "Customize the look and feel of the mobile app to distinguish between different instances"
description: """As an end user of the Server mobile apps, I might be logging into multiple Jira instances, and it would be ideal to have a visual indicator for which instance I am logged into without needing to go to the settings menu.  In many, cases administrators will change the look and feel of the desktop application with logos or colors. The mobile app should inherit those settings, or at least allow administrators to easily configure this look and feel for the desktop app so that end users can easily distinguish which instance they're logged into."""

summary: "Editing an issue with cascading select without child option clears the parent option"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Submitting the edit form for an issue containing a cascading select without child option in Jira 8.8.1 and 8.9.0 clears the parent option.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create a cascading custom field with any parent and child options  2. Create an issue or edit any existing issue so that the parent option is set and child option remains """"None""""  3. Open issue edit form (""""Edit"""" button on the issue view) and submit it without any changes    h3. Expected Results  The issue is not changed    h3. Actual Results  The parent option was removed from the issue    The information about the removed parent option is stored in the issue history    h3. Workaround  You can edit a velocity template for the cascading select custom field to fix this issue.  To do so:  # open the file _<JIRA_INSTALLATION>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/templates/plugins/fields/edit/edit-cascadingselect.vm_.  # There, edit the code on line 48 from:  #*     #* to:    # After applying the change, save the file and restart the Jira node.    In Jira DC, the workaround needs to be applied separately for every node."""
comments: "Hi Daniel  I tested successfully the workaround in our Test/Integration System  I edited ""edit-cascadingselect.vm"" in our Production System  the Service reStart will automatically happen tomorrow morning  I then inform my customers to test the cascading select field  I let you know abou the results  thanks  -Eckhard" [SEP] "." [SEP] "Hi Daniel  I implemented the Fix in our production system  the problem is fixed  thanks and take care  -Eckhard" [SEP] "[~eckhard.grutsch]  Thank a lot for testing the fix and fast feedback loop.     Cheers"

summary: "Update atlassian-plugins dependency to use official version"
description: """Jira ships with a custom build of {{atlassian-plugins}} library ({{5.2.3-96cf760eb}}). This creates a risk that Jira team themselves will need to backport any bugfixes that will apply to {{5.2}} branch."""
comments: "According to Server Platform's research (https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/platform-module-usage/wiki/atlassian-plugins.md) this problem appears since 8.5. Jira 7.13 uses older (but official) version of atlassian-plugins.    This issue is more a risk than a bug. My goal as a Server Platform representative is to work with Jira team so they can start using official version of atlassian-plugins and not the custom fork.   A perfect scenario for us is if also affected Jira ER version will update to the official version of the component.    cc: [~lvysochyn]" [SEP] "Atlassian plugins has beens bumped to 5.3.7 official version. It should be released in 8.12."

summary: "Webhook content should not be localized"
description: """h3. Summary  For issue webhooks, the content of the webhook (the representation of the issue) will be localized according to the language preference of the user who created or updated the issue. As webhook consumers may not use the same language, it makes more sense to always send webhooks in the system language.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new issue updated webhook  # Change your user language preference to a non-English language like Spanish or German or French  # Update an issue  # Observe the webhook that is sent. Notice that the issue type names, descriptions, and possibly resolution values are localized    h3. Expected Results  Webhook data is retained as per the default language set in General Configuration.    h3. Actual Results  Webhook data is localized as per the user language preference.       h3.Workaround  There is no workaround available. """
comments: "As a purely global company, you can imagine the impact.. there's a variety of language that was thrown by the payload    This is impacting us badly and causing alot of confusion previously. Now, its just making a mess to our middleware logic as we need to cater for all language if we need to utilize more details from the payload"

summary: "Projects sharing the same scheme are listed as hidden"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Whenever you access a scheme's page, Jira allows you to display a list of the projects currently using. However, if there are more than 15 projects sharing this scheme (permission, priority, field configuration), the application will display the first 15 projects and then list the others as hidden.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1  Assign more than 15 projects to the same scheme, let's use Y for this value.   # Step 2  Access this scheme's page on Jira's administrative panel.    h3. Expected Results    The entire list of projects using the scheme will be displayed.    h3. Actual Results    Only the first 15 projects are displayed and then Jira displays the following message:     {quote}  and X hidden projects.  {quote}    where 15 + X = Y. As we can see in the screenshot below:     !Screen Shot 2020-05-22 at 17.54.54.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Quite critical in administration of our instance which uses many shared schemes. Not having a quick way to see the list of affected projects is painful."

summary: "Duplicate To..."
description: """As a member of the UX team, we work closely with dev teams often. Because we're on separate teams, we manage tasks on separate Jira boards even though cards flow through parallel processes.     As a member of UX team alongside a dev team, I'd like to move stories to the UX Jira board so we can manage and track the story before handing it back to development.    Currently, cloning a matter exists but it's not directional; the card appears below the cloned Jira card. Instead, I want to be able to clone a Jira Card specifically to a different art board.    It would be awesome if I could track that same Jira card between both boards from one, but baby steps.    I also want to do this in bulk."""

summary: "Long description string expands column outside of filter results gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an issue containing a long string in the description field is displayed in a filter results gadget, the description column is expanded beyond the fixed size of the gadget. This pushes any columns following the description out of view, causing confusion for end users. Likewise, if the gadget is paginating results, one or some pages will resize the description field as above, while others display """"normally"""" with all of the field columns being displayed    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue and add an extremely long string to the description field.   # Create a search filter which includes this issue along with some other issues.   # Create a dashboard and add the Filter Results gadget; configured to use the filter above.   # Move the description field to the first position in the column order.   # View the dashboard and notice how the fields after the description column are not visible. (You can scroll to the right to find them)    h3. Expected Results    All fields/columns should be visible in the gadget at all times without having to scroll to the right to see them. A single column should not be auto-resized to take up the entire gadget.  h3. Actual Results    The description column is being resized ad infinitum based on the length of the longest string.  h3. Workaround    Break up the long strings with manual carriage returns so that the column is resized appropriately, and so that other columns are visible.  """

summary: "Development Activity breaks Sprint indexes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Performing development related activities such as creating commits, branches, pull requests will cause the related issue to disappear from the active sprint board.     Please note that this behavior cannot be reproduced reliably and the steps to reproduce will need to be repeated several times.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start a sprint   # Create a new issue  # Add the issue to active sprint by editing sprint field on the issue  # Transition Issue   # Create a branch for issue via Jira  # Create a Pull request   # Transition Issue     h4. Steps to Reproduce 2  # Start a sprint   # Create issue   # Add issue to active sprint by editing sprint field on issue  # Transition issue  # Commit a new message with the issue key  # Check the sprint board     h3. Expected Results  The issues should be visible on the active sprint board     h3. Actual Results  Check the Sprint board - the issue disappears from the board    # In this example, we can see issue *AH-3* is added to the Sprint field for *AH Sprint 1* but we cannot see the issue on the active sprint board:   !image-2020-05-26-22-52-47-459.png|thumbnail!    !image-2020-05-26-22-52-57-131.png|thumbnail!   # Perform a project re-index and issue re-appears   !image-2020-05-26-22-54-55-367.png|thumbnail!     (i) It is very hard to reproduce the issue reliably. After countless commits, we manage to reproduce the problem again ( AH-4 this time )   !image-2020-05-26-22-57-14-737.png|thumbnail!    !image-2020-05-26-22-57-25-506.png|thumbnail!     Checking this SQL output, the database returns correctly which confirms the problem with the indexes :       !image-2020-05-26-22-58-37-244.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Use the reindex rest API as a work around for the issues that are impacted: https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.9.0/#api/2/reindex-reindexIssues"""
comments: "This affects my team as well. what should be an easy look at the board to find the ticket turns into having to search our backlog for the appropriate one.    I ended up creating a separate project to store our backlog items due to this bug it's very tedious" [SEP] "I'm seeing this more and more.    Certain tickets won't stay on the active sprint board.    I have to regularly re-index to get tickets back onto the active sprint board." [SEP] "Voting up this bug fix - very frustrating for our development and project management team." [SEP] "Up voting as well. This happens every couple weeks and is very confusing for the team."

summary: "Grey out the Cancel button once transition is clicked"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Grey out the Cancel button once transition is clicked  h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Create a workflow  * Create the transition with screen    move the issue from one transition to another  h3. Expected Results    Cancel button greys out once transition is clicked  h3. Actual Results    Cancel is still available even though transition is already done.    !Jira-Issue 1.png|width=402,height=222!  h3. Workaround    no workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "*Issue Description:* When user clicks on state transition button(present on the pop up screen) and immediately clicks on cancel button, at this moment it's too late to cancel the action triggered and on UI user still remains on current state as he clicked cancel button but in the backend the issue has transitioned into target state. So when user again tries to perform any transition from current screen , he gets error as ""It seems that you have tried to perform a workflow operation (Request Approval) that is not valid for the current state of this issue (APPROVAL REQUIRED). The likely cause is that somebody has changed the issue recently, please look at the issue history for details"".     Graying out the cancel button(on pop up screen) when the transition button is 'clicked' will resolve this jira problem."

summary: "Jira Mail Handler Configuration can't be edited when using an incorrect project / issue type combination"
description: """h3. Issue description    Jira Mail Handler of types *Create a new issue or add a comment to an existing issue* and *Create a new issue from each email message* can't be edited if they have been created with an incorrect Project / Issue Type combination.  h3. Steps to reproduce   - Create 2 Jira projects using different Issue Type Schemes. For example:   -- 1 project only has 1 issue type (Bug) -> let's call this project """"Awesome""""   -- 1 project only has 1 issue type (Story) -> let's call this project """"Awesomeness""""   - Go to the page *⚙ > System > Incoming Mails*   - Configure a Mail Server (POP or IMAP)   - Click on the *Add incoming mail handler* button to create a new mail handler   - Select the type *Create a new issue or add a comment to an existing issue*, and click on the *Next* button   - Select the project """"Awesome"""" and the issue type """"Bug""""   !Step1.png|thumbnail!   - Change the project from """"Awesome"""" to """"Awesomeness""""   -- (!) Notice that the issue type field is now empty, because the """"Bug"""" issue type does not exist in the project """"Awesomeness""""   !Step2.png|thumbnail!   - Click on the *Add* button to save this mail handler (with an empty issue type)   -- (!) Notice that the mail handler was created with the Project """"Awesomeness"""" and the issue type """"Bug"""" *which does not exist in this project   !Step3.png|thumbnail!   - Click on the *Edit* button next to this mail handler   - Click on the *Next* button to edit the configuration of this mail handler    h3. Expected behavior    We should be redirected to the page that allows us to configure various settings such as Project, Issue Type, Strip Quotes, Catch Email Address, etc...  h3. Actual behavior   - We are redirected to the page *⚙ > System > Incoming Mail* and the screen is frozen/greyed   - The only way to get out of this page is to hit the *Esc* key   - The following error is displayed in the Browse Console logs    !Step4.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    The only know workaround is to delete this corrupted mail handler, and to re-create it with a valid combination of project and issue type."""

summary: "Allow roll up of custom numeric fields"
description: """h3. Scope    Advanced Roadmaps for Jira Server (JPOSERVER)    _(I don't seem to be able to select JPOSERVER as a project when creating this suggestion. Possibly due to the rename? Please move my ticket to the correct project.)_  h3. Problem Definition    Allow roll up of values from child issues to the parent for custom numeric fields.    Currently, when a user enables roll up in the view settings, Advanced Roadmaps performs roll ups for a set of predefined (system) fields:   * estimates   * teams   * sprints   * releases    h3. Suggested Solution    Perform roll up of values for any custom numeric field added to the view.  h3. Workaround   * Create a scripted custom field   * Use a different app (e.g. Structure)"""

summary: "Missing critical tomcat connector parameters in Running Jira over SSL documentation"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  We are missing {{relaxedPathChars=""""[]|"""" relaxedQueryChars=""""[]|{}^\`""""<>""""}} parameters, required since Jira 12, in the connector example provided in """"Step 2. Update Tomcat with the KeyStore"""" of our https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver0712/running-jira-applications-over-ssl-or-https-959314173.html documentation. Issue exists in all version documentations since Jira 12.2    h3. Suggested Solution  All server.xml samples in this documentation should include these parameters.     Jira will not start without them. See [Jira server startup fails with Tomcat misconfigured error|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-server-startup-fails-with-tomcat-misconfigured-error-958453799.html]"""

summary: "Identify non-production Jira instance in Jira Server mobile app"
description: """We have various Jira instances - production, staging, UAT. They all have different base urls. And also different header colors to make it easy to tell them apart. Making changes in a prod Jira when you thought it was staging is not good.    As I was using the Jira Server mobile app I realized that the only way you can tell which Jira instance you are connected to is to look at the Settings gear, where the Admin, Settings, Title is shown. The problem with this is that we don't change that title in our different Jira instances because doing that can break Application Links, and the title isn't really shown anywhere in the UI.    So at the moment it's easy to make a change in a Jira instance without knowing which one you are connected to.    My suggestion is to add some way to tell mobile users when they look at an issue that they are not connected to their production Jira. Production Jira issues should not look any different    One way to do this would be to have a custom renderer that adds a prefix to the content of some field saying """"Non Prod"""" based on checking the base url. But that's pretty specific to each customer.    If there were a setting in Jira admin properties that by default says the Jira is production, and then admins change that setting in non-prod instances, then the mobile app could use this info to change something about how the issue appears.    """
comments: "Here's my workaround, specific to our Jira instance. I created a custom renderer that extends the Atlassian Wiki renderer with a plugin. I set the renderer for the Description field to use the new renderer. When I view non-production issues in the mobile app I get a warning:    https://photos.app.goo.gl/UoHyfxXNQzpQrX3RA    Huh, no attachments in jac issues I guess"

summary: "Selecting both board types in View All Boards page result in not displaying any board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Selecting both board types in View All Boards page result in not displaying any board    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a board of type Kanban or Scrum;  # Navigate to Boards > View All Boards;  # In the filter section, expand *Board type*;  # Select _both_ Scrum and Kanban;    h3. Expected Results  Both scrum and kanban boards show up.    h3. Actual Results  No boards are displayed. Note that the selection is mutually exclusive, however the filter string says that """"all"""" boards are filtered.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Improve the message displayed when completing a Sprint."
description: """Whenever you complete a sprint on a board, Jira displays a message informing how many issues have been completed and how many remain incomplete.     Then the application displays a message informing that it will move all issues to the next sprint, but if an issue is part of the sprint A (i.e. sprint field = sprint A) and has been removed from the scope of the board (by its filter). Jira will not move these issues.    This appears to be the application's expected behavior. Would it be possible to account for these issues before displaying the message?     !Screen Shot 2020-05-20 at 12.57.31.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Sending an email to a Service Project with a Software/Core issue key, Jira marks the ticket as ""read"" but does nothing else"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In some cases, Software users will forward a notification from a Software project with the issue. The email is forwarded to a Service Desk mail handler to create a Service Desk case in reference to the Software project (not issue linking).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue in a Software project   # Receive an email notification   # Forward that notification to a Service Desk Project    h3. Expected Results    A new issue is created *or* Jira does not process the email with a failure reason    h3. Actual Results    Jira processes the email by giving a """"success"""" in the mail log in the UI and marks the email as read. Jira does not create a ticket or update an issue.    h3. Workaround     There is no workaround outside of customer retraining (to not include the issue number in the subject for a new issue)"""

In the actual result shows,  {quote}Du har för närvarande *inge* ärenden tilldelade till dig. Njut av dagen!  {quote}  h3. Workaround  no workaround at the moment"""

summary: "Canned Response (request.details) replace double quotes with '$quote' in Summary"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When we create a canned response with variable request.details. If the Summary field has double quotes, it get replaced by    . This behavior is seen only for Summary(Title). For other fields like Description, the issue is not seen.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Create a ticket with Title containing double quotes. For example: Error found """"Testing error"""" and update the same in description as well   2. Create a canned response and select variable request.details   3. In comment, section for Summary it shows as Error found """"Testing error""""   4. However, the Description shows correct fonts: Error found """"Testing error""""  h3. Expected Results    Summary should all show double quotes  h3. Actual Results    Summary it shows as Error found """"Testing error""""  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Audit log migration: The JSW 8.8 and JSD 4.8 upgrade nodes documentation is providing conflicting/wrong info. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrading Jira software server and Jira service desk server (not data center), upon restart, the Audit log migration is taking place and is taking a long time to finish. Adding the """"{{jira.advanced.audit.log.migration.limit=10}}"""" parameter had no effect.  Based on the Upgrade notes, this supposed to take place in the backgroud, but that is not the case.        In the [jira-software-8-8-x-upgrade-notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-8-x-upgrade-notes-990550394.html] and [jira-service-desk-4-8-x-upgrade-notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/servicedesk/jira-service-desk-4-8-x-upgrade-notes-989763769.html] at the top part of the document, it notes: """"The migration runs in the background for all Server and Data Center upgrade paths (ZDU included) and does not stop Jira being operational.""""     But then at the bottom of the [jira-software-8-8-x-upgrade-notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-8-x-upgrade-notes-990550394.html], under the below section, there is the conflicting note where only in case of ZDU (Datacenter Zero Downtime Upgrade) this process runs in the background. This appears to be the correct statement based on what customers are experiencing.     !https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/files/990550394/990550386/2/1587581317563/icon_upgradeprocedure.png!   Upgrade procedure         +As the result of introducing the Advanced auditing feature, we'll be migrating your old audit log (up to 10 million records) on upgrade. Depending on the platform you're using and the size of your audit log, the migration might take up to several hours.+    +If you migrate with ZDU, your migration task will run in the background, and Jira will still be accessible to users.+    You can use the\{{ }}{{jira.advanced.audit.log.migration.limit}} flag in the Jira properties file to limit the number of events you want to migrate or to turn off the migration altogether. In that case, you will only see the new events in your upgraded Audit log view, and the past events will remain invisible until you have performed the migration.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Migrate to JSW 8.8/ JSD4.8  h3. Expected Results    The Audit Log Migration should run in the background upon restarting Jira after the upgrade.  h3. Actual Results    Audit log migration is happening while starting Jira after the upgrade and it takes a while to complete.   h3. Workaround    No known workaround"""

summary: "Filter sensitive information leaked through JMX monitoring"
description: """There are cases where system accounts and passwords (in clear text) are reported via JMX beans. One example is in the database connector bean. Accessing JMX does require a password, but we did not realize that read-only JMX access may be exposing system-level accounts.    Access to the JMX metrics might be allowed to both monitoring tools and custom plugin developers for debugging. Some beans contain passwords that shouldn't be distributed, for example, the DB Connector Bean shows the database username/password as a """"metric"""" in the bean"""

summary: "Collapsible dashboard sidebar"
description: """It would be great to have the ability to collapse the sidebar showing all your current dashboards. It can be quite busy when your dashboard is full of gadgets and you have a dozen or so dashboards you're currently tracking."""

summary: "Jira attempts to create a Group name using Turkish capital I with accent (\u0130) but it fails as the group already exists"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you have a group_name with custom value options that contain Turkish character {{İ}}, Jira will fail when:    a) Checking which users are members of this group;    b) Checking group changes during the user sign-in, trying to insert a group that already exists:  {code}  com.atlassian.jira.exception.DataAccessException: org.ofbiz.core.entity.GenericEntityException: while inserting: [GenericEntity:Group][groupName,Business-IC KONTROL-RISK_UYUM][createdDate,2020-05-05 10:34:12.153][lowerGroupName,business-ic kontrol-risk_uyum][active,1][description,null][lowerDescription,null][directoryId,10400][updatedDate,2020-05-05 10:34:12.153][id,18732][type,GROUP][local,0] (SQL Exception while executing the following:INSERT INTO cwd_group (ID, group_name, lower_group_name, active, local, created_date, updated_date, description, lower_description, group_type, directory_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?) (ORA-00001: unique constraint (JIRA_DC2.UK_GROUP_NAME_DIR_ID) violated  {code}    This used to work fine in 7.0, but not in 7.13 or 8.x.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Using Jira 7.0.10 and Java version 1.8_11,  create a group name using the Turkish character İ;  # Use the option """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" for the directory;  # Add users to these group;  # Synchronize Jira and Crowd users and groups.   # Jira group's shows the members, users are able to sign-in.  # Upgrade to Jira 7.13.9, Java jdk1.8.0_241  # Sign in with one member of this group    h3. Expected Results  The user should be able to sign-in    h3. Actual Results  The user is not able to sign-in   The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      h3. Workaround  * Use a group that does not have a Turkish special character;  * Change the option """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" to """"Update for new users only"""", instead of """"Every time the user logs in"""".    """

summary: "In ""Issue Edit screen"" the options for 'Select List (cascading)' being defaulted to (not available) which is not being recognized by Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In """"Issue Edit screen"""" the options for 'Select List (cascading)' being defaulted to (not available) which is not being recognized by Jira, neither the value was inserted manually.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field of type 'Select List (cascading)' and give the options for the select list, this happens only for the second level value in cascading list.   # Go to filed configuration and make the field as """"required"""" that we just created.   # Create a new issue where give the parent value of cascading list an option, and secondary value leave as 'None' and click on """"create"""".   # Once the issue gets created click on """"Edit"""" button on the left side and try to update this will throw an error stating """"The filed is required"""" and the secondary field where we left the option """"None"""" will be defaulted to """"(Not available)"""" which you can't see while creating an issue and this value is being defaulted only in the """"Edit issue"""" screen.    Attached screen recording while reproducing the issue and this seems to be related to the bug JRASERVER-62455 where it was introduced to prevent the data loss. and this was introduced in Jira version 8.8.1  h3. Expected Results    When the field value is set to """"None"""" this should not default to """"(not available)"""" in the Edit screen.  h3. Actual Results    While clicking on the """"Edit"""" option the values which have """"None"""" as default being changed to (Not available), make Jira treat the not-empty field as empty. And issue editing is only possible when the value is manually changed to None. This thing is the main problem.  h3. Workaround    You can edit a velocity template for the cascading select custom field to fix this issue.  To do so:  # open the file _<JIRA_INSTALLATION>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/templates/plugins/fields/edit/edit-cascadingselect.vm_.  # There, edit the code on line 48 from:  #*     #* to:    # After applying the change, save the file and restart the Jira node.    In Jira DC, the workaround needs to be applied separately for every node."""
comments: "I'm closing this issue as a duplicate of https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-71099, which has been fixed in Jira 8.9.1 and 8.10.0.    I've also updated the workaround for those customers that can't easily upgrade their instances."

summary: "Allow users with Administer Projects permission to change the category of a project"
description: """In our multi department use of Jira, there are multiple project managers and we use categories to mark projects as closed.    Having to bother the admin everytime is non-efficient.    I would like to allow users with the """"Administer Projects"""" permission to change the """"Project Category""""."""

summary: "While editing an issue in two browser tabs the first committed changes are being overwritten with the least recent updates"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While editing an issue in two browser tabs the first committed changes are being overwritten with the least recent updates.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open the same issue in two tabs in the same browser.   # In the first tab click in """"Edit"""" to edit the issue and update the """"Description"""" and click on update   # In the second tab click """"Edit"""" butting of the same issue and change the """"Summary"""" and click on update.   # Now the changes that we have made to the description in the first tab, will be removed as in the 2nd tab we have updated only the summary but not Description.          Attached screen recording of the issue that has been reported:       h3. Expected Results    It should update both Description and Summary as by clicking on the """"update"""" we are committing changes.  h3. Actual Results    Only the least recently made updates(in this case updating summary)are being considered despite that we have updated the description in the first tab  h3. Workaround    When you observed the changes are not being reflected in the second tab ( in this case it's description) try to update the description as well along with the Summary."""
comments: "Perhaps a better description of the issue would be ""Edit screen updates fields which are not edited be the user"""

summary: "Improved error messaging for Administrator without Browse Project permission clicking Versions"
description: """As reported in JRASERVER-67162, a user with Administrator permissions to a given project but who lacks Browse permissions, is unable to click the Versions link in the settings left nav.    The result is a REST API failure message which is unfriendly to the user.    If an administrator who lacks browse cannot view/edit Versions, it would be preferred that when clicking the versions link they are presented with a more clear message to indicate they lack appropriate permissions to perform the function."""
comments: "!image-2020-05-19-22-13-47-169.png!" [SEP] "This has been tested by us in Jira 7.5.4 as well as 8.5.4 and the issue exists in both. Have a screen shot but forgot to add it when creating issue :)"

summary: "Allow configurable fields in REST API search using search request"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  While it is possible to return results via REST API search using search request, there are no options to select specific fields in the JSON response.     h3. Suggested Solution  Allow users to select specific fields to be returned in the JSON response.     h3. Why this is important  At times, not all data is required. With configurable fields, users have the flexibility to choose the fields that are needed.      h3. Workaround   No workaround at this point of time. """

summary: "JQL filter from JIRA 7.13 does not work on JIRA 8.5"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JQL filter from JIRA 7.13 does not work on JIRA 8.5  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Jira 7.13 a search like """"customfield ~ """"PRROJECT-*"""" showed all issues, with values like """"PROJECT-1234"""" in customfield.  In Jira 8.5 the same JQL search term delivers an empty result. This is a CHANGE of behavior or functionality.   # In Jira 8.5 a search like """"customfield ~ """"PRROJECT-"""" showed all issues, with values like """"PROJECT-1234"""" in customfield.  In Jira 7.13 the same JQL search term delivers an empty result. This is a CHANGE of behavior or functionality.    h3. Expected Results    JQL query should give constant results, where as result differ   h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround       Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Is the customfield a text field using the free text searcher?  Also I think the RR in the examples should be a single R, yes?"

summary: "Ability to restrict notification for all tagged issues on the board"
description: """*Current Feature*   At the moment, there is no feature to restrict the notification for the tag board.         *Suggestion*   It is good to have a feature to restrict the notification when the issues in the boards are tagged. So only to those issues that are tagged, restrict the notification being sent."""

summary: "Enterprise EOL should have date tied to Release date of new Enterprise Release"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, the Enterprise Releases have the same support time of two years after the first major iteration of the version release date as described on the [Atlassian Support End of Life Policy|https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-support-end-of-life-policy-201851003.html].  h3. Suggested Solution    The EOL of the previous Enterprise Release should be tied to the release date of the new Enterprise Release, giving additional time for customers to upgrade instead of the default two years since its own release date."""
comments: "We only use Enterprise releases. One year we were late with upgrading to the next Enterprise release. We talked to our TAM and he talked to Premier Support. We were still officially out of support but Premier Support worked on a best-effort basis to help us as needed. We felt that was a fair approach. I would imagine if for some reason the next Enterprise release was not available within two years of the last Enterprise release then Atlassian would handle that case when it came up. Just my thoughts"

summary: "Jira doesn't start using MySQL 5.7 and setting dbconfig.xml database type to mysql57"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira reports incorrectly that MySQL 5.7 database is MySQL 5.6 and won't start Jira when the dbconfig.xml has the database type set to mysql57.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install MySQL 5.7 and set the following.  {panel}  character_set_client utf8   character_set_connection utf8   character_set_database latin1   character_set_filesystem binary   character_set_results utf8   character_set_server latin1   character_set_system utf8   collation_connection utf8_general_ci   collation_database latin1_swedish_ci   collation_server latin1_swedish_ci   version 5.7.30-0ubuntu0.18.04.1  {panel}     # In the dbconfig.xml set the database type to mysql57   # Set URL to change the character_endcoding to utf8mb4   # Restart Jira    h3. Expected Results  Jira will start and use the MySQL 5.7 database.    h3. Actual Results  Jira won't start as a result of the default settings. with the URL set with character_endcoding= utf8mb4      h3. Old keeping for reference - Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround.    If you change the dbconfig.xml to use the database type mysql rather than mysql57 Jira starts however you will get a collation warning by the health checker stating utf8mb4 is not a supported collation.    h3. Note on Resulution  * Steps to reproduce *are not in sync* with Atlassian guidelines how to configure MySql database.   ** According to documentaion page [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver088/installing-jira-applications/connecting-jira-applications-to-a-database/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7] {{character_set_server}} variable should be set to {{utf8mb4}}, (!) not _latin1_ like here.  * We verified Jira 8.8.0 with MySql 5.7.24 running inside docker and MySql 5.7.30-0ubuntu0.16.04.1 running on Ubuntu using JDBC drivers 8.0.20 and 5.1.48. (i) There were no problems connecting to and using that database."""
comments: "Steps to reproduce are not in sync with Atlassian guidelines how to configure MySql database. According to page [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver088/installing-jira-applications/connecting-jira-applications-to-a-database/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7] {{character_set_server}} variable should be set to {{utf8mb4}}, not latin1 like here.    I verified Jira 8.8.0 with MySql 5.7.24 running inside docker and MySql 5.7.30-0ubuntu0.16.04.1 running on Ubuntu using JDBC drivers 8.0.20 and 5.1.48. There were no problems connecting to and using that database." [SEP] "Jira is now working with utf8mb4. Thank you." [SEP] "[~0ea6fafdf90b]  Thanks for confirming that the _utf8mb4_ character_set_server works fine."

summary: "Wrong translation for Edit Sprint in German"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Translation for the word """"edit sprint"""" in German is incorrect in the Jira platform. In German, it is translated as End sprint  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change the language to Germany from profile > language settings   # Create a software project Using """"Scrum Software development"""" template   # Add an issue to the backlog and create an active sprint   # Here if you try to click on Edit sprint option, the Edit sprint show as """"Sprint beenden"""" which means """"End sprint"""" in German.   # Here the german translation for Edit sprint is showing incorrectly on UI   # It should show Edit sprint (Sprint bearbeiten)    h3. Actual Results    The translation show that it means End sprint (Sprint beenden)         *EXPECTED RESULT*    The translation should show Edit sprint (Sprint bearbeiten)          """
comments: "It basically hides certain functionality and has an impact on several users on our team and causes frustration and support requests. *As this has been introduced as a regression* in 8.8.x (I guess) and it was working in 8.7.x and before, *I consider this to be more than low priority and hope for a quick fix of this Bug.* " [SEP] "Reported to the translation project: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/I18N-3123 - will be fixed." [SEP] "Hi [~tobias12] and [~skaur@atlassian.com],    I can confirm we have located the source of this error and corrected this. We're working with the Engineering team to see if we can have this fix available in the next language pack release which is due out in the next 1-2 weeks. As soon as I have confirmation on the timing, I'll update this ticket.         Many thanks,    Melanie" [SEP] "Hi [~tobias12] and [~skaur@atlassian.com],    I can confirm the updated German translation is accessible in language pack for version release 8.12 (8.12.0.v20200711000536 specifically).

summary: "High contention in reading java.lang.ThreadLocal variables due to large number of ThreadLocalMap$Entry elements"
description: """{panel:title=Not a Jira bug| borderStyle=dashed| borderColor=#3C78B5| titleBGColor=#3C78B5| bgColor=#E7F4FA}  (i) This is not a Jira core bug, ticket created to track the problem and for transparency.   The problem is a combination of different factors, Java platform and libraries limitations that currently exist.   {panel}    h3. Issue Summary  Under a very high load and a large number of Lucene operations on a large Lucene index, JVM starts having contention in reading {{java.lang.ThreadLocal}} variables.  To be specific {{java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap.getEntry}} and {{java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap.getEntryAfterMiss}} calls become very contended which leads to high CPU usage (and high LA).     In short, it’s a combination of 3 factors:  * High *allocation rate* of _ThreadLocal_  - caused by Lucene lib (it's tracking index segments in {{org.apache.lucene.util.CloseableThreadLocal}})  ** and as consequence very high number of {{java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap$Entry}} in the corresponding {{java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap}}  * *Slow GC collection* of ThreadLocal since they are weakly referenced - a """"feature"""" of modern GC  * High *pressure* on  _ThreadLocal_ by doing high concurency reads from cache  ** Jira uses _EhCache_, which uses _ReentrantReadWriteLock_ internally, which uses _ThreadLocal_     h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Large Jira instance with large Lucene (to have many segments)  * Apply high load:  **  a lot of Lucene operations   ** large number of cache reads (eg. CachingProjectRoleAndActorStore.getProjectRole)    h3. Expected Results  * Application is stable under load     h3. Actual Results  * Application is not stable, large CPU usage  * Growing number of {{java.lang.ThreadLocal}} and corresponding {{java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap$Entry}}     h3. Notes about the symptoms   * Example of the stack-trace collected during the peak time:    * Count of stack in the thread-dumps:      * memory allocation  || Class || Number of objects || Size ||  | java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap$Entry | 880,393 | 49,302,008 |   | java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap$Entry[]| 710 | 13,328,016 |   | java.lang.ThreadLocal | 51,109 | 1,226,616 |     h3. Other notes    Reference to similar problems:  *  with Elastic Search - https://discuss.elastic.co/t/indexing-performance-degrading-over-time/40229/38   * OpenDJ - https://bugster.forgerock.org/jira/browse/OPENDJ-4027  * http://cs.oswego.edu/pipermail/concurrency-interest/2018-October/016685.html  bq. When short-lived ThreadLocals are created and die at a rate linear to application throughout, this leads to ThreadLocal maps inactive threads holding thousands or millions of entries, to extremely long collision chains, and to situations where half the CPU is spent walking those chains in ThreadLocal.get().  * https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-6625724    Note on ThreadLocal:  * ThreadLocal variables use WeakReferences as internal implementation, which are subject to different cleanup rules than normal references.   ** On G1GC, we have a time budget for maximum GC pause (a soft goal), and JVM may opt to delay the collection of WeakReferences to a cleanup of tenured space to meet that goal (it will only clean up a subset of cleanable weak references in young GC). This may cause a build-up of entries in the ThreadLocalMaps of threads.  * the following JVM parameter will give more details in GC logs about how much _WeakReferences_ are cleaned up:  ** -XX:+PrintReferenceGC    h3. Workaround  A couple of options are available here:  * upgrading to Jira 8.x  ** due to changes in Lucene and Jira it produces less _ThreadLocal_    * tuning G1GC  ** give it less aggressive goal to allow more _WeakReferences_ to be collected  * switching to CMS   ** based on Elastic Search examples, resolve the problem as it collects _Weakeferences_ more aggressively   ** It's an old GC algorithm, may have more frequent peaks and longer average collection time"""
comments: "Obsoleted by Lucene upgrade in Jira 8.x"

summary: "Ability to view ""Create Issue"" transition"
description: """The special inbound """"Create Issue"""" (number 1) transition should be displayed in Text mode  and also in Diagram mode without having to create a Draft - and allow user to access its properties, conditions, post-functions    Creating a draft to review that specific transition's post-functions is really annoying and error prone - if draft has been forgotten and not discarded, we are in trouble to know if that draft contains relevant changes.  (I have such use case to be able to open Script Runner post-function last executions logs)    Related issues that would be relevant to fix at the same time:  - https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-63428  - https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-37695  """

summary: "Source maps are reported as errors in console in Safari/Firefox/IE"
description: """Both Atlassian and vendor plugins often reference sourcemaps in their js files. These comments are often parsed even in batched mode, e.g. the last sourceMappingURL in the file (it seems).   Source map files cannot be obtained through WRM because WRM does not handle that file type and also at the moment we do not generate separate web-resources for sourcemaps - .map files are either not included in web-resource at all, or they are in the same web-resource as the associated js file.   Chrome silently swallows these errors but other browsers report them as failures.    So far we have observed the behavior:   - if the sourcemap is not in the web-resource, browser gets 404   - if the sourcemap is there, browser gets 503    Currently known culprits:   - auiplugin includes source map URLs in it's static resources and adds .js.map to each web-resource   - Jira dev status plugin    Example output from Firefox console:      h3.   Note:    This is copied from CONFSRVDEV-11378 which leverages the same AUI framework as Jira."""

summary: "Update the EventPublisher to execute the UserDeletedEvent only once when a user is deleted"
description: """h4. Problem Summary    The Jira EventPublisher service continually evaluates whether a user was deleted or not upon synchronization with Crowd. If a third party plugin is listening for these events and logging them in a table or log, then this can result in a huge number of entries being recorded.  h4. Problem Scenario    Here are the conditions by which we were able to trigger the UserDeletedEvent being repetitively published for any disabled user in Jira:   * Within Crowd:   ** Add a user to a local Crowd directory.   ** Add the local Crowd directory to a Jira application configuration.   * Perform a sync between Crowd and Jira.   * This user now appears in Jira listed under Crowd directory.   * Assign this user to an issue. If the user is not assigned to anything and doesn't have any content, this problem doesn't appear to occur.   * Next, deactivate the user in Crowd   * Again, sync Jira with Crowd.   * The user appears in Jira as inactive.   * In Crowd, remove the Crowd internal directory from the Jira application configuration.   sync with Jira   * Again, sync Jira with Crowd.   * The user does not display in the Jira user listing though content still references his (inactive) name.   * After the sync, one UserDeletedEvent entry appears in the _Customfield Editor for Jira_ plugin's __ AO event table.   * But then, each time a sync occurs with Crowd, the Jira EventPublisher executes the UserDeletedEvent event and the _Customfield Editor for Jira_ listens for and reads this event and records it into its AO table. Over time, this table will fill up quickly.    This behavior affects not just the _Customfield Editor for Jira_ plugin, but any plugin listening to the UserDeletedEvent event executed by the EventPublisher.    h4. Possible Workarounds  * If the plugin has the option to disable it's event logging, do so and only enable the event log when needed to gather troubleshooting information for a specific problem.  * Another work around option might be to periodically purge old records from the AO event log table, if used."""
comments: "Thanks Steve, you explained it perfectly :)    I just wanted to outline why the Customfield Editor for Jira app listens to the event. You can read all the details in our data storage documentation.    As app vendors our app and many other apps, has an implementation to listen to the UserDeletedEvent to cleanup app data of a user once he is deleted. This complies with EU GDPR ""the right to be forgotten"". And also more technically: You can set ""user permissions"" in the app, and they should get cleaned up once a user deleted.    The Event Log of the App is just a debugging feature and can be disabled completely as steve stated. Then events will not get logged to the event log table of the app. But the root cause is still present and the app tries to cleanup permissions on every user directory sync for these users. Other apps will surely also listen to this UserDeletedEvent and cleanup their user app data accordingly. This might bring a huge load to Jira every 3-5minutes when the user directory sync happen

summary: "Timezone changes in Jira is updating issues with 1 hour forwarded"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the Default time zone changes from IST (GMT+5:30(Kolkata)) to the time zone (GMT+4(Baku)) the issues' timestamps are getting updated with 1 hour forwarded. (We have also verified if there are any Day list saving changes and also by updating system time zone and Jira's time zone to the same one but still, the issue persists.)    (on) This issue is reproducible only when the timezone is set to GMT+4(Baku)).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Check an issue's """"updated time """" when the time zone is set to (GMT+5:30(Kolkata))   # Change the time zone from (GMT+5:30(Kolkata)) to the time zone (GMT+4(Baku)) by navigating to (Jira administration > System > """"Edit settings"""" under General configuration and internationalization update the timezone)    h3. Expected Results    The issue """"updated time"""" should move backward by 1hour 30 minutes as per the difference in time zone but it's moving only by 30minutes.  h3. Actual Results    The """"updated time"""" is getting updated with timestamp 1 hour forward.  h3. Workaround   * Change the timezone to (GMT+04:00)Dubai, Muscat, Tbilisi, Yerevan from the time zone (GMT+4(Baku), the timezone changes will work normally."""

summary: """Release Version"" should allow moving issues without sending email notifications"
description: """When releasing a version that has unresolved issues, Jira offers to move the issues to another version (see attached screenshot). This will trigger an email notifications for each ticket that is moved to the new version.    I’d like to request an option to prevent email notifications for this action, similar to the """"Send mail for this update"""" option for bulk operations.    I’d argue that the fact that an issue is moved to a later version due to the release of a version is not usually interesting to issue watchers, as nothing specific to the issue has changed other than that it wasn’t included in a release. An option to suppress notifications would therefore prevent many uninteresting emails and allow watchers to focus on actual changes.    !image-2020-05-11-15-17-56-635.png!"""

summary: "Unable to specify ""Archive"" permission via workflow properties"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It is not possible to set a workflow property to customise Archive Issue Permission. For example via:   * {{jira.permission.archive.denied = denied}} _or_   * {{jira.permission.archive.user = username_to_allow}}    *Root cause*    When the Archive issue feature was added, we did not add the appropriate entry within the Jira API Project Permission definition to allow the get """"Short Name"""" method of ProjectPermissions.java to resolve the ARCHIVE_ISSUES project permission    Therefore, calls to {{systemProjectPermissionKeyByShortName()}} (which is used by Workflow Properties to specify which project permission it is) does not know which permission to use for """"archive"""", therefore, we are not able to specify a workflow property to disallow archiving at a particular workflow step.    *Notes:*   * See ARCHIVE_ISSUES implemented in the project permission jira-components/jira-core/src/main/java/com/atlassian/jira/plugin/permission/ProjectPermissionModuleDescriptorImpl.java   * But not implemented on the API side here - jira-components/jira-api/src/main/java/com/atlassian/jira/permission/ProjectPermissions.java    h3. Steps to Reproduce    In a workflow step, specify a Workflow Property such as {{jira.permission.archive.denied = denied}}  h3. Expected Results    The workflow property takes effect on that particular step and disallows archiving  h3. Actual Results    No effect  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The Message-ID of new and update notifications for an issue is different with MySQL 5.6 and later"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Message-ID of new and update notifications for an issue is different with MySQL 5.6 and later  h5. A message-id of the new notification email:    h5. A message-id of the update notification email:      *1563869260000* becomes *1563869261000* .  it is a different 1000.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue. and check the message-id of the notification email   # Update the issue. and check the message-id of the notification email.    h3. Expected Results  The message-id is same    h3. Actual Results  The message-id is not the same    This issue happens with MySQL 5.6 and later.  MySQL 5.6 has fractional seconds support from MySQL 5.6  https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.6/en/fractional-seconds.html    Check the DB recode      The corresponding data is *1563869261* on the DB at the time it was registered to the DB.  We believe this is because the created column has no decimals and has been rounded to the nearest decimal point and carried forward.  When creating a message-ID, milliseconds are truncated, but when storing the original time in the DB, milliseconds are rounded up and registered in the DB.  At the time of update, because the date and time referenced from the DB are used, it becomes the one that is carried forward, so it is seen that this phenomenon occurs as a result of the difference of 1000.  The milliseconds are truncated to *499ms or less* , so it just so happens to work as before.    h3. Workaround  no workaround at this moment"""

summary: "Updating plugin in Data Center may fail on one or more nodes, but still report successful completion"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Updating a plugin in Data Center with several nodes will report back """"Success""""    But when looking in the logs for one node an error can be seen:  {code:java}  2020-05-05 15:48:23,322 http-nio-8080-exec-2368 ERROR      [o.a.c.c.C.[.[localhost].[/].[action]] Servlet.service() for servlet [action] in context with path [] threw exception2020-05-05 15:48:23,322 http-nio-8080-exec-2368 ERROR      [o.a.c.c.C.[.[localhost].[/].[action]] Servlet.service() for servlet [action] in context with path [] threw exceptioncom.atlassian.plugin.IllegalPluginStateException: This operation must occur before the plugin 'com.almworks.jira.structure' is uninstalled at com.atlassian.plugin.osgi.factory.OsgiPluginNotInstalledHelperBase.getClassLoader(OsgiPluginNotInstalledHelperBase.java:43) at com.atlassian.plugin.osgi.factory.OsgiPlugin.getClassLoader(OsgiPlugin.java:233)  {code}    This also leads to errors during execution of the plugin on the node.    It seems, that the success of the installation is only guaranteed for the node the update is done one.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Install plugin in older (not latest version) on Data Center with several nodes  * Update the plugin    h3. Expected Results  Success should mean, that the update has been successfully implemented on all nodes  Alternative: have an option to control and track the plugin installation/update on each node.    h3. Actual Results    The update might fail on one or more nodes with the below exception in the application-jira.log file:      h3. Workaround  A node restart should redeploy the app and fix the situation."""
