summary: "Ability to restrict field list in Bulk Update"
description: """As a Jira Administrator, I want to limit the fields that users are able to Bulk Update so that I can preserve data integrity of calculated, read-only, or other system-generated fields.    We have several system-generated fields that are """"read-only"""" for users, and others that we don't want users to be able to modify once an issue reaches a certain state. Bulk Update allows users to bypass the controls we have implemented and edit any field value. We would like the ability to restrict the list of fields available in the Bulk Update operation.    (Similar to JRACLOUD-34281)"""

summary: "Update page: Creating a test environment for Jira"
description: """The [Creating a test environment for Jira|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/creating-a-test-environment-for-jira-966063324.html] doc page should include the following 3 steps.  h2. 1) Between """"Replicate your database"""" and """"Replicate Jira"""":  {panel}  h3. 3. (Optional) Update Jira base URL on gadgets and issue contents    Text gadgets, issue descriptions and issue comments can have text that mention the production Jira URL. This could cause confusion as users would be directed to the production Jira upon clicking on the test Jira links.   The below MDL commands should replace the old URL for the new one:  {code:sql}  update gadgetuserpreference set userprefvalue = REPLACE(userprefvalue, '//prod.jira.base.url', '//test.jira.base.url') where userprefvalue like '%//prod.jira.base.url%';  {code}  {code:sql}  update jiraissue set description = REPLACE(description, '//prod.jira.base.url', '//test.jira.base.url') where description like '%//prod.jira.base.url%';  {code}  {code:sql}  update jiraaction set actionbody = REPLACE(actionbody, '//prod.jira.base.url', '//test.jira.base.url') where actiontype = 'comment' and actionbody like '%//prod.jira.base.url%';  {code}  Again, replacing {{prod}} and {{test.jira.base.url}} with the respective production and test Jira base URLs. Eg. jira.atlassian.com and jira-test.atlassian.com.  The updates above, with the {{replace}} function, were written for Postgres DB. You may need to tweak them to work on your own database type.  {panel}  h2. 2) Right after """"Start Jira in test environment""""  {panel}  h3. 7. Update webhooks    So that the Test environment doesn't trigger production webhooks, they should be edited in the new environment to be either disabled or changed to Test webhooks.   Please refer to the [Managing webhooks|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/managing-webhooks-776636231.html] documentation on how to accomplish that.  h3. 10. (Optional) Change the look and feel    It's a good practice to change the look and feel of the Test instance to hav a different logo or color scheme than Production. This will help users to distinguish between the two environments and don't rely solely on the URL.   Refer to our [Configuring the look and feel of your Jira applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-the-look-and-feel-of-your-jira-applications-938846975.html] documentation for guidance.  {panel}  h2. 3) On the top panel with links/table-of-contents  {panel}  The suggested license to apply to a non-prod environment is a [Developer License|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/get-a-developer-license-for-jira-server-744526918.html], not an Evaluation License. The customer should only go for an Evaluation License if they don't have a Developer License tied to their main license.  {panel}       Here's how the numbering will show up after the suggestions above:  {code:java}  1. Replicate your database  2. Remove production/abandoned nodes from the clusternode table  3. Update production URL on text gadgets  4. Replicate Jira  5. Manage shared home directory  6. Start Jira in test environment  7. Update webhooks  8. (Optional) Replicate external user management  9. Modify application links  10. (Optional) Change the look and feel  {code}  """
comments: "That page is also missing the advice to modify webhooks, so production webhooks don't get called.  And also to change the color of the header background on the test instance. That alone saves you more headaches than anything!" [SEP] "Great additions, [~mdoar2]! :D    We could add a step to [review webhooks|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/managing-webhooks-776636231.html] after starting Jira, as no webhooks would be fired automatically without user interaction.    Changing the [look and feel|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-the-look-and-feel-of-your-jira-applications-938846975.html] is a great tip! Thanks for reminding us!   It could also be done by the Admin after starting Jira.    I'll update the issue description with those points!    Cheers!" [SEP] "Fixed to Jira latest (8.9) and later"

summary: "REST API for statuses does not work if its name contains forward slash"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  REST API for statuses does not work if its name contains forward slash    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Perform a GET request using CURL, Postman or directly in the web browser to <JIRA Base URL>/rest/api/2/status/\{StatusName} , where status name contains the character """"/"""" For example: """"Peer review / change manager approval"""".    h3. Expected Results  According to documentation for the endpoint [Get Status|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.6.1/#api/2/status-getStatus], the status information should be retrieved.    h3. Actual Results  An error 400 is returned  !image-2020-05-05-21-44-37-244.png|thumbnail!     Example: Having the name """"Peer review / change manager approval"""", which encoded to URL is """"Peer%20review%20%2F%20change%20manager%20approval"""", the entire URL would be:       and this produces an error 400.    h3. Workaround  Use the ID instead of the name"""

summary: "Cluster monitoring does not work when node name has parenthesis  ""()"" character"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The clustering monitoring page does not work in case the node name has parenthesis / bracket """":"""" character hence not providing CPU/Memory/Uptime information.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy JIRA Datacenter 8.6.1  # In the cluster.properties file, add the node name (jira.node.id) as """"node1(1)""""    h3. Expected Results  * CPU/Memory/Uptime information to be displayed:    h3. Actual Results  * CPU/Memory/Uptime information *is not displayed*:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. description: """Issue:     Based on latest JSD functionalities  (Manage multiple issue at once) [https://confluence.atlassian.com/servicedeskserver/using-service-desk-queues-939926462.html] all SD users are able to perform the bulk change.    Suggestion:    To allow restrict the functionality to the administrator only/disable the feature"""

summary: "Increase visibility of Epic-Story associations in Kanban board"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Identifying which parent Epic a Story issue is associated with on a Kanban board is not always straightforward.  While you can add the Epic Label to the cards, it won't necessarily contain enough information in the truncated lozenge to identify the parent Epic sometimes.  It's the same if, by some chance, you have two Epics with the same name and color.    h3. Suggested Solution  One possible solution to get around this is to crop the cards and add the parent Epic issue link at the top; similar to Subtasks."""
comments: "I don't personally use sub-tasks because I hate the cropped card view, and I'd say about a third of my users have the same opinion.    However, I agree that the linked Epic could use better visibility.    In a board, 'Epic Link' currently displays Epic Name, which the label already shows. On a board with more than 3 or 4 columns, this makes both fields pretty much useless unless Epic names are kept very short.    It would be nice if ""Epic Link"" could be displayed as the Epic key instead, for both display size and disambiguation purposes.    It would be amazing if the key could be displayed as a link, perhaps even inside an appropriately colored lozenge.     "

summary: "Two Dimensional Filter with Epic Link and filter on issuetype generates large JQL query"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When *Two Dimensional Filter* is configured with *Epic Link* on an axis and using a filter that has *issuetype* the generated JQL includes an additional *issuetype* check.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create filter to include {{issuetype != Epic}}   # Add *Two Dimensional Filter* gadget and select *Epic Link* as one of the axis.   # Select the hyperlink for any *Epic Link* entry    h3. Expected Results    Filter created for test {{project = BST AND status not in (Resolved, Closed, Done) AND issuetype != Epic}}    The expected JQL created is expected to use the *filter* and add the additional search criteria of the second filter. In the case of selecting all issue with *Epic Link* BST-501 we expect the search query to be  {code:java}  project = BST AND status not in (Resolved, Closed, Done) AND issuetype != Epic AND """"Epic Link"""" = BST-501 {code}  h3. Actual Results    The JQL query generated repeats the *project* check and *issuetype* but using an *in* against the already *not in*.    The result of adding in the duplicated search criteria results in customer query exceeding 10,000 characters when they have a large number of issuetypes which fails their maximum URL length.  h3. Workaround    The only workaround is to not use *issuetype* in the filter if *Epic Link* is to be used as an axis in the gadget display.    In the case of the customer they wish to exclude *Epics* from the returned data so the workaround is invalid."""

summary: "Prompt to close Epic when all Stories are closed"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When all stories associated with an Epic are closed, you must take extra steps to close the parent Epic.      h3. Suggested Solution  While some architectures might want to continue using Epics the way they are designed, it would be more seamless in some cases if there was an option which enabled a prompt when you close the last Story in an Epic; similar to how closing the last Subtask of a Story works."""

summary: "Show repository name on pull request list on Jira issue"
description: """While viewing a Jira issue, there is a """"# pull requests"""" link (where """"#"""" is the number of pull requests). Clicking this opens a pop-up with the list of the pull requests.    However, these pull requests do not show which repository they have been made in. Since the pull request name will often be the same as the Jira issue name, they are often all identical. The only way to know which repository each pull request is for is to follow each link and look there.    I would love to have the repository name be listed along with the pull request name, id, etc.     !jira-issue-pull-request-list-no-repository-names.png|thumbnail!     """
comments: "{quote}  P.S. If this should be opened in the Jira Server project I can do that instead. I assumed that this functionality was provided by the Bitbucket integration tool.  {quote}    No worries [~6ad4a87a960a], I've moved the issue for you :-)"

summary: "Archived flag is incorrect in Webhook for Version Updated"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Archived flag is incorrect in Webhook for Version Updated    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Setup a webhook on 'Project related events' > 'Version' > 'Updated'.   # Go to Project settings > Versions, and *archive* the version.    h3. Expected Results  The webhook shows the information of the version, including the archived flag as """"true"""".    h3. Actual Results  The webhook shows the information of the version, including the archived flag as """"false"""",     When archiving:     It seems the information is retrieved from before the change was made, as unarchiving a version will trigger the webhook and the flag archived will be """"true"""" this time:    When unarchiving:     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

description: """Hi,  after a short discussion with Ben Magro we will open this request.    Our Customer Unicredit (their security department) is requesting the following:    """"There  should be the following log in Jira, if a Jira user sees on a Jira screen GDPR related user data from others (like full name or email)....  This should be independant from read, modiy or delete access….So log file should be like : ” user x has seen the following data from other users  on Jira screen by doing the following acitivity (read,modify,delete)."""" summary: "Notify Jira Admin if a Customer has unsubscribed from the Email Notification"
description: """Whenever a customer unsubscribes himself from a Jira notification it is difficult for a Jira admin to troubleshoot why the emails are not being sent to that customer from a Jira instance. A customer might have unsubscribed sometime back to avoid the noise and after a while when he wants to know the updates on the issue, its difficult to backtrack and find why this customer is not receiving any email notifications. This at the moment is possible only by running SQL queries on the backend.  If there is a UI feature that says which customer or agent has unsubscribed from emails from a particular email handler, it would save time in analyzing the complete flow.    """

summary: "Enable/Disable Users - Fine tune users from Active Directory/LDAP"
description: """I use Jira User Server connected to our Active Directory to pull in users for a specific department.  Our confluence instance uses the Jira connection to also grant access to Confluence.  This is handy so that user directories for Confluence and Jira are all just handled in Jira.      I'd like however, to be able to fine tune which users are enabled/disabled (counting toward license) locally from Jira instead of relying on IT Department to remove them from the Active Directory security group.  They have a practice, as most do I believe of just moving users who have left into an archived group so that ex-employees continue to take up license slots unless I get IT (which is really slow) to remove the AD security group.    My feature request is that, users imported into Jira User Server can be enabled/disabled from within Jira Administration and that the User Directory LDAP integration (Optionally) just be an authentication platform. This seems like it would be entirely possible, as Jira can also manage users locally."""

summary: "Having outdated Team Management plugin installed causes REST API to stop working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Having outdated Team Management plugin installed causes REST API to stop working    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install an outdated and unsupported version of Team Management plugin (component of Portfolio for Jira);  # Restart Jira;  # Note that REST API calls do not work;    h3. Expected Results  Nothing out of ordinary happens and Jira accuses the plugin as outdated;    h3. Actual Results  REST API endpoints stop working as intended and Jira loses functionality.    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:      The most relevant and common Method that is missing is *{{com.atlassian.application.api.PlatformApplication.getLicense()Lcom/atlassian/fugue/Option;}}*    h3. Workaround  # Navigate to {{$JIRA-HOME/plugins/installed-plugins}};  # Verify if *{{team-management-plugin-X.Y.Z.jar}}* exists;  # Verify if the version is *unsupported* for the running Jira version;  # Remove the plugin;  # Restart Jira;"""

summary: "Quick search of a text that contains _ when the indexing and user profile language is set to German not giving any results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    A quick search of a text that contains _ when the indexing and user profile language is set to German not giving any results  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to *Jira administration > System* > under """"*Import and Export""""* choose """"*External System Import""""*   # Import the attached *CSV* file with *description* and *summary*   # Navigate to *Jira administration > System* > under """"*General configuration*"""" clock on *""""Edit settings*"""" and set the language to *English*.   # In """"*Quick Search*"""" in top navigation bar type in """"*Export_ISP_BR*"""" when the indexing and User profile language is set to *English*, you will see the issue results   # Navigate to *Jira administration* > *System* > under """"*General configuration*"""" click on """"*Edit settings*"""" and set the language to *German*.   # Again in """"*Quick Search*"""" type in """"*Export_ISP_BR""""* when the indexing and User profile language is set to *German*, you will see no issue results and it shows """"*No issues were found to match your search*"""".    h3. Expected Results    The results should be the same no matter to which the profile language is set to.  h3. Actual Results   * When the language is set to English it's returning 3 issues.   * When language is set to German it's displaying that """"No issues were found to match your search""""    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior except changing the profile language to English. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The version of Apache Tomcat included with Jira Server is affected by CVE-2020-1935, CVE-2020-1938, CVE-2019-17569"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The recently disclosed vulnerabilities regarding Apache Tomcat    * [CVE-2020-1935|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-1935]  * [CVE-2019-17569|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2019-17569]  * [CVE-2020-1938|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name= CVE-2020-1938]      Which affects the following versions:        Apache Tomcat 8.x from 8.5.0 before 8.5.51    We should bundle a more recent version of Tomcat so that Jira is not affected by this in the future.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Not applicable.    h3. Expected Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Actual Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Workaround    * Manually upgrade Tomcat according to our [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-upgrade-apache-tomcat-version-used-by-jira-879957866.html]."""
comments: "This is an independent assessment and you should evaluate its applicability to your own IT environment.    CVSS v3 score: 4.8 => Medium severity    *Exploitability Metrics*  || Attack Vector | Network |  || Attack Complexity | High |  || Privileges Required | None |  || User Interaction | None |    *Scope Metric*  || Scope | Unchanged |    *Impact Metrics*  || Confidentiality | Low |  || Integrity | Low |  || Availability | None |    https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N"

summary: "Two Dimensional Filter Statistics gadget should display the results using the flter's column configuration."
description: """When you feed a filter to the Two Dimensional Filter Statistics gadget, the results are divided into sections, that will display the results found for each axis defined in the filter properties. As we can see in the screenshots below:     !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 04.31.04.png|thumbnail!      !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 04.24.56.png|thumbnail!     You can click on the number of results to display the complete list of issues found. However, when you click on these sections, the majority of them will not display the information using the *Columns* settings defined by the filter. Instead, most of the sections will display the *Column* configuration selected by the user, under the *My defaults* option.    Below we can see a screenshot of the gadget and all the circled sections will display the results while applying the user-configured *Column* configuration, where we would expect to see the same *Column* configuration displayed by the filter used on the gadget:     !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 03.53.15.png|thumbnail!     It would be beneficial if the Two Dimensional Filter Statistics gadget displayed the results applying the *Column* configuration of the filter fed to the gadget.    Expected results:    The *Column* configuration applied by the gadget is the same used by the filter.     !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 00.24.29.png|thumbnail!      !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 00.26.19.png|thumbnail!     Actual results:     !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 00.45.12.png|thumbnail!      !Screen Shot 2020-04-30 at 00.45.31.png|thumbnail!     The *Column* configuration applied is defined by the user preferences instead."""

summary: "Workflow Current Step Integrity Check dies when an issue has an invalid status"
description: """When the Workflow Current Step Integrity Checker encounters an Issue that has an invalid status (another issue) it dies with a Null pointer and don't solve it.   An issue can have an invalid status (one that does not exist for the workflow) if a Workflow Migration or Bulk Move died halfway through.  {panel:title=Workaround|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#3c78b5|titleBGColor=#3c78b5|bgColor=#e7f4fa}  This problem can be noticed when browsing/searching for issues, but finding the problematic issue often happens in the integrity checker   * After running the integrity checker you should have the issue ID of the problematic issue   * Go to that issue to view it   * Clone it: (More actions > Clone)   * Delete it: (More actions > Delete)   OR   * Change the Issue Type of the issue   * Change it back to its original Issue Type    (!) Note that comments are lost during the cloning operation.  {panel}"""
comments: "This is a regression of the fixed Bug JRASERVER-8326" [SEP] "I have the same problem server v8.5.1" [SEP] "Same issue in Server Version 8.11.0" [SEP] "Seeing this more frequently since moving to 8.11.1"

summary: "Update Best practices for IT teams using Jira Service Desk documentation"

summary: "About Jira page can be accessed anonymously"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  """"About Jira"""" page can be accessed anonymously. This can expose the Jira application versions. Some customers might want to prevent this information from being available as it could be used to target other vulnerabilities specific to the version.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Access <JIRA BASE URL>/secure/AboutPage.jspa anonymously    h3. Expected Results  The user gets redirected to log in    h3. Actual Results  The page is shown:   !image-2020-04-29-16-59-12-723.png|thumbnail!     Notes  This happens even if the public access is blocked using a flag in the dark features     h3. Workaround  Create a block the URL from being accessed from the proxy side. Another option is doing it from Apache Tomcat: [How to block access to a specific URL at Tomcat|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-block-access-to-a-specific-url-at-tomcat-966668691.html]"""
comments: "BTW, also, the platform version can be found in footer, in page.        Sometimes, it's pretty good to investigate the internal ip, and {{i-0e769c3a32bec2a19-10.104.240.82}}"

summary: "Error 500 viewing saved filter that searches for EMPTY in a Labels type field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When accessing a saved filter with a search like """"mylabels in(EMPTY)"""" where """"mylabels"""" is a Labels type custom field, although the search works and the user is able to save the filter, returning to it afterward will return a 500 error.    If this filter is used by an Agile Board, the filter can't be edited.    This can be an intermittent behavior.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new custom field of """"Labels"""" type and name it """"mylabels""""   # Do a search for """"mylabels in(EMPTY)""""   # Save it as a new filter   # Navigate to """"Manage Filters""""   # Click on the saved filter    h3. Expected Results    The filter and results are shown  h3. Actual Results    500 Error page with the following error is shown:   """"Sorry, we had some technical problems during your last operation.""""    The below exception is thrown in the """"atlassian-jira.log"""" file:       (!) It also happens when adding other values to the search """"mylabels in (test, otherlabel, EMPTY)""""   The problem does not happen when searching for """"mylabels is EMPTY""""   It also does not happen with the default """"labels"""" field  h3. Workaround    The filter can be accessed when it is displayed on the Favourites bar on the left.   To fix the filter do:   # Go to Manage Filter   # Search for the filter that can be viewed   # Add it to your favorites by adding a star to it   # Click on any other filter that can be viewed correctly.   # Click the name of the filter now showing on the left panel as a favorite.   # Remove the """"EMPTY"""" search and hit Enter to search.   # Save the filter"""

summary: "Inefficient full table scans when deleting from cwd_membership during directory syncs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    At present, user deletions during directory syncs do not utilize any indexes on the *cwd_membership* table, meaning each deletion requires in a _*full table scan*_. When the table is large the DB rapidly exhausts any in-memory buffers and has to constantly re-scan the table.    Examining the SQL logging underneath the sync, the deletion has three predicates on _membership_type_, _child_name_, and _directory_id_:    No existing index is suitable for this query. There is an index that _would_ _be_ suitable if the query utilized _lower_child_name_ instead:      list of all indexes for the table *cwd_membership*:      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Generate a large number of users and groups in local Jira directory or remote directory; ensure users have one or more group memberships   # If using a remote directory, perform a full sync and observe elapsed time for deletions   # If using a local directory, delete group memberships for a single user using the following query and benchmark the time:      h3. Expected Results    Deletion should utilize a covering index to be efficient.  h3. Actual Results    Deletion results in a full table scan. Here's a practical example from a customer with over 3 million rows in their cwd_membership table, and a deletion of a single user's group memberships that took over 7 seconds:  {code:none}  explain analyze delete from cwd_membership where membership_type='GROUP_USER' and child_name='<username>' and directory_id=10000;  QUERY PLAN  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------  Delete on cwd_membership (cost=0.00..103367.51 rows=86 width=6) (actual time=7311.263..7311.263 rows=0 loops=1)  -> Seq Scan on cwd_membership (cost=0.00..103367.51 rows=86 width=6) (actual time=755.912..7308.464 rows=95 loops=1)  Filter: (((membership_type)::text = 'GROUP_USER'::text) AND ((child_name)::text = '<username>'::text) AND (directory_id = '10000'::numeric))  Rows Removed by Filter: 3003405  Planning time: 0.155 ms  Execution time: 7311.305 ms  {code}  When deleting several users this drastically slows down sync time. A sync example from the same customer that took 243 seconds to delete 134 users' group memberships:  {code:none}   Caesium-1-3 INFO [c.a.crowd.directory.DbCachingRemoteChangeOperations] deleted [ 134 ] users in [ 243102ms ]  {code}  Adding a covering index drastically reduces these sync times, deleting 243 users' group memberships in 1.7 seconds:  {code:none}  Caesium-1-1 INFO [c.a.crowd.directory.DbCachingRemoteChangeOperations] deleted [ 243 ] users in [ 1724ms ]  {code}  h3. Workaround    Add a new composite indexing covering query predicates:  {code:sql}  create index idx_mem_child_dir on cwd_membership (membership_type,child_name,directory_id);{code}  With this the query planner will use an index scan, rather than a full table scan:  {code:none}  explain (analyze,buffers) delete from cwd_membership where membership_type='GROUP_USER' and child_name ='<username>' and directory_id =1;                                                                      QUERY PLAN  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------   Delete on cwd_membership  (cost=0.28..11.84 rows=3 width=6) (actual time=0.096..0.096 rows=0 loops=1)     Buffers: shared hit=9 dirtied=3     ->  Index Scan using idx_mem_child_dir on cwd_membership  (cost=0.28..11.84 rows=3 width=6) (actual time=0.016..0.019 rows=3 loops=1)           Index Cond: (((membership_type)::text = 'GROUP_USER'::text) AND ((child_name)::text = '<username>'::text) AND (directory_id = 1::numeric))           Buffers: shared hit=4   Planning time: 0.113 ms   Execution time: 0.114 ms  {code}"""
comments: """Generate a large number of users and groups "" - how many is large here? Isn't it the size of the cwd_membership table that matters?" [SEP] "Should this one be in JRASERVER since it looks like core DB stuff?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]: It is indeed the size of the *cwd_membership* table that matters; the easiest way to inflate that is to add a large number of users and add them to groups. You should see the effect at any scale (which can you can verify via explain timings), but it does become more pronounced and evident the larger the table becomes.    I though I had raised this as Jira Core; thanks for catching that! I'll move it now." [SEP] "I wonder if there is a similar workaround for the query that searches for groups  bq. 020-04-30 15:32:57,180-0700 CrowdUsnChangedCacheRefresher:thread-2 INFO ServiceRunner     [c.a.c.d.ldap.cache.UsnChangedCacheRefresher] found [ 32294 ] remote groups in [ 101477ms ]  "

summary: "Ability to rearrange the sprint based on priority of tickets"
description: """As a Product Owner when I plan the future sprint, I should be able to re-organize the sprint based on the priority of the tickets in a click so that my time is efficiently used.    *Suggestions:*    Sprint drop down may have an option to re-arrange by priority      """

summary: "As the owner of a Dashboard and filter, I would like to be able to select any user and group in the system.   "
description: """*Problem*   As the owner of a Dashboard and filter, I would like to be able to select any user and group in the system.         *Description:*   As the owner of a Dashboard and filter and respecting the 'Browse Users' global permission, I would like to be able to select one or more groups of users that can use that dashboard.   This request is not the same as proposed by JRASERVER-15900 as we do not want to engage a Jira Administrator for this task.  Any user that does have both global permissions (to share and to browse users) should be able to share for any user and group.         *Steps to Reproduce*   Have the following configuration:   * group1 - member is user1, user1 has """"Browse Users"""" and """"Create Shared Objects"""" global permission   * group2 - member is user2, user2 has """"Browse Users"""" and """"Create Shared Objects"""" global permission   * Add a Custom field of type """"Group Picker"""" on an issue type """"bug"""" just to confirm that the user is able to select a group that the user is not a member of.    *How to reproduce:*   * User1 creates an issue of type """"bug"""", selecting group2 for the Custom field """"Group Picker"""" (user1 has enough permission to select a group that he is not a member of);   * User1 creates a new dashboard;   * Grant editor permissions to group1;   * User1 is able to edit dashboard (expected);   * Select group to grant editor.         *Expected behavior*    User1 is able to select group2 from the dropdown list         *Observed behavior*    User1 can only see the group1 from the dropdown list, as user1 is not a member of group2.         *Workarounds:*    1. Add user1 also into the group2   Caveats:   a) Users will be added into groups that they do not belong to;   b) May face the Issue https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70932         2. Ask the Administrator to share the dashboard   Caveats:   a) Few Jira Administrators too manage all User's requests;   b) The Administrator would need to be added on all groups as issue https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-15900 was not solved yet;   c) After had added group1 and group2, may face the issue https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70932"""

summary: "User picker does not find users starting with a capital I with a dot (\u0130)"
description: """There appears to have been a change in how Jira treats accented characters in custom fields and user names in between versions 7.0 and 8.6, as the customer is reporting a regression issue.     If the user starts with the accented character, like """"Ípek"""", the user picker does not detect it at all.  However, the same was working fine in 7.0.    Workaround:  Jira 7.0 was using old Java version java 1.8_11.  When using the same old Java version 1.8_11 with Jira 8.6 instead of Java version 1.8_202, it is possible to select the user.  """

summary: "Custom field allowing use of images or icons as options to select vs words"
description: """Add the ability to checklist field, radio button field, or create a new custom field that allows the use of images or icons as the options to select.    This would allow users to select an image design, emoji, etc that correlates with an option better than the words describing the options."""
comments: "I intended to create this suggestion in the Jira Server project." [SEP] "No worries [~james_m_brown], I've moved it to the correct project for you.    Regards,    John van der Loo  Developer, Bitbucket Server"

summary: "As an admin I want Jira Data Center to automatically start a foreground reindex if no index exists and no snapshots are received after a fixed amount of time"
description: """h3. Problem definition  A Jira Data Center node should request and obtain an index snapshot during its startup sequence, in case it doesn't have an index.    However, nothing will be done in the event of no other nodes being available, or not having indexes of their own. This causes the node to not have indexes until an admin manually signs in and requests a full re-index.    h3. Suggested Solution  Jira should have a configurable option to start a full re-index operation if no indexes are available, and no snapshots were received after a fixed amount of time."""

summary: "As an admin I want Jira Data Center to expose a health check which fails if no indexes are available on the node"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  It is not desirable to have users accessing a node with corrupt or missing indexes, since any operations that rely on searches will not provide accurate results. Jira should expose a health check to allow for the load balancer to remove such nodes from rotation.    h3. Suggested Solution  The existing *{{/status}}* endpoint could return an error if indexes are missing or corrupt. This would allow the load balancer to know this node is not healthy and remove it from rotation.  """

summary: "As a Jira admin, I want to be able to control the notifications mobile app users receive"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In the Jira Server mobile app (IOS and Android), the mobile app has its own settings in terms of the event based notifications that end users receive.  These are separate from the project notifications schemes that are defined by a project administrator within Jira Server and Data Center editions.    h3. Suggested Solution  Have the mobile app honor the project notifications settings  OR  Provide some mechanism that allows Jira administrators to control the notifications that mobile app users receive about Jira.    h3. Why this is important  Several good reasons to do this:  # Mobile users will tend to get more and unexpected notifications than typically expected, and perhaps they do not realize they can control these from within the app itself  # Consistent messaging experience between platforms  # Administrators are expected to have control and manage what their end users see      h3. Workaround   Currently mobile app users will have to manually adjust their own notifications levels from within the mobile app."""

summary: "Allow deep linking in Jira advanced formatting markup"
description: """Currently, Jira only allows {{http/s}}, {{mailto}} and {{file}} URL schemes in Jira markup. It would be nice to have other arbitrary schemes to be available for deep linking from within the marked up text."""

summary: "Bulk move subtasks to new type without changing the parent issues"
description: """Currently, when we do bulk move for sub-task to another sub-task issue type we don't have the option to use  the same issue type selected and retaining the parent issues for each sub-task.         For example:    Here are the available Move -> To options shown, with each of the 15 issues showing it's parent link:    -Project - no change   -Issue Type - I want to Move Sub-Task To  Document(another sub-task issue type)   -Select Parent Issue: - this shows the parent issue for EACH sub-task    User select the """"Use the above project and issue type pair for all other combinations."""", but there is no way to """"retain"""" the existing parent issue.    All issues are moved to link to the single parent issue from the first sub-task.         Please have a feature to allow user to bulk move sub-task issue type without changing the parent issues          """

summary: "Request for WCAG 2.1 Compliance in Jira"
description: """Jira 8.8.x is compliant with WCAG 2.0 AA as mentioned in [Accessibility improvements in Jira|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/accessibility-improvements-in-jira-993923419.html].    I would like to request future releases of Jira to be compliant with WCAG 2.1 AA and future versions of WCAG."""

summary: "While editing a worklog, the seconds are being ignored by Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  User is trying to edit a worklog entry. The dialog box which opens when clicking on the edit button is ignoring the value in seconds and hence even if the dialog box is saved without editing anything, the time logged is changing.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Add a time spent value - 0.2525h to a ticket.  Screenshot1, Screenshot2  * Now click on the edit option from the worklog. The dialog box contains 'Time spent' as 15m. 15sec gets ignored by Jira.  Screenshot3  * Click on 'log' without changing anything. The logged time gets changed to 0.25h i.e, 15m.  Screenshot4    h3. Expected Results  Time spent value should have been pre-filled by 15.15m when clicked on edit worklog.  h3. Actual Results  Time spent value is ignoring the seconds and just has 15m while trying to edit the worklog.    h3. Workaround  Manually edit the Time spent to the correct value."""

summary: "Automatically close an Epic once all the Epic's children are done."
description: """As a Jira Software user, I want the feature to Automatically close an Epic once all the Epic's children are done.     Currently, this is only available by using 3rd party plugin.  Example:    [Close an Epic when all the Issues Under that Epic are Closed|https://library.adaptavist.com/entity/close-an-epic-when-all-the-issues-under-that-epic-are-closed]   """

summary: "Portfolio Filters and View Settings in URL"
description: """I'm frequently sending links to coworkers of the view I'm looking at in the Portfolio Roadmap view, but my filtering and view settings are not applied to their view when I send the link, so I have to guide them through how I got to the settings I'm using to look at it. It would be very nice if those settings were available in the address (e.g. PortfolioPlanView.jspa?id=1&sid=1*&Teams=none&groupBy=Team&etc=anotherSetting*)"""
comments: "Now that I've updated to the latest Portfolio, it seems like what I'm after could also be links to saved Views." [SEP] "...and now seeing that this just made it in to the May 5 update. This suggestion could close."

summary: "Add supportability statement for S3 as shared file system for Data Center"
description: """h4. Official Support Request  Currently, we do not have a clear statement of whether S3 is a supported file shared system for Data Center shared home.     We need to make this clear to ensure this is a viable option for administrators to consider.  h4. Official Documentation    If S3 is officially supported, provide clear official documentation on the setup.    """

summary: "Custom field optimizer should include sort and screens column"
description: """By default, the optimizer sorts custom fields based on the performance impact they have on my Jira instance. Would like to be able to sort each column except Action and also add the Screens column same as is currently implemented on the Custom Field list page. Adding the Screens column would speed up the decision making for adding specific contexts."""

summary: "Creating a Kanban board that exists outside of a specific project."
description: """As a UX Designer, I use Jira to manage tasks across multiple projects. Some of the Jira boards are created and managed by PMs who assign cards to me, other Jira boards are managed by me.     I want to have BOTH Kanban boards for those specific projects and an over arching Kanban board that has all of my projects AND I want them all on one page so that I don't have to jump in and out of multiple tabs in order to manage each board.     A perfect example of this would be Favro. [https://www.favro.com/]. I'd highly suggest doing a competitive audit for user interactions and UI."""

summary: "Adding worklog via rest api round offs seconds"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Customer is trying to add worklog via rest api. When trying to add the time using 'timespentseconds' the seconds are getting rounded off by Jira.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Add a worklog via rest api to add the timeSpentSeconds - 119.    eg.   {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}   curl -D- -u admin:admin -X POST --data """"{\""""comment\"""": \""""I did some work here again.\"""",\""""started\"""": \""""2020-09-09T13:00:15.761+0000\"""",\""""timeSpentSeconds\"""": 119}"""" -H """"Content-Type: application/json"""" [http://localhost:8840/jira840/rest/api/2/issue/AAA-45/worklog]      h3. Expected Results  The response will have 119 seconds or 1m 59s added. Also in the UI, only 1m was added.    h3. Actual Results  The response has 1m 60s added    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""
comments: "Also note, when trying to log less than 60 seconds using {{timeSpentSeconds}}, the server responds with an error:    Post to {{/rest/api/2/issue/…/worklog}}:      Respose: Status 400  "

summary: "Implement a 'service node' for Jira Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, it is not possible to configure a specific Jira Data Center node to process certain jobs such as mail handlers.     h3. Suggested Solution  Implement a feature to allow mail handler(s) to be assigned per node.    h3. Why this is important  Provide a better flexibility to the administrators in managing the data center clusters.     h3. Workaround   No workaround at this point of time."""

summary: "Support for multi-user fields as Portfolio custom fields"
description: """I'm using Portfolio to track resources, and in some cases there are some tickets that group together the work for multiple people, or the best I can say about an issue is that these 4 people are working on this. I would love to be able to have multiple assignees, but in the absence of being able to do that, having a separate multi-user-picker field to track this would be very helpful.    It looks like this isn't supported though, in my experience and in the docs ([https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiraportfolioserver/custom-fields-967895171.html]):    The following custom field types are supported:   * checkbox   * date picker   * +_*user picker (single user)*_+   * number   * radio button     * label   * single-choice select   * multiple-choice select   * single-line text   * URL _(read-only)_    I would love for this custom field type to be supported by Portfolio. Even better, if I could """"Group by..."""" this custom field and have it show me all of the items that each user is connected to in that field, even better."""

summary: "Parameterize the amount of Threads for web hook processing"
description: """h3. The scenario    Currently (as of Jira 7.13.13 and 8.8.0), Jira only works with a Thread pool of 3 Threads for webhook processing:    The [source code|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-webhooks-plugin/src/atlassian-webhooks-3.3.0/plugin/src/main/java/com/atlassian/webhooks/plugin/WebHookPublisherImpl.java]'s hardcoded with a 3 Thread pool size.  h3. The ask    We ask that web hooks Thread pool size be parameterize in _some way_, through jira-config.properties, for example. Default could remain 3.  h3. Related issue    If we have [JRASERVER-39361|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-39361] implemented with [logs around each webhook processing|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-39361?focusedCommentId=2397852&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-2397852], we could assertively determine if the Thread pool for web hooks should be increased or not.         Cheers"""

summary: "Wrong Links in Jira Server Installation Documentation"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Incorrecty links in the Database expand macro for MS SQL server in the instalation documentation for all versions:  - https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/installing-jira-applications-on-windows-938846835.html#    In the table under the section *""""Is your database set up and ready to use?""""* under the *tell me more* drop down, The Documentation has the following two incorrect links on the AND and  SQL Server text:  {quote}  Set up your database before you begin. Step-by-step guides are available for PostgreSQL, Oracle, MySQL 5.6, MySQL 5.7, [and|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/DOC/Database+Setup+For+MySQL] [SQL Server.|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/ADMINJIRASERVER086/Connecting+Jira+applications+to+SQL+Server+2012]  {quote}  The *""""and""""* is linked to the Confluence MySQL Database set documentation:  * https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/DOC/Database+Setup+For+MySQL  The SQL Server is linked to the SQL Server 2012 documentation (Noting 2012 is deprecated in 8.8 and later)     h3.NOTE:  We have already corrected the """"and"""" link and the  MS SQL Server link in the Jira 8.8 document version updating the SQL server version to MSSQL 2016 documentation    AND We have updated the """"and"""" link in the 8.7 documentation BUT the corrections still need to be made to the 8.6 and earlier docs    h3. Suggested Solution  At a minimum every doc version needs to be reviewed and have the incorrect """"AND"""" link to the confluence database setup documentation removed as well as the rest of the compatible DB version links checked and compared to the supported platforms documentation to verify the correct DB versions are listed    Overall adding in a individualized database version link for each database type, in each documentation version will become unmanageable as database versions become EOL and deprecated noting the incorrect link to the 2012 version previously listed in the 8.8 doc version that we have since corrected edits viewable in Page history    I recomend updating this to a single Link to the *[Connecting Jira applications to a database|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-a-database-938846850.html]*  documentation page that has the detailed  steps for each supported database type on the noted application version    EXE text:  {quote}  Set up your database before you begin. Step-by-step guides are available for your Database version in [Connecting Jira applications to a database|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-a-database-938846850.html]  {quote}  """

According to the documentation of [JIRA 8.5.3|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.5.3/#api/2/group-addUserToGroup] when the user or group does not exist, an error 404 should be returned.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Do a POST request to the endpoint /rest/api/2/group/user sending a body with an invalid use like this:    h3. Expected Results  As per documentation, """"STATUS 404 Returned if the requested group was not found or requested user was not found."""".    h3. Actual Results  A message 400 is returned:  !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Error serving file for path ... PDF when using IE"
description: """h3. summary: "Allow Extended Project Administrators to set resolution field on workflow transitions "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    At this time, Extended Project Administrators are able to edit the workflow in use by a project by adding new statuses and transitions; however, they are unable to adjust the various properties of the statuses and transitions.    It'd be beneficial to allow Extended Project Administrators to designate specific transitions and statuses to set resolution values in, without necessarily having to grant permissions to modify post-functions.    h3. Suggested Solution     Implement a configuration for specific transitions to allow resolution fields to be set by Extended Project Administrators     """

summary: "REST API for Add actor users returns success (200) even if the user doesn't exist"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  REST API for Add actor users returns success (200) even if the user doesn't exist. In the documentation for [JIRA 8.5.3|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.5.3/#api/2/project/{projectIdOrKey}/role-addActorUsers]  h3. Steps to Reproduce  Do a POST request to the endpoint /rest/api/2/project/{projectIdOrKey}/role/{id} sending a body with an invalid use like this:    h3. Expected Results  The documentation indicates """"The project must exist and the user must have permission to view it."""". If the user indicated in the body does not exist, the expectation is that there would be an error 404 as indicated in the documentation: """"STATUS 404 Returned if the actor could not be added to the project role"""".    h3. Actual Results  A message 200 (success) is returned.  The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:   !image-2020-04-21-23-02-54-851.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Ability to set null value in single list when clicking outside field"
description: """h3. Current behavior  When we click on a custom field (for example select list, single choice), then change the field content (scrolling through the list), when clicking then on the ticket outside the field area, the value is still at previous.    h2. Suggested resolution  We expect that the change in the field is null or none when clicking outside the field list during selection."""

summary: "Implement password policy when user resets password via the 'Forgot your password?' link"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, the password policy does not apply when users opt for the 'Forgot your password?' link which sends them an email to reset password. For example, users can re-use the existing password.     h3. Suggested Solution  Implement the password policy when user opt for the 'Forgot your password?'     h3. Why this is important  The password policy should be respected in all occasion    h3. Workaround   No workaround at the moment. Consider third-party app (add-on/plugin) that offers password policy. """

summary: "Requests to `/jira/rest/internal/2/user/mention/search` where the parameters do NOT include a query are very slow"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  We had an outage on a Jira instance running JSD 3.16.11 today which appeared to be caused by requests to the `/jira/rest/internal/2/user/mention/search` endpoint.     Looking at request times for different queries, those that do NOT contain a query string appear to be significantly slower.     Requests with a query string:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 3.57.16 PM.png|thumbnail!     Requests WITHOUT a query string:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 3.57.46 PM.png|thumbnail!     Note the scale change - the longest with a query string was 13s, the _shortest_ without was 28s, with longest over 60s     h3. Examples:  # With a query string, took 0.14s: {{/jira/rest/internal/2/user/mention/search?issueKey=KEY-5678&maxResults=10&query=il&_=1587494420052}}, (referrer is /jira/browse/KEY-5678)  # Without a query string, took 62s: {{/jira/rest/internal/2/user/mention/search?issueKey=KEY-1234&maxResults=10&_=1587407226218}} (referrer is /jira/browse/KEY-1234?filter=-1)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  I am not sure what this endpoint is - I went into the referrer (for a lot of them this was filter=-1) and clicked around but was not able to hit that endpoint with anything I did.     h3. Expected Results  Searches return promptly with no performance impact.     h3. Actual Results  This actually caused an outage for us due to a spike in CPU which seems connected to GC activity.     CPU spike:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 2.46.58 PM.png|thumbnail!     Unhealthy hosts:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 2.47.14 PM.png|thumbnail!     Increased request latency:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 2.47.32 PM.png|thumbnail!     New Relic showing that this endpoint is the slowest:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 1.53.54 PM.png|thumbnail!     CPU activity by thread showing that GC is the main consumer of CPU. NB: we got the threads long after the request returned/timed out - it is likely still processing in the back end at this point:   !Screen Shot 2020-04-20 at 4.29.43 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When adding a hyperlink, focus on the URL"
description: """When selecting text and adding a hyperlink to an issue or comment, I want the text to immediately focus on the URL, not the link text, since I will always want to change the URL (which is initially set as [http://example.com).|http://example.com%29./]         Screen recording: https://share.getcloudapp.com/wbu0eExp"""

summary: "Sharing dashboard with multiple groups caused users to lost ability to edit the dashboard"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users are unable to edit a dashboard if the dashboard is shared with multiple groups.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Have the following configuration:   * group1 - member is user1   * group2 - member is user2    How to reproduce:   # Create new dashboard   # Grant editor permissions to group1   # User1 is able to edit dashboard (expected)   # Grant editor permissions to group2 OR to user2 individually   # User2 is unable to edit dashboard (not expected). User2 is able to view the dashboard but the """"Edit dashboard"""", """"Add Gadget"""" and """"Edit layout"""" options are missing.    !Edit Dashboard missing.png|thumbnail!    It seems that only the group permissions takes precedence while any subsequent group/users are ignored.  h3. Expected Results    User 2 should be able to edit the dashboard, as the user is a member of """"group2"""" which has edit permissions.  h3. Actual Results    Members of the """"group2"""" group cannot edit the dashboard  h3. Workaround   # Grant edit permissions to a single group and make sure all the relevant users that need to edit the dashboard are part of this group   # Grant permissions to individual users manually."""
comments: "NOTE: we are experiencing this same bug, and I did have a little more information / detail to contribute.    In addition to the permissions not resolving correctly when multiple groups are selected, the problem seems to persist when you have a mix of individuals and groups in the edit list as well. For example, if you have 1 group and several individuals in the permissions list with edit permission, the group membership seems to take precedence and any individuals added by name are excluded. As with the problem statement above, the users who are individually mentioned to inherit ""view"" permissions though, even if they aren't explicitly given ""view"" permissions elsewhere."

summary: "Have option to manually set the resolution"
description: """Currently, The resolution time and date are set automatically based on when the ticket has gone into the resolution status. This can sometimes throw off reporting if we are going off the time the ticket was first resolved changes due to repeat reopening or errors in the database. We would like the option to manually change the resolution timestamp. """
comments: "The resolution field and date often fails to be set. To set manually, first, we need to add a post transaction to set it to resolved, then execute it via the workflow (a loop). Then, the resolution date is not correct, and we have no way to correct it, making metrics of no value. We cannot use 'days to resolution' to indicate security compliance without this fix, among other things. This is extremely important. " [SEP] "This is a bug. The resolution is not always set, and manual intervention needs to take place (explained above). This manual fix then sets the resolved date, but it's not accurate, so we need a way to fix the resolved date via the ui. Writing a script against the db is not an option. It's hosted. " [SEP] "Isn't this a bug?"

summary: "Allow Apps to be installed programmatically without human interaction"
description: """Currently, Jira (7.13 / 8.8) doesn't offer a safe & supported mechanism for programmatically installing or upgrading Atlassian Apps (JSW, JSD, Portfolio) or 3rd party Apps that could be used in an automation of some sort.    Alternatives vary from [copying the installed-plugins folder from a temp instance|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Answers-Developer-Questions/Deploying-plugins-bundled-as-OBR-automatically/qaq-p/524825] that's been manually installed/upgraded or unpacking jar/OBRs into that folder — yet each App has it's own particularities (like not all jars from within obr/dependencies should be copied, etc).     """

summary: " issue type will be moved to the new issue type and status will be set to ""closed"" but : the resolution field is not set."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Resolution is empty after moving an issue to status closed using the move feature.         Without a resolution set, the resolution date also is missing. In that case, the issue data is inconsistent and you are not able to run reports based on it.    From our point of view that allows unwanted behavior. Is there any way to prevent this or to force to set the resolution on moving issues, too?  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. open a ticket  2. select more -> move  3. you see the following screen and set """"new issue type"""" to an issue type that uses another workflow (see firstscreen.jpg)  4. click """"next""""  5. since the target workflow does not contain current status """"Einspielbereit (Prod)"""", Jira asks for the status to be set. If the user selects """"closed"""" as new status, the ticket is closed (see secondscreen.jpg)  6. click """"next""""  7. click """"next"""" again  => the issue type will be moved to the new issue type and status will be set to """"closed"""" but: the resolution field is not set. Expected Results  h3. Expected Results    The issue type will be moved to the new issue type and status will be set to """"closed""""  with the correct resolution state.  h3. Actual Results    Setting the resolution field is triggered in all our workflow transitions to target status """"closed"""" (see workflow1.jpg & resolveissuescreen.jpg). Obviously with """"move"""", the issue type and the status are set, but the workflow rules of the target issue type are ignored.  h3. Workaround    No workaround """
comments: "We see this happen about once a week in our Jira. An issue is moved from one project or type to another, changing the workflow. The target status would usually have a resolution set. This is not enforced during the Move operation. We have to check the results of this query periodically to catch this problem:    statusCategory  = Done  and resolution is empty"

summary: "SSO for Websudo"
description: """During a LDAPS outage today I noticed that with SSO on DC that users in the synced directories can sign into Jira if their directory had not dropped due to LDAP issues.  However WebSudo is still Jira Authentication. It would be good that when SSO is activated websudo is handled through the same system for password verification and then Jira handles the group permissions for access to the area."""

summary: "Date/time range estimate when creating tasks"
description: """It would be super nice to put in a time range for an estimate for tasks. Some teams often have tasks where it could be 2-5 days, 8-13 days, or various other date ranges for stories."""
comments: "Thanks for listening to the feedback.    As a team, we each individually provide our estimate to a given task. Instead of picking the median or the mode score as the estimate, we would instead like to set it as the range of all the estimates we provide.     For example, if two people estimate 2 days, one person estimates 3 days and another person estimates 7 days. We'd like to set the estimate on the Jira as 2-7 days."

summary: "Enhance Visibility of Filtered out Line Items in Portfolio for Jira"
description: """When a filter is applied to Portfolio for Jira, the line items filtered out in light gray are difficult to see, especially when sharing screen to others.     ex.    !image-2020-04-17-14-25-39-481.png!         The ability to add color to line items that have been filtered out would add the ability to easily see the hierarchy, and still know what is filtered.    ex.    !image-2020-04-17-14-27-25-367.png!  """

summary: "Add confirmation before keyboard shortcut action is taken."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When users search for issues, they can accidentally assign the issue to themselves using keyboard shortcuts amoung other hotkey tasks.     However, this behavior results in issues being assigned unexpectedly as no confirmation is prompted by the hotkey action.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Look at a list of issues   # Press the 'I' keyboard shortcut   # The issue will be assigned to you with no warning.     h3. Expected Results    Provide a prompt confirming that the user wants to assign the issue to themselves.   h3. Workaround    Disable the hotkeys with the following steps:   * Ensure you are logged in and open the [Keyboard Shortcuts dialog box|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver074/using-keyboard-shortcuts-921047262.html]    * At the bottom of the Keyboard Shortcuts dialog box, click Disable Keyboard Shortcuts    (?) You can also disable or re-enable keyboard shortcuts by editing the Preferences section of your user profile. See Managing your user profile for more information."""

summary: "As an administrator I want to have more flexibility when searching archived issues"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Searching archived issues only allows for the following filters:  * Archived date  * Archiver user  * Project  * Issue type  * Reporter    Therefore, it is not possible to perform a text search on the issue's comments, or to search the issue summary or any other field.    h3. Suggested Solution  Either implement the possibility of adding new columns to the search, or preferrably mimic the current JQL search capabilities as much as possible."""

summary: "Documentation on Jira Batch Notification"
description: """When a batch notification is enabled in a system, the user may not get the notification precisely at the time set i.e, at 2 mins,5 mins or 10 mins.  There will be a delay of ~5 mins sometimes depending on when the even was created in Jira.    Suppose an event is triggered at 6:37 PM and there is a job that runs every 5 mins building the batched notification starting at 6:00 PM, then this event will be picked up at 6:40 PM and sent after 10 mins. In addition to this, the job will wait for an additional minute for the mail queue service to complete. There could be delays in relaying the information on SMTP as well. So put together, the mail will actually reach the customer only around 6:53 PM while the user expects it by 6:47 PM.    We should document it within the Batch notification KB article on this behavior."""

summary: "Concurrent editing of the screen tabs can cause Jira to throw IndexOutOfBoundsException"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User is not able to add new fields to the tab because Jira returns this error:   !image-2020-04-15-15-59-02-982.png|thumbnail!    and in the log file, you can find *IndexOutOfBoundsException*. Full stack trace can be found in _Actual Results_ section.    This is because Jira uses the sequence number of a tab as an index in the ArrayList data structure. Furthermore, all actions on the *fieldscreentab* database table are not performed in a transaction or using a lock - the last operation always wins.    As a result, concurrent modifications of the tabs can corrupt their sequences number due to a race condition. If one of the operations is a delete operation, one of the tabs can stay with the old sequence number causing IndexOutOfBoundsException.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    The easiest way to reproduce the problem is by using a debugger with breakpoints.    This is one of the possible reproduction scenarios. Those steps imitate two users who are performing DELTE and UPDATE operation at the same time.    1. Set up the breakpoints in those three places:    2. Create 3 tabs   !image-2020-04-15-16-22-58-239.png|thumbnail!    Your database sequence numbers should look like that:   !image-2020-04-15-16-30-13-200.png|thumbnail!    3. In two different threads perform two different operations:   * remove *Tab0* and skip all actions in debugger until you will land in the *removeFieldScreenTab*() method   * then swap *Tab1* with *Tab2* and skip all actions in debugger until you will land in the   * *updateFieldScreenTab*() method   !image-2020-04-15-16-23-17-018.png|thumbnail!    4. Perform all operations in the thread which removed the *Tab0*   5. Perform all operations in the thread which swapped the tabs *Tab1* and *Tab2*    Your database sequences should look like this   !image-2020-04-15-16-23-41-423.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results    The actions completed without any errors   !image-2020-04-15-16-24-37-903.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    An error message """"The Jira server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation."""" is displayed:    !image-2020-04-15-15-59-02-982.png|thumbnail!    The below exception is thrown in the log file:    h3. Workaround    Swap already existed tabs and their sequence number should be recalculated.   OR   Follow the steps in this [KB|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/adding-a-field-to-a-screen-tab-throws-java-lang-indexoutofboundsexception-947179250.html]"""

Issue Summary    With 8.7, the default behavior of the *Enable HTML in custom field descriptions* and list values items option will now be switched to OFF for new Jira installations and the upgraded ones that have never used it. Please check the link below for details :   *    ** [Upgrade notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-7-x-upgrade-notes-]    With this option set to off by default, many systems filed description that contains HTML characters are represented with HTML code there than plain text       !Screenshot 2020-04-15 at 7.34.26 PM.png|thumbnail!   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to field configuration tab>time tracking   # You will see under description the HTML code appears    h3. summary: "Manually deleting HTTP Access Logs cause them to not be recreated before an application restart."

summary: "Changing the configured CDN URL does not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After changing/updating the """"CDN URL"""" property (Admin → System → Content Delivery Network) with a new URL, I noticed that some resources were still being requested by my browser which referenced the prior CDN URL, even with the browser's cache disabled or when using incognito windows. On further investigation, I found that these assets were being requested as downstream components by CSS and JS via URLs referencing the old CDN, even when loaded directly from Jira (bypassing new CDN). E.g.: when _batch.css_ is loaded from new CDN or directly from Jira, it contains references to static asset _adgs-icons.woff_ on old CDN.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Jira with a CDN URL for the Content Delivery Network feature   # Browse some pages (issues, dashboards, etc) on Jira to ensure static assets which reference the CDN are created and cached by Jira   # Configure Jira with a new, different CDN URL    h3. Expected Results    No requests should be made to load assets from the previously-configured CDN URL.  h3. Actual Results    Browse some pages (issues, dashboards, etc) and view network traffic via the web inspector tool; see that some resources are still requested from previously-configured CDN.    This can also be seen by directly viewing the files located at {{$JIRA_HOME/tmp/webresources}} which will contain said references to files on the old CDN.  h3. Workaround   # Change CDN URL to new/desired value   # *Immediately* delete the files located at {{$JIRA_HOME/tmp/webresources/*}}   # If any pages were loaded between steps 1 and 2, you'll _also_ need to invalidate those assets on the newly-configured CDN itself, as they will already have been retrieved and cached there as well."""

summary: "Ability to connect JIRA to delegated mailboxes (IMAP) without password"
description: """As per customer description:     {quote}  We have some mailboxes that users would like to use for incoming Jira issues. However the accounts have no password and are access via delegation in Exchange. Microsoft IMAP provides a way to specify this in the username string, eg. mainacct@acme.com\delegatedacct.  {quote}    And this is not something currently doable on JIRA, as it requires a password to work successfully."""
comments: "The *only* change necessary to enable this to work is to not change the username string in any way. Currently it mangles the \ character. A password is still used. For the username 'mainacct@acme.com\delegatedacct', the password will be supplied for mainacct, but then the mailbox for delegatedacct will be connected. This works in a straight IMAP connection such as Pine or Thunderbird, but Jira is not simply passing the username through as it should."

summary: "Field values automatically cleared due to unfilled required fields when creating issues from Admin mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some field values automatically clear when leaving a required field empty when creating issues from Admin mode, but when using the Create Issue pop-up window the behaviour does not occur.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Creating a new issue out of Admin mode pops up a Create Issue window, where unfilled required fields will be notified:  #* !createpopup.png|thumbnail!  # Creating a new issue from Admin mode (e.g. from Administration -> Issues) will not pop up a Create Issue window, instead, the process will go through 2 phases:  #* !createadmin.png|thumbnail!  #* !createerror.png|thumbnail!    # Step 1: Click """"Create Issue"""" so that you are navigated to a separate browser tab (not pop-up).  # Step 2: Fill the section with """"Linked Issues"""". At the same time, leave any required field as empty.  # Step 3: Click """"create"""".  # Step 4: Jira shows that some required field must be filled.      h3. Expected Results    The point is, other field values will be remained if already filled, including Epic Link, Sprint, and Linked Issues:  !createremain.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  JIRA clears field with linked issues. The point is, if required fields are unfilled (and the page refreshes), then specific fields will lose their filled value or even go missing. In the following screenshot, Epic Link, Sprint, and Linked Issues are losing their filled value (automatically cleared) while Remaining Estimate is missing:  !createcleared.png|thumbnail!    h3. Note    This problem is not reproduced while trying to create an issue from pop-up – in this case, JIRA saves all filled linked issues as expected.    h3. Workaround  * Make sure required fields are filled before clicking on Create to create a new issue  * Use the Create Issue pop-up window out of Admin mode"""
comments: "Hi All,    Just to sum it up, *Test Case*:   # Click ""Create Issue"" so that you are navigated to a separate browser tab (not pop-up).   # Fill section with ""Linked Issues"". At the same time, leave some required field as empty (like in situation when someone forgot to fill it).   # Click ""create"".   # JIRA shows that some required field must be filled.    *Actual Results:*  JIRA clears field with linked issues. And even if you then fill required field and proceed with creating a ticket, it will be created without any links.    *Expected Results:*  JIRA saves linked issues in the field as, for example, it saves other fields.    This problem is not reproduced while trying to create an issue from pop-up -- in this case JIRA saves all filled linked issues."

summary: "Confusing command in Dockerfile of official docker image"
description: """I found the following command in Dockerfile of official docker image (docker history).    {{chown -R root. ${JIRA_INSTALL_DIR}/}}    The dot after owner confuses me, I'm surprised that chown accept such input.    [GNU coreutils manual|https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/chown-invocation.html]:  {quote}Some older scripts may still use ‘{{.}}’ in place of the ‘{{:}}’ separator. POSIX 1003.1-2001 (see Standards conformance) does not require support for that, but for backward compatibility GNU {{chown}} supports ‘{{.}}’ so long as no ambiguity results. New scripts should avoid the use of ‘{{.}}’ because it is not portable, and because it has undesirable results if the entire owner‘{{.}}’group happens to identify a user whose name contains ‘{{.}}’.  {quote}  Please replace dot with colon, for sanity."""

summary: "Allow the usage of the EQUALS operator (=) with the Summary field."

Currently, the (*~*) operator, will provide partial matches, but that is not enough for certain operations, especially when combining Jira with the additional functionalities offered by Automation for Jira."""

summary: "Split the ""create shared objects"" global permission into ""create objects"" and ""share objects"""
description: """The current """"create shared objects"""" global permission controls the """"ability to share dashboards and filters with other users, groups, and roles"""". Those who do not have this permission will still be able to create and save dashboards and filters, they just won't be able to share them.    It's perfectly reasonable to expect that a software such a Jira would be able to provide a true read-only user experience, but this is not currently the case. There is no setting currently in Jira, global or project-level, that can restrict a user from creating objects (or exporting data, for that matter). This seems like an important oversight and something that has a very broad use case, including allowing for temporary visitors from other projects, or even auditors who must have view-only access to a live project but also must abide by very specific restrictions."""

summary: "Allow board administrators to set resolution on statuses even when not using simplified workflows"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When a Scrum or Kanban Board is using a [Simplified Workflow|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/using-the-simplified-workflow-938845286.html], there is a feature available where the administrator may configure specific statuses to automatically set a Resolution value upon an issue entering that status. The effect of this configuration is that the underlying Simplified Workflow will have a Post-function added to each transition that sets that Resolution automatically.    When a board is not configured with a Simplified Workflow, this feature is not available.    It would be convenient to board administrators of this feature could still be implemented with non-Simplified Workflows. Enabling the feature on a status could insert the post-function into every step that transitions into that status, as well as all future transitions introduced.    h3. Suggested Solution     Implement the same functionality to be available for non-Simplified Workflows.    h3. Workaround     Manually manage all workflow transitions to add the Post-function where needed."""

The issue makes it impossible to logout from Jira using a mobile interface, or get to the user settings screen.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Switch to a mobile Jira interface, and login into SSO-enabled Jira   # Open the sidebar and observe yourself not being logged in    h3. Expected Results    Mobile interface reflects user login status correctly.    h3. Actual Results    User is reported as not logged in.    h3. Workaround    The following workaround is currently available until this issue is fixed:  - Rename their username not to contain the @ symbol. I.e. user@example.com -> admin    To logout, users can ignore incorrect menu item and manually navigate to the logout page: /jira/secure/Logout!default.jspa"""
