summary: "Enabling analytics prevents Jira from shutting down cleanly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Atlassian Analytics plugin prevents Tomcat from cleanly shutting down, as it leaves """"Analytics"""" non-daemon threads that are not able to be stopped by the shutdown process.     This results in the message in the log always, regardless of the shutdown timeout:    *Impact*   If Tomcat is able to shutdown all threads except for the Analytics threads, there is no impact.    The concern is that this problem will mask an administrators ability to set the shutdown value correctly since Jira will never cleanly shutdown. If Jira terminated while shutting down but is actually doing some useful operation, there may be a risk that operation is terminated half way through. Therefore, we should always aim for a graceful shutdown. Also, it will delay the shutdown time as the script will always wait for the timeout value to be reached before existing.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On Jira 8.5.3, *Jira Admin -> System -> Analytics -> Enable*   # Edit {{JIRAINST/bin/stop-jira.sh}} and modify the shutdown timeout on following two lines to 300. This allows enough time for Tomcat to attempt a clean shutdown       # On the console, execute {{JIRAINST/bin/stop-jira.sh}}   # The script will wait 300 seconds, then eventually terminate due to stuck Analytics threads:      h3. Expected Results    Jira shuts down cleanly without having to be killed:    h3. Actual Results    The script will wait indefinitely for Tomcat to shutdown until the timeout value in stop-jira.sh has been reached. The script will then forcefully kill tomcat         {{org.apache.catalina.loader.WebappClassLoaderBase.clearReferencesThreads()}} will alert that Analytics threads to not stop, and a thread dump will be written to catalina.out showing the non-daemon Analytics threads:       h3. Workaround   # Disable Analytics in Jira Admin -> System -> Analytics -> Disable   # Modify the timeouts in stop-jira.sh to a reasonable value that gives Tomcat enough time to stop cleanly. This will vary depending on the environment and plugins installed       # Stop and start Jira to apply the change   # Stop Jira via ./stop-jira.sh and measure success    (i) Note that Analytics threads will hang around until the Jira service is restarted. So, to validate the change, please reboot at least once before checking for success     """

summary: "Opening a JQL search link that is from ""Detail view"", in a ""List view"" instance, open the first Issue of the search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Opening a JQL query that was created in """"Detail View"""", into an instance that is in the """"List view"""", opens just the first issue o the query results, instead of the query results.    This is issue is not happening from """"List View"""" to """"Detail View"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Issue search.   # Change the view to *""""Detail View""""*   # Create an advanced/basic JQL query (for example): *order by lastViewed DESC*   # Search the query.   # Copy and save the search link (for example): [http://localhost:8080/browse/TDC-7?jql=order%20by%20lastViewed%20DESC]   (i) Note that the *TDC-7* in the query is the first result of the JQL query.   # Change the view to *""""List View""""*.   # Paste the saved link in the URL and search.   # The first issue *(TDC-7)* view will open, instead of the """"*Detail View*"""".    h3. Expected Results    Open the *""""Detail View""""* instead of the First issue view.  h3. Actual Results    The first issue of the JQL query returns, instead of the """"*Detail View*"""". This issue is not happening if have more than   h3. Workaround    Click on the *""""Return to search""""* link (or type *'u'*) and switch back to *""""Detail View""""*."""

summary: "Deleting an Issue Does not Remove Associated Labels"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When deleting an issue, the issue it's data should be removed.  But labels do not get removed, leaving an orphaned entry in the labels table.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue and add a label.  In this example I'll add """"testlabel""""  # Delete this newly created issue    h3. Expected Results  The issue is deleted, as well as the """"testlabel"""" association.    h3. Actual Results  The issue is deleted, but an entry remains in the database:    * issue 10300 does not exist as it was deleted, but we still see this entry associating the """"testlabel"""" to this non-existent issue.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "So make sure you don't enter your password as a Jira label and expect deleting the issue to get rid of it :)" [SEP] "This bug is degrading our Jira performance. So we need this to be prioritized and take it to the next level. If needed, we are available for a meeting to show the impact of this issue.    We opened a ticket with the Atlassian and they recommended to delete the entries manually from the DB, but that is not an optimal solution for us to perform this now and then, we are having the user base of 10,000 - 15,000 users.     So please fix this as soon as possible."

summary: "Disable Keyboard Shortcuts on search results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When users search for issues, they can accidentally assign the issue to themselves using keyboard shortcuts.     However, this behavior results in issues being assigned unexpectedly.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Look at a list of issues   # Press the 'I' keyboard shortcut   # The issue will be assigned to you    h3. Expected Results    Disable keyboard shortcuts unless you are accessing an issue directly.   h3. Workaround    Disable the hotkeys with the following steps:   * Ensure you are logged in and open the [Keyboard Shortcuts dialog box|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver074/using-keyboard-shortcuts-921047262.html]    * At the bottom of the Keyboard Shortcuts dialog box, click Disable Keyboard Shortcuts    (?) You can also disable or re-enable keyboard shortcuts by editing the Preferences section of your user profile. See Managing your user profile for more information."""

summary: "Jira API should return an object that is completely immutable"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In a lot of cases, Jira’s Java API returns the object as a mutable object. Sometimes API returns it as immutable interface implementation, but due to dynamic-typing transformation in Groovy code, it can just bypass the interface.     This could lead to problems with data consistency when executing the Groovy code, for example: assigning a value to the _Issue Status_ (operator ‘=') rather than the equivalence operator (’==').   In other words, the end-user _can modify arbitrary Java memory object in the heap through Groovy code_.    h3. Suggested Solution  Modify Jira API for critical actions and return immutable objects to protect the application state.     h3. Some examples to illustrate the problem  An example of how Groovy can violate the API contract   * Given these two files:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  interface ImmutableCachedObject {  }    class CachedObject implements ImmutableCachedObject {      void changeObm() {          throw new UnsupportedOperationException();      }        static ImmutableCachedObject getObject() {          return new CachedObject();      }  }    ** When you run it, you get:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  Caught: java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: You broke Jira.  java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: You broke Jira.   at CachedObject.breakJira(CachedObjectImpl.java:3)   at test.test(test.groovy:6)   at test.run(test.groovy:9)    ** you get:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  org.codehaus.groovy.control.MultipleCompilationErrorsException: startup failed:  /private/tmp/aaa/test.groovy: 6: [Static type checking] - Cannot find matching method ImmutableCachedObject#breakJira(). Please check if the declared type is correct and if the method exists.   @ line 6, column 5.         CachedObject.getObject().breakJira()         ^    1 error  {code}   ** (/) The code wouldn’t run, because Groovy was intructed to respect API boundaries.      In case of Jira [IssueConstants|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/8.5.1/com/atlassian/jira/issue/IssueConstants.html] API doesn't have a setters.     h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "And also ask vendors who have plugins that let admins run groovy code to warn their customers? Docs I guess" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]  Good point!     On the same note, I see that _Innovalog_ team is doing good things from their side and trying to protect assigning operator, see https://innovalog.atlassian.net/browse/JMWE-844 "

summary: "Simplify add links"
description: """When working with issues in Jira you need to add a linked issue before being able to add a linked Confluence or Web Link.    This can be simplified by adding it as a default option to add a Confluence article or Web Link."""

summary: "Tables in plain text are not formatted properly if comment is added by a mail handler"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira does a good job formatting plain text tables when they're added as comments. For example, a SQL result set will look nicely formatted the same way it look in the console.     However, when exact same plain text in the comment is added via an email handler, it looks misformatted.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a SQL output as a comment to a Jira issue between noformat tags. For example:     # Verify the table is displayed nicely:   !comment_ui.png|thumbnail!   # Post the same comment via an email handler.     h3. Expected Results  The comment should show as if it was added through the UI.    h3. Actual Results  The plain text table doesn't display nicely. Instead, rows are split across two lines and there are extra spaces added that make the output misformatted:    !comment_email.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Cache replication failure results into issue with publishing the workflow"
description: """h3. Scenario:  - User tries to publish a workflow, however scenario fails.   - Observed below mentioned message before failure of this event:  _localq-stats-0 INFO [c.a.j.c.distribution.localq.LocalQCacheManager] [scheduled] Running cache replication queue stats for: 10 queues..._  - Updated jira.cache.replication.localq.queue.stats.logging.interval.seconds value to increase the time interval between cache replications  - This resolved the issue.     h3. Suggestion:   Can we have the cache synchronisation between the nodes not to affect the publishing of workflows?"""

summary: "Make it possible to sort the releases list via selecting the column"
description: """In Jira under the releases tab you see a list of releases for different components which becomes quite long and difficult to handle after a while. It would be much easier to handle if it would be possible to sort the list via selecting the column to sort the list by. This would be useful for both components and releases. We have more than 10 components and therefore a multiple number of releases in our project, so it is difficult to find the right releases to set to another status and keep the list tidy. If we do not tidy up here we have struggle when selecting the affected or fixed version - or is there any other solution to that wich we dont see?"""

summary: "Can we have a CSV validator in place when using  External System Import"
description: """Hello Product Team,    Currently, there is an option to validate a CSV file before the actual import starts when using """"Import from CSV""""  functionality which comes under Issues Tab (as part of JIM plugin)      But we do not have such feature available when using  """"External System Import"""" option (by going to settings ---> Back up and Restore -->  External System Import)    By not having this, the failure of there imports are known only after the import starts and we have to wait for it to finish. But with the validate option provided with Import from CSV functionality, we could validate the CSV for any potential failures even before the import begins.     Can we have the validate option for Admins who use the """"External System Import"""" option as well?     """
comments: "Yes! I typically run the standard ""Import from CSV"" to validate before I load as a Jira Admin, but it won't validate the fields I can only enter as a Jira Admin. This would be great!"

summary: "Issue types are sorted/ordered inconsistently in different places in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  There are many places where Jira users/project admins/admins can see the list of issues types used in their projects.     The order of issue types in this list is not consistent everywhere in Jira.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Drag and drop issue types in an issue type scheme and save.   # Go to the summary page of a project (<BASE_URL>/plugins/servlet/project-config/<project_key>/summary) using the issue type scheme just updated and note the order of issue types.   # Go to the issue type list of the project (when you click on *Issue types* in the project settings page or go to <BASE_URL>/plugins/servlet/project-config/<project_key>/issuetypes) and note the order of issue types.     h3. Expected Results  Order of issue types should be consistent in all screens in Jira so project admins can see how they're ordered without having to see the issue type scheme itself.     h3. Actual Results  * Issue types in the issue type scheme are ordered manually by admins. This is the source of truth.   * The same order set by the admins in the issue type scheme is visible in the issue type dropdown on tickets. This is the order that should matter to the users.   * In the project summary screen, issue types are ordered alphabetically.   * In the project's issue types screen, issue types are ordered based on an unknown logic. Though, subtask issue types will always show last.    h3. Workaround  Project admins may look at the issue type dropdown to see what the order looks like.   (!) However, the latest issue type used will be always be shown first, no matter what position it's in the issue type scheme. """

summary: "Internal links (anchors) behavior control to open in the same window"
description: """Some users prefer to keep the external links (websites) to be opened in a new tab however to keep the internal links or anchors within the same issue to open on the same window.    To clarify and reproduce:    1. Edit your user profile and set """"External links: Open in new tab"""".    2. Create an issue with the following description.    Link: [#xx]  Should point here: {anchor:xx}    3. Open the new issue.    4. Click the """"xx"""" link.    Expected behaviour:    The link should open in the current tab, because it is an internal link, not an external link.    Actual behaviour:    The link opens in a new tab. Also, it does not seem to scroll to the correct anchor, but that may depend on the Web browser.    Workaround:    Set """"External links: Open in current tab"""" in your user profile.    Notes:    Adding JRASERVER-9380 Jira feature provided the option to chose the links' behavior however some users would like to control the specific links type (internal or external).    """

summary: "Jira should permit the use of regex when searching for issues"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The search (basic and advanced) in Jira today is limited in regards to when to return issues.  If the terms are found at all in the records being searched, the issue is always returned.  This does not allow for more restrictive search queries, such as specific word orders.         h3. Suggested Solution  If Jira supported [regular expressions (regex)|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression] in JQL queries it would easily be possible to do this.        h3. Why this is important  This would provide a much more comprehensive means to search for issues in Jira. regex is already a popular means to search for records in many libraries.  Having this option would also be beneficial to various other kinds of requested searching of Jira issues, which in turn would make things like filter results, and the subsequent dashboards gadgets based on those filters much more powerful, flexible, and potentially concise in the information they can return to users.    h3. Workaround   The use of regular expressions (aka regex) is not currently possible to use in native Jira.  However there are 3rd party add-ons in Marketplace that make this possible today.  For example Scriptrunner has a number of these functions that accept regex queries in order to return issues in Jira. One example of this is [issueFieldMatch|https://scriptrunner.adaptavist.com/latest/jira/jql-functions.html#_issuefieldmatch]"""
comments: "One approach to this would be to allow Lucene queries to be passed in as searches. That would expose the whole set of Lucene queries to Jira. "

summary: "Disabling a plugin during the upgrade might not be enough for the upgrade to complete"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On the [upgrade documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/upgrading-jira-data-center-976167016.html] mentions that is possible to disable plugins prior to the upgrade, and after the upgrade is finished, upgrade to compatible plugin version.   This does not seem to work always.   There are some disabled plugins that fail to load during the upgrade, causing the upgrade to fail, some examples are """"Adaptavist ScriptRunner for JIRA"""" """"Insight"""", etc.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Disable plugins trough Manage Apps   # Proceed with upgrade   # Jira will fail to start with an error like this one:      And the plugin is currently disabled:  ||Name||Status||Version||Vendor||  |Adaptavist ScriptRunner for JIRA|disabled|5.4.45|Adaptavist.com Ltd|  h3. Expected Results    Upgrade to finish with success.  h3. Actual Results    Upgrade fails because plugins are failing to start.  h3. Workaround    # Back up and then delete the contents of the /plugin folder in Jira. This will delete all the plugins you have.   #*  a. on Server, go to <local-home-directory>/plugins.   #* b. on DC, go to <shared-home-directory>/plugins.   # After you've upgraded, install the compatible versions of your plugins."""

It shows 0 watchers and does not show the created when viewing all watchers. This was tested in Jira 8.0.0 and it functioned as expected. Customer found the issue in 8.4.1 and it was reproduced in a test instance of 8.4.2 and was not functioning as expected    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to your user profile page   # Update the autowatch preference to enable for all tickets   # Create a new ticket    h3. Expected Results    The user is added as a watcher    h3. Actual Results    No watcher is added    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    no logged messages    h3. Workaround    Plugin functionality may automate this process thus working around the behavior, but there is no confirmed plugin or script to workaround. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Project dropdown menu in basic issue search does not respond on Internet Explorer / Edge browsers"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Project dropdown menu in basic issue search does not respond on Internet Explorer / Edge browsers.    This is reproducible only on instance with larger number of projects (1000 in reproduction scenario).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Make sure to have many projects on the instance.  #* On a test instance, [Jira Data Generator|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview] can be used to create projects data automatically.  # Using IE / Edge browser, go to Issues > Search for issues > make sure the Basic search is enabled  # Select """"Project: All"""" dropdown button    h3. Expected Results  The list of projects opens right after clicking the dropdown field.    h3. Actual Results  The list of projects does not open for a long time (or doesn't open at all). After some time, the following warning appears in the browser:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   The browser becomes unresponsive. Closing and reopening the browser helps with this.    h3. Workaround  * Use Advanced search mode (JQL) and specify the search query  * Use other browser (e.g. Safari, Chrome, Firefox)    h3. Notes  The issue is not reproducible if the instance does not have many projects."""

summary: "When a project is imported, the ""Start date"" of Versions in the ""Releases"" page, does not get imported"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a project is imported, the """"Start date"""" of Versions in the """"Releases"""" page, does not get imported. Only the """"Release date"""" will get imported.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Version in a Software project.   # Set a """"Start date"""" & a """"Release date"""".   #* The status of the version does not matter here """"UNRELEASED"""" OR """"RELEASED""""   # Create an XML backup   # Delete the project from Jira   # Create a new empty project with the same Project key   # Import the project from the XML back using the """"Project Import"""" tool.   # Once the import is complete check the version in the """"Releases"""" page of the project.    h3. The """"Start date"""" isn't getting imported with the project.      *Before the import*    *  !Before.png|thumbnail!     *After the import*    *  !after.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    You can add the """"Start date"""" manually for each version after the import, but you will have to save/take a screenshot *before* the import in order to have the data in hand."""

summary: "AWS Cloud formation scripts to deploy Atlassian Standard Infrastructure (ASI) inside an existing VPC"
description: """Hi Team,    Customers would like to migrate existing Atlassian applications (Jira & Confluence) from one AWS account to another with pre-configured VPC as per their organizational standards.    Currently the supported Quick Start [Atlassian Standard Infrastructure|https://aws-quickstart.s3.amazonaws.com/quickstart-atlassian-services/doc/atlassian-standard-infrastructure-on-the-aws-cloud.pdf] includes an AWS CloudFormation template that builds out AWS infrastructure components, which are required by all Atlassian Data Center products which is inclusive of VPC.    Hence would like to leverage the ASI cloud formation scripts to work inside their existing VPC provided by their IT teams and are struggling to get them working. So the request is to have ASI cloud formation scripts which allow customers to deploy the ASI inside an existing VPC.    Please help users to meet this requirement. """

summary: "Disallow HTML in custom field descriptions and list item values by default"
description: """h3. Problem  By default, Jira allows HTML in custom field descriptions and list item values. The configuration item that prevents this is in *Jira Admin -> System -> Enable HTML in custom field descriptions and list item values*, and is is +Enabled+ by default.     h3. Justification  This introduces scope for values to break the page in exciting ways - for example, adding <!--  break the rest of the page when the field is loaded. Actually, on Field Configuration and Custom Fields pages, this prevents the ability to edit to undo the breakage (aside from editing the database)    Some customers require this, but, we should encourage it's disablement. Disabling it by default would help.       h3. Suggested Solution  Disable this option by default    from https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-7-x-upgrade-notes-987138245.html  bq. ... It will now be switched to OFF for new Jira installations and the upgraded ones that have never used it.  ... We recommend that you keep this option disabled for security reasons."""
comments: "But if you're going to make it disabled by default, there needs to be some docs on how to detect whether you do have fields with HTML in their descriptions, and show you a way to fix that. Otherwise some one's upgrade is going to be a right pain finding this out" [SEP] "Hey [~mdoar2]  Good question!   It will be switched to OFF for new Jira installations and the upgraded ones that have never used it. So in case you describe it nothing will change. " [SEP] "Hmm, looks like some Jira system fields use HTML in their descriptions JRASERVER-70909  It would be helpful to show people how to find whether their custom fields have HTML in them before choosing to turn this on. A DB query might be a good start. The same query could be used to find any other system fields than Time Tracking that have this problem."

description: """JIRA Password Policy to be compliant with NIST 800-63  - https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-3/sp800-63b.html"""
comments: "Based on [https://github.com/UGPP/Ultimate-Global-Password-Policy/blob/master/Draft%20%231] a good Jira password policy (based on NIST 800-63 and some experiences) would currently be:    *Require:*   Has at least a minimum length (minimum should be at least 8 characters - make this one customer configurable, but at least 8)   Has at most a maximum length (*maximum should be +at least 64+ characters -* make this one customer configurable, but at least 64. Max it out at 1024 or so.)   * Allow at least 64 characters in length to support the use of passphrases. *Encourage users to make memorized secrets as lengthy as they want, using any characters they like (including spaces), thus aiding memorization.*   * *Do not impose other composition rules (e.g. mixtures of different character types) on memorized secrets. So drop the ' must use special, capital, lower, etc. ' rules or even better make this one customer configurable on the length. So for instance: passwords less than 12 chars (XX chars) do require* *c

summary: "VersionManager.createVersion does not check for existing versions with the same name before creating a new one"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a project version, The Java API method [VersionManager.createVersion|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/latest/com/atlassian/jira/project/version/VersionManager.html#createVersion-java.lang.String-java.util.Date-java.util.Date-java.lang.String-java.lang.Long-java.lang.Long-boolean-] does not check if there are versions with the same name in the project. This may result in duplicate version names and can cause bigger issues.     The method should now allow the creation of duplicate versions, especially that the JIRA REST API enforces uniqueness on versions names per project.     Since this seems to be an omission, it's logged as a bug.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Use the method in a Groovy script to create a project version with the same name as an existing one.   * Groovy scripts can be executed in Jira via the ScirptRunner app.   * Here's a Groovy script to duplicate all versions:       h3. Expected Results  Jira shouldn't allow duplicate project versions to created and the script should exit with an exception.     h3. Actual Results  Duplicate project versions are created.     h3. Workaround  Third-party app developers should program defensively and check for the existence of versions with the same name before creating new ones.   """

summary: "Shared filters are not working when the JQL contains decimal numbers and user profile set other than English"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When there are shared filters created when the user profile is set to """"English"""" if the others are using it whose profile language other than """"English"""" the """"JQL"""" is getting not getting translated when there are decimal numbers present in JQL and the """"shared filter"""" is not working as the decimals in JQL is not getting translated.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field with a name in the """"French"""" language of the """"number"""" type.   !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 4.18.46 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Give the custom field some """"Decimal"""" values.   # Create a filter with some """"JQL"""" like below, save the filter when the user profile is set to """"English"""" and share it with a group.   # Log in as a different user whose profile is set to a different language other than """"English"""" and try to access the """"shared filter"""".    h3. Expected Results    The """"JQL"""" saved in the """"shared filter"""" should be translated to the user's profile language and results should be displayed like below:   !Screenshot 2020-03-31 at 7.03.30 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    The below error is thrown if we use the same filter when the user profile is set to """"French"""", but the user is expecting it to be working despite the user's profile language.   !Screenshot 2020-03-31 at 7.07.04 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    As a workaround manually edit the decimal numbers in double-quotes with a comma(,) separator instead of (.) which will give results.    !Screenshot 2020-03-31 at 7.48.30 PM.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Audit Log for Deleted Custom Fields should reference the field ID"

summary: "As a scrum master, I want to prompt users for additional information when modifying the scope of an in progress sprint"

summary: "Attaching an image using the ""What you see what you get"" panel in the comment section does not update the file name correctly in the comment"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attaching a image using the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section does not update the file name attached.   Example:  Upload image001.png then check out the text mode in the comment, it points to the correct image which is image001.png, but when uploading another image with the same name image001.png the image uploads correctly with the new name image001-1.png but the comment does not get updated and remains with the original name of the image which is image001.png which is an incorrect behaviour as the comment should be updated to include the new name of the picture which is image001-1.png.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach picture attachment in the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section (Example image001.png)   # Post the comment    # Check the comment in """"Text"""" Mode, it should be      # Now create a new comment and upload another picture with the same name (image001.png) from the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section.    # The image is uploaded with the name (image001-1.png), which is the correct behaviour as this was fixed in Jira 8.6 and above (https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-2169)   # Check out the comment in """"Text"""" Mode, it shows  which is incorrect.    h3. Expected Results  When checking out the comment in text mode after uploading the second image, the comment should be  to show the correct picture attached.  h3. Actual Results  When checking out the comment in text mode after uploading the second image, the comment shows  which is incorrect because it shows the first picture attached not the second picture.    h3. Workaround    Currently, the workaround for this issue is to use the """"Drag and Drop"""" feature to add the image then *choose* the image from the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section.    OR     To use the """"Browse"""" feature from the attachment Section in the ticket then *choose* the image from the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section.    (+) You could also manually edit the image name from the comment in text mode.    Note that you can find both those features from the section shown in the picture below:    *  !Attachment.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Deleting a user from LDAP or Crowd server will not delete this user from the projectroleactor table"
description: """h3. Summary:    When a user is deleted from an external directory (LDAP or external Crowd server), references to this user will retain in the {{projectroleactor}} table.  h3. Steps to Reproduce:   # Configure an external directory in Jira via the page *⚙ > User Management > User Directories* (with an LDAP or Crowd server)   # Add a user to the LDAP (or Crowd) server (username: chibi6)   # Sync Jira with the LDAP (or Crowd) server   # Add that user to a Project Role in any Jira project   # Delete the user from the LDAP (or Crowd) server   # Sync again Jira with the LDAP (or Crowd) server   # Go to the *⚙ > User Management > Users* page, and verify that the user is no longer there   # Go to the Project Role page and verify that the user is not displayed there:  !Screenshot1.png|thumbnail!   # Run the following SQL query and verify that the user has been deleted from the {{cwd_user}} table    !Screenshot2.png|thumbnail!   # Run the following SQL query:      h3. Expected Results:    The user is deleted from the {{projectroleactor}} table.  h3. Actual Results:    The user is not deleted from this table, even though the user is not showing in the Project Role in the UI, causing some data discrepancy in the database:  !Screenshot3.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    The only workaround to avoid this discrepancy is to manually delete the rows from the {{projectroleactor}} table for the user(s) that have been deleted from the LDAP (or Crowd) server.    (!) If you decide to go ahead with this workaround, make sure to create a native backup of your Jira Database, so that you can revert the changes if anything goes wrong.  h3. Notes    *Note 1*   This bug does not occur when a user is deleted from the Jira Internal Directory. In this case, when the user is deleted from this directory via the page *⚙ > User Management > Users* (or from the Jira API via the end point {{api/2/user}}), the user is properly deleted from the {{projectroleactor}} table.    *Note 2*    This bug was reproduced in Jira versions 8.4.2 and 8.7.1, but it is likely that this bug has been there for a while in older versions (such as 7.x)"""

It shows """"No Matches"""" in the drop down.    !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 2.14.55 PM.png|width=548,height=205!    In that case, I was able to perform an advanced search and select the value, but when switching back to basic search, the value was not supported.    !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 2.13.05 PM.png|width=640,height=171!    !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 2.03.27 PM.png|width=636,height=157!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a custom field, use it in different context.   * Try to perform a basic search, using it as criteria along with the correct issue type.    h3. Expected Results    You should be able to to select that field's options.  h3. summary: "IN operator of JQL should allow its parenthesis to include functions and normal field values"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Putting some functions defined in [Advanced searching \- functions reference|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/advanced-searching-functions-reference-939937722.html] and field value in same IN () parenthesis doesn't return both result as it doesn't query with OR condition.       For example, the following JQL will return all projects' tickets that is lead by the searcher.        Meanwhile putting TEST project in the IN() parenthesis is expected to return all projects' tickets that searcher leads + TEST project.    But it actually returns only the tickets in TEST project.      You have to tweak the JQL as below to return the expected ones.      This isn't intuitive as IN() operator is usually expected to search with OR condition.       I've observed this behavior at least on   * {{projectsLeadByUser()}}   * {{projectsWhereUserHasPermission()}}   * {{projectsWhereUserHasRole()}}        h3. Suggested Solution    Allow IN operator to handle functions and field value in same parenthesis to search with OR condition. """

summary: "As a user I want to use the operator 'IS' with the field 'issueLinkType' so I can filter out issues without links"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The field *issueLinkType* does not support the operator *IS*, which makes it impossible to write a JQL query to exclude a specific issue link type from its results. That occurs because issues without links will also be matched, and there's no way to specify a null value for *issueLinkType*.    For example, when running the JQL query below:      All issues having an *issueLinkType* different than *blocks* will be returned, *including issues without any links.*    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement support for the operator *IS* on the field *issueLinkType*. That would allow the JQL query above to be rewritten as:      Which would return all issues in project SCRUM, having issuetype *Story*, having at least one issue link type different than *blocks*, and having a *status* different than *Done*.    h3. Workaround   One can create a special saved filter containing all currently available issue link types and use nested filters.    The downside is that any modifications to issue link types, such as adding a new one, or renaming/deleting existing ones, would break this functionality until the filter is updated.    {code:title=Filter named """"All issue link types""""}  issueLinkType in (""""is blocked by"""", blocks, """"is cloned by"""", """"is duplicated by"""", """"is caused by"""", """"relates to""""    project in (SCRUM) AND issuetype = story AND issueLinkType != blocks AND filter = """"All issue link types"""" AND status not in (Done)  {code}  """

summary: "Ability to purge RunDetails of Scheduler "
description: """In current implementation of the Atlassian Scheduler, the purge of old RunDetails is performed after each new execution of a Job.    It responds to most of usage case, when a job is concerned by a schedule.    But others usage cases may require the ability to purge RunDetails given to users or plugins :   * Case of RunDetails created by old versions of plugin or uninstalled plugins   * Case of RunDetails created by many occurrence Jobs in a plugin (My current case)     I am currently preparing a plugin where many instance of Jobs can be created, where I use a JobId based on a <FeatureKey>:<FeatureId>. I use the Job in order to benefit from the Asynchronous mode in most of case, and also (in more rare case) from Scheduler.    Currently a purge method - _*purgeHistory(Date before)*_ - is defined in OfBizRunDetailsDao, but it is not accessible.    Thanks     V.          """

summary: "provide the possibility to create users by sending an e-mail to Jira mail handler with the username not equal to the e-mail address"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Currently Jira allow to create user which are sending an e-mail to the configured mail handler and the newly created Jira user accounts will be the *email addresses* specified in the *From*: fields of these received messages.    Please provide the possibility to have the newly user created username equal to the mail address name propending to the mail server domain.    Example:  _e-mail address_: user@atlassian.com  _user created_: user  """
comments: "The suggestion is of me. I meant the configuration with LDAP Authentication and Internal Directory, where internal users are authenticated by Active Directory via LDAP and external users via Internal Directory.     In today situation, the user created from email has a name like username@example.com. If the same user will log-in in portal with Active Directory credentials, a new user with name username (without ""@example.com"") will be created. Such a user is a new user and issues referenced to username@example.com, will not be referenced to him/here.     I propose, that Jira should firstly check, if the new email address is an address from Active Directory and, if yes, create new user with a name from Active Directory. In the case, if the same user will later log-in in portal with Active Directory credentials, the issues, which where already referenced to his name, will be further visible and usable for the user.     "

summary: "Allow for Confluence users have a view-only permission to view Jira tickets in Confluence while not allowed to access Jira"

summary: "Jira serializes ""\r"" return characters into multi-line text custom fields, inflating the character count"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When reading in multi-line text field data from the database to display when viewing an issue, Jira serializes """"\r"""" carriage returns into each line, artificially inflating the character count. If the field content is close to or meets the character limit, you will be unable to make any edits to the issue in the UI; the validator will throw an error indicating that the text entered for that custom field is too long.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new multi-line text custom field and add it field scheme of test project   # Create a new test issue   # Import the attached repro1.txt file via REST. You can copy and paste the Curl command from attached curl1.txt, substitute username, custom field ID, and issue key as needed.   # View the newly-created record in the *customfieldvalue* table and check the character count:       # Attempt to edit the issue in the UI, note the validation error indicating the field data is too long.   # Create another test issue   # Import the attached repro2.txt file via REST. You can copy and paste the Curl command from attached curl2.txt, substitute username, custom field ID, and issue key as needed. This is a much smaller file to demonstrate the injection of the carriage returns to the data.   # Check the record and character count:       # Navigate to the issue in the UI and edit the text in the the custom field to append a final """"."""" character.   # Check the record and character count again    h3. Expected Results    In the first test, Jira should allow you to save changes to the issue as the custom field data is at the limit.    In the second test, the character count of the field should only increment by 1.  h3. Actual Results    In the first test, Jira serializes the """"\r"""" characters into the field data, causing it to exceed the character count.    In the second test, you can see this character injection when reviewing the data in the customfield. The character count has grown more than the expected single character and you can now see the """"\r"""" characters injected into the data.  h3. Workaround    There has been no workaround identified at this time."""

Expected Results    In confirmation screen, field name should display """"Object of Senior Manager's review""""  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "When adding attachments (in JSD queue view), Jira automatically increments + appends a number suffix even if the file does not exists"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When adding attachments in JSD, JSD automatically increments + appends a number suffix even if the file does not exists    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Jira 8.6.0/JSD 4.6.0   # Browse > select files, in the """"Add attachment"""" pop up, cancel the the upload   # Do the same thing again, observe that the number suffix is added, it is also incremented if this is operation is done repeatedly.   # Try creating a new issue, perform the same steps above and observe the number suffix is added, even if the attachments do not exist on the new issue.    Video clip demonstration: [^Recording #4.mp4]     Additional note:  * Once the above happens, try attaching the same files in another Business or Software type project, the number suffix will also be automatically added, even on a different project type/issue.    h3. Expected Results  Jira should not append the number suffix, since the attachment with the same name does not exists.  h3. Actual Results  Jira appends a number suffix, even on a new issue without any attachments    h3. Workaround  Disable {{jira.attachments.generate.unique.suffix.enabled}} feature flag, however doing this will cause the instance will be affected this bug: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-2169"""
comments: "The impact of this is if you have Canned Responses that include links to documents, the links will not work and the customer won't get the documents unless the agent edits each link individually with the different file name for each document." [SEP] "Also worth mentioning that it also affects Jira Software projects, not just Jira Service Desk" [SEP] "Hi,    First of all, this is a bug in Jira, not JSD. JSD fix (JSDSERVER-5054) only applies to attachments from comments and requests created via customer portal and emails. The behaviour described here is relevant for agent view e.g. Jira itself only.    Second of all, this behaviour could be disabled in Jira by disabling the _jira.attachments.generate.unique.suffix.enabled_ application property. *Be advised that disabling this will make you affected by JRASERVER-2169*.    Correspondingly, for JSD (customer portal and emails) the _sd.attachment.generate.unique.suffix.when.attachment.filename.clash.occurs_ feature flag is used to control the similar behaviour. *B

" [SEP] "if in case we open Jira from outside network and this information being show to all the users, its a security issue. I am gathering more information from my security team. will share." summary: "Extend membersOf JQL function to work with Group picker fields"
description: """h3. Problem definition  Currently, {{membersOf()}} works only with User picker fields, which means the function checks if a certain user is a member of the group. However, the function cannot check if a certain group belongs to a parent group (it doesn't work with Group picker field types).    h3. Suggested resolution  Extend the function (or add a new one) so that the function can check whether a group is a member of a parent group."""
comments: "Do we have any work around to check logged in user is existing in a group which is assigned in a Group Picker field by JQL query .    Kindly support on this."

summary: "Users are shown as logged in if they are watching a confluence space and a new confluence page with the Jira macro or the Jira Charts macro is added"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    users are shown as logged in (*into Jira*) if they are *watching a confluence space* and a new confluence page with the Jira Issue/Filter macro or the Jira Charts macro is added.    Everytime a linked application pulls data from JIRA for a user, it will use an OAuth connection towards JIRA to verify if the user has the correct permissions and credentials to view the specified data. This could be a macro within Confluence, but also a linked JIRA issue.    When showing any content to a user, Jira checks the permission of the user viewing the page as otherwise, it could end up in a security issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # make sure that Jira and Confluence share the same *userbase*   # create an *Oauth application link* between Jira and Confluence.   # create a *space* in Confluence   # add some users as *watchers* of the newly created space   # with a different user, create a new page under the newly created space and either insert in the page the *Jira Issue/Filters macro* or the *Jira Charts macro*.   # observe into *Jira access logs* into the Jira Web UI user management that the users watching the space in Confluence are shown as logged in Jira even if they didn't logged in Jira.    This behaviour has been tested with *Jira 7.9.2* and *Confluence 6.14.3*.  h3. Expected Results  Users should not login into Jira.    h3. Actual Results  Users *watching the space* are marked as logged in Jira.    h3. Workaround    The only workaround that I found consist into removing the *Confluence application access group* to the users which we don't want to see as logged in Jira."""

summary: "We need a more robust process for refreshing prod data to lower environments"
description: """We recommend creating a test and dev instances of Jira prod data. This helps our Enterprise customers test upgrades and features, workarounds and fixes, I'd say """"have you vetted this in a lower environment?"""" is one of the most common phrases I use.  The problem arises is we only take safety precautions in regard to the mail, we can set a flag in the JVM    or we can disable it upon XML restore. But this still leaves the mail server and handler data in the DB and any admin can easily re-enable these mail servers.     *There are other concerns when refreshing data:*   * DVCS syncs   * applinks    * cache replication issues if prod and dev can communicate over 40001 and 40011   * index replication if you're doing a DB dump Jira has several bugs where it will continue to try to communicate to """"offline or inactive"""" nodes even though they cannot reach the DB see    !https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/viewavatar?size=xsmall&avatarId=51505&avatarType=issuetype! JRASERVER-42916 - Stale node ids should automatically be removed in Jira Data Center IN PROGRESS  for more information, this can even lead to dev indexing showing up in the admin/index section in other environments     h2. Suggested Action:     Perhaps we could create a """"lower environment"""" exporter or importer that strips the following from the export or import or a startup/setup task that can be run manually.   * DVCS data   * applinks    * cluster node tables    * mail server info and mail handlers    h2. Workaround for Manual DB refreshes:     If you're _not_ using JIRA's XML you can do the following, if you're using an XML restore, you can perform the following after importing the data and stopping Jira.   # Refresh the data from prod to lower environment   # run the following _before_ starting Jira    h2. Index related tables for Data Center:    Truncate the following tables:  {code:xml}  clusternode  clusternodeheartbeat  replicatedindexoperation  {code}  h2. Mail server info    1. Delete all incoming and outgoing mail servers by truncating the following table:  {code:java}  mailserver{code}  2. Remove mail handlers  {code:java}  delete from serviceconfig where clazz='com.atlassian.jira.service.services.mail.MailFetcherService'{code}  h2. [Remove Applinks|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/remove-an-application-link-from-jira-server-using-sql-285839519.html]:     1. Find the applink ids:  {code:java}  SELECT SUBSTR(a.property_key,16,36) as """"Application Key"""", b.propertyvalue as """"Application Name"""" FROM propertyentry a join propertystring b on a.id=b.id where a.property_key like 'applinks.admin%name';{code}  2. After identifying the application key of the application that requires removing using the above SQL in the diagnosis section, run the following queries exactly in this order, you'll also need to run these for _each_ result from query 1.  {code:java}  DELETE FROM oauthspconsumer WHERE consumer_key IN (SELECT propertyvalue FROM propertystring WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key LIKE 'applinks.%resultsfromfirstquery%'));  -- NOTE: For Microsoft SQLServer use the following query:  DELETE FROM oauthspconsumer WHERE consumer_key IN (SELECT CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), propertyvalue) FROM propertystring WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key LIKE 'applinks.%resultsfromfirstquery%'));{code}  {code:java}  DELETE FROM oauthconsumertoken WHERE token_key LIKE '%resultsfromfirstquery%';{code}  {code:java}  DELETE FROM oauthconsumertoken WHERE token_key LIKE '%resultsfromfirstquery%';  DELETE FROM trustedapp WHERE application_id IN (SELECT propertyvalue FROM propertystring WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key LIKE 'applinks.%resultsfromfirstquery%'));   -- NOTE: For Microsoft SQLServer use the following query:  DELETE FROM trustedapp WHERE application_id IN (SELECT CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), propertyvalue) FROM propertystring WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key LIKE 'applinks.%resultsfromfirstquery%'));{code}  {code:java}  DELETE FROM propertystring WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key LIKE 'applinks.%resultsfromfirstquery%');{code}  {code:java}  DELETE FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key LIKE 'applinks.admin.resultsfromfirstquery1%';{code}  {code:java}  UPDATE propertystring SET propertyvalue = REPLACE(propertyvalue,'\n2c66970e-35f8-365f-bc65-f535d7edf1a1','') where id in (select id from propertyentry where property_key like 'applinks.global%'); {code}  h2. Remove DVCS ACCOUNTS:    # Delete the orgs from """"AO_E8B6CC_ORGANIZATION_MAPPING"""":  {code:java}  delete from """"AO_E8B6CC_ORGANIZATION_MAPPING""""; {code}   # Delete the repos from """"AO_E8B6CC_REPOSITORY_MAPPING"""":  {code:java}  delete from """"AO_E8B6CC_REPOSITORY_MAPPING""""""""{code}  This will remove all connection information from JIRA to GHE.    AFTER these steps have been done you can start Jira for the first time.     This should remove all mail servers, dvcs account info and applinks from the instance, we also have a comprehensive guide for this [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/creating-a-test-environment-for-jira-966063324.html]."""

summary: "improve reaction to LDAP synchronisation connection timeouts"
description: """Depending on outside factors the LDAP server might fail to respond to respond to new connection requests.  This only happens to new connection requests.    However, Jira is opening a new connection for every single group that is to be synced. IF the LDAP directory has many groups this can turn into an issue:  If one of these connections run into the timeout, then the entire sync is halted somewhere in the middle. This is quite bad in grand scheme of things, as sometimes 1% of the groups are skipped, sometimes 99% and other times somewhere inbetween.    There are two scenarios that could be thought of to avoid this:   * be able to configure a """"Retry count"""", if a connection times out, then rety X number of times.   * be able to configure the import to reuse the same connection for all group lookups. That way it is more of a """"all or nothing"""" success. This should default to off to retain todays behaviour as some environment will depend on it, but for environments where the entire """"truth"""" is found in one directory this would be a massive performance gain."""
