summary: "Ability to have nested group with LDAP directory"
description: """Hi,        * As we are using LDAP directories for our users we can not use nested group in our configuration    Please find below our context and need (easier to have all the informations in the ticket) :         So in our instance we have several usecases :   * Scrum Team     * SAFe programs  ....    We have a portal to create users for JIra/Confluence and several tools of a Devops toolchain. In order to manage the user we centralize the user management by this portal. We set three roles in JIRA. These are associated with JIra Groups.    By exemple in the projet DVSTEST. The user are managed like this :         Rôle : project-administrator  Group : <nameOfProject>-admin    Rôle : project-user  Group : <nameOfProject>-user    Rôle : project-reader  Group : <nameOfProject>-reader    In our SAFe organizations we have lots of users, lots of groups and lots of projects that have to interact with each other    So my need would be to create a """"super-group"""" for a SAFe organization and to have the possibility to add in this group Jira native Group (which are managed with our portal).  It would be usefull to give to all the Jira project Group reading rights.    Ex : Super Group : SAFe_Marketing_reader  In the delegated group : Put all the projects group that would be like to have the reader rights.    Then add the SAFe_reader super group in all the SAFe projects.    That would be a benefit of complexity and of time for us.    I think I would not be the only one who would be interested by this feature (especially SAFe organizations)    Thanks in advance for your consideration.  Please feel free to ask me more question if it is not clear.              Thanks for your interest.    Regards,         Antoine"""

summary: "Modification of Administer projects"
description: """Hi,         In the project permission would it be possible to divide the  Administer projet permission.    In my sense it is not the same to manage rôle membership and manage versions and Components.         manage rôle membership --> administration of a project    manage versions and Components --> manage functionnaly the project    As for the permission Manage sprints (which is a unique permission itself).         Thanks for your consideration.         Best regards,    Antoine"""
comments: "Totally agree. Our project managers should be able to add people, but we have a standard set of components across all projects that they shouldn't be able to change. I'd like to see the permissions split out as well."

summary: "The possibility to move the desired fields to the sidebar"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, there is no other way to move the desired field to the sidebar  h3. Suggested Resolution    It is good to have a feature that admin can arrange the desired fields to sidebar"""

summary: "Field drop down options are not read by voice assistants"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using any sort of assistive technologies that generate announcements when scrolling through options (should as VoiceOver on Mac OS) on a drop-down menu, such as projects and issue types in the Quick Create menu, the options will not be read as expected.    *This prevents (or at least makes it very hard) creating issues by visually impaired people*, as they can't reliably change issue types, projects, priorities, etc.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable Voice Over (or any other assistive technologies that provide announcements).   # Click *Create* to create a new issue.   # Tab to the *Projects* or *Issue Types* drop-down menu.   # Use the up and down keys on the keyboard to cycle through the options on the menu.    h3. Expected Results    Every option will be read out by the voice-over assistant.  h3. Actual Results    Options are not read when you cycle through them.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Webhooks intermittently stop working with Java 11 "
description: """{panel:title=Solution|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#3c78b5|titleBGColor=#3c78b5|bgColor=#e7f4fa}  This problem is caused by a bug in the Java runtime: _JDK-8214418 HttpClient falls in running with 100% cpu usage after an error signalled on channel_ (Backported as [JDK-8241054|https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8241054])    The fix has been verified to be available in AdoptOpenJDK 11.0.8 - [https://github.com/AdoptOpenJDK/openjdk-jdk11u/commit/8d1b63a4db2c6348a97b3cf45bd4d2caa7cad6b5]  {panel}    h3. Issue Summary    Similar to BSERV-12131, the HTTP Client will stop working on occasion whenever using Java 11.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Jira to run on Java 11   # Register and configure a webhook to the application.   # Execute the webhook.    h3. Expected Results    The webhook will trigger successfully.  h3. Actual Results    When using Java 11, webhooks will work briefly and eventually fail. Thread dumps will show these webhook publishers in a *BLOCKED* or *WAITING* state:    This issue is suspected to be caused by the following JDK bug:   * JDK-8214418 HttpClient falls in running with 100% cpu usage after an error signalled on channel   ** [https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8214418] is not public   ** PR for the fix can be found here: [https://hg.openjdk.java.net/jdk/jdk13/rev/5022a4915fe9]    h3. Solution    Upgrade AdoptOpenJDK to version 11.0.8 which has this issue solved.  h3. Workaround   # Run Jira Server using Java 8 or;   # The issue is observed with Java 11 that uses TLS v1.3. TLS v1.2 is unaffected by this. It's possible to force Java 11 to use TLS v1.2 by adding the following JVM parameter: {{-Dhttps.protocols=TLSv1.2}} to your startup properties."""
comments: "I'm not sure what exactly prompted this issue for us. It first appeared over a weekend. I'm guessing some listener/service ran and caused high CPU usage.    The symptoms were that all webhooks stopped firing. Log messages similar to this could be seen:      The first time this happened a reboot solved the issue. The second time (the following weekend) a reboot did not solve the issue.    The root issue appears to be JDK-8214418. The fix (as applied to JDK 13) can be seen here: https://hg.openjdk.java.net/jdk/jdk13/rev/5022a4915fe9    I don't have write access to bugs.openjdk.java.net. Thus, I cannot request a backport request myself. I [opened an issue|https://github.com/SAP/SapMachine/issues/618] to see if someone at SAP can open a backport request (to upstream OpenJDK)." [SEP] "Originally, we thought we might be hitting this issue: JRASERVER-61937    However, putting in that config, {{com.atlassian.httpclient.options.threadWorkQueueLimit}}, did not solve the problem (although it may have ultimately helpe

summary: "When an integrity check finishes with no errors, there's no indication in the log the check completed"
description: """I debugged the Workflow Status IC and found there's no logging except for exceptions. Our Jira is very large and it takes about 4 hours to complete the 1 check box.    Our VPN logs us out after ~20 minutes. If we log back in and navigate to the IC result page, it kicks off another IC scan, so if we're not careful, we'll have 2 scans running.    Since we are not logged in and watching the screen, we don't see the UI come back, and there's no indication the check completed without errors. It would be nice to see in the log that the IC completed passing or failing in the logs.           """

summary: "Delete Worklog with Automation for JIRA"
description: """As a user I would like an action to be able to delete a specific worklog (from a specifc author at a specific date) in Automation for JIRA.     Today I can only add a worklog. """

summary: "Improve the documentation about the CSV import"
description: """    h3. Problem definition    The documentation about CSV import ([Creating issues using the CSV importer|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver0713/creating-issues-using-the-csv-importer-964984721.html]) mentions this:  * If you have the Create Issue project permission and the Bulk Change global permission for the relevant projects, you can create issues in bulk using a comma-separated value (CSV) file but it does not mention anything about the Jira Admin permission.  This causes confusion to our customers    h3. Suggestion    The Paragraph should be modified as other document mentioned Jira Admin permission can perform CSV import and a Feature request has already added to provide support for other users like [Project Administrator|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66962]."""

summary: "Portfolio for Jira "
description: """Representatives of Likn4 TU SA as potential users of the Portfolio For JIRA system have identified use cases that we do not know if it can be handled in the system.         We've created two new hierarchy levels above Epic: Initiative and ‘Strategic lever’ (the last one is the highest level)         Request 1 (Parent link directly between the standard issue type and the initiative)    Is it possible to create a parent link directly between an Initiative level task and a Standard Issue Type task?    In our business practice, initiatives often consist of Epics that group tasks and individual tasks. It is not effective to create Epic tasks for individual tasks in order to connect this task to the initiative.    If this is not possible, will the functionality described above be created in the future?         Request 2 (Many parent links between one initiative and many strategic levers)    Is it possible to create multiple parent links from one task from the Initiative level to many different tasks at the ‘Strategic lever’ level.    We want to be able to define a strategy in the form of many issues at the highest level of the hierarchy, which constitute 'strategic levers'. We want to be able to align the level of initiatives with the strategy by linking the task from the initiative level with each corresponding task from the level of strategy leverage.    Can this demand be met using Portfolio for JIRA? How?"""

summary: "Misleading Errors with Integrity Checker"
description: """We observed some misleading errors with the Integrity Checker for Filter subscriptions.  h3. Steps to reproduce,   * With an AD or any LDAP user create a filter subscription   * Disable the user or AD itself and run the integrity checker for Invalid Filer Subscription. Errors, as attached in the screenshot, are observed. !Screenshot 2020-03-13 at 12.20.11 AM.png!    h3. Observations    When checked in the DB and UI these filters are present and the user has to manually resubscribe to them.    h3. Desired Output    There was a feature request, JRASERVER-18919 which was turned down a long time back, but it should be revisited again and the misleading errors need to be handled.    h3.Customer Case for Reference  [GHS-180709|https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/GHS-180709]     """

summary: "Cancelling project re-indexing on one node does not cancel it on the other nodes"
description: """Starting a project re-index on one node triggers other nodes to start it as well (as mentioned in [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/index-replication-jira-data-center-troubleshooting-966676040.html#IndexReplicationJiraDataCenterTroubleshooting-IndexReplicationonRe-index]). When you cancel project re-index operation on the one node, other nodes will still continue re-indexing.    *Stopping project re-index on one node should stop it on the other ones as well.* It will keep the indexes more consistent and won't create situations in which indexes on some nodes are locked, but they aren't on other nodes."""

summary: "Progress field in portfolio blank unless you hover"
description: """See attached screenshot    *Request 1*    I've discovered that hovering over the progress field of an epic, displays all hours in the stories/subtasks of that epic (which is very handy).    It'd be great if this progress bar showed this info without having to hover, can you fix this?    *Request 2*    Additionally, while I understand that the *estimate (h)* field needs to be manually populated, it'd be great if a field existed to show total hours of an epic so I can understand epic size at a glance that are far in the future (just as the hover state shows total hours)"""

summary: "Need ability to configure date time Format per project and at other places such as Gadgets."
description: """# On the activity stream gadget timestamp for the activity is not shown, instead it shows as for example 30 minutes ago. If we hover over with the mouse on the area it will show the actual timestamp but it's not much user friendly way. Jira Admins should have ability to configure the gadget to show the date/time values using standard format used by look and feel settings.   # On the service desk project SLA dates are always shown within days,hours, weeks etc. It can be helpful if we have project specific settings to show the actual date/time values in standard dd/mm/yyyy HH:mm or mm/dd/yyyy HH:mm format. Not all project owners find the default format for showing SLA dates useful and may prefer configuring it differently for their project.    As far as i researched we can force Jira to use a specific date/time format globally  by updating the config file on the server but this becomes global and will even affect the project which don't want to use this format. So it makes sense to give ability to configure specific date time format per project or Gadget."""

summary: "Value changes when typing a large number in a custom field type ""Number Field"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Value changes when typing a large number in a custom field type """"Number Field"""", using other languages than English. The actual number should be 7,5995612126860155 but instead, it shows 7,6    This issue occurs using the *German language* OR *Russian language* on Jira. As indexing language and/or default user language and/or default Jira language.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Switch Jira to German OR Russian language as mentioned above.    # Switch your keyboard to match that language.   # Add a custom field """"Number Field"""" type.   # Create an issue in Jira and fill up the custom field created earlier with a huge number E.g """"7,5995612126860155"""" (using your keyboard, please *don't* copy-paste this number) then click on """"Create""""    # Head to your issue.   # Check the field value.    h3. Expected Results    * The value entered in the custom field """"Number Field"""" type should stay the same """"7,5995612126860155""""    h3. Actual Results    * The value entered in the custom field """"Number Field"""" type changes to """"7,6""""  * Files attach below:  **  !Russian.png|thumbnail!   **  !Ticket.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available.    h3. Note    You cannot add such large number using Jira in the English language."""

summary: "Exporting to CSV numeric custom fields shows the incorrect value (German, Russian)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Value changes when exporting data from Jira using CSV export. So when opening the CSV file, the number has changed. The actual number should be 4,5995621216860155 but instead, it shows 45.995.621.216.860.100 OR 4.6.    This issue occurs using the *German language* OR *Russian language* on Jira. As indexing language and/or default user language and/or default Jira language.    * There is inconsistency across export file types.  * There is an inconsistency between system and exported value.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Switch Jira to German OR Russian language as mentioned above.    # Switch your keyboard to match that language.   # Add a custom field """"Number Field"""" type.   # Create an issue in Jira and fill up the custom field created earlier with a huge number E.g """"4,5995621216860155"""" (using your keyboard, please *don't* copy-paste this number).   # Add the same number to another field like """"Description"""" and click on """"Create""""    # Head to the Issue Navigator and search for your issue.   # Export using CSV (All fields)   # Export as HTML.   # Check the CSV file.   # Check the HTML file.    h3. Expected Results    In the CSV file and HTML file, the number stays the same as entered in Jira E.g """"4,5995621216860155"""" in the custom field """"Number Field"""" type.    JIRA should export the value as presented in the system. For example, the german system > exported as the german number.    h3. Actual Results    * In the CSV file, the number in the custom field """"Number Field"""" type changes to """"4.59956212168601"""" and sometimes to 4.6  **  !fields CSV.png|thumbnail!   **  !Russian fields CSV.png|thumbnail!   * In the CSV file, the number in the """"Description"""" field stays the same.  * Files attached below:  **  [^German.csv]   **  [^Russian.csv]     * In the HTML file, all values stay the same.  * Files attached below:  **  [^Russian.html]   **  [^German.html]     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Add a Condition or Validator to check the value of a custom field"
description: """What is required is to add a condition or validator in Jira which could be used to restrict the """"same"""" value to be entered in a custom field while creating an issue in Jira.    h3. Example    If you have filled up a custom field with a value of 123, in ticket ABC-2.  Then another user creates issue ABC-3, then that user enters the same value """"123"""" in the same field, it will detect that there is another issue with the same value """"123"""" of the same field, then stops the issue from being created, throwing one of the options below (which can be set by the admin):      # Not allowing the creation of the ticket and informing the Reporter, that another issue with specific value already exists.  # Or allows the creation of the issue but it creates a link between both issues automatically."""

summary: "Enable more aggregations "
description: """Jira is a great tool.  As the consumption of Jira getting increased,  more aggregated report requirements are popping up.   Simple queries like  """" Aggregated report  on story points of all deliverables in a project"""" ,   Reports on Kanban teams, combination of Scrum and Kanban (at the release level)  etc., will be helpful.    Thanks,    R"""
comments: "Our versoin Jira 6.13.x"

summary: "Special Character in Status Names can cause Invalid JQL Filters from Sprint Health Gadget"
description: """h3. Problem  When using the Sprint Health Gadget, users can click on the progress bar and choose to """"View in Issue Navigator"""".  If the Sprint has any statuses that contain """"&"""", this will result in a truncated JQL query and be invalid.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # On a scrum board, add a new status with """"&"""".  For example: """"Test & Approve""""  # Add the new status to a column, like """"In Progress""""  # On a dashboard, add the Sprint Health Gadget, referencing the Agile board we configured above.  # On the progress bar, click on the yellow """"In Progress"""" section  # Choose to """"View in Issue Navigator""""    h3. Expected Results  The resulting filter is correct and returns the issues that are part of the sprint    h3. Actual Results  JQL error is thrown showing:      The JQL is also truncated right after the word """"Test"""", losing the """"& Approve"""" portion.    h3. Workaround  * Rename statuses and remove the """"&"""""""

summary: "Sprint Health Gadget Uses Unmapped Statuses in Search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the Sprint Health Gadget reports on an Agile board and Sprint, it will consider all statuses, included any existing unmapped statuses.  This results in an complicating the search filter and may mislead users.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board with unmapped statuses  ** Create 2 projects that have different workflow statuses. ProjectA and ProjectB  ** Create a new board for ProjectA, which will contain issues from ProjectA and statuses from ProjectA  ** Modify this board's filter to only include ProjectB.  Refresh the configuration page and check columns  ** We'll see that statuses that existed from ProjectA are not marked as unmapped   # On a dashboard, add the Sprint Health Gadget  ** Configure this to use the new board and sprint  # Next click on the """"Sprint Progress Bar"""" and choose to """"View in Issue Navigator""""    h3. Expected Results  The resulting filter will only show relevant statuses that are used by the Sprint/Board    h3. Actual Results  The resulting filter will list out all statuses, including the unmapped ones.    h3. Workaround  Go into each board that the Sprint is part of and ensure that unmapped statuses are removed"""

summary: "Make ESCAPE key behavior consistent with Edit dialog for Assign, Link & Log Work, & Custom Workflow dialogs"
description: """This was reported by a user of our Jira server today, they reported that they lost work on a detailed comment they were writing in one of these dialogs.    When the escape key is pressed or page navigation is attempted in the Edit or Comment dialogs a popup prevents the user from closing or navigating and losing their work accidentally.    Previously this was made consistent for other dialogs in JRASERVER-41814 however this prompt does not pop up for the Assign, Link, and Log Work actions and work is immediately lost when pressing the escape key.    This also does not happen for custom workflow screens that include the comment field.    Can this be made consistent with the Edit and inline comment dialogs and prompt before closing if a form has been partially filled out?"""

summary: "Configuration tool (config.sh) running on text mode doesn't specify a schema for SQL Server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Running the utility *{{<JIRA_INSTALL>/bin/config.sh -c}}* in text mode will cause it to not ask for a schema name when selecting SQL Server. This will cause the file {{dbconfig.xml}} to be set without a schema, which is required in SQL Server.    Other issues might arise from this situation, for example, failed upgrade tasks in Jira Service Desk.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Jira.  # Run *{{<JIRA_INSTALL>/bin/config.sh -c}}*  # Select the following options:  ** *[D] Database Selection*  ** *[S] SQL Server (MS-SQL)*  # Enter the database details such as hostname, port, etc.    h3. Expected Results  The utility will ask for the schema name and write this value accordingly on *{{<JIRA_LOCAL_HOME>/dbconfig.xml}}.*    h3. Actual Results  Schema name is not requested, and the value {{<schema-name>}} won't be written to *{{<JIRA_LOCAL_HOME>/dbconfig.xml}}:*  {code:xml}  <?xml version=""""1.0"""" encoding=""""UTF-8""""?>    <jira-database-config>    <name>defaultDS</name>    <delegator-name>default</delegator-name>    <database-type>mssql</database-type>    <jdbc-datasource>      <url>jdbc:sqlserver://;serverName=localhost;portNumber=3341;databaseName=jiradb</url>      <driver-class>com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerDriver</driver-class>      <username>atlassian</username>      <password>atlassian</password>      <pool-min-size>20</pool-min-size>      <pool-max-size>20</pool-max-size>      <pool-max-wait>30000</pool-max-wait>      <validation-query>select 1</validation-query>      <min-evictable-idle-time-millis>60000</min-evictable-idle-time-millis>      <time-between-eviction-runs-millis>300000</time-between-eviction-runs-millis>      <pool-max-idle>20</pool-max-idle>      <pool-remove-abandoned>true</pool-remove-abandoned>      <pool-remove-abandoned-timeout>300</pool-remove-abandoned-timeout>      <pool-test-on-borrow>false</pool-test-on-borrow>      <pool-test-while-idle>true</pool-test-while-idle>    </jdbc-datasource>  </jira-database-config>  {code}    Here's a full shell session demonstrating the issue:      This in turn can cause some Jira Service Desk upgrade tasks to fail, such as those below:              h3. Workaround  * Use the visual mode. This requires a GUI (X11, macOS, etc), and can be done by running *{{config.sh}}* without the parameter *{{-c}}*.  * Manually add the parameter *{{<schema-name>}}*, following the example below:    (i) More information about connecting Jira to supported databases is available at the KB article [Connecting Jira applications to a database|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-a-database-938846850.html].          """

summary: "Allow the MIME type to be set for files without a consistent extension"
description: """When opening an attachment with an unknown extension, Jira will download the file instead of opening it in the browser. It's possible to add the unknown extension on the mime.types file, but this is not feasible when the extension is inconsistent, i.e. a random string of numbers as the extension. In such cases it's not possible to predict the extension of the file to add to the mime.types file.    It would be helpful to be able to use a wildcard character in the mime.types file. This way, it would be possible to set the MIME type of any extension type."""
comments: "Could this feature cause a security risk by renaming a carefully crafted file to .png say?"

summary: "When inserting a text into Rich Text Editor with a space near to some notation and swithiching between Visual and Text tab, the format is lost"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When inputting a text into Rich Text Editor, on the Text tab the *Dollar ($)* symbol next to some notation (see the attached example), it lost the format when changed to the Visual bar.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new comment;  # Create a NoFormat, quote or code box;  # Click on the *Text* tab;  # Write the text with *$*;  # Change to *Visual* tab to check it.     [^sup_demo.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  Formatting will be kept when going to *Visual* tab.    h3. Actual Results  Formatting parameters are being lost when writing a *Dollar ($)* Symbol next a the notation.     h3. Workaround  # Create a new comment;  # Create a NoFormat, quote or code box;  # Click on the *Text* tab;  # Write the text with *$*;  ** put a space between *$* and end of notation;  ** do a breakline after the *$*;  # Change to *Visual* tab to check it."""

summary: "Accessibility: Create Issue dialog does not work in Safari when using VoiceOver"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using VoiceOver while logged into Jira in Safari does not allow you to access the *Create Issue* dialog.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # On macOS 10.15.3 and Safari 13.0.5 (15608.5.11), enable VoiceOver via CMD-F5, and access Jira in Safari.  # Use the C (keyboard shortcut) to create new issue  # Tab to the different fields in Create new issue.    h3. Workaround    The issue is not reproducible using VoiceOver + Chrome or using NVDA + Firefox. The issue is isolated to Safari alone."""

summary: "Wrong message display even when component is not selected while raising a ticket in Jira."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Wrong message display even when component is not selected while raising a ticket in Jira.    *Environment*   Tested in JIRA 8.7.1, but also reported by a customer using JIRA 7.11.2  h3. Steps to Reproduce    In Jira:-   # Create an issue   # Type in issue summary   # Type a component name and then remove it   # Provide issue description   # Click on Create    h3. Expected Results    Issue XYZ-123 - New issue has been successfully created.  h3. Actual Results    A new issue is created, and a new component is added to the project even though the component was removed during the issue creation.    *The following message can be seen in the UI:*    h3. Workaround    Do not enter component if you don't need it.    """

summary: "As a Jira Admin, I would like to customize mail notifications by replacing the default sender by the email of the user who triggered the action."
description: """We would like to have the ability to customize the notifications, to display the email of the user who triggered the action, instead of the default *From address* value configured in the SMTP server.    If someone mentions any users currently you receive a notification similar to :    FW:[Jira] User1 mentioned you on TP-1  From: User1 (Jira) <donotreply@atlassian.com>    We would like to configure a notification as it follows:    FW:[Jira] User1 mentioned you on TP-1  From: User1 (Jira) <user1@atlassian.com>"""

summary: "Nested Groups Membership Missing when ""Update group memberships when logging in"" used"
description: """h3.    {panel:title=Atlassian Update – 22 September 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ebf2f9|titleBGColor=#ebf2f9|bgColor=#ffffff}  Hi everyone,    After reviewing the overall customer interest and impact of this bug report we have decided to close this issue down. Our analysis has shown that over time this issue hasn't collected a significant number of votes, watchers, comments, or support cases from customers and therefore has remained very low on our priority list. Given these findings we can conclude it will not be fixed in the foreseeable future and wish to be transparent about our priorities by closing it as *Timed Out*.    We understand that hearing a decision like this can be disappointing, but we hope you'll appreciate our transparent approach to product priorities and communications. We will continue to watch this issue for further updates, so please feel free to share any thoughts in the comments.    Thank you,    Mateusz Ostaszewski,    Software Engineer in Jira Server  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    In case that """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" is set while """"Nested Group"""" is enabled, the user will somehow be lost its nested membership after log in.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Integrate JIRA with AD using """"Nested Groups""""   # Create 3 Levels of group membership in AD such as:       # Sync the directory in JIRA and confirm that the users have """"First"""" and """"Parents"""" groups.   # Rename one of the Group level e.g. """"First"""" to """"One""""   # Sync the directory in JIRA and the user should have """"One"""" as an additional group.   # Let one of the users log in.    h3. Expected Results    None of the membership will be lost  h3. Actual Results    The user will only have the direct group membership (Seconds) and lost the nested groups  h3. Workaround    Setting the """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" to """"Never"""" appears to not cause the user to lose the membership.    The following can be found when the user somehow lost the memberships  """

comments: "Whenever performing the intel-reconnaissance process during penetration testing or security auditing, we need to pay attention to the current web-server’s exposed information.    That’s where banner grabbing comes in. Banner grabbing is the act of getting software banner information (name and version), whether it’s done manually, or by using any [OSINT tools|https://securitytrails.com/blog/top-20-intel-tools] that can do it for you automatically.    FTP servers, web servers, SSH servers and other system daemons often expose critical information about not only the software name, but also about the exact versions and operating systems they’re running—the ‘banner’ data in question..    ([https://securitytrails.com/])"

summary: "Add a note to Issue Linking documentation to clarify how Confluence page link might behave differently than a web link"

E.g. they may expect to be able to link to an anchor section in the page content like https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/linking-issues-939937913.html#Linkingissues-CreatingalinktoaConfluencepage.  * In the scenario described above, Jira disregards the anchor reference in the URL when creating the Confluence page link.  * Workaround for that scenario should be to link to the Confluence page content using Web link instead. Same applies to linking to a comment on another Jira instance.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add a note to documentation addressing this expectation and clarifying how page URL behaves differently from web URL when linking Confluence pages."""

summary: "Mail Handler might go into a loop when processing messages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Mail handlers will process messages in a loop if the following conditions are met:  * The mail handler has been configured to forward errors to a mailbox through the *Forward* field on its configuration.  * The e-mail has an attachment that is larger than the maximum size allowed by the instance (10 MB by default).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a mail server connection and Mail Handler.  # Configure an SMTP server in the *Outgoing Mail* section of Jira.  # When configuring the Mail Handler, enter an e-mail address on the *Forward* field.  # Send an e-mail to the mailbox used by this Mail Handler, ensuring that it has a file attached to it that surpasses the maximum attachment size configured in Jira.    h3. Expected Results  The Mail Handler will process the message, the file will not be attached to the corresponding issue, and then the Mail Handler will proceed to the next message.    h3. Actual Results  The Mail Handler will read the attached file until its size is larger than the maximum allowed - once this happens, an error will be thrown indicating that the file will not be attached to the issue, which will be forwarded to the e-mail address configured in the mail handler. Once this happens, the Mail Handler will try to process the same attachment again, entering a loop.        (!) One thing to note is that this will only be replicated if Jira is configured with a valid outgoing mail server.    h3. Workaround    Disable forwarding error messages to a mailbox by removing the value from the *Forward* field in the Mail Handler configuration."""

summary: "Allow nanoseconds in Date/Time picker"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    As a Jira administrator, I would like to use the following format for jira.date.picker.*java*.format and jira.date.time.picker.*java*.format:  yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SSS    But there does not seem to be a corresponding format for jira.date.picker.*javascript*.format and jira.date.time.picker.*javascript*.format    This will then cause errors when trying to use the date/time picker in the UI. According the Atlassian article below, the format should be using [https://www.adminschoice.com/unix-date-format-examples], but this format does not work:  %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S.%N  was hoping to do something like this:  %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S.%3N  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set the Java value to yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.SSS   # Set the Javascript value to %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S.%N or %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S.%3N    h3. Expected Results    The date/time picker values will match and will be able to use all available options down to nanoseconds.   h3. Actual Results    The values give an error and do not work.   h3. Workaround    Workaround discovered:  use this format to achieve nanoseconds:    %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S.%S    *Links*    [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/changing-the-due-date-input-format-192536.html]    In this article it states:  {quote} * For Java formats, specify date/time formats based on the [Java SimpleDateFormat|http://download.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html].   * For JavaScript formats, specify date/time formats based on the [Unix date format|https://www.adminschoice.com/unix-date-format-examples].{quote}  [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-the-look-and-feel-of-your-jira-applications-938846975.html]     """
comments: "The %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S.%S format does not achieve nanoseconds.  It doubles up on the seconds placeholder to present a format to the UI that makes the date/time picker happy.    IMO, Atlassian should not reference the following docs, if they don't support the formats:   * For Java formats, specify date/time formats based on the [Java SimpleDateFormat|http://download.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html].   * For JavaScript formats, specify date/time formats based on the [Unix date format|https://www.adminschoice.com/unix-date-format-examples].    Since it's an external link, is it not considered official documentation?  Maybe there should be a doc with the actual supported formats?  Thank you for your time and consideration on this!" [SEP] "What's the use case for wanting to view Date/Time fields down to nanoseconds?  Another approach would be to create a new custom field type named something such as Date/Precision Time  using a plugin" [SEP] "We log to the server with nanoseconds

summary: "As a reporter, I would like the Project to be set based on the current page when creating an issue"
description: """*Richard van der Meel* originally reported this issue as a *bug* back in 2014, JRASERVER-36904, but I couldn't determine if he ever resubmitted the issue as a feature request. This particular issue has been causing us problems, forcing us to move an issue from one project to the intended project, but only if someone even noticed that an issue was created in the wrong project.         I know that JRASERVER-18199 was marked as *fixed* while JRASERVER-1555 was marked as *won't fix*, which both seem to be related to this particular issue. JRASERVER-18199 seems to be what's causing the issue described here, but that feature seems like it should only take effect if the *Create Another* checkbox is selected. JRASERVER-1555 doesn't seem like the solution because it can be annoying to have to select a project every single time I create a ticket.         What seems more intuitive is to just set the *Project* field based on the project for the issue/project that's currently being viewed in the active browser tab instead of setting it based on the last project that was *loaded*.         Using the example described in JRASERVER-36904,         # Open JIRA in 2 tabs   # Go to the *first tab* and open *Project A*   # Go the the *second tab* and open *Project B*   # **Go back to the *first tab* and click *Create*   # The selected project on the *Create Issue* screen will be *Project B*   # **Close the *Create Issue* screen   # Refresh the *first tab* so the screen reloads   # Click *Create* again   # Now the selected project on the *Create Issue* screen will show *Project A* selected         By setting the *Project* field based on the project that's being viewed in the active tab, this would help prevent a lot of headaches of having to hunt down issues that intended to be created for one project, but accidentally got created in another project. This would be especially helpful for teams who have *a lot* of projects."""

summary: "Batch email notifications do not log anything to Outgoing Mail log"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When batched emails are turned on, the logs file {{atlassian-jira-outgoing-mail.log}} does not capture or log any entries to troubleshoot and investigate issues even it's enabled to DEBUG.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Run Jira instance with batched notifications turned on,   - Enable _Outgoing mail log_ in the _Logging and profiling_ screen,   - Trigger events that result in emails being sent, e.g. user mentions.    Observe that no entries are appended to the {{atlassian-jira-outgoing-mail.log}}.  h3. Expected Results   - Customer expects if there is an issue with outgoing email function it should write in the outgoing mail log (at least if it enabled to DEBUG)   - They also expect to have an admin console (*UI*) for Mails to monitor its flow and enquire past mails history with data (Advanced than Mail queue)    h3. Actual Results   - We do not have any way to troubleshoot the outgoing mail issues in the current design as it is not writing anything even if it's enabled to DEBUG.    h3. Workaround   - You can try using [Notification helper|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver080/configuring-email-notifications-967897768.html#Configuringemailnotifications-troubleshooting] to troubleshoot configuration issues (if only some emails are not being sent)   - Enable debug logging for the _com.atlassian.jira.plugins.inform_ package and look into the _atlassian-jira.log_ file"""
comments: "Just so I get this right.    You introduced as big enhancement ""batch mail notifications"" which broke the logging completely. There is no way for administrators or Information Security departments to check the outgoing mails. Even for monitoring purpose, what is going on with the mail function in Jira - Completely impossible, because you do not know what's going out.    Troubleshooting issues like ""user didn't get any mail"" is no longer possible. In the past it was very easy to grep for the issue key or user and find all related messages.    The new feature is in the system, logging is broken (actually: NO logging is possible right now) and it is a low priority bug that wasn't touched for 3 full moths now.    I hope there is a reconsideration on this bug, because we want to use batch mails - What intermittent breaks and does not work for us (we and Premier Support can not troubleshoot why). After a few hours with this feature on, users do not receive any mails anymore. Some receive mails hours later. We

Issue Summary    If you update the issue type to sub-task during a Bulk Update (even single issues), and this issue has an Epic Link, the link will be preserved even though a sub-task should not be allowed to be Epic Linked.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Story and assign it to an Epic >   # From the issue search screen, search for the project the Story exists in >   # Perform a 'Bulk Move', select only the Story, and choose the 'Move' option >   # While moving the issue in this way, the system allows you to choose a new issue type: sub-task, and give the issue a new parent. In this test, I moved the Story to a new Project, and chose another Story there to be the new Parent issue.   # Once this move was complete, and the Story attached to an Epic became a Sub-task - the Sub-task was shown in the 'Issues in Epic' section of the original Epic.    h3. Expected Results    Sub-Task would not be included as 'Issue in Epic'  h3. Actual Results    Converted Sub-Task shows as 'Issue in Epic'  h3. summary: "Documentation on configuring Jira Server with Apache AJP should note recent Ghost Cat CVE-2020-1938"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Atlassian ships products like Jira Server/Confluence Server with an Apache Tomcat webserver. Out of the box, we don't ship any products I am aware of that use the AJP connector.    However customers could enable this as a means to setup an apache reverse proxy with our products.    if they do this, they should be made aware of the recent [CVE-2020-1938: Ghostcat - Apache Tomcat AJP File Read/Inclusion Vulnerability (CNVD-2020-10487) - Blog  Tenable®|https://www.tenable.com/blog/cve-2020-1938-ghostcat-apache-tomcat-ajp-file-readinclusion-vulnerability-cnvd-2020-10487] and [Apache Tomcat® - Apache Tomcat 8 vulnerabilities|https://tomcat.apache.org/security-8.html#Fixed_in_Apache_Tomcat_8.5.51]      h3. Suggested Solution  We should at least update documents such as   * [Integrating Jira with Apache using SSL - Atlassian Documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/integrating-jira-with-apache-using-ssl-938847754.html]   * [Configuring Apache Reverse Proxy Using the AJP Protocol - Atlassian Documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-apache-reverse-proxy-using-the-ajp-protocol-938847753.html]  * [Integrating Jira applications with IIS - Atlassian Documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/integrating-jira-applications-with-iis-938847749.html]    to have some kind of banner/warning about this CVE.  This way we can at least try to prevent users on unpatched versions from making themselves vulnerable to a known attack.      h3. Why this is important  Help keep customers secure.  We should acknowledge these steps could be potentially leaving system vulnerable to an RCE attack."""

description: """h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a CSV of Jira issues that contain issue links   # Import the CSV    # Jira will fail to import the issue links due to the issue ids being changed.    Example of logging:      h3. Suggested Solution  During Import the system should be able to decipher that the Issue Keys are changed first and then the issue inks are imported.     h3. Workaround  When Jira imports issues into an empty project, it retains the numbering scheme of the Issue Key.   * Thusly, [OLD Project key]-337 becomes [NEW Project KEY]-337.     * To resolve this, find and replace in the CSV and change {{OLD PROJECT KEY}} to {{NEW PROJECT KEY}} and reimported the CSV.   * When reimporting Jira knows issues exist and do not need to be recreated/doubled.   * During the linking, as Jira could now find the Issue Key it could link the issues successfully. """
comments: "Could the Steps to Reproduce be updated to make it clearer that this is about issue keys that have changed? And a test CSV file perhaps" [SEP] "And did it ever work before, is this a regression?" [SEP] "Thanks for your follow-up [~mdoar2], vigilant as usual :)   As you pointed out, I've moved to the feature request. "

summary: "When viewing the activity tab of an issue you should be able to see the entire action, and not have to click a 'Read More' link to see the full comment"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When viewing an issue in Jira, there exists an Activity tab you can click on. This shows all kinds of actions in relation to the issue such as comments, issues updates, mentions etc. However at times when there is a lot of data to here, Jira will currently use a 'Read More' link so that it doesn't actually load the entire comment here.    !jraserver-readmorelink.png|thumbnail!  h3. Suggested Solution    If there was an option in Jira to allow either administrators or end users to prevent this read more link and instead show the entirety of the activity event in relation to that issue, this would be helpful in some use cases.  h3. Why this is important    Promotes more transparency in regards to data and changes within Jira  h3. Workaround    None"""
comments: "The problem I see is that if someone or more likely  a script has logged work with large comments, then the Activity tab will become unloadable due to size. Jira restricts the number of comments shown for this kind of reason. I wish it did so for the History tab. I'd like to see limits on the comment size for work I guess?"

Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira Data Center 8.6.0 or higher    # Add the two classes below to your {{pluginstate}} table  ||pluginkey|pluginenabled||  |com.atlassian.plugins.base-hipchat-integration-plugin| false|  |com.atlassian.plugins.base-hipchat-integration-plugin-api|false|   # Restart Jira   # Try accessing the Clustering page    h3. Expected Results    The Clustering page works regardless of old Hipchat classes in the database.    h3. Actual Results    The Clustering page breaks when those classes are present, what is common on installations that are being upgraded from old Jira versions.    h3. summary: "Trying to boost a term using a caret per the search syntax documentation does not appear to work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    [Jira Server search syntax for text fields documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939938747.html] indicates that users can boost a term in order to make Jira return more relevant search results. Details in [Search syntax for text fields: Boosting a term: ^|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939938747.html#Searchsyntaxfortextfields-Boostingaterm:^]    The problem with this section is that this does not appear to be true in the current versions of Jira Server today.  Boosting a term does not at all change the order in which the issues are returned in Jira, nor does it affect the number of issues returned by any JQL query.  Hence it appears to be doing nothing today.  In addition to this, it appears that Jira Cloud documentation has already acknowledge that 'boosting a term' using a caret \^ does nothing anymore and is unsupported, see [https://support.atlassian.com/jira-core-cloud/docs/search-syntax-for-text-fields/].    In turn I suspect that Jira did once behave in this manner, but it does not appear to be doing so in any of the recent versions where we have documented this (at least as far back as Jira 7.3 we have this documented).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try to follow the examples in [Boosting a term|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939938747.html#Searchsyntaxfortextfields-Boostingaterm:^]    h3. Expected Results  I would expect that either the number of results will vary by boosting a term OR the order in which those results are returned would be in some way affected by boosting one term over another.    h3. Actual Results  There appears to be no difference whatsoever in the search results when boosting any term in a text field search in JQL (either advanced or basic JQL searching)    h3. Workaround  None"""
comments: "Good catch!"

summary: "Jira Data Center clustering features might be only partially enabled if non-breaking spaces are present in cluster.properties"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira Data Center clustering features might be only partially enabled if there are [non-breaking spaces|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-breaking_space] in {{cluster.properties}}.    Using the tool {{hexdump}} it is possible to confirm these characters are present; look for the value {{c2 a0}}:   !hexdump.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Jira Data Center license.  # Place a {{cluster.properties}} file containing special characters such as a [Non-breaking space|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-breaking_space] in {{JIRA_LOCAL_HOME}}. Here's an example file: [^cluster.properties-with-non-breaking-spaces.txt].  # Restart Jira.    h3. Expected Results  Jira Data Center clustering features are enabled normally, including cache and index replication.    h3. Actual Results  {{JIRA_SHARED_HOME}} will be enabled and used, but cache and index replication won't:  {noformat:none|title=Startup logs}      ___ File Paths ____________________________                 JIRA Home                                     : \\some_location\share\Automation\Jira_Dev\sharedhome           JIRA Local Home                               : C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\JIRA           Location of atlassian-jira.log                : C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\JIRA\log\atlassian-jira.log           Index Path                                    : C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\JIRA\caches\indexesV1           Attachment Path                               : \\some_location\share\Automation\Jira_Dev\sharedhome\data\attachments    {code:none|title=Health checks}  Name: Cluster Index Replication  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Locks  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Scheduler  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Cache Replication  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Time Synchronisation  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Shared Home  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED  {code}    h3. Workaround  Remove all [non-breaking spaces|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-breaking_space] from {{cluster.properties}} and restart Jira."""
comments: "Another question is how those characters got into that file in the first place"

summary: "Add the ability to bulk edit a comment secured visibility "
description: """*Current behaviour*  Group A is an Originazaitional field.   When adding a comment only visible to Group A then the member of that group leave the company. summary: "User login fails because inconsistent data / uk_mem_parent_child_type "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Data inconsistency result in users not able to login. There is a number of related cases when for all of them the root cause isn’t found, for most of them DB manipulation/refreshing cache/re-adding directory and syncing it solved the problem.    This results in users unable to login, but flows/actions that could result in this state are plenty of. Mainly those are user login, directory Synchronisation.    For some customers that works only temporary so the workaround is done over and over again.    Inconsistency exists in DB between cwd_membership and cwd_user tables.    The most probable reason of it is cache corruption. It was confirmed in 1  case.    There is a KnowledgeBase article for that, number of cases with similar symptoms and the same workaround steps.   [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/ldap-users-and-groups-display-unexpectedly-in-jira-server-776799341.html]    The biggest problem with pointing out the root cause is that problem show off with the delay(like a couple of months later), when there are no available logs providing information how data inconsistency was created (due to failed CRUD operation, or cache corruption -> that's why there are different workaround - manual DB update / new user-directory + resync & cache refresh)  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Problem is not yet reproducible   # On affected/corrupted instances users are not able to login    h3. Expected Results    An active user is able to login. If a problem occurs, sync AD with Jira solves the problem.   -> There is no data inconsistency  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the Atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround   * *Refresh caches by full Jira restart (all nodes in case of DC)*   ** Full restart in Data Center is required because nodes with a corrupted cache will replicate it to other starting nodes, this means shutting down all nodes, validating the service is stopped and starting them back up, a rolling restart will not resolve this issue.     * *Add new directory, sync it, remove the 1st one*   ** Create a new user directory with exact same settings as Directory ID: 10000 (name: Active Directory server   ** Synchronize with this new directory   ** Move this new directory to top position (the first directory to be checked)   ** Test user access   ** Remove old directory(id:10000)   * *Remove* *blocking*(inconsistent data) *user* from cwd_membership and consequently update cwd_user"""
comments: """Refresh cashes + resync user directories"" - what exactly gets refreshed with this call, and how does it scale for large Jira instances    For the third option of deleting the inconsistent (corrupted) data, we had to remove the blocking user from access to Jira, remove the data, do an AD sync, then add the blocking user back to Jira access  " [SEP] "Also, this happened for us in 7.6.9, before we upgraded to 7.13.8" [SEP] "Please can the summary typo be fixed so people can find this issue more easily when searching" [SEP] "Um,. why is this a low priority?" [SEP] "What about a rolling restart to remove the localq directory on each node?"

There are many of issues where asking for this to be resolved. Hundreds of votes, watchers and comments... just wasting time. I don't know if creating this issue is the best way to address this. Is there any complain form anywhere in your web?    Ciao."""

Query is doing full table scans and scanning more than 4 lac rows for every execution.    * {{moved_issue_key}} table structure:       h3. Suggested Solution  Add an index on the *issue_id* column for the table {{moved_issue_key}}?    h3. Workaround   Add index manually """

summary: "As a user, I would like to be able to clone an issue and retain the epic link, even without permission to edit issue."
description: """Our customers are looking for the ability to clone an issue that has an epic link and have the new clone issue to retain the epic link from the cloned issue.    They want this feature to be accessible to all users that have the """"read"""" access, however without giving users """"edit issues"""" permissions to be able to clone the epic link.         Per our documentation at [Managing epics in a Scrum project|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/JIRASOFTWARESERVER086/Managing+epics+in+a+Scrum+project], the 'Edit Issues' permission is required to add an issue to an epic.    The ideal would be to have the ability to select if the users might or might not copy also the Epic link (specific permission for it), without being related to the “edit issues” permission.         *Steps to reproduce:*   1. Using an user account with permission to “Edit Issues”:  *  Create an Epic “Epic1”;  *  Create Story “Story1” and link to the Epic “Epic1”  *  Clone “Story1” as “Clone - Story1”  *  Observe that “Clone - Story1” was created and the link to “Epic1” was preserved.     2. Using a user account without permission to “Edit issues”:  * Clone “Story1” as “Clone2 - Story1”  * Observe that “Clone2 - Story1” was created but +without the Epic link.+"""
comments: "Our customers are looking for the ability to clone an issue that has X epic link and have the new clone issue retain the epic link from the cloned issue, without needing special permissions such as being in the ""Edit Issues"" group or the ""Manage Sprints"" group. They want this feature to be accessible to all users that have the ""read"" access.    Is this something that could be added or already exists? The customers do not want to have to give users ""edit issues"" permissions to be able to clone the epic link."

Regardless, being able to customize the length of time the notification appears and the text that appears would be useful." [SEP] "What about clicking on the Issues link. The new issue appears in the list of recent issues, at least in Jira Data

summary: "REST API issue create endpoint should have the ability to suppress notifications"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Derived from previous JRASERVER-34423 - There are times when a Jira admin might need to create a large number of issues and choose to use the REST API as a means to do this. However you don't always want to send out notifications to all the users in the notification scheme when this takes place. It would be very helpful if the issue create endpoint api/2/issue had a means to do this, much like the edit issue endpoint (api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey} endpoint) has the notifyUsers query parameter.  h3. Suggested Solution    Implement the notifyUsers query parameter on the issue create endpoint as well.  h3. Why this is important    End users are easily annoyed by bulk issue creation. Many users have complained about Jira being overly chatty when it comes to email. This suggested solution would provide Jira admins a quick means to create issues without flooding the inbox of everyone on your team. This implementation method would still log issue histories and trigger other events/listeners just like notifyUsers currently works on api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey} endpoint.   The workaround can provide a solution, but it's not ideal because it is not granular enough to only limit notifications for this REST API call, it's overkill to completely change the notification scheme for this single issue creation event, and likely to leave the admin to forget to change it back.  h3. Workaround    It is possible for Jira admin to temporarily modify the notification scheme in use for that project so that no users are sent out notifications for such events as Issue Created, issue assigned, etc. However this work-around is not ideal as it prevents all such notifications for any project using that scheme."""

description: """We are trying to get the Jira Mobile application to authenticate via Single Sign On and we cannot get it work.  We believe the issue is that the mobile app uses an Embedded Web View.  Is it possible to switch the application to use any of the following?   * Native Browser   * """"in-app browser tab"""" API (like Chrome custom tabs or ASWebAuthenticationSession) """
comments: "Hi [~canderson2]    The app should support  SSO. Could you provide more details:   * Mobile platform   * Screenshots" [SEP] "Artour,    We are having this issue on both iOS and Android devices.    Part of the issue is that we use PKI credentials for mutual TLS authentication with our ADFS server. On iOS, for example, we have PKI credentials provisioned to the iOS system keychain. They are accessible to Safari, the iOS Mail app, and other Apple apps, but not to 3rd party apps like yours because of Apple's keychain sandboxing. Our users do not have passwords; they can only authenticate to ADFS with PKI.    This is what we're seeing:   * Users launch the app and enter our server URL   * Users get our ""warning banner"" page and click the link to authenticate with ADFS   * Users get the ""Can't get a secure connection"" error    I'm aware that this is usually caused by a certificate trust issue, but this is not the problem because the login flow works fine in Safari; it only fails in the Jira Server app.    We'

summary: "Tasks can be cleaned too early in Jira DC 8.7.1"
description: """h3. Summary    A mechanism that cleans-up stale cluster tasks (JRASERVER-70585) contains a bug, where it takes a property set in minutes and passes it to a method expecting milliseconds, without converting between units.    As a result, a task can be cleaned up _way faster_ than it should.    Besides not being able to check the task's progress in the UI, errors like below might appear in the logs:    In the scenario above, the re-index is happening as it should and the errors concern only reporting the progress.  h3. Environment   * Jira DC with 2+ nodes, running Jira 8.7.1 (the _only_ affected Jira version)    (i) Note: Neither Jira 8.5.4 nor 8.8.0 are affected by this bug.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Perform a change that causes """"Bulk Operation"""" action or start a re-index   # Create a short database connection failure   # The task with the job will be cleaned-up.   (i) The task will still be executing. However, it will not be possible to check its progress and _NullPointerExceptions_ can appear in the logs.    h3. Actual Results    The property _is not_ converted from minutes to milliseconds.  h3. Expected Results    The property _is_ converted from minutes to milliseconds.  h3. Workaround    Either:   # Disable the feature by setting the """"jira.dc.cleanup.cluser.tasks.disabled"""" feature flag and *restart the node*. You can [follow our documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html] to learn how to set it.   # Set the _cluster.task.cleanup.offline.node.threshold_ property to a high value, e.g. 1800000 (which equals to 30 minutes).   ** *Remember to remove this override before upgrading Jira*, or else the mechanism will only clean-up tasks from nodes that are offline for ~3.5 years.   # Upgrade Jira to a version containing a fix once it's available, i.e. 8.7.2, 8.8.0 or higher.    (i) Please note that *the workaround needs to be applied to every node in the cluster*, because the tasks are being removed by other nodes, not the node that's executing the task.    To make sure that the first workaround works, you can enable DEBUG logs for _com.atlassian.jira.cluster.ClusterTaskCleanupService_.   During the service startup (when the node starts or after a plugin system restart) you should see the _""""ClusterTaskCleanupService is disabled""""_ message logged, instead of the _""""Registering ClusterTaskCleanupService""""_ message if the feature flag is still enabled."""
comments: "Can you confirm that *jira.dc.cleanup.cluser.tasks.disabled* is accurate?    Unless that refers to ""cluster user"" or something similar it seems strange it would be different to other values that are cluster.something etc.     " [SEP] "[~helpdesk56]  Thanks for paying attention to this!    I've doublechecked the code, it's {{jira.dc.cleanup.cluser.tasks.disabled}} as mentioned above.   Looks like a typo :/     CC: [~drauf]"

summary: "Export values are not consistent across different exported file type (csv, xml, html)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When exporting Number Custom Field values that are long, it would lead to some inconsistencies based on the file type exported. Below is the observed results:  (i) In this example, the value is *100,000,000,000,000*  || | Exported as | Opened as | Rendered ||  || | CSV | text file | 1.0E14 ||  || | CSV | excel file | 100,000,000,000,000 ||  || | XML | xml | 1.0E14 ||  || | HTML | text file | 100,000,000,000,000 ||  || | HTML | web page | 100,000,000,000,000 ||    In the Database, the value can be seen stored as *100,000,000,000,000* (Float Type). It is an intentional design choice that the value is stored as Float Type. Due to this, the value could be exported as the Exponent Form: *1.0E14*. However, the exported value should be consistent in all exported file types, either exporting as the Integer form or Exponent form.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Number Custom Field  # Add the field into a Screen  # Add  long numeric value into the field (e.g.: 100,000,000,000,000)  # The value can be seen as different formats:  | UI | 100,000,000,000,000 |  | Database | 100,000,000,000,000 (Float) |  | CSV (opened as text file) | 1.0E14 |  | CSV (opened as excel file) | 100,000,000,000,000 |  | XML | 1.0E14 |  | HTML (opened as text file) | 100,000,000,000,000 |  | HTML (opened as web page) | 100,000,000,000,000 |    h3. Expected Results  All exported file types should have consistent formats    h3. Actual Results  The exported file types have inconsistent formats    h3. Workaround  To avoid this, the long numeric values can be stored in Text Custom Field rather than the Number Custom Field. However, this would allow alpha-numeric value input."""
comments: "Please also consider that there is an issue when working with floating numbers and exporting these data to local files. The exporter currently doesn't care about the users language. E.g. a German version of MS Excel isn't able to identify ""1.23"" as a number."

summary: "Default Resolution causes issues to be committed with Resolution Date"
description: """h3. Summary    If we have a default resolution set in Jira, creating issues through Portfolio plans results in the new issues having a Resolution Date, though no Resolution.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project  # Create a Portfolio plan and add the project as an issue source  # Go to Resolutions page in JIRA (type g+g >> resolutions, under https://instance.atlassian.net/secure/admin/ViewResolutions.jspa)  # Select a resolution as default  # Add an issue to the plan via Portfolio  # Commit it so that it's created in JIRA    h3. Expected Results  The ticket is created without Resolution Date, as it doesn't have a resolution.    h3. Actual Results  It's created with no Resolution (as expected), but with a Resolution Date (not expected):  !resolution_problem.png|thumbnail!      h3.Workaround    h4. Workaround 1  # Unset default resolution (in 'Clear defaults') in Resolutions page  # Commit  # Set the default resolution back    h4. Workaround 2 (suggested in comments below)  # Change the 'Create' transition for this issue  # Add a post-function to clear the 'resolution' (not resolution date)  #* This should also clean the resolution date"""
comments: "Same problem here, but Portfolio is not installed. Maybe BigGantt causes the same issue. Not sure. Thank you for your feedback, Richard!" summary: "Better handle 'none' option in the list of priorities when creating issues via the portal"
description: """Currently, if the default priority in the priority scheme is not set (or set to 'none'), JSD Portal will not display 'none' to users as a priority when creating an issue. Instead, JSD will pick up the priority that resides in the middle of the priorities in the priority scheme for that issue type.  For example, if the middle priority is 'medium'  but the default is 'none', JSD will show 'medium' priority as the default priority when creating the request.    This creates confusion as some customers expect that 'none' priority should exist in the list and either should not allow the users to create an issue at all until the correct priority is set, or create the issue with 'none' priority so that the priority can be set later    h5. Workaround    # Chose valid default priority in the default priority scheme (i.e. not 'none')  # Create """"No priority' priority and set it to default"""

summary: "Configure Jira to always generate Thread Dumps on Shutdown"
description: """h4. Context  During a critical situation where Jira is unresponsive or extremely slow, it becomes very important to capture thread dumps *during the impacted period* to investigate potential causes.    Many admins may not be familiar with this and restart the server before proper data is collected, losing critical information.  This means that they will have to wait for another incident to occur before they can then collect proper information.    h4. Current Behaviour  Currently, Tomcat will generate a single thread dump in the event where the shutdown process has trouble closing threads in a timely manner.    h4. Suggestion  By default *every* shutdown should generate 3 thread dumps, 5 seconds between.  To extend this it can also have an easy method to disable this behaviour."""
comments: "Yes, with suitable timeouts for when the Jira process is completely wedged and we can't even get a thread dump!"

summary: "Jira: Restrict the version of Flash player being used by the user."
description: """Customer's Security team scans has noticed that Atlassian Jira does not restrict of version of Flash player being used by the user. They highlighted the following.    <embed src=""""'+a.options.moviePath+k(a.options.moviePath,a.options)+'"""" loop=""""false"""" menu=""""false"""" quality=""""best"""" bgcolor=""""#ffffff"""" width=""""100%"""" height=""""100%"""" name=""""global-zeroclipboardflash-bridge"""" allowScriptAccess=""""always"""" allowFullScreen=""""false"""" type=""""application/x-shockwave-flash"""" wmode=""""transparent"""" pluginspage=""""http://www.macromedia.com/go/getflashplayer"""" flashvars=""""'+e+'"""" scale=""""exactfit"""">  codebase=""""http://fpdownload.adobe.com/pub/shockwave/cabs/flash/swflash.cab#version=9,0,124,0    The security team hopes that Atlassian can remediate this by:  Adding the attribute such as  codebase=""""http://fpdownload.adobe.com/pub/shockwave/cabs/flash/swflash.cab#version=9,0,124,0"""" to the object tag in html will communicate to the users flash player that version 9.0.124.0 is required to run this flash object.    If the user's Flash Player is prior to that version, the user will be warned and a dialog will prompt him to visit Adobe's Flash Player page and update to the latest version."""

summary: "Invalid rows in nodeassocation will cause a cluster-wide outage"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  All nodes in a Jira Data Center cluster will become inaccessible if there are rows in nodeassocation that refer to a project ID that does not exist in the project table ( aka project schemes for a project that does not exist ).     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Not reliably reproducible, we only have reports of this occurring after the fact.   # However, you can create a row manually in nodeassociation using a project id value that doesn't exist in the project table to trigger this behavior    h3. Expected Results  If this occurs, for whatever reason, Jira at least remains accessible    h3. Actual Results  Jira becomes inaccessible, throwing 500 errors in the user interface until the rows are removed and every node is restarted    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      h3. Workaround  Remove the invalid rows as described in [500 CacheException error thrown during upgrade/restore of Jira server|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/500-cacheexception-error-thrown-during-upgrade-restore-of-jira-server-800858895.html], shut down all nodes, and start them up again as database values may remain in Jira's cache"""

summary: "Search: Allow the amount of Fields Displayed to be modified "
description: """Summary: Ability to change the number of fields displayed when searching and sorting  h3. Problem    When a user performs a search, only 10 fields are displayed by default. The rest of the fields are hidden and often missed by newer users.  New users are often unaware of the additional fields that aren't displayed by default.   h3. Suggested Solution    Add an option to change the number of fields displayed on search and sorting results.  h3. Why This Is Important    Newer users often miss important fields and aren't aware that they exist. The option to increase the number of fields displayed would increase the awareness for new users. It would decrease the learning curve for those newer to Jira.   h3. Workaround    N/A"""

Suggested Solution  NA   h3. Workaround   (Optional)"""

summary: "Maintain the bundled cacerts file when upgrading Jira"
description: """h3. Problem:    Jira 8.6 introduced [a new functionality|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-6-x-release-notes-978220007.html#JiraSoftware8.6.xreleasenotes-check] that allows the users to migrate the existing customizations from their server.xml and setenv.sh when upgrading Jira.    The server.xml allows the users to keep their SSL configuration(among other configurations), however by not keeping the cacerts information, all SSL connections like LDAPS, POP3S, IMAPS will be broken after the upgrade. Besides the external SSL connections, the Jira users will also experience failures in the Gadget Feed Health Check due to the loss of the trusted certificates.  h3. Resolution and Impact:    By also maintaining the cacerts file after the upgrade, we will be able to provide a more seamless experience to our customers when upgrading Jira."""

Example:   Firstname: Victor   Last name: AllanSmith   Jira Username: Victor.Allan (or) Victor.AllanSmith"""

summary: "Drag and drop image in Visual Mode in comment results in full image rather than thumbnail"

Steps to Reproduce    # Switch the comment to Visual Mode  # Add some text on the comment  # Then underneath that text, Drag and Drop an image    h3. Expected Results    The image shows up as a thumbnail in the comment    h3. Actual Results    Image shows up as a full blow image    h3. Notes    Prior to Jira 8, when images are dragged and dropped onto the comment in Visual Mode, the image is added to the issue, however, is not added to the comment. Only when switched to Text mode will Jira also include this in the comment as thumbnail    h3. Workaround    Switch to Text mode and then drag and the image. This will make sure that the image is added as thumbnail."""

summary: "Archiving sub-task issue type"
description: """*Summary*     Currently Jira does not allow archiving sub-task issue type independently. Archiving sub-task can only be done by archiving its parent task which will archive *ALL* associated sub-tasks. The behaviour also not consistent with delete operation whereas user can delete inidividual sub-task (without its parent).     *Ask*     Allow archiving individual sub-task without its parent.           """
comments: "business justification  from [https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/PS-53775]    ""Business justification for this is as follows:  Delete is like no go back but archival is considered as a recycling bin like a confluence trash to restore and which also improves performance as part of maintaining our instance we want to archive issues using archival rest call and archive anything which is older than 2 years to keep our instance clean and perform well in terms of indexing. Delete is considered little bit dangerous as you cannot restore the issue . So, Archival is what is driving this use case but we have seen that most of the users create sub-tasks as mistake which they want to remove or delete and it dosen't make sense as delete works with sub-task but not archive ? This is our use case and we want archive to work for sub-tasks."""

summary: "Incoming mail is consuming all caesium threads blocking other scheduled tasks from running"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In larger instances of the software, there may be dozens of mail handlers configured or there me so much mail load  that needs to be processed that it's possible for incoming mail to consume all four caesium threads causing other tasks such as notifications, DVCS syncs, etc to fail silently.  Ideally, we want to have a large number of caesium workes, see JRASERVER-65809.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # create a large instance    # configure dozens of mail handlers OR a few with a tremendous amount of load, in one instance there were 680+ new messages from one handler in a period of less than 24 hours    h3. Expected Results  JIRA gracefully handles all scheduled tasks and allows other tasks besides mail handlers to be processed  h3. Actual Results  incoming mail will hold all four threads until the queue is processed     Thread dumps show all four caesium threads running with a similar stack trace:      (!) These may vary based on mailbox type but the threads are all running and checking mail.     h3. Workaround    Until a fix is found, it may be best practice to stagger how often mail handlers are checking mail. """
comments: "Jira Incoming Mail Handlers have never scaled beyond single digits for us. Before in Jira 6.4 the Data Center implementation used these same threads to run some sync services. So when there was too much email arriving the DC sync broke! Thankfully DC now has its own threads for that. But I still think the workaround is to use the JEMH plugin or some such." [SEP] "You can add an Affects Version of 3.0 if you want!"

summary: "JIRA applinks fail if SSL certificate uses Subject Alternative Name (SAN)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attempting to create application link from JIRA to another application will fail if that application runs over HTTPS with SSL certificate that uses Subject Alternative Name (SAN) different from DNS name, e.g. rfc822name. JIRA 7.13 to 8.5 uses Apache HttpClient 4.5.5, which is affected by [https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/HTTPCLIENT-1906]        h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure JIRA and Confluence to run over HTTPS with SSL certificate using entry Subject Alternative Name: RFC822Name=SysIngSUN@example.com   # Ensure Confluence's certificate is accepted by SSLPoke on Jira side   # From JIRA, create an application link to Confluence    h3. Expected Results    The applinks creation is successful on both sides.   JIRA is able to load gadgets  h3. Actual Results    The applinks fails on JIRA side.    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround    Use certificate without SAN at all, or with correct DNS name entry.    """
