summary: "Limit number of available database connections for each plugin"
description: """h3. Problem definition  Currently, Jira doesn't have available controls to limit the number of available database connections to be used by individual plugins.  This means that each plugin can utilize all available database connections available and """"starve"""" Jira or other plugins.    h3. Suggested resolution  Implement control in Jira to allocate a maximum number of database connections to each plugin."""

summary: "As an admin, I want to be able to exclude/blacklist a user/role/group from a notification scheme"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When a notification scheme is defined, you cannot exclude users from a notification.   For instance, some users/roles/groups may choose to opt-out from Jira notification for a given project.   h3. Suggested Solution  Add a blacklist to notification scheme screens.   h3. Workaround  The users in questions have to setup a filter in their mailbox to archive the messages. """

summary: "Make ""me"" shortcut to ""reporter = currentUser()"" by analogy to ""my"""
description: """There is a handy shortcut """"{{my""""}} for the search field, that translates to \{{assignee = currentUser()}}.         Now, the """"my"""" shortcut is quite developer oriented, meanwhile much of the Jira user base crowd is quality focused, and would greatly benefit from a similar shortcut """"me"""" - named by analogy."""

summary: "Sprint reports should have an editable completed date"
description: """h3. Description  There are cases where a sprint is closed by mistake, then it is reopened, adjusted, and closed again. The *COMPLETE_DATE* column of the *AO_60DB71_SPRINT* table is the information used at the Sprint Report (and others), and this information is always updated each time you close the sprint again.    Sometimes, though, the sprint is completed by mistake and the reports  should consider the correct complete date, but instead it considers the latest complete date which, on these cases, will be after the reopening.    The End Date is more accurate, but it wouldn't make sense as the complete date could be different than what is filled at the End Date. For example:    # Create Sprint A and set it's End Date as 02/05/2020  # Complete sprint at 03/05/2020. The Completed Date will be 03/05/2020  # Reopen the Sprint A at 04/05/2020 because there were some missing stories from another sprint that were done at Sprint A. Include these stories at the sprint, and complete it again. The Completed Date will be 04/05/2020.  # The Sprint Report will be generated considering the COMPLETE_DATE, which was at 04/05/2020, which is understandable, however, the correct close date was 03/05/2020. Neither END_DATE not COMPLETE_DATE stores this date for the sprint to work correctly.     h3. Current Workaround  It is needed database manipulation to change this information, which is not supported by Atlassian and could lead to further errors.    h3. Suggestion  A few suggestions:  - Have the Completed Date field at the Sprint Report to be editable so the user can input the date he wants, or  - Have the COMPLETE_DATE field editable when completing the sprint, or  - Have a third field like """"ORIGINAL_CLOSED_DATE"""" which will always inform the date the sprint was first closed, by mistake or not.  """

summary: "CSRF in Application Links plugin allows network enumeration - CVE-2019-20100"
description: """Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center before version 8.7.0 use a version of the Atlassian Application Links plugin that is vulnerable to cross-site request forgery (CSRF). An attacker could exploit this by tricking an administrative user into making malicious HTTP requests, allowing the attacker to enumerate hosts and open ports on the internal network where the Jira server is present. See https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/APL-1390 for further details."""

summary: "Application Link Status page fails to load due to Case-sensitive checks for Headers"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Trying to configure a new application link or loading the Application link page to list the status of existing Applinks will show """"NETWORK ERROR"""" in red.    The REST client  {{atlassian-rest-module:3.2.16}} while reading headers (eg. Content-Type) does a _case-sensitive lookup_ for them (headers name should be _case-insensitive_). So when headers are in lower case it can't parse them properly. The problem is triggered by the proxy server, which is converting all HTTP header names to lower-case.    h3. Environment  * Use of proxy which converts the HTTP headers to lower-case.   ** Example HAProxy with native HTTP representation (HTX) enabled. This will force headers internally to be stored in lowercase.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup two Atlassian applications behind a proxy  # Setup HAProxy with HTX enabled on one of the application  # Configure Application link between the two applications    h3. Expected Results  The application link can be configured and established    h3. Actual Results  The application link is not configured correctly with """"NETWORK ERROR"""" due to wrong headers.    h3. Diagnosis  Generate HAR file in the process of configuring or loading the Application link page.   * On Application link page load, locate {{/rest/applinks-oauth/latest/applicationlink/<applinkid>/authentication}} and see *UNEXPECTED_RESPONSE* in its body.    h3. Notes  * As per [RFC 2616|https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec4.html#sec4.2], HTTP 1.1 expects headers name to be case-insensitive.   * HTTP 2.0 enforce the requirement for the headers to be all lowercase as per [RFC 7540 about HTTP/2|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7540#section-8.1.2], see JRASERVER-70517    h3. Workaround  On the proxy side, disable the handling of headers to all lowercase:  * Example HAProxy, set {{no option http-use-htx}}  * Example for Envoy - [header_casing|https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_conn_man/header_casing]"""
comments: "Regarding Workaround:    Option http-use-htx was removed in HAProxy release 2.1, because HTX is mandatory now.    Release 2.1 announcement: [https://www.mail-archive.com/haproxy@formilux.org/msg35491.html]    ""... HTX is now mandatory. That's it. As planned, only HTX remains implemented, and with the drop of the   18-years old HTTP engine ...""" [SEP] "We have an issue here with application links to systems that are behind ha-proxy.    As I understand this is an implementation failure of the rest module I honestly urge you to fix that asap. If you need chocolate, ask for that and fix it happily with chocolate in your labial angle :P" [SEP] "Faced same issue" [SEP] "Please make your product HTTP/1.1 compliant.    Or update your documentation to states that your products are not HTTP/1.1 compliant and won't work with modern proxy implementations.     " [SEP] "For HAProxy 2.1, there is the following workarounds that can help with *bogus* software such as the Atlassian products :    [https://cbonte.github.io/

summary: "Privacy issues when commenting in issue via email. "
description: """h3. Scenario:   - Issue is created and customer has communication with Agent   - During Email communication the reporter may forward the mail to another person that was not participant on the issue   - This person now answers to this email with the correct issue ID in the subject and a new issue is created    In System Settings """"Email requests - Public Email comments"""" the setting """"Yes, allow non-customers and external people to comment on a request via email"""" is activated. All Service Desk users are internal and read from the Active Directory.    Considering the user has access to add comments in the issue, comments are still not added in the issue. To get it working here is the workaround:    Create an Organisation and add all the users to this group and also add the Organisation on the issue.     However as it is necessary to put every customer into the same Organisation and add the Organisation to every issue. This would also mean that every customer would be able to read every issue. And this would be no solution due to privacy issues.  h3. Expected behaviour:    We need to find out some other solution to prevent the privacy issues.      """

But for now, *Only* new users who register after the new password policy change.    This feature is very important as Security is an important requirement in most organizations if not all. """

summary: "Project administrator cannot edit Project Lead/Default assignee if they are not granted the Jira Admin global permission"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This bug report is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding bug report|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-60081].  {panel}  h3. Summary    A user who is a member of the Project Role (Administrators) which has been added to the *Administer Project* permission is unable to edit the Project Lead/Default assignee, if this user is not grated the *Jira Administrators* global permission.    According to the [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/managing-project-permissions-185729636.html], the *Administer Project* permission allows any user w/ the permission to edit the Project Lead:  {quote}Permission to administer a project in JIRA. This includes the ability to edit project role membership, project components, project versions and some project details ('Project Name', 'URL', 'Project Lead', 'Project Description').  {quote}  However, with this bug, a user with the *Administer Project* permission is unable to edit the Project Lead. No errors appear in the UI or in the logs.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log into Jira as a user who has *Jira Administrators* global permission   # Go to the user management page, and create a user (referred to as """"testuser"""")   ## Grant this user Jira application access   ## (!) Do not grant this user with the *Jira Administrators* global permission   # Create a new project   # Go to the page *Project settings > Users and Roles*   # Grant the user """"testuser"""" the project role *Administrators*   # Click on the *Edit defaults* button and try to set any user as the project lead   # Verify that this user is properly added to the """"Project Lead"""" field   !Step1.png|thumbnail!   # Log into Jira the user """"testuser"""" and navigate to *Project settings > Users and Roles*   # Click on *Edit Defaults*   # Change project lead to another user and click on *Update*   !Step2.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    The update completes and pop-up window closes. User is able to update changes to Project Lead and you see the Project Lead correctly updated on the page:  !Step5.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    Instead, the following happens:   - The project lead does not change and a blank picture is displayed next to it:   !Step3.png|thumbnail!   - When the page is refreshed, the lead project shows the original user:   !Step4.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    Grant the user who is unable to edit the project lead with the *Jira Administrators* global permission. If you grant the user user with this global permission, they will be able to edit the Project Lead.   Or if that is not possible, another project administrator who has the *Jira Administrators* global permission can make the change."""
comments: "This appears to be a regression. This bug adds to the workload of Jira administrators." [SEP] "See JRASERVER-60081 for other users reporting this same issue on this and other versions." [SEP] "I'm bumping the priority for this issue to ""High"" because it's a pain point for many customers." [SEP] "The issue has been fixed and will be released in Jira 8.12"

summary: "Possibility to customize content appearing in Issue-History and Activity-Streams"
description: """Hi,    regarding our ticket GHS-175766 I would like to open a new feature request. We need be able to hide time tracking information from all other places than the worklog tab (as far as I know this is the issue history and all activity streams).    While we did discus, that it would be good that this information would only be displayed to certain groups, roles or  users with a specific permission, this request is for a new feature to be able to customize what contents are displayed in the history and activity streams.  The possibility to customize would not only prevent users to see this information but also it would prevent the history from getting overloaded with useless information such as several hundreds of created or edited worklogs.     """
comments: "Yes i support with this task"

summary: "Ability to Multi Select Projects while editing ISSUE FILTERS' Permissions or Ability to share same set of Permissions (View & Edit) across multiple Filters"
description: """Hello Product Team,    We are looking for two kinds of improvements in the Saved Filter's Permissions.     h3. *Suggestion # 1*    - If there are a number of filters that require the same set of Edit & View permissions, there should be a way to share the permissions across multiple filters.     For instance, if there are more than 10 saved filters with different JQL queries. All those 10 filters require the same set of view and edit permissions. We have to go to Each saved filter, click on Details, Edit permissions and then add Viewers and Editors. This gets tedious when the number of saved filters increases. There is, unfortunately, no provision to copy over these permissions to all these Filters which will save a lot of time.     (!) Please note that Copy filters functionality does not Copy permissions. So we cannot use this option.      h3. *Suggestion # 2*  While adding the viewers or editors for a particular Issue filter, there should be a way to multi-select values in the projects category. If we are trying to select around 15 projects, each time we are made to select just one value. then click on add viewers and select another value. This is time-consuming and ineffective. In Enterprise set up, we have seen filters having more than 20 project viewers and adding so many viewers or editors to 20 such saved filters makes it even more difficult and time-consuming.         Please feel free to comment or request more information. """
comments: "We agree. We set up the same filters for all of our projects and a way to bulk edit permissions would save us a considerable amount of time. "

summary: "Filtering archived issues by dates return incorrect results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When filtering archived issues by specific dates using the """"From"""" and """"To"""" filters, and inserting the exact period you want to bring issues, the last day is ignored and issues from it are not brought in the results.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup a clean Jira instance and create a sample project with a few issues  # Archive, let's say, 2 issues at February 3rd, for example.  # Go to the *Archived Issues* page and click *Search*. It will bring all 2 archived issues.  # Now, filter it *From: February 1st* and *To: February 3rd*.    h3. Expected Results  The above filter should bring the two issues archived at February 3rd.    h3. Actual Results  The filter doesn't bring any results.    h3. Workaround    If you change the filter to *To: February 4th*, the results will bring the issues from February 3rd. Basically, you always need to inform a day forward in the *To:* filter to bring issues from the date you want as the limit."""

summary: "Periodically clean-up cluster tasks from offline nodes"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Assume some node in JIRA Datacenter is executing long-running task with has cluster-wide status. If at some point progress abnormally stops before the job is complete, then job will be stuck for the whole cluster.  The stuck job is stored in an in-memory cache and is replicated to other nodes when they start. All nodes must be shut down at the same time in order for this job to be removed from cache.    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement the job which periodically cleanup tasks from the nodes that are offline for a long time.       h3. Implemented Solution  A scheduled job described [in this comment|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70585?focusedCommentId=2349285&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-2349285]    h3. Workaround   See JRASERVER-66722"""
comments: "Hi everyone,    I’m glad to announce that Jira 8.5.4, 8.7.1, 8.8.0, and later contain a remedy for this issue.    First, when a node starts up, it will remove any tasks that were assigned to it before the restart. It's a generalized version of the same mechanism Jira uses for cleaning up stuck re-index and user anonymization jobs.    Secondly, there's a new scheduled service that detects offline nodes and clears tasks assigned to them. For this mechanism, a node is considered offline if it didn't write a heartbeat to the database in the past 30 minutes.    You can disable the second mechanism by setting the _""jira.dc.cleanup.cluser.tasks.disabled""_ feature flag. You can [follow our documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html] to learn how to set it.    You can also customize its behavior (how often the service is run and how long it takes for a node to be considered offline) by modifying two properties:        You should refer to the [jpm.xml|https://c

summary: "When stuck operations are removed in DC, users should be notified"
description: """Some long-running tasks submitted by users (e.g. re-indexing, user anonymization, bulk issue edit, etc.) can become stuck in Jira Data Center, when the node that was executing them crashes or is shut down.    Jira can clean up those (since https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66204). However, only a log message is added when such task is cleaned.    It is very likely that the person that submitted the task does not have access to logs or simply does not (want to) monitor them all the time. *Jira should notify the user that submitted the task that is being cleaned up about doing so*, to allow them to react - e.g. by re-running the operation.    This could be an in-product flag message, an email or some other way."""

summary: "Jira should have logging when transactions are rolled back"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Some actions in Jira are run in long transactions - e.g. issue create or issue status transition.    When some code requests a rollback, the whole action is reverted. If that code doesn't provide any logging, it's impossible to check _what code_ and _why_ requested the rollback.    h3. Suggested Solution  Jira should provide an optional, low-level log that would generate a stacktrace, allowing Jira admins and Atlassian Support Engineers to troubleshoot such issues."""
comments: "There is a new DEBUG log in _com.atlassian.core.ofbiz.util_. After turning it on, Jira will log messages like:    "

summary: "Context restriction by issuetype is no longer visible on the Custom Field admin page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In previous versions, the Custom Field admin page (ViewCustomFields.jspa) displayed context restrictions by issuetype. This allowed customers to easily see which Issue Types the custom field was restricted to. As of 7.12, this information is no longer visible, and you must click into *Gear Icon* > *Configure* of the custom field to view the Issue Type restrictions. This makes it difficult to quickly access a custom field's restrictions.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new custom field (or use an existing one).   # Click *Gear Icon* > *Configure*.    # Click the *Edit Configuration* link.   # Select an issue type to restrict this custom field to.    # Click the *Modify* button.   # Navigate back to ViewCustomFields.jspa.    # Note there are no Issue Type restrictions visible on the page.     h3. Expected Results    Issue Type restrictions are visible on ViewCustomFields.jspa  h3. Actual Results    No restrictions are visible. See the following example:     !image-2020-01-31-11-19-07-540.png|width=831,height=30!    Prior to 7.13, this information was available. See the following example:     !image-2020-01-31-11-23-10-134.png|width=969,height=271!  h3. Workaround     It is possible to revert back to the old view, [using a feature flag|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html]: *jira.customfields.paginated.ui.disabled.* This will revert the entire UI back to the pre-7.13 view -  including the removal of the new filter options. """
comments: "I'm ambivalent about reverting the change because it was never that helpful. It listed projects and issue types but with no idea of how they were combined in different custom field contexts. I think I'll stick with the new way"

summary: "Add context restriction by issuetype and list of projects to the rest/api/latest/customFields endpoint"
description: """h4. Problem Definition    The endpoint *rest/api/latest/customFields* only contains a count of the projects to which the CF is restricted, but not the list of the projects themselves. In addition, there is no information returned in the payload about issue type restrictions (types and number of Issue types that the CF is restricted to). This makes it more difficult and time consuming for administrators to determine context restrictions.     h4. Suggested Solution    Return all context restrictions in the rest/api/latest/customFields payload, including: # of Issue types, list of Issue Types and list of projects.   h4. Work Around     Currently you need to manually find this information in the UI by navigating to the Custom Field administration page, and clicking on the Gear icon and Configure to view the restrictions. """

summary: "When hovering on a link to a Confluence page, show a preview of that page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When a Confluence page is linked onto a Jira issue, sometimes it would be helpful to be able to just hover over the link to that page to get a preview of what is on it, rather than needing to click that link and visit the other site to see content there.     h3. Suggested Solution  It would be great if the application link integration could be improved in some manner to permit the sharing of such data here in a preview mode without having to click on the link to see the content      h3. Why this is important  Could offer end-users an improved performance experience, but not having to always wait for to see what's on a page by clicking on it.  Instead you can get a sense of what is there just by hovering there, and if you need to edit it, then you can visit the page.    """

summary: "Atlassian Bot Killer plugin reduces all users session timeout to one hour"
description:
"""h3. Summary The *atlassian-bot-killer* plugin watches every request via a servlet Filter and checks if it has seen the session before. If not it must be the first request for that session. It then stores the original session timeout in the session itself and sets the session _inactivity timeout to be 1 minute_. If the session makes a second request then it gets bumped back to the original timeout of say 5 hours. It follows the same strategy on requests with a known user however to be conservative it sets the inactivity time out to be 10 minutes instead of 1. Context [getting-rid-of-unwanted-http-sessions|https://www.atlassian.com/blog/archives/getting-rid-of-unwanted-http-sessions] Due to wrong logic, all users are considered bots and all sessions have their inactive lifetime reduced, overwriting all normally configured settings. This limits user's session to one minute or one hour. h3. Steps to Reproduce Based on [~kczeladko] steps: # Configure session timeout to 300 = 5h in $JIRA_INSTALL/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/web.xml # Restart Jira # Create page in $JIRA_INSTALL/atlassian-jira/secure/sessionattributes.jsp to check session attributes # Do a 1st visit to Jira http://company.xyz/jira/secure/sessionattributes.jsp # Visit 2nd time Jira - sessionattributes.jsp # Visit 3rd time Jira - sessionattributes.jsp h3. Expected Results User session timeout is 5h (300 min, 18000 sec). * 1st call * 2nd call * 3rd call * Note the change of """"Max Inactive Interval"""": 18000 -> 600 -> 18000 h3. Actual Results * User session timeout is 1h * BotKiller take Max Inactive Interval value read session timeout from web.xml, but still overwrite MaxInactiveInterval to 3600 * Change of """"Max Inactive Interval"""": 18000 -> 60 -> 3600 h3. Notes * Based on comment from [~thomas.weissschuh893360640] [JRASERVER-60844?focusedCommentId=1598784|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-60844?focusedCommentId=1598784&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-1598784] {quote} In BotKiller.java the method isThereAUserInPlay is used to determine if a request is performed by an actual user instead of anonymously. For this it checks if the Jira usermanager is available to make sure this method will not fail during Jira startup or shutdown. To hook into the Jira lifecycle the BotKiller class implements to LifecycleAwareInterface and expects to be managed by the Jira component system to call its onStart method to enable its real functionality. Unfortunately the class is not actually registered as a component in Jira (as indicated by the plugin manager web UI) and instead is managed by the BotKillerFilter class. BotKillerFilter however does never call onStart, so that BotKiller never thinks that Jira has started and therefore classifies all requests as being from bots. {quote} * Related KB [When using a third-party authenticator, user sessions may terminate earlier than expected when idle|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/when-using-a-third-party-authenticator-user-sessions-may-terminate-earlier-than-expected-when-idle-933071468.html] * {{sessionattributes.jsp}} code: {code:Java|borderStyle=solid} <%@ page session=""""true"""" import=""""java.util.*"""" %>

Session attributes

<% Enumeration keys = session.getAttributeNames(); out.println(""""Session ID: """" + session.getId() + """"
""""); out.println(""""Max Inactive Interval: """" + session.getMaxInactiveInterval() + """"
""""); while (keys.hasMoreElements()) { String key = (String)keys.nextElement(); out.println(key + """": """" + session.getValue(key) + """"""""); } %> {code} h3.Workaround * Disable BotKiller Plugin"""
comments: "Can the gathered impact of JRASERVER-60844 also be attributed to this issue?" [SEP] "Hey [~thomas.weissschuh893360640]  I've copied all relevant cases from JRASERVER-60844 so it will affect the gathered impact.     And thanks again for your detailed investigation :)   Cheers. " [SEP] "I'm interested in this too. Right now, we just have the plugin with the dramatic name disabled" [SEP] "FYI we have been running a fixed version of botkiller (with the fix provided to our TAM) for some time and have not encountered any problems." [SEP] "+1  I am interested as well. "

summary: "The kanban board sub-filter experience could be better"
description: """I'm using Kanban on a project with multiple deliverables. We don't use versions for anything. Our team's issues collected in the """"done"""" column forever. The team thought this was weird, since if they're """"done"""", why aren't they disappearing?    The kanban board sub-filter is the answer. The default behaviour for kanban is to show issues with no `fixVersion`. Since we don't use those, they'll collect there forever.    (Aside: what would it mean to """"release"""" a project with no versions? Does that make sense?)    The other weird thing about the filter is its mental model. The correct model: things that match the query will be *visible* on the board; everything else will be *hidden* after a period of time. The problem with this, however, is the context in which the filter is changed. If you're like my team, you're going to change it when your """"done"""" column gets heavy... which means you're going there to *hide* issues, not *show* them. As a result the query can seem a bit nonsensical at first -- I accidentally made everything show up as """"done"""" the first time.    I think one of two things could be done here:    * the description of the kanban sub-filter could be updated to more clearly state that it makes issues visible, and anything that *doesn't* match it will get hidden.  * the field's purpose could be inverted; it would be about selecting issues that are """"done"""". This shift can drastically simplify the mental model for end-users (no double-negatives to think about; you're selecting a discrete set intersection, rather than defining two sets and taking the difference)."""

summary: "Using 'has Epic' and 'is Epic of' with 'linkedIssues()' JQL should return error message"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Users would expect to get outcomes for Epics related to the Issue when using this JQL:    (i) 'QQQ-1' is the Issue Key    However, when using the JQL, it would return no results nor error message:   !JQL1.png|thumbnail!     The expected behavior when using non-existing Link Types would be to receive an error message:   !JQL2.png|thumbnail!   This current behavior can be confusing    *Side note:* Creating a Link Type with """"has Epic"""" and """"is Epic of"""" would yield results should there be any issues using the Link Type. However, this can cause further confusion.    h3. Suggested Solution  Throw an error message when using the JQL to avoid confusing the user. Additionally, the error message could also include the alternative JQL to use.    h3. Workaround   Use the following JQL based on the use case:  * If the Issue with an Epic is known:    (i) Replace <key> with the Issue Key linked to an Epic. If there are multiple Issue Keys, add it in with ','  ** From the outcome, take note of the Epic Link column as that would indicate the name of the Epics  * If you want to display all Epics in a Project    (i) Replace <pkey> with the Project Key  ** The outcome would display the Epics in the Project. Take note of the Epic Name column to know the Epic name and Key column to know the Issue Key for the Epic  * To list out all the Issues linked to a known Epic:    (i) epic1 is the Epic Name/Link  ** The outcome would list out all the issues that have the 'Epic Link' field populated with the stated Epic Name/Link"""

summary: "Due date is shown 1 day less than actual date when importing issues from CSV files."
description: """*Issue*: When customers are importing the data from CSV files, the due date on the issues is displayed as 1 days less than actual dates mentioned in CSV file.     Currently this is due to the server timezone difference between server and client machines. As Jira converts the date/time to server timezone and saves the data, the same is displayed when users try to access imported issues.    *Impact*: When customers would like to merge projects or migrate data from different instances across different geographies, this results in data discrepancies.    *Example*: due date was """"12/30/2019 00:00 +05:30"""" in .csv file, however, after import Jira shows as 29th Dec 2019while browsing through the issue. The server timezone is 10:30 hours behind as compared to client timezone in this case.    *Suggestions for Improvement*: Jira should retain the value of timestamps when using CSV imports or provide users with ability to select timezone for the import so that there data remains consistent."""

summary: "JVM Code Cache memory check is failing in ATST version 1.23.1 with JDK 11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JVM Code Cache memory check is failing in ATST version 1.23.1 with JDK 11 (Open JDK or Oracle)  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira 8.5.3 with JDK 11   # Start it   # Upgrade Troubleshooting and Support Tools plugin to the latest (1.23.1) version   # Clear catalina.out log file under *<JIRA_INSTALL>/logs* directory because the health check may be responding to outdated messages there   # Navigate *Administration > System > Troubleshooting and Support Tools > Instance Health* to check the warnings    h3. Expected Results    It shouldn't show any warning messages for an empty instance because the default size of *ReserveCodeCacheSize* parameter is *512mb* which is quite sufficient.  h3. Actual Results    It shows a warning message like below:   !Screenshot 2020-01-29 at 14.52.09.png!    h3. Note  Possibly related: change since Java 9 [JEP 197: Segmented Code Cache|https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/197]   bq. Divide the code cache into distinct segments, each of which contains compiled code of a particular type, in order to improve performance and enable future extensions.    h3. Workaround    No action required. It's a false message. However, it still worths to search for *CodeCache is full. Compiler has been disabled* message in catalina.out file. If you see this message there;   * Increase *ReserveCodeCacheSize* by following [Full Code Cache Size Causes Jira Server to Crash or Perform Slowly|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/full-codecache-causes-jira-server-to-crash-or-perform-slowly-314447083.html] article   * Stop Jira   * Remove catalina.out log file   * Start Jira   * Raise a ticket to Atlassian Support in case of the same warning message."""
comments: "A new version of ATST (1.23.3) has been released that fixes this problem by removing the health check. We will be re-evaluating the value of this health check as it has caused multiple problems." [SEP] "Removed the Health check in ATST 1.23.3"

summary: "Initial requests cause subsequent requests to be not rate limited for some REST client tools"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira REST endpoint responses contain 'setCookies' header. In case of using REST client that includes cookies in subsequent request by default, rate limiting will no longer apply to these requests because they will contain characteristics classifying them as originating from UI traffic.    The affected clients may include not only client applications like Postman but also rest client libraries for programming languages.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Prerequisite: REST calls performed using Postman   # Start Jira DC Node   # Set rate limit to allow 1 request per 1 minute with burst size 1   # Send a request to rest/api/2/myself with basic authentication   # Observe that it's not rate limited but it response does contain rate limiting headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining - 0 being most significant)   # Repeat the request from 3. within 60 seconds    h3. Expected Results    Request gets rate limited. Response headers contain correct information about rate limiting  h3. Actual Results    Request does not get rate limited and its response does not contain rate limiting information  h3. Workaround    Depending on the REST client app/library used, disabling cookie preservation for subsequent requests causes the requests be correctly rate limited."""

summary: "Ability to export long numeric values stored in Number Custom Field consistently as full integer format across export formats"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, long numeric value in Number Custom Field would be stored as Float in the Database. This allow the value to be stored in Exponent form, which is good in processing terms but not legible to users. When exporting the values, certain formats would export it as Integer while other as Float/Exponent form. The latter form could lead to problems if the tool is strictly reading it as raw/plaintext.    *Replication steps:*  # Create a Number Custom Field and add it into the Screen of a Project  # Create a new Issue in the Project and input a long numeric value (e.g.: 100000000000000) in the Number Custom Field  # In the created Issue, see the value displayed as 100,000,000,000,000  # Export the Issue (including the Number Custom Field) as different formats (CSV, XML, HTML)  # Below are the noticed outcome of opening the exported files:  || | Exported as | Opened as | Rendered ||  || | CSV | text file | 1.0E14 ||  || | CSV | excel file | 100,000,000,000,000 ||  || | XML | xml | 1.0E14 ||  || | HTML | text file | 100,000,000,000,000 ||  || | HTML | web page | 100,000,000,000,000 ||  *Note:* Despite the CSV export would have the numeric value in Exponent form, importing it into Jira would allow it to be stored properly in the database and displayed in full integer format in the Jira UI.    h3. Suggested Solution  Make the Export tool to consistently export long numeric field as full integer format regardless of the format. The export tool should convert the Float format into Integer when exporting it.    h3. Workaround   To avoid this, the long numeric values can be stored in Text Custom Field rather than the Number Custom Field. This would allow the numeric value to be stored as-is in the Database."""

summary: "Make ExplicitGCInvokesConcurrent default for Jira"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  If you use the Garbage-First (G1) garbage collector, we recommend configuring your JVM parameters to avoid the risk of explicit STW (stop the world) GC, which causes Jira to become unavailable while the garbage collection cycle runs.    See [Health Check: Explicit Garbage Collection|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/health-check-explicit-garbage-collection-976778100.html] for more details    h3. Suggested Solution  Add '-XX:+ExplicitGCInvokesConcurrent' by default to Jira.   * It will be used by G1GC and CMS.   ** https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8160392   * It will be ignored by ParallelGC since there is no Concurrent phase for OldGen.     Context:  bq. Other causes than Allocation Failure for a Full GC typically indicate that either the application or some external tool causes a full heap collection. If the cause is System.gc(), and there is no way to modify the application sources, the effect of Full GCs can be mitigated by using -XX:+ExplicitGCInvokesConcurrent   from https://docs.oracle.com/javase/9/gctuning/garbage-first-garbage-collector-tuning.htm#JSGCT-GUID-2428DA90-B93D-48E6-B336-A849ADF1C552    h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "Made -XX:+ExplicitGCInvokesConcurrent a default JVM flag."

summary: "Documentation in updating the Cloud Formation Template of already running stack."
description: """h3. Problem Definition  At the moment, the Cloud Formation Template will be periodically updated but while the template is updated on Github/Bitbucket site, it would not be reflected the already running stack that was deployed using an older template. This means that if there is any improvement or changes made in the new template, it would not affect those stacks.    Such information can't be found in the following:  - https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/administering-jira-data-center-on-aws-938846969.html    h3. Suggested Solution  To have proper documentation on the steps to update those templates in which at the moment would be to deploy the new template and migrate the data from the previous template to the new one.    h3. Why this is important  The user would not understand this until they are not able to successfully update their template due to some changes over the Github/Bitbucket side. Also, when the template does not behave as expected after updating its values."""

summary: "Last Directory Sync time is not updated on every node"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It appears that when one node is doing directory sync when it is completed, the timing (""""Last synchronization at"""" )would only be updated on the node that is doing it. The other node would still use the time when is the last time they are doing the sync    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set up a Cluster with at least 2 nodes.  # Set up the cluster with External Directory.  # Manually sync the directory in one node (Node A).  # After a few minutes, sync it again in the other node (Node B).  # Check back the timing in the other node (Node A) after the last directory sync    h3. Expected Results  The Node will be updated with the latest directory sync timing that the cluster has done    h3. Actual Results  The Node will still retain the timing when the first time the sync happens.    h3. Workaround  Restart the node that the directory sync timing is not updated yet"""
comments: "This behavior can be seen a little clearer in the screenshots below.     !Node 1 Sync Details.png|thumbnail!!Node 2 Sync Details.png|thumbnail!    Node 1 and Node 2 are reporting the details for the last sync that occurred on the nodes themselves. The nodes after a successful incremental sync will update the cache that is shared by all the nodes." [SEP] "Yes, this is one of the 4 or 5 screens that appear to be for the whole cluster but in fact are just for the one Jira node you are logged into. E.g. mail queue failures" [SEP] "I would assume that this bug only relates to update of """"Last synchronization at"" on every node in the cluster and the basics of user cache synchronization (post user directory sync completion ) would still work without any issues between nodes. Right?"

summary: "Inconsistent behavior when creating a Relates to issue link "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Specific to *Relates to* issue link, we have been able to identify that creating the issue link from the two different methods result in two different behavior:   # From *More > Link* is always creating an Outward link.   # From *Edit Issue* screen is always creating an Inward link.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    In a Jira issue, creates two links to the same issue using the *Relates to* issue link   # Create the first issue link from *More > Link*.   # Create the second issue link from *Edit Issue* screen.  (Order is not important, as long as both links are created from these two methods)    h3. Expected Results    Jira should already detect the existing issue link in the database and should not create a reciprocal link.  h3. Actual Results    As for both methods Jira creates the link from different sources and destination, the second link is created.   !bug-issuelink.png!   For more context from the SQL logging:   # First attempt via Edit Issue screen     # Second attempt via More > Link    This will create a duplicate link for the Relates to issue linking because it has the same name for both inward and outward link    And in the DB we see this is actually two different links      h3. Workaround    Currently no workaround is available."""

summary: "Updating custom field value during issue deletion can  cause stuck threads"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Any update actions on custom fields value during the deletion process of the same issue can cause that thread to be stuck on the database. This could cause the delete transaction and thread to run indefinitely or delete operation will be rolledback, this can also cause deadlocks any type of databases.    The issue is therefore never deleted and eventually, you will get a timeout message in the GUI.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install CPrime Power Script Plugin - the script is necessary to trigger custom field update action,   # Create new """"Number Field"""" custom field,   # Create SIL scripts which edit new custom field:   # Create synchronous SIL listener on Issue Link Delete Event   # Create issue A and B,   # Set on both the custom field value,   # Link issue A to B - A blocks B   # Remove issue A.    SIL Script example    h3. (!) This can occur when using any scripted function that attempts to update the parent issue that is deleted, Script Runner etc.   h3. Expected Results    Issue A is deleted.  h3. Actual Results    Issue A persist    *Before database connections are terminated:*   Two stuck connections on a database, originating from the same transaction    !6.png!    Thread dump with similar stack, if you have thready installed you may see the /url:/secure/DeleteIssue.jspa endpoint for each of the following stacks as three separate threads:         *After database connections are being terminated:*   Exception in the logs:    h3. You'll also see locks on the following tables in the DB:     userassociation    customfieldvalue    issuelink  h3. Workaround    Until this bug is resolved, it may be a good idea to not listen on issue link deleted events """

summary: "Performance degradation due to contention in CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore cache"
description: """h3. Summary  Under high load, there could be performance degradation of requests using {{CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore}} cache while loading the data and populating the values. This is due to contention in cache internals. {{CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore}} is used a cache for {{propertyentry}} table. If DB performance is low, that affects loading the values into the cache, increasing the contention.     h3. Environment  * Datacenter     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Apply high load to put {{CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore}} cache under pressure.  # Start updating different elements of the cache (some examples):  #* Update user locale  #* Last login time (see JRASERVER-70468)  #* Dismissed banners     h3. Expected Results  READ and UPDATE operations for a different part of the cache (different keys) can run concurrently without blocking each other.     h3. Actual Results  READ and UPDATE operations for a different part of the cache (different keys) both in WAITING state waiting for the same lock (in the same stripe). So updates are blocking reads for other keys.          h3. Notes  A typical symptom of the problem:   * Large (majority) of the threads are waiting while executing {{CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore.resolve}}   * at the same time and same lock:  ** Updates on some elements of the cache in {{CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore.setEntry}} method.   *** (i) Updates from other nodes from cluster add to the contentions, eg:    ** or loading cache in {{CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore.loadPropertySetData}}. If loading the cache is slow due to DB problems, that will worsen the situation.     +Example from the thread dump:+  *  70%+ threads are in WAITING state in the _CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore.resolve_ method  * Different threads reading different values from the same cache but blocked on the same lock - 0x0000000085534e48:  * _getCustomFieldNameTranslation_ - READ    * _removeDismissFlagForUser_ - UPDATE      Example rows from {{propertyentry}} table:      h3.Workaround  * Way to reduce the impact - increase the number of stripes in Ehcache to significantly low down the chance of collusion, see  JRASERVER-70518"""
comments: "[~ayakovlev@atlassian.com] please fill the description field according to the form. Thanks" [SEP] "h3. Extra context     h4. Details from the thread-dump review  h5. Merged traces for CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore.loadPropertySetData  * all runnable, loading different keys:       h5. Merged traces for CachingOfBizPropertyEntryStore.setEntry  * waiting:      * runnable       h4. propertyentry table  Stats from {{propertyentry}} table:      Example rows:    "

summary: "Increase number of cache stripes for EHCache cache"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira Datacenter uses _EHCache_  as a cache implementation with a local wrapper {{LoadingCache}} (com.atlassian.cache.ehcache.LoadingCache), which is configured by default with a low number of stripes (mutexes).    In some cases, we see a very large contention inside the cache due to cross locking of different keys in the same stripe of the cache.   * Due to low number of stripes (64) there is a high chance of collision in the same stripe for large caches.    * End-users observer a performance degradation. Response time spikes are accumulated because of threads are waiting and not being able to utilize the hardware during that time.     +Details+:  * EhCache protects cache consistency by having a read/write lock for data per stripe (segment). It’s not as granular as by keys (!)  * Reads/Writes to the same stripe will share the same lock, so reads for Key-A might be blocked by writes for Key-B if they are in the same segment.  * In Atlassian cache wrapper we have 64 stripes. The default number of stripes in EhCache is 2048 (DEFAULT_NUMBER_OF_MUTEXES) which will allow higher concurrency and low chance of collision.     h3. Suggested Solution  Increase number of stripes (NUMBER_OF_MUTEXES).     Related code from {{net.sf.ehcache.concurrent.StripedReadWriteLockSync#getSyncForKey}}   {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}   /**       * The default number of locks to use. Must be a power of 2.       * <p/>       * The choice of 2048 enables 2048 concurrent operations per cache or cache store, which should be enough for most       * uses.       */      public static final int DEFAULT_NUMBER_OF_MUTEXES = 2048;      private final ReadWriteLockSync[] mutexes;  ...    """
comments: "Hmm. I would expect that the system was performance tested before the number of mutexes was changed from 2048 to 64. I'm surprised that the problems were not found earlier.    Anyway, do you have a way to tell when the workaround is appropriate, beyond thread dumps and reading the info in the linked issues?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]  It was tested :), but things changed since then: caches became bigger, usage patterns are also different. So previous assumptions are not valid anymore.     bq. do you have a way to tell when the workaround is appropriate  Excellent question!   It's safe to apply the workaround for all Jira instances (we are planning to make it default). It will use a couple of more MBs from JVM heap after that. In a worst-case, from a performance perspective, there will be no changes.  " [SEP] "Got it, thanks. Any idea after which version the workaround would be a good idea?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]  From the cases I see, usage of this cache increased significantly in Jira 7.10 (and 8.x, due to Project archiv

summary: "Officially Support HTTP/2 protocol"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Some parts of Jira application do not fully support HTTP/2 yet.     For example, the header lowercase requirement. This can break the Application link status checks.    h3. Suggested Solution  Have Jira application to support HTTP/2 protocol    """
comments: """the header lowercase requirement."" - what does that mean?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]  From RFC 7540 about HTTP/2:    ""Just as in HTTP/1.x, header field names are strings of ASCII     characters that are compared in a case-insensitive fashion.  However,     header field names MUST be converted to lowercase prior to their     encoding in HTTP/2.  A request or response containing uppercase     header field names MUST be treated as malformed (Section 8.1.2.6).""    https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7540#section-8.1.2" [SEP] "I don't agree with ""Some parts of Jira application do not fully support HTTP/2 yet."". The problem is not with HTTP/2 - even HTTP/1.1 mandates case-insensitive comparisons, which we're not doing. So the violation is a general HTTP standard violation, not a HTTP/2 incompatibility." [SEP] "[~pbruski],    There is still the concern that has been highlighted in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7540#section-8.1.2:  bq. However, header field names MUST be converted to lowercase prior to their encoding in H

summary: "Error when editing an issue with inactive users in multi-user picker field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When attempting to edit an issue where there is a multi-user picker field with inactive users, an ERROR appears and I am not able to perform the edit action.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a multi-user picker custom field, disable the filtering for it (JRASERVER-40963) and add it to create and edit screens.   # Create a user.   # Create an issue and set the custom field to this user.   # Under _Administration>User management_, click on *Edit* next to the new user and uncheck the *Active* box.   # Try to edit the issue created in step 3 (any field).    h3. Expected Results    Should be able to edit the issue.  h3. summary: "Adding log work during transition screen will silently failed for incoming transition if the status has  *jira.permission.work.denied* enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Adding log work during transition screen will silently failed for incoming transition if the status has  {{*jira.permission.work.denied*}} enabled.    As mention in this [KB|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-restrict-log-work-on-a-specific-status-834225982.html] we can restrict user from logging work by adding {{*jira.permission.work.denied*}} parameter to the specific status.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create new simple project with simple workflow as below:   !Workflow - BB_ Project Management Workflow - Jira - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.19.31.png|thumbnail!   # Make sure the workflow has both transition:  #* *TO DO -> IN PROGRESS*  #* *IN PROGRESS -> TO DO*  # Create a new screen with Log work field   !Configure Screen - Jira - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.22.13.png|thumbnail!  # Associate the Screen into the both transition form step 2:   !Workflow - BB_ Project Management Workflow - Jira - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.23.31.png|thumbnail!  !Workflow - BB_ Project Management Workflow - Jira - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.24.15.png|thumbnail!   # Click on *TO DO* status > On Options > Click Properties > Add below properties:   !How to restrict Log Work on a specific status - Atlassian Documentation - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.25.47.png|thumbnail!  # Go to the project > Create one new issue and transition from *TO DO -> IN PROGRESS*.  # In *IN PROGRESS*, transition back the issue to *TO DO* status.  # On Transition screen, add *1 hour* as *Time spent* > Transition back to *TO DO*.   !To do - Jira - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.31.34.png|thumbnail!  # Go to History tab on the issue > The time spent is not added in the JIRA issue history.   ![BB-1] Test issue_ - Jira - Google Chrome 2020-01-20 11.34.37.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  *Time Spent* get added to the History tab and database.     h3. Actual Results  Due to the default Post function for the transition, JIRA will set the workflow status first from *IN PROGRESS -> TO DO* (Step 1) before Update change history for and issue (Step 3) which will result the Time spent did not get updated due to {{*jira.permission.work.denied*}} properties in *TO DO* status:   !Photos 2020-01-20 11.38.33.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Anonymize use activity news"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, JIRA stores all data related to users including inactive users. The data is including emails, reported an issue, assigned issues, log work and more. GDPR (European Data Protection) includes *Right to be Forgotten* option which should allow JIRA Administrator to remove all data related to the user.    Currently, JIRA does not have a feature like this and Administrator will have to manually delete the user or bulk actions to disassociate data from the specific user.      h3. Suggested Solution    Introduce tool in JIRA to disassociate all data from inactive users to an anonymous user.      h3. Workaround    None."""

summary: "Supported platforms documentation update request: Edge browser stable releases"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Under the supported browser section in [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html]  {quote}  h2. Browsers  *Desktop browsers:*     Chrome (latest stable version)     Microsoft Edge      Mozilla Firefox (latest stable version)      Safari on Mac OS X only (latest stable version)  {quote}     !edgestableyn.png|thumbnail!     All the browsers here note (latest stable version), except for Edge.  That is likely because Edge was still in the preview version, but that has just changed this week.  Details in https://blogs.windows.com/windowsexperience/2020/01/15/new-year-new-browser-the-new-microsoft-edge-is-out-of-preview-and-now-available-for-download/    h3. Suggested solution  Since Edge is now out of preview, we should likely change this to have it fall in line with the other browsers.  Or perhaps change all browsers to simply note that we only support the latest stable versions of each.    h3. Why this is important  Reading the supported platforms today makes it appear as if all versions of Edge ever created would be supported within Jira Server.  And that seems misleading to end users.  We should be more explicit about managing that customer expectation here."""

summary: "The incoming mail handler in jira should have the feature to create issues only for unread emails, and also give flexibility for users to configure to process the emails starting from a specific date"
description: """Hi Team,    Users would like to see these additional features to be implemented in Jira Mail handlers.   * The mail handler for jira should have the feature to scan and process only unread emails.   * In addition, offer flexibility for users to configure the mail handler to process the emails starting from a specific date.   * Currently Imap mail handler is deleting the processed emails, which is not working as per documentation. This may be a related issue (Issue Report: JRASERVER-67745).    h4. Business Reason:   * This is resulting in problems where new users would like to use mail handlers but at the same time would stick to use the standard Inbox without migrating old issues.   * Same is applicable when organizations would like to use the old email boxes, but would like to process only recent data post configuration of mail handlers.    h4. Current Implementation:     * Current implementation for incoming mail handler, processing all (read/unread) emails from the inbox (or specific folder) and creates ticket or add comments."""

summary: "Issue Collectors won't work for clients using Chrome 80 which enables new samesite cookie controls"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73683].  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    Chrome version 80 is set to ship in February 2020. With this release there are some new cookie security features coming that will force Chrome clients to enforce a SameSite check policy. Right now, this policy would break all the functionality of issue collectors that appear on separate domains.   If the issue collector could set the SameSite=None in the cookie it appears that it could at least allow the issue collectors to work on different sites.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Inside a Chrome browser, go to address of chrome://flags and find entry called 'SameSite by default cookies' change this from default to Enabled (this feature is expected to ship in official Chrome 80 version on by default, hence this is something we should look to support, even if this specific version is not yet officially supported).   # Then in my Jira Cloud site, I created a new issue collector   # Copy the javascript of the issue collector and enter it into a site on a different domain    h3. Expected Results    Issue collector works as expected.  h3. Actual Results    Issue collector does not work. When the user click the expand button, they are presented with an error asking them to enable 3rd party cookies    !issuecollectorChromeSamesite.png|thumbnail!    The browser console log has an info message of    h3. Notes:    This feature can be enabled on most modern versions of Chrome such as 79.0.3945.117 (my current version) and also within Canary versions such as Version 81.0.4029.0 (Official Build) canary (64-bit).    The expectation though is that Chrome will implement this feature as on by default when a stable version of v80 is released. [https://blog.chromium.org/2019/10/developers-get-ready-for-new.html] explains this further.  {quote}Mozilla has affirmed their support of the new cookie classification model with their [intent to implement|https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/mozilla.dev.platform/nx2uP0CzA9k/BNVPWDHsAQAJ] the *_SameSite=None; Secure_* requirements for cross-site cookies in Firefox. Microsoft recently [announced|https://groups.google.com/a/chromium.org/forum/#!msg/blink-dev/AknSSyQTGYs/8lMmI5DwEAAJ] plans to begin implementing the model starting as an experiment in Microsoft Edge 80.  {quote}    h3. Note on Fix  XSRF token check was disabled for this API:  bq. You don’t have to enable 3rd party cookies to make the issue collector work. We’ve removed this requirement, also dropping some error messages that reminded about it.  from https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-7-x-upgrade-notes-987138245.html  and https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-5-x-upgrade-notes-976781036.html    h3. Workaround   # In Chrome you could go to chromes://flags   # Find the entry for 'SameSite by default cookies'   # Set this to Disabled   # Relaunch Chrome"""

summary: "Stay at the current page after performing an action in workflows, workflow schemes, screens, custom fields pages"
description: """Let's take an example that you have a long list of custom fields which spans across 10 pages. And you want to browse through the list to clean up unused fields. Now you are in page 5, and want to delete a field from that list.     You click the gear button and select Delete (for example). A confirmation screen is shown, and after confirming, the field is deleted, but the list is refreshed and now put me back to page 1 of the list again.     And now, I have to find my way back to page 5 and to the point of the next field after the field that I have deleted, to continue the work.    This happens to any administrator's lists (of workflows, workflow schemes, screens, custom fields ...), in some case I was in the Inactive list section, I will have to even expand the Inactive list again and again after each deletion of an Inactive workflow."""

summary: "Upgrade Tomcat to 8.5.50 to fix CVE-2019-17563 & CVE-2019-12418"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The recently disclosed vulnerabilities regarding Apache Tomcat   * [CVE-2019-12418|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2019-12418]   * [CVE-2019-17563|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2019-17563]    Which affects the following versions:    * Apache Tomcat 8.x from 8.5.0 before 8.5.50        We should bundle a more recent version of Tomcat so that Jira is not affected by this in the future.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Not applicable.    h3. Expected Results   * Not applicable.    h3. Actual Results   * Not applicable.    h3. Workaround   * Manually upgrade Tomcat according to our [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-upgrade-apache-tomcat-version-used-by-jira-879957866.html]."""

Steps to Reproduce   # Start a Jira 8.5.2 instance on Java 11   # Observe the error messages in the startup logs (_catalina.out_)    h3. Expected Results    No warnings are shown in the log  h3. """

summary: "Database connectivity issues break scheduled jobs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Similar to JRASERVER-62072, scheduled Jobs will be lost track of when a database operation fails at the moment the job is claimed by the Scheduler. When connectivity resumes, these jobs will not run again. This seems to be a regression as the Caesium Recovery Job does not bring jobs back to their functioning state.    This seems to affect both Jira Server and Data Center but a DC instance can recover from this after time.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Schedule a Job such as a mail handler.   # Interrupt database connectivity to the application.    h3. Expected Results  Jira will run the job as soon as connectivity is established back between the database and application.    h3. Actual Results  Jira will not run the job until a restart is performed.    h3. Workaround    Restarting Jira will allow jobs to resume their functions."""

summary: "Allow Original Estimates of Parent Task to be calculated against Logged Work in Sub-tasks"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, the way Jira is designed would treat the Estimates from Parent and Sub-tasks as separate entities. It is possible to have the Estimates from the Sub-tasks calculated in the Parent Task, but this can only be done if the Original Estimate is set in the Sub-tasks, which will be added into the Parent Task.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow Estimates from Parent Task to be calculated against the Logged Work in Sub-tasks without setting the Original Estimate in the Sub-tasks. This would allow users to easily track the Work logs using Parent Task's Original Estimate and count it against the Sub-tasks' Logged Work.    h3. Workaround   Create the Parent Task without any Estimates. Then in each Sub-tasks, allocate the Estimates. This would be calculated in the Parent Task as long as the 'Include sub-tasks' option is enabled."""
comments: "To reproduce the case, the steps are the following:   # Create a *task* with Original estimate (2 days). summary: "Filter search for creating board from existing filter does not work in some cases"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some filters with '_' characters cannot be found while searching for filters while creating a board. Ex. """"MY_Filter_search"""" cannot be found while """"MY filter search"""" can be found. It happens for all users.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Boards - > Manage board. Click create board.   # Board from a existing saved filter   Select one of the available filters such as   ma_test_filter   Mya_Test_Filter   pa_test_filter   x_filter_test   zz_filter_test    You will notice that typing ‘mya_’ or ‘zz_’ or ‘x_’ finds the filter. But searching ‘pa_’ or ‘ma_’ or ‘my_’ does not return any result. The endpoint /rest/greenhopper/1.0/savedfilter/list.json?query=MY_&_=1577992092564_ used in finding the saved filters returns only 20 filter results at a time. This number of filter results is hardcoded and not customizable. So if the users have a number of filters with 'MY' stemming word, filters with 'MY_' are never found and it reports no search results found, since the 'MY_ is pushed below the first 20 results.  h3. Expected Results    Search returns the filter matching the pattern.  h3. Actual Results    It throws 'No matches' found  h3. Workaround    You may still be able to search for filter names with no underscore characters by providing the entire filter name, even if it does not appear in the first 20 results. It would require to rename the filters with the underscore to a name without any underscores for those stem words which can have more than 20 results."""

summary: "Implement SameSite policy support"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Starting in February 2020, Chrome version 80 (and later Firefox and IE as well) will start enforcing different requirements on cross-site cookies:  * The new secure-by-default model assumes all cookies should be protected from external access unless otherwise specified. Developers must use a new cookie setting, `_SameSite=None_`, to designate cookies for cross-site access.  * With Chrome 80 will treat cookies that have no declared SameSite value as `_SameSite=Lax_` cookies.    Reference [https://blog.chromium.org/2019/10/developers-get-ready-for-new.html] for full details on the change in Chrome's behavior.    With this new functionality enabled in Chrome, functionalities such as (not limited to)  * _Confluence's avatars_ in the activity stream (see JRASERVER-70419)   * _Issue collectors_ embedded in external websites will stop working - Fixed (/) JRASERVER-70494     h3. Suggested Solution  As a Jira Administrator, I would like to be able to configure `SameSite` policy for Jira (None, Lax, Strict). With a reasonable secure default.     h3. Workaround   * _None_ at Jira side.   * Disable `SameSite` change at Chrome. """

summary: "As a Jira Administrator I want to configure user accounts for integration jobs with low login overhead"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  _Basic authentication_ for REST API call has a performance overhead. There are a couple of things which contribute to that:  * Authentication itself, also a possible roundtrip to LDAP/AD/Crowd server  * Updating (refreshing) user attributes from LDAP  * Updating login attempt data _recordLoginAttempt_   * Updating UserAttrubutes with updateLastLoginTime  * EventPublishing Security event    h3. Suggested Solution  Add option to configure automation (service) user account which has fewer actions during login (auth):  * Doesn't update Login counter for every successful login (possibly every X requests)  * Doesn't update LastLoginTime for every login  * Doesn't update user attributes from LDAP for every login (example {{updateUserFromRemoteDirectory}} method)     +Possible solution:+  OAuth 2.0 authorization via tokenization should allow for users to self generate a token in order to provide a secure and easy way to integrate their apps at a project level. Tokens should be associated with the corresponding user account limiting permission to the platform based upon the user's access.     h3. Note  Make sure that service account doesn't have Jira Admin privileges. In addition to having a security risk, it also has an extra performance overhead due to processing {{removeDismissFlagForUser}}.    h3. Workaround   Try to avoid _Basic authentication_ for each REST API call, instead try to use _cookie-based authentication_     This is how cookie-based authentication works in Jira at a high level:  * The client creates a new session for the user via the Jira REST API.  * Jira returns a session object that has information about the session including the session cookie. The client stores this session object.  * The client can now set the cookie in the header for all subsequent requests to the Jira REST API.     More details: https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/cookie-based-authentication/"""

summary: "As a Jira admin, I want to have an easier way to set the flag -XX:+UseG1GC when using Java 11+"
description: """As a Jira admin, I want to have an easier way to set the flag -XX:+UseG1GC when using Java 11+    h3. Problem Definition  Java 11 no longer supports some GC parameters that were supported in Java 8, such as:      Because of that, we now have the script {{JIRA_INSTALL/bin/set-gc-params.sh}} to detect the currently used Java version and apply the correct flags. However, when using Java 11 we set {{-XX:+UseParallelGC}} by default:  {noformat:title=JIRA_INSTALL/bin/set-gc-params.sh}  #!/bin/sh    GC_JVM_PARAMETERS=""""""""    if [ $java_version -ge 9 ]  then      # In Java 9, GC logging has been re-implemented using the Unified GC logging framework.      # See http://openjdk.java.net/jeps/158 or https://docs.oracle.com/javase/10/jrockit-hotspot/logging.htm      GC_JVM_PARAMETERS=""""-Xlog:gc*:file=$LOGBASEABS/logs/atlassian-jira-gc-%t.log:time,uptime:filecount=5,filesize=20M ${GC_JVM_PARAMETERS}""""      GC_JVM_PARAMETERS=""""-XX:+UseParallelGC ${GC_JVM_PARAMETERS}""""  else      # Set the JVM arguments used to start JIRA. For a description of the options, see      # http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/tech/vmoptions-jsp-140102.html        #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------      # This allows us to actually debug GC related issues by correlating timestamps      # with other parts of the application logs.      #-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------      GC_JVM_PARAMETERS=""""-XX:+PrintGCDetails -XX:+PrintGCDateStamps -XX:+PrintGCTimeStamps -XX:+PrintGCCause ${GC_JVM_PARAMETERS}""""      GC_JVM_PARAMETERS=""""-Xloggc:$LOGBASEABS/logs/atlassian-jira-gc-%t.log -XX:+UseGCLogFileRotation -XX:NumberOfGCLogFiles=5 -XX:GCLogFileSize=20M ${GC_JVM_PARAMETERS}""""  fi    CATALINA_OPTS=""""${GC_JVM_PARAMETERS} ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  export CATALINA_OPTS    Error occurred during initialization of VM  Multiple garbage collectors selected  {noformat}      h3. Suggested Solutions  * Check if another garbage collector has been specified, and don't set *{{-XX:+UseParallelGC}}* in this case.  * No longer set *{{-XX:+UseParallelGC}}* by default.       h3. Workaround   # Manually remove the flag *{{-XX:+UseParallelGC}}* from *{{JIRA_INSTALL/bin/set-gc-params.sh}}*.  # Follow the steps in the KB article [Setting properties and options on startup|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html] to add the following Java flag to *{{JVM_SUPPORT_RECOMMENDED_ARGS}}* in the file *{{JIRA_INSTALL/bin/setenv.sh}}:*  {code:none|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}  -XX:+UseG1GC  {code}    """
comments: "Removed -XX:+UseParallelGC from set-gc-params.(sh|bat), which means that the default GC will be used when running Jira (ParallelGC for Java8 and G1GC for Java11)." [SEP] "For quick reference on changes:  set-gc-params.sh      setenv.sh      So we should *no longer use* the GC args to the JVM_SUPPORT_RECOMMENDED_ARGS    *use* JVM_GC_ARGS instead"

summary: "Upgrade shouldn't be aborted from Jira7 to Jira8 if MySQL Database Collation is utf8"
description: """h2. Problem  [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL - Jira 7.11|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver0711/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-955167744.html] tells that it's needed to set *utf8* collation while having MySQL 5.7 with Jira 7.x whereas [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL 5.7 - Jira 8.5|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7-966063305.html] says that it's needed to use *utf8mb4* which includes a change within *my.ini* file regarding *character_set_server* parameter.  For the customers who have more than one Jira environments on the same database with *utf8* collation and if they want to upgrade one of them (let's say staging environment) to Jira 8, it gives an error regarding the database collation and upgrade is aborted.    h2. Suggested Solution  Ignore the warning and continue to upgrade    h2. Workaround Solution  Setting *character_set_server* parameter to *utf8mb4* within *my.ini* file."""

summary: "Changing commit message does not reflect in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    This issue has been discussed in with Atlassian Support, and Atlassian has stated that there are currently no plans to fix this issue. Creating this bug report so there is a user-facing record that this has been discussed.    The problem:  We have Subversion repositories indexed in Fisheye. When log messages for Subversion commits include Jira issue keys, those commits show up in the Development panel for those issues.    Our Subversion users have the ability to change log messages. If a Jira issue key is added or removed from a Subversion log message after-the-fact, and the revprops for that revision are re-scanned in Fisheye to pull in those changes, Fisheye updates appropriately, but Jira does not always update appropriately.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    More specifically, to describe the behavior we have seen:    If we add a Jira issue key to a log message that has already been pulled into Fisheye, and that new Jira issue already has commits in the development panel, that commit will show up on that issue (though the """"# commits"""" link doesn't update to the correct number)  If we do the same thing, but with a Jira issue that doesn't have any commits associated with it already, the commit never ends up showing on the Jira issue    h3. Workaround  The only workaround for this is to modify the database, which is not a good/safe practice for a production environment.    h3. Expected Results    The issue works as expected.    h3. Actual Results    Described in the steps to reproduce."""

summary: "Apply nested group relationships across all corresponding groups coming from all directories in Jira"

This means these memberships won't be applied for all other directories.    h3. Suggested resolution  We suggest replicating the nested group memberships across groups coming from other directories on the list, so that we have consistent situation."""

summary: "Change the default option to remove an application's configuration when removing its license."
description: """Whenever you remove an application license through the *Versions & licenses* page, you are prompted to select whether you want to remove the application's configuration or not.     The option to remove it is marked by default and that could cause issues in specific situations, such as changing Server licenses for Data Center licenses.    It would be great to leave that option unmarked by default to avoid removing application settings by mistake."""

summary: "Allow me to use JQL to exclude issues from a Jira Portfolio Plan"
description: """Within the Jira Portfolio plug-in, when creating a new Plan or editing the Issue Sources for an existing plan, I'd like the ability to add an _{color:#de350b}inverse{color}_ issue filter. Basically, my idea is that any issue returned by the {color:#de350b}_inverse_{color} issue filter would be excluded from the Plan, even if it would've otherwise been included based on the other Issue Sources in the plan. This would make creation of plans from large, messy projects (especially those migrated from other systems such as Rally) much easier because users would no longer need to manually find and exclude old, never completed """"junk"""" from the Plan as long as they could wite JQL that would return them."""

summary: "As a Jira DC Administrator I want to be able to run scheduled tasks at specific nodes"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In Jira datacenter when a task is scheduled it could be picked up by any node. So there is no task affinity to a specific node.      There are cases when a small Jira instance can handle all web traffic and only some tasks require more resources (due to heave processing required only for that execution).   That makes scaling of the environment hard, since it requires all nodes to be equal.      h3. Suggested Solution  Able to dedicate resource extensive tasks to a non-user facing node aka task affinity.    h3. Workaround   None.  """
