summary: "NodeReindexServiceThread can stop checking messages in a Disaster Recovery cutover"
description: """h4. Problem:    {{NodeReindexServiceThread}} is capable of entering a state where it is no longer checking messages. This can cause inconsistency between JIRA Data Center Nodes.  h4. Customer situation:    In a standby environment, during the cutover, the snapshot was recovered but NodeReindexServiceThread was not checking messages, causing the nodes to get behind.  h4. Environment   * JIRA Data Center    h4. Expected Results    {{NodeReindexServiceThread:thread-1}} *Timed_waiting* in a parkNanos state, sleeping until wake up to check messages again:    h4. What was observed instead:   * Thread dumps showed that {{NodeReindexServiceThread:thread-1}} was *Waiting* in a park state:      h4. Perceived results:   * Health Check detects that index data is behind the Database date and reports the delay, that keeps increasing as {{NodeReindexServiceThread:thread-1}} is not getting the messages.    h4. Notes    As part of Lucene changes in Jira 8.x, code was improved in this area, which makes the problem less likely to occur, eg. {{com.atlassian.jira.index.ha.DefaultNodeReindexService#reIndex}} now catches the *Throwable* exception.  h4. Workaround:   * Stop Jira in all nodes at the same time   * Start the first node. Wait until the snapshot is restored and then restart the node.   * After the restart and after NodeReindexServiceThread:thread-1 updates the index, start the second node   * If there are more nodes, start them sequentially. """

summary: "Improve the incoming email handler to connect to Gmail with OAuth"
description: """In order to connect our Gmail account to the Jira Mail Handler, now we have to set the """"Allow less secure app access"""" to be ON and use username and password to log in, which makes our Google account not secure enough. However, this way won't work anymore in the near future, since Google decided to change their authentication rules lately:    [https://gsuiteupdates.googleblog.com/2019/12/less-secure-apps-oauth-google-username-password-incorrect.html]  {quote}Access to LSAs will be turned off in two stages:   * *After June 15, 2020* - Users who try to connect to an LSA for the first time will no longer be able to do so. This includes third-party apps that allow password-only access to Google calendars, contacts, and email via protocols such as CalDAV, CardDAV, IMAP, and Exchange ActiveSync (Google Sync). Users who have connected to LSAs prior to this date will be able to continue using them until usage of all LSAs is turned off.    * *After February 15, 2021 -* Access to LSAs will be turned off for all G Suite accounts. {quote}  Could you please let me know your plan about this?"""
comments: "Hi Aaron,         Thank you raising this issue. Yes, we are aware of Google's plans to not support Basic Authentication for Gmail from June'20. Google has recently announced that they have suspended their decision to turn off Less Secure Apps until further notice. More details at: [https://gsuiteupdates.googleblog.com/2020/03/less-secure-app-turn-off-suspended.html]    However, the work to enable OAuth support for Incoming Emails is already underway at Atlassian and we plan to launch these updates soon so that customers can move to OAuth if they so wish. I hope this allays any concerns you may have in this regard.    Regards.    Arjoon Som   Jira Senior PM     " [SEP] "I understand that they have moved the deadline back but does Atlassian have a guide to when the new feature will be ready?"

summary: "Prevent Accidental Deletion of Projects"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  There are several cases where users accidentally deleted a project in Jira and needed to recover them from the XML backup. This can be a taxing process to do as Project Import requires the [many pre-requisites|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/restoring-a-project-from-backup-938847691.html#Restoringaprojectfrombackup-prepareJIRAPreparingyourtargetJirainstance] to meet.    In a situation where a deleted project was from 30 days ago and Jira has gone through a number of changes (scheme cleanup, plugin updates), the Project Import to restore won't be as smooth. Work has to be done to restore the old XML Backup to a temporary instance, set it up, then create a new XML backup to be used to Project Import to Jira.    h3. Suggested Solution  * Option 1: """"Delete Project"""" action to archive the project for 90 days (or configurable X days) before deleting the project from Jira  * Option 2: """"Delete Project"""" action will _only_ appear on Archived projects  * Option 3: (Works with Server) """"Delete Project"""" to have Trash bin with retention policy     h3. Workaround   The only way to recover a deleted project is to use Project Import feature from an XML backup."""
comments: "I think many admins use a permission scheme named ""Admin only"" that effectively hides the project from other users. Then periodically go through and delete/archive such projects"

summary: "As an admin, use the Priority Scheme when sorting by Priority using JQL. "

Issue Summary    When using JQL and ordering by Priority, the order issues by their priorities (e.g. descending), they will be ordered according to the importance of the priorities on the global list of priorities.     The order from priority schemes is not relevant for JQL.     This is confusing for users as they would expect the priority scheme order to designate the results when viewing from a project.     This caveat is documented in [Associating Priorities with Projects|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/associating-priorities-with-projects-939514001.html]  h3. Steps to Reproduce  ||Global list (Priorities page)||Custom priority scheme||  | # Urgent   # Highest   # Medium| # Highest   # Urgent   # Medium|    After appending a JQL query with e.g. {{ORDER BY priority DESC}}, the Urgent issues will be at the top of the list (followed by Highest, and then Medium), even if they’re in a project that uses the custom scheme. You can change the order in the default scheme on the [Priorities|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-priority-field-values-938847101.html] page.  h3. Expected Results    When ordering priorities in the context of the project, the order should be determined by what is configured in that project's priority scheme.   h3. summary: "DC index replication delays are affecting end-users due to single thread processing and re-computation of CF values"
description: """h3. Summary  JIRA Datacenter will automatically keep all the copies of the index up to date automatically. This synchronisation is not fully synchronous, but aims for eventual consistency. This means that there is some delay before index changes are seen on other nodes in the cluster.     Each indexing operation writes a row to the database table _replicatedindexoperation_. All of the nodes then look for entries in this table that were inserted by nodes other than themselves. They then apply the changes to their local Lucene index by recollecting the CF values and recreating Lucene document.     see [Lucene Index Synchronization in Jira Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/lucene-index-synchronization-in-jira-data-center-800694054.html] for more details.     In some cases, DC index replication takes longer due to the high volume of changes and expensive Custom Field computations.   This causes the replication queue to grow significantly and delay changes applied to the node. Those delays are affecting end-users.     h3. Environment  * Jira DC  * Large number of Custom Fields    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # TBD    h3. Expected Results  Index replication is relatively fast and doesn't cause problem to end-users (eg, within 5min)    h3. Actual Results  Index replication queue grows significantly. Delay is 20+ min.     h3. Notes  * To check the _replication queue_ size, you can run API call {{/rest/api/2/index/summary}} and check _queueSize_ value.      h3.Workaround  * Reduce the number of CF   * Reduce complexity of expensive CF. """
comments: """expensive Custom Field computations"" - what kinds of things in custom field implementations affect the index replication? I can see that if a custom field has to do more work to display its info, then that will add load to the node where the value is generated, but I would expect the value that is indexed is now just added to the index updates table.     Do some custom field types cause more problems that others?" [SEP] "Hi [~mdoar2],    > what kinds of things in custom field implementations affect the index replication    The re-indexing itself. Currently Jira DC is replicating the index by replaying the indexing operation on each node. The replaying process (on other nodes) is aggregating (like removing duplicates) the ""things"" that needs to be indexed from a short period of time (5sek). The replay of index operation is done by a single thread. Note that the index operations to replay ""are produced"" by all the threads doing any create/update in the cluster (lets say this could be something like 10%

summary: "Create Lucene document based replication for Jira DC for index replication"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JIRA Datacenter will automatically keep all the copies of the index up to date automatically. This synchronisation is not fully synchronous, but aims for eventual consistency. This means that there is some delay before index changes are seen on other nodes in the cluster.     Each indexing operation writes a row to the database table replicatedindexoperation. All of the nodes then look for entries in this table that were inserted by nodes other than themselves. They then apply the changes to their local Lucene index by recollecting the CF values and recreating Lucene document.     see [Lucene Index Synchronization in Jira Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/lucene-index-synchronization-in-jira-data-center-800694054.html] for more details.     h3. Suggested Solution  Replicate Lucene document between nodes.   Thus reduce overhead to recollect the CF values and recreate Lucene document.      h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "In Jira 8.12 we have added to Jira Document Based Replication: documents are only created on the source node (where the action triggering the change happened) and the document is then distributed to all other nodes.     Read more about DBR [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/document-based-replication-in-jira-data-center-1021214730.html]."

Expected Results  At this moment, it is unknown which of the behaviors is the intended one, requires clarification.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When Chrome enforces SameSite=LAX setting, Avatars from Confluence in the Activity Stream are broken"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Starting in February 2020, Chrome version 80 (and later Firefox and IE as well) will start enforcing different requirements on cross-site cookies:  * The new secure-by-default model assumes all cookies should be protected from external access unless otherwise specified. Developers must use a new cookie setting, `_SameSite=None_`, to designate cookies for cross-site access.  * With Chrome 80 will treat cookies that have no declared SameSite value as `_SameSite=Lax_` cookies.    Reference [https://blog.chromium.org/2019/10/developers-get-ready-for-new.html] for full details on the change in Chrome's behavior.    With this new functionality enabled in Chrome the avatars from Confluence users in the activity stream are broken.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # (prior to the Chrome version 80 rollout) In Chrome version 77+ go to """"chrome://flags/""""   # Search for """"SameSite"""" and enable """"SameSite by default cookies"""" and """"Cookies without SameSite must be secure""""   # Navigate to the Jira System Dashboard (with an application link to Confluence already in place)   # View the """"Activity Stream"""" to see amongst other things, updates from Confluence    h3. Expected Results    You will see a list of activity from Confluence with the user's avatar next to it    !image-2019-12-19-18-35-46-402.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    Activity is displayed but avatar is broken   !image-2019-12-19-18-18-30-520.png|thumbnail!    h3. Note  Cookies from Jira doesn't have any *SameSite* policy attached, because of that Chrome forces """"SameSite=LAX"""". That breaks content inclusion since avatars loading requires a valid Jira session to be provided.     Jira (JAC):   !JAC_cookies.png|thumbnail!    Blogger:  !blogger_cookies.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    # On Chrome, navigate to chrome://flags/  # Search for *SameSite*.  # Make sure the following options are *Enabled*:  ** SameSite by default cookies  ** Cookies without SameSite must be secure  """
comments: "I believe this is now breaking the JSD integration with Confluence – Attachments are getting authentication errors/http 302 so the KB article page in JSD is not functional for these users anymore.      {color:#de350b}*Edit:* {color}Yes, disabling SAMESITE cookies in Chrome Flags allows this to work. :("

summary: "Able to edit task dates (start date/due deate) and estimates in filter view without opening the issue"
description: """It would really be beneficial if we could update the """"original estimate"""" """"start date"""" and """"due date"""" from a filter without having to leave the filter to go into the issue.  Often we make date updates in a meeting (not always are the issues moving to the same date), and it would be helpful if this was an option.    !image-2019-12-16-15-02-12-132.png!"""

summary: "Allow now() function to take parameters"
description: """{panel}  *I'm running Jira Server 7.2 ATM.*    *If this is possible in newer versions please let me know. I haven't found that information.*  {panel}       Much in the same way the other time functions like startOfDay() can be given a parameter it would be very nice to be able to use *now(-x)*.     Use case:   * I want to set up an """"issue collector"""" that avery certain ammount of time returns every created issue since in the last specified ammount of time that also matches a series of conditions.    Let's say:   * On monday mornings notifies of every matching issue created since I last left office. That would be automate a task executing every monday at 8h with the following JQL as a filter *""""createdDate >= now(-2d 14h) AND _extra conditions_""""*   * During office hours execute an automate task every half hour with the following JQL as a filter *""""createdDate >= now(-30m) AND _extra conditions_""""*   * From tuesday to friday notifies of every matching issue created since last day. That would be automate a task executing everyday at 8h with the following JQL as a filter *""""createdDate >= now(-14h) AND _extra conditions_""""*     """
comments: "Nevermind... I've just learnt about *createdDate >= -1h*"

summary: "Ability to export JIRA users in a excel sheet"

description: """h3. Problem Definition    It would be nice for JIRA Server to have this feature which will improve RTT for JIRA Server users.     h3. Workaround    There are some examples at Community page, you may want to check if those work for you - [CDN-CloudFront-and-Atlassian-Server-apps|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-discussions/CDN-CloudFront-and-Atlassian-Server-apps/m-p/731230]  - Starting Jira 8.3, Jira Data Center supports CDN. summary: "When using PostgreSQL and passing null character to text custom field during issue creation results with failure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  PostgreSQL database doesn't allow null ({{\0}}) character to be saved as text field ([link|https://www.postgresql.org/message-id/alpine.BSO.2.00.0906031639270.2432%40leary.csoft.net]). This is not correctly handled by Jira's ORM.    The issue by itself should only make it impossible to save the issue with the incorrect data, but it is also a trigger for JRASERVER-70333.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up Jira on PostgreSQL database,   # Create new project,   # Create new text custom field and add it to issue scheme for this project,   # Generate any non empty string which is not allowed by the PostgreSQL, for instance:   ** based on [this|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1347646/postgres-error-on-insert-error-invalid-byte-sequence-for-encoding-utf8-0x0] generate string with empty character,   ** open developer tools in you browser,   ** in console invoke """"*console.log('a\0a')*"""" function(the output will be """"*aa*"""" but for real there will be an empty character between the '*a*' letters),   ** copy paste the output to the custom field,   # Try to create the issue   # Check the log    h3. Expected Results  Either:  * The problem is handled by ORM  * Null character is forbidden on the field validator level  * Null character is removed from the field    h3. Actual Results  Error message presented to the user reads as follows:    In the Jira log files you can find out stack trace:      h3. Workaround    Create custom plugin which will add validator to creation workflow. More info can be found [here.|https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/workflow-modules/]"""

summary: "Bulk issue edit operations are slow and performance is degraded with small EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister cache"
description: """h3. Summary    In JRASERVER-68806 we decreased the size of the {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} cache from infinite to 10. This works well for operations such as CSV export and indexing, which access each issue only once. However, there are some bulk operations that iterate over the same issues multiple times. So far we have identified these:   * bulk issue edit   * changing project issue type scheme (requires issue type mapping) (70 mins for 21k issues)    In these cases, the cache that holds values for the last 10 issues is constantly being reloaded, which makes it practically as there _is no cache_ (see notes below).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # <TBD>    h3. Expected Results   * Bulk issue operations are fast   * DB is not overloaded with requests    h3. Actual Results   * bulk issue operations are slow   * Large number of SQL operations querying {{customfieldvalue}} table:      h3. Notes    Example of thread doing _BulkMigrateOperation_, merged by operations, you can see that cache constantly being reloaded:       h3. Workaround   * Increase the cache size to hold required number of issues (default 10). This is done by introducing the following [JVM startup argument|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html], for example, to increase the cache size to 1000:  """
comments: "h3. Workaround    In case this fix significantly increases the number of DB calls for some scenario, the cache size can be increased via system property    from [comment in JRASERVER-66555|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66555?focusedCommentId=1972883&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-1972883]  {panel:title=file - bin/setenv.sh| borderStyle=dashed| borderColor=#3C78B5| titleBGColor=#3C78B5| bgColor=#E7F4FA}  JVM_REQUIRED_ARGS=' -Djira.customfield.values.cache.max.size=1000 '  {panel}"

summary: "New line character is accepted for Component and Label field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Newline characters are accepted in Component and Label fields when copy-pasting from another text editor.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit the current issue.   # Type in Label and Component field to have a Newline character.   # Jira treats the newline character as a separator between two labels OR components.   # Open a text editor, type in a text with newline characters in it.   # Copy-paste this text to Label OR Component field   # Jira treats this as a single label OR component field   # Tested in all the browsers, observed similar behavior.     h3. Expected Results    Behavior should be the same when user typing in or copy-paste. Jira should or should not accept the new line characters.  h3. Actual Results    Jira accepts newline character when copy-pasting the text, however, does not accept when typing in.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Improve documentation for developers for the Server platform."
description: """The Jira Cloud platform documentation has a few pieces of documentation that help guide developers in a great way, with examples and scripts, this kind of documentation does not seem to be available in Server. Other than users having additional trouble getting some things to work without examples, this can also cause confusion as they might find the documentation for Cloud, and try to apply it to Server, which sometimes could work, but often it will not.    The suggestion is to give the documentation for Server an update to have examples like the following link from Cloud: https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/connect-cookbook/"""

summary: "Add a Remember Me function to the Jira Mobile App"
description: """The Jira mobile app does not have a feature to remember users' credentials. After a certain time, it will ask for the user to log in again, and this can be annoying, considering that other mobile applications have integrated this function in one way or the other (""""remember me"""" checks, fingerprint login, etc).    The request is to add a function to allow the Mobile app to stay logged for long periods of time (like 30 days, for example)."""

summary: "Copying Issue ID in board sprint view > issue detail view copies some hidden text into clipboard ""Actions (Type . to access issue actions)"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Copying Issue ID in board sprint view > issue detail view copies some hidden text into clipboard """"Actions (Type . to access issue actions)""""    h3. summary: "As an administrator I would like to be able to delete change issue history"
description: """As an administrator, I would like to be able to delete the change issue history rows.  One of our customers stored some confidential/sensitive information in the fields.   They cleared out that info now, but it still shows in the change history.    There is a third-party plugin able to accomplish this, but not available for Jira DC.  There are some Script Runners scripts, but they delete all the change history. It doesn't allow to delete/update a particular row on the history.  And finally, DB manipulation to accomplish this.    It would be nice to have an OOB feature, so admins can easily remove rows from issue's history tab."""

summary: "Mention emails should contain issue summary in the subject line."
description: """Presently all other email notifications in Jira contain issue summary in the subject line other than @mention notifications which do not contain issue summary in the subject line at the moment. It would be good to have the issue summary in the subject line of the email notification we get when someone mentions you in a comment.    Enclosing the screenshot of the current mention email notification.   !image-2019-12-12-21-55-40-039.png|thumbnail! """

[See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-41654].   {panel}  Currently Audit log does not log any changes to the global list of Statuses. description: """h3. Summary    The webhook JQL filter configuration goes missing only in Firefox.     Not reproducible on Chrome.     h3. Environment  *  Mozilla Firefox    h3. Steps Support used to Reproduce  # Open Chrome  # Create a webhook with JQL for an issue related event :   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   # Save the webhook  # Open the same page on firefox ( You may need to refresh the page a few times )    h3. Steps Customer provided to Reproduce  a) Enter a query into the webhook and save.  b) On the webhook overview hit reload / F5 in the browser -> query gone in the overview -> go to edit -> it's still there in the edit view.  c) Log out of Jira and log back in -> query gone in the overview -> go to edit -> it's gone there as well.    h3. summary: "Issue level security field is missing in Mobile App for Jira Server"
description: """h3. Summary  Currently the mobile app for Jira, it is not possible to select the issue-level security, like it would be possible in Jira and in the web browser mobile view.    h3. Environment  JSW 8.x onwards    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable [JIRA Issue Security|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/configuring-issue-level-security-776636711.html]  # Create an issue in JIRA desktop version, set security level on a new or existing issue  # Try to perform the same in JIRA mobile app    h3. Expected Results  JIRA mobile application should display the security level field at the time of creating or editing issues    h3. Actual Results  Security level field is missing on new or edit screen in JIRA mobile application    h3. Notes  (Optional - If Necessary)    h3. Workaround  Security level can only be set in JIRA web browser version"""
comments: "Hi,     This is a key field for some of our projects. For us this is a breakdown for these projects. Now I need to find a way to prevent creating issues with the app. Any suggestions?     Laura          " [SEP] "converted as a bug" [SEP] "Opened tickets in our backlog:  *iOS*: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/SM-16075  *Android*: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/SM-16076"

summary: "Jira Project Key must contain Issue ID in Regular expression for smart commits"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  With connected applications with Jira, Smart commit will check only for Jira Project Key set.    If using the default Jira project Key will not detect Jira issues if they have special characters   """"CAPS_DEVSIX-1111""""     Jira Project Keys must contain regular expression as  (([A-Z][A-Z])-([0-9]+))  and be used in Smart commits to detect issue ID instead of the default ([A-Z][A-Z]+)     h3. Suggested Solution  Use [Use Smart Commits|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bitbucket/use-smart-commits-298979931.html] as default.   """
comments: "As the problem description looks hard to understand to my view, I'm attaching the original (less technical) problem description here as well:    We have Jira and Bitbucket integration set up, and in each issue in Jira there is ""Development"" section that shows branches and commits from Bitbucket that are related tot he Jira ticket (namely, they have the ticket ID in branch name or commit message, respectively).  This works fine in most of the cases, but in some cases branches are not picked up by Jira while they still have Jira ids in their names. I suspect it might have something to do with the patterns used for branch look up.    For example, our project in Jira is named ""DEVSIX"", and if branch is named ""feature/DEVSIX-3082"" or ""hello_DEVSIX-1111"" it is picked up by Jira just fine. However, if the branch is named ""CAPS_DEVSIX-1111"", it is not mentioned in ""branches"" of the Development section of the Jira issue." [SEP] "Also adding a bit more detail about the suggested solution since I think it d

summary: "Priority Scheme Default not applied when switching projects during issue create"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a user opens the Create Issue screen, it sets the Priority to the default for the selected Project. If the user then switches to a different Project that's using a different Priority Scheme, it does NOT apply the new default Priority. It simply retains the original value selected.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Pre Conditions:  - ProjectA is using a Priority Scheme that has the Default set to P3  - ProjectB is using a Priority Scheme that has the Default set to P2    1. View ProjectA.  2. Open the Create Issue screen.  3. Confirm it loads with ProjectA as the Project and the Priority is correctly set to P3.  4. Without making any changes, switch the Project to ProjectB.        h3. Expected Results  It should apply the new P2 default.    h3. Actual Results  The Priority remains P3.    h3. Workaround    Close the create window and open again"""
comments: "The error persists also in version Jira 8.5.1."

summary: "Clear NGINX's cache after Jira upgrade to 7.13"
description: """h4. Summary    Please add the following section to [Jira 7.13 upgrade notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-7-13-x-upgrade-notes-957135908.html]    {quote}    h4. Proxy Server    Some odd behavior regarding user sessions is observed where we see a 404 error using REST API, randomly with a different account ID and session ID, etc after Jira is upgraded from 7.6 to 7.13. This is because some proxy servers like NGINX can act as a caching proxy. In order to prevent the user session issues, NGINX cache needs to be cleared during the Jira upgrade process.   {quote}"""
comments: "Personally, I'm going to clear our nginx cache after all Jira upgrades. Why not if we can avoid this kind of hard to debug problem"

summary: "/status returns 'Running' despite the instance being Johnsoned"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    {{CustomisedConfigurationsHealthCheck}} failed, the instance showed Johnson page about modifications in setenv.sh. Any request issued to the instance ended with HTTP 503. Meanwhile, querying """"_/status_"""" endpoint returned """"_Running_"""" status.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Modify the HealthCheck to always fail   # See the Johnson page or issue a REST call   # query the """"_/status_"""" endpoint    h3. Expected Results    When Johnson page is shown and the instance is not working, /status should never return a """"RUNNING"""" state.  h3. Actual Results    h3. Workaround    I don't think there's a stable workaround. Technically, one could ping an endpoint other than /status, but that would possibly break under other circumstances."""

summary: "Jira Server for Outlook"
description: """h4. Summary  At this moment, [Jira Cloud for Outlook (Official)|https://appsource.microsoft.com/es-es/product/office/WA200000056?tab=Overview] is only available for Jira Cloud users but not for Jira Server users. It would be great if we can also roll out this feature for the server users so that it would be possible for them to also create issue from Outlook."""
comments: "There are so many companies whose mail servers stand in in-house environment,    Thus it will be against company rules to integrate Outlook to Jira Cloud since the email which may contain confidential information should not be sent to environment that is outside of the company."

summary: "Enable Due Date field in Issue Collector"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  At the moment we just can use the following fields in the Issue Collector feature:   * Standard Fields: Summary, Description, Components, AffectsVersion, Environment, Priority, Attachment   * Custom Field Types: Date Time, Radio Buttons, Multi-Checkbox, Multi-Select, Number, Select List, URL field, Version Picker, Cascading Select, Project Picker, Single Version Picker, Text Field, Free Text Field    h3. Suggested Solution  Add """"Due Date"""" to the list of fields that can be used in the issue collector.  """

summary: "Workflow transition button cut off in small browser screen if ops.bar.group.size.opsbar-transitions setting is set to large number"

summary: "As a Jira administrador I would like to be able to get push notifications for issue update event not only when the description or attachment is added/changed"
description: """I want to be able to expand the push notifications (Issue Update) so that we can choose if we want to get notified when people use the """"Edit"""" button.     I don't know if this works the same with in-line edits, but a lot of our users rely on the Edit button to make updates.    The fact that the Issue update event only supports *Description* and *attachments* is simply not enough. What about a crucial custom field update? What about a change in priority? We could be missing the timely windows to receive updates while using Jira Mobile."""

summary: "Inserting { } in a table in the comments while in visual mode breaks the table format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Inserting """"{ }"""" in a table in the comments while in visual mode breaks the table format.    Jira automatically adds \   Which breaks the table format.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a table in the comments in visual mode in any issue.   # Add a second row   # Add a text   # Save the comment, you will see the table is viewing as expected.   # Edit the comment, add """"{ }"""" between all the text   #  Save the comment, the table format will break.     (+) A video of the steps to reproduce the issue is attached here  [^bug.mp4]                           h3. Expected Results   Table format does not break and shows the text between""""{ }""""  h3. Actual Results    The table format breaks and Jira adds \   which you can see if text mode, which makes the table to break when adding """"{ }"""" to text  h3. Workaround    Use """"( )"""" instead of """"{ }"""""""

summary: "Allow the Mail server configuration to use outbound proxy settings"
description: """h3. Summary  As indicated in the guide *[How to Configure an Outbound HTTP and HTTPS Proxy for JIRA applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-configure-an-outbound-http-and-https-proxy-for-jira-applications-247857187.html]:*  {quote}This Outbound Proxy configuration only works for HTTP and HTTPs request only. If you are trying to connect JIRA to a Mail Server (Gmail or Office365) or to an LDAP Server, this configuration will not work.{quote}    h3. Suggestion  The suggestion is to improve the Mail server configuration so it works using the proxy like any other Jira function when an outbound proxy is configured."""

summary: "Project Component page is empty when more than one issue is associated with a Component and the user language is Slovak."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Project Component page is empty when more than one issue is associated with a Component and the user language is Slovak.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change the user language to Slovak(slovenčina):   # Create a new component and associate one issue with it. The component page loads without issue:   !image-2019-12-09-23-40-56-603.png|thumbnail!   # Now create more issues and associate with the same component.   # Once two or more issues are associated, the component page is blank:   !image-2019-12-09-23-41-38-073.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    The component page should load when two or more issues are associated with the component and the instance language is Slovak:  h3. Actual Results   - The component page is not loading when two or more issues are associated with a component and the instance language is Slovak   - The following error is thrown in the Browser Console:      (i) Tested for the English language as well and it works without any problem. The problem seems to be happening just for the Slovak language:   !image-2019-12-09-23-42-39-758.png|thumbnail!    The issue is reproducible on version 8.2.x  h3. Workaround   # Navigate to the folder <JIRA_INSTALL>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins   # You should see a file called {{jira-core-language-pack-sk_SK-8.5.0.v20190920192957.jar}}. (!) *Note that the name might change slightly depending on the Jira version that you are using*   # Copy this file to a temporary directory outside of the Jira installation folder   # Change the filetype of this file from JAR to ZIP as well to make it extractable   # Unzip the file into a new folder   # Look for the file that contains the incorrect Slovak translation for the project components page. In this case, the file is {{com/atlassian/jira/web/action/JiraWebActionSupport_sk_SK.properties}}   # Look for the following line:     # Replace it with the following line:     # Save the file   # Using the terminal, navigate to the directory where all the files were extracted from the original jar file   # From inside the directory, run the following command:    # The new Jar file {{jira-core-language-pack-sk_SK-8.5.0.v20190920192957.jar}} will be created including the fixed translation   # Go to the page ⚙ > Manage Apps > Manage Apps, and upload the new jar file using the *Upload App* button    (!) Important Note: after the new Jar file is updated, the changes will only take effect *after the browser cache is cleared*. Another way to check if the translation is working is to either open the browser in Incognito mode, or by using a different browser"""

summary: "Support for Amazon Aurora Database (Jira Server)"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Support for Aurora was implemented for Jira Data Center 8.4.0 according to [JRASERVER-41985|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-41985], but it is still unsupported for Jira Server instances    h3. Suggested Solution    Add official support for Amazon Aurora Databases on Jira Server environments."""

description: """h3. summary: "Add customizable ""privacy"" and ""imprint"" Links in Footer"

We encountered that there is no easy way in Jira to provide that Information.  As you as software provider need to keep us as customer your might assist us keep Jira running by providing at least a simple ability to set custom """"privacy"""" and """"imprint"""" links on every Jira site (I think in that footer where also """"about jira"""" and """"Report a Problem"""" and so on is linked is a good place). summary: "As a Jira Admin, I want to hide the version number showing to unauthenticated users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira displays it's own version number in several pages, including the page markup and application footer.     Some of these pages are available unauthenticated, for instance, the login and logout pages.    Jira administrators may wish to hide these version numbers from users as there may be a requirement to not identify the running product version, particularly to unauthenticated users.   h3. Actual Result    Version number can be found in the page markup, for example:   * {{<meta name=""""application-name"""" content=""""JIRA"""" data-name=""""jira"""" data-version=""""8.5.0"""">}}   * {{<input type=""""hidden"""" title=""""JiraVersion"""" value=""""8.5.0"""">}}   * {{jira/s/...-CDN/dexrks/805000/.../.../_/download/contextbatch/css/jira.login,_super/batch.css}}    And is displayed in the Jira footer (eg):   * {{Atlassian Jira Project Management Software (v8.5.0#805000-sha1:8875eca)}}       h3. Suggested Result      A) By default, hide the version number being displayed on unauthenticated pages (EG, login/logout)    _and/or_    B) Provide a toggle switch in Jira Admin to hide the version number being displayed in other """"logged in"""" areas of Jira   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira have outgoing connections even though disabled Analytics"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira have outgoing http connections even though disabled Analytics  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # _Jira Administration > System > Analytics_ Disabled analytics   # Restart Jira and see outgoing connection through forward proxy or [debug outgoing connections|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-enable-debug-logging-for-outgoing-http-connections-in-jira-952073102.html]      h3. Expected Results    There is no outgoing connection related analytics if it's disabled.  h3. Actual Results   - Every 6 hours, Jira sent request to {{server-notifications.atlassian.com}} to get latest news from atlassian.  {code}  172.20.0.5 - proxyuser [05/Dec/2019:06:13:14 +0000] """"CONNECT server-notifications.atlassian.com:443 HTTP/1.1"""" 200 - """"-"""" """"Apache-HttpClient/4.5.5 (Java/1.8.0_232)""""  172.20.0.5 - proxyuser [05/Dec/2019:06:13:14 +0000] """"CONNECT server-notifications.atlassian.com:443 HTTP/1.1"""" 200 - """"-"""" """"Apache-HttpClient/4.5.5 (Java/1.8.0_232)""""  {code}  {code:java|title=clusteredjob table}  id     job_id                                       job_runner_key                               sched_type  interval_millis  first_run      cron_expression  time_zone  next_run       version  parameters    -----  -------------------------------------------  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ---------------  -------------  ---------------  ---------  -------------  -------  ----------    18514  com.atlassian.whisper.plugin.fetch.FetchJob  com.atlassian.whisper.plugin.fetch.FetchJob  I           21600000         1575514489169  (null)           (null)     1575536715200  2        (null)        {code}     - Jira sent request to {{btf-analytics.s3.dualstack.us-east-1.amazonaws.com}} when start Jira application to get hash code that is used for analysis  {code:java}  172.20.0.5 - proxyuser [05/Dec/2019:05:13:11 +0000] """"CONNECT btf-analytics.s3.dualstack.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:443 HTTP/1.1"""" 200 - """"-"""" """"Apache-HttpClient/4.5.5 (Java/1.8.0_232)""""  {code}    h3. Workaround    Disable {{Analytics Client Plugin}} and {{Atlassian Notifications Plugin}} from _Jira Administration > Add-ons > Manage add-ons > System add-ons_    (!) Please DO NOT disable Analytics Client Plugin if you have JSD installed. It will disable service desk reports plugin immediately."""

summary: "When trying to force garbage collection in Jira ViewMemoryInfo user gets XSRF Security Token Missing error"

summary: "Uploading attachments from the clipboard into a comment with text mode using Safari will fail"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When pasting a screenshot from the clipboard into a comment in text mode using Safari, Jira will display a message stating that the browser is not supported and will fail to attach the file.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Take a screenshot. On a Mac, we can use {{Shift + Control + Command + 3}} to take the screenshot into the clipboard.  # Using Safari, add a new comment to an issue. While using text mode, attempt to paste the screenshot present in the clipboard in the comment box.    h3. Expected Results  A pop up will show up with instructions on how to perform these steps, and asking for confirmation for the screenshot to be uploaded.    h3. Actual Results  The pop up will show without the screenshot present in the clipboard. Additionally, another pop up will be present in the top right corner of the screen instructing to use either Firefox or Google Chrome.    h3. Workaround  # Instead of copying the screenshot from the clipboard, save it to a file and drag the file into the comment box.  # Alternatively, use *Visual Mode* to upload the image. The downside of this method is that it doesn't add the *thumbnail* tag to the image."""

summary: "Add Ability to Configure Bulk Edit, Move and Transition Functionality."
description: """Our organization has applied a number of customizations to Jira Issues which force users to perform specific actions at certain points in the workflow. We've noticed that users are able to bypass those actions by using the Bulk edit, move, or transition functionality. We'd like to see if it is possible to allow Jira system administrators to edit or configure the bulk functionality in some way so that we can add the same customizations to those pages in addition to adding the functionality to the issues themselves."""

summary: "Extend Jira REST API for Changelog History"
description: """As a typical developer, scripter, or user of ETL (extract, transform, load) tools, I need to ability to include parameters specific to Changelog History data in the REST API call so that I can limit the volume of JSON data returned.    We are a large enterprise using the REST API to pull Jira data into Alteryx to perform ETL functions in preparation for loading the transformed data into Tableau for reporting. Currently there is a large volume of Changelog History data returned when using """"...?expand=changelog"""". Quite often the vast majority of changelog data is not needed or wanted, so we need the ability to limit what changelog data is returned in the following ways:   * Changes to a specific field or list of fields (both system and custom fields; could be a single field or a comma-separated list of fields)   * Changes made to a specific field match or are similar to <text>, e.g. ~""""<text>""""   * Changes made to a specific field equal an option or comma-separated list of options for that field   * Changes made to a specific field from one option to another option, e.g. from <option A> to <option B>   * Changes made within certain time frames, e.g.: -1w, EndOfMonth(), before or after <date>, between date range, etc."""

summary: "Extend JQL parameters for Changelog History"
description: """As a typical scrum master, project manager, team lead, I would like the ability to filter the results of my query in a number of ways related to Changelog History data so that I can gather and report on relevant change events, as well as develop metrics to measure and improve.    Specifically I need the ability to use JQL to filter issues by changelog history events in the following ways:   * Changes to a specific field or list of fields (both system and custom fields; could be a single field or a comma-separated list of fields)   * Changes made to a specific field match or are similar to <text>, e.g. ~""""<text>""""   * Changes made to a specific field equal an option or comma-separated list of options for that field   * Changes made to a specific field from one option to another option, e.g. from <option A> to <option B>   * Changes made within certain time frames, e.g.: -1w, EndOfMonth(), before or after <date>, between date range, etc."""

summary: "Option to Limit @ Mention to just people with access to the Project"
description: """We have a large and active user population working on many different projects. It would be nice to be able to limit the users that can be @ mentioned in the Comments to just people with access to the project.   # Makes it easier for the mentioner   # Avoids annoying mentions to things people do not have access to. """

Problem Definition    When an issue has a resolution set, it's not displayed strikethrough in """"Issues in Epic"""" as it's in boards and normal links as seen on the screenshot below:   * !Screen Shot 2019-12-03 at 12.01.21.png|thumbnail!    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement the ability to have the strikethrough in the """"Issues in Epic"""" panel in order to follow the same pattern as the """"Issue links"""" panel.  h3. Workaround    It may be possible to use a front-end workaround, depending on your Jira version.     The following code was tested on Jira 7.13.9 and 8.5.3. Since the Jira front-end code is subject to change at any time, it is possible that this workaround will stop working in the future.     +Workaround Steps+    # Place the following into the [Announcement Banner|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-an-announcement-banner-938846985.html]    # Any Issue in the _Issues in Epic_ panel with a """"Done"""" type status (green) will now be striked through. """

summary: "Incorrect implementation of functions"
description: """Hello,    Soon, we started a collaboration with a old client of us but this time with Atlassian applications, they got a servicedesk and a Jira server license and they had a security audit ofer them, this is what they've got from it and they need to solve these issues asap.         Summary:    Many of the problems that applications are solving require limits to the number of times a function can be used or action can be executed. Applications must be “smart enough” to not allow the user to exceed their limit on the use of these functions since in many cases each time the function is used the user may gain some type of benefit that must be accounted for to properly compensate the owner. For example: an eCommerce site may only allow a users apply a discount once per transaction, or some applications may be on a subscription plan and only allow users to download three complete documents monthly.         Vulnerabilities related to testing for the function limits are application specific and misuse cases must be created that strive to exercise parts of the application/functions/ or actions more than the allowable number of times         Attackers may be able to circumvent the business logic and execute a function more times than “allowable” exploiting the application for personal gain.    If anyone intercept the request when he try to recover any password, the application configuration permit to send unlimited request to server:         [http://prntscr.com/q5jfvr]    In anyone intercept the request, after completing all filed from """"Initiere"""" menu, send multiple request to server with a ticket. The application permit to create another ticket without captcha security code:         http://prntscr.com/q5jg1h    In anyone intercept the request, after completing all filed from """"Maturare"""" menu, send multiple request to server with a ticket. The application permit to create another ticket without captcha security code:    [http://prntscr.com/q5jg85]         Countermeasures:    The application should have checks to ensure that the business logic is being followed and that if a function/action can only be executed a certain number of times, when the limit is reached the user can no longer execute the function. To prevent users from using a function over the appropriate number of times the application may use mechanisms such as cookies to keep count or through sessions not allowing users to access to execute the function additional times.         We would be very glad if this issue will be taken as a security risk.         Best regards,    Cristian L."""
comments: "@closerequest"

summary: "ATL_TOKEN Variable should be hidden from public access"
description: """Hello,    Soon, we started a collaboration with a client that had an security audit over the Atlassian stack products(Jira server,ServiceDesk) therefore, they found out the following issue;         Summary:    If session tokens (cookies, username, sessionID) are used as parameters for some POST or GET requests, an attacker can modify them to impersonate another user and gain privileges or data manipulation.    Quote:     Session token exposed in url when we try to upload a file in comment section    Expectations:    Sensitive session information should not be exposed in POST parameters that can be manipulated.    http://prntscr.com/q5jdog    Thank you in advance,    Cristian L.     """
comments: "@closerequest"

summary: "Requesting password reset link results in error 500 after clicking on the link if External user management flag is set to ON"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Requesting password reset link results in error 500 after clicking on the link if External user management flag is set to ON.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to Jira settings > System > Edit Settings > set """"External user management"""" to ON  # Create a new user in internal directory, do not set the user password while creating. Check the """"Send notification email"""" box.  # User gets the message in the mailbox:   !image-2019-12-03-12-05-48-291.png|thumbnail!   # In case the password reset request gets invalid after 24 hours, user can request new password using {{/secure/ForgotLoginDetails.jspa?username=<username>}}    h3. Expected Results  The reset password request form opens and user gets a fresh link in the mailbox.    h3. Actual Results  User gets error 500 in browser with message {{User login details can not be reset for this JIRA site}}.   !image-2019-12-03-12-08-29-516.png|thumbnail!     The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      h3. Workaround  *Workaround 1*  Administrator can delete and recreate the user and ask them to set the password via """"Set my password"""" button within 24 hours. This way, the user will be able to set the password.    *Workaround 2*  Disable """"External user management"""" flag on General Configuration page."""

summary: "Add ""Rich Preview"" meta tags + image for nice sharing on chat / slack / whatsapp / ..."

My suggestion is to add Rich Preview meta tags to make it look a little better and for example add a Jira / Atlassian logo as image (""""meta og:image"""").    Details on the available / necessary meta tags:    [https://medium.com/@richardoosterhof/how-to-optimize-your-site-for-rich-previews-527ed13a6d69]     """

I can do this by configuring Custom Fields (see JSWSERVER-3922), but this has a drawback of adding an additional text row to each card, making a list harder to scan and greatly reducing the number of issues that can be shown at a time. I would instead ask that custom fields be displayed inline as pill labels, similar to how GitHub displays labels next to issue summaries. (See attached image.)    I believe that this is a very sought after feature. (Refer to the long stream of +1 comments in [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Software-questions/Displaying-labels-on-the-backlog/qaq-p/185391].)    !image-2019-12-02-14-44-03-812.png!"""
comments: "It looks like users can't upload/attach images. Here's a link to a GitHub project whose issues page contains many tagged issues, illustrating the desired inline display of tags after the issue summary: [https://github.com/bazelbuild/bazel/issues]"

summary: "JIRA Cloud needs a vertical scroll bar to access the Formatting Tool Bar when editing"
description: """*Issue*:  * We just transitioned from JIRA Server to JIRA Cloud and the vertical edit scroll bar is gone in JIRA Cloud. This makes editing anything longer than the height of a screen very impractical and time consuming because the top formatting bar scrolls off the top of the page and you have to used the browser window's scroll bar to access this menu, but whatever selections you make are not visible until you scroll back down to where you are making the edits.    *Steps to Reproduce*:    (1) Do anything in JIRA Cloud to get into edit mode, such as adding a comment to a JIRA.    (2) Write a long comment which is longer than the height of your screen.    *Expected*:  * The top text formatting bar remains visible on the screen - I don't really care how you do it, either by making this a floating menu, or by restoring the removed vertical scroll bar.    *Actual*:   * The top text formatting bar scrolls out of sight.    *Workaround*: None.  * With the removal of the """"Text""""/""""Visual"""" option in JIRA Cloud, I cannot even switch to """"Text"""" mode anymore to make manual markup edits to bypass having to use the scrolled out of view formatting menu."""

summary: "Ability to prevent using the same name for Dashboard and Filters"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira only prevents using the same name for Dashboards and Filters to each user themselves. However, other users are able to create new Dashboards and Filters using the same name by an existing Dashboards or Filters created by other users. This can cause confusion should the Dashboards and Filters made accessible to the other users, which would make it look redundant.    Example:  # User A creates a Dashboard called """"Red""""  # User A creates another Dashboard and tries to call it """"Red""""  ** Jira will throw an error that a Dashboard with the name exists  # User B creates a Dashboard called """"Red""""  ** Dashboard gets created. Should users have access to both Dashboards, they would see two """"Red"""" Dashboards made by User A & B.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add a configuration option (can be enabled/disabled) in Jira to prevent using the same name for Dashboards and Filters across the whole instance.    h3. Workaround   None"""

summary: "Failure in issue creation can save issue in corrupted state despite message saying it couldn't be created"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Corrupted or wrong data in custom field can lead to situation where the user is informed that the issue hasn't been created, but the corrupted issue is in fact saved and rollback doesn't happen.    Process of creation of Jira issue consists of 3 steps:   # Issue is created,   # Custom field is filled by data,   # Issue is indexed.    If the second step fails the issue persist in the database with corrupted state and the third step will not happen.    There are 3 problems when the situation mentioned above occurs:   # User is informed about issue not being created, although the issue exist   # User is able to repeatedly click the create button. This creates another corrupted issue, because the form with error message stays open   # Issue is not indexed, which causes it to not show up until next reindex, making it even harder to understand that the issue was created    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use steps to create issue from JRASERVER-70398 (click the """"Create"""" button multiple times)   # Cancel the creation operation and go to the project to find out how many issues are in it   # Reindex project   # Find out that there are new duplicated issues in the project    Other triggers of this issue:   * JRASERVER-65114   * JRASERVER-65762    h3. Expected Results    Message returned by Jira correctly describes the result of issue creation operation.  User is not allowed to create multiple issue with the same form without selecting the """"Create another"""" checkbox.    h3. Actual Results   # Error message presented to the user reads as follows:   !image-2019-11-29-12-51-41-253.png!     Issue with corrupted data is created and will not show up in the issue search nor it will be possible to transition it to other status. The form used to create the corrupted issue can be used to create another issue without any warning.     # In the log files you can find out stack trace:      h3. Workaround    The root cause of the problem has no known workaround currently, however it is possible to prevent the problem from being triggered by properly validating the input.     You can check workaround of JRASERVER-70398 if you are affected by null character being passed to PostgreSQL database."""
comments: "Edit:   Section called ""_Process of creation of Jira issue consists of 3 steps_"" was wrongly numbered. Now there are 3 steps not only 2."

summary: "Filtering out a group from an external directory does not remove it from the shared object (filter or dashboard) permissions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the *Group Object Filter* attribute in the external directory connector is used to filter out a group, this group is deleted from Jira.    However, it there's a filter or dashboard shared with this group, the share permissions are not updated. The shared object will still be seen as shared with the group that has already been deleted from Jira.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Connect an external directory and sync groups   # Share a filter with an external group   # Filter out the group so it doesn't get sync'd   # Verify the group is deleted from Jira by the next sync   # Go to *Shared filters* in the admin part of Jira.   # View the share permissions of the filter.    h3. Expected Results  Deleted group shouldn't show in the filter permissions.  h3. Actual Results  Deleted group still shows in the filter permissions.   +*Notes*+  * If the deleted group is local, it will also be deleted from the filter share permissions.  * The issue also impacts shared dashboards.  * Due to this bug, changing the owner of an impacted shared filter is not possible. The old owner will not be changed and no error message will be prompted to the admin. This behaviour can't be reproduced with shared dashboards.     h3. Workaround   * The filter owner or a user with edit permissions should remove the group from the filter share permissions.   * The groups that were deleted by have filters shared with them can be retrieved by running this query:       * The corrupted share permissions can be removed by running  {panel:bgColor=#ffe7e7}  (i) Take a backup of your DB and stop Jira before running this.  {panel}  """

summary: "ResultSet Closed when doing a lock and reindex on Jira 8.0+"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update - 9 October 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ddfdec|titleBGColor=#ddfdec|bgColor=#ffffff}  We found out that the {{ResultSet closed}} error is caused by a misalignment of various properties that control batch and buffer sizes during indexing.    We aligned these properties in the upcoming Jira 8.14.0 release.    If this problem affects your instance running Jira < 8.14.0, you can apply the fix yourself by setting the following values in [{{jira-config.properties}}|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/edit-the-jira-config-properties-file-in-jira-server-317194938.html]:    To be double sure, you can ensure that your instance does use the default value of {{jira.index.background.batch.size = 4000}}. You can check this on the [Advanced Settings|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-advanced-settings-938847828.html] page (requires Jira System Administrator rights).  {panel}     h3. Issue Summary    Customers are experiencing upon upgrading to Jira 8.0+ from 7.0+ that full lock and re-index runs into {{ResultSet closed}} error from their database (happens on SqlServer and Postgres so far).    The trend from customers is that most of the time their {{dbconfig.xml}} has default max pool size of 20 threads, and Jira 8.0+ has default re-index thread of 20.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Unable to reproduce, requires data and load.  h3. Expected Results    Reindex completes successfully.  h3. Actual Results    Reindex fails with the error message -    h3. Workaround    Current workaround is to increase the default {{dbconfig.xml}} max pool size to be greater than 20 or at least 5-10 more than the reindex thread and restart Jira - rerun the full lock and reindex of Jira.  h3. September 2020 Update:    _We are closely monitoring_ _JRASERVER-70327__, and, as it is a complex issue, we’ve decided to address it in parts. As a first step, we released a fix for_ _JRASERVER-65457_ _that should should mitigate some of the problem. We will continue to closely follow the current ticket and will take further steps based on how it develops._"""

summary: "Remove thread dump from support zip"
description: """h4. *What is the problem*    Jira support zip includes three thread dump but they are not particularly useful due to the following reasons.   * *Lack of time accuracy* - thread dump is a moment in time snapshot of threads activity. To be useful, it must be captured precisely at the time of a problem or otherwise it can be misleading. The current implementation doesn't allow enough timing precision for troubleshooting a problem   * *Lack quantity control* - The quantity of the thread dump must be proportionate to the problem behavior. e.g., troubleshooting 15 second slowdown would require 5 thread dump taken with 2 second interval to give a clear picture of what happened during the 15 second slowdown.     h4. *Impact of this problem*    Administrator often think the thread dump included in the support zip are valid and rely on that to troubleshoot performance problem. This makes the Support team had to explain the difference between the thread dump captured by the support zip, and the manual thread dump generation   h4. *What to improve*    From the support standpoint, there is very little upside from having the current thread dump implementation, and it's probably raised more questions than answer.    Ideally we should improve the tool to address the above gaps, or otherwise remove it from the support zip (and rely on native thread dump tooling which is more accurate)          """

summary: "Calling unimplemented methods in Embedded Crowd endpoints exposed by Jira shall return an error instead of success codes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira exposes embedded Crowd REST resources (i.e. /rest/usermanagement/1) along with Jira REST resources. However, some of these resources are not implemented in Jira, see [NoopForgottenLoginManager|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/8.5.0/com/atlassian/jira/crowd/embedded/NoopForgottenLoginManager.html].    With the current implementation of this class, the REST requests return various codes indicating successful processing (i.e. methods with void return types (see below for an example) return HTTP [204|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/204] that indicated success because *NoopForgottenLoginManager* does not throw exceptions.    However, according to REST conventions, these methods shall return HTTP 405 _Method Not Allowed_ response status code, which indicates that the request method is known by the server but is not supported by the target resource (see discussion of response codes [405|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/405] and [501|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/501].  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a vanilla Jira installation, go *gg* > *User directories* page, click *Jira User Server* on the left-hand side menu and create an application definition. Define a username and password (*username/passw0rd*) at this step which will be used on next step   # send a REST request using username/password above to Jira like below for an existing user (i.e. *admin*) like below  {code:bash}  curl -i -u username:passw0rd -X POST <JIRA_BASE_URL>/rest/usermanagement/1/user/mail/password?username=admin --header 'Content-Type: application/json' --header 'Accept: application/json'  {code}    h3. Expected Results    We receive an HTTP 405 response  h3. Actual Results    We receive an HTTP 204 response, indicating a password reset mail has been sent to this user, however, no such mail is sent  h3. Workaround    Use similar REST API that's provided by JIRA [User REST endpoint|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.5.0/#api/2/user]"""
comments: "In my case this has become urgent and has gone to 'broken' instead of unimplemented.    Artifactory goes to /rest/usermanagement/1/config/cookie which used to return a simple json response, but now returns an error (500).    I can no longer use the Jira User Server with artifactory 6.21.0 because of this ... so for me, this is urgent.    There is a work-around of using an internal user for everyone but it's not security compiant.         While this is 'minor' per definition of the bug policy, I think authentication issues should be anything but.  "

summary: "Make DEBUG com.atlassian.cache.event as default logging"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Due to the following bugs, a cache flush event can cause cascading performance issue on  Data Center.   * https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-61166  * https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66013    As such, we would like to make the cache flush event debug as the default logger in Jira, so that administrator can track the event and correlate with other performance metrics (ie, response time)    The debug logger consist of one line per event as below.      h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Setup  3 nodes data center  * Create 5000 custom field (to exagerate the prorblem)  * Change a customfield configuration   * Notice slow drop on performance across nodes due to cache flush event    h3. Expected Results    The debug log should be enabled by default since it is necessary and is not verbose    h3. Actual Results    The debug is not enabled hence we don't know if there is cache flush event happened    h3. Workaround    Enable DEBUG logging manually (see JRASERVER-66031 for context)  * navigate to Administration > Logging and Profiling   * Click *Configure debug for new package*  * Add  DEBUG on com.atlassian.cache.event  """
comments: "[~vkharisma],    could you please confirm what you would like to have logged at {{DEBUG}} level? To me it looks like the ""was flushed"" messages are already logged at {{INFO}}.    This logging comes from the {{atlassian-cache}} library. In Jira we use the following {{atlassian-cache}} versions:    Both of these versions have logging at {{INFO}}:    From what I can see in the {{[jira-components/jira-webapp/src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties|https://stash.atlassian.com/projects/JIRASERVER/repos/jira/browse/jira-components/jira-webapp/src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties#588-598]}} file, the logging for all but two caches is turned off:    Is this ticket about changing these to {{INFO}}, or am I missing something?"

summary: "Project creation using shared config with Issue Scheme associated with large number of projects returned timeout error"
description: """h3. Summary  * When creating a new project in the GUI or via REST with the shared configuration, the system 'hangs' about 20~50 seconds per active node in the cluster.   * Then it runs into an error {color:#de350b}Request timeout when creating project{color}.   * The project is using an issue scheme that is attached to about *10k projects*.   * The project is created, just the propagation of the data needs so long.    h3. Environment  * Jira Data Center 8.5.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 10000 projects  # Create an Issue Scheme  # Click """"Associate"""" in the newly created Issue scheme  # Select all projects and click Next  # Create a new project  # Select *Create with shared configuration* link under the project create a screen window.  # Select the Existing project from the list, which has been associated with the Issue scheme.  # Enter the project name and the Key and Submit.    h3. summary: "Switching to an issue type with a different configuration is not prohibited"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When attempting to change the issue type to another one with a different configuration (e.g required fields) in view mode or edit mode, the operation is not prohibited. However, in Jira 7, this kind of operation is not permitted which makes more sense to avoid any misconfiguration in the target issue type.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field (Eg, text field).   # Configure the custom field to only apply to Bug issue type.   # Set this custom field *required* in the field configuration.   # Add the custom field to the used screens.   # Create an issue as Task.   # Change the issue's issue type value to Bug in View mode or Edit mode.    h3. Expected Results    You should not be able to update the issue type since Task and Bug are not sharing the same configuration.    I tried to reproduce the issue in Jira 7.13.11 and here are the expected results:   * Tried to edit the issue and change it from """"Bug"""" to """"Task"""" in *edit mode*:    !EditBugEditMode.png|width=250,height=136!   * Tried to update an issue and change it from """"Task"""" to """"Bug"""" in *view mode*, you can see that the """"Bug"""" issue type doesn't show in the list.    !EditTaskViewMode.png|width=249,height=134!  h3. Actual Results    In view mode: I am able to change the issue type from Task to Bug without being prompted to insert/update the required field that I created for Bug.    In edit mode: I am able to change the issue type from Task to Bug, and I can see the required field added to the edit screen to be updated.  h3. Workaround    Currently, the best way to update the issue type, in general, is by moving the issue (as in Jira 7) to make sure no required field/configuration is missed during the transition. """

summary: "After parsing Jira's gadget feed in Confluence, gadgets not listed on ""Select macro"" page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After parsing Jira's gadget feed in Confluence, gadgets not listed on """"Select macro"""" page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Confluence, go to system settings > External Gadgets > Gadget Feeds   # Add new Jira's gadget feed URL in format: {{<baseURL>/rest/gadgets/1.0/g/feed}}    #* example:  [https://jiraserver/rest/gadgets/1.0/g/feed|http://jiraserver/rest/gadgets/1.0/g/feed]   # Once the feed gets added, go to some Confluence page > Click """"+"""" icon > Other macros (e.g. Jira Sprint Burndown)   # Wait about *1 - 2 hours* and then refresh the Confluence page    h3. Expected Results    Jira gadgets are listed on the feed and can be selected in Confluence macros    h3. Actual Results    Jira gadgets are not listed in Confluence macros.    h3. Workaround  Adding specific gadgets' URLs to Confluence will fix the issue.    h4. Environment    Jira that got upgraded to version 8.4.x or 8.5.x  h4. Notes  (!) In some situations, Confluence is not even able to parse the gadget feed from Jira. The following message arises in the UI:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    Temporary workaround to parse the feed is to restart Confluence and then, Jira will be able to parse the gadget feed (but only for some time)  (!) in some case cases, you will be able to add the gadget from the feed, and it will work initially, but it will stop working in 1h (no changes were made during this time on Jira and Conf side).   !Screen Shot 2019-11-20 at 4.06.15 PM.png|thumbnail! """

Cheers,    Pawel Drygas, Jira Server Bugmaster" [SEP] "Hello everyone!    The issue is resolved and available in Jira Server version 8.5.2 and the correspondent Data Center version. summary: "JVM Code Cache memory check is failing in ATST version 1.23.1"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users that have updated to the latest ATST version available in the marketplace, that was 1.23.1, may receive a failed health check regarding JVM Code Cache memory.    The failed health check error shows up regardless of the Code Cache memory size specified in the JVM parameters.    * Due to high false positives, *the health check was removed* in ATST _1.23.3_. See [focusedCommentId=2348387|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70281?focusedCommentId=2348387&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-2348387] from the team.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    This problem has multiple causes, so isn't directly reproducible.  h3. Expected Results    The troubleshooting tools only show up an error in Code Cache memory size when there's an actual problem  h3. Actual Results    An error is displayed even though the code cache size is adequate  h3. Workaround   * The actual ReservedCodeCacheSize may still be too small. Increase it by following [Full Code Cache Size Causes Jira Server to Crash or Perform Slowly|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/full-codecache-causes-jira-server-to-crash-or-perform-slowly-314447083.html] article.   * Then, clear catalina.out log file under *<JIRA_INSTALL>/logs* directory because the health check may be responding to outdated messages in. Here is the sample log:     * Restart Jira node(s)    (!) If there is still a health check warning although ReserveCodeCacheSize is increased, raise a ticket to Atlassian Support for further investigation."""
comments: "Does this affect Jira as well as Confluence?" [SEP] "Hi [~mdoar2], yes, here's the confluence bug: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-59131    However, a fix has also been released to Confluence, so you should be fine by just upgrading the add-on again." [SEP] "Sorry, do you mean that it does affect Jira or does not affect Jira?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2] There are actually two different problems: We recommended a larger code cache size for Confluence than most customers were using, 384 vs 256MB, so the health check was showing up for the current default configuration. Secondly, there was a bug in the health check that it would fail when the JVM is using the segmented code cache. This is the default on JDK 11 and I believe that affected both Jira and Confluence. So the bug does affect Jira, but only if you are using JDK 11." [SEP] "This should be addressed because the Health Check system is handy but not very trustworthy if it lies..." [SEP] "another workaround is to add    to setenv's JVM_SUPPORT_RECOMMEND

summary: " Search using URL to link an issue uses global search variable"

Issue Summary    Search using URL to link an issue uses global search variable i.e. when you are trying to link an issue on the existing ticket and we search using URL for the issue we want link but search results are based on global search instead of the URL    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open existing ticket   # Choose more option > Link   # Using the Jira Issue option on the linking method.   # Under the Issue on the right side put your URL    # Now the result will be based on your previous global search    h3. Expected Results    It should be picking up the right results based on the URL entered.    h3. Actual Results    Search will take keyword from the old global search and result will be displayed    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "DefaultUserPickerSearchService doesn't allow unlimited search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Methods in _DefaultUserPickerSearchService_ take _UserSearchParams_ or _maxResults_ value as a parameter to most of the methods.    The Java doc on _UserSearchParams_ says:    However, inside _DefaultUserPickerSearchService_, there is an explicit check for the null value (and an explicit special value meaning unlimited results) that changes this value to _MAX_MAX_RESULTS_, which currently is equal to 1000:    This means that the only way to obtain more than 1000 users from the search is to explicitly provide a big number as _maxResults_.   This approach is problematic though, as it can cause a high memory usage due to the way Lucene will handle this number - it will eagerly create an array of the passed-in size, so a caller providing MAX_INTEGER value to the method will user over 8GBs of memory.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    N/A  h3. Expected Results    _DefaultUserPickerSearchService_ supports unlimited search, according to the Java doc.  h3. Actual Results    _DefaultUserPickerSearchService_ returns at most 1000 users when unlimited search is requested.  h3. Workaround    A workaround is to explicitly set _maxResults_ to a high number.  *Note that this will use a lot of memory due to Lucene eagerly creating data structures, even if the final search will return 0 results.*"""

summary: "Allow admins to add Custom Events to Batch Notifications"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Since Jira 8, admins are able to choose notifications to be batched, causing a single notification to be sent for multiple events. Currently, this is limited to some system events, and custom events that might've been added by admins are not picked up by the batch notification, generating a notification of its own.    h3. Suggested Solution    Allow admins to add custom events to be picked up by batch notifications.    h3. Why this is important    Customers might have a deeply customized environment, where multiple custom events are used, which might take a majority of the notification noise in the instance. Allowing admins to choose which custom events are batched can help with reducing the noise."""
