summary: """Browse Users"" Global Permission to give a clear distinction that it is not affecting Service Desk Customers"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  For Service Desk Customers, the ability to search the other customer in the project through the Customer Portal (Share Request and Approval) is configured in each project which is:  - [Choose who customers can share requests with|https://confluence.atlassian.com/servicedeskserver/managing-access-to-your-service-desk-939926273.html#Managingaccesstoyourservicedesk-Choosewhocustomerscansharerequestswith]    In comparison with standard JIRA Users or Users that has Application Access, such config is controlled in """"Browser Users"""" Global Permission. But, the description of the permission does not specifically mention that it will not affect the customers which can be confusing for admin that expect to add the customer group to this permission to be able to search for users.    h3. Suggested Solution  To specifically mentioned in the description that it will not affect Service Desk Customers like:  bq. Ability to select a user or group from a popup window as well as the ability to use the 'share' issues feature. Users with this permission will also be able to see names of all users and groups in the system. *This does not affect Service Desk Customers*, for this, check the documentation [Choose who customers can share requests with|https://confluence.atlassian.com/servicedeskserver/managing-access-to-your-service-desk-939926273.html#Managingaccesstoyourservicedesk-Choosewhocustomerscansharerequestswith]."""

summary: "Ability to edit options associated with workflow transitions in same browser tab"
description: """+*Description:*+    Every time when users edit a workflow transition to modify/add/remove conditions, triggers or post-functions, a new browser tab is opened. Once the changes are published, current page goes back to the workflow screen. However, it doesn't refresh the original screen.    Regardless of what user does in the old tab becomes invalid and throws an error upon clicking links to suggest refreshing the page. However, users can continue with newly opened tab without any issue.     To continue editing, user has to click the edit button again within the new tab, which will open one more tab and the chain continues. When users have multiple tests to do on workflows and won't close tab on every edit, they end up in large number of tabs (redundant efforts) and sometimes even browser crashes.    +*Steps to Reproduce:*+    1) Edit a workflow  2) Click on a transition  3) Click on one of the options links like conditions, triggers, post-functions.  4) Please go through the video attached, which is more elaborative.    +*Expected Results & Improvements:*+    1. Options associated workflow transitions should open in the same tab, as the old tab becomes useless after a typical workflow edit; even in the case of discarding any changes. Once the changes are published, they should reflect.    2. *An alternate option*, the new tab being opened for edit should be closed after the Publish or Discard buttons are pressed and the parent tab to be refreshed. To achieve this, we can think of providing these edit options as popup windows, once changes are made it automatically refreshes the workflow.    One other improvement, would be to make the workflow edit screen go back to the same view originally selected (in this case the Diagram view).  """

summary: "Timestamps in the database should reflect the time the transaction was executed/committed"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Timestamps in Jira are generated at the application level. When DB update transactions are executed by threads, any timestamps in transaction will already be generated and won't reflect the time the data was """"committed"""" to the DB.     It is impossible to compute deltas between two states of the data based on the timestamp info in the DB.   h3. Suggested Solution  All Jira timestamps should reflect when the transaction was """"committed"""".   h3. Workaround  None"""
comments: "We will be happy to see this implemented. Use cases and more details available at PS ticket [https://getsupport.atlassian.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/30/PS-49355]"

summary: "Jira is trying to do index recovery on invalid issue data coming from Lucene indexes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira is trying to do index recovery every time Jira is restarted in Standalone mode by reconciling the issues difference against DB.   It has been noticed since Jira 8 was released, that logging for index recovery happens after every Jira restart.   The recovery process does kick in even if the indexes are not corrupted and tries to recover issues with null keys and thus is returning zero ids.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira 8.5.0   # create a project and start creating issues, transition one of them and add comments.   # run a background re-indexing on the instance.   # check the background reindexing logs.   # enable debug logging on the package: *com.atlassian.jira.ofbiz.IssueGenericValue* in the log4j.properties   # do a restart and check the logs.    h3. Expected Results    Jira should have a clean start up, index recovery shouldn't be trying to deindex issues with zero ids.   Background reindex number should match up without any discripancy.  h3. Actual Results    On start up Jira is trying to deindex issues with zero ids even though the database doesn't have any issues with zero ids.   The below messages are shown in the Jira start up logs in atlassian-jira.log:    On doing background reindexing, the numbers reported doesn't seem to match.   Lucene API reports more issues that what is actually existing in the database:     * (!) note:       * actual number of issues - _15_ (vs 21 in logs).    h3. Note:    The start-up logs don't appear all the time, though it could be reproduced eventually especially with large instances.   Doing a Full foreground re-indexing helps to restore the index into a more tidy state, but changes to Jira issues will eventually cause indexes to have entries with null issue keys.  h3. Workaround:     A background reindex should resolve the JQL search issues, but the ongoing task is to periodically clear the null values out of the DB.     You can clear these out of the database by either:   # running the integrity checker   # deleting the null entries from the database:   As always make sure you test these types of changes in a lower environment and make sure to back up the DB prior! :)    *validate the issues:*    *delete the issues*  """
comments: "This issue is duplicate of  JRASERVER-61339,  updated field loaded from Lucene is truncated to seconds, so it is different to DB field" [SEP] "What is the difference between JRASERVER-70242 and JRASERVER-70248?    Are the same problem? JRASERVER-70242 is fixed in 8.7 while JRASERVER-70248 is fixed in 8.12.    We have over 48,000 false positive warnings in our log file. That's a lot of noise. Which Jira server version should I upgraded to?"

summary: "Restrict access to users through the User Interface"
description: """As a Jira administrator, I would like to have the ability to restrict users' access to the instance. This would be extremely helpful for customers that have large and heavily customized environments. It is also worth mentioning potential benefits during upgrade processes:    Without this feature, users can log in before the administrators have the opportunity to re-apply modified files/content. Additionally, it is important to deny users access to the instance before plugins are correctly upgraded and post-upgrade checks have been performed, and functionalities have been tested and are ready for use. Furthermore, once the upgrade is completed, the integrations between Jira and other applications will be reestablished and that can be another potential source of issues in the future.    Also, for instances that have users worldwide in different timezones, it can be extremely difficult to program a maintenance window where no users will be accessing Jira. In some cases, these maintenance windows are used to perform system administration tasks such as the installation of new plugins, extracting data for year-end processing or auditing by regulatory agencies.    The current options available, outlined in the article: [Preventing users from accessing Jira applications during backups|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/preventing-users-from-accessing-jira-applications-during-backups-938847685.html], are not feasible for some of our customers, as it can require network and security skills that may not be available when this functionality is needed.    Because of that, this feature could benefit these customers allowing them to restrict access to the instance through the User Interface, similarly to the Full Reindex process (that is already available), not allowing users to alter data inside the instance while the process is executed.  """
comments: "Exactly! Upgrades that need plugins reinstalling are hard to do accessing just one Jira node. It would be so much easier to have the load balancer and reverse proxy all working as intended and just lock out users until everything has been tested"

summary: "On startup, Jira logs ""Issue X has empty field 'num'. Returning null for 'key'."""
description: """h3. Issue Summary    During JIRA start there is code that attempts to recover JIRA's issues index to consistent state. As part of this process issues that are indexed but are no longer present in database are removed from index. There is a bug in that code that tries to load issues keys while they are not populated.    This bug manifests itself by following warnings in log:  {code:java}  2019-11-08 00:41:13,706 Caesium-1-2 WARN ServiceRunner     [c.a.jira.ofbiz.IssueGenericValue] Issue (id=0) has empty field 'num'. Returning null for 'key'.  {code}  or   {code:java}  2019-11-07 16:05:39,909 Caesium-1-1 WARN ServiceRunner     [c.a.jira.ofbiz.IssueGenericValue] Issue (id=479837) has empty field 'num'. Returning null for 'key'.  {code}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create or delete some issues in Jira.   # Restart Jira.     h3. Expected Results  Index recovery should not generate errors.  This was fixed in version 6.4.5 but has resurfaced.     h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      h3. Notes  * (i) This is safe to ignore those messages in the context of Index recovery (/)   * In another context it might indicate a problem.     h3. Workaround  To temporary resolve, run the database integrity checker and then a full Lock & Rebuild of the index.  """

summary: "Combining schema modification DDL commands for improved performance"
description: """When upgrading Jira the entityengine component (""""DatabaseUtil"""") will create new columns for added entity fields.  Currently this will create a new """"ALTER TABLE"""" statement for each new column.    Most database engines have to rewrite the whole existing table to add new columns.  Performing this complete table rewrite for every column take quite some time.  Jira could create all new columns in a single SQL """"ALTER TABLE"""" so the database can perform the whole table upgrade with a single table rewrite.    For example the """"Issue"""" entity (a fairly central concept for Jira) received three new fields in our upgrade from 7.6 to 8.5 and therefore had to be rewritten three times."""

summary: "Performance degradation of create project with per context fields configuration"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * Project copy based on existing configurations performs very slow or fails when there are custom fields configured per project context.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Prepare 30-40 custom fields and configure them for selected project.   # Create number of issues for this project (10-20)   # Provide constant background load (20 - 30 users) that retrieves random issues (via REST service /issue/\{key})   # Run concurrent project create operation based on existing configuration (with per context fields) on behalf of ~7 users      h3. Expected Results    New projects are crated successfully , there are no timeouts/errors for background  issue read operations    h3. Actual Results    Threads locked on getContexts operation should be observed within thread dump    h3. Workaround    Run create project operation during maintenance break with administrator's exclusive access."""

summary: "Issue Link names should be different from their descriptions"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Jira Server*. Using *Jira Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73372].  {panel}    h3. Issue Summary    With the addition of the JQL function [issueLinkType|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/advanced-searching-fields-reference-939938743.html#Advancedsearching-fieldsreference-issuelinktypeIssuelinktype], Jira Server now has the ability to let users search by the issue link's Name, Outward Description, and/or Inward description. The problem with this is that by default the issue link type 'Blocks' also has an outward description by the same name 'blocks'. Since these are the same, you can't actually only get back results with that outward description. Instead you get back all issues with that link name, which includes both outward ('blocks') and inward ('is blocked by').   By default only the Blocks link type has a description with the same value as the link Name. The suggested solution would be to have new Jira sites use a different change the outward link name from 'blocks' to something different such as 'blocking'.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Link one issue to another with the blocks issue link type   # Search JQL with:      h3. Expected Results    Would expect to only get back a single issue with that outward description of 'blocks'  h3. Actual Results    You actually will get back all the issues of that link name (both inward and outward description) because the term that is being searched here matches the link name.  h3. Workaround    You can edit the link descriptions so that they do not exactly match the name of the link type. For example you could change the outward link name from 'blocks' to something different such as 'blocking'. This would at least permit users to make these specific JQL searches based on the description and not the name."""

summary: "Context path duplicated when Jira performs self-request for Activity Stream Gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Context path is duplicated when Jira performs self-request for Activity Stream Gadget, if the subdomain contains the same word as the context path. For example: {{jira.domain.com/jira}} or {{something.domain.com/something}}    The symptom looks similar to CONFSERVER-53081, but in this case the instance does not need to be linked to a Confluence instance.    h3. Environment  # Jira 8.3.4  # Unable to reproduce in Jira 7.13.11    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Let's say Jira instance hostname is {{jira.domain.com}}  # Set context path to {{/jira}}, so the base URL would be {{jira.domain.com/jira}}  # This can also be reproduced if the hostname is {{jira-something1.domain.com}}, and context path is {{jira}}  (!) If you are testing this with a dummy domain, add the domain/IP into both your PC and Jira server's host file to point to the Jira server's IP address    h3. Expected Results  # Activity Stream Gadget works fine    h3. Actual Results  # Activity Stream Gadget displays the following error:   !image-2019-11-08-14-41-20-166.png!   # In the HTTP access logs, it shows that the self-request fails:      h3. Workaround  # Avoid having the same context path as the subdomain.  OR  # Add a rewrite rule in Tomcat to remove the duplicate context path  #* Add {{<Valve className=""""org.apache.catalina.valves.rewrite.RewriteValve"""" />}}  into server.xml just above {{</host>}}  #* create a file named {{rewrite.config}} in {{Jira_install/conf/Catalina/localhost}} path  #* Add the following into {{rewrite.config}}  """

summary: """Plan your upgrade"" tool is not recognizing Jira version 8.5 as an Enterprise Release"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using """"Plan your upgrade"""" tool it does not recognize Jira version 8.5 as an Enterprise Release. It only recognizes Jira versions from 7.13.1 until Jira version 7.13.9 as an Enterprise Release.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install or access any Jira version from 7.13.1 until Jira version 7.13.8. (I am using Jira version 7.13.3 as an example for this case)   # Go to Administration --> Applications --> Plan your upgrade.   # Click on the """"Upgrade to"""" Jira version and look for Jira version 8.5, you will not see an """"Enterprise Release"""" next to it.   # Scroll to Jira version 7.13.9, you will see """"Enterprise Release"""" next to it. This proves that Jira recognizes Jira version 7.13.9 as the latest """"Enterprise Release"""".    h3. Expected Results  When using the """"Plan your upgrade"""" tool it should recognize Jira version 8.5 as an Enterprise Release.  h3. Actual Results  It only recognizes Jira versions from 7.13.1 until Jira version 7.13.9 as an Enterprise Release.    *  !1.png|thumbnail!   *  !2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Allow specifying first name and last name when creating users"
description: """Right now the values for first_name and last_name are automatically generated from a supplied """"displayName"""" value by splitting it by space, which causes invalid values to be saved into the database, e.g., displayName """"Charlie of Atlassian"""" is being converted to the first name """"Charlie"""" and """"of Atlassian"""" as a last name, or """"Doe, Jane"""" is converted to """"Doe,"""" being a first name and """"Jane"""" being a last name."""

summary: "Configure fields option in a screen is unable to display all fields if too many fields are associated to the screen scheme"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    *Configure fields* option in a screen is unable to display all fields if too many fields are associated with the screen scheme.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Associate many fields (let's say 30 fields) to a screen scheme.   # Perform any operation in Jira which uses the particular screen, (i.e Create Issue screen)   # Click on *Configure Fields*    h3. Expected Results    All the fields are displayed in the configure fields menu.  h3. Actual Results    It is not possible to scroll down to view all the fields.   !1280_720.PNG|thumbnail!    (i) Lower resolution will have a worse effect, despite the [minimum supported display resolution|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver084/supported-platforms-979404246.html] is 1024 x 768.  h3. Workaround    Reduce the number of fields associated to the screen scheme."""

summary: "Adding the same group from different directories has unexpected results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding the same user group from local and active directory causes unexpected results. Variation on this can occur using the same name but with one letter cased differently.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # add at least one active directory in Jira's User Directories  # create a local group, for example 'Production'. Add a global share permission to the group, in Jira.  # create a group in the added User Directory, for example 'production', add some users to it    h3. Expected Results    The groups are seen separately in Jira and function independently. Global share permission can be removed or added to each group.    h3. Actual Results    The groups are merged. The local user memberships are no longer visible.   Also some functionalities like removing global share permissions can no longer be controlled (as removing fails silently).    h3. Workaround    Global share permission can be removed from database directly, for example:      Notes    see also:  # https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66032 (when using same group name)  # https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66599 (when using same group name + space)"""

Steps to Reproduce   # Create a dashboard   # Add Sprint Health Gadget and Filter Results Gadget to the dashboard and configure to display issues in different statuses   # When displayed next to each other gadgets use different colors for the same statuses     h3. summary: "Adding Issue fields to batch notifications emails"
description: """h2. Preamble    As of JIRA 8.x, the way the email velocity templates are modified is different from the previous JIRA versions.   Before JIRA 8.x, customers could include an *Issue Field* by following this article: [adding custom-fields to email|https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/adding-custom-fields-to-email/]    As of JIRA 8.x, the method no longer works as the structure of the batched notifications is now different.   A new [method|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/adding-custom-fields-to-emails-batched-notifications-968669988.html] exists to add *Custom Fields* to the Velocity templates, but the method is not suitable for *Issue Fields*.    The purpose of this feature request is to track customers interest in being able to add *Issue Fields* to batch notifications and also to document a workaround that customers can implement if adding system field to document is a must in the meantime."""

Simple check with  #** Cоздано - the bug. """"C"""" is from the English keyboard.   #*** !English C.png|thumbnail!   #** Создано - correct. """"С"""" is from the Russian keyboard.  #*** !russian C.png|thumbnail!     h3. """

summary: "Two Dimensional Gadget shows filter with special characters eventhough the filter is using the custom field ID for searching when clicking on the results."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    As special characters are not stored in index, you are unable to perform searches if a JQL query includes a special character. Due to that, as an alternative, we use the custom field ID instead.    For example, we cannot search for custom field 'Client[s]' as it will return as Clients. So we amend it to look like this :    However, if using the JQL query above for a Two Dimensional Gadget, then, clicking on one of the results, it will instead show filter with special characters when clicking on the results with the error """"Field: 'xxx' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a filter that includes the ID of a custom field that has a special character. For example, the custom field 'Client[s]'.         # Save the filter as test.   # Go to a dashboard. Add the 'Two Dimensional Filter'. Choose the filter 'test'. Add the field 'Client[s]' as an X-axis and Assignee as Y-axis.   # Once the results is displayed, click on one of the results.    h3. Expected Results    Jira will redirect us to the filter :    h3. Actual Results    Jira will instead redirect to the filter with the following JQL Query.    The error  """"Field: 'xxx' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it"""".will be seen.  h3. Workaround    Remove the custom field with special character in the JQL query"""

Steps to Reproduce   # copy an image, and past it into the comment section   # *resize* (click and drag) it before sharing with the customer   # image is broken in issue view   # image is broken in portal view    !JCPphmPy77.gif|width=371,height=579!  h3. Expected Results    Image is view-able from customer portal  h3. Actual Results    Image is broken when viewing from the customer portal  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Include SSL configuration within Jira Admin section"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    SSL configuration for Jira is unnecessarily cumbersome and fiddly. We can resolve part of that by including the Configuration Utility and keytool functionality within the Jira UI.     h3. Suggested Solution    HTTPS configuration should be part of the Admin section in JIRA itself, not in a separate utility.      h3. Why this is important    The UI-based instructions in [Running Jira applications over SSL or HTTPS|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/running-jira-applications-over-ssl-or-https-938847764.html] require a sysadmin to download a third party utility (Portecle) and then attempt to modify the {{server.xml}} using the Jira Configuration Utility. For this reason, many admins prefer to simply use keytool and modify the {{server.xml}} manually, but this can still be a somewhat taxing and error-prone process.     Including the necessary libraries and select functionality within the Admin section of Jira would reduce the burden on admins. """

summary: "Ability to filter audit reports by using api/2//auditing/record/ REST"
description: """As an administrator, I would like to have a parameter for this endpoint to only return audit records for a specific project."""

summary: "Define group membership for the Invited Users"
description: """h3. Definition  At the moment, you can only define Application Access when you invite users in Jira which is not efficient in a way that for some Projects, the Browse Project permission is restricted to some groups, therefore, the users won't be able to view the projects assigned to them.    h3. Suggestion  Have the possibility to define group membership when inviting Jira users. """
comments: "It will be very useful as in many cases we invite external users for specific Projects, so we manage the permissions with different groups from the Jira default groups in order to separate users to have access only to their projects."

summary: "Allow to have a similar layout as the 'Create Issue' pop-up when accessing the 'Create' button via new tab or window"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  If clicking on the 'Create' button, it will prompt the 'Create' issue pop-up:   !JiraCreate.png|thumbnail!   In this screen, the Project and Issue Type field are in the same screen.    However, accessing the 'Create' button via new tab or window, it will access the CreateIssue!default.jspa URL which result in this:   !JiraCreate2.png|thumbnail!   In this screen, the Project and Issue Type field are in a separate screen than the other fields. This would require to click the button in order to access the other screen, resulting in a 2 steps process.    h3. Suggested Solution  Have a configuration to display the CreateIssue!default.jspa URL in a similar layout as the 'Create Issue' pop-up    h3. Workaround   In the new tab or window, click the 'Create' button or press the 'C' key to prompt the shortcut."""

summary: "When Jira is running against Java 11, TLS 1.3 requests can cause httpclient threads to become blocked"
description: """{panel:title=Solution|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#3c78b5|titleBGColor=#3c78b5|bgColor=#e7f4fa}  This problem is caused by a bug in the Java runtime: _JDK-8214418 HttpClient falls in running with 100% cpu usage after an error signalled on channel_ (Backported as [JDK-8241054|https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8241054])    The fix has been verified to be available in AdoptOpenJDK 11.0.8 - [https://github.com/AdoptOpenJDK/openjdk-jdk11u/commit/8d1b63a4db2c6348a97b3cf45bd4d2caa7cad6b5]  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    When Jira is running against Java 11, TLS 1.3 requests can cause httpclient threads to become blocked  h3. Steps to Reproduce    This is possible with any app that uses TLS 1.3 in an environment running Java 11.x. We were able to reproduce this using the Jira Mobile app Android client.   # Have Jira Mobile app plugins enabled   # Have Jira Mobile Android users register/sign in via the mobile app    h3. Expected Results    Users can sign in and use Jira. Everything functions normally.   h3. Actual Results    All http threads eventually become blocked on one I/O Dispatcher thread. The instance stops accepting incoming connections. After a restart, the instance stays online/available for some period of time before all connections are consumed again.  h3. Symptoms    Top output on the Jira process shows one CPU core running hot on an I/O dispatcher thread. Other cores are silent/idle.    Analysis of thread dumps shows over 150 threads are blocked on an I/O Dispatcher:    Further analysis of the {{http-nio-xxxx-exec-xx}} threads may help identify the exact app, plugin, or package(s) stimulating the problem. In this example, we saw a large number of http threads pointing to the Jira Mobile app.    Heap dump analysis may be required.  h3. Solution    Upgrade AdoptOpenJDK to version 11.0.8 which has this issue solved.       h3. Workaround    Disable TLS 1.3 on the JVM running Jira   # Stop Jira   # Add the following option to Jira's [startup options|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html]:       # Start Jira    Alternate workarounds:   - Disable the app, plugin, or module found to be the cause of the stuck I/O Dispatcher thread   - Install Java 8 and point Jira to that JRE/JDK instead of Java 11"""
comments: "We have run the investigation on this and it's probably caused by https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8230785. I'm lowering the priority since we cannot fix this in a product and there is a reasonable workaround for it.   " [SEP] "We have tested Jira with AdoptOpenJDK 11.0.8 and the issue is gone. JVM upgrade solves the problem.  I'm closing this issue since there is nothing else to be done."

summary: "Adding special character to title of newly created ticket mangles Slack notification"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If you create a Jira task with the summary """"test <> test"""", and then have Slack alert on it, it generates a notification with a correct, but un-pretty URL.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Slack integration to notify on issue creation for a project   # Create a new issue using the summary """"test <blah> test""""    h3. Expected Results  Slack notification should read:  $user has created a Task ISSUE-1: test <blah> test    h3. Actual Results  Slack notification reads:  $user has created a Task <$BASE_URL/browse/ISSUE-1/atLinkOrigin=XXXX...XXXXISSUE-1: test <blah> test    h3. Workaround  Refrain from using the following characters in an issue Title '<' '>'"""

summary: "The Jira configuration tool will incorrectly edit the server.xml"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Jira configuration tool will incorrectly edit the server.xml  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Download Jira 8.4.2 zip   # Extract   # Create home dir   # Edit line 72 of server.xml to add proxy params to it   # Run config.sh and configure the home dir and the DB   # Save the config    h3. Expected Results    Server.xml is unmodified  h3. Actual Results    The {{server.xml}} has been updated and the connector now enables SSL, and added keystore params. Also, we get an error when saving the config warning about the missing Keystore Path as shown in the screenshot below.   (!) If we do the same through the console, we get no warnings/errors and the server.xml file is overwritten as reported, adding the keystore parameters    !sup_tool.png|width=567,height=467!  h3. Workaround    If we do the same through the console, we get no warnings/errors and the server.xml file is overwritten as reported, adding the keystore parameters"""
comments: "If you do this via the console, then the same thing happens on Jira startup instead of while being in the config tool. It bricks an instance and causes customer / partner to have to troubleshoot logs and understand that the server.xml is misconfigured."

summary: "Edit, set, change jql definition or hide the system filters"
description: """Hi,    I know there is already some suggestions about the system filters and hiding. Mainly the reason is that when you do not use resolution then you get the error for example for """"Open Issues"""". I think this is a bug and should be fixed.    But my suggestion is about the system filters itself. And I would like not only hide them but also edit or set or change its jql definition. So the administrator can customize them.    The main reason is the user experiance with Jira so the user do not see any messed filters that are not relevant even that make some error!    If the user open Filters then there is a list of system filters that are not relevant for custom projects.    Pretend for example filter """"Done issues"""". It has this jql definition:    """"statusCategory = Done order by updated DESC""""    and for our project this is irrelevant because our issues that are """"Done"""" are not ony that with """"statusCategory = Done"""" but also that with custom field """"MyTypeClose"""" set to """"Fine"""".    So our jql definition should look like: """"   statusCategory = Done and MyTypeClose = Fine order by updated DESC    If there will be possible to edit the definition of system filters then then a lot of users can do anything with filters and they can fix its error with resolution and also others can set their own filter for all system filters. This would be very nice because then the Jira will again be more customizable and nice :).    So my solution and suggestion is:   1) please make editable the system filter defintion   - either on the whole jira   - or on the project level    2) please make possible to hide the system filters   - as in here: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-63317         Please others see this suggestion and if you would like to have it vote for it.          Best Regards"""

summary: "Can't access page with Jira Software sources download links"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When going into https://my.atlassian.com/download/source/jira-software or https://my.atlassian.com/download/source/jira  the page shows:    Oops! Looks like something out the back is broken.    Ref: 7917d5e3-b361-4e12-8e08-289789ccce05    h3. Expected Results    Other Jira source distributions pages work fine:    https://my.atlassian.com/download/source/jira-servicedesk  https://my.atlassian.com/download/source/jira-core  The page for Jira Software is expected to work the same way as the 2 pages listed above.     !Screenshot 2019-10-23 at 17.38.20.png|thumbnail!     h3. summary: "Recently Created gadget language does not follow the language chosen by the user in their user profile."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Recently Created gadget language does not follow the language chosen by the user in their user profile. It will also not follow the system language, it will only follow the language that is used during the initial setup of Jira.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira.   # Use 'Nederland' as a default language.   # Log in as a user with 'English' as their language.   # Create a Recently Created gadget on the dashboard.     h3. Expected Results    The gadget language will follow the system language/user profile language.  h3. Actual Results    The gadget language will follow the first language used when installing Jira.  h3. Workaround    If you want the gadget language to follow the system default, you can follow the steps provided [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/change-locale-for-jira-server-314450433.html]."""

summary: "Better error when user has no access rights to ou=AccessControl attribute"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Incorrect error message when Jira not able to fetch groups because the LDAP user does not have the access right in LDAP to read the ou=AccessControl attribute. This is the stack trace seen in the logs """"        h3. Suggested Solution    Provide better error if the LDAP user has no access right to read the ou=AccessControl attribute """

summary: "Ability to remove the 'Update For' line in mail notification when using batched notifications"
description: """h3. Summary  When batched notifications feature is enabled, the email received will have the line 'Updates For' in the subject. For example :       As this can be quite tedious when viewing dozens of letters in the mail client, it is difficult to quickly find the mail notification that you need."""

summary: "Using the Visual editor does not honor img tag attributes that use percentage characters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Images added inline to either a description or a comment in Jira can be configured (in the text editor) to use specific image attributes such as height, width, valign, etc.  While these can use specific number of pixels, they can also be setup to use a percentage of the space, such as width=""""100%"""".   When you set this up in the text editor, it works just fine.  However any subsequent edits of that description/comment from any user using the visual editor will inadvertently drop the % character entirely from that img tag, leaving the image sized in pixels rather than percentage of the field on the screen.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a Comment to an existing issue and include an image citation with a width=100% attribute, such as:    (this could also be done in the Description field as well)     # Click Save and notice that the image shows full width of that comment/description field.   # Click the pencil to edit the comment. Make sure you are in the Visual edit mode.   # Make any type of change (but don't modify the image).   # Click Save and notice that the image is no longer scaled to the full size of the field.   # Click the pencil again and go back to Text editor mode   # Notice that Jira removed the """"%"""" character after the """"100"""". You can reinsert the """"%"""" to fix things, but the problem will recur every time you edit the comment using the Visual tab.    h3. Expected Results    The image should remain unchanged and still appear as scaled to the full width of that field (100%)    !scales.png!       h3. Actual Results    The image now is exactly 100 pixels wide instead of 100% the width of that field in the users browser.    !scales.png|width=100!  h3. Notes:   * Does not appear to affect the Text editor at all, only the visual editor   * Credit to [~dave898462241] for reporting this bug in https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Software-questions/quot-width-100-quot-on-image-tags-gets-broken-on-subsequent/qaq-p/1211588    h3. Workaround    None currently exist. Should a work-around be found, this will be updated to reflect this change."""
comments: "Thanks for moving this forward. It's a fantastic, under-documented feature. Frankly, I'd like to make it default behavior. But the % problem is quite troublesome since I frequently update posts for completeness and clarity."

summary: "Pasting text in comment on Visual mode doesn't work properly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When there is a @mention in a comment in *Visual mode*, pasting text from the clipboard will insert it after the @mention and not in the right place (right after the cursor).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open comment section in the ticket and click the *visual* mode.   # Add some mention.   # Write text.   # Use paste (CTRL+V) to insert text at cursors position.    (Attaching a video to reproduce)    [^Jira-8.2.5-comment-paste-issue.mov]  h3. Expected Results    Text gets inserted at the cursor's position at the end.  h3. Actual Results    Text gets inserted after the mention, before the already written text with the cursor at the end. (see attached video)  h3. Workaround   * Use text mode.   * Before pasting the text (CTRL+V), click to set the cursor manually at the end. In this way, you make sure the text will be inserted in the right place.    h3. Note  This is not reproducible in 8.5"""
comments: "Closing this one as it's a duplicate of JRASERVER-69037."

summary: "If a custom field contains a special character, when searching for a custom field using will result in the error ""Field xx does not exist or you do not have permission to view it"". This can be misleading as special characters cannot be searched anyways."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a custom field contains a special character, when searching for a custom field using Advanced Searching will result in the error """"Field: 'xxx' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it""""    This issue is due to JRASERVER-66244, however, I do think that the error thrown is misleading and should be replaced with an error 'Special characters cannot be included in JQL searches'  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field with a special character in it. For example, 'Client[s]'.   # Head to the Issue Navigator. Click on *Advanced Searching*.   # Create a JQL filter as follows.       # This error will be shown.   !image-2019-10-28-17-00-55-413.png|width=1174,height=90!    h3. Improvement    Instead of showing the error """"Field: 'xxx' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it"""", you should instead change it to """"Special characters cannot be included in JQL searches. Search cannot be executed. Please remove the special characters from your search query""""  h3. Workaround    Search using the custom field ID instead."""

summary: "Clicking the label in chart gives different number of issues in filter than what the gadget shows"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On gadget view if we have 1 ticket for """"a"""" label and click on it, JIRA navigates us to filter that contains 4 tickets (all tickets in filter), not 1. As well, as we have 3 tickets for """"A"""", we will be navigated to the same filter with 4 tickets.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 3 issues and put the Label as """"A""""  # Create 1 issue and put the Label as """"a""""  # Create a Gadget to display the issues with the Label """"A"""" & """"a""""   !Gadget View.PNG|thumbnail!   # Click on any Label, we would expect to see the issues that match the number. However, will be shown the total instead   !Filter Results 1.png|thumbnail!    !Filter Results 2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Seeing the issues that match with the number was shown in the Gadget    h3. Actual Results  Get the total issues of the Labels instead    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Upgraded Dashboard | Deck it out"
description: """It would be great to have automatic roadmaps, delivery on sprints, some status reports, or whatever, automatically generated on the dashboard."""

summary: "File upload via More > Attach files not working intermittently"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Intermittently, the More > Attach files option doesn't upload the selected file.    Issue can be reproduced with JIRA 8.2.4 and JIRA 8.3.3.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access JIRA application via Chrome internet browser   # Browse to an issue   # Attach a file via More > Attach files option    h3. Expected Results    File should be uploaded.  h3. Actual Results    File is not uploaded, intermittently.    *Investigation Done*   * No issue drag-and-drop or upload the file via 'browse' option in the attachment panel   * Problem is not isolated to specific file type or file size   * Issue is reproducible with Chrome 76.0.3809.100 and 77.0.3865.90 (regular and incognito windows)   * No error or log message indicative of problem with the file upload   * Attached is the HAR file capturing the 2 failed attempts, follow by a successful file upload – [^fileattach.har]   * Reviewing the HAR file, I noticed that the main difference between the failing and successful requests is the Blob URL. The successful request created a Blob URL and proceed with the file upload, but the failing request doesn't:    !blob.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    Drag and drop/ browse and select the file at the Attachments panel."""

summary: "Jira displays wrong GMT on User Profile when under Daylight Savings"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira can display the wrong GMT information if the User Time Zone is under Daylight Savings.    h3. Issue Impact  There's no impact aside from wrong information being displayed to users on their Profile. Pages and blogs are saved with the right time information.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Login to Jira as any user.  # Click on the Profile Picture in the right upper corner.  # Click on Profile.  # Click on the Settings Tab.  # Observe that Eastern Time is displayed as GMT-0500 even though from March to November it is supposed to be GMT-0400 due to DST - This is one example, other timezones may be affected as well:    h3. Expected Results  Jira should be considering the DST offset to display the right GMT on that screen when the user timezone is affected by DST.    h3. Why this happens  Jira is leveraging the [Java Time Zone getRawOffeset|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/TimeZone.html#getRawOffset()] method. It does return GMT-0500 as EST Timezone. It then parse it to display the information to users on screen"""

summary: "Rollback issue creation if the Issue Created notification fails to be sent/delivered"
description: """When creating an issue, Jira says the issue was successfully created, but if the Mail Server blocks the issue created notification because of something like the attachment size, Jira doesn't rollback the issue creation.    It would be nice if Jira doesn't confirm that the request is received until it's actually received."""
comments: "This would be a huge help in both confidence building and managing issues as they happen (which sometimes are many)    Doing this manually in the midst of an outage and then checking an hour later only to realize it was never sent is a problem. Our mail system blocks most, but not all attachments depending on the size. So it would be much better if the reporting tool connected directly or at least checked the success rather than erroneously saying all is well when it is, indeed, not.    Thank you."

summary: "Allow Jira administrators to hide the X-AUSERNAME header"
description: """h3. Description  Currently, Jira passes the X-AUSERNAME header to identify when a request is sent from a valid user account or an anonymous user. Under certain circumstances, this may be undesired to expose the user name on the request.    h3. Suggestion  Add a configuration option to allow this header to be hidden from the requests."""

summary: "Jira mobile app removes GEO location data from the image"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * When an image file is attached via Jira mobile app. The GEO location of the image is not retained when it is downloaded from the Web version.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach any image that has GEO location data as shown below via Jira mobile app.   #*  !Geo location1.jpg|thumbnail!    # Download it from the WEB and the data is removed as shown below:   #*  !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  * Data is retained    h3. Actual Results  * Geolocation data is removed from the image    h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "This fix is important for a client of mine, as this bug prevents them for adding a new jira project with more than 250 new users to their Jira." [SEP] "This bug were found om SERVER not Cloud."

summary: "Add an option to the Jira Core Mail Handler to create new users without using a license, when linked to a Service Desk project"
description: """h3. Issue    When a Jira Core Mail handler (configured in ⚙ > System > Incoming Mail) is setup with a Service Desk Project and with the """"Create users"""" option enabled, the creation of new users from email will fail if no default application access it set in the Jira instance.    The following error will be thrown in the Jira Incoming Mail Logs:      The behavior is intended by default, since the Jira Core Mail Handler is not designed to be used with a Service Desk project, as it expects users to have application access to create emails (but Service Desk customers are by design users without application access).    Due to the fact that the Jira Core Mail Handler contains more configuration and features than the Service Desk Mail Handler (configured in *Project Settings > Email Requests*), some customers prefer to use the Jira Core Mail Handler rather than the Service Desk Mail Handler.    h3. Suggestion    Add some configuration to the Jira Core Mail Handler to allow the creation of customer-type users (users without application access), when it's configured with a Service Desk project and when no default application access is configured."""

summary: "Add an option to the Jira Core Mail Handler to create new users without using a license, when linked to a Service Desk project"
description: """*Issue*    When a Jira Core Mail handler (configured in ⚙ > System > Incoming Mail) is setup with a Service Desk Project and with the """"Create users"""" option, the creation of new users from email will fail if no default application access it set in the Jira instance.    The following error will be thrown in the Jira Incoming Mail Logs:      The behavior is intended by default, since the Jira Core Mail Handler is not designed to be used with a Service Desk project, as it expects users to have application access to create emails (but Service Desk customers are by design users without application access).    Due to the fact that the Jira Core Mail Handler contains more configuration and features than the Service Desk Mail Handler (configured in *Project Settings > Email Requests*), some customers prefer to use the Jira Core Mail Handler rather than the Service Desk Mail Handler.    *Suggestion*    Add some configuration to the Jira Core Mail Handler to allow the creation of customer-type users (users without application access), when it's configured with a Service Desk project."""

summary: "High CPU usage from IO Dispatcher threads when using Java 11"
description: """{panel:title=Solution|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#3c78b5|titleBGColor=#3c78b5|bgColor=#e7f4fa}  This problem is caused by a bug in the Java runtime: _JDK-8214418 HttpClient falls in running with 100% cpu usage after an error signalled on channel_ (Backported as [JDK-8241054|https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8241054])    The fix has been verified to be available in AdoptOpenJDK 11.0.8 - [https://github.com/AdoptOpenJDK/openjdk-jdk11u/commit/8d1b63a4db2c6348a97b3cf45bd4d2caa7cad6b5]  {panel}    h3. Issue Summary    High CPU usage is noticed on I/O Dispatcher threads.   Jira is up on Java 11.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    No particular steps to reproduce, issue happens randomly even when Jira is not under load.  h3. Expected Results    I/O Dispatcher threads shouldn't be using lots of CPU.  h3. Actual Results    At least One I/O Dispatcher thread is using up 99% CPU.   You can see from the below Thread dump snapshot that the I/O Dispatcher is stuck in runnable and is using a lot of CPU time:    The CPU data also shows the same thread is using more than 90% of CPU:    h3. Note:   * The problem is related to a bug in the httpcore library that ships with Apache Httpclient used by Jira to communicate with remote services: [https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/HTTPCORE-583].   * The bug in httpcore was fixed in *4.4.12*.   * The bug is triggered when Java 11 is used in combination with a version of httpcore that has the problem.   * Jira ships with multiple versions of the httpcore library, main Jira 8.4.2 uses *httpcore-4.4.9.jar*, while some other bundled plugins use other versions:   ** jira-workflow-sharing-plugin-2.2.3.jar-embedded uses httpcore-4.4.1.jar   ** atlassian-remote-event-common-plugin-6.1.0.jar-embedded uses httpcore-4.4.10.jar    h3. Solution    Upgrade AdoptOpenJDK to version 11.0.8 which has this issue solved.  h3. Workaround     * Downgrade your JDK version from jdk 11 to jdk 1.8, Java 1.8 is not known to cause this problem.   * The issue is observed with Java 11 that uses TLS v1.3. TLS v1.2 is unaffected by this. It's possible to force Java 11 to use TLS v1.2 by adding the following JVM parameter: {{-Dhttps.protocols=TLSv1.2}} to your startup properties."""
comments: "related - https://webtide.com/openjdk-11-and-tls-1-3-issues/ " [SEP] "We have run the investigation on this and it's probably caused by https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8230785. I'm lowering the priority since we cannot fix this in a product and there is a reasonable workaround for it.  Also check https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70189 - the issue is not present when TLS 1.3 is disabled." [SEP] "This looks like the result of JRASERVER-70189 / JRASERVER-70780, namely a TLSv1.3 bug in JDK 11.0.7 and earlier. The stacktrace is the same (SSLEngineImpl.java:136). If you 'strace' the native thread you'll see it's in quite a tight loop, resulting in high CPU." [SEP] "We have tested Jira with AdoptOpenJDK 11.0.8 and the issue is gone. JVM upgrade solves the problem.  I'm closing this issue since there is nothing else to be done."

summary: "Hide Log Work"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, Jira shows all user's time logs to other Jira users. Due to the GDPR reason, some Jira admins would like features to either hide or anonymize the time logs entry.      h3. Suggested Solution    Add a feature in Jira to either hide or anonymize the time logs entry added by users."""
comments: "GDPR also means having deletion rules in place, e.g. delete Worklogs older than X (e.g. in Germany the ""normal"" timeline ist 10 years). Anonymizing user data completely does not help here, since it might relate to users still active."

summary: "No Button to Install Service Desk in JIRA 8.5"
description: """h3. Summary    In the Application section there is no button to install Service Desk in JIRA 8.5  h3. Steps To Reproduce   # Navigate to Administration Setting > Application > Versions & licenses    h3. Expected Result    We should see a button a to install Service Desk  h3. Actual Result    There is no Button   !Screenshot 2019-10-25 at 09.46.58.png|thumbnail!        h3. Workaround   # Download the .obr file [Download Service Desk|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1213632/jira-service-desk/version-history]   # Use the """"Upload an application"""" option to install ServiceDesk on JIRA"""
comments: "Hi all,    The bug has been fixed. As the root cause was an invalid entry in [https://marketplace.atlassian.com|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/], it requires no additional changes to your affected Jira instance. It is simply already working properly.    Cheers,    Pawel Drygas, Jira Server Bugmaster"

summary: "Custom Plugin Web Item doesn't preserve the ""id"" attribute when <a> rendered from Issue Operations in Issue Navigator List View"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    View any issue in JIRA and, for example, look at the DOM element created for “Watchers” used to toggle watching an issue:  h3. Steps to Reproduce    We can notice the “id” attribute value (watching-toggle). If a plugin provides this attribute:    h3. Expected Results    One would expect it to be included in all menu item renderings.  h3. Actual Results    However, if you look at the “Actions” for a given issue in the Issue Navigator you’ll notice the id attribute is missing (watching-toggle):    The net affect of this is when writing a plugin that relies on the “id” attribute being part of the element, this inconsistency causes problems.  h3. Workaround    There is no workaround for now."""

summary: "Checkbox options consisting of blank spaces no longer render in Jira 8.4+ on Create Issue screen"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Starting with Jira 8.4.0, checkbox options that consist of a single blank space will no longer render on the Create Issue screen. See below screenshots for clarification.  h3. Environment    Jira 8.4.0 and above.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a new custom field of type *Checkboxes*   * Add an option to the field consisting of a blank space:   !Screen Shot 2019-10-04 at 7.37.23 AM.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    Empty checkbox option should render on the Create Issue screen, as per this 7.13.8 example:   !Screen Shot 2019-10-23 at 9.00.27 AM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    Empty checkbox option does not render in Jira 8.4.0+:   !Screen Shot 2019-10-04 at 7.37.39 AM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Instead of a blank space, use the    HTML entity to designate a whitespace. This will render as an empty option on the Create Issue screen:   !PS-47909_nbsp.png|thumbnail!"""
comments: "And ... why would someone want to use an option name that was a single blank space?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2] , I've seen it used for cosmetic reasons when there is only one checkbox (as in a boolean value)"

summary: "Ability to auto-delete Archived issues"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  While Archiving issues is a useful feature in Data Center instances. Having a means of deletion after a certain time would be a nice function to have.     h3. Suggested Solution  For example, in a specific use case, we have a retention period of 18 months, and anything after 12 months can be archived. Being able to auto-delete these archived issues after 18 months would be really nice.    h3. Workaround   Create a script similar to one mentioned in [Is there an easy way to archive a lot of issues?|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/is-there-an-easy-way-to-archive-a-lot-of-issues-976766896.html] """
comments: "Hi,  auto-deletion would also be an interesting topic for us!  If I may add to that: It would also be great to still be able to delete archived issues directly e.g. via REST. Currently the response is a ""403 Forbidden"". The way to first restore and then to delete an issue simply requires an unnecessary step (the ""unarchiving"") when we'd simply like to have an easy and quick way to not only delete something from the index (archive) but also entirely from the db (delete).  BR matthias"

summary: "Ability to Archive Sub-task issues alone"
description: """Currently, in Jira Data Center, we have the ability to archive Issues.  In the most recent versions, we are only able to archive the sub-task if we archive the parent issue.    In some use cases, it would be useful to archive the sub-task alone and keep the parent issue."""

summary: "`opsbar-operations` plugin location does not render WebItem's `param` in anchor element"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Contrary to what's described in [https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/web-item/] (refer to screenshot below), the `opsbar-operations` plugin location does not render WebItem's `param` in anchor element.      h3. Steps to Reproduce    With below web-section & web-item nodes added to atlassian-plugin.xml:            h3. Expected Results  The `bar_param_name=bar_param_value` param is rendered as an attribute in the anchor element.    h3. Actual Results  The generated html for the anchor link is    The `bar_param_name=bar_param_value` param is not rendered as an attribute in the anchor element.    h3. Notes    The issue was first raised by the plugin vendor (for ASW Service Catalog project) as question #23 in [https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/AWS/pages/714571788/Questions+Log.]    They referred to [https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/issue-view-ui-locations/#adding-custom-sections-and-items] for adding the new web-section & web-item.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Apps on marketplace.atlassian.com not updated to be compatible with Jira 8.5 release"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira 8.5 was released on Wed 22 Oct 2019 but MAC is not aware of the 8.5 release. Apps still showing Jira  8.4.x as the latest compatible version, see below.     !sr.png|thumbnail!    This will mark existing Apps as Incompatible in the UPM.     h3. Environment    Jira 8.5    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upgrade Jira to 8.5 ER   # Navigate to Administration > Manage Add-ons page     h3. Expected Results    Apps have option to update to 8.5 version    h3. Actual Results    Apps have no option to update to 8.5 version    h3. Workaround  Apps that are compatible with Jira 8.4.2 will work with 8.5.0.  As a workaround, we can use UPM's file upload method to upgrade/install the app versions that are compatible with 8.4.2 into Jira 8.5.0:  * https://confluence.atlassian.com/upm/installing-add-ons-273875715.html#InstallingMarketplaceapps-Installingbyfileupload    h3. Note  This is being actively fixed with marketplace.atlassian.com, as the problem is not in the Jira product."""
comments: "Some examples of missing compatibility. TO be used for verification of restoration of correct data.    [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1213697/actions-for-jira-service-desk?hosting=datacenter&tab=overview]    [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1216386/queues-for-jira-service-desk?hosting=cloud&tab=overview]    [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1216144/ultimate-theming-for-jira-service-desk?hosting=datacenter&tab=overview]    [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/5167/better-pdf-exporter-for-jira-pdf-view?hosting=cloud&tab=overview]" [SEP] "Resolving the issue after verifying plugin compatibility. All apps with Jira 8.4.2 compatibility have been updated to version 8.5.0.    Regards  Kamil Kolonko"

summary: "upgrade.reindex.allowed=false leaves a pending reindex request after 7.x->8.x upgrade"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If reindexing is disabled for upgrades ({{upgrade.reindex.allowed=false}}), a pending reindex request stays in the database, leading to a persistent """"reindexing needed"""" message. Removing this property will make Jira run a full reindex on the next restart.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Disable automatic reindexing after an upgrade per: [How To Disable Automatic Re-indexes After Upgrading|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-disable-automatic-re-indexes-after-upgrading-935383670.html] by setting {{upgrade.reindex.allowed=false}} in {{jira-config.properties}}   # Upgrade   # Do not remove the property   # Start Jira and log in to see the message: """"While upgrading Jira an upgrade task made changes that require reindexing.""""   # Run foreground (locking) reindex manually   # Observe the message is still appearing   # Click 'x' and see the message is gone   # Restart Jira and see the message re-appears   # Remove the {{upgrade.reindex.allowed=false}} property   # Restart Jira   # (x) A full foreground (locking) reindex is triggered, just as if Jira had just been upgraded    h3. Expected Results    Full lock and reindex would satisfy the system and stop messaging.    More importantly, once the full reindex is run, another one must not be triggered.  h3. Actual Results    Messaging continues to appear after full lock and reindex while the variable is still enabled.   Full re-index is triggered once the variable is disabled.  h3. *Recommendation*    Keep the variable set until you upgrade to 8.5.2.  h3. Workaround #1    Remove variable and restart Jira. Wait for Jira to perform full re-index.  h3. Workaround #2    Stop Jira.   Remove the pending reindex request by updating the DB:    Start Jira."""
comments: "I have replicated this on a number of instances, including those running 8.2.4 & 8.4.1" [SEP] "[~dsafady],    Thank you for reporting this.   From my tests I found out that the issue is much more serious than an annoying flag. Once you remove the {{upgrade.reindex.allowed}} property, Jira will perform a locking reindex on the next restart. Did you observe the same behaviour?  I updated the description with a workaround to clean the reindex request from the DB.  " [SEP] "Kamil, yes, that is the behaviour I saw." [SEP] "Hello everyone!    The issue is resolved and available in Jira Server version 8.5.2 and the correspondent Data Center version.  Version 8.6.0 which includes the change would be released soon.    Cheers,  Ignat Alexeyenko  Senior Engineer at Jira Server"

summary: "Add support for Office 365 Modern Authentication in Jira"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Jira Server*. Using *Jira Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73240].    {panel}    h3. Problem Definition  Office 365 is planning to discontinue support for basic auth on services like POP and IMAP on October 13, 2020.  As of today, basic auth (username and password) via IMAP/POP is the only way to make Jira able to use an Office 365 mailbox for receiving email.  More details about the upcoming changes to Microsoft's products can be found in https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/office/blogs/end-of-support-for-basic-authentication-access-to-exchange-online-apis-for-office-365-customers/    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement some other method of allowing Jira to authenticate to an office 365 mailbox, such as OAuth2.    h3. Why this is important  Without this feature, regular office 365 accounts won't permit applications like Jira to connect to them.  It seems that the ProPlus accounts might still permit this after the set date, however it seems clear that improving security is the goal of this forced change, and perhaps it would benefit Jira to follow suit.      h3. Workaround   None currently, if one should become available this will be updated to reflect that state."""
comments: "Hi,    we, as an atlassian partner company, are also interested in this topic, because our customers are affected,    Possible Solutions: MAPI or IMAPS with OAuth2.  Is there any possibility?    Big thanks in advance!    Best regards,  Omid" [SEP] "With all due respect, I'm already planning a migration away from Atlassian due to this.  The end of POP3/IMAP support in Office 365 is looming in Oct. 2020.  Unless of course OAuth or some other method is implemented in Jira/Confluence for connecting to MS 365 mailboxes, we'll probably be going to another platform." [SEP] "{panel:title=Atlassian Update – 3 December 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ebf2f9|titleBGColor=#ebf2f9|bgColor=#ffffff}  Hi everyone,    Thank you for your comments here as well as reaching out to our support team to signal the urgency of the issue.    We are aware how important the integration with Microsoft 365 is for Jira customers and plan to provide a solution well ahead of the timeline for dropping basic authentication methods anno

summary: "Manage sessions within Jira DC"
description: """*Issue:* Implementation of a Jira DC cluster requires a load balancer to store a persistence record of a users session so requests with the users session ID are routed to the specific Jira DC node where that session is present. In turn, an administrator is obliged to maintain this configuration on the load balancer as part of their Jira DC implementation. This is undesirable as it has both an implementation and operational overhead in addition to users being logged out if a node fails or is taken offline for maintenance.    *Goal:* Session ID's are shared between Jira DC nodes eliminating the need for persistence records on a load balancer and in turn allow any http request to be served from any Jira DC node.    *Benefits:*    Reduction of Jira DC implementation complexity.    Jira DC nodes can fail or be disabled without users loosing their sessions.    Enhanced HA."""

summary: "The following link results in 404 instead of 200. https://myhost/includes/jira/admin/group-browser"
description: """h3. Summary:     In a clean browser with no cookies I can browse to:    [https://myhost/includes/jira/admin/group-browser]    Our security team says that 404 error messages give the impression that there is a directory there, vs a 200 is an authentication error.  h3. Explanation    There are numerous risks associated with presence of directories with easy-to-guess names and locations. Through enumeration, attackers can fingerprint platforms and applications that are known to use specific directory names.    These directories can also lead to exposure of sensitive information and privileged functionality. Predictable directory names and locations could expose admin interfaces, log files, configuration files, source code files and allow an attacker to add, remove or modify files.    Reconnaissance is a fundamental requirement for a successful attack. Predictable directory names and locations can make it very easy to map their target and obtain information essential for compromising the target."""

summary: "JIRA to handle its JS and CSS more efficiently"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira loads large CSS and Javascript files on every page load, and it does look like some of this heavy lifting is avoidable. The size of these .js and .css files does cause inefficient client-side performance, especially when there is some network latency or on low resource client machines. After static content has been cached load times will improve significantly, but that initial experience for users who often re-login after browser cache has expired can be quite bad. The implications of this inefficiency with heavy static content handling is a lot worse on certain browser e.g. IE than others.  * Agencies with old client machines tend to deal with the inefficient client-side performance more frequently.    Chrome's Google Lighthouse scores for a vanilla instance are lower than 20 as illustrated in screenshots below. A quick test can be done with a public JAC URL:  -  http://jira.atlassian.com/issues/?jql=     !jira.atlassian.com.png|thumbnail!  !little to no plugin vanilla jira 8.1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  To look into reducing the size and amount of Javascript, CSS and other static content that JIRA requests on every page and avoid requesting huge static files when it can be avoided."""

summary: "Storing the customfieldoption ID in customfieldvalue in Numbervalue instead of Stringvalue"
description: """Hi,         something really bugging me out, is there a reason why the IDs of the customfieldoption is stored in the """"Stringvalue"""" Column of the customfieldvalue Table instead of the more appropriate """"Numbervalue"""" Column ?         Not only is this highly misleading, i don't see any reason for this as even for multiselectboxes this Information is stored in seperate data sets. And for all the datasets where this reference is linked, the numbervalue is always NULL.         This not only leads to massive performance problems when transfering the Data to a DWH, I suspect it is also a massive performance loss for the Application itself because of matching string values with numbervalues all the time (join nvarchar with decimal). For every issue which includes a Customfield with predefined options Jira has to cas the string to a number for every field.    And i dont see any reason for not fixing this , which would be relativly easy anyway.         Example:    SELECT *   FROM customfieldvalue cfv   inner join customfield cf on cfv.CUSTOMFIELD = cf.ID   where cf.CUSTOMFIELDTYPEKEY like '%:multicheckboxes'   _and cfv.NUMBERVALUE is not null_    !image-2019-10-16-09-20-52-899.png!"""
comments: "forgot to mention, version is     *v7.13.88*"

comments: "This issue also affects mentions within the Description field. " [SEP] "If the affected mentions are also in the Description field, you can run this query. :)     " [SEP] "{panel:title=Atlassian Update – 10 Mar 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ebf2f9|titleBGColor=#ebf2f9|bgColor=#ffffff}  Hi everyone,    We solved this issue by providing a dedicated app - [Cloud Compatibility for Jira available at Atlassian Marketplace|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1221117/cloud-compatibility-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview]. You will need to install it directly from your Jira Server instance by going to ""Jira Administration"" > ""Manage apps"" > ""Find new apps"", search for ""Cloud Compatibility for Jira"" or ask your Jira Server admin to install it for you. This app is compatible with Jira Server 8.    Please bear in mind that this app is listed as ""Unsupported"". Please visit [Atlassian Supported Apps|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/Support/Atlassian+Supported+Apps] for further details. Yo

summary: "Option to search for Text custom fields in Backlog search"
