Problem Definition:  It would be beneficial to be able to search for values in a custom text field using the backlog search option covered in the [""""Using your Kanban backlog""""|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/using-your-kanban-backlog-856846180.html] documentation which currently only allows you to:  {quote}  Search for an issue by name, code, or assignee.  {quote}    This would align the alternate search locations functionality and feature set with the similar global quick search functionality that was added to Jira back in 2012 via Jira 5.2:  - https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-7686    h3. Suggested Solution  Add custom text field context to Backlog search functionality     h3. Workaround  Use the issue navigator to narrow down results with a standard search"""

summary: "development[deployments].environment JQL function doesn't return all matched environments"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a Jira issue matches more than 4 successful deployments. The development field summary on the issue navigator would indicate that the issue is deployed to environments 1,2, 3 and x more... where 1,2,3 for example are different environments and x are as many more environments as are not explicitly listed in the issue navigator summary, but are collapsed. When searching for issues that match a deployment environment collapsed under the *... and x more* collapsed list using development[deployments].environment JQL, the issues are not matched.    h3. Environment  Jira 8.0.2+   Any version of Bamboo    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Integrate Jira with Bamboo  # Ensure there are Jira issues deployed successfully to more than 3 deployment environments as shown below for example:   !deployment-summary.png|thumbnail!   note that """"taiwo environment 5"""" is the environment not explicitly mentioned, but is actually collapsed under *and 1 more* in screenshot above:   !deployment-details.png|thumbnail!    # Execute JQL such as:    Where """"..."""" is any of the environment shown in the deployment details for an issue.    h3. Expected Results  For every environment used in that JQL for example, Jira should return the issue (DEMO-24 in this example).    h3. Actual Results  Jira only returns DEMO-24 for the environments specifically named in the development field summary on issue navigator   !deployment-summary.png|thumbnail!    !deployment-match.png|thumbnail!   environments that are collapsed under *and 'x' more* (in this example """"taiwo environment 5"""" )are not matched by {{development[deployments].environment =}}   or  {{development[deployments].environment in}}   !deployment-not-matched.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently no workaround    h3. Notes   * Issue is neither caused by stale indexes nor resolved by re-indexing. Note that both the issue navigator's development field summary and detail shows the collapsed environments, however the JQL only returns the ones explicitly named in the summary and doesn't match the collapsed environments.   * issue.property[development].environment is broken in similar manner"""

summary: "Jira mobile application is ignoring the context path in server.xml."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When context path is configured in server.xml mobile application doesn't resolve url correctly leaving the context path out.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set a context path in the server.xml like /Jira   # Navigate to Jira and login.    h3. Expected Results  Jira displays the correct page no errors.    h3. Actual Results  It renders the incorrect URL example [https://myjiraserver/favicon.ico]  Should actually be [https://myjiraserver/Jira/favicon.ico]      h3. Workaround  * Remove the context path from the server.xml and restart Jira."""
comments: "Removing Context path is not an option.    Please fix this nasty bug ASAP." [SEP] "Why is this a low priority bug? it PREVENTS USE for any company that must use a Context Path in server.xml!" [SEP] "Our firm Indiana University is now able to use the Jira Mobile app even though the above problem of not respecting context path in the favicon.ico URL.  As far as I'm concerned this is no longer a problem."

summary: "StackTraceCompressor (Atlassian Logging 2.x) omits stackframe in log"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Atlassian Logging 2.x is shipped with Jira 8.5 EAP. The class NewLineIndentingFilteringPatternLayout code eats up a stacktrace because of a simple count error.    It is *very* confusing when the error logs are missing the most crucial information without any indication. It cost us quite some time...    h3. Environment    h3. Steps to Reproduce    In a particular stack trace, we notice that there is no call to Preconditions.checkArgument inside DelegatingEntityPropertyService.setProperty().  I think the stacktrace is missing one frame (for whatever reason).    h3. Expected Results    The entire stack trace is printed.    h3. Actual Results    A line is missing from the stack trace thrown.     h3. Notes    I investigated some more into the issues with the missing stackframe in the log.  This issue seems to have been fixed somewhere in a 3.X release of the Atlassian Logging library which Jira also uses.   Jira 8.5 (tested with 8.5.0-EAP02) will only ship a version 2.X of Atlassian Logging that contains this bug.    This is the fix, for completeness sake:        h3. Workaround    We have another log appender that save the same log and there the frame is indeed present."""
comments: "atlassian-logging is in this version since Jira 8.0.0" [SEP] "We originally experienced this in 7.6"

summary: "Users are able to the link issues from projects which they don't have ""Browse Projects"" & ""Link Issues"" permissions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Linking Issues: Users without """"Browse Projects"""" & """"Link Issues"""" permissions in Project B but have these permissions in Project A, Users can the link issues from project B with issues in project A using a known issue key from project B.    h3. Environment    - Jira 7.13.5    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Let's say we have project """"A"""" and project """"B"""";   # One group have the """"Browse projects"""" and """"Link Issues"""" permissions only in project """"A"""", but not in project """"B"""";   # Users in that group will be able to see issues from project A but not from project B, the user obtained an issue key from project B (Ex. from a colleague);   # If you link an issue from project A in an issue from project A, the user will be able to link and see the """"linked issues"""" just fine;   # If you try to link an issue in Project A;   # With an issue from project B which you don't have the """"Browse projects"""" and """"Link Issues"""" permissions;   # Using the issue key known, you still can link them, but you won’t be able to see the link;   # Other users who have """"Browse projects"""" permission for project B will be able to see the link you have made;    h3. Expected Results   Users *shouldn’t be able to* Link issues from projects which they don’t have """"Browse Projects"""" & """"Link Issues"""" permissions in.    h3. Actual Results  Users *can* link issues from projects which they don’t have """"Browse Projects"""" & """"Link Issues"""" permissions in.    h3. Workaround  None.  """

summary: "Share Issue > Note formatting is lost when ‘Email Type’ preferences is set to ""HTML""."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Share Issue > Note formatting is lost when ‘Email Type’ preferences is set to """"HTML"""".    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to User Profile > Click on edit icon in Preferences section > Select “Email Type” as HTML > Click Update.   # Now from Issue page, click on the share icon, enter the user email and in the Note field, enter some text having newline.   !image-2019-10-15-16-06-33-530.png|thumbnail!    # Click 'Share'.   # The email received by the user is not formatted correctly:   !image-2019-10-15-16-04-48-105.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  The note text in the email should be in the format the same as shared originally in the note on the issue.    h3. Actual Results  The note text in the email is not formatted correctly and all the new lines are skipped.    h3. Notes  The users only receive Notes field in the proper format if they meet the following pre-requisite   * The user with whom the Jira issue is shared has set their “Email Type” Preferences to 'Text'.  * If the issue is shared with External Users, who are not Jira users, then they will receive """"Notes"""" in improper format as they cannot update their """"Email Type"""" preferences.  * Able to replicate this issue on Jira version 7.90, 7.13.0, 8.2.2,    h3. Workaround  If sharing the issue with Jira users, you can ask the user to change the “Email Type” Preferences to Text. No workaround is possible for external users at the moment."""

summary: "Support for wildcarded group names in group filters when syncing with Delegated LDAP"
description: """Historically Jira supported wildcarded group names in group filters for groups to sync for synched ldap directories but not for delegated ldap directories.    Further detail: With Delegated we can specify what groups get imported into the local directory when the user logs in by means of the group filter.  But wildcards don't get respected for that filter, with the delegated directories,  names have to be fully enumerated.     With Connector LDAP, Synced the group filter can contain wildcard and we use that feature today. But we want to switch to delegated and lack of wildcards there is a showstopper for the customer.    The docs never really reflected the problem that LDAP connector (Synched type) supported wildcards and Delegated didn't.  """

summary: "Mouse Click and return/enter in JQL edit have different outcomes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If you open saved filter and edit it you must execute a search before you can save, this is working as intended.  However there is an error occuring on the method used to trigger the search and there a difference in outcome when you trigger the search via the enter/return key or if you click the """"Search"""" button with your mouse before you save, Noting this is a cosmetic error only but does cause confusion as to wether the save was successful not leaving behind a """"Edited"""" identifier intended to tell the user the filter needs to be saved although it already has been saved.    h3. Environment  Jira Server verified on multiple 7.x and 8.x versions  For Jira Cloud See corresponding BUG: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73145    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open a Saved filter in the issue navigator  # edit the filter in any way  # Note that next to the JQL's title a object """"Edited"""" appears noting a change occurred to the filter and it needs to be saved  # Click the search button with your mouse  # Click Save  # a popup noting the save was successful will appear in the upper right corner of the active browser window  # Note the """"Edited"""" notation goes away   # Note that the filter is saved correctly  # Make another change to the filter  # Note the """"Edited"""" notation re-appears  # This time Hit return/enter key to trigger the search  # Select save  # a popup noting the save was successful will appear in the upper right corner of the active browser window    h3. Expected Results  It is expected that the Edited notation will go away noting the current filter displayed is correct without any pending edits needed to be saved before navigating from the page    h3. Actual Results  While the filter is correctly saved it will still display the """"Edited"""" object causing confusion to the end user that is not sure if the filter has actually saved correctly or not   !Screen Shot 2019-10-14 at 10.59.02 AM.png|thumbnail!    h3. Notes  The """"Edited"""" notation is class """"filter-edited-object""""    h3. Workaround  Using the mouse click to search before saving will make the edited notation go away to provide verification the filter was saved, BUT it is not necessarily needed as the save action is completing and and a popup will note successful as well so the edited notation is only a cosmetic error,  But just to be on the save side you can also re-open the filter in a new tab just to verify the save was successful so you do not navigate away and loose track of the filter changes."""

summary: "Support for IMAP/POP OAuth 2.0 token based auth"
description: """We need to support IMAP/POP OAuth 2.0 token based auth:    Accordingly to this [post|https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/Exchange-Team-Blog/Improving-Security-Together/ba-p/805892] in the Microsoft Tech Community post, Microsoft will stop to support basic authentication for IMAP and POP protocols for Exchange Online by Oct/2020. The only way to authenticate in Exchange Online service for POP/IMAP protocols will be through OAuth 2.0 token based auth.    It may affect our customers that have IMAP/POP Jira Cloud external Mail Handlers configured pointing to this service."""

summary: "Add word counter to Jira tickets"

If it's not viable to increase the word count, can a word counter be added so that we know how many words need to be deleted? At this stage it only says it exceeds the limit, but not how many characters you have and we have to copy paste our text in word and amend it there."""

summary: "Upgrading Jira from 7.X to 8.4.2 causes tables and columns to not be created."
description: """h3. Summary    Upgrading Jira from 7.X to 8.4.2 causes tables and columns to not be created.  h3. Environment    Jira 7.12.3 | Windows Server | MySQL 5.6  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upgrade Jira data from 7.12.3 to Jira 8.4.2 running an *in place* upgrade.    h3. Expected Results    The upgrade concludes gracefully  h3. Actual Results    All indexed entities fail to be displayed and Jira is unusable as a result.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround    Option 1) Step into 8.4.1 first then upgrade to 8.4.2    Option 2) So far, the issue has been reported to only occur while upgrading using the installer. On the other hand, upgrading manually is considered to be free of this bug and is the recommended way for 7.x - > 8.4.x/8.5.x path as detailed in [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/upgrading-from-jira-7-x-to-jira-8-5-results-in-data-base-tables-and-columns-missing-978222133.html]"""
comments: "This also impacts Jira in combination with SQL Server" [SEP] "Hi!    I have read the KB article and upgraded Jira server 7.10.2 manually to 8.5.3 using the TAR GZ archive.  I noticed that atlassian-jira.log has the following entries:      Does this mean that the problem still exists? How can I verify it?    Thanks." [SEP] "This problem happen when I upgrade from Jira 7.13 to Jira 8.x. I tried all of the workaround above but the problem still persist. I think we can fix that by manually adding column to the table"

summary: "When referencing an attachment in a comment, wiki renderer can incorrectly superscript text in bullet or numbered lists"
description: """h3. Summary  Within a Comment or Description type field, adding an attachment link in the comment, and creating a bullet list or numbered list will sometimes incorrectly superscript the text that follows    h3. Environment  * Replicated in Jira 8.4.1 with Chrome 77.0.3865.90      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Attach a file to a Jira issue  # Create a new comment on that issue, be sure to use the visual text editor in the comment  # Using the paperclip icon in the text editor, add a link to that uploaded attachment  # Then click either the bullet list OR numbered list  # Move the cursor to the end of the line (either via mouse click or keyboard arrow keys)  # Press Enter/Return to get a new line in the list  # Enter some additional text    h3. Expected Results  The entered text should appear normally.    h3. Actual Results  The entered text instead is superscripted     h3. Notes  Switching over to Text editor mode we can see that the caret symbol appears ot be getting added to the text on that line, and subsequent lines.    h3.Workaround  Using the Text editor mode instead of Visual editor mode is one way to work past this bug."""

summary: "Make number of results configurable in ""link issue"" JQL search"
description: """h4. Problem definition  The """"Link issue"""" functionality has a """"search for an issue"""" link that allows the creation of a JQL search. The maximum number of values returned, however, is 10. This is because the request includes a """"{{maxResults}}"""" parameter which is hardcoded directly into the file ({{jira-components/jira-plugins/jira-issue-link-remote-jira-plugin/src/main/resources/js/issuelink-jira-search.js}}).    h4. Suggested solution  Add a widget to the page to allow clients to configure the number of returned results with the same JQL query.  """

summary: "Add the ability to disable Jira XML backup within GUI"
description: """h4. Problem definition    The method to disable auto-export as in [Disabling auto-export|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver072/disabling-auto-export-828787648.html] is to modify the {{jira.config.properties}} file and setting the property {{jira.autoexport=false}}.  h4. Suggested solution    Add an on/off option in the General configuration section of the Jira Administration System settings. This would also minimise potential to corrupt the configuration file."""

summary: "Brazil's daylight saving"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This bug is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? See the [corresponding bug|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73153].  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    In Brazil, we won't have daylight saving in 2019 and it may impact our products.    On October 20, our timezone in Brazil would change to GMT -2, but in 2019 it won't happen, so the timezone in Jira and Confluence may change and affect Due Dates, SLAs, Team calendar among other things.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Any field tied to a timezone change will be affected.    It may also affect SLA for Service Desk issues.  h3. Expected Results    On October 20, it should keep GMT -3 for Sao Paulo's timezone.  h3. Actual Results    It will show the wrong information because the timezone will be GMT -2.   h3. Workaround    Change the timezone manually to GMT -3 using another country's timezone, like Argentina (Buenos Aires)."""

summary: "Add ability to use due date of custom date field as a valid predicate"
description: """I would like to execute the following query in JQL:    project in (<Project>) AND issuetype in (<Issue types>) *AND status changed FROM testing TO Closed BEFORE {color:#ff8b00}_due_{color}*    Where due can be a custom date field.    Is there a chance to do this without any extra plugin and just using pure JQL? Or maybe my query is incorrect?         currently I have the following error:  !image-2019-10-10-10-21-06-675.png!    {color:#FF0000}Date value 'due' for predicate 'before' is invalid. Valid formats include: 'yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm', 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm', 'yyyy/MM/dd', 'yyyy-MM-dd', or a period format e.g. '-5d', '4w 2d'.{color}    The format of my due field is like 'yyyy-MM-dd'         My current version of Jira is 6.4.9 but I can updated if it is required.         Thank you in advance for your attention."""

summary: "Warn users if the Windows Service couldn't be stopped during an upgrade"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, if the Windows Service fails to stop when upgrading Jira, the user will not be able to proceed with the upgrade and will receive an unclear message as described [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/upgrade-fails-to-remove-previous-installation-directory-in-jira-server-313463494.html].    h3. Suggested Solution    The installer should detect that the Jira Service is up and warn the user to avoid the need to investigate the root cause from the error."""

summary: "Disabling Jira Analytics should stop sending POST requests to /jira/rest/analytics/1.0/publish/bulk"
description: """Currently the Jira analytics collection can be disabled from Administration - > System - > Advanced - > Analytics and this will stop logging in the $JIRA_HOME/analytics-logs directory. But this does not remove the javascript that fires the POST rest call  /jira/rest/analytics/1.0/publish/bulk. This unnecessarily floods the access logs. The rest endpoint should also be blocked."""
comments: "I agree with this.  When measuring page load time, it always waits for that last bulk and adds 5 seconds or more to the time.  Why is batch.js shipping analytics if analytics aren't being recorded?  This makes no sense to me." [SEP] "Why does this not happen?" [SEP] "Ditto. "

summary: "Subtasks hidden by the filter is ignored in Kanban board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Dragging the last subtask assigned to the current user prompts the user to update the parent task to *Done* status because all of its subtasks are now *Done* where in fact there are still other subtasks hidden by the Kanban sub-filter.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue with four subtasks   # Assign two of the subtasks to the current user while the other two to another user.  !2.png|thumbnail!  # Create a Kanban board which includes the parent issue of this issue, SKP-1  in this example.   # Go to Board > Configure and set the following:  #* Swimlanes to Stories  #* Kanban sub-filter query to """"assignee = currentUser()""""  # Back to the board, we will see only two subtask from the parent SKP-1, as expected.   # Drag these two subtasks to *Done* column (one at a time)  # Notice that when you drag the last subtask, there will be a prompt   !1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  There shouldn't be a prompt saying that *all* the subtasks are done because there are two more issues (hidden by the filter) that are not done.       h3. Actual Results  Message prompt appears:      h3. Notes  Reproducible in Jira 7.10.2    h3. Workaround  Check the parent issue's issue detail page to double confirm if all the subtasks are indeed done. """

summary: "Audit log for application links being created/updated"

summary: "Allow JIRA to communicate Via Microsoft APIs O365"
description: """h3. *Problem Definition*  Currently, mail connection configuration for SMTP/Incoming mail server can only be achieved via the port connection.    h3. *Suggested Solution*  Allow mail connection configuration to use API such as Microsoft APIs O365 instead of using the ports.    h3. *Why this is important*  Due to strict security compliance connection via the port is not possible for certain organization as it potentially provides security hole which can be used by attackers.    *Note*: Microsoft is planning to retire SMTP access for Exchange:  https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/Exchange/article-id/27095"""

summary: "Bubble chart does not have Show XML link"

As of Jira 8.4, the Bubble chart gadget does not have an XML link, which means that it cannot be added to Confluence.    The suggestion is to either add the XML link to the gadget, to allow using it on Confluence or to add the chart itself to the list of Jira charts in Confluence: """

summary: "ampersand in URL within HTML email becomes encoded"
description: """I've tried sending emails with Jira Workflow Toolbox and cPrime's SIL scripting language that contain URLs with parameters being passed as """"&param1=value1&param2=value2""""  - in both cases the email arrived with the ampersand encoded (with """"amp;"""" appended).  The plugins say they're not doing it... so it seems some HTML validation in the mail subsystem is a bit overeager.    Some browsers handle the encoding in the URL well, but as far as I can tell most email clients do not."""

summary: "Admin CSV Importer changing the issue key causing data corruption"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Whenever an issue key is changed for an issue as a result of changing projects and then trying to use the admin importer to make a change using the older issue key, the data gets corrupted.  h3. Environment    Jira Server 7.13.8   Jira DC  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1) Create a new issue in Jira in project > Issue gets created with issue key of *ABC-123.*   2) Now 'Move' that issue to a new project > The issue key now becomes *DEF-234.*   3) Now export this new issue to a CSV. Ensure before export that the issue navigator, the project and created columns are included in the search results.   4) Open the exported CSV file and change the create date on it in the column which lists the 'created date' and change the format on the column to dd/MMM/yy h:mm   5) Change the issue key and project name from *“DEF-234”* to *“ABC-123”* and the corresponding project details as well (such as project key and project name)   6) Ensure your csv file only has the following columns: Issue Key, Summary, Project Type, Project name, Project Key, Created   7) Now save the CSV file as Comma delimited CSV UTF-8   8) Using the Admin importer, perform an import using CSV option.   9) For Project select 'Defined in CSV' , for date format select dd/MMM/yy h:mm   10) Map the columns appropriately (""""Project name”, """"Project key”, “Issue key”, ”Project type”, ”Summary"""" and """"Created"""" ) in Jira and DO NOT select 'Map Field Value'   11) Begin Import   12) After import finishes - Search for *DEF-234*   - You will notice that this issue is no longer found, but if you search for *ABC-123* from Step 1, it has now become *ABC-234.*    h3. Expected Results    Admin CSV importer to carry out data as intended during the CSV edition, Issue key ACB-123  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "An ugly workaround would be to run a REST script to check the current issue key for every row in the CSV file. And update the issue key to the new value if the issue has been moved."

summary: "Jira Data Center AWS CloudFormation templates won't deploy additional nodes correctly if the database collation has been changed"
description: """h3. Summary  Jira nodes created after the cluster has been deployed using the Atlassian-provided AWS CloudFormation templates will fail to install and run Jira if the database collation has been changed to a supported one.    h3. Environment  * AWS CloudFormation deployment using the Atlassian-provided templates.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy a Jira Data Center cluster in AWS following the instructions in https://aws.amazon.com/quickstart/architecture/jira/. The database will be created using the unsupported collation *{{en_US.utf-8}}*, as reported in JRASERVER-70077.  # Change the database collation to *{{C}}* and table collation to *{{DEFAULT}}* by following the steps in the KB article [Unsupported collation error thrown in Jira server using PostgreSQL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-fix-the-collation-of-a-postgres-jira-database-776657961.html].  # Update the CloudFormation stack, increasing the parameters *{{ClusterNodeMax}}* and *{{ClusterNodeMin}}*, so a new node will be deployed.    h3. Expected Results  The node is deployed correctly, Jira is installed and is able to run normally.    h3. Actual Results  The EC2 instance will be launched, but Ansible automation will fail to install Jira, reporting the following errors:      h3. Workaround  None.  """
comments: "Jira Quickstarts should be handling this case now; we explicitly set collation to C, and check for the case of an existing DB:    https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/dc-deployments-automation/src/051afc7c09d3eba460629adbaf289bc297b64f0e/aws_jira_dc_node.yml#lines-17  https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/dc-deployments-automation/src/051afc7c09d3eba460629adbaf289bc297b64f0e/roles/database_init/tasks/main.yml#lines-34    Can you confirm that this is still an issue?"

summary: "User cannot be found for role assignment after a directory change"
description: """h3. Summary    A user is a member of two LDAP connector type user directories (i.e. LDAP1 and LDAP2) defined on same Jira instance. A user is discovered from the first of these directories according to the order of directory definition (LDAP1). When we delete this user from LDAP1, Jira discovers the user from the second directory (LDAP2) just fine. The user can be searched for, added to local groups, however, cannot be added to a project role.     !search_user.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce   * We can't reproduce this on a vanilla Jira installation   * Reproduced reliably at client instance.     h3. Expected Results   * After being disabled on LDAP1, the user, as discovered from LDAP2 with the same username (which was shadowed before) shall be available for project role assignments.    h3. Actual Results   * User cannot be assigned to a project role (see attached images)   !assign_user_to_role.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     * Restarting the server. """

summary: "Cloning issues with attachments should attach the files at creation time"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Cloning an issue with the attachments will not create the issue with the attachments in one go. Instead, it create the issue then attaches the file.   This is problematic is the create transition requires a file to be attached.   h3. Suggested Solution  Make the clone operation create the issue with the attachment.   h3. Workaround  If a validator is present in the create transition, it has to be removed before cloning issues. """

summary: "As a user, I want to be able to create a an issue with an attachment via the REST API"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The current REST API doesn't allow users to add an attachment while creating the issue.   h3. Suggested Solution  Add a JSON field to enter a base64-encoded attachment when hitting the [Create issue|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.0/\#api/2/issue-createIssue] endpoint.   h3. Workaround  # Create an issue and capture the issue key.   # Call the [Add attachment|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.0/#api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey\}/attachments-addAttachment] endpoint.    h3. Note(s)  The workaround doesn't help if the workflow has a validator to ensure there's an attachment at the time of issue creation. """

summary: "Running a JQL Query Involving Change History Using a TemporaryIndexProvider Misses Search Results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There's a bug in how we build Lucene queries from JQL when using the DefaultTemporaryIndexProvider. As part of the JQL->LQL translation process, we sometimes run further sub-queries (for cases like issue status change to/from). These always end up targeting the Jira-wide change-history index rather than the short-lived one created by TemporaryIndexProvider. The end result is that the translated JQL query doesn't work as expected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    As part of an action in response to an issue change event, say a transition to """"In Progress"""":   # call `TemporaryIndexProvider.indexIssuesAndSearch(..)` with a jql query that references the change history index. Something like:      h3. Expected Results    The action returns the id of a newly-created issue when it is transitioned to """"In Progress""""  h3. Actual Results    The action fails to find the issue in its search results.  h3. Notes    This bug initially manifested itself in JSD SLAs not being triggered.  h3. Workaround    Re-running the search once the main Jira change history index has been updated may work in some situations. (For the example above, transitioning the issue to 'not done' and then once more to 'in progress' works.)"""
comments: "Same issues reported in this ticket:    [https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/JST-521809] "

summary: "When a remote user directory is disabled, user display names are changed to lower_user_name on issues and comments"
description: """If the remote directory in which a user resides is disabled, then the user's username appears on issues and comments instead of the user's display name. This may create significant confusion when trying to search for issues and comments.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a remote directory connector with active users.   # Create an issue and assign it to a user who resides in the remote directory. Example, {{""""Jim Botsfarm""""}}. Note that the user's Full Name (display name) is shown in the Assignee field.   # Delete the user from the remote directory.   # Disable the remote directory (*⚙* > *User management* > *User directories*) in which this user resides.    h3. Expected Results    The user's full name (display name) appears in the Assignee field appended with {{[X]}}. Example, {{""""Jim Botsfarm [X]""""}}.  h3. Actual Results    The user's lowercase username appears in the Assignee field instead. Example, {{""""jbotsfarm""""}}.  h3. Notes    This is unexpected because the data in the {{cwd_user}} table is not changed. We see {{deleted_externally}} set to 1, and the {{""""[X]""""}} we expect is present in the {{display_name}} column.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Also Affects in Jira Server Version 7.4.5"

summary: "Search does not work for Confluence Cloud Application Link"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When trying to manually link a Jira issue to a Confluence Cloud page, the search function does not work. Returns 501    h3. Environment     * Jira Server 8.3.1   * Confluence Cloud    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up application link between a Jira server instance and a Confluence Cloud instance   # Create an issue in Jira   # Try to link the issue to a Confluence Cloud page using the """"Search for a page"""" function to find the page   !Screenshot 2019-10-04 at 16.02.39.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2019-10-04 at 16.02.57.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    * Able to search for pages/contents on linked Confluence Cloud instance    h3. Actual Results    * Gets error:    (!) Unable to gather information from the selected Confluence instance.   !Screenshot 2019-10-04 at 16.03.09.png|thumbnail!     HTTP log from enabling DEBUG logging for {{org.apache.http}}:      h3. Notes  * Deprication notice:   bq. The XML-RPC and SOAP APIs are deprecated since Confluence 5.5, and may not work as expected.  from [confluence xml-rpc and-soap apis|https://developer.atlassian.com/server/confluence/confluence-xml-rpc-and-soap-apis/]  * Removal   bq.  Confluence’s SOAP, JSON-RPC and XML-RPC APIs were deprecated ... While they continue to work today, Confluence Cloud will be officially removing these APIs as of March 29th, 2019.  from [confluence-cloud soap-api migration|https://blog.developer.atlassian.com/confluence-cloud-soap-api-migration/]    It looks like the search function uses RPC which is deprecated/not supported on Confluence Cloud. Maybe time to switch to using REST for search instead?    h3. Workaround    Paste the link to the Confluence Cloud page directly instead of using the search function. No workaround if you wish to use search."""
comments: "We're being impacted by this bug after waiting out https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFCLOUD-68643 , which was resolved in 12/2019 so we could connect our Jira Server to Confluence Cloud.    I'd like to ask the team to please raise the priority on this as the ""*Search For a Page*"" feature is crucial to many of our user base - including myself (It's extremely inconvenient and inefficient to need to past the exact URL to a Confluence page using the workaround)." [SEP] "We are also effected by this bug, is there any insights when this will be resolved?"

summary: "When using new browser tab to create an issue (as compared to pop-up) - Value inside Linked Issues field disappears if validator on creation fails"
description: """h3. Issue Summary:    When users try to create an issue using new browser window (tab), the value specified in Linked Issues field is reset (is removed) if validator on creation fails; rest of the values like sprint, labels, component etc retained.    The issue is reproducible in 7.13.2, 7.13.3, and 8.4.1  h3. Environment:    (Optional - If Applicable)   * Can be reproduced in any of the following versions: 7.13.2, 7.13.3 and 8.4.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce:   # Click """"Create"""" button, and open create screen NOT as pop-up option, but in new browser window.   # Put issue into Linked Issues field.   # Leave any of the mandatory fields empty (example: leave field """"Summary"""" empty).   # Click create.    h3. Expected Results:   # ++ System doesn't allow to create request.   # ++ System throws an error """"You must specify summary of the issue"""".   # ++ System leaves user's input within Linked Issue field in place.    h3. summary: "Enhance processing  of hyperlinks in WikiRenderer"
description: """WikiRenderer does not recognize many useful URL schemes.  “[name|url]” notation works correctly for ‘http’, ‘https’, ‘ftp’, ‘file’, and ‘mailto’ schemes in URL.   It does not work for other standard URL schemes like ‘data’ and ‘tel’.  It also does not work for custom schemes like 'doors’.  Surprisingly,  'Default Text Rendrer' recognizes 'doors' URLs e.g.  doors://dewup-ap71:36677/?version=2&prodID=0&view=0000001b&urn=urn:telelogic::1-4c57d7d104e43e04-O-8-000083a9      We suppose that enhancing functionality of renderers in Atlassian software if quite an easy task: extend regular expressions to detect multiple URL schemes and then, when user clicks the link,  just forward ‘open url’ request to the web browser."""
comments: "How can I attach a picture to the suggestion I have just created ?  How can I edit its description ?"

summary: "Wiki markup is inconsistent in how it treats empty cells in a table."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Wiki markup is inconsistent in how it treats empty cells in a table.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try creating a table where the empty cell is there, but there is no space within this empty cell.   For Example:    # When it tries to render, wiki markup considers this empty cell as no cell. Hence the result is like this:  |1|ABC|Yes|Yes|Yes|  |2||No|No|Yes|  |3|XYZ|Yes|  |4||No|Yes|No|  # Now create a table where empty cells are there, and there's space in the empty cells. For example:    # In this case, wiki markup translates it as a cell with no content/empty cell. Hence it gets rendered like this:  |1|ABC|Yes|Yes|Yes|  |2| |No|No|Yes|  |3|XYZ|Yes| | |  |4| |No|Yes|No|    h3. Expected Results  The table should be rendered correctly.    h3. Actual Results  The table is not rendered correctly.    h3. Notes  In the first example, it does not require space to consider a cell as a cell. See |Sunday|, it gets rendered as a cell even though there is no space there. Now, when there is || which doesn't have space either, but it doesn't take it as a cell. Both of them have no space.  Able to replicate this behavior on 7.13.0, 8.2.2    h3. Workaround  The workaround is to have space in the empty cells so that the table is rendered correctly."""

For example, IssueUpdateBatcher-header.vm only has *issueKey* and *issueUrl* and does not contain the complete issue details such as issue types. So customizing this template is limited to what the template has prepared.    A use case scenario would be to display the parent task in the header of the mail (project / issue / subtask instead of project / subtask)."""

Suggested Solution     Add these details to the docs. """

summary: "Allow advanced options when connecting to Oracle database"
description: """h4. Issue    * Currently, when Jira connects to the Oracle database in the installation wizard, it only has the option of configuring hostname, port, service, username, and password.   * Because Jira doesn't have a way to point to a tnsnames.ora file or manually construct the connection string to utilize either Oracle Wallet OR the Java Keystore (JKS) methods, we are not able to use Oracle Autonomous Transaction Processing, which is very crucial to our business.     h4. Sample from tnsnames.ora      h4. sample from ojdbc.properties:      h4. Suggestions    * Ideally, during the installation wizard, after you selected """"oracle"""" as your DB type, there would be an """"advanced"""" button, where when you clicked it, it would allow a user to input a fully formed JDBC connect string. (Or even better, an """"Oracle Cloud"""" option which would just allow the user to select the downloaded wallet.zip file, read the file, and select the proper connect stanza)    * In addition,  when the user clicked """"next"""" on the bottom of the DB setup screen that the installer would test for a file named something like 'oracledbsetup.xml'. If the file existed, that the connect string in the file would over-ride whatever data was inputted in the DB setup dialogue and allow us to provide the connection string that way so that we would be able to use Oracle Autonomous Transaction Processing.     """
comments: "At the very least, let's get a published hack.  Installing against a temp db, then stopping the installer as soon as dbconfig.xml is created, migrating the db from the temp db to ATP, then resuming the installer is a pretty time and resource consuming hack.     *At the end of the day, these are security issues*; whether Oracle Wallet, or JKS.  *Let's do the right thing here and encourage good behavior by not making security too difficult.*     "

summary: "Add support for the WAS operator to project field when JQL searching"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Jira Server*. Using *Jira Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73086].    {panel}    h3. Summary    Currently when trying to search JQL and using the [project field|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/advanced-searching-fields-reference-764478339.html#Advancedsearching-fieldsreference-ProjectProject], the only supported operators are  {quote}= , !=, IS , IS NOT, IN , NOT IN  {quote}  It does not currently support the use of the WAS or WAS NOT operators.  h3. Why this would be helpful    Extending tracking to an issue's project history here would be very useful in order to try to search for issues that were not initially created in the current project, but rather were moved from another project. Right now, JQL does not have a native way to search for issues that have been moved from another project.  h3. Workaround    It seems to be something that 3rd party plugins have tried to solve, such as described in [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/View-issues-that-have-been-moved-from-one-project-to-another/qaq-p/790346]   However to have Jira be able to do this natively would be preferred."""

summary: "As an admin, I want to Export Custom Field values so that I can administer custom fields more effectively. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira currently has no way of exporting the Custom Fields, their values, and their context.     Using the UI, the admin will have to search through multiple pages of results and no way to copy the values to quickly view the data.   h3. Suggested Solution    Add a way to export all the Custom Fields, Custom Field Values, and the context.    This will allow admins to quickly browse a list of the Custom Fields to know their context among other information.   h3. Workaround    Using the UI, you can filter through the search box for issues that have a global context.     The following PSQL will retrieve the customfield names that are Global.   """

summary: "Preference for auto-adding Editors in new filters"
description: """As a Jira user who needs to create a lot of filters and grant Editor permissions to the same set of users, I'd like to count with a user preference for my new filters to be automatically editable by my selected option (Group, Project or User), so that I don't have to manually add the same editors over and over again.    There's a similar preference for auto-sharing filters (well, just with _public_ or _private_ as selectable options)."""

summary: "As a Jira admin, I would like to be able to display a Confluence page's contents in a Jira issue."
description: """Currently, it is possible to link a Confluence page to a Jira issue through the usage of application links. This link will tell users that there is a page linked to the issue, and to access the page's contents you have to click on the link and access Confluence. However, as a Jira admin, I would like to be able to directly display the contents of a Confluence page in the *View Issue* page."""

summary: "Update ""Installing Jira Data Center"" docs to reflect default ehcache.listener.socketTimeoutMillis"
description: """In the """"Installing Jira Data Center"""" docs, under Cluster.properties file parameters, it lists  |{{ehcache.listener.socketTimeoutMillis}}|No|By default, this is set to the EhCache default.|    In the EHCache docs, it lists this default as 2000ms. [https://www.ehcache.org/documentation/2.8/replication/rmi-replicated-caching.html#configuring-the-cachemanagerpeerlistener]         However looking at the source code, it is clear this is being set by Jira to 5000ms. [https://stash.atlassian.com/projects/JIRASERVER/repos/jira/browse/jira-components/jira-core/src/main/java/com/atlassian/jira/cluster/DefaultEhCacheConfigurationFactory.java?at=refs%2Ftags%2F7.13.7#48]         Please update the docs to reflect this."""

summary: "Allow webhook access to Jira DVCS admin"
description: """Currently we have to manually add every single GitHub repository into our Jira DVCS configuration as we need them. Ideally we'd like to be able to add repositories automatically via webhook when they are created in GitHub, or at the very least it would be nice if there was an option to automatically add all repos from the account. New repos are in the DVCS account's dropdown, so Jira knows they exist."""

summary: "Add support for AWS SecretsManager to Docker images"
description: """*Background*    We now have an officially supported Docker image for Jira Software server (+ others) - good work Atlassian!    *Request*    When we deploy inside an AWS ECS environment (i.e. AWS' managed Docker service), we like to inject secrets (DB credentials, etc) via a service called AWS Secrets Manager.  This simply injects the decrypted secret value via an environment variable. Unfortunately it's a single, JSON-formatted string with multiple values    *Suggestion*    Add a variable called *ATL_AWS_SECRETSMANAGER_DB_CREDS*, and parse the individual values (host, port, user, pass, etc) from this, instead of passing individual values via individual env vars."""
comments: "*Bonus information:*    AWS Secrets Manager supports plain text secrets, but the most useful ones are DB credentials. They come in a JSON string formatted as such:    Everything you need in one string. This also provides the opportunity to pick the DB type (note the ""engine"" field)"

summary: "Allow Project Admins to make changes to the Permission Scheme used by the project"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    On 7.4, we started allowing Project Administrators to make changes to workflows and screens of the project, taking certain restrictions in considerations. However, there are other settings that can be project-specific that we only allow Jira Administrators to perform the changes.    h3. Suggested Solution    In a similar method, we can also enable project-level admins to make changes to the permission scheme used by the project, taking into consideration the same restrictions that we have on other functionalities.    h3. Why this is important    This helps by eliminating a possible bottleneck on project configurations - usually, there are multiple admins in a project, while we have just a few global admins for a multitude of projects."""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Not selecting any sub-task while performing Bulk operation loses the current search  h3. Environment    Tested on Jira Software   * 7.13.5   * 7.13.6   * 8.4    h3. summary: "Share ipa file for Jira server mobile App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Users from some organizations  are not able to run the app in the MDM (mobile device management) context. This is necessary to be able to access internal services such as Jira, which is only accessible within the company network. Because of security reasons, Jira won't be made accessible outside of their company network and must therefore run the app in the MDM context for example using Citrix Secure Hub.    Citrix Secure Hub is the launchpad for the mobile productivity apps. Users enroll their devices in Secure Hub to gain access to the app store. From the app store, they can add Citrix-developed mobile productivity apps and third-party apps (source: https://docs.citrix.com/en-us/citrix-secure-hub).    h3. Suggested Solution    Create and share the ipa file so that it can be added to the Citrix Secure Hub.     h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Option to manage email batching per project"
description: """It is cruicial for for Jira Service Desk projects or Core/Software projects with a JSD-like model to send notifications immediatelly vs batching. The good example is when customer replies to JSD ticket and support engineer receives the update with several minutes delay (plus email routing/delivery time overhead). This may be not acceptable for high priority tickets and at the moment forced us to disable notifications batching.    We would like to see the ability to enable this feature per project or at least for the beginning per project type. Not sure if this is easier or more userful than per-user (JRASERVER-69332), but it certainly can improve JSD communications with customers."""

summary: "Audit log time tracking admin events (enable/disable/configuration change) "
description: """h3. Problem Definition   * In order to track who enabled/disabled time tracking, admins have to inspect the access logs.   * It's not possible to track when and by who config changes were done to time tracking.    h3. Suggested Solution    Add any time tracking admin changes to the audit logs.  h3. Workaround    When time tracking is enabled, an HTTP POST is sent to *<CONTEXT_PATH>/secure/admin/TimeTrackingActivate.jspa*.    Therefore, admins can find out when and by who time tracking was enabled by searching for the request in the access logs under *<JIRA_INSTALL>/logs*:    (i) Be sure to replace */<CONTEXT_PATH>* below or remove it if you're not using a context path.   * For time tracking activation:       * For time tracking de-activation:  """

summary: "The Application Navigator should ignore the trailing slash while comparing URLs"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When web links are added to the Application Navigator in Jira (or any other Atlassian product) and Application Links to another product are created, those links will be shared across the two products. This may create duplicates in the Application Navigator if the links only differ in trailing slash.   h3. Suggested Solution  While comparing links to display in the menu, the Application Navigator should ignore the trailing slash.   h3. Workaround  * Delete the links and re-create them without a trailing slash.   * Make it a practice to never include the trailing slash when creating the Application Links or adding links to the Application Navigator."""

summary: "Support for custom plugin fields for issue creation and view"
description: """We have custom fields from a 3rd party plugin (Elements Connect) that are required for all issue types.    As the Jira Server mobile app currently doesn't support custom fields from plugins, we cannot create any issues using the app.    When viewing the issues, the field is not visible, but it contains critical information for us and needs to be there for the app to be useful in our setup.    Custom fields and custom fields from plugins are a critical factor in making sure the mobile app experience lines up with the desktop one."""

summary: "Clarify documentation regarding Bulk mail"
description: """When configuring a mail handler it is pretty straightforward that when receiving Bulk mail a decision will be indicated to let the handler know what to do with Bulk email.    This is not a problem by itself but in the documentation, it is not really explained that the Bulk mail """"filter"""" will apply over every other validation in the handler, and it is possible that users might expect that other validations will be done first, like the """"Catch email address"""".         The suggestion is to specify that the Bulk email validation is done before any other validation."""

summary: "Plans to end support for IE11"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  Microsoft has not released any major updates for IE11 since 2015, and has [recently discouraged the use of IE11 as a default browser|https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/Windows-IT-Pro-Blog/The-perils-of-using-Internet-Explorer-as-your-default-browser/ba-p/331732].     To allow us to continue to take advantage of modern web standards to deliver improved functionality and the best possible user experience across all of our products, we have decided to end support for IE11. *Jira 8.5 Enterprise Release will be the last IE11-supported version.*    End of support means we will not fix bugs that are specific to IE11, and will begin to introduce features that aren't compatible with this browser.     Find out more about this change from our [announcement|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Feedback-Forum-articles/Retiring-IE11-support-for-Atlassian-cloud-server-and-data-center/ba-p/1185312].    If you have questions or concerns about this change, please comment on this issue.    {panel}"""

summary: "Importing backup fails because of the NullPointerException "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    h3. Environment  Any Jira instance  h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Set up and run one node in the Jira DC  2. Perform back up   3. (!)_This configuration shouldn't be used on production it's only create for purpose to force Jira to perform actions need to reproduce this bug. On your environment nodes should have different IP addresses or different cache listener ports._(!) Add to the *clusternode* table new node with same IP address and cache listener port as the current running node. For instance:   !image-2019-09-24-13-21-51-077.png|thumbnail!   4. Import backup    h3. Expected Results   !image-2019-09-24-13-15-55-342.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results   !image-2019-09-24-13-15-18-369.png|thumbnail!   The NullPointerException is thrown in the log file    h3. Workaround  On one node -> prevent serialisation by removing bad configuration. In the example presented above:  * change proper IP address/cache ports for all nodes or  * change for not running nodes their state in database to OFFLINE   !image-2019-09-24-13-28-37-529.png|thumbnail!     Couple of nodes -> perform Project Import only on one node, do not open Import Project  page on other nodes"""

summary: "Prevent Password recovery spam"
description: """As of Jira 8.4, it is possible to abuse the password recovery system by requesting the recovery, going backwards in the web browser, and requesting it again, repeating the process as much as wanted. This can be exploited to create a possible overload of Jira Mail queue as well as the email service.    The suggestion is to implement a feature to detect when the same password recovery like a CAPTCHA to prevent automated spam of this case. """

summary: "Jira should not go into read-only mode when the license limit is exceeded"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:  Currently, going one single user over the license limit of the Jira license installed in the server will place the instance in """"read-only mode"""", with the following warning being displayed:    {{You will not be able to create new issues because the user limit for your JIRA instance has been exceeded, please contact your JIRA administrators.}}    This essentially causes an outage until admins are able to adjust the licensed users to go under the limit.    h3. Suggested Solution:  Instead of blocking all users from creating tickets, we could either:  * Block only the users who recently were granted a license that went over the limit.  * Deny users from being granted a license if that will place the instance over the license limit.    h3. Why this is needed:  The current behavior essentially causes an outage on Jira instances that face this behavior, which can be very troublesome."""

summary: "Ability to lock work logs so no changes can be made"
description: """We are currently using tempo for time tracking and although tempo does not allow users to delete worklogs from a locked time period, you are still able to do so from the """"Work Log"""" tab of the issue detail page. Tempo informed us this is out of their control and suggested to hide the """"Work Log"""" tab from the issue detail page. However, we still need the """"Work Log"""" tab of the issue detail page for reporting purposes and are requesting JIRA to add a feature to not allow deleting work logs from locked period or the ability to lock periods similar to tempo to avoid this issue from happening in the future."""

summary: "Ability to configure custom field to view date and time without applying change globally"
description: """I have searched this for a long time and did not find a straight answer or information in the documentation that provide instructions on how to custom a date field in a way that it will appear in YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM where HH is in 24 hour format, without affecting all date fields already existing in my Jira instance or project.    For our business case, we need a field that will show a time the task is due. Yes, completing a task at 10 am or at 3 pm can make it or break it for us.    If this is already implemented in Jira Server, please let me know where to find this information.    If this is not already implemented, I would like to request this.     Thanks,    Roberta     """

summary: "Add role_type_param_pid index to the projectroleactor table"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Table {{projectroleactor}} is used for storing project level permissions, example:      Table config:      * Columns {{ROLETYPE}} and {{ROLETYPEPARAMETER}} is being used during data loading in *getProjectIdsContainingRoleActorByKeyAndType*  * which is used in API *getProjectIdsContainingRoleActorByNameAndType*   * and it's being used in *ViewUserProjectRoles* page (URL: secure/admin/user/ViewUserProjectRoles!default.jspa)    Produced SQL:      h3. Suggested Solution  Add an extra cover index (ROLETYPEPARAMETER, ROLETYPE, ID) (in that order since _ROLETYPE_ has only 2 values) to the table to reduce DB load and improve loading time.      h3. Workaround   Add index manually.  * Example for MSSQL  """

summary: "Add COLUMNLAYOUT index to the columnlayoutitem table"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Table {{columnlayoutitem}} is used for storing Fields schema configs, example:      Table config:      So the table doesn't have index for the *columnlayout* column.   {{columnlayout}} is being used during data loading:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  com.atlassian.jira.issue.fields.layout.column.DefaultColumnLayoutManager#transformToColumnLayoutItems  ...   final List<GenericValue> columnLayoutItemGVs = ofBizDelegator.getRelated(""""ChildColumnLayoutItem"""", columnLayoutGV, ImmutableList.of(""""horizontalposition ASC""""));  {code}    h3. Suggested Solution  Add an extra index to the table to reduce DB load and improve loading time.    h3. Workaround   Add index manually.  * Example for MSSQL  {code:sql|borderStyle=solid}  CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX [idx_cli_columnlayout] ON [dbo].[columnlayoutitem] ([COLUMNLAYOUT])  {code}"""

summary: "Issue collector page inside Project Admin is broken when user's language is set to french"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The *Issue Collectors* page that is contained inside the *Project Settings* section of a project will not load correctly if the user has its language set to french.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go into your profile and change the display language to french.  # Load the *Issue collectors* page inside the *Project settings*.    h3. Expected Results  Page loads as expected, showing all the existing issue collectors.    h3. Actual Results  The page will load with some formatting errors, not all issue collectors will be displayed and the following exception will be thrown in the {{atlassian-jira.log}} log file:    h3. Workaround  Change the profile language to any other available language and load the page."""

summary: "View voters results in N requests"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  View voters results in N+2 requests. (N = number of votes on the issue)    h3. Environment   * Jira issue with many votes (N)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open 'View issue' page   # Click on number of voters   # Check network tab in browser    h3. Expected Results  * Dialogue with voters opens  * 1 REST call is performed    h3. Actual Results  * Dialogue opens  * N+2 requests to {{rest/api/2/issue/<ISSUE-KEY>/votes}} are performed  * Browser tab freezes    h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Hello everyone!    The issue is resolved and available in Jira Server version 8.5.2 and the correspondent Data Center version.  Version 8.6.0 which includes the change would be released soon.    Cheers,  Ignat Alexeyenko  Senior Engineer at Jira Server"

summary: "Allow setting properties and options on startup from the web interface"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Setting properties and options on startup requires accessing the application server directly per [Setting properties and options on startup|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html].     h3. Suggested Solution   As a customer, I would like the option to set properties and options on startup from Jira's web interface. """

summary: "New global permission ""create projects"""
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}   *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-12724].   {panel}  There should be a new global permission """"create projects"""".  Project Adminstrators cannot create projects. But they administer some projects of a certain domain and want to create a new project in this domain."""

summary: "Consistent and usable experience for linking issues on Jira Cloud from ""Edit Issue"""
description: """Linking issues in Jira Cloud from the """"Edit issue"""" interface seems to be much harder than it needs to be.    The auto-updating """"history search"""" only retrieves recently viewed issues. It is opaque how this is cached (browser or Atlassian server?) and how many issues it caches.    Clicking """"+"""" only allows you to select recent issues or issues from a pre-made filter. This """"Issue Selector"""" interface is more or less useless because it is unreasonable to expect the user to have set up filters for every issue in the project (filters where the issue must be in the first 50 found)    This means that you either need to have visited the issue you wish to link to recently or to memorise the issue key to find it. Most users (understandably) do not think in terms of AB-456, they think """"I want to link to that issue called """"Test the Blue Widget"""".""""    Bizarrely, when you link to something via the (old) issue view a different interface appears which allows you to perform a normal text search by keyword.    The New Issue View is very much better for linking issues so thank you for considering this in the redesign, but has so many oft-reported drawbacks it is not an option for most of your users in it's current """"beta"""" state."""

summary: "Allow hiding empty fields when using the variable ${request.details}"
description: """As of Jira Service Desk 4.4, when configuring custom notifications it is possible to use the variable ${request.details} to show fields from the issue. The problem here is that all empty fields are also included. This by itself would not be a source of confusion if it wasn't by the fact that the issue view screens do not show these fields.    The request is to allow hiding empty fields when using the ${request.details} variable to keep a consistent look over the issue information across notifications and Jira."""

summary: "jira.permission.createclone.denied workflow property breakes new createmeta REST API"
description: """h3. Summary    When an administrator adds {{jira.permission.createclone.denied}} -- to disable clone -- in a workflow property, running the *[Get create issue meta fields|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.4.1/#api/2/issue-getCreateIssueMetaFields]* (see JRASERVER-69897) fail.      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Add {{jira.permission.createclone.denied}} in a workflow property of a status.  # Verify that users won't be able to clone issues in that particular status  # Run [Get create issue meta fields|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.4.1/#api/2/issue-getCreateIssueMetaFields]    h3. Expected Results    The metadata should be returned normally    h3. Actual Results    When you are using the workflow property, the json response is empty:      As opposed to when you remove the property:      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Are Jira workflow properties going to be supported now?" [SEP] "[~pdrygas] - this is not a delta warranty, this is actually not a warranty at all; this is more of a new feature; " [SEP] "Thanks for your interest!    This issue turned out to be a feature-request for a new permission rather than a bug. Jira doesn't actually have a ""createclone"" permission. Applying “createclone” is equivalent to using the “create” (CREATE_ISSUES) permission. Differences in how permissions are checked for cloning issues and creating new ones in the frontend made it seem like 'createclone' worked for some use cases."

summary: "Workflow validators results in error when renaming class names instead of keys"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Smart Attachments vendor was refactoring their app, they encountered an issue with workflow validators. They renamed class names for their validators and conditions and looks like Jira uses class names instead of the keys as when they did the update the validators failed to operate:        h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Clone https://bitbucket.org/Vinceal/rename-workflow-item/src  # Start Jira 7.7.0  # Create """"Process Management"""" project  # Install SmartAttachements 1.17.4  # Activate SmartAttachments in project and add categories  # Edit project workflow and add validator """"Category Not Empty"""" on Create of an issue  # Upgrade to SmartAttachements 1.18.0  # Try to create an issue   !4ba3988c-d4fb-4c71-a677-7a070661787e.png|thumbnail!   Recording:  [^Jira validator class name.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  Class name is not used, the key is used instead    h3. Actual Results  Class name is use, The key is ignored.    h3. Workaround    Use the old class name"""

summary: "Add severity as a Sorting option in jira.view.issue.links.sort.order"
description: """There is a request and a requirement to have an option to order/sort the issue links based on severity instead of 'priority'.     """

summary: "Allow negative time for worklogs"
description: """We need to be able to create negative worklogs so that big corrections can be done.  The use case for this feature would be that accounting needs to be able make big corrections for a past time period. However, without the option to log e.g. """"-100h"""" this would only be possible to achieve by removing/adjusting a lot of worklogs from issues manually. Not only is this a lot of overhead, but it also distorts the worklogs of the employees which don't add up afterwards (or it would be necessary to log their billable work to a ticket for non-billable work so that it adds up again).For a big company this gets very tedious or even impossible to manage."""

summary: "Keyboard shortcuts should work in backlog view "
description: """Currently as per the KB , [Keyboard shortcuts|https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile066/jira-agile-user-s-guide/using-keyboard-shortcuts] can only work from Jira's View Issue and Issue Navigator screens only.    It would be helpful if the same shortcuts are available with Jira backlog view ."""

summary: "Jira issue history tab inconsistent date and time"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Jira issue's history tab, there is a short version of time """"updated 5 days ago"""". When you hover over the time, it'll show a longer version of time """"updated 6 days ago <time>"""".    !Time.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results    The shorter version and the longer version should display the same time.     h3. Actual Results    The longer version of time is always one more day than the shorter version of time.     h3. Workaround    [Disable relative dates in Jira|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/disable-relative-dates-in-jira-applications-414187622.html] to avoid any confusion. """

summary: "Set different default values across different projects for the custom field (Select List (single choice)) "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Not able to configure different default fields for various project in a custom field (Select List (single choice)).    Say there is a custom field called """"Levels"""" with option 1,2,3,4. Say Project A needs to have a default option 1, Project B needs default option 2, Project C needs default option 3.  h3. Suggested Solution    Option to configure the default option for each project in one _Context_ in Custom field configuration without creating a new context for each Project and repeating the options.    h3. Workaround    Add a new context for each Project and configure the options and set the default value.    This workaround is tiresome when there are a lot of projects that have the custom field which needs a different default value. Say if 10 Projects are having the custom field and each of them should have different values, we might need to configure 10 contexts and repeatedly configure the same options to set a default value.    """

summary: "When issue has multiple components, the default assignee is not set Unassigned, when following the rule assignee will be set to the default assignee of the component that is first alphabetically, rule"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When issue has multiple components, the default assignee is not set as Unassigned, when following the rule assignee will be set to the default assignee of the component that is first alphabetically, rule    h3. Environment  8.2.4 8.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce  #Create a new project   # set Default Assignee - Unassigned  # Add components    Mac Book, Lead - Mark, Assignee - Component Lead  Dell, Lead - Dave, Assignee - Project Default  # Create issue with components Dell and Mac Book  # Check Assignee it will be set as Mark    h3. Expected Results  The Assignee should be Unassigned as stated in [Managing components|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/managing-components-938847187.html]     _ If an issue has multiple components, and the default assignees of components clash, the assignee will be set to the default assignee of the component that is first alphabetically._  h3. Actual Results  Assignee set is as Mark    h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "This is expected behaviour.  However we've slightly updated our 8.4+ documentation to remove uncertanties." [SEP] "Hi    How this is not a bug?    This is in ACP-600 certification.    This mean that a lot of people tried it in Jira and got it wrong ...     " [SEP] "I have tested it in latest version." [SEP] "Hi [~izinoviev]    Looks like that even documentation is not quite right ..    It states that (https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/managing-components-938847187.html)  {quote}If an issue has multiple components, each with a different Component Lead, the default assignee will be set to the Component Lead of the component that is first alphabetically.  {quote}  So in our example it should be set as Unassigned and it will be reflecting ACP-600 one of the questions.    Currently it is setting Assignee to Mark who is the lead of Mac component which comes after the Dell component alphabetically.    So there is a BUG or something has changed really which is not documented.    Please correct me if

summary: "Ability to remove the possibility to select ALL for ""Users per page"" on the User Management page (can cause OutOfMemory)"
description: """Under *Administation > User Managment > Users* there is a dropdown menu _""""Users per page""""_.    If the total number of users is very large (read: millions), selecting """"All"""" can cause Jira to run out of memory since the frame with the user list is built into one single char object that grows to several gigabytes!    !users-per-page-all.png|thumbnail!    _*+Suggestion+:*_ A switch to take away the option """"All"""" from the _""""Users per page""""_ dropdown menu – or remove that option from being available altogether."""
comments: "Even though this only affects a small number of users of Jira the impact is very large on my current Jira assignments we have experienced a complete node failure of 7 times in the last 30 days, it's killing my SLA's it's so bad that as a tempory fix we have had to add another complete node.     "

summary: "Provide more REST API endpoints to manage screens and workflows"
description: """h3. Problem Description    There is no end point for the following for now.  * Delete Screen  * Delete Workflow  * Get all WorkflowSchemes    h3. Suggested Resolution    Provide the above REST API end point."""
