description: """We need    https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JSWCLOUD-15988    for Jira Server."""

summary: "Add ""Share"" modifications to issues to the changelog"
description: """Our users have requested changes to an issue via """"Share"""" appear in the changelog for an issue."""

summary: "Support scalable memory allocation (JVM_MINIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE and JVM_MAXIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE)"
description: """As of Jira 8.3, when configuring Jira we need to set the minimum and maximum memory using the -Xms and -Xmx parameters, and in the case of trying to use scalable parameters JVM_MINIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE and JVM_MAXIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE, Jira will still ask for -Xms and -Xmx.    Since Jira supports Java 1,8 it technically does accept the JVM_MINIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE and JVM_MAXIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE but since Jira *requires* the -Xms and -Xmx parameters, they override the percentage ones, defeating the purpose.    The request is to allow Jira to use the JVM_MINIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE and JVM_MAXIMUM_MEMORY_PERCENTAGE parameters to allow scalable memory allocation."""
comments: "This is a basic functionality in the new Java Versions, and Jira can't work with that because still watching to -Xms and -Xmx, please, Jira should't do this comprobation when starting, is a JVM configuration. "

summary: "Make UPM update not impact all other installed apps"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Upgrading UPM  triggers all apps/applications to restart because of the licensing API provided by UPM.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a Jira Server admin I want to update UPM, but I don't want all other apps/applications to depend on UPM."""

summary: "Eliminate apps downtime while performing app update in Jira Server"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Apps downtime during the app upgrade process in Jira Server ends with users not being able to use the app.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a Jira Server admin I would like apps/applications/plugins updates to never affect my end users work.  Apps upgrades cause no downtime for the app end users in Jira Server."""

summary: "Add possibility to do a rolling apps update (extend ZDU functionality)"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Apps downtime during the app upgrade process in Jira Server DC ends with users not being able to use the app.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a Jira DC admin I would like plugins/apps/applications updates to be triggered sequentially and not in parallel on my DC nodes.    The node which is currently performing the plugins/apps/applications update should be switched to MAINTENANCE mode (/status endpoint) so users are never affected by plugins/apps/applications updates.    The described above functionality might extend the existing Zero Downtime Upgrades (ZDU) functionality in Jira Data Center.    More on Zero Downtime Upgrades: [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/upgrading-jira-data-center-with-zero-downtime-938846953.html]"""

summary: "Allow the mobile app to generate application logs"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, the mobile app does not support any logging that can be easily shared to troubleshoot any problem with the app.      h3. Suggested Solution    Allow users to generate logs specific to the mobile app so it can be shared with the support team.      h3. Workaround     * For Android: https://www.appdome.com/no-code-mobile-integration-knowledge-base/gathering-android-device-logs-using-adb/  * For iOS: https://www.hexnode.com/mobile-device-management/help/obtain-ios-device-logs-using-mac-and-windows/"""

summary: "Add warnings to UPM upgrade pop-up and UPM update button"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As a Jira Server admin I do not want to be encouraged to perform operations which can have destructive results on my Jira instance like:  * clicking yes to UPM upgrade pop-up.  * clicking update UPM button  in order to prevent the apps being unintentionally restarted and unavailable for my end users.    h3. Suggested Solution  Possible solutions:   * remove the upgrade UPM pop-up  * add warnings in the UI after clicking the upgrade button  * add confirmation pop-up with warnings."""

summary: "More information about the impact of upgrading an app"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In order to know what are the implications of hitting the _""""Update""""_ button (precisely I would like to know how many plugins/apps/application will be temporary disabled).    h3. Suggested Solution  As a Jira Server admin I would like to know, what will be the impact of updating an app in Jira before I hit app/application """"Update"""" button."""
comments: "Can you give an example of where clicking Update, presumably on an app in Manage Add-ons, will make other apps (plugins) unavailable?" [SEP] "Hi [~mdoar2] - the best example would be UPM which contains the licensing API, so when UPM is disabled for updating all plugins depending on UPM are disabled and re-enabled after UPM update.     mac"

summary: "Deleting AD group does not automatically remove it from the Application Access page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Deleting an AD group does not automatically remove it from the Application Access page if you have granted this group with Application Access. Now, when you navigate to Application Access page, you will see the below error:  !app.png|thumbnail!    h3. Environment  Jira 8.3.2  Active Directory    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a group from AD  # Sync the group in Jira  # Add the group in the Application Access  # Remove the group from AD and sync the directory to Jira  # Notice that it will be removed from the Jira Groups page  # Access the Application Access page    h3. Expected Results  No error and the group is automatically removed from the Application Access    h3. Actual Results  An error message is presented and there's no way we can get rid of the error.  !app.png|thumbnail!      h3. Workaround  # Recreate the group from AD  # Sync it back to Jira  # Remove the group from the Application Access page  # Delete the group once again from AD side  # Synchronize Jira"""
comments: "As a workaround, restarting Jira would remove AD groups (deleted) from Application Access page and we can new AD groups." [SEP] "Impact: We would not able to add new AD groups (at global level) and even if we add Ad groups at Jira Project level users won't be able to login Jira." [SEP] "We have run into this bug using the Jira Internal Directory. I deleted a group without removing application access first. I was able to add the group back but then I was unable to make subsequent changes to application access via the UI. I can only make them via the api."

Cloning an issue triggers the same notification as issue created. Workaround   (Optional)"""

Disabling the notifications for creation (Request Created under Customer Notifications) in order to not receive notifications when cloning is not an option. "

summary: "Jira doesn't start when Docker variable CLUSTER=true is set"
description: """h3. Jira doesn't start when Docker variable CLUSTER=true is set  When CLUSTER=true is set on docker variable, the {{cluster.properties}} file is automatically created.   * The file includes all variables which can be manually set ([Reference - Installing Jira Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/installing-jira-data-center-938846870.html])  * Unless those variables are set in the docker config, they are generated without any value    * Source: [Bitbucket - docker-atlassian-jira - cluster.properties.j2|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian-docker/docker-atlassian-jira/src/master/config/cluster.properties.j2]    h3. Environment  * [Docker Hub - atlassian/jira-software|https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/jira-software]    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy docker image with variable CLUSTER=true  ** Example:   {code}  docker run -v jiraVolume:/var/atlassian/application-data/jira -v jiraSharedVolume:/var/atlassian/application-data/jira/shared --name=""""jira"""" -p 8080:8080 -e=CLUSTERED=true -e=JIRA_NODE_ID=node1 atlassian/jira-software  {code}  # Use """"I'll set it up myself"""" option  # Select """"Built In (for evaluation or demonstration)"""" to speed setup steps along  ** The same error is created even using your own database    h3. Expected Results  Database creation continues and Jira starts up as expected    h3. Actual Results  The Jira process is terminated.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      h3. Workaround  * Edit the {{cluster.properties}} file to remove unused parameters and restart the container"""
comments: "Fixed in latest release of Docker images "

summary: "Board icons do not match project icons for Business Type projects"

For instance: project = 'Business Type Project'   # Check the board icon.    h3. Expected Results    The board icon would be the same as the project icon.    !Screen Shot 2019-09-10 at 18.02.04.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    The board icon is not the same.    !Screen Shot 2019-09-10 at 18.02.44.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Workflow's Diagram View does not display screen associated with a transition."
description: """  h3. Issue Summary    If you select a workflow and choose to view, selecting a transition should display the screens associated with this transition. However, if you select the Diagram View and click on the transition, Jira fails to display that there is a screen associated with the transition, which can lead to confusion.    (i) Choosing to view the workflow as text displays the correct results.    h3. Environment    Any clean instance.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Click on the *Jira Administration Icon* > *Issues*.   # Select *Workflows* at the sidebar.   # Select a workflow from the list and click on *Edit*.   # Click on *Diagram* to enable the diagram view.   # Click on a *transition* such as the *Close issue* transition.   # The screen associated with the transition should be displayed at the box created after the transition has been selected. However, the screen is not displayed.    h3. Expected Results    The screen associated should be displayed.     !Screen Shot 2019-09-10 at 12.07.18.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    The screen associated is not displayed.    !Screen Shot 2019-09-10 at 12.39.01.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    If you click on *Edit* and """"add"""" the same screen, this information will be correctly displayed. """

summary: "Add Watcher Without Browse User Permission's Behaviour depends on the URL Jira is being accessed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When accessing an issue for example, via Quick Search, the URL will be like """"http://localhost:8021/jira_821/browse/TEST-3?jql=project%20%3D%20TEST"""" upon clicking TEST-3 issue. Without the browse user permission, the user should be able to add a watcher by typing its full username as per [Unable to Browse for Users and Groups|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/unable-to-browse-for-users-and-groups-120521888.html] but that's not the case.   !error.png|thumbnail!    However, accessing the issue via """"http://localhost:8021/jira_821/browse/TEST-3"""" works perfectly fine. As soon as I enter, the user will be added.  !full.png|thumbnail!    h3. Environment   * JIRA 7.10.2   * JIRA 8.2.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a user without Browse User Global permission but with *Manage Watchers* and *View Voters and Watchers*   # Ask the user to login and access a ticket via quick search where the URL in the browser will be like """"http://localhost:8021/jira_821/browse/TEST-3?jql=project%20%3D%20TEST""""   # Add watcher    h3. Expected Results  He should be able to add a watcher as per the documentation    h3. Actual Results  An error message is returned """"The value 'xxxxx' is invalid"""".     h3. Workaround  Add the watcher by accessing the issue on the full view with URL """"http://localhost:8021/jira_821/browse/TEST-3"""""""

summary: "As an JIRA Administrator I want check Lucene Index and Issues consistency "

Suggested Solution  Implement Lucene Index status and Issue consistency check    h3. Workaround  * Use REST endpoint:  GET {{<JIRA context>/rest/api/2/index/summary}}  ** Please see docs for details:  https://docs.atlassian.com/jira/REST/7.2.10/#api/2/index/summary  """

summary: "com.atlassian.jira.issue.customfields.manager.OptionsManager allows to create options for customfields of type 'multi-line text'"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    com.atlassian.jira.issue.customfields.manager.OptionsManager allows to create options for customfields of type 'multi-line text'  h3. Environment    Jira Software  h3. Steps to Reproduce    (Script)   # get a custom field (e.g. text field multi-line):   cf = ComponentAccessor.getCustomFieldManager().getCustomFieldObject(...)   # get a specific field config from that field -> I get the field config based on an issue as:   config = cf.getRelevantConfig(issue)   # create a new option for that config:   option = ComponentAccessor.getOptionsManager().createOption(config, ...)   # this last call retrieves the created option -> I can read out anything from that option, the ID, name, etc. (which from my perspective is somehow unexpected)   # what I also can do afterwards when written down the option ID: access the option from another script via:   option = optionsManager.findByOptionId(<optionID>)   by this I receive also the option and can again read out any info such as name/value, if it is disabled or not, etc.    h3. Expected Results    An error message is prompted when trying to create options for a field different from type 'select', 'radio button' and 'checkbox'.  h3. Actual Results    When the script runs on a text field, it creates an option and it is possible to disable and enable it programmatically.     """

Steps to Reproduce   # Start Jira   # Go to <Jira_URL>/secure/PostUpgradeLandingPage.jspa   !Screenshot 2019-09-09 at 21.06.53.png|thumbnail!    # Click """"Take me to Jira"""" button in the lower right corner    h3. Expected Results  You are taken to the Jira home page (by default System Dashboard)    h3. Actual Results  Button does nothing    h3. Workaround  You can use the top panel to navigate to any of the Jira views, e.g. to Dashboard.    h3. summary: "Add some validator and error message in JIRA when a user enters a date using dd/MM/yy, while the input date format is set to dd/MM/yyyy"
description: """*Steps to Reproduce:*   # Go to _Administration > General Configuration > Advanced_.   # Change the *jira.date.picker.java.format* from *d/MMM/yy* to *dd/MM/yyyy*.   # Create or edit an issue and type a number date in the Due Date field with format *d/m/yy* such as *1/1/14* for example   # Then click create or edit.   # After it is created or updated, go to the issue and click edit.    *Expected Results:*    It will give that the format entered is wrong or the date typed in the field 1/1/14 it will 01/01/2014 stored in the field.    *Actual Results:*    The date typed is accepted and the Due Date field is storing 01/01/0014 instead of 2014.    *Notes*    It was decided in this original bug ticket that this behavior was actually not a bug, but an expected behavior due to the following reason:    source:   [http://docs.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/index.html?java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html]    Having said that, in order to avoid any confusion for JIRA users, it would be great to do a check on JIRA side on the input data before the date gets processed in the backend by the Java class.     *Suggestion*    Add some validator and error message in JIRA when a user enters a date using dd/MM/yy, while the input date format is set to dd/MM/yyyy, so that the wrong year does not get set in JIRA."""

Example 2:   We have server cancellation emails that create Jira tasks, and the due date is two weeks from the date of cancellation. WE put the date in the subject, but it would be best if we could also set the due date, for the same reason noted above.    I propose having something parsable from the subject or body, such

summary: "Template injection  in Jira importers plugin - CVE-2019-15001"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There was a server-side template injection vulnerability in Jira Server and Data Center, in the Jira Importers Plugin (JIM). An attacker with """"JIRA Administrators"""" access can exploit this issue. Successful exploitation of this issue allows an attacker to remotely execute code on systems that run a vulnerable version of Jira Server or Data Center.     Affected version:   * Versions of Jira Server & Jira Data Center starting with 7.0.10 before 7.6.16, from 7.7.0 before 7.13.8 (the fixed version for 7.13.x), from 8.0.0 before 8.1.3 (the fixed version for 8.1.x), from 8.2.0 before 8.2.5 (the fixed version for 8.2.x), and from 8.3.0 before 8.3.4 (the fixed version for 8.3.x) , and from 8.4.0 before 8.4.1 (the fixed version for 8.4.x)  are affected by this vulnerability.     h3. Fix    We have released the following versions of Jira Server & Jira Data Center to address this issue:    * 8.4.1 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/download  * 8.3.4 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 8.2.5 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 8.1.3 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 7.13.8 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 7.6.16 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update    We have released the following versions of Jira Software Server to address this issue:    * 8.4.1 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/download  * 8.3.4 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 8.2.5 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 8.1.3 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 7.13.8 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update  * 7.6.16 which is available for download from https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update    For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/jira-security-advisory-2019-09-18-976766250.html]."""
comments: "This is an independent assessment and you should evaluate its applicability to your own IT environment.    CVSS v3 score: 9.1 => Critical severity    *Exploitability Metrics*  || Attack Vector | Network |  || Attack Complexity | Low |  || Privileges Required | High |  || User Interaction | None |    *Scope Metric*  || Scope | Changed |    *Impact Metrics*  || Confidentiality | High |  || Integrity | High |  || Availability | High |    https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H" [SEP] "Is a fix planned for Jira Server 8.0.3? -> 8.0.4"

summary: "Documentation on Bulk Issue Creation references fields only available on External System CSV Import"
description: """The CSV Importer offers two functionality to users, one available to general users and other restricted to Jira Admin.    {color:#ff0000}*The documentation of the first references fields only available to the other.*{color}    {color:#172b4d}Target documentation to change: {color} [Creating issues using the CSV importer|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/creating-issues-using-the-csv-importer-939937906.html]  h2. Creating issues using the CSV importer (general users)   * Users must have Create Issue and Bulk Edit permissions.    Documentation: [Creating issues using the CSV importer|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/creating-issues-using-the-csv-importer-939937906.html]   Documentation references these 5 fields that are only available on the """"Admin Version"""" of this functionality:   * Worklog   * Project Key   * Project Name   * Attachments   * Comment Body    h2. Importing data from CSV (Jira Admins)   * Only available to Jira Admins on the Cog > System > Import and Export    Documentation: [Importing data from CSV|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-csv-938847533.html]      h3. Additional info    It'd be interesting if, on the [bulk creating doc|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/creating-issues-using-the-csv-importer-939937906.html], we presented an infobox somehow alerting about the the fields only available on the [Jira Admin's External System Import|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-csv-938847533.html]:  ||Field||  |Worklog|  |Project (key, name and type)|  |Attachments|  |Comment Body|  |Status|  |Resolution|  |Date Resolved|  |Date Modified|  |Date Created|  h3. Steps to reproduce   # Create a csv file with the following fields, besides whatever fields are required to the project: Worklog, Project name, project key, project type, Attachments, Comment Body, Status, Resolution, Resolved Date, Updated Date, Created Date   # Map the fields accordingly and perform a successful import on Admin's External System CSV Import   # Save the configuration file   # Perform a Bulk Issue Creation on Issues > Import Issues from CSV and use the same csv file and same configuration file    The result is that the fields listed above won't be available to map, forcing the user to select other fields instead.     """

We also made the decision to setup confluence. For now we want to have a very limited pilot project to learn how we could integrate confluence in our processes. Therefore we found the following helm scipts [https://mox.sh/helm/charts/confluence-server/]. But we

summary: "Using certain JQL functions causes a ClauseTooComplexSearchException when many values exist in the database"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Certain JQL functions can trigger a ClauseTooComplexSearchException if too many search results are returned when using the functions, including (but not limited to):  * worklogDate  * worklogAuthor  * fixVersion  * closedSprints() - JSWSERVER-20283     In the UI, the search fails, and this error is presented:   !image-2019-09-05-16-52-00-762.png|thumbnail!     By default, Jira is configured with a limit of up to 65000 search clauses - if the function will return more than this number of issues, the exception will be thrown. This is true even if the overall JQL query will not return less than 65000 issues; for example, the overall query can be constrained with a term such as (issuekey = TEST-123), which should only return 1 issue, and it will still fail.    h3. Environment    Tested in Jira 7.6.9 and Jira 7.13.6    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create at least 65000 issues which have worklog entries. The [Data Generator for Jira|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview] is effective for creating these issues, but should never be used in a Production environment.  # Re-index the environment as needed to ensure that all issues are searchable  # Perform a query that uses the affected function (i.e. worklogDate < now()) so that over 65000 issues will be returned.    h3. Expected Results    The search succeeds, and the full result set is returned.    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      The following error is shown in the UI, and the search does not return any values:  {quote}An unknown error occurred while trying to perform a search.{quote}    h3. Notes    This is very similar, or identical, to [JSWSERVER-16401 : Having a huge set of versions causes Boards to break|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-16401], which is the same problem when using *fixVersion*.    h3. Workaround    It is possible to work around this issue by increasing the number of allowable clauses used in searches.     * (!) Not recommended. Increase the default limit of 65000, but it is not known how this will affect future performance of the system. This can be done by increasing the value for jira.search.maxclauses in *jira-application.properties* file located in following directory:    """
comments: "A user of mine ran into this again today. It's strange that the issues are expanded to issues prior to other filters. That at least would allow limiting the results to be small enough to be searchable." [SEP] "This problem could be avoided if JQL order of operations was controlled by the end user.    For example: _project = XYZ and worklogDate <= 2020-09-09_  Let's assume that this project has 1000 issues, _worklogDate <= 2020-09-09_ returns 100000 issues, and the overall query should only return 10 issues.    Currently, both searches are performed independently and then the results are merged. However, the second clause would break and thus the entire query would break.    What if the query was processed in the order is appears: from left to right?     In that case _project = XYZ_ would go first and return 1000 issues. Then _worklogDate <= 2020-09-09_ would be applied in that subset of 1000 issues and 10 issues would be the result of the query.    If the user wants the second condition to be process

summary: "NullArgumentException (HTTP 500) when anonymously browsing a dashboard that has edit permissions"
description: """h3. Summary  Browsing a public dashboard anonymously (with no login) gives an HTTP 500 in the UI.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a dashboard   # Make the dashboard public (view permissions: Anyone)  # Grant edit permissions to one user  # Grant the global permission to create shared objects to Anyone  # Browse the dashboard through its direct URL    h3. Expected Results  The dashboard should display fine    h3. Actual Results  The following is shown in the screen  {quote}  Sorry, we had some technical problems during your last operation.  {quote}   The following exception is logged:       h3. Notes  The issue doesn't occur if:  * the global permission to create shared objects is not granted to Anyone; or  * edit permissions to the dashboard are not shared with a user, a group or a project role    h3. Workaround  # Revoke the global permission to create shared from Anyone; or  # Remove all edit permissions to the dashboard."""

summary: "Copying an workflow in new browser tab causes error 500"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a double copy of a simplified workflow occurs, trying to edit the workflow or create another draft results in 500 errors    h3. Environment    Jira Server 7.6.4, 7.6.14 and 7.13.6 were used to reproduce this bug.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to System / Issues / Workflows  # Right click on Copy link for a workflow, to open copy operation in a new window  # Double-click on Copy    h3. Expected Results    A new workflow gets created via Copy.    h3. Actual Results    Different results occur depending on the copied workflow.  For a simplified workflow, a 500 error page titled """"Oops..."""" appears.  For other workflows, the error appears on the page that a workflow with this name already exists, there is no data corruption however.     The access log shows an error 500 on the return response.  The application log shows an error like:        Furthermore, in the case of this error 500 happening with a duplicated simplified workflow, all the workflows disappear from the admin page. Afterwards workflows are no longer editable. When clicking on workflow in a project, the error appears:    {quote}  The Jira server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation.  {quote}    h3. Notes    This happens because of wrong user action (double-click), however the page logic should prevent a double-click from occurring.    h3. Workaround    The extra workflow needs to be deleted from the database (the duplicated workflow from 'jiraworkflows' will have the same name as the previous copy).  """

summary: "Allow Jira/Confluence to link issues automatically"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, In order to show Jira issues in """"Jira links"""" in Confluence page, users will have to mention the Jira issue in the Confluence page.     Some users would like function where they would like to mention the Confluence page (Eg: in the Description field) and they would like to see both the Jira issue and Confluence page linked together. When this action is done, Confluence should also display the Jira issue which the Confluence link is added under """"Jira links"""".      h3. Suggested Solution    Allow Jira/Confluence to create a reciprocal link when a Confluence page link is added to the issue description.      h3. With the Atlassian Marketplace growth, there are many Apps supporting the need to cutover completely from Salesforce to JSD and maintain a CRM-like schema. However, the JIRA External System Import and Salesforce integration component is missing to make the cutover a success. Will there be any effort to making this Import Feature available with option to import attachments from Salesforce?"""

summary: "ALM JIRA Links - missing resolution field along with the Status field"
description: """For any ALM JIRA project, the Links list is missing for each linked JIRA issues, their respective Resolution field next to the Status field.    Not seeing the Resolution field for each linked issue is problematic as unable to know at first look the reason why a linked issue was closed. Ex.: Is a linked issue closed resolved, closed no change, closed duplicate, or closed withdrawn?   For each case a different outcome when time comes to close a parent issue, reviewing all linked blocking descendants for their respective closure resolution.    We had a couple issues where parents got closed resolved from combination of descendants that were closed - no change or closed withdrawn, leading to prematurely closing the parent as resolved when none of its linked descendants actually did any fix. The parent was therefore not fixed at all, but got advertised to our customer as having been fixed.    So to improve WP closure efficiency and accuracy, and minimize mistakes, it would be a premium to have both the Status and the Resolution fields shown side by side for each linked descendants on the Links list."""

summary: "Incorrect profile URL when clicking on an assignee profile from the version page"
description: """h3. +Issue description+    If the username of a user is changed, when clicking on the Assignee of a ticket from the page *Project Settings > Versions > Click on any version*, the profile URL is generated using the old username. If we click on that profile URL, we are redirected to an invalid page and we get the error:  {quote}  The specified user could not be found.  {quote}      h3. +Steps to reproduce+    # Create a new user in *⚙ > User Management > Users* with user name jrey20  # Assign this user to a Jira ticket  # Create a version in the project the ticket was created in, and add this version to the ticket  # Change the username from jrey20 to jrey21.  # Open the issue search page  # Hover over the assignee and verify that the profile link is correct and points to jrey21:  !IssueSearch.png|thumbnail!  # Open the page Releases and click on a version (1.0) to view the list of tickets with this version    h3. +Workaround+    Change the username back to the original username"""

summary: "CSV exports of JQL results may include all custom fields even when only a single project is represented in the result set"
description: """When performing a search with a JQL and exporting the results to CSV file, the CSV file will include custom fields that has specific context for projects that are not included in the result set.  h3. Steps to Reproduce:    1) Having the following scenario with 2 custom fields, each associated with a different project contexts:    _Custom Field A_   _Context: Project A_    _Custom Field B_   _Context: Project B_    2) Create a JQL query that contains results for just one of the projects, for example:    JQL: {{text ~ """"profit""""}}   Results: 1 (ProjectB-1)   Projects included in results: Project B    3) Export the results to a CSV file (All Fields):  h3. Observed behaviour    Both custom fields are exported to the CSV file: """"Custom Field A"""", """"Custom Field B"""" as columns.   We can see """"Custom Field A"""" column in the CSV file, however, there are no issues in the query that belongs to Project A.  h3. Expected behaviour    Only """"Custom Field B"""" should be exported to the CSV file."""

summary: "Change in sub-task work calculations"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}   *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-25057].   {panel}  The way that Jira handles sub-tasks is inconsistent with standard project management methodology.  A sub-task, by definition, is a fractional component of a parent task.  In other words, a parent task can be broken down into multiple smaller steps, each of which is a sub-task.  Assuming there is an complete listing of sub-tasks, once all of the sub-tasks are completed, the parent task is also complete.  So that if a parent task is estimated to take 10 hours to complete, it can be broken down into, for instance, five two-hour sub-tasks.  The time required to complete the sub-tasks does not add to the total time of the parent task; rather, the time for the parent task is already inclusive of the sub-task time.    Jira, however, treats sub-tasks as """"related tasks,"""" which is a completely different concept. When a sub-task is created and a time estimate entered, that time estimate adds to the total time of the parent task, which then results in inflated project time estimates and an inability to effectively track and report on the remaining work because time is getting double counted -- once in the sub-task and once in the parent task.  The only workaround for this situation is to enter a zero time estimate for the sub-task and to log work against the sub-task in the parent task, which does solve the problem but removes the ability to track estimate accuracy against the sub-tasks within Jira.    To resolve this situation, Jira would be improved by not adding time estimate for a sub-task to the total estimate for a parent task unless the total estimated time for all of the sub-tasks for a given parent task exceeded the original estimate for the parent task.  Logging time against a sub-task should then reduce the total remaining time for the sub-task as well as the parent task.  """
comments: "I think the option to change the calculation needs adding to the project admin area so this can be determined by the individual project."

summary: "Finding membership for groups which has duplicated name can cause slowness access issues to certain pages of Jira instance "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Having duplicated names for the group while having a large number of groups in Jira instance will cause DB  """"_duplicate key value violates unique constraint_"""" error. This will cause the error while loading the data for {{readUsersInGroupsByDirectory}} method. Which will lead to different side-effects and possible instance slowness.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create more than:   ** 30 000 memberships for postgresql database,   ** 2 000 MSSQL database    h3. Expected Results    Jira works healthy without downtimes  h3. Actual Results    A lot of threads are waiting on the *activeUsersCountForLicense* cache in the *DefaultApplicationRoleManager* class   For instance you can find such threads:    and you can find in the log stack traces which show that we failed to refresh *activeUsersCountForLicense* cache for instance:    You may also see 500 access issues when loading the Manage Apps page or Versions and Licenses:  h3. Workaround    1. Decrease number of groups to less than 30 000. It can be checked how many records is in database by this sql:    2. Filter out unneeded groups/users from [LDAP|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/reducing-the-number-of-users-synchronized-from-ldap-to-jira-applications-938847054.html] by editing [LDAP filters|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-write-ldap-search-filters-792496933.html].  or    3. Make sure that you do not have duplication in the lower names of the group and nested groups."""
comments: "Hello everyone!    The issue is resolved and available in Jira Server version 8.5.2 and the correspondent Data Center version.  Version 8.6.0 which includes the change would be released soon.    Cheers,  Ignat Alexeyenko  Senior Engineer at Jira Server"

summary: "Tool to massively export issues in readable format that doesn't affect performance"
description: """We need to massively collect Jira information (millions of issues) and export it to an analytics tool, and we need to have access control in these massive operations.    We know two ways of doing this: generating an output file (XML) or a Bulk API operation. These two significantly affect Jira's performance. Today we already do this via API generating a huge volume of IO accesses due to pagination of queries.    Would be great if there is a built-in tool to easily export a massive number of issues while not affecting so much the performance of the application."""
comments: "What about using a read-only real-time replica of the Jira database? Then a view on the DB to restrict which projects' data can be used.  This plugin https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211840/inquisitor-plugin-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview adds a REST API that will let you download about 1000 issues/second but not their custom fields"

summary: "Improve the description under the Catch Email Address setting of JIRA Mail Handlers"
description: """h3. Issue Description    When configuring a mail handler in *JIRA > ⚙ > System > Incoming Mail*, we can see that following description is displayed under the *Catch Email* Address* field:  {quote}  If set, only emails having the specified recipient in fields To, Cc or Bcc will be processed.  {quote}      The problem is that since the *Bcc:* header will be removed *most of the time* by the mail server that is sending the email to the recipients mentioned in *Bcc:*, there might not be any scenario or use case where the mail handler will actually catch the email address from the *Bcc:* (since it might never be there).    The only scenario where the *Bcc:* won't be removed (tested in gmail) is when the email address in the *From:* and in the *To:* headers are the same: in this case, the email received by the sender (who is the same as the recipient) will still contain the *Bcc:* header. But this use case does not seem to be a scenario that will be used by our customers, since most of the time the email in the *From:* and in the *To:* headers will be different.    h3. Suggested solution    Improve the description of the *Catch Email Address* setting by mentioning that:  - the JIRA Mail Handler will catch the email address in the *BCC:* header *only if it's there*  - since the mail server will likely remove the *Bcc:* header, therefore we don't recommend adding the catch email address to the *BCC:* header, but to the *To:* and *Cc:* header instead"""

summary: "Updating an issue causes TransactionRuntimeException or ""current transaction is aborted"" errors"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a node tries to register a cluster lock, it will try to register it in a local memory, and then register it in the database, which we expect may fail as another node may have already created this entry.    The problem happens if this code (that is creating the cluster lock) is executed within an existing database transaction. In that case, the whole transaction is marked to be rolled back, rather than accepting the error as part of the algorithm.    h3. Environment  * Jira Data Center 8.3+    h3. Steps to Reproduce  This issue can be reproduced with regular actions such as:  * archiving multiple issues in a row.  * transitioning issues.    h3. Expected Results  Operations would complete with no errors.    h3. Actual Results  Users may see an error on the screen:  |!example1.png|thumbnail!|!example2.png|thumbnail!|     These exceptions are thrown in the {{atlassian-jira.log}} file:              h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Remove old CreateMeta API"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Based on JRASERVER-42282, executing requests to {{issue/createmeta}} on larger instances can take over a minute to complete and generate large content (500MB+).  The main problem with the old {{createmeta}} endpoint is that it is duplicating values in the response. Although the response can be filtered, due to the design of the endpoint it is very easy to cause downtime.    h3. Suggested Solution  We have been working on a fix for this problem most of the solutions made the endpoint harder and more complex to use. So we decided to *deprecate* the old endpoint and introduce new endpoints that will replace the old createmeta endpoint.    (!)   Changes will be released with Jira 8.4.0 and the *old createmeta will be removed in Jira 9*. We suggest anyone who is using the old endpoint to migrate to the new endpoints.    You can find more details [Createmeta REST endpoint to be removed|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/createmeta-rest-endpoint-to-be-removed-975040986.html]     h3. Workaround   None  """

summary: "Create new REST API to list issue types and fields with a scope"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Based on JRASERVER-42282, executing requests to {{issue/createmeta}} on larger instances can take over a minute to complete and generate large content (500MB+).    h3. Suggested Solution  Create the new endpoints will return projects, issue types and fields separately. You can find more details [Createmeta REST endpoint to be removed|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/createmeta-rest-endpoint-to-be-removed-975040986.html]     h3. Workaround   None  """
comments: "What is the plan for bringing these new APIs to Jira Cloud? The same problem with slow responses on createmeta calls exists there too. When the APIs for Jira Cloud and Jira Server diverge it makes it hard to support the same plugins & integrations across both."

summary: "ProjectComponentManager.findByComponentName shouldn't be case sensitive"
description: """The findByComponentName method of the ProjectComponentManager class accepts the “String componentName” parameter, but currently, it's case sensitive. The issue is that cannot have components with the same names but different cases, so it shouldn't matter the casing in for this parameter.    The suggestion is to use """"toUpperCase()"""" method to ignore the case in this search.    The current workaround is to use the """"findByComponentNameCaseInSensitive"""" method, although this one doesn't accept the project ID as a parameter and you need to filter the projects."""

summary: "Improve the documentation about connecting JIRA to LDAP to explain what happens when a user is deleted from LDAP"
description: """*Issue Summary*    In our official documentation [Connecting Jira to an LDAP directory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-to-an-ldap-directory-938847052.html], there is no mention of what happens to a user who has already been sync into JIRA when this user is deleted on the LDAP side.    *Suggested improvement*    Add a section to the documentation that explains what happens when a user is deleted from the LDAP directory, based on the following information:  {panel}  If a user is removed from the LDAP directory, the following will happen:  - if the user does not have any activity in Jira, this user will be automatically deleted from Jira on the next sync between Jira and the LDAP  - if the user already has some activity in Jira, this user will be set to """"inactive"""", which means that this user will still exist in Jira's user database, but won't consume any Jira license and won't be able to login    A user is considered to have some activity in Jira if this user:  - added comments to Jira tickets  - is set to any user picker field in Jira tickets (for example: assignee, reporter, etc...)  {panel}"""

summary: "Include option to always allow for status change when moving issues between projects"
description: """h3. Problem Definition     When moving an issue between two projects, if the issue has the status that is present in the other project, Jira will skip the prompt to change status.     In addition, Jira will not allow you to go to the status change tab.    Admins at times want to allow the option to change the status even when the statuses are the same.   h3. Suggest Solution    Allow the option to still change statuses even when they are the same.   h3. Workaround    Before moving issues to another project, change the status to a status not available in the workflow of the new project.      """

summary: "Improve the documentation about the Jira Mail Handler (catch email address setting)"
description: """h3. Issue Description    In our [documentation about the Jira Core Mail Handler|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver079/creating-issues-and-comments-from-email-950288929.html], we mention the following point related to the *Catch Email Address* setting:  {quote}  If specified, only email messages whose To:, Cc:, Bcc: lines contain the recipient specified in this field will be processed — for example, issues@mycompany.com  {quote}    The problem is that since the *Bcc:* header will be removed *most of the time* by the mail server who is sending the email to the recipients mentioned in *Bcc:*, it seems that there won't be any scenario or use case where the mail handler will actually catch the email address from the *Bcc:* (since it might never be there).    We have 2 open bug tickets about the fact that the *Catch Email* setting does not work when the email address is in *Bcc:*:  - for JIRA Cloud: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-44680  - for JIRA Server: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-44680    However, it is not JIRA bug, since the problem comes down to the fact that by design, mail servers will remove the *Bcc:* header.    The only scenario where the *Bcc:* won't be removed (tested in gmail) is when the email address in the *From:* and in the *To:* headers are the same: in this case, the email received by the sender (who is the same as the recipient) will still contain the *Bcc:* header. But this use case does not seem to be a scenario that will be used by our customers, since most of the time the email in the *From:* and in the *To:* headers will be different.    h3. Suggested solution    Improve our documentation by mentioning that:  - the JIRA Mail Handler will catch the email address in the *BCC:* header *only if it's there*  - since the mail server will likely remove the *Bcc:* header, therefore we don't recommend adding the catch email address to the *BCC:* header, but to the *To:* and *Cc:* header instead"""
comments: "fixed for 7.13 and later"

summary: "Not possible to search for Confluence Cloud spaces when linking Confluence page from Jira issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It is not possible to search for Confluence Cloud spaces when linking Confluence page from Jira issue. User get an error """"Unable to collect information from the selected Confluence instance"""".  h3. Environment   * Jira Server, application link to Confluence Cloud    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Link Confluence Cloud instance to Jira Server   # Go to an Issue > Link > Confluence page > Search for a page    h3. Expected Results    The space search is performed and results are returned.  h3. Actual Results    The following error is thrown in the browser:  {code:java}  """"Unable to collect information from the selected Confluence instance""""  {code}  Enabling {{org.apache.http}} log reveals that Jira is hitting Confluence Cloud endpoint that does not exist:    h3. Notes    *The actual cause for this bug is SOAP / XML-RPC deprecation on Confluence*: [https://blog.developer.atlassian.com/confluence-cloud-soap-api-migration/]. However, due to the fact that the endpoint has been preserved on Confluence Server, we cannot reproduce that issue if Jira is linked to Confluence Server.    The page search method should be replaced with {{GET /wiki/rest/api/space}}.  h3. Workaround    Link a Confluence page directly to Jira issue."""

summary: "Perform exports from snapshot rather than live data"
description: """Original problem raised in PS with data consistency of Jira XML export:    We have detected that the broken foreign key relation is broken because the AO_8B1069_ACTIVITY_ENTRY gets some rows added during the export. We removed all modify permissions from the Permission Schemes while the export was done, but these are inserted via XRay REST API. So, those rows don't appear in the activeobjects.xml file, but rows from AO_8B1069_ACTIVITY_ITEM referencing them appear.    The suggestion was made regarding the data consistency of exports:    I would suggest that the export is done from a snapshot of the database (maybe create the export from inside a serializable transaction). I cannot tell for sure, but I think some of the data (maybe entities.xml) is taken from a coherent snapshot, as we suffered some issues with the size of the rollback/undo tablespace in Oracle, which suggests that a long transaction is being run during the backup. However, the information from the active objects or Add-ons tables seems not to be coherent during the backup.      It might be as easy as running the whole export inside a transaction if the database backend supports it (maybe it needs to be a """"serializable transaction"""", the most strict type, to avoid that inserts done by other transactions during the export are visible). Running inside a transaction might require a big rollback/undo buffer, so maybe this should be included as an option (the user can choose to run the export inside a transaction to ensure integrity at the cost of performance or requiring additional space on the database server).    This should not be quite difficult to achieve and would fix many issues related to exports, that are not detected until you try the import."""

summary: "Drag/drop issues in a Sprint (planning mode)  would cause the issue to go back to Backlog"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When dragging/dropping issues in a Sprint, it will initiate 2 AJAX requests (noticeable in instance with performance problem). In between the requests (after the 1st request but before the 2nd request), it is possible to drag/drop other issues. Once the 2nd request is made, it will cause the issue to be moved to the Backlog.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  (!) The Bug is heavily dependent on the instance's performance  # Have a Backlog with over 1000 issues, create a Sprint  ** To increase the probability, expand the backlog to show all the issues  # Drag/drop any issues in the Sprint  ** This will initiate the 2 AJAX requests (the screen will be greyed out twice)  # In between the 2 AJAX requests, drag/drop some other issue in the Sprint  ** You will notice that the dragged issue will appear to have a duplicate:   !DoubleIssue.png|thumbnail!   # Once the 2nd AJAX request occurs, the dragged issue will be moved to the backlog    h3. Expected Results  Issues would be moved in the Sprint and not moved out to the Backlog    h3. Actual Results  The issue will be moved out to the Backlog    h3. Suggested solution:  There should be a lock between the 2 AJAX requests, hence users would not be able to drag/drop issues.    h3. Workaround  None"""
comments: "We used to have this issue all the time. After installing Jira 8.5.1 the situation has improved, but once in a while issues still jump from the active sprint to the backlog. So I think the fix did not fully address the issue."

summary: "UX: Facilitate natural discovery of global looping transitions"
description: """Natively, Jira counts with a great and very convenient feature: [global reflexive transitions|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-articles/How-to-get-to-the-next-level-in-workflow-design/ba-p/1135968].    However, that feature is really difficult to discover, as it currently requires to:    # Enter the workflow editor in diagram mode.  # Click on the Add transition button (please, note that the most popular method for adding transitions is drag&drop on status nodes).  # Select Any status in the From status dropdown field.  # Select Itself in the To status dropdown field.  # Add a Name & click on the Save button.    Counting with more intuitive ways through the UI for creating this kind of transition would be desirable.    Maybe it would be a good idea prompting a dialog on creating a looping transition (a transition from a status to itself). The dialog could show a concise text and a couple of buttons:    * Text: Looping transitions are frequently useful from other statuses too. Would you like to create a global looping transition instead?  * Button 1: Make global  * Button 2: No, thanks    Additionally, a Read more or icon would ideally link to a documentation page which explained the advantages of using global looping transitions."""

summary: "JIRA renders wrong value when entering a large value in Estimated Time field when time tracking format is set to 'days'."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA renders wrong value when entering a large value in Estimated Time field when time tracking format is set to 'days'.  h3. Works fine for small values.    h3. Expected Results    The estimated time should render the correct values for even large values entered in the estimated time field.  h3. Actual Results    The values are rendered as three-dimensional and much bigger than the original number.  h3. Notes    Able to replicate this behavior on 7.13.0, 8.2.2  h3. summary: "Looping Transitions Workflow returns a Broken link for Advanced Configuration after creation."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Creating a Looping transitions on a workflow from """"Any Status"""" to """"Itself"""" after creation, you cannot be able to use the Advanced Configuration options as the links to it would be broken.    h3. Environment  Jira Server    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Goto any Workflow, add the """"Any Status"""" to """"Itself"""" transition and give it a name   # Click on the newly added transition, on the right side of the screen where the Workflow Advanced configuration pops up for ( Properties, Conditions, Post-function etc.)    # Clicking on any of those URL links shows a 404 error.    h3. Expected Results    The URL link should be able to work    h3. Actual Results    The Link appears to have appended a workflow step parameter with """"undefined"""" value and thus returns a 404 page when clicked.     !404-error.png|thumbnail!     h3. summary: "As a Jira administrator, I would like to bypass permissions and be able to browse all projects"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira administrators would benefit from having a way to bypass permissions and browse all projects and issues. This would be useful for troubleshooting issues such as JQL queries returning unexpected resiults, since issues from projects for which the user doesn't have *Browse Project* permission wouldn't be returned.    In large Jira instances it might not be practical to change all permission schemes in order to include all administrators before troubleshooting an issue.    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement a feature to toggle bypass of permissions for some users or groups. For example, Jira System Administrators.  """
comments: "Even in a large instance (we have 75 permission schemes) you can change the permission schemes to give the jira-administrators group the Administer project permission. There are ScriptRunner and other scripts out there to make these kind of bulk changes. Creating new permission schemes is not usually a common operation, and you do have to remember to make this change. I think the harder case to detect is when Issue Security Schemes stop Jira admins from seeing specific issues within a project. "

summary: "Load balancer status URL improvements"
description: """Having a """"/status"""" endpoint for load balancer health checks is good, but some simple enhancements could make load balancer integration much more functional.  I can see two compelling use cases:  1. When shutting down one cluster member for maintenance/upgrade:  As of today (8.3/4.3), the minimally disruptive way to shut down one cluster node is   -- Go to load balancer, disable node1   -- Shut down Jira on node 1   -- Start up Jira on node 1   -- Go to load balancer, re-enable node1   and repeat for node 2 in (say) a rolling upgrade.     If Jira would change the return code from /status to (e.g.) """"STOPPING"""" for a small period of time before shutting down, the manual interaction with the load balancer could be avoided. The shutdown delay would need to be tunable (e.g. via a property), so customers could match behaviour with the load balancer health check interval.    2. When performing an offline re-index.    The post-upgrade procedure for a rolling upgrade suggests that you should:  -- Remove one node from the load-balancer  -- Perform an offline index  -- Add the node back in to the load-balancer  -- Copy the resulting index to the other nodes    Now, this is /possible/ by adding additional listeners to server.xml and connecting directly to the target host, but when combined with SAML login it starts to require serious gymnastics.      It could be done automatically, however, by adding a 'cluster-aware offline rebuild' mode that :  -- set /status to return (e.g.) """"OFFLINE""""  -- wait long enough for the load balancer to notice  -- perform the offline rebuild  -- set /status to return """"RUNNING"""" again  -- copy resulting index to the other nodes.    Regards,"""

summary: "Database Latency Check fails on SQL Server when using a schema other than dbo"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Database Latency Check feature implemented on JRASERVER-66980 fails on SQL Server when Jira's database uses a schema other than 'dbo'.  h3. Environment    Jira 7.13.3 or later   SQL Server: 2012, 2014, 2016  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a database for Jira   # Create a database user which Jira will connect as   # Create an empty 'schema' in the database for the Jira tables   # Install Jira and connect to the newly created database    h3. Expected Results    The query to normally execute and show results under *Jira admin > System > System Info > Database latency measurement milliseconds*.  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    Also, *Database latency measurement milliseconds* field gets replaced in Jira's UI and shows: *Error accessing database (more in logs) java.lang.RuntimeException: Exception while measuring database query select count( * ) from productlicense*.    If the Jira Admin generates a Support.zip file, the line above can be spotted on the *application.xml* file almost as if it was a system property right after the database collation settings:    h3. Notes    The query that checks latency doesn't take into account the database schema. Instances running under the default 'dbo' schema will likely never see this problem.    This also affects *Integrity checker* and *support zip* generation.    h3. Workaround    If SQL Server hosts only the Jira database, then it's possible to change the schema for all tables to use dbo by following the steps at [Changing the schema for all JIRA tables under a specific schema.|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/changing-the-schema-for-all-jira-tables-under-a-specific-schema-282173461.html]"""
comments: "This is a severe problem for our organization.   We can not upgrade Plugins.  And the two that I attempted to upgrade are now disabled.         Using Jira Version 8.3.2 with UPM version 4.0.7" [SEP] "The workaround contradicts [Atlassian's instructions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-sql-server-2012-938846858.html] for Jira installation, specifically the instructions to *create* a schema called _jiraschema_.    So which is it?  Stay consistent with the database installation instructions, or follow this workaround?" [SEP] "This is a problem. I can't reindex my Jira system and it's blocking me from doing an upgrade. I'm running Jira 8.2 on Windows 2012 with MSSQL 2012." [SEP] "It's amazing how the Atlassian documentation for setting up a database calls to specifically create a schema for the Jira tables.  Yet, for things like this, and other knowledge articles they always assume that you don't have a separate schema as all queries are done non-qualified.

summary: "Allow admins to grant user access to the External System Import functionality"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    At the moment, only admins are able to perform full CSV imports. For all other users in the instance, the access is limited to the *Bulk Create* functionality, which allows issue creation from CSV files. The *Bulk Create* option is limited, as it doesn't allow for fields like *Resolution* and *Status* to be imported due to the nature of the functionality.    h3. Suggested Solution    Add a Global Permission which can grant user/group access to the *External System Import*.    h3. Why this is important    This will allow users that are not System Administrators in the instance to import some of the fields not supported by the Bulk Create functionality. With the permission, admins will be able to dictate which users in the instance will have access to this functionality."""

summary: "Do not base user keys on usernames"
description: """h4. Current state    When a new user account is created in Jira, the user key (used as an immutable foreign key in the database) is the same as username, lowercased. For example, user {{Admin}} will get a user key {{admin}}. If there’s a naming conflict, the user key will be set to a value like {{ID10100}}.    This is problematic, because usernames are often based on full names. As a result, user keys contain personal information that is later scattered throughout the entire database, making GDPR compliance harder.  h4. Expected state    User keys in Jira do not contain any personal information, but are somewhat random."""
comments: "Starting from Jira 8.4.0, user keys are generated using a new strategy.    Please refer to [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/gdpr-changes-in-jira-975041009.html#GDPRchangesinJira-userkeys] for more details."

summary: "Make the automatic Kanban board refresh interval configurable"
description: """Currently, Kanban boards are configured with a hard coded 30 seconds refresh interval to check for board updates. If the board usage in the instance is high, and users tend to leave on for prolonged periods of time (e.g. as wallboards), boards will continuously poll Jira for updates, with each poll being a somewhat expensive operation on the Jira server resources.    For such environments, it would make sense to decrease the automatic polling interval to decrease the load on the server.     h3. Workaround    Currently changing the automatic refresh interval is only possible by repackaging the greenhopper file with the updated refresh value:    h4. Compile an updated greenhopper file:    # Unpack the _jira-greenhopper-plugin-*.jar_ plugin from {{$JIRAHOME/plugins/installed-plugins}}   # Update 3 files {{includes/js/rapid/ops}} to change the interval to 60 seconds:  ## *Poller.js*: {{DEFAULT_INTERVAL = 60000}} (was {{DEFAULT INTERVAL = 30000}});  ## *Poller-source.js*: {{DEFAULT_INTERVAL=60000}} (was {{DEFAULT INTERVAL = 30000}});  ## *Poller-min.js*: {{var r=60000}} (was {{var r=3e4}})  # Repackage the JAR file    h4. Replace the original file with the modified version    # Stop Jira instance.  # Replace the original greenhopper file in folder {{$JIRAHOME/plugins/installed-plugins}} with the modified version (take backup)  # Remove the file {{$JIRAHOME/plugins/install-app-info/jira-software-application.properties}} (this is needed to force Jira to reload greenhopper plugin from the source)  # Start Jira instance"""

summary: "Operation on worklogs may results in inconsistent timespent when done in parallel"
description: """h3. Summary    When a specific JIRA issue worklog is being filled concurrently from different sources at the same time, some worklog entries are missed; resulting in inaccurate timespent value. In the case where the user also uses Tempo timetracking, there is a difference between JIRA's timespent and Tempo's.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an integrated system that operates in Jira  # Do several work logs operations such as adding work logs to the same issue  # Run the following queries to verify {{timespent}} and {{timeworked}} data        (i) Replace _<issueID>_ with the [issue ID|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-get-issue-id-from-the-jira-user-interface-827341428.html]    h3. Expected Results    The time spent of the issue (shown as logged in time tracking panel) equals the sum of all existing work logs.  h3. Actual Results    Because the time spent is stored separately and there isn't a control of parallel changes in that, it may not be equal to the sum of work logged.  h3. Notes    We carried out 2 tests as proof of concept:   # A script run twice sequentially (waiting for first run to finish to start another one) where we add 60 seconds of work logs 100 times   # Same as above, but both are run at the same time in parallel    In both cases we expected 12000 seconds of time spent on that issue and that is what we got in the first test, but we got 9840 on the second as in some cases, the creation of a work log interfered with another running at the same time.  h3. Workaround    No perfect workaround, but avoiding running 2 work logs operations in the same issue prevents this problem."""

summary: "'ORDER BY' in JQL on text Fields sorts by ASCII values causing upper and lowercase to be separated"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Noting in our documentation [""""Search syntax for text fields""""|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939937723.html] we state:  {quote}  Note: All query terms in Jira are not case sensitive.  {quote}    However when using the 'ORDER BY' function on a text field the sorting appears to be using a ASCII sort function which causes the sorting of uppercase and lowercase letters separately and sort punctuation marks and symbols in various places between numerals and letters, for example, """"Z"""" precedes """"a""""      A Reference to ASCII ordering can be found at:  - http://www.asciitable.com/    Using the order of character sets designed for computer sorting does not necessarily produce a sort sequence that is acceptable for human readable fields such as a text field where having values such as a,A,b,B,1,Z,# in the fields would be sorted as #,1,A,B,Z,a,b in ASCII so that the upper case A & B are separated from lowercase a & b where alphabetically the uppercase and lowercase letters should be grouped together.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create multiple issue adding one of these values in each issues Summary {{A, a, B, b, #, Z, 1, 2}}  # In JQL search add in 'ORDER BY summary'    h3. Expected Results  The issues are sorted with Alphanumeric sort order of Special characters then numbers, then letters: {{#, 1, 2, A, a, B, b, Z}}    h3. Actual Results  Issues sorted by ASCII value priority {{#, 1, 2, A, B, Z, a, b}}  !Screen Shot 2019-08-16 at 4.41.55 PM.png|thumbnail!    h3.Note:  IN ASCII sort, the special characters for a dash """"-"""" and underscore """"_"""" occure between Uppercase and lowercase and are not grouped with other special characters causing an additional layer of confusion    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira can index stale custom field values in DC"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira DC can insert stale custom field values into its Lucene index while replicating index changes from another node(s).   This happens due to receiving node reading from the stale {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} cache.   This will cause JQL search not returning the expected result on the affected node.     +Notes:+  * System fields are not affected since they are not using same cache.     h3. Environment  The problem is visible in DC setup with 1+ nodes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * create the issueA on a node A  ** the less load DC has the more likely the problem will be reproduced.   * edit a custom field with """"value A""""  * wait until this issue is replicated to other node(s)  * edit the same custom field once again and set """"value B""""  * wait until this issue is replicated to other node(s)    h3. Expected Results  * All nodes see """"value B"""" in the index.   * JQL search """"CF = value B"""" shows the issueA    h3. Actual Results  * Other nodes see stale CF value - """"value A""""  * JQL search _""""CF = value B""""_ doen't show the issueA  * JQL search _""""CF = value A""""_ shows the issueA  ** Opening issueA will show the correct value, since it will be loaded directly from DB    h3. Notes  * _NodeReindexServiceThread_ doesn't clear thread-local request caches on each consecutive run of reIndex.  * Problem is caused by JRASERVER-68806. _NodeReindexServiceThread_ is caching 10 last issues, so when reindexing replication happens for the same issue it uses the old value to create Lucene doc. Since Lucene contains the old value, JQL search doesn't reflect the change.   * Since {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} cache stores 10 issues by default, creating or modifying 10 distinct issues with CF on nodeA will kick issueA out of the cache on all nodes. The next update of issueA on any other node will be replicated properly to the index.  * Jira 8+ is not affected.    h3. Workaround  * Running Full reindex will temporarily fix the consistency problem.   """
comments: "Considering the impact this has on data integrity, Adobe would like to strongly encourage this Bug be backported to the latest 7.13 ER, since we are still months away from the release of v8 ER.    Thank you!" [SEP] "Agreed. We have also discussed a 7.13.x release for this with our TAM. " [SEP] "[~mdoar2] [~baldin1697836153]  Hey Guys,    To clarify, this bug doesn't affect the 8.x line. That being said, it will be definitely fixed in corresponding 7.x branches.     Cheers. " [SEP] "We face the same issue    and I agree with Dominic's comment " [SEP] "Panera also supports the backporting to 7.13 ER.     "

summary: "Renaming problem with Special Character in SQL Server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It appears that JIRA will append a user with """"#1"""" in {{app_user}} table if a User that is using a Special Character is renamed to use the normal one e.g. """"user.tést"""" to """"user.test""""    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install JIRA with SQL Server database with the supported collation.  # Prepare an AD and create a user called """"user.tést""""  # Integrate JIRA with the AD and sync it.  # Clarify that the user is created.  # In the AD, change the """"Logon Name"""" to """"user.test""""  (i) In case that AD complains, change it to something else first before the above and don't let JIRA sync the AD before it.  # After it is changed, Sync the AD again in JIRA.    h3. Expected Result  JIRA will change the lower_user_name to """"user.test"""" in the {{app_user}} table.    h3. Actual Resutl  JIRA will change the lower_user_name to """"user.test"""" in the {{app_user}} table but appended with """"#1""""    h3. Note  - Tested with SQL Server 2012 and 2017 with both supported collation  - In the logs:      h3. Workaround  Most probably an issue will happen towards the user, the following KB should fix the issue:  - https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-login-fails-with-user-exists-but-has-no-unique-key-mapping-373261121.html"""

summary: "User email visibility is inconsistent with Webhooks"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Service Desk Server*. Using *JIRA Service Desk Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-73311].    {panel}  h3. Issue Summary  Currently the """"User email visibility"""" setting affects webhooks by determining whether or not user emails are sent with the message body.   However, the behaviour is inconsistent as certain webhook actions will include email address, while others do not.    In this case, setting """"Show to logged in users only"""" will result in:  * """"webhookEvent"""": """"jira:issue_updated"""". - """"issue_event_type_name"""": """"issue_commented""""  ** Email Displayed - YES  * """"webhookEvent"""": """"comment_created"""".  ** Email Displayed - NO    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to *Jira Admin > System > General Settings*  # Set the """"User email visibility"""" to be """"Show to logged in users only""""  # Configure webhooks to trigger on """"Comment created"""" and """"Issue updated""""  ** This can be configured to a site like https://requestbin.com for testing  # Trigger both these event types:  ** Add a comment into an issue  ** Edit the issue and change a field value  # Examine the results    h3. Expected Results  That both webhooks will show the same user data.  Either have email address or not.    h3. Actual Results  """"Comment created"""" will not show the user email, but """"Issue updated"""" will.  | !comment-created.png|thumbnail! | vs | !issue-updated.png|thumbnail! |    h3. Notes  Have not tested all potential webhook events for this behaviour.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
