description: """Currently the [atlassian/jira-software|https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/jira-software] image allows you to set connector settings through Docker environmental variables, however, we would like to configure multiple connectors as it is good practice to have a backdoor connector for instance administration.  h3. Workaround.    Create a custom Docker build from the base [https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/jira-software] image, and COPY the desired server.xml into /opt/atlassian/etc/server.xml.j2. Startup will copy that over and it will not be altered by the merge field functionality.     Example Dockerfile:  """
comments: "Thank you very much for the workaround! I had to also copy dbconfig.xml and chmod var and opt folder in my Dockerfile: [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-throws-severe-message-during-startup-due-to-unable-to-create-directory-for-deployment-389781040.html]" [SEP] "[~4f0e3cc31d7e] we've fixed the issue where dbconfig.xml is created with the incorrect permissions. Let us know if you have any other issues.    For what it's worth, answer suggested for customising your own server.xml will likely be considered the proper solution, rather than a workaround. It allows us to provide a default template, and for you to provide a substitute template that can also use runtime merge fields or else provide a static file that will be used as-is.    I'm curious as to why you need to chmod the /opt folder, however. For safety reasons we lock down the static installation files, and make only temp and log directories writable. Were you running into a specific issue with permissions inside /opt, or were you simply ch

summary: "Add available transitions to Card view alt-click menu on Kanban and Active Sprint boards"
description: """Currently (v8) on a Kanban or Current Sprint board  transitions other than drag-drop are cumbersome and slow. To select a transition where multiple transitions exist to a single status, or select a transition to a status in the same column, the user has to either:   * Click on the card > (wait for Issue detail view to render) > dots menu > More Actions > select transition   * Open the full detail view (wait for Full issue view to render) > select the transition, possibly by clicking the Workflow dropdown    These are all 4+ clicks plus render time. To be forced to do this for a transition feels like punishment for using workflows with more than a few statuses and/or transitions. Not every business process can or should be simplified to that degree.    I suggest that transitions be offered in the Alt-Click menu for the card. This would reduce the picking a transition to a 2 click process. It would also potentially solve the """"transition to the same column"""" issue. Bonus points to the dev if they putting the To status next to the transition name in that Alt-click menu.    This may be an enhancement request elsewhere. Please link if it is.     """

summary: "Show who is Viewing the JIRA ticket Real Time"
description: """I would like to have the feature that will show who are the users currently viewing the JIRA ticket that I am looking at that particular time.   I have looked into Atlassian Community and this seems like the only related post that I can find : https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Answers-Developer-Questions/How-do-you-see-who-s-looking-at-an-issue-i-e-viewing-the-issue/qaq-p/550302  The plugin suggested has been archived as well.  It will be great if we can have this feature in JIRA someday in the future.    Thanks and Regards,  Sam"""

summary: "Allow reordering of Move Issue screen fields"
description: """When the user is using the Bulk edit of issues, the fields on 'Move Issues' screen (step 3) do not follow the order of any screen scheme and they also don't follow the ID order from the database, they seem to be randomly ordered.    The request is to have a configuration option or scheme where you select this order."""

summary: "PDF autorotate or Manual rotate on Atlassian JIRA - Plugins - File viewer plugin"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently PDF autorotate or manual rotation is not possible using default _Atlassian JIRA - Plugins - File viewer plugin_         h3. Suggested Solution    Add autorotate or manual rotation ability for default _Atlassian JIRA - Plugins - File viewer plugin_    h3. Workaround    n/a"""

summary: "Ability to detect if an event listened by WebHook was triggered from GUI or API"
description: """h3. Summary  When a WebHook is triggered by Issue Updated event, the JSON body contains all the information about the issue details but none about whether it was updated from GUI or API.    In certain cases, it would be useful to be able to tell whether an event was triggered from GUI or API, so that further actions can be taken by the WebHook app.    h3. Suggestion  When a WebHook is triggered, the JSON body should contain additional info about whether the event was triggered from GUI or API.    This should be implemented for all supported events."""

summary: "Ability to filter linked issues by type in view column configurations"
description: """When we configure a dashboard gadgets to list certain Jira tickets, we can select """"Linked issues"""" that will print all linked issues. It would be nice if we can narrow the linked issues by a specific link type only."""

summary: "Accessing decorators/admin.jsp leads to a detailed error page"
description: """h3. Summary    Accessing <JIRA-BASE-URL>/jira + decorators/admin.jsp without logging in leads to a page with detailed errors.       h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Log out of JIRA  # Access <JIRA-BASE-URL>/jira + decorators/admin.jsp    h3. Expected Results  No error page should be displayed.     h3. Actual Results  500 error page will be displayed with detail errors :        !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!           """

summary: "Feature Suggestions"
description: """     1.Ability to do archival of sprints in scrum boards    2.Time tracking of task and sub-task.    3.Time tracking download excel format should be excel sheet  by default and currently it is defaulting to web page."""

summary: "Include more information on the Audit Log"
description: """There are changes that a system admin can make that are not logged in the Audit Log. These include configuration changes to system-level settings like changing date and time format. Without seeing who made the change, organizations have difficulty tracking admin activity for potential security risks.       These settings include but are not limited to:    * jira.date.picker.java.format  * jira.date.picker.javascript.format  * jira.date.time.picker.java.format  * jira.date.time.picker.javascript.format  * jira.projectkey.pattern            """

summary: "Hide / Show panels on detailed issue view screen per project"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    If a company works with Jira Software and Jira ServiceDesk, and uses a lot of different plugins. There are multiple panels displayed even though they are used only for few projects.    h3. Suggested Solution    Provide an option for hiding / Showing panels on issue screens view per project.     h3. Workaround  NA     """

summary: "Grant / revoke permission within multiple schemes"
description: """h3. Problem definition  Currently, granting / revoking permission can only be done to current permission scheme.  If we would like to grant same permission to many schemes, we would need to iterate through all these schemes.    h3. Suggested resolution  Implement permission modification screen where user could grant a permission to multiple permission schemes at once."""

summary: "Cannot select reporter when creating an issue while not signed in."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Permissions are set to allow a user that is not signed in to create a ticket and assign the report through the user picker. Recent releases of Jira prevent this action unless the user signs in.  h3. Environment    Jira 7.13.3+  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up a new install of Jira 7.12.x   # Set the global permission """"browse users"""" to anyone and the project permissions """"create issues,"""" """"modify reporter,"""" """"browse projects,"""" & """"assign issues""""   # Log out   # Attempt to create an issue and assign it to a user in the instance   # Complete issue creation   # Set up a new install of Jira 7.13.5   # Grant the same permissions   # Log out   # Attempt to create an issue and assign it to a user    h3. Expected Results    Same results as 7.12.x  h3. Actual Results    An error prompting for sign in (401 error)  h3. Notes    An unintended symptom of https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-69242  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""
comments: "Bug Found in Jira 7.13.5, and 8.2.4 also." [SEP] "Is this issue fixed? Any workaround?"

summary: "Ability to upload attachment without filename via email"
description: """{panel:title= Implementation of New Features Policy|titleBGColor=#e0efff}    At the moment, we can't set an ETA for this feature to be released, since there's a number of factors that determine how our product team prioritizes new features.   You can learn more by reading [Implementation of New Features Policy|https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/implementation-of-new-features-policy-201294576.html].  {panel}  h3. Problem Definition    Mail handler fails to attach a file to Jira issue if the file's name is unavailable such as due to:   * filename is not specified   * filename is improperly encoded    You can reproduce the problem with a scenario like:   # Create a JSD project   # Send an email inquiry which contains an attachment without specifying the file's name   ** script: [^jpns27074.zip]      No error is recorded at audit log in web UI or application log:     !jpns27074-audit-log.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution    The file should be attached to the issue even without filename. The name can be something like timestamp or a sha1 fingerprint of the payload.  h3. Workaround    None. Consider adding yourself as a watcher to be kept informed as to the state of this feature request moving forward. With that way, if our development team updates the ticket, you'll be notified via email."""

summary: "Documentation bug: Missing arguments for JMX Monitoring article for modifying setenv.sh/.bat"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    This applies to the documentation at:   [Live monitoring using the JMX interface|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/live-monitoring-using-the-jmx-interface-939707304.html]    See the section *Monitoring Jira remotely*    For customers who use {{JAVA_OPTS}} (instead of {{CATALINA_OPTS}}) within {{setenv.sh/.bat}}, our documentation is not so helpful.    h3. Expected Results    The syntax for {{JAVA_OPTS}} would be included in addition to {{CATALINA_OPTS}}    h3. Actual Results    Only {{CATALINA_OPTS}} is mentioned in the article    h3. Notes  For more context see serverfault article [Tomcat : Where to configure jmx JAVA_OPTS or CATALINA_OPTS|https://serverfault.com/questions/641103/tomcat-where-to-configure-jmx-java-opts-or-catalina-opts]    h3. Workaround    Add the following arguments to {{setenv.bat}} or {{setenv.sh}}:    For Windows, {{setenv.bat}}  JAVA_OPTS=-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote %JAVA_OPTS%  JAVA_OPTS=-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.port=8099 %JAVA_OPTS%  JAVA_OPTS=-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.authenticate=false %JAVA_OPTS%    For Linux, {{setenv.sh}}  JAVA_OPTS=""""-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote ${JAVA_OPTS}""""  JAVA_OPTS=""""-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.port=8099 ${JAVA_OPTS}""""  JAVA_OPTS=""""-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote.authenticate=false ${JAVA_OPTS}""""  export JAVA_OPTS  """

summary: "The Activity Stream gadget doesn't properly show all activities of a user profile, suppressing a few days until clicking Show More."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  For a user's public profile Activity Stream gadget, the order of the activities loads in a weird order, missing a few days that will only eventually appear once you click *Show More* a few times.    h3. Environment  Jira 7.9.0 - Jira 8.3.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Go to the public profile of a user and check the Activity Stream. It will appear a few activities of some days. Clicking Show More a few times, it will eventually show activities from day in between the previous dates that were not showing before as well. It doesn't happen to all users.    Please note maxResults=10. Request steps are executed as follows   # Identify latest changes registered for the user combining the following data:   ## Get 10 latest issues created by the user (IssueSearch.java:51)   ## Get 10 latest entries from {{changegroup}} table and translate them to issue keys (DefaultChangeHistoryManager.java:393)   ## Get 10 latest entries from jiraaction table and translate them to issue keys (DefaultChangeHistoryManager.java:398)   ## Combine results from (1.2) and (1.3) by sorting by latest issue updates and limit the results to 10 (DefaultChangeHistoryManager.java:348)   # Combine results from (1.4) and (1.1) getting the list of issues that were subject to latest user changes (IssueFinder.java:51)   # Extract activities for identified issues (JiraStreamsActivityProvider.java:101)    h3. Expected Results  It would show all the activities in chronological order correctly.    h3. Actual Results  It doesn't show the proper activities per day chronologically as expected, hiding a few dates that will only appear after clicking Show More a few times.    h3. Identified bugs:   # Step (1.4) from the workflow above sorts previously calculated results by latest update date *ignoring* the author of the changes. The result is then trimmed to 10 issues only.   # The code in general executes the same action several times as it's first getting the latest activities for a given user from database, then translates the activities into issue keys and then calculates the activities one more time but now based only on previously identified issue keys.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "User Picker Search to reserved the first result for the most match user"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  At the moment, in case that there are several users that have a similar Name or Email Address, searching for those users, it will be listed in ascending alphabetical format. Due to this, if we search for example a user with letter 'K' as the first character, it will be listed down below, require to scroll the result to click on the user. Also, even though the search string matches the Display Name or Email Address, this does not get the user prioritized.    h3. Suggested Solution  To reserve the first result of the User Search function for the most match user based on the search string.    h3. Note  Due to this and JIRA Service Desk limitation in showing the User Search List result in the portal, there is a possibility that the user can't be selected as it is not one of the results."""

summary: "In Quick Search, searching for issue key like strings assumes we want that issue, rather than searching for that issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When text looking like an issue key is searched for via the Quick Search bar, Jira will assume it's a valid key and attempt to load the issue, even though that issue key or project with key does not exist.     The end result of this is that you cannot search for an issue-key like string directly, if you have that string somewhere in a ticket and want to search for it, for example, in the summary field or description field.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In a test project, (EG TEST), create an issue with summary field containing an issue key like string, for example """"This is a test issue BROKEN-01""""    # Note there is no project in the system with key """"BROKEN""""   # In the Jira quick search bar, enter """"BROKEN-01""""   # Note that the auto complete searches for and shows our correct issue. However, press ENTER to complete the search    h3. Expected Results    We are taken to the Jira issue browser with the search results, in this case showing the issue with the issue key like string.  h3. Actual Results    Jira assumes we want the non-existent issue BROKEN-01, and redirects us to [http://JIRA/browse/BROKEN-01|http://jira/browse/BROKEN-01], resulting the message """"You can't view this issue""""  h3. Workaround    Do not press ENTER after searching for the issue key like string. Rather, click on the result that shows in the auto complete popup below the search bar."""

summary: "As a Jira System Admin, I want more control over in product notifications (aka whisper notifications) that appear to all my end users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Since about Jira 7.3, Jira Server and Data Center installations have started including the whisper plugin. This plugin provides in product notifications in applications like Jira. These notifications are controlled by Atlassian. These can be used to inform users of new features or notify users of critical updates.    As a system administrator, I want to control which of my Jira end users see this message, and be able to stop showing users this message when I choose.  h3. Suggested Solution    Update whisper plugin to provide more granular control over these notifications by a system administrator. Such as the ability to dismiss a specific message from appearing for other users. And the ability to see which user-roles will see a specific message  h3. Why this is important    It can help provide system administrators more control over the messages and content that appear to their end users. Many times end users could be seeing messages that urge them to upgrade Jira when Jira has already been upgraded, or when that user does not have privileges to actually perform the upgrade themselves. In turn these can lead to many users reaching out to the Jira system admin to remind them of an upgrade.  h3. Workaround    It is possible to disable the whisper plugin entirely, however this is not recommended by Atlassian as it would prevent the system from getting any such notifications at all."""

summary: "Add memory probing to Atlassian Diagnostics bundled plugin"
description: """h2. Problem Definition    Currently, there's no functionality in Jira to determine _which_ threads are consuming high amounts of memory in frequent full GC/OoM situations without having to manually parse through heap dumps.    Tracking _exact_ memory usage by calculating the dominator tree is expensive, but probing per-thread allocation rates might be a useful signal for identifying suspect threads.  h2. Suggested Solution   # Extend app diagnostics to probe threads for memory allocations.   # Add new alerting property to mark threads exceeding a threshold allocation rate.    This may allow for more rapid identification of problems by highlighting specific threads with high memory allocation rates that can be correlated to access logs and thread dumps to identify users and activities that are expensive in terms of heap consumption."""

summary: "Setting a property through BaseEntityPropertyService#setProperty and skipping the security check results in an error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When setting a property through {{BaseEntityPropertyService#setProperty(final ApplicationUser user, @Nonnull final SetPropertyValidationResult propertyValidationResult)}} and skipping the security check, it's not possible to do so without getting an error (You do not have permission to browse issues in this project) because there’s no way to pass EntityPropertyOptions    h3. Environment  * Code base can be seen in {{master}}, I'm guessing it's 8.2 or 8.3    h3. Steps to Reproduce  From the app developer  {quote}  If you want to set a property and skip the security check it is not possible to do so without getting an error. The problem here is not the null user value itself but the fact that setProperty() has no way to pass EntityPropertyOptions).    Here is the problem:    Setting a property is a two-step process:    # Call validateSetProperty() with property input (skip security check possible - method takes EntityPropertyOptions)  # Call setProperty() - if input is valid (skip security check NOT possible, because *method implementation does not take EntityPropertyOptions and ends with a call to getProperty() version without EntityPropertyOptions)    Because step 2 has no option to pass EntityPropertyOptions to skip the security check trying to set a property without security checks will always fail (Error will be: You do not have permission to browse issues in this project.)  {quote}    h3. Notes  * Issue originally raised in Developer Support.  * Discussion added as an internal comment    h3. Workaround  From app developer:  {quote}  We simply ignoring the permission error - however, this is not good because we cannot tell the user if there is actually a problem with the set property operation in our plugin upgrade task  {quote}"""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    When logging work with small numbers, Jira fails to display them although the values are stored on database. summary: "8.3.0+: Pre-seeding cluster.properties causes Jira Software to fail to start"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Pre-seeding cluster.properties in a new install causes Jira to fail to start.     I have marked this critical as it affects the [Jira quickstarts|https://aws.amazon.com/quickstart/architecture/jira/], which rely on pre-seeding the database and clustering.    h3. Environment    Linux (confirmed on Amazon Linux 2 on EC2, and Ubuntu 18.04 in VirtualBox).    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Install Ubuntu 18.04 on a local VM.  * Install packages {{postgresql-10}} and {{openjdk-8-jre}}  * Setup Postgres, add a blank database {{jira}}  * Download {{atlassian-jira-software-8.3.1.tar.gz}} and unpack  * Create {{\~/jira/}} and {{\~/jira-shared/}}  * Create the files {{\~/jira/dbconfig.xml}} and {{\~/jira/cluster.properties}} (see attached for versions I used).  * {{export JIRA_HOME=\~/jira/}}  * Start Jira with {{./bin/start-jira.sh -fg}}    Connecting to http://localhost:8080 hangs indefinitely.    The logs contain a lot log lines like:      Additionally, the following lines appear, however they also appear in a clean install (no-pre-seeding):        h3. Notes    * This occurs on both Software and ServiceDesk, but not on Core.  * Seeding only {{dbconfig.xml}} does not seem to produce the error.  * Two logs have been attached, one from a clean (unseeded) install, and from a run seeded with both {{dbconfig.xml}} and {{cluster.properties}}.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "@mention and general update emails from batch notification are not grouped together, despite having the same email subject."
description: """h3. Summary    @mention and general update emails from batch notification are not grouped together, despite having the same email subject.   The issue only happen to email with double-byte characters in the subject.    !outlook_grouping.png!  h3. How to Replicate   # Enable batching email notifications   # Create an issue with summary in Korean characters   # Add a regular comment to the issue   # Add a comment with @mention in the issue   # Check the emails from mail client    h3. Investigation Done   * We noticed the email grouping problem with MS Outlook and Thunderbird. Problem is not affecting the grouping in MacOS Mail client.   * From the emails that we received from batch notifications, we can confirm that the subject line looks identical from the email client.   * Reviewing the email headers, we noticed the differences in the email subject in encoded format (though they decoded to the same value):   ** @mention email subject       *    ** General notification email subject:       * The header from email subject in English:      h3. Please Help With    Please let us know if we can standardise the encoded email subject or encode it one-go, instead of doing it in chunks e.g:    h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "Set comment visibility in JSON import"
description: """h3. Context  A site administrator is importing a project full of issues into Jira using the JSON import wizard. The issues come from a system that supports internal comments so some of the imported comments need to be visible to staff only.    h3. Problem  While the JSON import allows issues to have comments, they do not have any visibility property so we cannot mark comments as visible to staff group only.    h3. Solution  Add a property to comments in JSON imports that sets the group visibility in Jira, so we can set internal comments as only visible to a specific group of users who are staff.    h3. Workaround  Only alternative is to leave out the internal comments when generating the JSON for import, which means we lose content when moving issues to Jira.  """
comments: "Hi,    I am resolving this one as a duplicate of [JRASERVER-69221|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-69221].    We will track our work on the original issue due to its history, votes and watchers aggregated over time.    Mateusz Marzęcki  Jira Server"

summary: "Add-on for project planning"
description: """I would like to know if there is a Jira add-on to help plan a project    The tool takes into account the available capacity, the committed EPICs and the FixVersion it the following way:    For every committed EPIC (R&D commit = COMMITTED) and for every release (FixVersion), it calculated the total original estimation (of the Epic),    and it compares it to the team’s available capacity.    The bottom line would be a list of all the EPICs that make it to the release and the remaining capacity"""

summary: "Issue collector mixed content - Endpoint issue"
description: """JIRA Administrator might not want to embed the issue collector form in a page but access the form directly instead. In some cases, their website is running on HTTPS but JIRA instance is running on HTTP, embedding HTTP content in an HTTPS page will result in some users unable to see the embedded content due to browser blocking mixed content.    The following KB article details this issue and it presents a workaround by accessing a rest endpoint to open the form directly. Unfortunately, this endpoint doesn't work anymore, the HTML code for the form is returned but it stays hidden, not showing the fields to fill. """

summary: "Jira In Product Notifications (aka Whisper messages) should display the destination URL in the statusbar for any hyperlinks the user hovers over"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Rarely, Atlassian has issued IPN (in product notifications) for Jira Server and Data Center customers. This feature has existed in Jira since about 7.3 or 7.4. These messages could be used for letting users know about new product features, or to let Admins know about security vulnerabilities.    The nature of these messages is very short, which usually has them including one or more addition hyperlinks to a related Atlassian site, such as Community, or documentation, etc. However the dialog box that appears on an end user's screen will not display the hyperlink URL in the statusbar of the browser when you hover over it.    This is a small feature that would help us be more transparent about where a link is going, before you try to go there. There is a fear among users, coupled with the infrequency of such notifications, that maybe a link that does not indicate where it is going could be malicious.  h3. Suggested Solution    Make adjustments to the way hyperlinks are displayed in the in product notifications (whisper) so that hovering over those links shows the destination URL in the browser statusbar, like a real link.  h3. Why this is important   * Transparency with end users.    * Helps to build there trust,   * Knowing what you are clicking before you click it.    h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Limit the number of elements in RequestCachingGroupManager cache"
description: """h3. Summary  Class {{RequestCachingGroupManager}} may accumulate too many objects in _RequestCache_ during processing   * Users (JRASERVER-67502 )  * link.IssueLinkImpl  * QueryCacheLiteralsKey  * etc     See more details  JRASERVER-66555      h3. Suggested Solution  Introduce the limit to number of objects stored by http request in it's cache. The default limit will be _1000_    h3. Note about the fix  The fix is _not enabled by default_. It will be applied only when [setting the JVM argument|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver076/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-945111794.html]:    Jira node restart is required.  To verify that the option was enabled correctly see if the Jira application log contains message:      The default limit is 1000, but can be modified by setting JVM argument, e.g.:    The limit won't be applied if {{jira.cache.request.bounded.enabled}} is not set."""
comments: "[~pczuj], hi.    What are our plans to enable it in 8.4 and backport on-by default to enterprise releases?"

summary: "Enabling a second context for a custom field causes the default behavior to no longer function."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When configuring a custom field, there are options for multiple contexts to allow for the custom field to behave differently in different projects or issue types. If you enable a second context, the default global context is now ignored. When using the """"Where is my field"""" link, it shows:  {quote} The field 'CUSTOM FIELD' is not configured in scope of the project 'PROJECT' and issue type 'TYPE'   To solve this issue, go to 'CUSTOM FIELD' configuration page and add it to the scope{quote}    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a custom field (tested with single select)  # Open the field configuration  # Add new contexts with different options for a specific issue type  # Go to a screen that contains the field based on the default context    h3. Expected Results    See the default context on the screen (with the new context showing in the corresponding screen)   h3. Actual Results    The field is not included in the default context (new context shows as expected)    h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "VersionHealthCheck fails due to version string contains new line character"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  VersionHealthCheck pre-upgrade fails due to version string """"Oracle Database 12c Enterprise Edition Release 12.1.0.2.0 - 64bit Production\nWith the Partitioning option"""" :        h3. Environment  Jira 8.3.0  DB Oracle 12c R1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira 8.3.0 and connect it to DB Oracle 12c R1 64bit With the Partitioning option enabled   # During VersionHealthCheck the above error is thrown out    h3. Expected Results    According to the documentation Oracle 12c R1 is supported and there are no limitation related to the Partitioning option enabled    h3. Actual Results  Health check code uses the folowung Java regex pattern:    so it doesn't expect the oracle version string to span more than 1 line.    Actual string is:  {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  """"Oracle Database 12c Enterprise Edition Release 12.1.0.2.0 - 64bit Production  With the Partitioning option""""  {code}  * the 2nd string could be different    h3. Workaround    The only option the customer would have is to drop the Partitioning option of Oracle so that the version string becomes a single line and Jira is able to work with it."""

summary: "Add support for Oracle Advanced Compression Option (ACO)"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As per our Supported Platforms documentation (link [Supported platforms|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html]), we do not officially support Oracle Advanced Compression Option (ACO).  {quote}  Jira will not work on: Oracle Advanced Compression Option (ACO)  {quote}    Several DBAs do ask about this compression feature as a must have.    h3. Suggested Solution    h3. Workaround   Not available. """
comments: "Customer Use case:    We are moving to Oracle Exadata, which has ACO on by default.    We wouldn't be manually tinkering with the feature but leaving Exadata to auto-tune. " [SEP] "Note that having Oracle Advanced Compression Option (ACO) available in a database instance does not automatically activate any of ACO features for database objects - it's Administrators who have control over various ACO features over database tables, partitions, etc.    Jira does not use ACO, however, running with ACO-capable Oracle instance should not affect Jira operation unless one enables compression for any of the Jira tables.    Jira Server Team has no plans to start Oracle ACO usage and we do not support configurations, in which ACO features get enabled by Customers on Jira's database tables.    ACO simply available in a database instance - that’s how I understood ""ACO on by default"" - does not pose a problem."

However, should the System language is changed to English, the message states that the database is supported. This causes misleading messages.    h3. summary: "Shared Dashboards shows /searchEmptyMessageKey when no result is found"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When searching for Shared Dashboards from JIRA Administration -> System -> Shared Dashboards, if no result is found, JIRA displays a technical message (*{{/searchEmptyMessageKey}}*) instead of a user-friendly one.    h3. Environment  Affecting JIRA 7.12.x and above.    Not affecting JIRA 7.11.x or below.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to JIRA Administration -> System -> Shared Dashboards   # Search using a keyword that won't return any result    h3. Expected Results  JIRA displays this message:  {panel}(i) Your search criteria did not match any dashboards.{panel}    h3. Actual Results  JIRA displays this message:  {panel}(i) /searchEmptyMessageKey{panel}    h3. Notes  # This is just a visual issue. The above message literally means there's no result.  # Shared Filters is not affected.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Option for the original estimate not change after sprint start.(Epic Burndown)"
description: """Currently, in Jira Software, the original estimate for an Epic(In the Epic Burndown) can be changed during a sprint if the stories within the epic change. The problem is that issue split after the start of an Epic are removed from the point totals of all sprints back to the start of the Epic and that the newly created issues from the split are treated as scope increase while the points removed from the source issue are not reflected as offsetting scope decrease for a net 0 effect.    The suggestion is to enable an option so the original doesn't change after the sprint start regardless of the changes in the Epic's stories."""
comments: "Can the title and description be updated to indicate that this is specifically related to the Burndown reports?  This problem affects both the Epic and Release Burndown reports.  If people are going to watch/vote for this, it should have something to indicate what the proposal is meant to address.    The description is misleading as well.  The problem is not that 'issues added to the Epic after the sprint start don't get counted in the original estimate'.  If a new issue is created, it should correctly show as scope increase during the current sprint.     The problem is that issues split after the start of an Epic are removed from the point totals of all sprints back to the start of the Epic and that the newly created issues from the split are treated as scope increase while the points removed from the source issue are not reflected as offsetting scope decrease for a net 0 effect."

summary: "Ability to disable complete application link activity"
description: """We have the following use case:    We are Using Jira and have 2 Confluence instances (A and B) linked with application links. Now in the Jira activity stream the activity of both Confluence instances are shown. As an Administrator I want to disable the activity stream for a specific linked application (confluence instance B) for all users. Because in this Confluence instance B nobody has a user and when a user creates a dashboard with activity stream gadget in JIRA, the activity of Confluence B is always active.    With this active activity the performance of Jira is worse. Everytime a user opens a dashboard with activity stream active Confluence B activity in Filters, the activity stream gadgets need up to 15 seconds to load. When the user disable the Confluence B activity in Filters, the gadget loads in 3 seconds."""

summary: "As a Jira user I would like have configurable list of default Custom Fields returned by REST search"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  By default, if no fields are specified when searching via REST, all fields are returned. This could be slow and also adds extra overhead to the system, in some cases, a specific Custom Field could significantly slow down the response (see JRASERVER-68602)    h3. Suggested Solution  * Introduce the configurable list of default Custom Fields (CF) returned by REST search  * Return the list of default CF if no fields are specified  **  unless it was explicitly requested to return all fields     h3. Workaround   When performing REST api searches, ensure to specify the fields you need.   {code:Java|borderStyle=solid}  {base_url}/rest/api/2/search?jql=text%20~%20task\&fields=summary,description,reporter,assignee  {code}"""
comments: "based on a comment from  JRASERVER-68602  >  A good resolution would be to remove the field from navigable and make it return in all or when specified." [SEP] "Agreed. "

summary: "Avatar files are stored as 13x PNGs for each user in a flat directory structure"
description: """We no longer use gravatar and therefore have 13 avatar files per user (we have ~ 880000 users and ~590,000 unique files) saved to {{data/avatars}} in a flat directory . We can’t even run an {{ls}} or {{find}} on that directory (we gave up after 6.5 hours). Only specialized tools like fpart are even able to traverse the directory, which takes ~42 minutes. This has caused us some pain with creating backups of our service, which means we are unable to reguarly backup avatars AND hit RPO targets.    Confluence handles this by having hierarchal avatar storage which is complicated, but effective ([https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/hierarchical-file-system-attachment-storage-704578486.html])    In addition, we have noticed that user initials avatars are rendered into 13 unique PNG files per user (small to very large), which uses ~90kb of disk space, which seems like an inefficient compared to styling via CSS client side or storing one SVG per user.    Are there any plans for implementing a similar (or better) storage mechanism for avatars in Jira?   h3. Workaround:    Do not backup avatars."""
comments: "We have the same problem. This causes a risk to DR data replication since it takes a long time to determine which new avatar files need to be copied. We have around 13K users and 4.7GB in data/avatars"

summary: "Jira Agile REST parameters are case sensitive"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Parameters for Jira Agile's REST endpoints are case sensitive, while most of Jira's REST endpoints are not.  This inconsistency causes incorrect results and usage.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have multiple projects/boards in Jira.  ** In my case, one of my projects is SSP and on a scrum board  # Request the following endpoint: http://localhost:8080/rest/agile/1.0/board?projectKeyOrId=SSP  ** Notice that the proper results are returned  # Request the following endpoint: http://localhost:8080/rest/agile/1.0/board?projectkeyorid=SSP  ** Note that I simply set all letters to lowercase in """"projectKeyOrId""""  ** Notice that all results are returned    h3. Expected Results  REST parameter will be recognized regardless of letter casing    h3. Actual Results  The REST parameter does not take effect and all results are shown.  There is also no errors to indicate the that parameters are not recognized, so users will not know that this failed.    h3. Notes  This was found with the """"projectKeyOrId"""" parameter, but the same behaviour also occurred with """"type"""" and probably others as well.    h3. Workaround  Ensure to double check the casing in the parameters to ensure they match the REST documentation exactly.  * https://docs.atlassian.com/jira-software/REST/7.3.1/"""

summary: "As a Jira Service Desk (JSD) user with administrative privileges, I would like the ability to access administration screens prior to creating a projects in JSD."
description: """Users that have been added to the admin group do not have access to administrative screens prior to project creation.   Admins are unable to perform configuration changes, or project import, until a """"dummy"""" account is created, or as the local default admin.  For some enterprises, the infrastructure admin, application admin, and project admins may be different users.   * Default system administrator, created during installation, is the only local user.   * User Directory connected to corporate Active Directory.   * Subset of users added to jira-administrators group.   * Admin user is unable to access system administration screens (i.e cog icon is missing) until at least one project is created in JSD.   * In order to workaround this issue:   ** The default system account has to be used   ** A ‘dummy’ project has to be created and then later removed"""

summary: "Splash screen does not appear in DC mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When running a DC instance, the Jira Startup / splash screen does not appear. Instead, Tomcat appears to refuse connections altogether until the node has started up.     This impacts troubleshooting / load balancer configuration - ideally one should be able to monitor the {{/status}} endpoint of a node to keep it out of the pool during startup, but still hit that node directly to monitor startup progress. A more complex LB configuration would then allow only proxying users to running nodes, but during a _full_ restart when all nodes are down, then proxying users any nodes that are starting rather than just 503'ing. This is currently not possible.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up a new standalone instance - run through the setup and choose the default options (built in DB, etc)   # Apply a DC license, but do not configure DC yet   # After setup is complete, restart and confirm that you see the splash screen   # Stop the instance. create $JIRA_HOME/shared, and create a cluster.properties file with {{jira.node.id=node1}} and {{jira.shared.home=/var/atlassian/application-data/jira/shared}}   # Start the instance once more, and confirm that the splash screen no longer appears. Tomcat will refuse connections until the node is ready / finished startup    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Steps to Reproduce  # Set up a Jira Data Center environment with two nodes.  # Name one node {{node1}} and the other {{node2}}  # Once they are both up and running, shut down {{node2}} and change the nodeID {{node1}}.    h3. Expected Results    Jira should prevent the second {{node1}} from joining the cluster.    h3. Actual Results    You are able to start the second {{node1}} and access it directly. There will be a single record in the database regarding {{node1}}, which will have the IP value as the IP of the last node with that ID that joined the cluster.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

They are located in {{id.atlassian.com}}.    For this reason:  - user avatars are not included in the Cloud backup  - user avatars can't be migrated from Cloud to Server    h3. summary: "Update the documentation about the migration from Cloud to Server (avatar/gravatar)"
description: """h3. Issue Description    There are several issues in our official document related to the Cloud to Server migration.      *Issue 1*    There are 2 different pages explaining how to perform the migration:  - one that resides in the Cloud space: https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/migrating-from-jira-cloud-to-server-776636236.html  - one that resides in the Server space: https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/migrating-from-jira-cloud-to-server-applications-938846963.html     The documentation that resides in Cloud is incomplete and is missing some steps that are in the documentation Server, therefore adds confusion.    *Issue 2*    Because of the [latest changes that occurred in Cloud|https://confluence.atlassian.com/cloudkb/user-avatars-are-suddenly-missing-969533903.html], User Avatars now reside outside the Cloud database: they are located in {{id.atlassian.com}}    For this reason:  - user avatars are not included in the Cloud backup  - user avatars can't be migrated from Cloud to Server    h3. Suggested solution    # Remove the migration documentation from the Cloud space, so that we only keep the one from the Server space which is more complete and not missing any step  # In the documentation that resides in Server, let's add something like this (please reformulate if necessary) in the *Limitations* section:  {quote}  Due to the [recent changes that took place in Cloud|https://confluence.atlassian.com/cloudkb/user-avatars-are-suddenly-missing-969533903.html], User Avatars now reside in id.atlassian.com and therefore are not included in the Cloud backup and cannot be migrated to Server  {quote}          """
comments: "Docs have been updated and will remain in-sync from now on. "

summary: "Using the Between option for date fields in Basic search filters the second date incorrectly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using the Between option for date fields in Basic search filters the second date incorrectly  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the Issue navigator   # In the Basic search, add a datetime field to the search (like the Created field) and use the option """"Between"""".   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    The search returns all the issues that are between the """"start"""" and """"finish"""" date.  h3. Actual Results    The results include all issues from the """"start"""" date but do not include the issues from the """"finish"""" date, only issues from one day before. For example, if we search using 5 Jun to 17 Jun, the search includes issues from 5 Jun but not the one from 17 Jun: !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    If we move to Advanced search, it seems that the problem is that the date is not including the time, which may mean that the time is set as 00:00. From tests, it seems that adding the time as 23:59 would solve the issue   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workarounds    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   * To add one more day to the basic Between search.   * To use advanced search"""

summary: """ALL"" is not one of the option in the AWS Quick Start Template"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If we refer to the template guide provided in the link below, it mentions that we will be able to select JIRA Core, Software, Service Desk or ALL of them.  - https://aws-quickstart.s3.amazonaws.com/quickstart-atlassian-jira/doc/jira-software-data-center-on-the-aws-cloud.pdf   !image-2019-07-22-17-25-22-366.png|thumbnail!     But, it appears that such selection is not available when we deploying the Template.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Login to JIRA AWS  # Navigate to the page below and select """"Deploy into a new ASI""""  # Follow the Wizard in AWS to go to Step 2.  # Click on the """"JIRA Product""""    h3. Expected Result  * The """"ALL"""" option will be available as in the guide.    h3. Actual Result  * There is no """"ALL"""" option available   !image-2019-07-22-17-25-55-342.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround.  * Install the other Application manually from one of the nodes as in the page below:  *- https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-install-jira-software-jira-service-desk-into-an-existing-jira-instance-849158594.html"""

summary: "Asynchronous cache replication can cause extra overhead in case of large number cache updates and many stale nodes"
description: """h3. Summary  Asynchronous cache replication can cause extra overhead in case of large number cache updates and many stale nodes.    h3. Environment  * Jira DC    * A large number of stale nodes (see JRASERVER-42916)  * Plugin (code) generating a large number of cache update events, eg reaching 2000 messages/min.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open a URL which produces the cache update event while computing the business logic  #* Eg. #* {{/rest/servicedesk/1/<PRJ>/webfragments/sections/sd-queues-nav,servicedesk.agent.queues,servicedesk.agent.queues.ungrouped}}  # Measure response time and number of replication events    h3. Expected Results  Performance doesn't degrade with a number of old nodes.     h3. Actual Results  Performance degrades with a number of old stale nodes.     * While taking thread dumps you can see a lot of threads busy in the following stack:    * From client's case, we saw 15 - 20% of all threads doing {{replicateToQueue}}    h3. Notes  None    h3.Workaround  Clean-up old node data manually, see JRASERVER-42916   """

summary: "Basic Search default Boolean inside a Group is incorrectly mapped to ""+"" operator"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Please Note the current Documentation Inconsistency BUG about the expected default behavior when a Boolean is used:  - https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-69649    Clarification Reference from Basic Search documentation found here: [""""Search syntax for text fields""""|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/search-syntax-for-text-fields-764478343.html], Noting that the expected default behavior of not using a boolean value between two terms is the """"AND"""" operator not the """"OR"""" operator as both variations are currently listed in the document and the following statement is true:  {quote}  """"If you combine multiple terms without specifying any Boolean operators, they will be joined using AND operators.""""  {quote}    However when doing a Query that has two terms missing an Operator nested inside a Group the behavior defaults to a """"+"""" rather than an """"AND""""  EXE using a very simple text search:  in the Text field search for *test (singleTerm OR term OR compound word)*  noting that compound word is actually two separate terms where Term 1 = """"compound"""" and term 2 = """"word"""" per definition criteria of a Term:  {quote}  There are two types of terms: single terms (e.g. """"hello"""") and phrases (e.g. """"hello dolly"""")  {quote}    This would be consider a Phrase if placed in quotes, as there are no quotes the default  conjunction operator should be """"AND"""" however we are seeing behavior similar to the """"+"""" Operator unless explicitly flagged as """"AND"""" or as """"OR"""" in the query.    h3. Environment  Cloud & Server affected Tested and verified behavior on current Cloud version and server version in the range 7.2, 7.13, 8.0, & 8.2    If using Jira cloud See corresponding Cloud bug Report here: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-72542    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create Requests containing only one of the following in each issues Description and any other combination that you want to verify against:  {quote}  test word  test compound test word  {quote}  # Run the search using Control search    #run the search using verification search        h3. Expected Results  Expected that control query *test (singleTerm OR term OR compound word)*  and the """"AND"""" test query *test (singleTerm OR term OR compound AND word)* would return the same result set which would be the issue containing """"test compound test word""""    h3. Actual Results  both text combinations are returned and this is the same result set as the """"+"""" operator test query    h3. Notes  It appears to be a conflict of the double AND application on the distributive action to the nested query inside the (grouping), as the portion """"test()"""" is technically triggering the default conjunction operator as a """"test AND ()"""" with the additional distribution of the alternate default conjunction operator portion """"AND word"""" making it """"test AND AND word"""" causing some conflict in logic at this point,      As an additional visual exe manual distribution of operators in the string *test (singleTerm OR term OR compound word)* to a advanced query should look like this:    BUT is actually being treated as:    OR        h3. Workaround  First NOTE the """"AND"""" is expected not the """"OR"""" per the linked documentation BUG, so use the """"AND"""" operator variable Manually rather than relying on the query to use a whitespace to default to the default conjunction operator """"AND""""  especially when used in nested queries to verify the correct search operator is being used.  OR   Rely on Advanced Search queries as defined in:  - [Advanced Searching|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/advanced-searching-939938733.html]"""

summary: "Incorrect information in Basic Search Documentation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the Documentation for Basic Search:  * [Search syntax for text fields|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver074/search-syntax-for-text-fields-921472949.html]    Quote1: In the section """"Query Terms"""" it notes:  {quote}  """"If you combine multiple terms without specifying any Boolean operators, they will be joined using AND operators.""""  {quote}    Quote 2: In the """"OR"""" operator section it notes a conflicting point:  {quote}  """"The OR operator is the default conjunction operator. This means that if there is no Boolean operator between two terms, the OR operator is used.""""  {quote}    Quote 1 States between 2 Terms a """"AND"""" operator is used and Quote States between 2 terms a """"OR"""" operator is used    Using the defined search criteria in the example of the documentation in the OR operator section, the second Quote is proven incorrect and the """"AND"""" Operator is confirmed to be used instead of the """"OR"""" operator as the Default conjunction operator.    h3. Environment  Cloud & Server Docs affected  Tested and verified behavior on Cloud and server version 7.2, 7.13, 8.0, & 8.2  - If using Jira cloud See corresponding Cloud Documentation inconsistency Report here: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-72541    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 3 issues   #* Place """"atlassian jira"""" in a text field on one issue (exe description)  #* Place """"Confluence"""" on the second  #* Place """"atlassian jira"""" and """"confluence"""" on the third  # using the Example in the OR section search for:    # all three issues are returned  # Remove the """"OR"""" operator to align with the statement """"This means that if there is no Boolean operator between two terms, the OR operator is used.""""    h3. Expected Results  All three issues are returned    h3. Actual Results  Only the issue with both """"atlassian jira"""" and """"confluence"""" is returned as the AND operator is being used    h3. Suggested Correction Needed  NOTE: the Documentation error exists in all current versions 7.4 - 8.2 and also exists on legacy EOL version of the Server documentation, please review all versions for necessary corrections for Current and legacy documentation consistency.    Remove the notation under the """"OR"""" operator section noting:  {quote}  The OR operator is the default conjunction operator. This means that if there is no Boolean operator between two terms, the OR operator is used.  {quote}    Then under the """"AND"""" operator section add in:  {quote}  The AND operator is the default conjunction operator. This means that if there is no Boolean operator between two terms, the AND operator is used.  {quote}    """
comments: "Glad to see there is a plan to fix this confusing doc error"

summary: "Updating a Dashboard, while two dashboards are open, always redirects to the last opened dashboard instead of the one that was edited"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If two Dashboards are open in separate tabs, the last opened dashboard is what you will see after editing the another dashboard's settings.       h3. Steps to Reproduce      1. Open two tabs - One tab have dashboard 1 and Second tab have dashboard 2  2. first tab, Edit permissions and hit update on dashboard 1.  3. Second browser tab, Edit permissions and hit update on dashboard 2  4. The update on dashboard 2 will re-direct to dashboard 1  h3. Expected Results    Take user back to dashboard that was edited.  h3. Actual Results    User is taken back to the last opened dashboard.       h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira returns incoherent search results on Service Desk queues, Agile boards and issue search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira returns incoherent search results. The problem manifests on Service Desk queues, Agile boards and issue search.    (!) At this stage, we believe *this is not a Jira bug* but a misuse of API. Jira API changed in version 8 and some add-ons don't properly use Jira API.   The issue does not appears always and in every instance, even with the same add-ons installed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Service Desk queues   ## Go to Service Desk project > Queues   ## Go to one queue and observe the issue count   ## Switch to another queue   * Agile boards   ## Go to an Agile board that contains swimlanes   ## Move ticket from one swimlanes to another   ## Observe the result   * JQL search - issue list   ## Open an issue list and apply JQL search   ## Observe the number of returned results   ## Keep refreshing the page and observe results    h3. Expected Results   * Service Desk queues:   ** Queues display correct and the same issue counts while navigating through queues   * Agile boards:   ** Moving tickets between swimlanes works as expected   * Issue list:   ** Issue list JQL search return the same results every time upon page refresh    h3. Actual Results   * Service Desk queues   ** Ticket count changes while “jumping” from one queue to another. Let’s say we have “Unassigned issues” queue with issue count 5; after focusing on “All open” issue queue, the number changes to 4   * Agile boards   ** When moving tickets from one swimlane to another, the ticket does not move (although its status has changed)   * Issue lists:   ** After applying JQL search, issue list showing different different number of results with each refresh    h3. Notes  Currently, on several instances, we noticed this behaviour being caused by one of the following add-ons:   * [Eazy BI|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211051/eazybi-reports-and-charts-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=versions]  ** upgrading the add-on to the latest version should resolve the issue   * [Elements Copy & Sync|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211111/elements-copy-sync-formerly-exocet?hosting=server&tab=overview]  **  the fix was implemented in version 2.17.2  **  see the bug ticket for more information CS-1419:_ Inconsistent search results in Jira 8 with Copy & Sync_   * [PII Protector for Jira|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1215574/pii-protector-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=versions]  ** No fix yet    h3. Probable cause  A plugin calls thread searcher context but never closes the context. Affected threads are using this old context which contains a snapshot of data from the time when this context was opened. As a result, the user observes issues """"jumping"""" back and forth on boards, Service Desk queues and other issue lists.    h3. Workaround  Follow the workaround from [Inconsistent Search Results in Jira 8.X|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/inconsistent-search-results-in-jira-8-x-968673104.html]:  {panel}  To confirm if one of custom add-ons is causing the issue:   # Stop Jira   # Remove the add-on .jar file from <JIRA_HOME>/plugins/installed-plugins directory   # Go to <JIRA_HOME>/plugins directory and remove the contents of .osgi-plugins and .bundled-plugins directory   # Restart JIRA   # Perform a full locked re-indexing   # Check if the issue still appears  {panel}"""

summary: "Adding Jira Service Desk or Jira Software via ""Versions & licenses"" panel in Jira 8.2.3 results in installation of application in higher version (4.2.4/8.2.4)"

Issue Summary  Incorrect version of Jira Software or Jira Service Desk is installed on top of Jira Core 8.2.3 via """"Versions & licenses"""" panel.  * For Jira Software it will install version 8.2.4  * For Jira Service Desk it will install 4.2.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start Jira Core 8.2.3   # Go to ⚙️→ """"Jira Administration"""" → """"Applications""""   # In """"Versions & licenses"""" ensure to have valid Jira Software license (can be trial)   # Click """"Install"""" button near """"Jira Software""""   !Screen Shot 2019-07-19 at 14.19.24.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Jira Software version installed is 8.2.3    h3. Actual Results  Jira Software version installed is 8.2.4    h3. Workaround  *It's fine to leave Jira Software 8.2.4 and Jira Service Desk 4.2.4 with Jira Core 8.2.3.* However, we recommend to upgrade to Jira 8.2.4    It's possible to install the older version of application manually by downloading it from:   * [Link for Jira Software|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1213607/jira-software/version-history]   * [Link for Jira Service Desk|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1213632/jira-service-desk/version-history]"""

summary: "Ensure that Default Group Membership works with third-party SSO"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When configuring Jira to use SSO with third-party tools such as Jira SAML SSO using default group memberships, Jira does not populate its tables to include all related data to ensure that users can log in properly for the first time. Due to that, it is necessary to manually insert each user in the required group on Jira to ensure that they will be able to log in.      h3. Suggested Solution  Detect when an user tries to log in using SSO to include the necessary data in the *cwd* tables in the first login to ensure that he/she will receive the group membership properly and will be able to access Jira."""

summary: "Triggering notifications if a user has a certain avatar fails to send notification"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Notifications cannot be sent to users if the user triggering the notification is using a specific avatar.    h3. Environment  Jira 8.0.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Define a notification scheme so that comments on issues trigger a notification.  # Login as user_A.  # Change user_A's avatar to    !image-2019-07-18-17-23-22-704.png|thumbnail!   # Add a comment to the ticket.    h3. Expected Results  # Notifications are sent to recipients.    h3. Actual Results      The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      h3. Workaround  Use a different avatar."""

summary: "German letters such as umlauts are not displayed correctly when using batch notifications and text format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  German umlauts are not displayed correctly when using batch notifications and text notification format       h3. Environment   * Jira 8.2.3      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable Batch notifications   # Change your user profile to get Text email notifications (away from default of HTML format)   # Enable profile preferences to get notifications of own changes (makes it easier to test this, but changes made by other users would also test this too)   # Create/edit an issue that has a summary that contains an umlaut character   # wait to receive batched email notification    h3. Expected Results  All characters are represented correctly in the notification message    h3. Actual Results  If the summary contains special German characters, such as those with umlauts, they are replaced by the HTML code character value instead such as     instead of the expected values of      example email received where this happens:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  SCRUM / SCRUM-26 [To Do]  HELLO WORLD ö ä ü ß test 12345    ==============================    Here's what changed in this issue in the last few minutes.  This issue has been created    View or comment on issue using this link  http://localhost:8823/browse/SCRUM-26    ==============================   Issue created  ------------------------------    admin created this issue on 17/Jul/19 3:10 PM  Summary:              HELLO WORLD ö ä ü ß test 12345  Issue Type:           Task  Assignee:             Unassigned  Created:              17/Jul/19 3:10 PM  Priority:             Low  Reporter:             admin  Description:          hi      ==============================   This message was sent by Atlassian Jira (v8.2.3#802003-sha1:5986657)  {code}    h3. Notes  Does not affect HTML notifications, nor does this appear to affect the other notifications outside the batched notifications      h3. summary: "As a user, I would like the horizontal scrollbar for a table to show at the size of the comment field "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently if you *edit a comment* and this comment has a long table, then the horizontal scrollbar would only appear after scrolling the table to the very end:    [^longcomment.webm]  h3. Suggested Solution    Instead, the horizontal scrollbar should show the bar at the width of the comment box so users can scroll to the right without scrolling all the way down."""
comments: "Hi Team,    It is the same with ""Description"" field as well. Also if we put table into ""Panel"", it is not convenient to manage data inside it (especially if there are 10-20 panels in Description with huge tables).     Is it possible to fix it somehow? To add horizontal scrollbar to particular panel/table?"

summary: "Example text for custom fields"
description: """For custom text fields, as an admin, I would like the option to put in an example text that is not the default value. Having an over-writable example (that does not save if the field is left empty) or having example text next to the field (like with time tracking) would help users more intuitively understand the nature of a field. This is especially useful for larger organizations."""
comments: "Placeholder for custom field would be really helpful." [SEP] "Better even: a list of potential values, with still the Option to write something else."

summary: "Change Jira to use HTML5 for embedded videos instead of QuickTime"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Currently, Jira uses QuickTime to embed videos on comments/descriptions. This works for Chrome and Firefox, but Safari has blocked the QuickTime plugin, which makes it so that embedded videos will not play on Safari.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open Jira on Safari.  # Upload a .mp4 video to an issue.  # Embed the video in a comment or issue using:     # Reload the page.    h3. Expected Results  Video will load and begin playing as soon as the page finishes loading.    h3. Actual Results  The video will not play, with a message showing {{Unsupported Plug-in}}.    h3. Workaround  There are multiple workarounds found around the web on how to re-enable QuickTime for Safari, but these no longer seem effective. Currently, we found no way of enabling this plugin on Safari."""

summary: "Improvement on installation documentation for Manual upgrade"
description: """In the documentation for the [Manual upgrade|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/upgrading-jira-server-manual-938846939.html] there is a step that indicates the following:  {quote}h2. Extract the files  Extract the archive you've downloaded, and start the upgrade.   # Extract (unzip) the files to a directory (this is your new installation directory, and must be different to your existing installation directory).    # Point Jira to your *existing* Jira home directory.{quote}  And in another line you have:  {quote}We recommend that you do it by setting the {{JIRA_HOME}} environment variable. For more info on how to do this, see [Setting Jira home directory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-your-jira-application-home-directory-938847747.html].{quote}    For users that are unfamiliar with Jira, it may be better to change these instructions to be more explicit on *how* to point Jira to a Jira home directory, something like this:  {quote}h2. Extract the files  Extract the archive you've downloaded, and start the upgrade.   # Extract (unzip) the files to a directory (this is your new installation directory, and must be different to your existing installation directory).    # Point Jira to your *existing* Jira home directory which in turn will allow it to connect to the existing database and upgrade the information in it. This can be done by either setting the {{JIRA_HOME}} environment variable or by modifying the jira-application.properties file. For more info on how to do this, see [Setting Jira home directory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-your-jira-application-home-directory-938847747.html].{quote}  """
comments: "I think the whole thing could be a lot clearer which commands included - similar to the installer instructions, which for example on Linux state doing a chmod to make the .bin file executable and then sudo to run the installer.  Similarly including instructions on how to untar etc would be helpful for the manual version."

summary: "Table in Jira issue description with over several columns causes table to be cramped"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a table in the description field has several rows, instead of the table displaying in a clear way with scroll bar, the data is cramped together.  h3. Environment    Reproduced in Jira 8.2.2, 7.13.5  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a table in the description field with several columns so that this flows out of the width of the description field    h3. Expected Results    The table should show as is with the scroll bar  h3. Actual Results    Scroll bar is missing and the table is cramped:   !description.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes   * The scroll bar shows up correctly when editing the description field   * The scroll appears both in the edit and view for the comment fields.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Automatic workflow triggers does not send an 'Issue transitioned event' in Jira server with DVCS integration"
description: """h3. Summary  From app vendor codebarrel:  {quote}  Steps:    # Setup Jira with bitbucket integration in in Jira server  # In your Jira workflow setup a trigger that automatically transitions the issue when a pull request is merged in Bitbucket  # Now setup an Automation for Jira rule, that is triggered on """"Issue transitioned""""  # Next merge a Pull request in Bitbucket with an issue linked to it in Jira.    Result:    The issue will be transitioned correctly through the workflow by the trigger    BUT - the automation rule will not execute, because Jira doesn't trigger a proper workflow transitioned event    See https://codebarrel.atlassian.net/browse/AUT-1448 for more details  {quote}    h3. Expected Results  Automatic triggers should fire a proper issue transitioned event in server    h3. Actual Results  Automatic triggers don't fire any issue transitioned event    h3. Notes  * Happens to all versions  * Impacting both Bitbucket Cloud and Github integrations    h3. Workaround  Setup an issue property updated trigger listening for changes to the automatic_transition_events_history property (will need Code Barrel's automation)"""

summary: "Support for Amazon Aurora Serverless Databases"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server including Data Center*.  This request to also covers ONLY Aurora Serverless deployments, for Aurora alone see [JRASERVER-41985|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-41985]  {panel}    Amazon is using a compatible database server called Aurora Serverless, for use in the AWS. It would be great if we can have support for this database, which according to Amazon, is designed to be compatible with MySQL and Postgres    """

summary: "Add logging for batch.js changes"
description: """Currently, there is no easy way to identify why a batch.js file is re-generated except some events like a new plugin install/update/removed.     h4. Feature request    * As a Jira admin, I would like to find out whenever there is a new batch.js file is generated and why it was generated in Jira's log. This would help me to understand when a new batch.jb file needs to be downloaded by a user."""

summary: "Version Report - distinguish between bugs and non-bugs"
description: """When looking at any specific version report, I want to be able to distinguish between scope creep and new bugs being created and added to the backlog (provided they have an estimate). It would also be interesting to have that distinction available on time spent.         !image-2019-07-15-16-07-13-035.png!"""

summary: "Add option to hide time tracking example"
description: """When entering time on a ticket through the time tracking feature, there is a preview that gives an example of how time can be added using time units. The time display can be adjusted to only show certain values (like only hours) but the preview still shows all values. For organizations that have standardized time tracking for their employees, the preview can be confusing for users. We would like the option to remove or customize the preview to align with the expectations for the user entering the time. """

summary: "robots.txt is served as text/html instead of text/plain"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    robots.txt bundled in Jira is served with an incorrect MIME type which may impact the way search engines index and position instance's pages   h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Visit JIRA_BASE_URL/robots.txt    h3. Expected results    The response's MIME type is text/plain  h3. Actual Results    Note that it's rendering as HTML (MIME type is text/html)  h3. Notes     This behaviour is a result of changes introduced in Tomcat which was upgraded to version 8.5.40 in Jira 8.2.2.   h3. Workaround    As robots.txt is a static file, Content-Type header for the response from accessing it can be easily overridden using e.g. a reverse proxy     Currently no workaround based on altering e.g. web.xml or server.xml (Tomcat config files) is known. If one is discovered, it will be posted here."""
comments: "Hi everyone,    This bug has been misleadingly labelled with the ""High"" priority for the last few months. In reality, neither is there any ongoing development aiming at fixing, nor are we planning to address it in the foreseeable future. I reduced the priority to ""Medium"" to reflect the actual state.    Although we understand this issue is important to you, we want to be clear by managing your expectations regarding the likelihood of a fix for it anytime soon. The Jira team do their best to prioritise the issues that have high and critical impact with broad pervasiveness reflected in series of different factors. You can learn more about this by reading our [Bug Fixing Policy|https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-bug-fixing-policy-201294573.html].    To see what the Jira team is currently working on and has recently delivered see the following dashboards:   * [Jira Server and Data Center: Recently resolved issues|https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/Dashboard.jspa?selectPageId=85597]     * [Jira

summary: "Sort order of sub-tasks is lost when cloning the parent issue"
description: """I have a template issue (Task) with 43 sub-tasks. One of the great enhancements in JRIA 7.6 was the ability to sort sub-tasks on the parent issue by dragging and dropping. However, when I clone the parent issue, the cloned issue (a deep clone, including the sub-tasks) loses the sort order that had been applied to the sub-tasks on the original issue. This is something I do fairly regularly so it would be useful if the sub-task sort order could be preserved for the cloned issue. """
comments: "Definitely important to have the sub-task order stay the same with cloning.  Otherwise, it's hard to convince Jira as a solution when cloning of templated tickets is a key part of a working team here.  From a user perspective this is very unexpected behavior and adds unneeded waste to regular ticket creation."

summary: "Add a functionality to Jira to make it easier to import certificates for secure connection"
description: """Due to the fact that keytool is not very comfortable and do not have a GUI for the system (Ubuntu server) Jira should a have a graphical tool to display and edit java keystore. For Windows we have programs like keystore explorer. It could be a welcome feature if jira have a tool like this."""

summary: "Ability to link external JIRA issues on edit or transition screen"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    It is currently not possible to link external JIRA issues from the edit or transition screen for eg https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/How-to-link-to-an-external-issue-on-a-transition-screen/qaq-p/880456    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement this feature.      h3. Workaround    # Click issue from another instance via More > Create linked issue  # Create a link to an issue from another instance via View issue screen. This can be done by clicking the '+' on the Issue Links section."""
comments: "This is relevant for us, because of the following use case:  We want to make the process for the staff working with our Jira instance A as convenient as possible. A common situation is that an issue in Jira instance A has to be linked to an existing issue in Jira instance B. Instead of having to go to ""More > Link"", we want to create a new workflow transition (which could e.g. be named ""Link to external issue"") that does show a screen only containing the ""Linked issues"" field. This can only be realized, when linking an external issue is made possible for the ""Linked issues"" field."
