summary: "Installing Jira as a Service from service.bat on Windows will produce an unusable service"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When installing Jira from a zip file or tarball, using {{service.bat}} to create the service will succeed, but the service won't start.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set the {{JRE_HOME}} environment variable  # Run {{$jira-install/bin/service.bat install Jira}}    h3. Expected Results  The service starts successfully.    h3. Actual Results  The service does not start successfully.    The error message can be observed when attempting to start the service:      h3. Workaround    # Edit {{service.bat}}, and find the following line:    # Change this line to:    # Remove the original service, using {{service.bat uninstall jira}}  # Create the service again, using {{service.bat install Jira}}"""
comments: "I found that this issue occurs when a 32bit Tomcat attempts to load 64bit Java.     The default setting in the Jira ZIP distribution is 32bit Tomcat.     When using the Windows EXE Installer distribution, the tomcat bit will be configured appropriately during the install process.      We should consider changing the default setting in the ZIP to 64bit, as 32bit Java becomes less common"

summary: "in labels-view.vm, 'labels' passed as 6th argument (customFieldIdAsLong)  to labelsLozenges macro, thus being rendered as custom field, instead of system field"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary From app developer: {quote} I'm trying to render html value of Jira fields like OrderableField field = fieldManager.getOrderableField(""""labels""""); FieldLayoutItem fieldLayoutItem = fieldLayoutManager .getFieldLayout(issue) .getFieldLayoutItem(field); return field.getViewHtml(fieldLayoutItem, new FakeAction(ComponentAccessor.getJiraAuthenticationContext(), ComponentAccessor.getGlobalPermissionManager()), issue, MapBuilder.newBuilder(""""view_issue"""", true).toMutableMap() ); FakeAction is same as com.atlassian.jira.projects.issuenavigator.DefaultIssueRenderService.FakeAction And i've found that: labels field has invalid template. It's passing 'labels' in customFieldIdAsLong macro parameter, while the one used by jira in issue view is passing falseTemplate with issue: templates/jira/issue/field/labels-view.vmTemplate used for issue view: $jira-view-issue-plugin$/viewissue/fields/labels.vmBecause of this, label links have invalid url:
  • asd
  • saa
priority field doesn't render icon, while template for issue view does That's the ones i've found on first glance, maybe there's more.Maybe, there's a another method to render html field values, which doesn't include custom rendering logic on our side for each system field? {quote} h3. Environment AMPS and SDK are latest available, Jira 8.2.1 h3. Expected Results Fields are rendered like for issue view h3. Actual Results Fields are rendered differently, sometimes incorrectly h3. Notes - Templates are resolved correctly - There's an issue in template itself. In labels-view.vm: 'labels' is passed as 6th argument (customFieldIdAsLong) to labelsLozenges macro, and because of that, it's being rendered as custom field, instead of being rendered as system field. - The template in jira-view-issue plugin doesn't have such issue, and {{false}} is passed as customFieldIdAsLong macro arument. h3. Workaround Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira does not respect single user permission in Edit and Share Dashboard"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If an Admin provided """"Add Editors"""" in a dashboard to a group (Eg: test Group) and provided the same permission to a single user (Eg: Test User), if the Test User does not belong to Test Group, the user will not be able to edit the dashboard.      h3. Environment    * Jira 7.13.X  * Jira 8.2.X      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Edit a dashboard sharing configuration and provide """"Add Editors"""" permission to a group and a single user.  # Ensure that the single user does not belong to the group.  # Login as the single user and check if you can edit the dashboard.      h3. Expected Results    The single user is able to edit the dashboard including the gadget.      h3. Actual Results    The single user is unable to edit the dashboard including the gadget.      h3. Workaround    # Add the user to the group which given the edit dashboard permission.  # Add a project where the user belongs to. This will allow the admin to set a role which the user belongs to."""
comments: "Hi, I think this is a duplicate of JRASERVER-69192." [SEP] "I would agree (duplicate) (y)"

summary: "Keyboard shortcuts change behaviour in Issue Navigator after issue modification"
description: """Using the keyboard Up and Down arrows to navigate from one Issue to another in the Issue Navigator breaks if you transition or open any other dialog (such as assign / edit) you lose the ability to continue using the keyboard arrows as it will just scroll the issue list up and down instead of going to the next issue.    h3. Steps to reproduce    # Go to the Issue Navigator in *Detail View* and use the Up and Down arrows of your keyboard to navigate from one issue to another.  # After moving a couple times try to assign one issue or transition it.  # The next time you use the arrows, you will not be able go from one issue to another, but the list will scroll up and down.    h3. Actual Results    Instead of keeping the functionality working the same way, we change the behavior after making a change in an issue.    h3. Expected Results    You should be able to continue using the keyboard to navigate from one issue to another as previously.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When trying to force garbage collection in Jira ViewSystemInfo user gets XSRF Security Token Missing error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a Jira Admin visits the System Info Page /secure/admin/ViewSystemInfo.jspa    There exists a resource on this page that shows Jira's Java heap size and a link that allows you to """"Force Garbage Collection'.  When trying to click this link in Jira 8.2.3 it does not work.  h3. Environment   * Jira 8.2.3   * Tested against multiple browsers, Firefox 67.0.4, Chrome 75.0.3770.100 same results    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Jira as a system admin   # Navigate to /secure/admin/ViewSystemInfo.jspa   # Scroll down to {color:#172b4d}Java VM Memory Statistics{color}   # Try to click the link for Force garbage collection    h3. Expected Results    Garbage collection is run against the heap.  h3. Actual Results   # User gets an XRSF error page displayed   # GC does not run   !Screen Shot 2019-07-12 at 10.23.44 AM.png|thumbnail!   # Logs show      h3. Notes    This works correctly in Jira 7.13  h3. Workaround   * No way to work around this from within Jira itself.   * However you could still force the JVM to perform a garbage collection event.  More details in [How to manually force a full garbage collection in Confluence - Atlassian Documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/how-to-manually-force-a-full-garbage-collection-in-confluence-943978676.html] (while this KB was created for Confluence, the steps can be applied to Jira in the same way)"""
comments: "thank you for providing the workaround. i have found this doc when i was searching a solution in the knowledge bank. i think this workaround should be compatible for JIRA....? When GC becomes critical, I will exercise this workaround. " [SEP] "Thank you for versioned the solution.    Looking forward to updating my Jira with the version.         I am feeling good because Atlassian has been providing very good visibility.   I feel a part of team and Jira becomes my app.    A big thank to the Team!"

summary: "Jira randomly throws JS warnings about com.atlassian.plugin.webresource in the logs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  JIRA *randomly* throws {{ScrollIntoView.js}} and {{DefaultMessageOptions.js}} warnings in the logs, especially when *searching for issues* using Issue Navigator. This is reported to cause *slowness* in several cases.    h3. Environment  *JIRA 8.2.2 and 8.2.3* - *any browser* can trigger the issue.    *Not* reproducible in JIRA 8.2.1.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  It's not possible to reproduce the issue at ease, but the following steps are known to trigger it:  # As user 1, access an Agile board and rank some issues  # Go to Issue Navigator by clicking Issues drop-down menu -> Search for issues  # Click Issues drop-down menu -> Current search  # As user 2, log in a different browser and repeat steps 1-3  # As user 3, log in a different browser and repeat steps 1-3  # Check {{JIRA_Home/log/atlassian-jira.log}}    h3. Expected Results  No JS warning about *{{com.atlassian.plugin.webresource}}* is thrown in the logs.    h3. Actual Results  These warnings may be thrown in the logs at any of the above replication steps, usually at steps 2-3:      h3. Notes  This behavior is reported to cause performance issues in several cases i.e. it takes longer to load the page from which the warnings are thrown.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Cannot Upgrade Jira connected to an Oracle database due to a query in the installer"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update – 31 August 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ebf2f9|titleBGColor=#ebf2f9|bgColor=#ffffff}  Hi everyone,         We have investigated the root cause of this bug and it appeared that it does not lay in Jira, but rather in Oracle driver, hence we closed it as Invalid.    To resolve the problem we advise to switch to Oracle jdbc driver 19.3 which is supported in jira 8.4+ versions.    For more details about root cause and other potential workarounds, please see this KB article ([https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-operations-may-fail-with-an-sql-exception-1018773564.html]) and this Oracle support article ([https://support.oracle.com/knowledge/Middleware/2184878_1.html]).         Cheers,   Mateusz Ostaszewski   Jira software developer  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    Upgrading to Jira 7.13.4 and performing a clean install using our development database. created a new user and granted it the limited permissions as specified in the install instructions. When the database is being setup the following error is present in the atlassian-jira.log    this is followed by several java stack traces and one Oracle error, _""""ORA-01427: single-row subquery returns more than one row.""""_  h3. Environment   * Oracle Database   * Existing production data    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Connect Jira to an Oracle database with multiple schema   # Upgrade Jira to any version after 7.6    h3. Expected Results    Jira upgrades to the later version and rewrites the information in the database as needed  h3. Actual Results    The upgrade fails. The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Notes    In our database we have a type installed in another schema which shares the same name as an Oracle type. This is from an install of ESRI ArcGIS software in the database.    Jira is trying to get a list of all the table names for some reason. When it does this it is accessing the Oracle view ALL_TAB_COLS which returns all the columns of tables that the Jira user can see including those in other schemas. It's not limited to just the tables that Jira itself has created. I won't repeat the whole query shown in the log file but it can be simplified to the following.    In a default Oracle install that query will """"work"""", but the query itself is poorly written. The type_name field is not a unique key for the ALL_TYPES table and the subquery will break as soon as you have the same type_name created in multiple schemas as we do. That query can be correctly written by adding a check for the owner to the subquery.    h3. Workaround    (untested)   # Install Jira connected to an empty Oracle database   # Move the data over as described in [Switching Databases|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/switching-databases-938846867.html]   # Complete setup"""

summary: "Incorrect license.txt for pocketknife-querydsl "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  We are using your [pocketknife library|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-pocketknife-querydsl/src/master/] in our marketplace apps and love it!    The issue now is the [license.txt|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-pocketknife-querydsl/src/master/LICENSE.txt] of pocketknife is linked to  http://www.atlassian.com/end-user-agreement but this is now a redirection link to the Atlassian cloud terms. These terms won't match to such code as is there written that we need to create our own license contract with each customer.     h3. summary: "Copying and pasting an image file using Jira's visual editor won't add the file to the Description or comment field of an issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Copying and pasting an image file using Jira's visual editor won't add the file to the Description or comment field of an issue    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Select a file and use COMMAND+C (on a Mac) to copy a file  # Edit an issue's Description or comment field and use COMMAND+V to paste the file into the field    h3. Observation    h5. Using the Text Editor  * When the steps to reproduce are followed using Jira's text editor, you are prompted to Attach a Screenshot:   !attach.png|thumbnail!  * The image is also added to the text comment as: \!screenshot-1.png|thumbnail\!     h5. Using the Visual Editor  * The image filename is added as text  * The image is neither attached to the issue nor added to the Description or comment field using the visual editor  * If you copy the image directly from an image viewing or editing program and past it into the Description or comment field, the image is both attached and added to the field.     h3. Expected Results    * When pasting an image file into the Description or comment field using the visual editor the image is attached to the issue and added to the comment:  !visual attach.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    * The image filename is added as text  * The image is neither attached to the issue nor added to the Description or comment field using the visual editor:  !visual.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    * Use the attachment button from the Description or comment header to attach the image file:  !attachment.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Improve user synchronization errors"
description: """Currently when Jira fails to synchronize a user directory due to missing attributes in the LDAP server user/group object, we get errors like the ones below without a mention of the specific object causing the problem        It would be great if we can add the user/group object causing the problem to easily find the culprit to fix it.    *Expected*:  The LDAP synchronization should skip the problematic DN objects and proceed to add the remaining objects in the search, showing in the logs that it encountered invalid arguments (the specific DN in the error) but was able to synchronize the remaining ones."""
comments: "Ideally, in addition to provide more information in the log, the Crowd routine should not fail because of a bad record. It should log the error and continue to process the rest of the LDAP records. In the current scheme, if there is any error in the LDAP, it causes the synchronization to fail. There should be a more graceful way to handle this." [SEP] "Related with more detail: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-71319    In our case, a ""bad record"" that JIRA won't tell us any details of has prevented people logging in (""Sorry, we had some technical problems during your last operation."").    This bug appears to be serious.  "

summary: "Allow adding existing Sprint to a board, instead of just Creating new sprint"
description: """In many cases, we need to have multiple scrum boards for the same project. And we still want them to share the same sprint schedule (ie. the same Sprint set).    Now, from one board, you can create a new Sprint, this sprint is assumed to be for all the boards within the same project. But going to the other board, you may or may not see the new Sprint, if none of the stories there are tagged with the sprint.    The suggestion is to add a button next to Create Sprint in Backlog board, say Add Existing Sprint.    Clicking on this new button Add Existing Sprints, you can see a list of available Sprints (not closed one) and pick those you want to be seen in the board. And these sprints will be shown in the board for users to drag stories in     """

summary: "Improve how Jira exports SLA values to Excel when having the improved SLA rendering disabled"
description: """h3. Summary  When trying to export SLA values to Excel after disabling the Dark feature sd.sla.improved.rendering.enabled to revert the SLA rendering format (as explained on [How To Revert The SLA Rendering Improvement Changes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-revert-the-sla-rendering-improvement-changes-943970007.html]), Microsoft Excel displays #VALUE! for negative SLA values. This does not happen on OpenOffice.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Follow the workaround suggested in this article: [How To Revert The SLA Rendering Improvement Changes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-revert-the-sla-rendering-improvement-changes-943970007.html]  # Try to export SLA values using the Excel export option through the Issue Navigator  # Check that the negative SLA values are displayed as #VALUE!    Since Atlassian provides a workaround to disable the new rendering, Jira should be able to export the negative SLA numbers in a proper way as well."""

Steps to reproduce:    # Open a web page.  # Perform a JQL filter.  # Open a new web page.  # Perform a JQL filter.  # Perform a bulk edit on the first opened web page.  # The bulk edit will bring up the results of the second JQL filter.    Reproducible on Version: 7.6.11"""

summary: "Project Admins Should be able to Modify Notification Scheme"
description: """Currently only system admins can set the notification scheme for a project. Project admins should be able to change the notification scheme as well as modify it to their projects liking. System admins shouldn't have to create a custom notification scheme for every project that wants one and I don't know any two projects in my company that want the same notification scheme. Project admins should be able to get the notifications they want, when they want them, and shouldn't have to rely on system admins to set that up for them."""
comments: "As I recall, project roles were added to reduce the number of different permission schemes needed.  If the Jira admin uses project roles in the permission schemes, then project admins can change who is in each project role." [SEP] "We use project roles to limit who can transition certain issues and for issue sign off. The roles match our internal processes so we can't just put who we want in the different roles." [SEP] "Jira admins can also create new project roles "

summary: "Project Roles should not be global"
description: """Our company has various groups that use Jira and each of them requires different roles for their workflows. Would be nice to have the ability to limit what roles are visible in different projects so that teams don't get confused about what each role means. We have over 3000 users, so training each of them on the roles and expecting them to remember is ridiculous especially as new roles continue to be added to our growing Jira server. """
comments: "We also find this crucial and urgent for our organisation, we have dozens of projects which don't necessarily have the same roles, problem is all roles are shown for every project, which makes role management quite painful."

summary: "JavaScript endsWidth implementation broken"
description: """atlassian-gadgets-publisher-plugin-4.3.9 implements the following endsWidth:    This implementation is broken, if the search strings occurs multiple times in the string.    So endsWidth(""""ABA"""", """"A"""") returns incorrectly false.    This eg. breaks gadets proxy urls (similar to CONFSERVER-53081), if base URL like http://jira.atlassian.com/jira are in use.    A similar broken function is also shipped in strings.js of base jira."""

summary: "Adding workflow properties that include non-latin alphabets will result in the workflow properties not being able to be deleted."

Steps to Reproduce   # Head to a workflow.   # On any status, click on *Properties*.   # Add a workflow property that include a non-latin alphabet such as 'Feldauflösung umfassen' in the Property Key and Value. Workaround   # [Export your Workflow as XML|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/sharing-your-workflow-938847433.html].   # Open the workflow in the text editor, search for the workflow property. summary: "When a board creator is removed from the only group given Editor permission, the user loses the 'Add gadget' and 'Edit layout' buttons on the dashboard."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a board creator is removed from the only group given Editor permission, the user loses the 'Add gadget' and 'Edit layout' buttons on the dashboard.  The user can still edit the dashboard by browsing to Dashboards -> Manage Dashboards, but this causes confusion to users who are not familiar the multiple ways a dashboard can be modified.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Login as a non-admin user and create a dashboard (notice the 'Add gadget' and 'Edit layout' buttons at the top of the dashboard)  # Add the user to a group  # Edit the board to provide Editor permissions to the group  # Remove the user from the group  # Login as the board creator user and browse to the dashboard (notice the 'Add gadget' and 'Edit layout' buttons are missing)  # As the board creator user, browse to Dashboards -> Manage dashboards page -> click the cog to the right of the dashboard -> click Edit  # Remove the group from the Editors section  # Browse back to the dashboard and notice the 'Add gadget' and 'Edit layout' buttons return    h3. Expected Results  A board creator should never lose the 'Add gadget' and 'Edit layout' buttons.    h3. Actual Results  A board creator can lose access to the buttons until the unassociated group is removed.    h3. Workaround  As the dashboard creator, browse to Dashboards -> Manage dashboards page -> click the cog to the right of the dashboard -> click Edit -> remove the group from the Editors section.  """

summary: "Moving a card in the backlog is slow in Chrome 75"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When clicking on a card in the backlog of Jira 8+ the card is slow to change state (XHRReadyStateChange) so that it can be moved. The response becomes slower relative to the number of cards in the backlog, but can be replicated with about 100 cards.  h3. Environment    Jira 8, Chrome 75. This can not be replicated in any other browser.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Software project.   # Create 100+ issues in the backlog.   # Navigate to the board > backlog.   # Attempt to click a card and rank it.    h3. Expected Results    The card can be moved immediately.  h3. Actual Results    There is a delay between the click and the time that card can be 'picked up' and dragged to a new location.  h3. Notes   * Observing the network traffic in the browser, the GET request to URL [http://localhost/contextpath/rest/greenhopper/1.0/xboard/issue/details.json?rapidViewId=1&issueIdOrKey=BLAH-32&loadSubtasks=true&_=1562013311017] is slow.   * A screen recording of the behavior can be viewed here: [https://cl.ly/b83d71cde59c/Screen%252520Recording%2525202019-07-01%252520at%25252002.54%252520PM.mov]    h3. Workaround    Use a browser other than Chrome."""
comments: "Using Firefox with a Kanban board having 250+ issues also results in about ~10 seconds waiting for any action o nthe board (selecting an issue, dragging/dropping an issue etc.)" [SEP] "This is most likely caused by this particular regression in Chrome 75: [https://bugs.chromium.org/p/chromium/issues/detail?id=979363]    As I understand comments on that page, there is an undergoing bugfix process in chromium project.    In fact it is correlated with 8.x changes we introduced to agile boards. See [this blog post|https://blog.developer.atlassian.com/upcoming-performance-improvements-for-boards-and-backlogs-in-jira/] (""Updated CSS layout"" section)" [SEP] "Comparison of performance profile for this issue in Chrome Dev Tools shows almost 10x slowdown: [https://pasteboard.co/IqgEt6f.png]   * Jira left: v7.12.3#712004-sha1:5ef91d7   * Jira right: v8.3.0#803001-sha1:679cb7f   * Chrome: Version 75.0.3770.142 (Official Build) (64-bit)    URL in ""Warning: Forced Reflow is likely a performance bottleneck"": [http

description: """h3. Issue Summary  When viewing an issue in the Issue Navigator, clicking a multi-text box will lead to a display error in Edge. The upper navigation bar is hidden and a gray box is displayed at the bottom of the page.      h3. Environment  * Browser : Edge     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open JIRA in Edge.   # Click """"Issues"""" and """"Search for issues""""   # Search for some issues   # Open an issue in detail view   # Click in a multi-line text box containing text    h3. Expected Results  Issue is displayed correctly.    h3. Actual Results  Issue is displayed with the upper navigation bar is hidden and a gray box is displayed at the bottom of the page.   !image-2019-07-03-22-42-05-314.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  The issue can be fixed by going back to the previous page before the bug occurs or by restarting the browser.    You can also use other browsers as another option."""

summary: "The (Confluence Wiki) Markup is painful to use and rather unpredictable"
description: """First off: I prefer to use the text mode and I would protest if it wasn't there. It's a shame that Atlassian hasn't implemented Markdown natively as part of Jira and that there seems to be no _official_ plugin supporting it. Markdown is one of the reasons I love Bitbucket.org.    Anyway, given that we as users are stuck with the Confluence Wiki Markup (as it was at least called in the past), I am running into problems time and time again. This ticket is about _that_.    I am using all kinds of programming and markup languages and with Jira I am struggling to format something as simple as (in words) """"backslash, 'usepackage', opening brace, closing brace"""" inline. Most of the problems occur when attempting to format something inline, even when something like {{\{noformat\}}} (<- the rendered HTML suggests that the braces are not even part of the <tt> element created by the double-braces) will work.    Case in point:        Now let's try to make this properly render inline with Confluence Wiki Syntax:    h2. First attempt    Trying to escape the """"special"""" characters:        ... even though the current documentation doesn't even mention the backslash any longer.    Outcome: {{\\usepackage\{\}}}    NB: that line break after """"Outcome:"""" above isn't from me.    h2. Second attempt    Adding a leading backslash:        Outcome: {{\\\usepackage\{\}}}    h2. Third attempt    ... let's at least get those braces to show up somehow:        Outcome: {{\\\usepackage{{}}}}    ... argh. Now the closing brace is no longer part of the monospaced text.    h2. Fourth attempt    ... maybe by escaping the closing braces somehow?        Outcome: {{\\\usepackage{{\}}}    ... argh.    h2. Summary    ... can we agree that this appears to be an arbitrary/unpredictable outcome? I _really_ hope someone at Atlassian can see how frustrating this user experience is. Since the problem still exists with the Jira version used for this tracker, I assume I needn't give a Jira version. But if it matters: {{v8.1.0#801000-sha1:2e1cd1b}}.    I've been fighting these issues (on and off) since Jira 5.x. And it's not just LaTeX code which cannot be properly formatted inline (which is really a must-have inside bullet lists), it also affects several constructs in C and C++, though I don't remember the issues from the top of my head (and cannot look them up as I don't have access to that Jira instance any longer).    Any chance that the technical audience (as opposed to the mouse-pusher faction) can see that fixed? How about supporting MarkDown as a first-class-citizen in the Jira world, for example?    btw: this is an in-house instance of Jira. Hope this was the correct project I picked. If not, please move the ticket around."""

summary: "Gadget Feed URL Problem on JIRA Data Center AWS Quickstart"
description: """h3. Summary  It appears that deploying the JIRA Data Center Quickstart template below will cause a Gadget Feed URL if 0.0.0.0/0 is not used as its CIDR Block. This will also cause loading the Dashboard really slow:  - https://aws.amazon.com/quickstart/architecture/jira/    h3. Steps to Replicate.  # Deploy a JIRA Data Center instance using the quickstart template above (Software/Service Desk)  # Set the """"Trusted"""" and """"Permitted"""" IP Range to anything beside Public Access(0.0.0.0/0).  # Go through the Wizard and let the instance deploy.  # Access the Load Balancer URL from the Cloud Formation Template Output and login to the dashboard, also the Support Tool page with the following:      h3. Expected Results.  - The dashboard will load and health check will report that everything is ok.    h3. Actual Results.  - The dashboard will load really slow and the health check will complain about """"Gadget Feed URL""""    h3. Note  The suspected issue here is that the JIRA unable to reach to itself through the Load Balancer like the following article:  - https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-fix-gadget-titles-showing-as-__msg_gadget-813697086.html    In case that the configured CIDR Block still did not allow the access to JIRA Administration Page, for easier access, add an Inbound Rule """"My IP"""" for HTTP to the Security Group.    h3. Workaround.  At the moment, the problem will not happen if the Security Groups(Inbound) are set to accept 0.0.0.0/0 which is public access."""
comments: "Another workaround is to allow inbound rules (in the Jira security group) for the interface IPs for the NAT gateways that were deployed with the ASI, that is of course if you set the trusted IP range to the VPC CIDR range."

summary: "Restore from Cloud to Server fails with mismatched data type error on columns PROJECT.ID and AO_2C4E5C_MAILWHITELISTS.PROJECT_ID"
description: """h3. Summary  The restore from Cloud to Server fails with the following message in the UI:   {quote}  There was a problem restoring ActiveObjects data for the <unknown plugin> plugin. Caught exception with the following message: Column 'jiraschema.PROJECT.ID' is not the same data type as referencing column 'AO_2C4E5C_MAILWHITELISTS.PROJECT_ID' in foreign key 'fk_ao_2c4e5c_mailwhitelists_project_id'. Please check the log for details.  {quote}    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Export a backup from Service Desk Cloud.  # Restore the backup into Service Desk Server.    h3. Expected Results  Backup should be restored with no issues.     h3. Actual Results  The restore fails, the following errors is thrown in the log (example: SQL Server):        h3. Notes  * This issue is very similar to JRASERVER-67706.  * The problem is caused by a line in *activeobjects.xml*:   {code:xml|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}      <foreignKey fromTable=""""AO_2C4E5C_MAILWHITELISTS"""" fromColumn=""""PROJECT_ID"""" toTable=""""PROJECT"""" toColumn=""""ID""""/>  {code}    h3.Workaround  The problematic line has to be removed from *activeobjects.xml* file:  {code:xml|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}      <foreignKey fromTable=""""AO_2C4E5C_MAILWHITELISTS"""" fromColumn=""""PROJECT_ID"""" toTable=""""PROJECT"""" toColumn=""""ID""""/>  {code}    The following steps can be used to perform and validate this change:  # Unzip cloud backup  {code:java}  unzip cloudbackup.zip  Archive:  cloudbackup.zip    inflating: entities.xml    inflating: activeobjects.xml  {code}  # Expand xml to multiple lines:  {code:java}  xmllint activeobjects.xml --format > activeobjects_fixed.xml && mv activeobjects_fixed.xml activeobjects.xml   {code}  # Remove the foreign key entry.  # Zip backup back together.  {code:java}  zip -r cloudbackup_fixed.zip activeobjects.xml entities.xml  {code}  # Move the backup to the import directory on the destination instance and perform the import  """
comments: "What is the fix version?    Was it fixed in Jira Cloud or Jira Server?" [SEP] "[~peto.ferenc],    This was fixed in Jira Cloud - see https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-72563"

summary: "Add an option to remove the scroll bar from the Complete Screen window"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When trying to complete a Sprint, a new pop-up will show with a scroll bar on the right side, even though the whole text already fits in the pop-up.    h3. Steps to reproduce    Issue Reproduced on JIRA 8.1.0 using Chrome as a browser    - Create a new SCRUM project in JIRA Software  - Go to the backlog view and Create a new Sprint, including issues that haven't been closed yet  - Open the Sprint page, and click on the """"Complete Sprint"""" link: a new window will pop-up letting you know that some issues were complete  - There is no scrollbar at this point  - Mouse over this pop-up window, and try to scroll up and down: you'll see a scroll bar that appears on the right side of the pop-up, even though the whole text fits in the window   !Screen Shot 2019-07-03 at 2.15.21 PM.png|thumbnail!        h3. Suggested Solution    Add an option to remove the scroll bar from the Complete Screen window          """

summary: "Add the ability of restricting the possible Start and End Dates of sprints in a board"
description: """h3. Summary  Currently when we start a sprint, we can populate any start and end dates. We can also edit those dates anytime. We need a way we can add/store those dates somewhere in the board or project or admin panel so that user can only select those dates as start and end dates of the sprint.    h3. Description  Basically, whenever we start a sprint in a scrum board, we have to enter sprint start and end dates. We would like to have an ability to store those dates and use them while starting a sprint. Currently, a user can select his own sprint start and end dates via calendar. These dates can also be updated anytime. We would like to restrict this feature and allow user to select sprint schedules as planned by the enterprise. This will allow users to select sprint start and end dates from the list of dates defined by the enterprise only and hence remove the random selection of dates.    """

summary: "Trying to uninstall an application results in error ""Unable to match and invalidate token for user (myusername)"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a system administrator that has rights to uninstall an application, i.e.: Jira Software or Jira Service Desk, the uninstall loading doesn't finishes and returns an error *""""Unable to match and invalidate token for user (username)""""*.    h3. Environment  Jira Software 7.11.x, 7.12.x, 7.13.x, 8.0, 8.1 and 8.2.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access the application with any user with rights to uninstall either Jira Software or Jira SD  # Go to Administration > Applications > Versions & Licenses  # Click Uninstall on any of the applications (Jira Software or Jira Service Desk)    h3. Expected Results  The chosen application is uninstalled as expected    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file (not all the time):      h3. Notes  Upon investigation, it was found that this was caused due to: https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/UPM-5974   It was changed the REST API in UPM, now it requires passing xsrf token as parameter.      h3. Workaround    # Schedule downtime  # Backup Jira  # Stop Jira  # Navigate to JIRA_HOME/plugins/installed-plugins directory  # Delete/Remove out all  jira-servicedesk*.jar or servicedesk* files, depending on what application you're looking to uninstall.  # Start JIRA"""
comments: "The workaround describes an procedure to remove Service Desk.    Which jars would need to be deleted to remove Jira Software and keep Jira Core?" [SEP] "Affects Jira 8.2.3, too" [SEP] "Also affects 8.3 (uninstalling Service Desk)" [SEP] "Can confirm, 8.2.3 is affected. Unable to uninstall Jira Service Desk to re-install for missing packages." [SEP] "Unable to un-install Jira Software which was installed on a trial basis. Will the work-around properly uninstall the application including any tables in the database? I suspect not" [SEP] "Also affects Jira 8.3.3. Unable to uninstall Jira Service Desk using the UI" [SEP] "Confirmed affects 8.3.3 when attempting to un-install 8.3.3 Jira Software.  Also, need to know if the work around will work for Jira Software, and is removing the language packs also required? summary: "Provide analytics for add-ons usage across Jira Server"
description: """h3. Summary    Nowadays, users are more and more using add-ons to complement Jira functionalities, for site-admins it's tough to know if the add-ons is being widely used or if the project it was used for has been finished and thus the add-ons is not required anymore.    h3. Suggestion    Implement an analytic for Jira admins to follow add-ons usage by endusers.    This is similar to the functionality already provided by Confluence Cloud - Macro Usage.    With such functionality as the above site-admins would be able to manage their sites bills more easily.  """
comments: "Very surprised this hasn't been added as a feature request before. Google tells me this has come up often and the consistent answer is ""can't be done en masse"".   There are solutions for researching the usage of a single add-on but not all of them.  Over the past 6 years we've added dozens of add-ons and now that our user count is upwards of 40K, we'd like to trim that but can't without an extensive time investment to find which ones are heavily used and by which projects.  "

summary: "Audit log for change to Status configuration"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}   *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-41654].   {panel}  Currently Audit log does not log any changes to the global list of Statuses. (JIRA _Administration > Issues > Statuses)."""
comments: "{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#e2e2e2|bgColor= #e8f8f5 }  Hi all,    Thank you so much for your votes and watches on this suggestion.    We are beginning greater research on the topic of *advanced auditing* and would love to hear from you.    We intend to better understand:    * What information you need to log and keep about your Atlassian applications and environments    * What are the questions you need to answer, or specific insights your are looking for when auditing logs    Responses can be in regard to recording product usage, information that needs to be tracked for internal policies (i.e. security) or compliance standards (i.e. SOC2 or SOX)    What’s involved in the research:    * Sessions are 1 hour and conducted over video-conference, so you can participate from anywhere around the globe.    * After scheduling, you'll receive a calendar invite with a video-conference link.    * During the research, we'll start with a general chat to get to know you and your company, then try to understand

summary: """Comment added"" filter in Activity Stream gadget is also triggering on status changes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The *Comment added* filter in Activity Stream gadget is counting status changes as comments and displaying them in the activity stream. This is causing user confusion since if for example a bulk status change is performed, the activity stream will show what appears as a random list of issues as the latest to be commented on while they don't actually have new comments.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to *Edit* in the *Activity Stream* gadget  # Create a filter that is *Activity* *is* *Comment added*  # Go to any issue and change the status (without adding a comment)    h3. Expected Results  Activity Stream should remain unchanged    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Links in the issue description needs to stand out"
description: """Links in the issue description needs another color and be underlined. They are invisible now. Not good, easy to miss."""

summary: "Make Component autopopulate in Advanced JQL search project-aware"
description: """When we search for Component in Advanced JQL search, it autopopulates with all components from all projects, rather than with components belonging to selected projects in the same JQL query. This is is contrast to the behavior of Simple JQL search where Component field only contains entries from selected projects, so in that sense, Advanced search is """"dumber"""" as compared to Simple search."""

summary: "Searching of Custom fields in ViewCustomFields Page is slow"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Searching of custom fields in the *ViewCustomFields.jspa* page is slow. It can take 30+ seconds to filter the results of the searched fields.    h3. Environment  * Jira 7.12+  * 1500 custom fields  * 6000 screens    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to ViewCustomFields.jspa page  # Search for a custom field    h3. Expected Results  The results of the filtered custom field return quickly     h3. Actual Results  The results of the filtered custom field take a long time (30+ seconds) to return the custom field    h3. Notes  The searching for the custom field will have the REST API {{/rest/api/2/customFields}} to iterate over screens of each custom fields.     h3. Workaround  Perform spring cleaning to try and reduce  * the total number of custom fields  * the total number of screens    h3. Note on Fix  The number of operations needed to perform the search has been reduced by avoiding obtaining a list of all screen fields for each custom field. """
comments: "CC [~dborshchov] :)" [SEP] "This problem possibly could be resolved by JRASERVER-69219"

summary: "Allow for admin configuration of preselected ""Configure fields"" buttons"
description: """As an admin of Jira, it would be very useful for a manager to be able to have certain fields always preselected for a user when they select the """"Configure fields"""" button in a Jira issue screen. This would allow for a manager or administrator to keep certain fields in a user's screen initially without having all fields displayed.    The only workaround right now is to make a field required, but some user's would prefer to still keep these fields as optional while making sure they are automatically checked to show in """"Configure fields""""."""

summary: "Issue Transition Report"
description: """We would like to have a way to run a report on a project for all issue transitions. The report would include the issue number, previous status, new status, and date/time of the transition. We want to change the ordering of the report between chronologically and issue number. Currently, there is no way to generate a report of this type for an entire project from the UI, only for individual issues. """

summary: "Enable preview of txt files in Jira"
comments: "Problem Definition  In Jira, .txt files are directly downloaded instead opening the preview.    Suggested Solution  The file preview is available for image files. It would be helpful to have this feature with text files as well.    Why this is important  Sometimes, you just need to view the attached text file, and if you want to do so, you must download them first.    Workaround  None."

summary: "Exclude Stories from WIP Limit"
description: """h2. Actual Behavior:   - In the Agile board configuration, Column management, I can select a column constraint from the following options:  ## None  ## Issue Count  ## Issue Count, excluding sub-tasks    h2. Expected Behavior:  - There should be fourth option """"Only sub-tasks""""    h2. Context  Our main working items after Planning 2 are sub-tasks we have created with the whole team. Each sub-tasks should be a maximum of 1 day effort. I as a scrum master want to restrict the number of open sub-tasks using the WIP limit. The problem is, that also the stories will count in. To work around that, we keep the story {{OPEN}} until we have resolved all sub-tasks an then also resolve the Story. That has the disadvantage that we cannot calculate cycle time for stories.     """
comments: "@Mods: Please consider renaming the issue to ""Support Only Subtasks for WIP Limit"" which makes the feature more clear since ""Story"" is just only possible issue type." [SEP] "After thinking about it a bit more: This is actually a bit tricky to implement, I guess, from a user's perspective I would want something like:  - Count every issue into the WIP like unless it has sub-tasks.    So the fourth option in the drop down should be ""Issue Count, excluding issues with Sub-Tasks""" [SEP] "For reference, there is a discussion in the forum: https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-discussions/KANBAN-WIP-Limits-only-for-Tasks-Subtasks-and-Bugs/td-p/791264#U1121468" [SEP] "This is definitely needed if we want to limit WIP and also work with sub-tasks. Counting stories w/ sub-tasks into WIP does not make any sense and can screw you up. " [SEP] "Well, I hope my vote helps. It would be good to see this feature soon. "

summary: "Jira will fail to start plugins due to spring scanner library problem v.2 in some plugins"
description: """h4. Summary  When Jira is starting up, it scans spring annotations for plugin classes. As part of this scanning process, it tries to ensure that these spring annotation classes are contained only in single jar. Responsible class for this action is AtlassianScannerBeanDefinitionParser.     If for some reason plugin can see spring annotations from another plugin that bundled them, plugin initialisation fails with error    {noformat:borderStyle=solid| borderColor=#6A8EB3 |bgColor=#F8F8F8}  2019-06-24 19:17:52,028 ThreadPoolAsyncTaskExecutor::Thread 28 ERROR      [c.a.p.osgi.factory.OsgiPlugin] Unable to start the plugin container for plugin 'com.radiantminds.roadmaps-jira'  org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanDefinitionStoreException: Unexpected exception parsing XML document from URL [bundle://263.0:0/META-INF/spring/portfolio-root-context.xml]; nested exception is java.lang.IllegalStateException: Cannot execute atlassian-spring-scanner-runtime: plugin has an extra copy of atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation classes, perhaps embedded inside the target plugin 'com.radiantminds.roadmaps-jira'; embedding scanner-annotations is not supported since scanner version 2.0. Use 'mvn dependency:tree' and ensure the atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation dependency in your plugin has <scope>provided</scope>, not 'runtime' or 'compile', and you have NO dependency on atlassian-spring-scanner-runtime.  {noformat}    The problem here is that plugins from error message have nothing to do with bundling spring scanner. Because of OSGI non-deterministic wiring, these bundles see spring annotation from 'wrong' bundle.    (!) Note: Default Jira installation doesn't have this problem. All bundled plugins are compatible (/).     h4. Cause  What is causing these errors is the fact that some of the plugins use 2.x version of {{atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation}} dependency in a wrong way.  Plugins are known to have this problem:  * de.demicon - Image custom field  * de.demicon - Field converter  * Customfield editor plugin v 2.5.0, fixed in 2.5.1      h4. Expected scenario  All of the plugins which use 2.x version of {{atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation}} should be fixed. They should use <provided> scope instead of <compile> or <runtime>. After new versions of these plugins are released, they should be used in Jira.  Instructions on upgrading can be found at [atlassian-spring-scanner ReadMe|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-spring-scanner/src/master/]    h4. Note  (i) How to find the plugin with {{atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation}}.  # Since Spring Scanner v2 classes should not be bundled, you can find reduce the scope of suspected plugins by running the following on:  #* JARs in the current directory (such as $JIRA_HOME/plugins/installed-plugins) as follows:  {code}  for i in *.jar; do unzip -v $i | sed """"s/^/$i:/"""" | grep com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/; done  {code}  In my case, this returns the name of the offending JAR:  {code}  jira-greenhopper-plugin-7.13.0-DAILY20190329134024.jar:       0  Stored        0   0% 08-21-2014 06:30 00000000  com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/  jira-greenhopper-plugin-7.13.0-DAILY20190329134024.jar:     422  Defl:N      257  39% 08-21-2014 06:30 28d693c1  com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/Scanned.class  jira-greenhopper-plugin-7.13.0-DAILY20190329134024.jar:     731  Defl:N      394  46% 08-21-2014 06:30 345abc0d  com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/ConfluenceComponent.class  ...  tomcat-filter-0.3.1.jar:       0  Stored        0   0% 06-21-2019 16:25 00000000  com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/  tomcat-filter-0.3.1.jar:     574  Defl:N      343  40% 05-14-2019 05:35 285fa055  com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/ClasspathComponent.class  tomcat-filter-0.3.1.jar:     728  Defl:N      396  46% 05-14-2019 05:35 6cd2cf1f  com/atlassian/plugin/spring/scanner/annotation/component/BitbucketComponent.class  ...  {code}  # Verify with the source code (pom.xml) which _atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation_ is used.   #* In case of V1.x those files are expected   #* In case of V2.x mentioned plugin will cause the problem, in this specific case - tomcat-filter-0.3.1.jar.     h4. Potential Workaround  # For any plugin that failed to start, go to the 'Manage Add-Ons' page in Jira and attempt to manually enable the plugin.  # Re-start Jira, and the race condition may not be triggered"""
comments: "Please note that the plugin Thready, version *0.3.1* can also cause this issue.    Because of that, we removed this version from the marketplace and have *delivered a patch* for this issue in *Thready 0.3.2*: [Thready Versions|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1214899/thready-give-tomcat-threads-a-name/version-history]" [SEP] "In our testing, the Customfield editor for Jira v2.5.0 can cause this issue as well. It needs to be upgraded to v2.5.1."

summary: "Create API Call to Get Mail Server Information"
description: """As an administrator of the product, I believe it would be useful to be able to pull information on current mail servers via Jira's API. This would allow for further setup of server automation behind the scenes and allow for a much easier way to pull this information in real time."""

summary: """Personal"" issues + Kanban board"
description: """Instead of having to create a project per person for them to manage day-to-day non-project tasks, or do it in another tool, how about a personal & private task list & kanban board?    Another person on twitter suggested creating a single project shared with everyone but having issue security limit visibility of issues to only assignee/reporter.    This suggestion applies to all version of Jira - Core and Software."""

summary: "Value of date picker type custom field contains date and time when exported to .csv format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Value of date picker custom field contains date and time when exported to .csv format  h3. Environment    Jira Server  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Export value of date picker type custom field to .csv format   # The date picker type custom field in .csv file would contain date and time 26/06/2019 12:00am (has default time as 00:00am)    h3. Expected Results    The date picker type custom field should only contain date when exported .csv format.  h3. Actual Results    The date picker type custom field in .csv file contains date and time 26/06/2019 12:00am (has default time as 00:00am)  h3. Notes    The date picker type custom field contains date only if exported  to excel format  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira recognizes space behind the text for Fix Versions/Component/s/ Filter & Dashboard name "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira recognizes space behind the text for Fix Versions, Component/s, Filter & Dashboard name.  h3. Environment    Jira Software  h3. Steps to Reproduce for Fix Versions   # Navigate to Jira Software Project > Releases   # Create version with a space behind the text (eg: """"3.12 """"   # Add version and apply the fix version to issue   # Navigate to issue navigator and place the following JQL (project = test AND fixVersion = """"3.12 """")   # Jira will display the issues with the following fix version with """"space""""   # If space is removed JQL (project = test AND fixVersion = """"3.12""""), issues are not reflected    h3. Steps to Reproduce for Filter name.   # Navigate to Issues > Search issues   # Create a JQL and save filter as """"alpha """" (with a space)   # Search for filter JQL *filter = """"alpha""""* (without space)   # Error message *The value 'alpha' does not exist for the field 'filter'.*    h3. Expected Results    Jira should not recognizes space behind the text for Fix Versions, Component/s, Filter & Dashboard name. (This may cause error)  h3. Actual Results    Jira recognizes space behind the text for Fix Versions, Component/s, Filter & Dashboard name.  h3. Workaround   # Remove space in Fix Versions, Component/s, Filter & Dashboard name.   # Adjust filters accordingly to the new value."""

summary: "Allow Jira software project integration with Devtools to be configurable"
description: """There is a customer use case where they would like to configure a given Jira Software project to integrate with only 1 Development tool, eg: Bitbucket server with Project """"A"""", while to be able to configure another project """"B"""" to integrate only with Github.  Project """"B"""" shouldn't show any commits from Bitbucket, Project """"A"""" shouldn't show any commits from Github.    Currently there is no way to control which project is integrated with which tool, this applies on tools linked using Application Links and tools connected using DVCS plugin.  All Jira Software projects are linked with all tool defined on the Jira instance.    The above scenario is also not covered by configuring Dev panel permissions.  The Dev panel permission either allows every thing to be shown to the user on the panel or every thing to be hidden from the user.    The configuration could be either through project permissions or using project settings."""

summary: "Previewing images attached to Jira issues show inaccurate color values when compared to the original and thumbnails"
description: """h3. Summary    When an image attachment is added to a Jira issue the image preview of the uploaded file are not always displaying color accurate versions of the original file  h3. Environment   * Tested against Jira 7.13.1 and 8.1.0, but I suspect this affects many more older versions of Jira as well.   * Tested again Chrome and Firefox browsers with same results.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # upload a sample image like the one attached  [^orig.png]   # Use a color picker tool to examine the red color of the displayed thumbnail of that image OR   # Click on that uploaded file to go into the preview mode, to examine the red color in that preview    h3. Expected Results    Expecting that these previews will display an RGB color value of 255,0,0 for the large red section of that original image  h3. Actual Results    The large red area of those previews is actually RGB value of 252,13,27  h3. Notes   * The original uploaded file can still be downloaded and that download file has the correct colors.   * the thumbnail file created by Jira also has the correct color value for red in this case   * this leads me to believe that the problem is being caused by the way those image attachments (and thumbnails) are being displayed within the issue detail view on the page.   * This problem extends to both the preview itself (when you click on the image in Jira), and the thumbnail as it is displayed in a Jira issue (not the actual thumbnail file stored in the $jirahome/data/attachments/\{projectkey}/\{issuekey}/thumbs/ directory, just the image displayed on the issue itself.)    h3. Workaround    There currently is no work-around to this behavior of the image preview."""

summary: "As an admin, allow for multiple database connection strings and to switch over when one fails."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    In our environment, we have a PostgreSQL DB hosted within a dedicated Enterprise Compose Cluster. This cluster provides two different portal access connection strings.    As dbconfig.xml only allows for one connection, when the link to the database goes down, Jira cannot change to the other database connection automatically.  h3. Suggested Solution    We would like for Jira to be able to detect and switch portals automatically if one connection string fails.    This functionality will dramatically enhance enterprise data center environments by providing a native high availability functionality when connected to hosted enterprise databases such as Compose PostgreSQL."""

summary: "Add a StartOfQuarter() function to JQL"
description: """You have StartOf functions for all other time units. Why not this one? Lots of business reports are done quarterly. It would be very useful to be able to easily say """"last quarter."""" With this function, it would be easy."""
comments: "We need this too. Very strange with such a simple function omitted. This forces us either into som thirdparty payware solution, or into Excel, which is NOT what we want. We want to stay in Jira for our dashboards etc."

summary: "Link Jira Project to a Specific Bitbucket Repository"
description: """# There should be a way to link a Jira Project to a SPECIFIC Bitbucket repository.   # When creating a new branch from within Jira, it should ONLY be possible to create a new branch in that SPECIFIC repository. It should not be possible to create a new branch in any other repo. At the very least, make it a configurable option. It would also be helpful to follow/support the repo branching model   # There should also be a way to link a Jira Issue to a SPECIFIC repo AND branch.  Maybe support multiple repo/branch links, but it should be possible to limit it to 1."""

summary: "Bulk mail sender should batch e-mails"
description: """When you're using AWS simple e-mail service, there's a limit of sending 50 e-mails per batch. Using the bulk send e-mail feature, it won't batch / queue these messages so selecting more than 50 users will result in an error.     As Atlassian is pushing AWS as a hosting platform for Jira Server / Jira Data Center, this should be fixed. """

summary: "CVE-2019-11581 - Template injection in various resources"
description: """There was a server-side template injection vulnerability in Jira Server and Data Center, in the ContactAdministrators and the SendBulkMail actions. For this issue to be exploitable at least one of the following conditions must be met:   * an SMTP server has been configured in Jira and the Contact Administrators Form is enabled; or   * an SMTP server has been configured in Jira and an attacker has """"JIRA Administrators"""" access.    In the first case, where the Contact Administrators Form is enabled, attackers are able to exploit this issue without authentication. In the second case, attackers with """"JIRA Administrators"""" access can exploit this issue. In either case, successful exploitation of this issue allows an attacker to remotely execute code on systems that run a vulnerable version of Jira Server or Data Center.        *Affected versions:*   * All versions of Jira Server and Data Center from 4.4.0 before 7.6.14, from 7.7.0 before 7.13.5, from 8.0.0 before 8.0.3, from 8.1.0 before 8.1.2, and from 8.2.0 before 8.2.3 are affected by this vulnerability.      *Fix:*   We have released the following versions of Jira Server & Jira Data Center to address this issue:   * 8.2.3 which is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/download].   * 8.1.2 which is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update].   * 8.0.3 which is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update].   * 7.13.5 which is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update].   * 7.6.14 which is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/update].         For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/x/AzoGOg]."""
comments: "This is an independent assessment and you should evaluate its applicability to your own IT environment. CVSS v3 score: 8.4 => High severity; ref: [https://www.atlassian.com/trust/security/bug-fix-policy]    *Exploitability Metrics*  ||Attack Vector|Network|  ||Attack Complexity|Low|  ||Privileges Required|High|  ||User Interaction|Required|    *Scope Metric*  ||Scope|Changed|    *Impact Metrics*  ||Confidentiality|High|  ||Integrity|High|  ||Availability|High|      [https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H]" [SEP] "How is the possibility for an attacker to get a Jira admin access?    Are we safe if our incoming mail is not enabled?     " [SEP] "Hi Kamal, do you have an open ticket with us? We'll be able to provide support specific to your case on your ticket if you do.    Hope that helps!" [SEP] "Hello [~kamalkailasa.babu]! It's not the incoming mail that needs to be disabled, but actually some other settings which are described in the full advisory.    You ca

summary: "Long filename in attachment drop down list is truncated"
description: """If there are multiple files with long file names attached, if the filenames are similar, there is no way to differentiate the files in the attachment drop down list without selecting it first.    The drop down list should display a tooltip that shows the full filename when the user mouseover each file to help user identify which file is which.     !image-2019-06-26-12-02-08-157.png|thumbnail! """
comments: "We would need this implemented in Jira as this would cause confusion from users on which file to attach. " [SEP] "redirect from https://getsupport.atlassian.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/22/JSP-412364    file {{*batch.css*}} - JIRA version 8.5.1, there is a rule for class  {code:html}  .aui-dropdown2 {      max-width: 300px;  }  {code}  Removing this rule should fix this issue (regression aside)    Please resolve as this is a global issue"

summary: "As an JIRA admin I would like to restrict the ability to view certain issue tickets"
description: """Hi      We deal with external client and would like to hide certain tickets on a ticket by ticket basis. Currently there is permissions for project level or type level. I would like to see a public or private issue type so only certain groups can view these tickets."""

summary: "Drag and Drop Subtasks in the Issue Detail View on Agile Boards"
description: """With JRASERVER-24547 it's now possible to drag and drop subtasks on the View Issue screen. However this is not possible in the Backlog issue detail view on Scrum/Kanban boards.    The same functionality to move the subtasks up or down with a drag and drop feature should be available on the Issue Detail View – it's possible to reorder the subtasks directly on an active sprint board or Kanban board but not within the detail view. Having a drag and drop feature would allow for quicker organization of the subtasks. """

summary: "Quick search returns no results, unless you press enter."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Quick search doesn't return any values, unless you press enter.    h3. Environment  JIRA 8.1.0, 7,13,3    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new ticket   # Type in the ticket number example CD12345  # Nothing is returned and displays no results.  # Press enter now ticket shows.    h3. Expected Results  Ticket displayed in quick search.    h3. Actual Results  Quick search returned """"*no results*""""    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "!Screen Shot 2019-06-22 at 6.12.32 PM.png!"

summary: "Report on project configurations"
description: """h3. Problem definition  Sometimes it is beneficial to have a summary of project configurations, e.g. which schemas and configurations are being used with a project or being shared among projects.    h3. Suggested resolution  Please implement a report which we could use to create such summary."""

summary: "Post function to copy a value of a field to another"
description: """*Suggestion*  It would be great if the """"Update Issue Field"""" post function that comes with Jira is enhanced to include the feature of updating a field value from another chosen field's value. While there are third-party plugins that achieve this, we would prefer if the solution comes built-in with Jira."""

summary: "Upgrade Jira 7.11.2 to 8.1.1 breaks Issue collector."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Issue collector breaks after upgrade to 8.1.1.    h3. Environment    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upgrade to 8.1.1   # Issue collector breaks.    h3. Expected Results  Either in the release notes a warning that you have to update the issue collector after an upgrade or some warning.    h3. Actual Results  Java script code portion of our Issue Collectors changed so we had to update the HTML for all instances using Issue Collectors.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Hi all,    The situation described in this report has been foreseen and accounted for during the jQuery upgrade in Jira 8.0. In order to remediate it, follow the steps described in _jQuery path changed in embedded issue collectors_ section of the article available here [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjira/jquery-upgrade-958436848.html]    Cheers,    Pawel Drygas, Jira Server Bugmaster"

summary: "Broken Application Links will not be loaded instantly and will cause duplicate issues to be created"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the JIRA instance has some broken Application Links, it will not be loaded instantly when accessing *Administration > System > Application Links* page. This will cause users to think that the Application Links do not exist in the instance. Waiting on the page for several minutes will allow the broken Application Link to be displayed.    Due to the broken Application Links, it will cause some problem where the Issue Create screen will take few minutes to close and creates duplicate issues in the background.    h3. Expected Results  All Application Links will be loaded instantly and issues created will not have duplicates.    h3. Actual Results  Broken Application Links will take few minutes to be displayed and created issue screen will also take few minutes to be closed and duplicate issues are created.    h3. Workaround  Disable the """"Workbox - Common Plugin"""" and """"Workbox - JIRA Provider Plugin"""" can help avoid running into the duplicate problem."""

summary: "As a Jira user I would like to know if JQL might miss some archived issues"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Since Jira Issue Archiving can remove individual issues from the index, for _Users_ who are not aware of the archive, the JQL search might not return expected result(s) since those issues are not searchable.      h3. Suggested Solution  At this point, we believe that _Users_ should contact _Jira Admin_ and clarify if issues were archived.     The expectation is that _Jira Admins_ will only archive issues that are no longer needed.    At this point, we believe it will confusing from UX perspective if we start displaying such a message.     h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "There are two helpful things I can think:    1. Jira admins keep a page listing all projects that have been archived. Not so good for issue archiving  2. The view screen of every archived issue should have a big, bold warning that the issue has been archived and will not be returned in JQL searches    I'm one of the Jira admins that will be contacted by users who cannot find their issue with a JQL search. I do not want to be contacted about this every single time, because that will not scale. Please do something to reduce the number of questions to the Jira admins!    " [SEP] "Once users know that an issue is archived, some of them will want to unarchive the issue. I don't want to have to do this manually for every request. Some way to batch up unarchive requests from users perhaps?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]: This can be achieved through Permission scheme. You can grant ""Restore issue"" permission to selected users in selected projects." [SEP] "True, and that will help reduce the number of users contacting the Jira

summary: "Batched notification in Icelandic language causes Velocity template error which is also included into notification"

Steps to Reproduce  # Turn on """"Batching email notifications"""" option is enabled (Go to Jira settings > System > Batching notifications)  # As a Jira User A, change profile language to Iceland.  # As User B, leave a number of comments that trigger batched notification to User A    h3. Expected Results  User A receives batched notification that is normally rendered.    h3. Actual Results  User A receives batched notification that contains error and stack trace:    !image-2019-02-20-15-53-40-638.png|thumbnail!     The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:        h3. Workaround  # Change profile language to other language (e.g. English)  # Turn off """"Batching email notifications"""" feature"""

summary: "Tooltips in History tab not displaying absolute times"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you hover over dates in the History tab, the tooltip provides a relative date as displayed in the attached screenshot (history.png). This is different from what is displayed in the Comments tab, where an absolute date is displayed in alignment with [our documentation here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-the-look-and-feel-of-your-jira-applications-938846975.html#ConfiguringthelookandfeelofyourJiraapplications-Howtochangedateandtimeformats] (comment.png).  h3. Environment    Reproduced in 7.12 and 8.2  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Comment on an issue   # Hover over the time in the Comment tab   # Note the time displayed in the tooltip is absolute (comment.png)   # Transition an issue   # Hover over the time in the History tab   # Note the time displayed in the tooltip is relative (history.png)    h3. Expected Results    It is expected that the tooltip would behave consistently and always display absolute times  h3. Actual Results    The tooltip output is different for various times.  h3. Workaround    Short of [disabling relative times completely|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/disable-relative-dates-in-jira-applications-414187622.html], there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "It is a low impact in most of case.    But as soon as Jira is connected with third part system with automatic action on issues, we may require to know the exact absolute time of these actions. Without this absolute time displayed, the analysis of some integration incident will required systematically an access to Database to find the real time ... It will be painful and time consumer.    V." [SEP] " We have some back-end scripts which update the issue. Also we have issue updates through REST API. Its difficult to find the exact time of updates.    Regards,    Mayuresh" [SEP] "The latest Enterprise version 7.13.6 is impacted too." [SEP] "Hi all,     the flaw has been fixed - starting with Jira 8.5.1 and 8.6.0 tooltips in history tab will display absolute date and time information which will make them consistent with other places in Jira and other Atlassian products.     Cheers,    Pawel Drygas, Jira Server Bugmaster " [SEP] "@Pawel Drygas,         Is there any plan to move this fix in the Enterprise ver

summary: "Creating a user named ""Unassigned"" causes issue assignees to be mixed up"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a Jira instance has a user called 'Unassigned' then the assignee related searches get mixed up.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new user on the Jira instance  # Use the username 'Unassigned' and Full name and email as anything.    h3. Expected Results  Nothing should break and all searches should work normally.    h3. Actual Results  The searches for this Unassigned user and the unassigned issues get mixed up.   Searching like {{assignee is EMPTY}} or {{assignee = Unassigned}} both show issues that are either unassigned or that are assigned to the 'Unassigned' user.     h3. Workaround  Delete the user 'Unassigned' from the instance."""
comments: "I'd like to clarify that the only workaround is to delete such user directly from database using SQL like this    Any other attempt (Java or REST API) will fail since application will faulty assume that user is assigned to the large number of issues (those real unassigned) and won't allow to delete such user with the error that it has assigned issues. Yet query for listed issues like the next one    will return {{null}} value, which means that in fact database doesn't have any link between such user and issue.    Deleting such user via the database require caches flushing which can be done with:  * application restart;  * flush caches with 3rd party plugins (eg. Power Scripts);  * force synchronise external directory, if any; this triggers users caches reload which is sufficient.    And another problem related to this one is that once user with username ""Unassigned"" is created, any subsequent renaming of this user will not cause Jira to ""drop"" its subsitution from the real ""Unassigned"" meta-user. Inste

description: """Add Project restricted to Group functionality requirements:  1. A Project can be associated with zero, one or many Groups  2. A Group can be associated with zero, one or many Projects  3. If a Project is not associated with a Group then the existing Permission scheme access applies  4. Where a Project is associated to a Group/s then only members of that Group/s can access the Project (from any screen/area) and then the existing Permission scheme access is applied"""

summary: "Add project re-index specification to JIRA Indexing FAQ document"
description: """h3. Summary    Document mentioned background reindex does not reindex Comments, Work Logs and Change History but there is no information about project reindex.   - [JIRA Indexing FAQ|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-indexing-faq-776654790.html]  {quote}4. Configuration changes never require Comments, Work Logs and Change History to be reindexed and so a background reindex does not reindex these entries. (Some of these may be reindexed for some issues to ensure correctness of the index for issues that are updated otherwise while the reindex is running).  {quote}    h3. Suggestion    According to the source code, Single Project Re-indexing reindex Issue and Work Logs. Suggest to update what data are included to project reindex.  h3. Note    """
