description: """Batch notification was [introduced in Jira 8|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjira/batching-email-notifications-960702680.html] to reduce the number of emails a user gets when issues are being updated. However, this setting is applied globally in a Jira instance.     In some cases, a user might want to receive the individual notifications.    As the plan is to make batch notification a default configuration in the future, it would be great if a user can opt out from it."""
comments: "this should be a project and/or user setting.    Unbelievable that it has been shipped as a system setting." [SEP] "I am also very, very surprised. The batch notification style is so different from the past one. When my organization switched it on, this had considerable effect on my work efficiency. While in the past I was able to work with keyboard only in Outlook, quickly filters and skimmed through the notifications to decide if ot not to act on it, now I even have to SCROLL in the reading pane to see the relevant details. White space might be a nice thing for visual beauty - but in this case the change was disruptive.     On top of that, the notifications now seem to come with a bigger delay than in the past. Another very relevant change."

summary: "Jira needs to escape the URLs when it loads the issue table from a custom filter"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira needs to escape the URLs when it loads the issue table from a custom filter.    Currently it is sending the url with the brackets, should use the html escape codes instead.    It is forbidden to use square brackets in a URL (except to designate the host if it is an IP address). See the [reference|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-3.5]     A host identified by an Internet Protocol literal address, version 6     [RFC3513] or later, is distinguished by enclosing the IP literal     within square brackets (""""["""" and """"]"""").  This is the only place where     square bracket characters are allowed in the URI syntax.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Unable to export a filter and filter box is missing when viewing it in saved filters  #  Open configuration file server.xml (should be in conf directory)  # Find all connectors your application is using  # Add {{relaxedPathChars=""""[]|"""" relaxedQueryChars=""""[]|{}^\`""""<>""""}} to properties for example:     # Restart Jira  # Unable to browse URLs with """"["""" and """"]"""" cause there's no way to escape the characters.     !Screenshot_20190619_154447.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    Replace brackets in url cause they are not allowed in the URI syntax  Example URL:  https://domain_name/jira/rest/api/2/filter/12112?expand=subscriptions[-5:]&_=1560935888167    *(see part subscriptions[-5:] )*    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Using 'Create linked issue' does not automatically populate the 'Created issue' linktype/relation field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In a Jira Service Desk or Jira Software project, the *Create linked issue* screen should automatically have the important fields populated in the screen namely:  - Project  - Issue Type  - Created issue (linktype/relation)  - Linked issues    In this bug, it appears that the Created issue linktype/relation dropdown field isn't populated with the default value. It should have selected _is blocked by_, as the default value.      h3. Environment  * Reproducible in Jira 8.2.2  * Not reproducible in Jira 8.2.1  * Browsers: Google Chrome, Safari    h3. Steps to Reproduce  In a Jira Service Desk or Jira Software project, click a linked issue from *More > Create linked issue*.    h3. Expected Results  * All related fields above are populated    h3. Actual Results  * Created issue (issuelinks-linktype) field is empty  !issuelinks-linktype.jpg|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  Although this is not a required field, when a user overlooks at this, and quickly creates the linked issue, an error 500 is returned. This is expected, because the linktype is needed as the relation for a linked issue.      h3. Workaround  Manually select the Created issue linktype/relation from the dropdown.  """

summary: "Username Rename not working properly"
description: """h2. Issue Summary    When an admin creates a user with mixed case characters in JIRA and decides to rename the said user to all lower case, JIRA maintains the mixed case character when he clicks save after making the changes.    h2. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a use with username: William.Shakespeare  # Edit the user and update username to: william.shakespeare  # Save your changes.    h2. Expected Result    New username will take effect.    h2. Actual Result    On the JIRA UI, the username will still appear as William.Shakespeare    h2. Affected Version    Tested on *JIRA 7.13.2*    h2. Workaround    Give the user a new username entirely:  # Original username: William.Shakespeare  # New Username: william.shakespeare.change  # Actual Username: william.shakespeare  # Save your change.    This is the only way I found to be able to edit the user to fix the username."""

summary: "DNS resolution can cause Jira services to stop running"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Under very specific circumstances Jira services may stop running due one thread awaiting a response from a DNS server. While there is a 10 second connection timeout by default it appears to be possible that a connection is established but the DNS server itself doesn't respond.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Jira mail handler   # Have an unreliable DNS server    h3. Expected Results    The mail handler is timed out, fails, and other services continue running  h3. Actual Results    The mail handler thread continues to run, blocking all other service threads as shown   !running-service-thread.png|thumbnail!    !locked-service-threads.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Restart Jira in order to restore services, including the mail handler service"""

summary: "ClusterManager reports node as Active when node is down"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a node is suddenly terminated (ie not shutdown gracefully), {{ClusterManager}} API still return the terminated node as active    h3. Background    From the app developer  {quote}  I have 2 node cluster of JIRA 7.9.0 (node1, node2). Each node is a docker. When I turn off node2 by stopping the docker container the other node still sees second node as active.    I've checked that in {{clusternode}} table node2 is active. I've checked that in {{clusternodeheartbeat}} table beat data are not updated when node2 is down.    The way I am getting node data:      When instead of killing node2 I shut it down gracefully (/etc/init.d/jira stop), then node2 status in {{clusternode}} table is updated to OFFLINE.    Jira should make use of {{clusternodehearbeat}} table to determine whether node is OFFLINE    Tables data after node2 was terminated - not gracefully  2.1) clusternode    2.2) clusternodeheartbeat    3) I killed a note to simulate a failure in DC cluster. In my case I stopped a docker on which Tomcat with Jira node was placed.  {quote}    h3. Environment  7.9.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have 2 nodes  # Terminate node 2 instead of shutting down gracefully    h3. Expected Results  When node2 is down other nodes should see it as offline.    h3. Actual Results  When node2 is down other nodes see it as online.    h3. Notes  * I haven't personally replicated this. I'm moving this in this project for more expertise    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Strip trailing whitespaces from the Single sign-on issuer field"

Problem Definition    Trailing whitespaces in the Single sign-on issuer field will break SAML authentication, resulting in an [Invalid Issuer in the Assertion Response|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/saml-login-fails-with-invalid-issuer-in-the-assertion-response-962344182.html].  h3. Suggested Solution   * Strip leading and trailing spaces from the string entered in the Single sign-on issuer field."""

summary: "Jira overrides Date.prototype.setFullYear by the ""jira.webresources:calendar-lib"", breaking the default Javascript functionality of the function"
description: """h3. Problem:    The standard Javascript function Date.prototype.setFullYear gets overwritten by the """"jira.webresources:calendar-lib"""" (file: Calendar.js) module in such a fashion that it breaks any other javascript code or framework that depends on its original behavior of the function.    This is a non-standard practice and should be deprecated by Jira.    Plugin developers spend lots of time trying to figure out a workaround to be able to access the original method.    h3. Workaround:  The original behavior of *Date.prototype.setFullYear*  is saved in *Date.prototype.__msh_oldSetFullYear*  so the user can just reassign the original value in his script.  """
comments: "This behavior is already deprecated in Jira 8.0.0 release.  " [SEP] "Can confirm that this is an issue on Jira 7.13.3.     Related Stack Overflow discussion: [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/56605220/how-to-reset-an-overridden-prototype-method-back-to-its-original-behaviour]    Related community post: [https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/javascript-date-setfullyear-weired-behaviour-in-jira/28558]"

summary: "Option to load custom field's description in issue view"
description: """h3. Summary  JQuery within custom field is not loaded while viewing JIRA issue with the custom field.    h3. How to Replicate   # Create a custom field with following in the Description:     # Load an issue that has value set for this custom field:   ** JIRA 7.13.1 - Script is triggered (JavaScript Console log: Running script from custom field description)   ** JIRA 8.1.0 - Nothing relates to the script is logged in JavaScript Console log    If we edit the issue in JIRA 8.1 release, JQuery is triggered.    h3. Diagnosis  The behavioural difference is that Jira 7.13.1 eagerly loads """"Edit issue screen"""" on each page view while 8.1.0 requests the data only after """"Edit"""" button is clicked. This works in favor of Jira 8.1.0 since the initial page load is faster.    Tracing the HTTP requests between web browser and Jira site, the custom field description (JQuery) is associated to the response of Edit issue request (/secure/AjaxIssueEditAction!default.jspa). With the above behavioural changes, the description/JQuery associated to the custom field will only be loaded when """"Edit"""" button is clicked in Jira 8.1.    h3. Suggestion  Provide the option to revert the behaviour, should users are not concern with the performance impact."""

summary: "As an user, when splitting stories/tasks I would like to move arbitrary sub-tasks"
description: """h2. Current behaviour:    When seeing issues in the backlog view of Agile Boards, it is possible to Right Click > Split Issues but that will only copy the current issue into a new one and link them as split.  h2. Suggestion:    When attempting to split an issue that contains sub-tasks, users should be able to select sub-tasks to move along to the new split issue.  h2. Workaround:   # To move incomplete or complete work done in sub-tasks, it is necessary to either move or clone them into the new parent task, one-by-one.   # Bulk edit after filtering for all existing Sub-tasks that match the criteria     """
comments: "Splitting a Story without having the capability to split the sub-tasks of the original one to the newly split one making split functionality a half-bake feature.    How can we split story and quickly move the sub-task today to the new one? The only way is to go through at least following steps:   * Switch back to the Backlog board because you can NOT split a story from an Active Sprint board (while the place we truly need to split story is Active Sprint board instead)   * Right click on the story you want to split, and right click to select Split. Now you have 2 stories   * From the old parent Story, open the sub-tasks into the Navigation screen,   * from there use bulk edit -> select only the tasks we want to move   * Move to new parent, you have to remember the new parent Story to specifiy it here   * Accept it without/with email sent out.   * Go back to the original screen (active board)    You can see that how clumsy it is to do it for just one Story.     Please help to improve this experience. Any chan

summary: "Changing install directory and port affect import to new instance."
description: """Changing install directory and port affect import.    *Install paths from export source:*   C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\ *JIRA*\data\attachments\insight\icons   C:\Program Files\Atlassian\ *JIRA*\atlassian-jira\images   *Port:* 8080    *Install paths from new instance:*   C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\ *JIRA SQL*\data\attachments\insight\icons   C:\Program Files\Atlassian\ *JIRA SQL*\atlassian-jira\images   *Port:* 8081        *2 Issues:*   # Custom images does not work, such as priority icons. The new instance looks for the images in the *JIRA* folder instead of *JIRA SQL.* Not sure if other paths are affected.   # Custom images does not work on *Insight Asset Management* plugin. New instance looks for the images with old port number. This happens because, after import Jira suggests to change base url, and when we click change url by default it chooses the default port 8080 instead of the port selected on installation.        *Workaround*: On new instance, make sure install path stays the same and to fix the port issue, change the base url in settings."""

summary: "Permission Scheme incorrectly shows that a different user has been granted a permission"
description: """h3. Summary    If user2's current username is the same as user1's old username, granting a permission to user2 through *Single user* will incorrectly show that user1 has been granted the permission.    (i) This is a more problematic version of JRASERVER-65115    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create 2 users in Internal directory as follows:  ||username||Display Name||  |user1|User 1|  |user2|User 2|   # Edit a Permission Scheme -> grant user2 a permission through *Single user* option   (+) Notice that *User 2* is displayed   # Rename user1 to _user123_   # Rename user2 to *user1*    h3. Expected Behavior    The Permission Scheme still displays *User 2* as having the permission  h3. Actual Behavior    The Permission Scheme now displays *User 1* as having the permission  h3. Impacts    (i) There's no functionality impact because the permission is still granted correctly to user2 (User 2) who now has the username of user1. This can be verified by using Permission helper.    (flag) In other words, this is only a visual bug, but it may cause a lot of confusion to JIRA and/or Project administrators.  h3. Notes    This issue usually manifests when:   # Multiple user directories are used and 1 or more users exist in more than 1 directory   # Users have been renamed mostly for user migration purposes across user directories    (i) In JIRA db, table *app_user* looks somewhat like this:  ||user_key||lower_user_name||  |user1|user123|  |user2|user1|  and table *cwd_user* now looks somewhat like this:  ||username||Display Name||  |user123|User 1|  |user1|User 2|  (+) Table schemepermissions shows that the permission is granted correctly to User 2:  ||perm_type||perm_parameter||  |user|user2|"""

summary: "Add health check warning for Jira bundled system plugins in disabled state."
description: """This is to suggest adding a health check warning to allow Jira to report all disabled Jira bundled system plugins to avoid having unexpected functionality loss.  May of those plugins are essential and should always be enabled, among those is the *Atlassian HTTP Client*, *Entity property conditions*,  *JIRA Feature Keys* and others.    It is also useful to integrate this with current Jira support zip health checks summary to provide more help in troubleshooting issues.    This should mitigate plugins going disabled without being noticed by Jira admins."""

summary: "When searching for issues by Created Date, placeholder text suggests using incorrect date format"
description: """h3. Summary    When searching for issues by Created Date, placeholder text uses incorrect format. This can cause frustration for users who are manually entering dates rather than selecting from the date picker.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Select *Issues* > *Search for issues* > *Basic search* > *More*  # Select *Created date*  # Note the date format in """"from"""" and """"to"""" fields is {{d-Mmm-yyyy}}  # Enter any two dates in this format  # Select Update    *Expected:* Jira will display issues created between the dates specified.    *Actual:* An error occurs. """"Invalid date format. Please enter the date in the format """"d/MMM/yy"""".""""    h3. Workaround    Use the date picker to select dates, or enter dates in the format {{d/MMM/yy}} instead.    h3. Notes     This was originally reported at JRASERVER-36905, but was closed/timed out. """
comments: "For what it's worth, I checked my System Info page, and do see that all date formats use the slashes:       |*jira.date.picker.java.format*|d/MMM/yy|  |*jira.date.picker.javascript.format*|%e/%b/%y|  |*jira.date.time.picker.java.format*|dd/MMM/yy h:mm a|  |*jira.date.time.picker.javascript.format*|%e/%b/%y %I:%M %p|         So it seems that the day-month-year that is used in the placeholder is simply hard coded text.  Ideally the placeholder text would be formatted using one of the above format specifiers as appropriate." [SEP] "Hi,    Date pciker and any of the date format are working for me. How could I do ?"

summary: "Mark the objects from class DefaultVelocityManager as deprecated in Jira Java API "
description: """As a result of an investigation done by [meta-inf-kft|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/vendors/4907/meta-inf-kft] about how the [Email this issue Plugin|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/4977/email-this-issue] uses Velocity Templates in Jira, it was discovered that the objects  from class *DefaultVelocityManager* might be causing memory leaks.  *DefaultVelocityManager* is currently part of our Jira API that can be used by Plugin vendors.    Turns out that Jira uses another object to handle Velocity templates: *JiraVelocityManager* which is safer to use, and is confirmed by Atlassian development team to be the recommended class to be used for working with Velocity templates.    Thus, we need to make the objects from the class *DefaultVelocityManager*  marked as deprecated so that our plugin vendors know that it is not supposed to be used any more."""

summary: "Removing actor from project role can make Jira unresponsive"
description: """h3. Summary    In specific situations with few roles defined in the instance and large amount of users assigned to them, removing actor from a specific role can take several seconds causing all incoming requests that require permission validation to hang.  h3. Steps to reproduce   # Set up a vanilla Jira instance   # Populate {{projectroleactor}} table to get ~100k results for the following query    e.g. by:     ## Generating ~100k users and assigning them to a single role (e.g. """"Developers"""") in a single project, *or*   ## Generating any amount of users, assigned to a single role in multiple projects so that users*projects=~100k   # Remove a user from role    h3. Expected result    Action completes successfully and lasts up to 100ms  h3. Actual result    The action is executed for several seconds which in total includes:   # Query to remove user from {{projectroleactor}} table:    executed for several seconds.     # Removing entries for specified role from {{projectRoleActorCache}}   # Loading all users for specified role into cache by executing:      which lasts for additional several seconds.    If the request is triggered several times, this can lead to *minutes* of system unresponsiveness as described in: [PS-40349|https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/PS-40349?focusedCommentId=18044069&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-18044069]  h3. Workaround   # Add index on {{PROJECTROLEID}} column in {{projectroleactor}} table   # Limit number of actors assigned to a given project role by:   ## Archiving unused projects   ## Splitting roles - e.g distributing users from large """"Developers"""" roles as in example above into """"Developers"""", """"Testers"""", """"IT Ops"""" etc.   # Threshold    requests or temporarily block them   # Do not trigger removing role actors in peak hours and do not trigger removing same actor"""

summary: "Associating a field configuration scheme with a new project is very slow in large instances if the scheme is reused"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  As an administrator, I want to use a single fieldConfigurationScheme for new projects. However, when we have a large number of projects using a single fieldConfigurationScheme, it takes a very long time to associate a new project with the fieldCongurationScheme.    {{fieldConfigSchemeManager.updateFieldConfigScheme(...)}} takes as a parameter a list of associated projects (i.e. all of them), and updates the database for each project one-by-one (com.atlassian.jira.issue.context.persistence.FieldConfigContextPersisterWorker.store(....) ) also flushing the fieldConfigScheme cache each time.  h3. Environment  Large Jira Instance, >2000 projects, using a specific fieldConfigurationScheme    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # create >2000 projects   # create a fieldConfigurationScheme   # associate all projects with the fieldConfigurationScheme   # create a new project   # set *DEBUG* for the *com.atlassian.cache.event* package   # associate new project with fieldConfigurationScheme and see in the logs that *com.atlassian.jira.issue.context.persistence.FieldConfigContextPersisterWorker.configContextsBySchemeId* cache is flushed 2000 times    h3. Expected Results  Persistence is handled efficiently, i.e. only add the association for the new project instead of updating all associations, hence causing a single cache flush.  h3. Actual Results  {{*com.atlassian.jira.issue.context.persistence.FieldConfigContextPersisterWorker.store(....) *}} is called once for each project that's already associated with the same fieldConfigurationScheme.    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "During new ticket creation, if there is some validation rules failed. New fixed version will still be created."
description: """h3. Issue Summary   During new ticket creation, if there is some validation rules failed. New fixed version will still be created.    h3. Environment    JIRA Version: 7.13.3, 8.0.2  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Prerequisites:   ** A User with Resolve Permission   ** Fix Version in Create Field Screen   ** In Workflow Create Transition: add a 'Permission Validator' on Close Permission   ** On Permission Scheme revoke all Close Permissions   # Create a new ticket   # Enter Summary and a new Fix Version   # It shall trigger a validation error due to """"*Creation blocked due to missing Close Permission*""""   # Left the issue creation page open.   # Open a new tab and grant your account with Close Permission   # From issue creation browser tab, where you have always the previous Create screen with failed validation, re-submit.   # JIRA showing error mention fix version had been created.    h3. Expected Results    Fix version not created as validation failed  h3. Actual Results  Fix version created even when validation failed    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "h3. The other Expected Results (probably preferred)    Since the Fix version has been already created during the 1st try, no error should be raised on the 2nd try."

summary: "Jira should be able to add or create new status via a REST API endpoint"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When migrating issues from external trackers to Jira Server, it becomes very tedious to first have to create each of the issue statuses that you want to use before importing issues. For large environment that could have had several hundred different statuses in them it then takes a lot longer than it should to import this data correctly.  h3. Suggested Solution    Create REST API endpoint that allows users to create new statuses.  Currently the endpoint [GET /api/2/status|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.2.1/#api/2/status-getStatuses] will return all statuses in Jira.  But there is no corresponding POST verb that could be used to create these same statuses via REST.    h3. Why this is important    Makes it easier to migrate data into Jira. CSV importer is not able to create new statuses within Jira, and the lack of a REST API endpoint to create a new status means more manual status creation work for an admin. With such a POST or PUT endpoint and admin could create a little REST script to simply call Jira and tell it specifically what statuses should be valid within that site.  h3. Workaround    none"""

Use Chrome or Firefox and attempt to use the browser's print to PDF or save as PDF functionality.    2. Expected result: The full sprint report saves as a PDF    3. Actual result: Only the first page of the report saves."""

summary: "Improve Azure Template documentation regarding versions/upgrades"
description: """h3. Summary    There were recent changes in the way Azure Jira templates default to versions when installing/upgrading. Most of our documentation suggests that the default version is the latest Jira version when in reality this was recently changed to the latest enterprise release version (Jira 7.13.0 at this specific time).  h3. Suggestion    Clearly document this change and add detailed instructions on how to upgrade an Azure cluster when dealing with a Jira 7.13.0 environment and trying to get to the latest Jira 8 environment.    [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/administering-jira-software-data-center-on-azure-938846973.html]    There's a Zero Downtime Upgrade limitation where you cannot upgrade across major versions, so going from Jira 7.13.0 to Jira 8.x.x needs to be detailed.         E.g. Zero downtime upgrade is not available when upgrading from Jira 7.x to Jira 8.x. You'll need to use one of the [regular methods|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/ADMINJIRASERVER/Upgrade+methods]. If you're already on Jira 8.x, you can use zero downtime to upgrade to any later version."""

summary: "Users cannot be removed from the Project Roles if the user is renamed in JIRA 8.2.0 "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If we rename a user in Jira 8.2.0, add a renamed user in a project role, Jira won't allow the user to be removed from the project role. Based on our observation, adding the users in the project role inserts _jira_ (user_key) value of the user in the {{projectroleactor}} table    {{app_user}}  |id|user_key|lower_user_name|  |10803|jira|jira123|    {{projectroleactor}}  |id|pid|projectroleid|roletype|roletypeparameter|  |11209|10000|10002|atlassian-user-role-actor|jira|    however, removing the user, JIRA tries to look for _jira123_ which is the {{lower_user_name}} in the {{projectroleactor}} table. In the above example, since it was _jira_ (user_key) that has been inserted in the {{projectroleactor}} table, _jira123_ (lower_user_name) is nowhere to be found. Thus, JIRA does not allow this user to be removed from the project role.  h3. Environment    Jira 8.2.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a user in Jira   # Rename the user   # Add the user in any project role   # Attempt to remove the user from the project tole    h3. Expected Results    The user will be removed from the project role  h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens.  h3. Workaround    Execute the following REST endpoint to delete the user from the role: [Delete actor|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.2.0/#api/2/project/\{projectIdOrKey}/role-deleteActor]      h3. Note    Tested in JIRA 8.0 and the bug can't be reproduced"""

summary: "Reporting on Jira issues does not include linked items such as Zendesk details or Bitbucket related items"
description: """As a manager of Jira issues, I sometimes need to include items in a Jira report that give me an overall picture of the status or attributes of linked objects.    As a manager, I need to be able to report inconsistencies such as why a Zendesk issue is Closed but the Jira issue is sitting in Open status.   * Report on Zendesk issue number, status and other fields from a Zendesk linked issue. Allow a reference like Zendesk(key) to return a list of tickets to which the current object refers to.   * Report on Bitbucket status such as whether commits or pull requests exist and how many of each. Allow a reference like Bitbucket(commits) which would return a list of commits related to the key of the current object.   * Report on fields from a parent-linked object such as a Story referring to an Epic. Allow a reference like parent(status)."""

Issue Summary    Using Firefox browser when a user goes to add an attachment in the comment using the visual text editor it shows up with a broken icon -   !brokenarrow.png|thumbnail!        h3. summary: "add workflow property which persists over all transitions"
description: """For some workflow implementation and customer requirements it would be good to implement the possibility of adding a global property to the entire workflow, which persists all over transitions.    For instance, I could add jira.permission.comment.projectrole to disable comments whilst in a transition but customer suggestion is to add the possibility to apply it to the entire workflow so that it can persist over all transitions.    Another possibility suggested by the customer would be to add the property directly in the transition, so that the transition could be used to allow/disallow comments.    As per customer comment:         """
comments: "Just to provide some background to this issue as I don't think the description describes the requirement that clearly.    We raise literally hundreds of incidents a day.  Some will be as straight forward as ""I can't login"" or ""network in office xyz is flaky"".  But a few are a lot more serious, such as ""Outlook is down"".  In all cases those working on the incident communicate updates by commenting on the issue, developing a thread of tests and changes as they go along.    The problem is that users also like to chime in with their comments, a common refrain is ""I have this issue too"".  In the case of an Outlook outage this can (and has) result in hundreds of comments that simply hide the important comments.  What would be useful is the ability to limit comments to the technicians for the duration of the investigation.    At this point I expect people to chime in and say, ""but Jira already has this"", so let me explain more.  Yes, Jira does already have this (the ability to upload an attachment w

summary: "Update ""Create, edit or remove a user"" documentation"
description: """h3. Problem definition    When there are multiple applications license on Jira instance (e.g. Jira Core and Jira Service Desk), """"create new user"""" dialog has all """"Application acces"""" checkboxes unchecked.  However, when there is only one, single application installed on Jira instance (either Jira Core, Software of Service Desk alone), Jira will automatically tick """"Application access"""" field for this installed application:   !image-2019-05-29-09-58-32-170.png|thumbnail!    !image-2019-05-29-10-04-36-702.png|thumbnail!    As this is not documented anywhere, administrators are not aware of this behaviour and they unintentionally grant licenses to their users (this is more problematic for Jira Service Desk users as Customers doesn't need to have a license assigned to them and suddenly they become Agents although they are not supposed to).  h3. Suggested resolution    Modify [Create, edit, or remove a user|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/create-edit-or-remove-a-user-938847025.html] documentation so it does documents this behaviour as default and expected."""

summary: "Improve Cluster Cache Replication HealthCheck to recognise nodes locked for re-indexing"
description: """h3. Current Behaviour    When a node is locked for a foreground re-index, the node enters an ERROR state but remains part of the cluster. This causes Cluster Cache Replication to halt and triggers this HealthCheck error. For instances with distributed management, it may cause undue alarm.  h3. Suggested Behaviour    This error should in this specific case be replaced with a warning as the node was explicitly requested by an administrator to be temporarily unavailable.     This new warning should also present the progress URL from the TaskDescriptor and, if possible, inform the administrator of what administrator or utility locked the node for reindexing.  h3. In addition..    The [Cluster Cache HealthCheck documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/healthcheck-cluster-cache-replication-738722400.html] should be updated to reflect that a node locked for reindex is a common cause for this error.     """
comments: "https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATST-966"

summary: "Jira Permission schemes should not allow you to attempt to configure nonsensical permission states"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Jira Server*. Using *Jira Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-72153].  {panel}  h3. Problem Definition  Several past cases have been reported of Jira admins trying to use a user custom field value or a group custom field value to set a project permissions such as 'Create Issue' or 'Browse Project'.  The intention of the admin here is to only allow the possible users in that custom field options to have that specific permission.   This does not work though for these specific permissions, because in order to evaluate these permissions the issue has to already be created in Jira.  These custom fields would have to have a value set within them on an issue already created.  The potential values of that field are not valid as a means to impose a permission grant on those specific permission options.  In the case of creating issues, it can't possibly work because the issue is not created yet.    h3. Suggested Solution  Change the way permission schemes work and edit the screens there in to prevent admins trying to set permissions that can never possibly be valid within Jira:  #  This could be done by specifically removing 'user custom field value' and 'group custom field value' from the objects that cannot possibly honor their hypothetical values yet.  We know this includes at least create issue and browse project, but there could be others in play here as well.   # Another solution could be to change the current behavior of the browse project permission when granted to the 'user custom field' and 'group custom field' when these 2 fields are not set to any value. In this case, instead of allowing any user to view the issue, no one should be able to view the issue until these fields are set.    h3. Why this is important  Lots of admins in Jira have attempted to configure this because Jira allows it, But can't possibly honor it.  It causes high levels of frustration and angry users to have Jira let you do something that it has no expectation of being able to do.  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-26659  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-30783  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-66317  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-21613  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-30783  https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Software-questions/JIRA-Bug-with-Group-Custom-Field-Permission-Settings/qaq-p/734856    h3. Workaround   none"""

summary: "Creating a project with shared configuration should also copy custom field contexts"
description: """In a scenario where you have a few contexts for a given custom field of type _Select List (single choice)_, as an example:    Context 1   - Project: Default   -- Values: 1, 2, 3    Context 2   - Project: MJ   -- Values: MJ1, MJ2, MJ3    Context 3:   - Project: GS   -- Values: GS1, GS2, GS3    Create a new project with shared configuration and select project """"GS"""" for example.  h3. Requested behaviour:    In the newly created project, the values of the custom field should be the same as """"Context 3"""" since the project was created based on project """"GS"""""""
comments: "While we wait for Atlassian to make this fix, and we know that it will probably be a while, I have found a workaround (fwiw).  It requires the add-on ""Configuration Manager"".    Create a snapshot of the original file    Deploy the snapshot as a ""New Project"" and give it the new project name/key.    The contexts that are set for the original project, will now also apply to the new project.     "

summary: "Jira displays incorrect result while searching for users from multiple user directories"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If Jira is configured with multiple user directories, single user which exist in multiple user directories, each of the identical username has a different email address and someone tried to search using with email of the user (from lower user directory, Jira will only display the user detail from the topmost directory.      h3. Environment    * Jira 8.1  * Jira 7.13.0  * Jira 7.13.1      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a username *test* with email of *atlassian123@gmail.com* in Internal Directory.  # Create another username with *test* with email of *supporttest@gmail.com* in a LDAP directory.  # Connect Jira to the LDAP directory.  # Move the LDAP directory to be above Internal Directory.  # Access *Users* page and search using *atlassian123@gmail.com* keyword.      h3. Expected Results    Jira shows either:    # No result or  # Some hints that user with the email address is hidden because it's in lower user directory.      h3. Actual Results    Jira shows the detail of identical username from top most directory.      h3. Workaround    If admin required to view the detail of the user from different user directory, reorder the user directory to make the user directory where the affected user belongs to as the highest user directory."""
comments: "We are affected by the same issue and it was eating into user licenses as the hidden users were active and thus used license slots.    We only found out about this during a database export of all users."

summary: "Documentation for SQL Server should include setting the autoCommit parameter in JDBC URL"
description: """This is a regression to [JRASERVER-61328|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-61328]    {quote}  {panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-61328].    {panel}    h3. Problem Definition  The """"connecting JIRA to SQL Server"""" documentations do not mention the autoCommit parameter in the JDBC URLs. This parameter is needed to prevent deadlocks as per https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/deadlock-when-running-jira-with-sql-server-283640022.html and so it should be a required configuration in JIRA's dbconfig.xml.    h3. Suggested Solution  Modify the documentation to include {{autoCommit=false}} parameter by default:  https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira063/connecting-jira-to-sql-server-2012-683541742.html  https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/JIRA064/Connecting+JIRA+to+SQL+Server+2008  {quote}"""

summary: "Frequent lucene-stats in logs triggered when closing temporary index (RAMDirectory)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Log can be flooded with {{[lucene-stats]}} entries when using {{TemporaryIndexProvider}} Jira service.    h3. Expected Results    Closing an index writer from a {{RAMDirectory}} should not trigger printing {{[lucene-stats]}} as closing this index is done frequently and is a cheap operation.     h3. Actual Results    The following log can appear frequently when service: {{TemporaryIndexProvider}} is used.      h3. Workaround    Add """"com.atlassian.jira.index.MonitoringIndexWriter"""" custom package to logging and profiling in JIRA and set the debug log level to ERROR.   That is until JIRA is restarted. To get the setting permanent, it'll need to be added to log4.properties file as per this article:  https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/logging-and-profiling-861253813.html  Note that this log should be turned back to default when this issue is fixed.   For any other type of index this log provides valuable statistics allowing to detect performance problems with indexing. """

summary: "Lexorank Management ""Next Rebalance"" status is confusing"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    In LexoRank Managment UI, (System > Lexorank management.), there is a Rank Status column, the """"Next Rebalance"""" status """"scheduled"""" is confusing / unintuitive.    !image-2019-05-23-17-16-13-353.png|thumbnail!         According to [Documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver076/managing-lexorank-945111766.html] the Next Rebalance will be scheduled, when Status is """"Warning"""".  |Status|  |OK|the rank length is in a healthy state|  |Warning|a rebalance has been scheduled|  |Critical|an immediate rebalance has started, you are approaching a state where rank operations will be disabled|    *When is a re-balance job is triggered or required?*   When the the ranking value splits in 2 different buckets.   The number of characters on the lengthiest rank field on the table is what defines the need for a rebalance operation, and here are the thresholds:   * 50 characters: a rebalance operation is scheduled to occur in 12 hours;     * 100 characters: a rebalance operation is immediately started;   * Issues with > 254 characters RANK can't be ranked.    But we also have this on the documentation:   |Next rebalance|  |Scheduled| - once the next threshold is reached, a rebalance will be scheduled|  |Immediate| - once the next threshold is reached, a rebalance will start immediately|  h3. Suggested Solution    We need better """"Next Rebalance"""" statuses, when Rank Status = OK. then """"Next Rebalance"""" Status should be something like """"Not scheduled”, so we can make this message more intuitive, less confusing for admins.    Also, Additionally documentation may be provided/updated to clarify on the different balancing statuses available, and what are the threshold / limits that will trigger the re-balance.    Or an information dialogue that the admin can hover a mouse over to learn more about what the status means."""

summary: "Ability to Filter Out What will be Displayed in the Development Panel"
description: """h3. Definition/Use Case  It would be great if we can exclude failing Bamboo plans from issue development panels. But to be more precise, we only need this for a few specific Bamboo plans. We create and test Bamboo jobs in a separate experimental plan und during this process, the corresponding builds may fail a lot (which is OK). However, since these builds also use our main source repositories (and their branches), the failing experimental plan is shown as a link in Jira issues. Furthermore, such failed builds lead also to warnings for releases that include the corresponding Jira issues.    h3. Suggestion  To have an option to configure what data will be displayed in the issue development panel.    """

summary: "Differentiate among Local and External gadgets when two Jira instances are linked"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When I have two Jira instances application linked and a user adds a gadget to a dashboard, duplicate gadgets with the same name appear (for example Filter Results) . These are labeled *Local* and *External* depending upon whether they are from the local Jira instance or the application linked instance.     *This causes confusion for users:*  * They don't know the difference between Local and External, or do not notice  * The gadget list loads external gadgets by default, so it's easy to select the wrong one by mistake  * The gadget name does not make it clear that the results are from a different Jira instance  * Once on a dashboard, the only way to modify the header is by updating the filter it uses  * If I have more than two Jira-to-Jira application links, it is impossible to tell at a glance which gadget belongs to which Jira instance.     h3. Suggested Solution    Instead of """"External,"""" list the name of the application link for the external application.     Include the name of the application link in the title of the gadget.     h3. Workaround     Users can be coached to select """"Show XML Link"""" to verify the URL of the instance they're linking from. """

summary: "Jira using double encoding for characters in referrer URL when loading dashboard "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira using double encoding for character in referrer URL when loading dashboard  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Refresh Jira dashboard while recording network traffic in browser (developer tools)   # Once the page is loaded, search for """"up_rendering"""" text in browser network console   # Click on one filtered request and observe double coded characters:   !image-2019-05-22-16-34-34-309.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    Jira doesn't double encode the characters, which means that space character is encoded as """"%20"""".  h3. Actual Results    Jira double encodes chracters, which means that """"%20"""" appears as """"%2520"""" (as """"%"""" characters corresponds to code """"%25"""").     This is also visible on this piece of URL extracted from the HAR file:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Pasting tab-separated text ignores line breaks in the editor's Visual mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Pasting a block of text that contains any tabs ('\t') will make the editor ignore line breaks.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit any field in Jira that uses the Rich Text Editor - for example, Comment and Description;   # Paste the following while using the """"Visual"""" mode:    # Note that, even though the second line doesn't have a tab, it will still paste with no line breaks;    h3. Expected Results  Line breaks should be interpreted regardless of other characters.    h3. Actual Results  Line breaks are ignored and have to be manually inserted.    h3. Notes  If the same block of text above has columns separated with spaces, this doesn't happen.    h3. Workaround  Use the """"Text"""" mode and, to preserve the spacing set by tabs, paste the text into a ''\{noformat}' macro."""

summary: "Activity stream filter should be able to hide actions of anonymous users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When you have a project in Jira that allows anonymous user access to things like commenting, the activity stream can become very full with anonymous user comments.  This makes it much harder to find actions or comments from other authenticated users.  The filtering options in the activity stream gadget do allow you to filter upon a username.  However since Anonymous users do not technically have a username, there is actually no way to limit or restrict those unauthenticated user's actions from the stream    h3. Suggested Solution  It would be helpful if the activity stream had some way to filter out for anonymous users.      h3. Why this is important  Many users find that anonymous comments are not as important to them as the actions of users with accounts.  This can help to make the activity stream full of more useful, directed information.    h3. Workaround   none"""

Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Nevertheless, I can see how this can become annoying and clutter logs. Please vote and watch in order for it to gain traction.    Cheers,    Pawel, Jira Server Bugmaster"

summary: "Project names should be unique and not equal to any existing project key"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Two projects can share the same name. A project can also be named exactly like the key of another project.   This breaks JQL searches and returns misleading results.   h3. Suggested Solution  Add conditions so that:   * Project names are unique (no duplicate project names)  * Project names should not be equal to any existing project keys    h3. Workaround  Rename the project  """

summary: "Ability to individually order the comments"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JIRA is only able to order the comments as a whole based on the timestamps (Ascending/Descending). It is not possible to individually change the comment order.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow users to be able to change the comments order individually. For example, users should be able to change the comment order of the 5th comment to the 2nd position.    h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "Suppose the default sorting is in ascending order. Then the user should also be allowed to change the comment order of ""ascending"" to ""descending"" independent of the default setting."

summary: "On Startup if CLUSTERUPDATESTATE = Running_Upgrade_Task, recheck if upgrade tasks need to be run"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    During a Zero Downtime Upgrade, all the nodes were reset during the upgrade.    This left the nodes unable to complete the upgrade tasks and they could not progress from {{running_upgrade_task}} in the {{CLUSTEREDUPDATESTATE}}    Due to this, the nodes were never able to successfully run the upgrade tasks and the ZDU update page showed the nodes as """"Running Upgrade Tasks"""" despite not running those upgrade tasks  h3. Suggested Resolution    Upon startup, if the last ID in the {{CLUSTERUPDATESTATE}} equals Running Upgrade Tasks, check to see if upgrade tasks need to be run.     Otherwise, revert back to {{Ready to run upgrade Tasks}}. This will allow Admins to manually change to {{Running Upgrade Tasks}} which will allow the upgrade tasks to be manually started.   h3. Why this is important    If nodes are not allowed to complete the upgrade tasks and transition from {{Running Upgrade Tasks to Stable then}} the upgrade will never be allowed to be finalized.    In addition, while nodes are in the status of {{Running Upgrade Tasks}} the upgrade cannot be canceled.  h3. Workaround     If nodes have been rebooted and startup with the status of {{Running Upgrade Tasks}} in the {{CLUSTERUPGRADESTATE}}    You can remove the entry and make sure the highest ID is {{READY TO RUN UPGRADE TASKS}} this will allow you to revert back to be allowed to run """"Finalizing Upgrade Tasks"""" which should then restart into {{RUNNING UPGRADE TASKS}} and then complete the upgrade tasks successfully."""

summary: "As an admin, please add batching to UpgradeTaskBuild03:Migrating Rows"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When upgrading Jira, certain upgrade tasks can take a long time to complete. The task below recently took an hour to complete which caused concern as we were unsure that it was still running.     {{plugin.devstatus.upgrade.UpgradeTaskBuild03: Migrating rows}}  h3. Suggest Solution    Please add batching of the migrated rows so admins can better follow the progress of this upgrade task.   h3. Why this is important    It would provide admins with the reassurance that the upgrade task was still running. """

summary: "Race condition when updating actors to project role"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   - Update project role actors functionality is vulnerable to race condition.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to """"Project"""" -> """"Settings"""" -> """"Users and Roles"""" menu   # Trigger multiple """"Add users to a role"""", """"Remove user from a role"""" actions interchangeably on the same project role.    h3. Expected Results    All actions are executed successfully and the resulting project role actors state expresses the sum of completed operations.  h3. Actual Results    Some executed operations are overridden by other threads and not present in the final result e.g. users still assigned to specific project role despite triggering an action to remove them.  h3. Workaround    Execute project role actor updates synchronously.     """

Set the Language to 'Russian'.   # Head to any issue and click on *More > Log Work*.   # Fill in the details in the *log Work* page.    h3. (Należy podać datę wykonania pracy.)       h3. Workaround    As for now, set the global language to Russian, then set it back to English. Then, the log work feature will work successfully."""

summary: "LDAP directories with URL containing ""unsupported symbol"" leads to unhandled error in health check"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the URL to a LDAP directory contains any """"unsupported symbol"""", the health check for LDAP directories will fail with 'IllegalArgumentException: hostname can't be null'. This is because the URI library only supports hostnames with alphanumeric characters or the hyphen symbol.    See JavaDocs for the library indicating this [here|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/net/URI.html#getHost--].    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Connect Jira to a LDAP directory with a URL like {{ldap.domain.com}} after that, change the URL/Hostname to one containing a symbol other than hyphen - e.g. an underscore, as in {{ldap_server.domain.com}};  # Eventually, you'll get the following error message in Jira's logs:      h3. Expected Results  Considering URLs may contain a number of different symbols, including the underscore mentioned in the example above, the check shouldn't fail.    h3. Actual Results  Hostnames with """"unsupported"""" symbols will cause the check to fail.    h3. Workaround  There are a couple of options:  # Ignore the error entirely - it doesn't affect any functionality  # Use URLs with no """"unsupported"""" symbols for configuring LDAP directories  # Use the IP address or an alternate hostname for the affected LDAP directory"""
comments: "https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATST-965"

summary: " the JQL worklogDate parameter search filter results are incorrect while using it in combination with NOT operator."
description: """*Summary*   the JQL *worklogDate* parameter search filter results are incorrect while using it in combination with *NOT* operator.    *Steps to Reproduce*   From the Jira Issue Search Advanced Mode launch the following query as examples:    *Example 1:*     *Example 2:*    Furthermore if we launch the following example query on a test instance:    The issue TEST-299 which has his last worklog added on 15/Jun/19 is instead retrieved:    !Screenshot 2019-05-17 at 13.59.53.png|thumbnail!    *Expected Results*   The expected results would be issues where the the worklog entries date are not > of """"YYYY/MM/DD"""" or not > startofday(-10).    *Actual Results*   The actual results seems like the operator *worklogdate* is not working when using NOT in front of it: instead of looking for issues which don't have any work logged after a certain date, it is looking for issues for which the first logged date did not occur after a certain date. So basically, instead of looking into any work log in an issue, it is looking for the 1st work log entry which is incorrect.  h3. Workaround    There is a workaround to fix the which consists in avoiding using NOT with the worklogdate operator, but to use an intermediate filter instead:  # First create a filter with the following JQL query:    # Then run the following query by replacing FILTER_ID with the ID of the filter created in the previous step:  """

If no work has been logged recently (but only longer then 5 days ago) we can take a look at those issues and see if action is needed.    Checking on the update field does not help as making any change (ie adding a comment) to an issue will change the update date as well.....and for adding a simple comment no work will be logged."

summary: "REST API: Get all permitted filters by user"
description: """Now I found this REST API in Jira cloud version:    [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/?utm_source=%2Fcloud%2Fjira%2Fplatform%2Frest%2F&utm_medium=302#api-rest-api-3-filter-get]    Would it possible to be available on Jira server version? It is really useful for us.     Or please advise whether there is a workaround to use REST API to get all permitted filters by user.         Thanks a lot!"""
comments: "NOTE: If it proves too complex to implement this as a fully supported feature in the REST API, perhaps a simple hack / workaround could be to return the ID of a filter in the error response when you try to create a filter that already exists. So, for example, if you weren't sure if a filter named ""my-cool-filter"" existed, you could just try and create a filter with that name. If the call fails and the response indicates that a filter with that name already exists, you could just parse out the ID of the existing filter from the response and perform another GET request to get the filter data that way.    Might be easier to implement something like this in the short term, which I think would have a lot of value to the Jira users given how many questions and comment threads there are about this spread across the internet atm."

summary: "Add escaping characters for special chars in quick search"
description: """In previous Jira versions, you could search in Quick search for text with special characters in, because an escape \ was always added into the search.   This doesn’t apply to Jira 8.x.    For example, while searching for a text in summary with special characters in, you have to add manually the \ char into the search.   Else the issue is not found.    As per [escapingSpecialcharacters|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939937723.html#Searchsyntaxfortextfields-escapingSpecialcharacters]:    *Special characters*   + - & | ! ( ) \{ } [ ] ^ ~ * ? \ :   Special characters aren’t stored in the index, which means you can’t search for them. The index only keeps text and numbers, so searching for """"  [Jira Software  ]"""" and """"Jira Software"""" will have the same effect — escaped special characters ([]) won’t be included in the search.    In previous Jira versions, you could use special characters to combine two separate terms into a phrase, for example """"Jira+Software"""" or """"Jira/Software""""."""

summary: "'Plan your Upgrade' page gives a warning 'You're using an unsupported database' when using Oracle 12c R1 as a database."

Issue Summary    In the [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html] page, it is stated that the supported database for Oracle is Oracle 12c R1 with the 12.2.0.1 driver.     When heading to the 'Plan your Upgrade' page, the 'You're using an unsupported database' is shown even when the supported Oracle database is being used.  h3. Expected Results    As the database is supported, there should be no warning thrown in 'Complete pre-upgrade checks'.  h3. Actual Results    The warning 'You're using an unsupported database.' is shown even when the supported Oracle database is used.    !plan your upgrade.PNG!  h3. Workaround    Proceed to upgrade the instance as the correct database is being used anyways."""

It would be great if this would be also possible in a sprint, but than move a user story to the top or bottom of that specific sprint backlog (so not to normal backlog).   So for example a function: """"move to top or bottom of this sprint"""""""

summary: "Atlassian Jira Diagnostics plugin may cause an OutOfMemory error"
description: """h3. Summary  In situations where the number of """"expensive"""" JQLs identified by the *Atlassian Diagnostics* plugin is high enough, Jira might end up running out of memory due to the number of objects stored in the heap.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    TBD    h3. Expected Results  Non-optimized JQLs will be stored in the database.    h3. Actual Results  JQLs that generated an alert in the plugin will pile in the heap causing constant full GC cycles and OOM errors.    h3. Workaround    We can increase the threshold of the alerts based on the query time with the following JVM argument:    * Note that the default value for this parameter is 400.    Alternatively, we can disable the *Atlassian Diagnostics* plugin through the *Manage Apps* page - this plugin will be found in the *System Add-ons* section."""

summary: "Any way to track if a marketplace app has been certified for migration to DC"
description: """Bloomberg recently upgrade to the latest enterprise release of Jira and migrated to Data Center.  As part of the migration, Bloomberg had challenges merging information from the Big Picture App.  While it is understood that the challenge is on the vendor they would like the marketplace to somehow detail if apps are 'certified' for migration to data center (rather than just it works as a fresh install with Data center).  This would help them better plan migrations and the effort involved."""

summary: "Individual nodes report used or available capacity value"
description: """Use Case:    As an administrator I would like to see some capacity or load value for each node. This would be the available capacity or the used capacity on that node.    I would like this value to be available so that it can be used in real time to determine the load on each individual node.   * This could be used in a load balancer to direct traffic to nodes with available capacity  * This could be used in external integrations that retrieve data from JIRA. The external integration could check for available capacity prior to making requests."""

summary: "Update documentation for Jira Archiving a project"
description: """Can https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/archiving-a-project-938847621.html  please be updated to include information with respect to Confluence?    Had a client ask me:    if a user has a link to a JIRA issue in an archived project, they will still be able to view the issue right?    so if a JIRA issue is displayed on a confluence page, it will still show?    I know that Confluence looks at the Jira index to pull out this information, but it would be helpful to update this information for all customers who have Jira DC and Confluence.    *Don't #@!% the customer.*    Cheers."""

My guess would be that direct links to an archived issue will continue to work, but anything that uses JQL to find the set of issues, e.g. jiraissue macro, will not work. Archiving an issue basically means removing that issue from the Jira Lucene index, but not the database"
