summary: "Improve ""Restoring data from an xml backup"" to reflect information on re-indexing during restoration "
description: """h3. Problem definition  When Jira user restores XML backup, the latest step that is being executed is re-indexing of the restored data, which appears when procedure reaches 90%. In many situation, re-indexing can take hours to finish and sometimes, the percentage gets frozen at 96% for some time.    As users don't have information that the re-indexing is going on in the background, they may get impression that something is wrong and the application got stuck, which is likely the case - the procedure will finish and reach 100% at some point.    h3. Suggested resolution  Improve documentation article so that it includes this information as mentioned above."""

summary: "Add REST endpoint to manage Issue Dev Details (git repo, deployment and build data) like Jira Cloud"
description: """As a Microsoft Partner, our company gets free access to TFS / Azure DevOps and use it for source code management, builds and deployments. We've been using Jira server for quite a long time but never quite integrated TFS data into Jira Issues the way it should (ie just like Bitbucket and Bamboo do):    !image-2019-05-15-10-29-46-840.png|width=237,height=204!    Upon reading [this article|[https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-articles/6-integrations-every-Jira-Software-Cloud-team-NEEDS-to-know/ba-p/1069040]] and looking at the Rest API for Jira Cloud, it seems it would be feasible to do just that. Unfortunately, the following API are only available for Jira Cloud:   * [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/#api-group-Development-Information]   * [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/#api-group-Deployments]   * [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/#api-group-Builds]    We think it should also be ported / made available in Jira Server as well to enable custom integration."""

summary: "Do not remove users if there is Work Log data associated with the user."
description: """Currently, an existing AD user would be deleted from Jira if:  * An AD synchronization happens where the user is outside of scope.  * And if the user is not an assignee or reporter on any Jira issue.    However, if the user is an assignee or a reporter on any issue, then the user would only be de-activated.    *Suggestion*  We have a use case where we do not want the user to be deleted if there is logged work associated with the user. Instead, de-activate the user."""

summary: "When a custom field is created with the name similar to 'Root cause', the search syntax in Advanced Searching will follow the syntax of the bundled custom field 'Root cause'."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Jira by default, there is an existing custom field named 'Root cause' with the custom field type 'Multi Line (Text)'. When using this custom field in Advanced Searching, the syntax should be :     !bundled jira Root cause field.png!    However, when a custom field is created with the name similar to 'Root cause', the search syntax in Advanced Searching will follow the syntax of the bundled custom field 'Root cause'.        h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field called 'Root Cause' with the custom field type 'Select List (single choice)'.   # Head to Issue Navigator and perform a search for the custom field 'Root Cause' with the JQL Query :       h3. Expected Results    When the new custom field 'Root Cause' is selected, the syntax should show :   !expected root cause.PNG!   And the search should be successful.    !expected search.PNG!  h3. Actual Results    The syntax shown will be for the custom field 'Root cause' instead.    !New Root cause syntax.PNG!    And the search for the correct syntax will not be successful.    !error.PNG!       h3. Workaround    Rename the 'Root cause' field if you are not using it or perform a search using the ID instead.    !root-cause-same-name.jpg!"""

summary: "Basic issue search retain custom field value which is applied to a specific project after switching to a project which doesn't have it in the context"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Basic issue search retain custom field value which is applied to a specific project after switching to a project which doesn't have it in the context  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Custom Field   # Set the context of the custom field to one project   # Create an issue on the project which has the field in the context and fill the field   # From basic issue search filter the project **which has the field in the context and also filter the custom field.   # Issue with the field in are shown   # Then remove the filter from the project which has the field in the context and switch to another one which doesn't have the field in the context.   # No issue are shown for that project   # Observe that it is impossible to remove the flag from the custom field from *More*   # **Switch to Advanced issue search and look at the JQL which still contains the custom field    I'm attaching a screen recording which explain better the weird behaviour:    [Basic search broken by custom field with context |https://transfer.atlassian.com/link?key=GHS-151863%2FScreen+Recording+basic+search+broken+by+custom+field+with+context_2019-05-15T12%3A03%3A38.557.mov]  h3. Expected Results    After switching to a project which doesn't have the field in the context, the custom field should be cleared.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour.     """

summary: "Option to configure batching email notifications per user"
description: """In Jira server version 8.1.0, when you enable batching email notifications they are enabled across all projects and applied to all users.    Can this be made a user preference instead of a global setting."""
comments: "When we trialed batched notifications this was our biggest thing too because some users really like batch notifications and others detest them, it'd be useful to have it configurable per user." [SEP] "An absolute must - some users prefer batching, but some users require quick notifications (e.g. service desk agents).         Forcing a single setting to all users is never a good option especially for large instances with many different teams."

summary: "System settings can affect how gadget text is rendered"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Currently JVM inherits file and system encoding from the operating system, unless JVM parameters are specified    Usually this is not a problem as the operating system is configured for UTF-8, however, a slight can cause this problem.    h3. Environment    Any operating system not using UTF-8 for file or system encoding    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure OS to use Latin-1 encoding (or ISO-8859-1 or Windows-1252) instead of UTF-8  # Use a multi-byte character such as *{{è}}* (e-Grave, U+00E8)    h3. Expected Results    Character renders as *{{è}}*     h3. Actual Results    Character renders as *{{Ã, ¨}}*    h3. Notes    We should ship the UTF-8 settings by default because there is no good reason to inherit the file/system encodings from the operating system.    https://www.i18nqa.com/debug/bug-utf-8-latin1.html  [Jira Application internationalisation and encoding troubleshooting|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-application-internationalisation-and-encoding-troubleshooting-203394762.html]    h3. Workaround  Add the following [JVM parameters|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-861253969.html]:       """

summary: "The ability for the 'Statistic Type' in Pie Chart gadget to be dynamic following the project selected"
description: """h3. Summary    As for now, when you create a pie chart gadget and choose a specific project, the *Statistic Type* does not respect the project selected. This can be confusing for instances that have a lot of custom fields and for the users to choose which custom field that needs to be selected.    In the future, we should make the *Statistic Type* dynamic following the project selected."""

summary: "Add a setting which will allow users to see empty Sprints in any board"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    For a sprint created in Board A to be visible in another Board B, there needs to be at least one issue in the sprint which respects the filter configured in Board B.    If the sprint is empty, it will only show in the Board where it was created, but not the other boards.    This behavior can be an issue when there are a lot of teams and a lot of stories and teams need to work on their own board. It would be nice to have empty sprints available in all backlogs so that the teams can plan their stories.     h3. Suggested Solution    Add a new setting in JIRA to allow empty sprints to be visible in any board.      """

summary: "Track announcement banner changes in audit log"
description: """Current Behavior:    * Changes to the announcement banner are not recorded in the audit log.    Desired Behavior:   * Record changes to the announcement banner in audit log. Show before and after like what shows when a user is renamed."""

summary: "Web links are not exported"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Web links are not exported    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a ticket  # Navigate to _More > Link_  # Select """"Web Link""""  # Add an URL and a link text  # Click on """"Link""""  # Select _Export > XML_ (or any format)    h3. Expected Results  The web link included should be exported    h3. Actual Results  The web links are not getting exported in any export format (XML, Word or Printable)    h3. Workaround    Use the following REST API call to get the web link information from a ticket:    {{http://JIRA_URL/rest/api/latest/issue/SCRUM-8/remotelink}}"""

summary: "When linking a Jira Project to a Confluence Space, there should be a quick option to sync the avatar between project and space"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-72033].    {panel}    h3. Problem    When working in Jira, a project avatar is one common way that admins will customize to help users quickly sort through to identify what project they are working in. When you then have to switch applications to Confluence or alike, there is no means to automatically use the same custom avatar icon for that linked space. This can cause confusion or delays until users can be sure they are working in the correct space/project  h3. Suggested Solution    It would be awesome if when you link a Jira Project to a Confluence space, if Jira would prompt you to sync the avatar between these two. Granted, it would be understood that the user creating this link would need to have admin rights in both applications likely, and there might be a question of which avatar to use when initiating the sync. However to be able to do this automatically for admins would be really helpful in making it very clear to end users which content is linked to what other content.  h3. Note:    This suggestion does not seek to sync avatars of user objects.  Just that between spaces and projects."""

summary: "Enabling batched notifications causes ""Issue Created"" notifications to be batched instead of sent immediately"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When I have batched email notifications enabled, Issue Created notifications are included in the batched notification instead of sending a separate notification.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable [batching notifications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-email-notifications-938847633.html#Configuringemailnotifications-batching]   # Have a notification scheme that will alert you when a new issue is created in a given project   # Create a new issue in that project   # Make some changes to issue fields    h3. Expected Results    Per the documentation on [configuring email notifications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-email-notifications-938847633.html#Configuringemailnotifications-batching], I expect:   * I immediately receive an email notification alerting me that a new issue has been created.   * Up to 10 minutes later, I get a second email notifying me of changes to issue fields/status/etc.    h3. Actual Results    I do not receive an email notification immediately. Instead, I receive one email within 10 minutes notifying me that the issue was created and of my changes.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Using capital NOT inside a string on a JQL will cause Jira to not match any issues to the search"
description: """h3. Summary    If capital {{NOT}} is used inside a string when searching for issues, Jira will fail to match any issues to this search.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue that contains the word {{not}} in the summary.  # Go into the issue navigator and search this issue with the following JQL:      h3. Expected Results    Issues that match the query will be returned on this search.    h3. Actual Results    No issues are returned from this query.    h3. Workaround    Adjusting the JQL and replacing {{NOT}} with {{not}} will ensure that the query works as expected."""
comments: "Interestingly the Lucene query generated is both cases is the same. So it must be something else in the JQL parser I guess"

summary: "Rename CommentAssignIssue to TransitionIssue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When transitioning an issue that contains a screen, the action called is """"CommentAssignIssue"""".  This can be misleading when we have to investigate user activity, such as identifying slow actions, etc.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a workflow transition with a screen   # Perform the workflow transition in a ticket    h3. Expected Results  When checking the requests, either via access_log or another method, we expect to see something like: {{TransitionIssue.jspa}}    h3. Actual Results  Instead we see see that the Jira request: {{CommentAssignIssue.jspa}}"""

summary: "When adding a long site title, the menu items are not lining up with selected menu item"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When adding a large site tile, the menu items are not lining up with selected menu item    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to """"Look and feel"""" (_Administration > System > Look and feel_)  # Check the option """"Show Site Tile""""  # Navigate to the """"General configuration"""" menu (_Administration > System > General configuration)  # Add a long site tile  # Check the menu header   !sup_navigation.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Jira should keep the alignment    h3. Actual Results  Jira is not lining up the menu items with the selected menu    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Cannot set Security Level value on sub-task when the field is hidden"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Cannot set Security Level value on sub-task when the field is hidden. This applies when using custom add-on to explicitly set the field value.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure Security Level with the project  # Hide Security Level field on all screens and hide it in Field configuration  # Utilize custom add-on to set the Security Field value when the sub-task gets created    h3. Expected Results  After the sub-task gets created, the field value is set.    h3. Actual Results  After sub-task gets created, although the add-on is configured to set the value, the value is not set, but it is empty.    h3. Notes  * This does not occur with some other tested system fields (like Due Date).  * *When a user hides Security Level field on the parent ticket, custom add-on is able to set a value, although the field is completely hidden. Here we can see difference in behaviour between sub-task and other issue types.*  * The behaviour was tested with Automation Lite and Copy and Sync add-ons    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When inserting line breaks (i.e.: ""//"") to control line spacing in Jira Visual Editing Mode, in Jira Service Desk Queue Page, visual editor gets broken."
description: """h3. Summary  When inserting line breaks (i.e.: """"//"""") to control line spacing in Jira Visual Editing Mode, in Jira Service Desk Queue Page, visual editor gets broken.    h3. Environment  Jira Version : Tested on Jira SD 4.0.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open JIRA Service Desk Project.  # Click on *Queues* to show all open issue.  # Click on any issue to open it. (The Queues should show at your left hand side)  # Insert a line break (i.e.: """"//""""), a table and other hmtl macro formatting in visual view editing mode.  # Add in the Comment.  # It will show broken line breaks, additional line breaks and broken tables.  # Issue will not happen if we are using text editing mode or open the issue without queues at your left hand side.    h3. Expected Results  Formatting should be show correctly.    h3. Workaround  Use text editing mode while inserting this formatting.    """

summary: "HTML specification for Favicons"
description: """h3. Definition  As per https://www.w3.org/2005/10/howto-favicon, JIRA doesn't comply with the standard HTML format for favicons. In JIRA 8.0, it shows as     for example. However, based on the above link, it should be:      h3. Suggestion  It would be great if JIRA would follow the standard HTML formatting for favicons."""
comments: "The missing   <link rel=""shortcut icon"" ...  seems to create issues with Firefox bookmark icons."

summary: "JIRA dates don't show in Confluence Team Calendar"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding an event in Confluence Team Calendar, the custom date fields in JIRA are not showing.     h3. Environment  JIRA 8.0 onwards  Confluence 6.12.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have an application link between JIRA and Confluence and ensure that the OAuth token is generated in both apps.   # Create a custom date picker field in JIRA and add it in a screen.  # Create an issue in JIRA and fill up values in the custom date picker field from Step 2.   # Navigate to Confluence's Team calendar and left click on any date.  # Select the JIRA project where the custom date picker field is configured    h3. Expected Results  The custom date picket field should show on the fields section  !792.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  Only the default date fields are there.  !800.png|thumbnail!    h3. Notes  It used to work in JIRA 7.9.2 but not in JIRA 8 onwards.     h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available      """

summary: "Fixing error on Integrity Checker is saying No error were corrected when it actually fixed something"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Fixing error on Integrity Checker is saying """"No errors were corrected"""" when it actually fixed something. This is a misleading message because the errors are actually fixed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Run Integrity Checker.  # If there is an error being flagged out, hit the Fix button.    h3. Expected Results  Integrity Checker to fix all the errors and report back to the user saying that all the errors have been corrected.    h3. Actual Results  Integrity Checker fixes all the errors and reporting to the user that """"No errors were corrected"""".  !integrity-checker.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Bloomberg - Feedback on Sprint Goals"
description: """Had a recent call with Bloomberg who are in process of upgrading to 7.13.  Couple of pieces of feedback on the 'Sprint Goals' field that their user community provided that I thought I'd share:    1/ They asked if there was any plans to expand the sprint goals field to be more than a single line?  In the past, they have create a ticket per sprint with a series of bullet points.  The are having to now list out the goals separated by '|' for groups wishing to use the functionality.  Thus, they would like the sprint goals to be multi-line    2/ Can sprint goals be searchable?  They'd like to identify common goals across multiple sprints.         I understand these request may be more 'Jira Align/Portfolio' but I was asked to raise them.     """

summary: "Ability to restrict label visibility"
description: """Would be helpful if a Label could have a visibility level set similar to Comments."""

summary: "Adding a comment to an issue via the update issue API endpoint sends wrong transition in webhook data"
description: """h3. Summary  When using the [update issue|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.0/#api/2/issue-editIssue] API endpoint to add a comment, Jira fires a webhook and sends the last transition of the issue in the data. This makes the data look as if the issue was just transitioned, which is wrong.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup webhooks to fire on issue update events.  # Create an issue an transition from status A to status B.  # Update the issue via the API but send a PUT request to */rest/api/2/issue/{issueIdOrKey}* with the data:   {code:json|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}  {""""update"""": {""""comment"""": [{""""add"""": {""""body"""": """"Comment body""""}}]}}      h3.Workaround  Use the [Add comment|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.0/#api/2/issue-addComment] endpoint to add comments to issues. This fires the issue updated webhook with the correct data."""

summary: "Ability to have the Websudo functionality working with SAML / SSO"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When implementing SAML either through JDC or through a vendor plugin, the net result is you have to turn off websudo because you can't get websudo and SAML to work. The effect is you can go straight into administration functions without confirmation that you should. This poses a security risk.     h3. Suggested Solution  I am requesting an enhancement to allow websudo to work with both your JDC SAML setup as well as to allow websudo to work with other marketplace SAML plugins."""
comments: "I had a discussion with Scott Farquhar during the Atlassian Summit about the real need for Atlassian to improve in areas that will help large Enterprises be able to use (at scale) Atlassian products.    This is one of those areas, that should be an easy win. This is a big Security risk! Please advance this issue ASAP.    Thanks,    Michael Dennis    Manager Atlassian Enterprise Services - Disney" [SEP] "Looking forward for Atlassian to add this feature in their roadmap." [SEP] "With Jira Data Center 8.5.4 websudo and Okta using the standard Data Center SSO integration seems to work for us. No plugins used"

summary: "DC Re-indexing progress & events aren't fully conveyed by the UI"
description: """While the re-indexing progress bar does show when the re-indexing step itself is being done, there isn't similar visibility around operations that are DC-specific. Things like snapshot creation, compression, and distribution are not accounted for.    These other events should be shown as well when they're underway."""

summary: "After creating project from sample data on instance with German language, Workflow names include non-ASCII characters "
description: """h3. Problem definition  Currently, after creating project from sample data (Projects > Create project > Create sample data > select Project management template) on instance with German language, Workflow names include non-ASCII characters (hyphen):   !image-2019-05-09-10-36-08-885.png|thumbnail!    However, as per JRASERVER-41875, manually created workflows can't be named by using non-ASCII characters.    This makes confusion when copying workflow, as user gets message """"Please use only ASCII characters"""", if name of copied workflow still refers a hyphen.    h3. Suggested  resolution  Change workflow naming for worklows created from templates, so they don't include non-ASCII characters."""

summary: "The /rest/api/2/issue/<issueKey>/worklog endpoint does not support pagination or ordering"
description: """h3. Summary  As per JRASERVER-34746, the {{/rest/api/2/issue/<issueKey>/worklog}} does not support pagination or ordering    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue that has a reasonable number of worklogs  # Attempt to request the worklog endpoint, using the {{startAt}}, {{maxResults}}, {{total}} or {{orderBy}} to filter the results. For example, {{http://localhost:8080/rest/2/api/issue/TEST-1/worklog?maxResults=10}}.    h3. Expected Results  The rest endpoint returns the results in line with the parameters specified in the URL. In the example given, only 10 results should be returned (and the value should be reflected in the JSON output).    h3. Actual Results  The rest endpoint returns all results, from oldest to newest. The {{maxResults}} value in this example would show all worklogs for that issue.    h3.Workaround  There is no workaround at this time. Additional processing must be done by the REST consumer to manipulate the results for an issue's worklogs."""
comments: "With my server setip, using APi v2, it does NOT return all results, but only the first 20. This is a showstopper for a Jira utility my company is trying to write."

summary: "Component values are not captured in History Tab properly. Only the last value added  is recorded "
description: """In History Tab only the last value added for """"component"""" is recorded and the previous values are ignored  h4. Steps to Reproduce:   # Create 2 components(eg.comp1 and comp2) in a project   # Create a new issue   # Edit issue and Add comp1 into the component field   # Edit issue again and Add comp2 into component field    h4. Expected Behaviour    The second row history for comp2 should be:  ||Original Value||New Value||  |camp1|camp1,camp2|  h4. Actual Behaviour:  ||Original Value||New Value||  | |camp2|      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior.    h4. Note    This seems to affect  """"Component"""" and """"Affect Version"""" and """"Fix Version"""" fields.          """

summary: "Allow admins adjust delay for email notifications batching"
description: """Since Jira Server 8.0 admins can switch on email notifications batching per instance. The current interval for notifications is 10 minutes and cannot be set to a different value.     Admins should be able to configure the batching delay time because some teams cannot afford to wait 10 minutes. There are also instances where longer delay would be useful."""
comments: "It would be great if you could set certain things to be exceptions, and send immediately. For instance, comments and Assignment change are items our teams want faster notification on than status or description edits."

summary: "Ability not to include sending user details in ""Send email"" functionality in JIRA"
description: """Currently, when JIRA admin sends an email from secure/admin/SendBulkMail!default.jspa , JIRA will include the current user identifying information - such as email address and name.    It will be good to have an ability to let the administrator decide whether to include the sending user details or not as otherwise it creates a risk when customers start responding directly to the administrator email, thus actual JIRA cases don't receive the updates"""

summary: "For newer versions of Jira, the old favicons should be removed to prevent it to be loaded up"
description: """h3. +Summary+    Since old favicons are not being used anymore, it is better for us to completely remove all icons related to the old versions to prevent it from being loaded up as the icon in web browsers in the future. The difference in icon can cause confusion for some users."""
comments: "Started with 7.10, 8.0.1 affected." [SEP] "Somewhat related to https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-69259"

summary: "Creating or editing an issue with an invalid value for a custom field (or invalid default value) will produce a large stack trace in the logs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-15424 (and https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-17260) we prevented Jira from throwing large stack traces when _viewing_ an issue that has an invalid value in a custom field.    However, if Jira has an invalid field in a custom field (or an invalid default value), editing or creating the issue will still throw a large stack trace.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Text Field, and set the default value to something that is not a URL.   # In the database, change the {{customfieldtypekey}} to {{com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.customfieldtypes:url}} in the {{customfield}} table; and change the {{customfieldsearcherkey}} to {{com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.customfieldtypes:exacttextsearcher}} (these are the types used by a URL field by default)   # Restart jira, and attempt to create an issue    h3. Expected Results   * A warning should appear in the logs with information for an administrator to correct the problem    h3. Actual Results  When creating an issue, the following will appear in the {{atlassian-jira.log}} file:  {code}  2019-05-08 14:09:38,863 http-nio-8810-exec-13 ERROR admin 849x54x1 1ncbb3n 0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1 /secure/CreateIssue.jspa [c.a.j.i.customfields.impl.AbstractSingleFieldType] Incorrect formatted custom field stored as default com.atlassian.jira.issue.customfields.impl.FieldValidationException: Not a valid URL  {code}    When editing an issue, you'll see a little more detail, with a full stack trace:  {code:java}  2019-05-08 14:25:29,078 http-nio-8810-exec-3 WARN admin 865x123x1 1ncbb3n 0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1 /browse/KEY-1 [c.a.j.i.customfields.impl.AbstractSingleFieldType] Issue KEY-1 has an invalid value 'something that is not a url' stored in the field 'Converted to URL field'. summary: "Print option in the browser will not print all information of a report even if there is more than one page worth of information to print"
description: """h3. Summary  When trying to print reports like the Burndown Chart or Sprint Report using right-click > Print option in a browser (Chrome), the preview it's cutting off information when we have more than 1 page worth of it to show. This will not only affect the preview but will also cut the pages in the printed report itself.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a project with enough issues in a sprint, let's say 40 issues   # Go to the Reports page, and select Burndown Chart. It will show the chart, and below a list of the issues in the sprint selected and it's event status.   # Right-click on the page   # Select option """"Print""""    h3. Expected Results  The print preview display all issue event status available, even if more than one page is necessary.    h3. Actual Results  The print preview is not showing all issue events available, cutting it all into one page and the rest is missing. Attached is screenshots showing this behaviour.    h3. Notes  Tested on a clean Jira Server 8.0.2    h3. Workaround  No workaround found at the moment. Please, if workaround exists, share it in the comment section of this ticket and add to the description if possible."""
comments: "I think fixing this bug should be marked as a higher priority. It breaks a very fundamental Jira feature for our dev team, and we are now having to spend a lot of time manually copying reports into documents as a workaround. It is also worth noting that we have tried Edge and Firefox browsers as well, and the problem persists.    We have been a customer for several years now, and this bug has lingered for the past several Jira versions. Atlassian Support emailed me and suggested a 3rd party app, but we do not have the funds to do that." [SEP] "+8.2.3" [SEP] "I agree with earlier comment, this issue should be fixed with highest priority.  It is adversely impacting our business." [SEP] "Add this one to your regression suite! It's an expected and key feature used by many!" [SEP] "+8.0.1" [SEP] "Hi everyone,    This bug has been misleadingly labelled with the ""High"" priority for the last few months. In reality, neither is there any ongoing development aiming at fixing, nor are we planning to address it in the

summary: "ReIndexing reporting wrong numbers on Jira Datacenter with Archived projects"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira Datacenter is reporting wrong numbers after finishing foreground re-Indexing.    The re-Indexing finishes unexpectedly before it reaches 100% and no errors are being logged.    The following appears in the atlassian-jira.log:    Also the below could be seen in the logs:    h3. Steps to Reproduce    No known steps to reproduce this issue so far.  h3. Expected Results    Re-Indexing should finish at 100% and Jira should report the right number of issues responding to the call:    h3. Actual Results   * Jira reports wrong progress while doing Re-Indexing   * and the above call shows zero issues in archive (_""""countInArchive"""": 0_) which is wrong:      h3. Workaround    The workaround for this issue is simple, we need to correct the values of the cache by flushing it and rebuilding the number.   This can be done by adding or removing a project.    Please follow the below steps:   # As a workaround to clear the cache, create a new empty project   # verify if countInArchive has changed in */rest/api/2/index/summary* response.    The above issue is also described in the KB: [ReIndexing reporting wrong numbers on Jira Datacenter with Archived Projects|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/JIRAKB/ReIndexing+reporting+wrong+numbers+on+Jira+Datacenter+with+Archived+projects]."""

Therefore, we would like to monitor the problem and won’t be closing issue yet.    Regards,   Marcin (Jira Developer)" [SEP] "We are on Jira Data Center 7.13.9 with around 600 archived projects. Structure's error: /rest/structure/2.0/poll [c.a.

summary: "Use correct spelling of Ukrainian timezone Kyiv, not Kiev"

Here is the link to the official information from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine [https://mfa.gov.ua/en/page/open/id/5418]    #KyivNotKiev"""
comments: "Here is the answer from tz database supporters: [https://mm.icann.org/pipermail/tz/2019-May/027918.html]    Key statements:  # ""timezone names"" are really just internal identifiers for database use,  and interfaces that ask you to choose a timezone shouldn't directly  present them to the user.  # So the best way to improve the situation would probably to improve timezone  selection interfaces so they no longer force users to choose somewhat crytic  and misleading timezone IDs, but use a better interface, such as selecting a  geographical location."

summary: "HTML in custom field descriptions doesn't work on the Issue View"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Custom Field with the following description:  {code:html| borderStyle=solid| borderColor=#F4F5F7 |bgColor=#F4F5F7}  <script type=""""text/javascript"""">    alert(""""Javascript in Custom Field"""");  </script>  {code}  # Add it to all screens.  # Edit the issue and add a value to the custom field.  # Reload the page    h3. Expected  The JavaScript would be executed on page load, and the user would receive an alert in the browser.    h3. Actual  The JavaScript is not executed, unless the custom field is edited (through inline editing).    h3. Notes  * This happens even with the *Enable HTML in custom field descriptions and list item values* set to *ON*.    h3. Workaround  Adapt the JavaScript to add it to the *Announcement Banner* instead."""

summary: "JIRA SAML to syncs the user from IdP"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  At the moment, to integrate JIRA Data Center with SAML, the required configuration is to have the same DC Instance connected to the same directory that the IdP is connected to. Hence, JIRA needs to be connected to the IdP and the directory at the same time. If it is not, JIRA would not be able to detect which user is currently authenticating through SAML until the user is registered.    h3. Suggested Solution  Rather than connected to both, for JIRA to only connect to IdP and automatically sync the users from it so it will be easier. This appears to be available for our Cloud service as in [ACCESS-33|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/ACCESS-33] which will be great if Server has it as well.  """

summary: "Password is not correctly set when deploying Jira Data Center on Azure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    h3. Environment  * Jira Data Center  * Azure    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Follow [Getting started with Jira Data Center on Azure|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/getting-started-with-jira-data-center-on-azure-938846970.html] to deploy Jira.  # Try to access Jira with the password that was configured for the administrator account.    Example of configuration: [^config.png]    h3. Expected Results  You'd be able to log in with the password set during the deployment.    h3. Actual Results  The password does not work. In the database, the users' password is set as {{$\{user.password\}}}    h3. Workaround  Follow the [Retrieving the JIRA Administrator|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira/retrieving-the-jira-administrator-192836.html] guide to reset the administrator's password."""
comments: "Hi,    As part of the Azure deployment, we download some Atlassian jars to generate server ids and encrypt user-defined passwords. Unfortunately due to the Atlassian Maven repository deprecation  [https://developer.atlassian.com/server/framework/atlassian-sdk/atlassian-maven-repositories-2818705/] some of the jars became unavailable and the deployment errors were silently swallowed. We've fixed the links and are now resubmitting the updated templates to Azure Marketplace. In the meantime, users can install via our Bitbucket GIT repo at [https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-azure-deployment/src/master/] which will always have the latest updates.    Sorry for any inconvenience." [SEP] "Due to the Atlassian Central Maven deprecation, we had to update some links and harden the deployment to highlight script failure. We deployed updates to Azure Marketplace and MS have just made them live so should be resolved."

summary: "Archiving a release will not prevent it from showing up in JQL filters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create three new releases;   # Set one as released, one as released *and* archived, leave one as unreleased;   # Run the following JQL;      h3. Expected Results    Only what is released and not archived should be displayed.  h3. Actual Results    Both the released version and archived released version will be displayed;  h3. Notes    There isn't a native way to filter for archived versions. JRASERVER-66325 suggests implementing a check for {{archivedVersions()}}  h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Need ability to report of number of users that use any Add-on Application"
description: """Atlassian needs to add a """"unit of usage"""" to the Add-on framework, so that each Add-on application can report the number of users per month, week and per day, and the list of users that run any add-on application.    Add-on applications are not cheap, and many times we have to decide to buy one add-on over another, based on how much money we have to spend.  And we wonder if we should renew an add-on.  We'd like to make those hard decisions using actual user data.  Why should we renew an expensive add-on application, if less than 5% of the Jira usage population regularly uses it?  Or if 90% of the user population uses an add-on application multiple times per day, that add-on app should certainly be renewed.    Atlassian prepares the framework that developers use to write and publish Add-on Applications for Jira. So I believe Atlassian could add to that framework a way for the Add-on Apps to record what they consider an instant of usage, and then record that usage to the usage area of the framework. From which Atlassian could then easily publish usage of each Add-on.    For example, when I go the """"Manage apps"""" link, the Atlassian Add-on Framework could show the number of users using this app in the last month, week, day, or hour etc, that each application has passed back to it. For each """"User-Installed apps"""" that is listed.   There could also be a link that takes the user to the Application Usage Metric, that some charts that could indicate heavy usage over time of day, etc.          """

summary: "As a System Admin, I would like to export specific project configurations"
description: """As a System Admin that handles staging and production instances, it would be beneficial to export *certain* project configurations from one instance to the other, without also exporting issues or an entire project. This way a roadmap of functionalities can be planned and developed in parallel, changes could be deployed as they become necessary."""

summary: "As an administrator, I would like swap enabled by default in Jira Azure templates so that I can prevent outages due to lack of physical RAM"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently swap is not enabled by default in Azure templates, which can lead to JVM crashes when the host server runs out of available RAM.     h3. Suggested Solution    Enable swap by default.     h3. Why this is important    To prevent JVM crashes due to exhausted physical RAM.      h3. Workaround     * Deploy a larger sized Azure Jira cluster  * Manually enable swap on the Azure host"""

summary: "When configuring JIRA indexing, Service Properties Will Be Overwritten (Cleared)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When configuring JIRA indexing, Service Properties Will Be Overwritten (Cleared)    h3. Environment  Tested on JIRA 8.1, 7.13    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup a JIRA Instance  # Configure *Index Recovery* set *Enable index recovery* to *ON* in *localhost/secure/admin/IndexAdmin.jspa* page  # Access to *Services* configuration page *localhost/secure/admin/ViewServices!default.jspa*  # Look for *JIRA Index Snapshot Service*  # Edit it, set *Number of backupsp to retain* to a number for example *5*  # Update it  # You will see properties showing *backupCount:5*  # Access to *index recovery* page and click *Edit Settings*  # Set the *Schedule* or *Interval* and *Update* it  # You will see *backupCount:5* gone missing in services configuration page now    h3. Expected Results  Services properties should retain unless we disable the service    h3. Actual Results  Properties cleared after we update it via *Index recovery* page      h3. Workaround  -"""

summary: "Option to redirect user to Profile page after first login"
description: """We suggest implementing functionality that would give us option to choose whether the user needs to be redirected to Profile page during first login.    This would be useful for both new users and Jira administrators, as new users would be able to update their information and make sure it's correct."""

summary: "Browse archived issues page"

The in-app page with list of archived issues would help admins in such cases."""

summary: "Create a new field which includes the last user that updated an issue, and which can be displayed in the issue search page"
description: """    h3. Problem Definition    There is currently no native field in JIRA out-of-the-box which includes the username of the last user which updated an issue.    The unsupported add-on [Toolkit Plugin for Jira|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/5142/toolkit-plugin-for-jira] provides a field which contains this information, but the problem is that this field cannot be displayed in a column in the issue search page, therefore this add-on only partially helps.    h3. Suggested Solution    Create a new built-in field in JIRA which contains this information and which can be displayed in a column in the issue search page.        """

summary: "Allow Linked Issue column in Issue Navigator to show the Linked Issues' Status"

It would be great if the column can include further information of the Linked Issue such as the Status.    h3. We would like for Jira to simplify the error shown in mail as this will cause confusion to users.     For example, the error below when you create an issue with a priority that does not exist :        We would like the error to be simple and shown as :   """

summary: "Using Autocomplete on assignee field may not return Autocomplete matches."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Autocomplete on the assignee field may not return matches although a match to the search string exists.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create more than 50 users with similar names. Example:  #* PersonA  #* PersonB  #* PersonC  #* PersonD  #* ...  #* PersonXXX  # Go to Issue View, and trigger Autocomplete by typing PersonXX    h3. Expected Results  Autocomplete returns a suggestion which is PersonXXX.    h3. Actual Results  Autocomplete returns """"No Match"""".    h3. Notes  The problem happens because the Autocomplete REST API call to retrieve the user suggestions limits searches to 50 results as seen from the request URL below.    Autocomplete works as expected and suggests the user if the user is within the first 50 results, sorted alphabetically. However, it returns """"No Match"""" if the name is outside the first 50.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Built-in Interactive Search instead of Pagination"
description: """*Background*  Atlassian Applications such as Jira and Confluence implements _pagination_ as a user interface design feature. The purpose behind pagination is also to improve on Performance whereby loading of items are limited.    *Suggestion*  Most modern graphical user interfaces are using _Built-in Interactive Search_ which can be a better way instead of _pagination_. A search function would be able to return accurate results quicker instead of having the person to click on pages based on """"guesses"""" on where the items are.     If _pagination_ is still the way to go, then at least implement a global setting which defines the maximum number of entries one sees per page.    P.S: This might be a good source of reading, https://www.nngroup.com/articles/item-list-view-all/  """

summary: "When creating linked issues from the Issue Detail view of requests in 'Boards', user is redirected to 'Active Sprints' page."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating linked issues from the Issue Detail view of requests in 'Boards', user is not redirected to the 'Create Linked Issue' page, however, the page refreshes and user is redirected to 'Active Sprints' page.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board from an existing Business or Service Desk project (the bug does not affect Software projects).   # Head to 'Backlog' and click on an issue to view it.   # Click on (..) and choose 'Create linked issue'.    h3. Expected Results    User will be redirected to 'Create Linked Issue' page.  h3. Actual Results    User is redirected to 'Active Sprints' page.       h3. Workaround    Create linked issue via Issue View."""

summary: "When creating issues via HTML links, the default value for custom field (Text field(multi-field line)) is not set"

Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field with the type 'Text Field - Multi-line' and add a Default value to it. Add it to the Create/Edit/View screen of the issue.   # Then, [create an issue via link|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/creating-issues-via-direct-html-links-159474.html].    h3. Expected Results    The Default value is added to the custom field in the Create Issue screen.  h3. Actual Results    The Default value is missing in the custom field in the Create Issue screen.  h3. Workaround    Create issue manually for projects that have default value set in it."""

summary: "Reflect Subtask creation in Issue History and Last Updated on Parent Issue"

summary: "Data import verbiage from Migrating Jira Applications documentation are confusing"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  *Documentation Bug: [Migrating Jira applications to another server|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/migrating-jira-applications-to-another-server-938846962.html]*    Please revise and clarify important data import steps from all versions of the article:  h3.Details  From the two newest versions of _Migrating Jira applications..._ (Jira 8.0-8.1):    Within the section *Start Jira for the first time*, steps 4 and 5 (""""Select My own database""""..."""""""") make it unclear that they are actually separate screens in the setup wizard/installation process. Additionally, the link """"Import your data"""" is not very prominent on that second screen of the setup wizard, and the fact that it's a link (not a button), leads one to believe it's a link to our online KB and not a functional step in the process. (And other links in the Setup Wizard process redirect to online KB articles)    For pre-Jira 8.0 versions of the article:     e.g. [Migrating Jira applications to another server|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/migrating-jira-applications-to-another-server-938846962.html], the relevant section is   *4. Start Jira and connect it to the database*, the same steps are number list items 3 and 4."""
comments: "Yes, I was the one who requested this. In its current form a screenshot in your KB article(s) with an arrow to the appropriate section or a highlighted box around it would be helpful.    And to further clarify, I had accidentally breezed past this import step by typing in a (new) site name and filled in the obvious fields I saw (obliging the prompts) on the page at first and proceeded to the next screen, only to realize afterward that you could click the hyperlinked text to take you an alternate setup route, even though I was in fact expecting a page with an XML import step.    As far as future feature improvements: I wonder if a clear divisor line (almost like an HTML <HR> tag which draws a line) could be placed on that page with something like ""Option 1"" on top for import and ""Option 2"" below for new sites, or alternately a radio button that activates the relevant part of the menu based on an ""Import / New"" selection step.    Any thoughts on whether that may be improved in future versions?" [SEP] "@t

summary: "SQL query to check workflow current step entries in Integrity Checker"
description: """Hi,    We get a timeout in browser when we check *workflow current step entries*. Even, we have increased the proxyTimeout to 20 minutes, but timeout still reproducing.    We need a SQL query for check *workflow current step entries*, like this page: https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-run-the-workflow-integrity-checks-in-sql-658179102.html.    Could you update the previous page with a SQL query for this check?    Thank you."""
comments: "we also have this problem, what is recommended timeout value in proxy? 900s is not enough? is it worth to increase it?" [SEP] "Hi,    Same here, with 1 hour of timeout.    Regards"

summary: "Adding attachment(s) in a comment results in the file not being attached at cursor position"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In a Jira Core/Software Projects, when adding an attachment(s) in a comment (using the 'text' editor) the file is not being attached at the cursor position, instead it's being attached at the beginning of the comment.  h3. Environment    Jira Server Core (Software & JSD Projects)  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Single Attachment:   # Go to write a comment on a JIRA Core project issue.   # Tag someone at the beginning and write a sentence   # Hit spacebar and insert a file (for example a .txt file)   # Look a the location where it was inserted. (directly after the tag)    Multiple Attachments:   # Upload multiple attachments to the issue.   # Type text into the comment box, using the 'Text' editor.   # Insert attachment (attached at the cursor position, expected results).   # Insert another attachment (attached to the beginning of the issue, not expected).    h3. Expected Results    !image-2019-05-02-16-42-59-488.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    !actual result.PNG|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Hit enter once sentence is completed and add the attachment."""

summary: "Add ability for Project admins to archive Projects"
description: """Customer from PS-40537 requested the feature to add the ability for *Project Admins* to be able to Archive projects.    Currently, only Jira admins are allowed to archive/restore projects."""

summary: "Ability to add Worklog components as columns when viewing results with 'List View'"
description: """h3. Summary    As in Advanced Searching, users are able to search for Worklog components such as WorklogAuthor, Worklogdate and so on. However, these fields are not able to be added as a column in List View. We would like the feature to do this for better reporting of user's worklog per day / issue."""

summary: "JIRA Pie Chart does not display values from Tempo's Account field correctly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  JIRA Pie Chart does not display values from Tempo's Account field correctly    h3. Environment  JIRA 8.1  Tempo 10.2.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add an account in Tempo Account configuration, link it to a project   # Open any issue from the project and set a value for the account field   # Add a pie chart with a filter/project that includes the issue above and select the Account field as """"Statistic Type""""    h3. Expected Results  Accounts are listed correctly (in 7.x)   !image-2019-05-02-12-01-32-034.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results  Accounts are not listed, instead all the issues are grouped under """"none""""   !image-2019-05-02-12-03-28-021.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  This works fine in JIRA 7.x, with latest compatible Tempo version 9.4.2    h3. Workaround  N/A"""
comments: "I escalated this to Tempo before reporting the problem to Atlassian. The Atlassian support team asked me:  {quote}We would also recommend you to reach out to Tempo directly on this as well to investigate on their end, they may be able to provide some insights on this as they know what exactly was fixed or changed on the latest Tempo relese.{quote}  The Tempo support team subsequently told me:  {quote}  Please note, the account fixes mentioned by Atlassian are not related to the Account custom field. Also, the same fix was deployed for Timesheets 9.4.0 which is compatible with JIRA 7.11.2 where the Atlassian gadget was working.{quote}" [SEP] "I reportet the problem to Tempo, the answer was:  {quote}Regarding jira widgets/Pe charts it is entirely out of our control as it will depend on jira/atlassian to fix it.  {quote}  Project Reports are also affected by this.  There are ways around the Problem with the Tempo Gadgets / Reports. Does anybody see something in the logs or have a workaround?" [SEP] "We have the

summary: "Cascading choice custom field values are not imported when Creating issues using the CSV Importer"
description: """h3. Summary    Only the top hierarchical value of a cascading choice custom field is imported when [Creating issues using the CSV importer|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/creating-issues-using-the-csv-importer-939937906.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Log in as a user with Bulk Change and Create Issue global permissions  # Have an issue import with cascading choice custom field values specified in the format described in the above KB (eg, {{""""foo -> bar""""}})  # Follow the steps in the above KB to import the issues     *Expected:* Issues are created with cascading choice custom field values of """"foo"""" and """"bar"""".    *Actual:* Only the first-level value of """"foo"""" is imported. No validation errors or warnings are thrown.    h3. Workaround    Use the Admin import instead, as described in [Importing data from CSV|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-csv-938847533.html].    h3. Notes     [JRASERVER-68758 - Documentation fix for Import Issues from CSV|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-68758] indicates that this is a problem with the documentation, not with the functionality available."""

summary: "Clicking on UPM Update results in ""Cannot install a lower version than the currently installed version."""
description: """To recreate:    Go to Jira > Manage apps.  UPM shows update available but the version listed as newer is 3.0.3 whereas installed version is 4.0.1.  Attempting to update results in error message: """"Cannot install a lower version than the currently installed version.""""    Atlassian Marketplace also shows the lower version at the top of the version list.     _NOTE: This issue is starting to trend as L1 tickets in GSAC._   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    !image-2019-05-01-14-42-44-787.png!    !image-2019-05-01-14-39-49-273.png!"""
comments: "HI [~samann@atlassian.com]    Has this bug been reproduced in 7.x?    Cheers" [SEP] "I think this problem was caused by an error in the marketplace listing of the upm plugin itself.  Specifically the 3.0.3 version (For server, not Data center) was listed to show Jira Server 8.1.0 as being compatible.  This should not be because Jira 8.1.0 default bundles a major version higher in 4.0.1.      Looking at that listing today, it appears to be resolved by the removal of the 3.0.3 version entirely. [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/23915/atlassian-universal-plugin-manager/version-history]     " [SEP] "Hi all,     The issue has been resolved and I'm closing this ticket now.  The UPM now shows the correct versions for Jira 8.x.  [~mwalas], We are not sure if this affects 7.x versions because it is now resolved and we can no longer reproduce it.      Thanks all!    Sarah Amann    Atlassian | Jira Server Support    [Learn new skills with Atlassian Training & Certification|https://www.atlassian.com/uni

summary: "using backslash and At ""\@"" character in string input to comment is truncating the slash"
description: """h3. Summary  When inputing a string value in a comment that contains a backslash """"\"""" character before an """"@"""" character the backslash is removed, this is impactful if attempting to input a file path as a comment where the file contains the @ character in the path exe:  the \ before the @ is truncated    h3. Environment  * Jira Cloud and Jira Server 8.1.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Type the following in a text field outside a code block and post, EXE: Comment on issue      h3. Expected Results  comment displays       h3. Actual Results  comment displays only the """"@"""" as the slash is truncated like the following which does include a backslash but will not render noting https://jira.atlassian.com is currently affected on 8.1.0:   """"\@""""     h3. Notes  Does not affect other Special characters and seems to be limited to the backslash character speciffically """"\"""", tested with:    Text Entered:   !Screen Shot 2019-05-01 at 12.12.43 PM.png|thumbnail!      h3.Workaround  Add the string value to a """"code"""" macro or """"noformat"""" macro and the backslash will not be removed"""

summary: "Ability to customize the Issues in Epic panel"
description: """Provide the ability for Jira administrators to customize the fields displayed in the *Issues in Epic* table on Epics.    In _Advanced Settings_, we can modify the columns to show when viewing sub-task issues in a table by modifying the *jira.table.cols.subtasks* setting.    We should be able to do this with the *Issues in Epic* table as well."""

summary: "Bulk Avatar Functions"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Jira does not offer methods to *bulk* edit avatars e.g. updates, deletions. For example, a method for an admin to set a default avatars for users meeting certain criteria.    h3. Suggested Solution    Allow more options for admins to bulk change avatars rather than relying on individual changes, which are not scalable for large organizations. """
comments: "Seems like a good case for an admin script using ScriptRunner or similar app. Depends on how common the need for this is"

summary: "REST API endpoint to get less detailed result set for issue"
description: """h4. Background  When an issue has many linked issues (in the thousands), getting the issue details through the /rest/api/latest/issue endpoint is very slow compared to XML/HTML export.  The reason for the disparity is the amount of data returned by the REST API that is not included in the XML/HTML export:  h5. XML Export:  {code}<issuelink>  <issuekey id=""""10001"""">TEST-6</issuekey>  </issuelink>  <issuelink>  <issuekey id=""""10000"""">TEST-1</issuekey>  </issuelink>{code}   h5. REST API results:      h4. Feature Request  I would be very helpful if we had an endpoint which only returned very basic linked issue data; similar to what the XML export returns."""

summary: "Custom Fields With Global Contexts Feature For Non Data Center JIRA"
description: """It would really great JIRA is releasing a new feature """"*Custom Fields With Global Contexts*"""". However, it seems only available in Data Center Version. It would be great if this available in JIRA Server (no data center) as well.    https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/custom-fields-with-global-contexts-956713282.html"""
comments: "Seems like a commercial decision was made to make this available for DC only.  Shame really as this wasn't made clear in the presentation at Summit."

summary: "Provide a way to whitelist IPs for REST API basic authentication"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  * Currently Jira doesn't provide a way to restrict or whitelist source IPs (JRASERVER-67452) for remote API interaction at all. One of the challenges of implementing a workaround for this at proxy/network environment level is that the REST API URL patterns for the public API are also often used by requests triggered from the UI. So there is a risk of blocking normal UI based requests while intending to block remote API calls.    h3. Suggested Solution  One way to constrain remote API usage to some extent may be to limit or restrict the use of basic auth for REST API. Provide facility or configuration to restrict basic authentication requests to whitelisted IPs."""

summary: "The expiry date and time of an license is unpredictable"
description: """h3. Problem definition    The time of a license to be expired is unpredictable. Some licenses might even expire a day earlier than the expected date. This is an issue similar to https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66259  h3. Steps to reproduce the issue   # Generate a few evaluation licenses for Service Desk in different time zones.   # Apply the evaluation license in Jira.   # Change the server date to the expiry date of the evaluation license.   # Adjust the server time to estimate the time that the evaluation license gets expired and cause https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JSDSERVER-3217. Note down the estimated time.   # Repeat steps 2 - 4 with another any licenses.    h3. Expected result    The licenses stop working only on next day of the expiry date.  h3. Actual result    The licenses might expire a day earlier or any time on the expiry day."""

summary: "Unicode Characters in JSON Import Display Incorrectly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When importing issues with the External System Import -> JSON importer, if any of the fields in the exported JSON file contain Unicode characters (like in the table below) they will not display property in some environments after successful import:  ||Unicode code point||UTF-8 (dec.)||name||  |U+2018|226 128 152|LEFT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK|  |U+2019|226 128 153|RIGHT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK|  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Copy the JSON attached in the next internal update   # Change the following items:   ** Project key (must be an existing project on JIRA instance)   ** Status (must be valid for issue type)   ** Reporter/Assignee (for ease of permissions to access)   # Import the file using the External System Import -> JSON option    h3. Expected Results    In a few instances, the characters show up correctly in the Summary and Description fields:   MySQL 5.7.19 - JIRA 7.12.3   MSSQL 2017 - JIRA 7.12.3   postgres 9.5 on 8.1   postgres 9.5 on 7.8.1  h3. Actual Results    Most of the time, the characters show up incorrectly. This has been reproduced in:   PostgreSQL 9.3 - JIRA 7.8.0   PostgreSQL 9.3 - JIRA 7.12.3   MSSQL 2012 on 7.8.1   MSSQL 2014 on 7.8.1   MSSQL 2016 on 7.9.2    (!) When looking at the value in the jiraissue table -> summary or description fields, the characters are stored *incorrectly* in the database. It would appear that the problem is either in the JSON importer or perhaps with some of the specific database versions where the behavior is exhibited.  h3. Workaround    Replace unicode characters with non-unicode characters where possible"""

summary: "Users receive duplicate notifications when mentioned in issue comments"
description: """h3. Summary  Jira is sending duplicate notifications when batched notifications are enabled and users are mentioned.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have batched notifications enabled for a project.  # Leave a comment on an issue with a mention directed at my user name.  # Take no other action on this issue for at least 20 minutes.    h3. Expected Results  An email notification about the mention is received, and no other email notifications.    h3. Actual Results  An email notification about the mention is received, and later an additional email notification with batched changes is sent.   Suggested Solution      h3.Workaround  No workaround available."""

I couldn't find any similar issue in the JRASERVER project." description: """When we attach an inline image from email with an unknown attachment format, JIRA did not add the image to its description but showing below error:-      Our mailbox was able to recognize the file type as Image MIME but JIRA failed to recognize it. It would be great it JIRA could recognize the file automatically. Others application such as Zendesk was able to handle it, but in JIRA this is not the case. It would be great if JIRA able to handle these file type too.    """

summary: "When browsing for attachments using operating system which shows no scrollbars by default, the last element should be shown as ""cut off"" so that users will know there are more than four entries."
description: """h3. *Summary*    When using operating system which shows no scrollbars by default (such newer macOS or Windows), as the scrollbar only appears when we hover to the attachments list, the last element should be shown as """"cut off"""" so that users will know there are more than four entries. As the max-height of that menu is defined, this will show no indication that there are more entries at first sight.     To fix this, we should set max-height to a height which ensures that the last element is shown """"cut off"""" as a design affordance for """"there is more below, just scroll to it""""    The example can be seen from the attachments attached to this ticket."""

summary: "Unable to rename a future sprint due to the start date of a sprint must be before the end date"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    * When future sprint has 'START_DATE' and 'END_DATE' configured in AO_60DB71_SPRINT table due to unknown reason, users will not be able to rename the future from the board in the UI.   * No errors will be returned during the re-naming. In the har file, we will see 400 error unable to rename due to """"the start date of a sprint must be before the end date"""".   * If the user refreshes the UI after renaming the sprint, the name will revert back to its original name as the sprint was never renamed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Manually insert the same value of 'START_DATE' and 'END_DATE' for multiple future sprints in the same board. Or insert the same value of 'START_DATE' and 'END_DATE' for one future sprint, then insert a different value of 'START_DATE' and 'END_DATE' for another future sprint in the same board.    # Rename any of the future sprints on the board, you will experience the issue for one of the future sprints where after renaming and refreshing the browser, the name reverts back to its original name.   # If you collect the har file during renaming, you will see the error """"the start date of a sprint must be before the end date"""".     h3. Expected Results    Jira should return an error indicating that the future sprint can't be renamed due to it has 'START_DATE' and 'END_DATE' configured.     h3. Actual Results    User was able to rename the sprint without any error, but the name reverts back to its original name after refreshing the browser.     h3. Workaround    * Remove 'START_DATE' and 'END_DATE' column in the AO_60DB71_SPRINT table for all the future sprints that were affected by the issue.   * Or click on 'start sprint' to edit the sprint name and set a future date. Using this method will start the sprint a even when a future start date is configured. """

summary: "Copying description content of a Jira issue in IE 11 while in the visual editor mode causes scrollbar to jump to the top unexpectedly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you have a very long description in a Jira issue, so long that a scrollbar appears when you edit the issue, in the visual edit mode, copying any section of text with Ctrl + C causes the scrollbar in that edit dialog to jump to the top of that box unexpectedly. Happens only in visual editor mode, not Text mode, and while using the in-line edit feature. Only happens in IE 11. Firefox and Chrome not affected.  h3. Environment   * IE11   * Jira 7.9.2, 7.13.1, 8.1.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit a multiline text field using the wiki-renderer, (e.g. the description) in an issue   # Make sure this description has a lot of content, enough to make sure that a scrollbar appears in the edit mode.   # Make sure you're in """"Visual"""" mode, as this problem does not happen in Text mode   # with your mouse, click and drag to highlight some text at the bottom of this dialog, while still in edit mode   # Press Ctrl+C to copy that text    h3. Expected Results    The view does not change and the scroll bar stays where it is.  h3. Actual Results    The scroll bar jumps to the top  h3. Notes   * IE browser console does not appear to show any error or changes when this happens.   * I was only able to reproduce this with IE11, with Chrome and Firefox I was not able to reproduce it.       h3. Workaround   * Press an arrow key after the scroll bar jumped. This takes you back to the cursor.   * Or use """"Text"""" mode when copying (won't preserve formatting in the same way)     """

Jira 8.2 comes with additional filters on export archive page which allow admins to limit the set of exported issues:   * by providing number: limit only last X archived issues (sorted by archived date)     * filtering with properties: project, issue type, reporter, assignee, archiver and archived date"""

Workaround    None         *Notes:*   * When exporting to HTML, both fields are exported correctly   * In Jira 7.1.x, when exporting to Excel, both fields were exported correctly"""

summary: "Enhance Epic Report"

summary: "CSV Import of existing issues shouldn't require the summary field."
description: """Currently, when importing issues from a CSV file, using the """"External System Import"""", to UPDATE existing issues, Jira requires the """"Summary"""" field.    This is required even if you're not changing the summary of the issue.    The only required field should be the issue key itself which is used to find the issue you're trying to update."""
comments: "Agree. In addition, allowing us to update issues using just the issue key will provide us with the ability to update the Summary field using External System Import when needed." [SEP] "Sooooo, when performing updates to existing issues, I discovered that the External System Import doesn't _really_ require the Summary field. It merely requires something to be _mapped_ to the Summary field.    Just create an empty column in your CSV file, call it Summary, map it to the Summary field, and the External System Import process will accept it and update your issues accordingly based purely on the issue key."

summary: "Add comment visibility option to JSON importer"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently there is no way to import comments into Jira with the visibility set to Private in *Jira Service Desk*, or set to a specific Role or Group in *Jira Software*.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add an option in the JSON importer to set the comment visibility, as it is currently possible in the Jira comment endpoints for *Jira Service Desk* and *Jira Software*."""

Is there anything I can do to help? I'll learn Java to get this resolved because right now this one little overlooked step is posing a huge risk to our migration to Jira." summary: "Add the possibility to configure Text style mail notifications to filter out any markup and scripting "
description: """Currently, if a field has been saved with HTML or wiki markup, and the field is using a Dafault Text renderer, the notifications are being sent using the value of the field as-is, including all code, tags and scripting. This can make notifications unreadable.    This behavior is also present when viewing the History tab in the issue view.    The suggestion is to:  # Allow a better handling of the issue history to show only text, without markup.  # For Jira to send notifications cleaning up all markup/html/scripting detected so the code will be readable."""

summary: "Incorrect results for custom field quick search "
description: """h3. Summary  Custom field page had a new look and feel introduced in [Jira 7.12|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/jira-core-7-12-x-release-notes-955179462.html#JiraCore7.12.xreleasenotes-new_cfs]. Also added was a quick search box.      However, using the quick search may return results that are not related to the search string as the search incorporates results from screen name as well. In other words, the search is not limited to custom field name.     h3. Environment  This is reproducible on a vanilla instance    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a screen. For example 'CustomizedUniqueScreen'  # Create 2 custom fields. For example 'CustomField' and 'NothingRelatedHere'  # Associate both the custom fields to the new screen  # Go to Jira Administration > Issues > Custom field   # Search with the word {{Custom}} or {{custom}} or partial word {{cus}}  # Verify the results    h3. Expected Results  Only custom fields with the name that has the string _{{Custom}}_ or _{{custom}}_ or partial word _{{cus}}_ should be returned    h3. Actual Results  Custom fields 'CustomField' and 'NothingRelatedHere' are returned (based on the above example). This is due to the 'NothingRelatedHere' field was associated to a screen with the name that contain the search string    !customfieldsearch.png|thumbnail!    h3.Workaround  Ensure screen names are unique  """
comments: "We are adding 3 filters:   * project   * custom field type   * screens    Also the search (field) functionality is reduced to search only by custom field names, and not more to search by projects, custom field types and screens, to avoid the mix problems." [SEP] "I would like to add that having the custom field search on the field Description would be VERY beneficial as I will enter notes into the description field about fields I am contemplating deleting or why it was created/archived.  One of the reasons I have not upgraded from 7.9 to  8.1.0 is because I would lose the ability to search by Description if I did."

summary: "Invalid error output on API endpoint"
description: """h3.
