Steps to Reproduce   # [POST /rest/api/2/filter/{id}/permission | https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.1.0/#api/2/filter-addSharePermission]   # using invalid option for data package    h3. Expected Results  The error output will show Valid values (Noting case sensativity): project, group, projectRole, global, authenticated    h3. Actual Results  The API return with the following error:      h3. Notes  This gives improper values as valid options noting invalid case sensitivity as well: """"Should be one of [PROJECT, GROUP, PROJECT_ROLE, GLOBAL, AUTHENTICATED, USER]""""    h3. Workaround  Use the Valid values (Noting case sensitivity): project, group, projectRole, global, authenticated"""
comments: "Is this issue fixed? Also the documentation for this API needs to be updated accordingly.     https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.12.0/#api/2/filter-addSharePermission"

summary: "Correct REST API Documentation for ReindexIssues"
description: """h3. Summary    The REST API document ([https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.1/#api/2/reindex-reindexIssues]) mentions that the POST /rest/api/2/reindex/issue endpoint can take JQL as a parameter  {quote}Use either explicitly specified issue IDs or a JQL query to select issues to reindex.  {quote}  However, the endpoint does not explicitly take JQL as a parameter and does not work as described in the document.  h3. Analysis    If we look at the code for the rest endpoint, the logic is simply not there for JQL. It uses [*issueFinder|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/7.1.4/com/atlassian/jira/rest/v2/issue/IssueFinder.html] .getIssueObject(issueId))*, which only takes issue keys or id's:    h3. Suggested Implementation    Correct the documentation or add some details on how to make this work with JQL as outlined in the guide. Otherwise, add the logic to make this work with JQL using the traditional Jira syntax of JQL, E.G.   POST rest/api/2/reindex/issue?jql=project=PROJECTKEY"""

summary: "Do not create attachment directory during deleting Issue"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  During deleting Issue Jira calls _*DefaultFileSystemAttachmentStore#deleteAttachmentContainerForIssue()*_ method. This method under the hood gets directory from _*FileSystemAttachmentDirectoryAccessor#getAttachmentDirectory()*_ and if directory exists this method removes this directory.    If the directory for issue does not exist, the method _*FileSystemAttachmentDirectoryAccessor#getAttachmentDirectory()*_ creates this directory.   That leads to the following course of actions:  * Jira creates a directory   * and after that immediately removes it.     This situation can cause the performance issue if the customer makes a lot of deleting operations. The I/O operations can be a bottleneck for Jira in that case.    h3. Suggested Solution  Do not create attachment directory during deleting Issue    h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "While you've got the hood up, deleting an attachment does not delete the thumbnail. It should do so that PII is properly removed"

summary: "Improve CSV Import Validator to check fields in 'Create Issue' screen"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When creating issues from CSV (*Issues > Create issues from CSV*), should the field is not present in the 'Create Issue' screen, the CSV import will be completed without the missing fields' value in the created issues. Though seems to be expected, it is however very misleading as the validator will not detect this as a problem. Also, the fields can be mapped prior to executing the import process, further making the user think all the mapped fields will be imported over.    h3. Suggested Solution  Have the validator detect if any of the mapped fields are not included in the 'Create Issues' screen.    Alternatively, prevent or warn users when trying to map fields that are not included in the 'Create Issue' screen.    h3. Workaround   None  """

summary: "Ability to to limit the search only to custom field name in custom fields management view "

The problem is if we search for something like """"OS"""", instead of the custom field named """"OS"""" it gives us 7 pages worth of results. There's no way for us to know what page the actual """"OS"""" field is on.    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement the ability to limit the search to custom field name only, and not description. Otherwise, this is literally usable for many generic custom field names.      """

summary: "Update server.xml automatically between upgrades to contain relaxedPathChars"
description: """As described in our documentation at [Changing server.xml to handle requests with special characters|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/changing-server-xml-to-handle-requests-with-special-characters-958453799.html], the {{server.xml}} has been modified to handle the Tomcat upgrade to 8.5.32.    Also listed in the documentation is that brand new installs 7.12.3 and on will contain the characters already listed in the file but upgrades from a version before 7.12.3 will require the characters be added in manually to the {{server.xml}} file.     This can add manual work in during an upgrade and while it can be easily fixed, it would be good if the upgrade could manually handle modifying the {{server.xml}} to contain the characters in an uncommented out connector."""

summary: "Project Key change triggered though REST API doesn't trigger Project Reindex like UI does"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you initiate a project key change through REST API _+PUT+_ /rest/api/2/project/\{projectIdOrKey} , the operations does not trigger a project reindex/background reindex. The same operation done through the UI triggers a project reindex.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to a project and edit the key through the UI ([http://HOST/secure/project/EditProject!default.jspa?pid=XXXXX|http://host/secure/project/EditProject!default.jspa?pid=XXXXX] )   # Notice a project reindex is triggered   # Now update the key again, but using REST API _+PUT+_ /rest/api/2/project/\{projectIdOrKey}   # Notice that the project reindex is not triggered    h3. Expected Results    The UI and REST API behavior should be standardized, which means that the REST API should trigger a reindex or the UI behavior should be changed.   h3. Notes    Changing the project key on the UI generates a taskId for the project reindex while the REST API does not even try to create a task:    !image-2019-04-23-09-00-18-918.png|width=680,height=227!  h3. Workaround    Trigger a manual project reindex if editing the project key via REST API"""

summary: "Add a system field for ""last updated by"" and set for each issue"
description: """Currently Jira captures the last time an issue was updated.  And while Jira 8.x introduces JQL to search based on who last updated an issue, it would be helpful to have a real system field """"lastUpdatedBy"""" that gets set to the user name of the user who last updated an issue.  This field could be displayed along with other """"People"""" fields.  It would also be available in JQL as in """"lastUpdatedBy = currentUser()"""" or similar.    This is preferable to each customer implementing their own custom field and updating via a custom listener or post-functions."""

summary: "issueManager.getIssueObject(defectId) provides inconsistent status results "
description: """h3. Summary  Inconsistent issue data for bug, Jira 8 and 8.0.2 - we got while changed the bug status from TO - In Progress    h3. Environment  * Jira 8.0.x    h3. Steps to Reproduce   - created the bug (issue) with status TO-DO.   - created the 3 test cases(issue) in test case.   - mapped the same bug to all above 3 test case.   - open the same bug and modified the TO-DO status into In Progress,then i got an inconstant data from the jira, like instead of In Progress status, I got TO-DO status.   - for creating and modifying the issue i called the jira - IssueEvent class.   - for retrieving the issue from the jira i used web-hook class below, after so much investigation and debug i fond this below method is giving inconsistent data.  Issue issue = issueManager.getIssueObject(defectId);   - once we modify the bug status from TO-DO to In Progress,  Issue issue = issueManager.getIssueObject(defectId);    h3. Expected Results    Once the status is modified from TODO to In Progress the webhook should return In Progress    h3. Actual Results  The webhook returned TODO when the status was In Progress.     h3. Notes    Raised by customer:    [https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATLASSDK-166]    Also, raised in community by customer:  [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/Inconsistent-issue-data-for-bug-Jira-8-Issue-issue-issueManager/qaq-p/1035407#M331528]    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

This seems more intuitive to me than creating a [Custom Event|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/adding-a-custom-event-938847495.html] named """"Issue Created Unassigned"""" and adding it to all my workflow creation transitions.     """

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using the REST API rest/api/2.0/attachments allows you to upload attachments with invalid characters in the name.    When you go to access those images, Jira presents the error    !Screen Shot 2019-04-22 at 12.06.20 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an attachment with any invalid characters in its filename.   # Use the REST call rest/api/2.0/attachments and upload the attachment with the bad filename.   # Attempt to load the attachment.     h3. Expected Results    The REST call should fail if there are any invalid characters in the filename.  h3. summary: "Importing multiple FogBugz projects does not create Issue Links correctly"
description: """h3. Summary    Jira Importer Plugin allows multiple projects to be imported from FogBugz (On Demand) . The importer however, does not import the links correctly when multiple FogBugz projects are mapped to a Single Jira projects.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create multiple projects in FogBugz (On Demand)   # Import the projects from Jira    h3. Expected Results    All projects' issues and their links are imported successfully.  h3. Actual Results    All issues are imported successfully. However, the links are only created for the last project that was imported.  h3. Notes    The FogBugz project import [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-fogbugz-for-your-server-938847577.html] mentions that the projects are imported on a 'Per-project basis'. However, this does not clearly explain why the links are not created as the import logs show that all the issues are created first and the links are only imported after that.  h3. Workaround    Re-import the projects again."""

summary: "Deleting 'causes' on Issue Linking administration defaults Created Issue link to blank."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a linked issue from More -> Create linked issue, Jira Service Desk always defaults the Link Type option to causes, which is taken from the Problem/Incident link type created by JSD from Issue Linking administration page.    This can be seen in the HAR file where the request explicitly looks for 'causes':      If 'causes' does not exist in the Issue Linking administration page (deleted or modified by the user), Jira defaults to show a blank option.     The behavior of first defaulting to 'causes' and subsequently defaulting to blank if 'causes' cannot be found is not consistent with the option that appears on other issue linking methods (Eg: Jira issue creation, where it defaults to the first item on the Issue Linking administration page).     h3. Environment  Jira 8.1.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to any Jira Service Desk issue and click on More -> Create linked issue.  # Observe that the Created Issue option defaults to 'causes'.  # Navigate to Issue Linking administration page and delete 'causes' (Problem/Incident)   # Navigate back any Jira Service Desk issue and click on More -> Create linked issue.  # Observe that the Created Issue option defaults to blank.    h3. Expected Results  We expect Jira to default to the first item on the list instead of blank.    h3. Actual Results  Jira defaults to blank.    h3. Workaround  A workaround is to create a new link type and ensure 'causes' is Outward Description or Inward Description. This would default the option to 'causes'. However, no workaround exists yet to default to the first option on the list to make it consistent with other issue linking method in Jira.  """

summary: "SortableCustomField executes too many db queries"
description: """h3. Description    There's a fallback implementation of ordering issues by custom field which can't be sorted using Lucene.    This will cause {{n!}} executions of {{CustomField.getValue()}} which causes {{n!}} db queries for custom fields which are not loaded using {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} (EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister has some extra caching for CFs which store their values in {{customfieldvalues}} table)  h3. Reproduction scenario   # Create custom field which is not stored in {{customfieldvalue}} table.   (I was using lexorank CF with disabled indexing)   # Create 25 issues   # Execute jql """"{{ORDER BY <your custom field>""""}}   # Measure, how many times {{ImmutableCustomField#getValue}} was called   ER: 25 (one {{getValue()}} for each issue)   AR: 125 (much more {{getValue()}} than needed)    h3. Proposed solution    Reuse caching of custom field values inside {{IssueImpl}} and {{DocumentIssue}}"""

summary: "Tool to check for existing JIRA Service Desk data prior to JIRA upgrade"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The often case, where users would have used JIRA Service Desk (JSD) in the instance but then decided to uninstall it. This will cause the JSD data to remain in the instance. At a later time, after the instance have been upgraded across major versions, the users may decide to use JSD again, without realizing they would run into some problems due to the old JSD data that needs to be upgraded according to the upgrade path ([Problem described here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/after-upgrading-to-jira-service-desk-3-2-x-or-greater-service-desk-project-s-navigation-bar-shows-unable-to-render-element-due-to-an-error-845943102.html]).    h3. Suggested Solution  Have a tool that will check the JIRA instance for any JSD data, even if JSD is currently inactive/not installed in the instance. The tool should notify the users that there are some JSD data in the JIRA instance and should prompt them to either upgrade it or remove it. Perhaps the tool can also be bundled with the installers for upgrades or included as part of the upgrade pre-requisite in the documentation.    h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "Hi Irfan,    Thanks for logging this issue!    Just a slight precision: when the user agrees to completely remove the tool and its data, *all* the remnant tables of the tool in question should be removed.    Currently it is not the case when you remove the application under _Applications > Uninstall_ . which has actually caused the issue with JSD incompatibility when we proceeded with further Jira Upgrades    Thanks,    Kind Regards,    Oksana [Valiantys]"

summary: "Officially provide support for internal and external base url for JIRA"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    There is currently no official way to configure JIRA with two url and it is very common for organizations to have one internal and one external url.     It is currently only possible to configure one baseurl and this is mentioned in our documentation :    # https://confluence.atlassian.com/upm/diagnosing-base-url-configuration-problems-273875734.html  # https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-jira-application-options-938847824.html    bq. The base URL of this Jira installation. You can only configure Jira to respond to a single URL and this setting must match the URL that your users request for accessing your Jira instance. You cannot (for example) have a different hostname or URL for internal and external users.    h3. Suggested Solution    Provide the functionality and official documentation on how to configure JIRA with internal and external or more than one url.     h3. Notes    While one can configure multiple Tomcat connectors with different URLs, due to the nature of our current OAuth implementation signing requests with the base URL setting ( see https://docs.atlassian.com/DAC/javadoc/applinks/3.6.1/reference/com/atlassian/applinks/core/auth/oauth/RequestUtil.html ), using a different URL other than the base url will break integrations relying on OAuth for authentication    """

summary: "DVCS does not pick up an issue key from commits released through vRealize"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  DVCS extracts an issue key from a commit message using a hardcoded advanced Java regular expression:    {code:java}private static final String SEPARATOR = """"[\\s\\p{Punct}]"""";  private static final String KEY_PREFIX_REGEX = """"(?:(?<="""" + SEPARATOR + """")|^)""""; //zero-width positive lookbehind  private static final String KEY_BODY_REGEX = """"(\\p{Lu}[\\p{Lu}\\p{Digit}_]+-\\p{Digit}+)"""";  private static final String KEY_POSTFIX_REGEX = """"(?:(?="""" + SEPARATOR + """")|$)"""";  //zero-width positive lookahead  private static final String ISSUE_KEY_REGEX = KEY_PREFIX_REGEX + KEY_BODY_REGEX + KEY_POSTFIX_REGEX;{code}    However, this is not working in case a release management system uses a special template formatting, which is the case with vRealize Suite Lifecycle Manager (part of vmWare suite) that creates commits like that:        A solution to this problem could be to make a regex pattern for the issue key extraction configurable (this is how it is done [in Bitbucket|https://confluence.atlassian.com/stashkb/integrating-with-custom-jira-issue-key-313460921.html?_ga=2.171235616.1618673674.1555320550-861429217.1533299002]).    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Creating linked issue from one project in another project does not apply default Issue security level"
description: """While in Project A (no security level), creating a linked issue that exists in Project B (issue security applied) does not apply Project B's default issue security level.     *Expected Result:* New linked issue inherits projects B's issue security level.    *Actual Result:* New linked issue for project B has no issue security applied.    *Steps to Reproduce:*    **You will require two Jira projects. Project A has no issue security. Project B has issue security with default level configured.    1) Create an issue that belongs to Project A   2) On that issue click on """"More""""   3) Click on Create Linked Issue   4) On the Create Linked Issue dialog, select Project B   4.1) Jira project B is using an Issue Security Level Scheme with a Default level   5) On the Create Linked Issue dialog, select the issue type Task   6) Fill in all the required fields, and then click on Create   7) The resulting created issues in project B, let's say B-1, does not have any Security Level   8) Hit the Create button on the top Nav Bar   9) On the Create dialog select project B and issue type Task   10) Fill in all the required fields, and then click on Create   11) The resulting created issue in project B, B-1, does have a Security Level, which is the Default Level of the Issue Security Scheme configured in project B    *Workaround:* None known at this time."""

summary: "Drag and drop behavior on issue navigator gets in the way of selecting content to copy"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The Issue Navigator allows re-ordering of items in search results temporarily (mostly for visualization apparently - since this doesn't actually update the issues, re-prioritise or re-rank them in any way in the backend). One draw-back with this feature, is that it gets in the way of simple copy and paste of content (e.g. copying (HTML) rows of issues that can be embedded into an email, document, blog post etc. - very useful) from the issue navigator. Select and copy of rows becomes impossible except when selecting from the very top of the table because the cursor switches to the draggable-mode when you try to select any rows except from the top row.    h3. Suggested Solution  The ability to select and copy content from the issue navigator may actually be of more practical advantage than the ability to drag things around.  * Improve the issue navigator styling so that it's still possible to select and copy any rows from the issue navigator table.  * Provide a way to disable the dragable feature in favor of ability to select and copy, if we can't have both at the same time.     h3. Workaround  * If you switch the issue navigator to printable view, there is no drag and drop feature in that layout, and copying and pasting text is possible from any part of the table.  ** Ofcourse this adds one additional step that is hard to get all users to be familiar with or remember just for a simple 'copy text' support that should always be available consistently on a web interface.  * It is possible to disable the dragging of subtask through the use of a dark feature.    *# Login to Jira as an administrator   *# Navigate to $JIRAbaseURL/secure/SiteDarkFeatures!default.jspa   *# add the value of  *{color:#333333}com.atlassian.jira.issuetable.draggable.disabled{color}* to the enable dark feature and click Add  However this will prevent the dragging of subtasks both on the issue details page and through the issue navigator.  Note:  In the issue details view you will still see the up and down arrows that can be used to rearrange the order of these subtasks.    h3. Notes  This isn't a trivial requirement if we consider that versatility of interacting with content is supposed to be a key advantage of web interfaces. Having select and copy disabled or impaired unintentionally like this, is a regression of basic web browsing functionality.  * Taking screenshots of parts of the issue navigator as an alternative to select and copy is far less useful, as it doesn't have the advantage of clickable links that you get when you select and copy the content instead."""

summary: "Search with multiple nested filters is slower than pure JQL"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Filter with multiple nested filters within it  # Perform the search  # Now, break the filters into their JQL directly  # Perform the same search.     h3. Expected Results    The queries take the same time to provide the result.    h3. Actual Results    The JQL using multiple nested filters is way slower.    h3. Workaround    Currently, no work arounds other than avoid using nested filters."""
comments: "As far as I know, I'm the original reporter for this issue and would like to add a few comments that don't seem to be here from my conversation with Benito.    Nested filters are pretty powerful and allow for a lot of flexibility and extendability.  The ability to have multiple nested filters depend on layers of other filters means you can change a common filter in one place, instead of having to manually change the JQL in 10's or 100's of filters.  Nested filters are quite logical from this point of view.  Reducing the amount of work that needs to be done to maintain the view of the data that you need.    It seems what exists currently is a pretty straight forward and simple implementation of handling nested filters.  While simple, it is also the most inefficient implementation possible, both from the client and server side.    A much better implementation is to first detect nested filters in a JQL, unroll the nested filters to a single JQL, then execute that single JQL.  This is more efficient for all

summary: "Atlassian Jira Diagnostics bundled plugin high memory usage"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    {{DailyAlertAnalyticsJob}} is a part of Atlassian Diagnostics integration plugin.   Its responsibility is to produce daily analytics summary on all alerts fired with Atlassian diagnostics framework.    Its memory usage can get quite heavy with lots of alerts fired daily. Memory will be accumulated in {{Caesium-x-y}} thread  h3. Expected Results    {{DailyAlertAnalyticsJob}} efficiently handles its memory footprint by properly streaming alerts instead of loading all into memory.  h3. Actual Results    {{DailyAlertAnalyticsJob}} loads all alerts from a given day into memory causing high pressure on GC, eventually leading to OOMe on big instances.  h3. Workaround    *Option 1* - *recommended*    Alerting thresholds are set by several application properties.   Setting them high enough to produce less alerts per day is a viable workaround for the issue.    Currently implemented alerting thresholds:  ||Property||Default value||Explanation||  |jira.diagnostics.thresholds.slow-query-millis|400 ms|Alert for slow JQL query|  |jira.diagnostics.thresholds.number-of-results|1000|Number of returned issues from a single JQL query|  |jira.diagnostics.thresholds.query-complexity|1000|Lucene query complexity - number of claues the query is constructed of|    Setting these values high can reduce the number of alerts triggered daily.   * These values can be modified either from the $JIRA_HOME/jira-config.properties. For example,       * Or they can be modified using the following JVM parameters,    (!) Make sure there is no space between the equal sign in both methods.    *Option 2*  - *recommended*    Retention period for alerts is 30 days by default. You can decrease database size by changing it to e.g. 1 day.     In order to do that you can use the Jira property *com.atlassian.jira.health.diagnostics.alerts.retention-period-days.* To do this, open the jira-config.properties and add/edit the following metric:    - {{com.atlassian.jira.health.diagnostics.alerts.retention-period-days}}: <number of days to retain Diagnostics Alerts> (It is 30 by default)   * For more information on how to configure the file, see [jira-config.properties file|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-edit-the-jira-config-properties-file-317194938.html].     *Option 3*    You can temporary disable the plugin via UPM. However we *do not recommend* disabling it as problems with Diagnostics could be only a symptom of underlying root cause (it might be harder to track without Diagnostics plugin)."""
comments: "In our experience, the only workaround that works is #3. The others had no effect. In fact, records are not even being purged from that table even after setting retention to 1 day. This is very concerning because the workaround steps posted originally were wrong. We worked with Premiere Support to figure out how to properly update those parameters, and they ended up having to update the steps in the docs after that. Were the workarounds steps even tested or reviewed prior to posting?    After looking at the query our DBA suggested we add an index for Created Date to speed up the execution time of the query. Was this ever tested with millions of records in that table? If so, this would definitely have been discovered. This is not the first time we would be creating an index on-the-fly to resolve a gap in the database architecture.    All of this makes me very concerned for how this tool was developed and rolled out to enterprise customers in the Enterprise Release. Does not appear this tool in its entirety is

summary: "Comment content goes missing when Edited on ""Visual Mode"" using Safari."

It would be the same if it is in Text Mode. The problem here is that if the last operation was in Visual mode, editing an existing comment in Safari would not be shown any content at all. It will only show when it is in Text mode after that.    h3. Steps to Replicate.  # Access a ticket from Business or Software Project.  # Add a comment to the ticket in Visual mode.  # Edit the same comment.    h3. Expected Result.  - Comment content will be shown.    h3. Actual result.  - The Comment content goes missing    h3. Workaround  - Before clicking """"Cancel"""" when editing the comment, make sure it is on """"Text Mode"""". After that, Edit the comment again and now the content would not go missing when Visual mode is selected."""

summary: "Using JIRA Remote Copy Plug-in results in heap exhaustion and full GC"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using [JIRA Remote Copy Plug-in|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/678725/jira-to-jira-issue-copy] results in heap exhaustion and full GC when using Jira version 8.1.0 and plugin version 6.0.0.    This does NOT occur in Jira v7.6.7 with plugin version 5.1.0.  h3. Environment   * JAC   * JAC stg     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to an issue.   # Select More in menu.   # Select Remote Copy   # Select a project.   # Continue through the wizard to copy an issue, press next.   # Wait 20-30 seconds while the JVM exhausts heap space and a full GC will be triggered. The copy fails and returns to a """"copy issue"""" screen with an alert message:       # Review Jira GC logs to confirm. I'm including screenshots of GC activity.    h3. Expected Results    Plugin copies issue from one Jira service to another Jira service.  h3. Actual Results    Issues do not appear to be copied. Heap exhausted triggering full GC.    See attachments.     !JAC Prod 10s GC.png|thumbnail!   !JAC stg repro.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior other than disabling the plugin."""
comments: "Does this happen in Jira 7.x?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2] Good question.    I attempted to repro on Jira v7.6.7 with plugin v5.1.0. Works as expected. No increased memory usage. Jira version 8.1.0 and plugin version 6.0.0 continue to exhibit this behavior. I'll update the issue above with this additional information.    Thanks!"

summary: "JQL function to display all issues linked by the linked issue's resolution"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As per [JRASERVER-25640|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-25640], there is now a JQL to search issues based on the issue link type. However, it is not possible to further refine the search by filtering the results based on the linked issues' resolution.    *Useful scenario:*  * A filter gadget is used to display all the issue the user can work on.   * The filter is set to display all the issues that do not have linked issues with the type """"block"""", as this is a blocker to the user.   * Should the """"block"""" linked issue has been resolved, the parent issue should be displayed in the filter gadget so that the user can work on it.    h3. Suggested Solution  Introduce a JQL that can search based on the linked issue's resolution.    h3. Workaround   Use third-party plugins. Refer to this Community discussion: [How to compare the status of linked issues ?|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/How-to-compare-the-status-of-linked-issues/qaq-p/825619]"""
comments: "It was 8 years between when JRASERVER-25640 was created and when it got implemented. It is most unfortunate that the resolution of JRASERVER-25640 didn't go as far as allowing filtering of the status of linked issues, as is done in JQL and Scriptrunner.    It is astonishing that providing a list of issues that a user can work on is +*not*+ a standard feature of Jira and the expectation is to purchase additional plugins to achieve this basic functionality. Hopefully, it will not be another 8 years before this feature is added!"

summary: "Web resource cache doesn't recover properly when it's temporary files are deleted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira uses files to cache web resources compiled in runtime (less->css, etc).  These files are stored in {{$JIRA_HOME/tmp/webresources}}.    When one of these files is deleted/disappears during application uptime, cache file is not being restored and web resource manager triggers compilation for each request to that resource.    That faulty situation results in a very high CPU load/usage.    h3. Environment    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Remove one/many files from {{$JIRA_HOME/tmp/webresources}}   # Observe load average and CPU usage    h3. Expected Results    - missing cache file is properly detected,  - recompilation happens only once,  - cache file is being restored  - no significant spike on load average / CPU usage is observed (apart from that single compilation event)    h3. Actual Results  - resource files are recompiled for each request  - significant load average / CPU usage is observed    h3. Workaround    Restarting an instance is currently the only known workaround for the issue"""
comments: "Take a look at Nic Brough's reply in https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/can-we-able-to-remove-the-opt-atlassian-jira-temp-files-or-not/qaq-p/801527    Just because a file is in a directory named tmp or temp doesn't mean you can delete it while the app is running. The name of the directory might be better as ""cache"" or ""will_be_regenerated_at_startup"". Just don't mess with the app's files behind its back." [SEP] "This is tremendously impactful to our Jira instance, taking the service down to restart each time this happens, especially on an 'enterprise release' of Jira is unacceptable.   Please expedite this." [SEP] "Hi [~vendor-atlassian1],    I think the workaround section can be extended by another option: cause any plugin system event to be triggered, for example by disabling/enabling/installing/uninstalling any plugin. The cache is cleared on plugin system events so the cache should recover." [SEP] "Wait, the workaround is easy. Just don't delete files from a running Jira instance!" [

summary: "BeeHive DatabaseClusterLock leaves left-over rows in 'clusterlockstatus' table."
description: """h3. Summary   Jira uses Beehive library to manage locks on DC, lock is held internally in JVM and also implemented through external table {{clusterlockstatus}} which is shared between nodes.   Lock is allocated through {{lock()}}/{{unlock()}} methods, which is expected semantics and stardard API.      Problem:    *unlock()* implementation removes the lock but keeps the record {{clusterlockstatus}} table. It's fine in case of the lock with the same name, but there is a problem in case of locks with unique names.  There is no method to clean the old locks, the 'locked_by_node' for that row will be empty, but they pile up in large numbers.    h3. *Steps to Reproduce*  * Run Jira DC  * Create the unique lock  * Lock the lock  * Unlock the lock    h3. Expected Results   Once the operation is done, the entry in the 'clusterlockstatus' should be removed (or could be cleaned after some time).      h3. Actual Results   'lock_name' entries like """"com.atlassian.jira.dashboard.JiraDashboardStateStoreManager.dashboard-69114"""" pile up in the 'clusterlockstatus' table.     See JRASERVER-69113, JRASERVER-68477 for some examples.     h3. Workaround    If necessary, delete the unnecessary 'lock_name' entries manually."""
comments: "This is happening to us. What is the impact of this problem?    Thank you"

summary: "JiraDashboardStateStoreManager leave left-over rows in 'clusterlockstatus' table."
description: """*Summary*  A problem similar to JRASERVER-68477.   Method {{JiraDashboardStateStoreManager}} after updating/deleting Dashboards on Jira Data Center, allocation the entry in the 'clusterlockstatus' table remains left behind. The 'locked_by_node' for that row will be empty, but they pile up in large numbers.      h2. *Steps to Reproduce*  * Run Jira DC  *  Update/Delete Dashboard    *Expected Results*   Once the operation is done, the entry in the 'clusterlockstatus' should be removed.     *Actual Results*   'lock_name' entries like """"com.atlassian.jira.dashboard.JiraDashboardStateStoreManager.dashboard-69114"""" pile up in the 'clusterlockstatus' table.    One customer had over 7000 of them.    *Workaround*    If necessary, delete the unnecessary 'lock_name' entries manually."""
comments: "Yup, 6804 of these rows for us"

description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Replying JIRA Notification To Different Mailbox, JIRA Will Create Comment In Original Ticket    h3. Environment  Tested on JIRA 7.6.2, JIRA 7.3.9, this is impacting JIRA Service Desk Mail Channel as well.    h3. Actual Results  JIRA added 2 comments in project 1 regardless of which mailbox is receiving the email reply.    h3. Workaround  -  """

summary: "Upgrade Tomcat to 8.5.38 to fix CVE-2019-0199"
description: """h3. Denial of service in Apache Tomcat *CVE-2019-0199*    A vulnerability was found in Apache Tomcat version from 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.14 inclusive and 8.5.0 to 8.5.37 inclusive. The HTTP/2 implementation accepted streams with excessive numbers of SETTINGS frames and also permitted clients to keep streams open without reading/writing request/response data. By keeping streams open for requests that utilised the Servlet API's blocking I/O, clients were able to cause server-side threads to block eventually leading to thread exhaustion and a DoS.       *Workaround*    Upgrading to Tomcat 9.0.16 will fix the issue.  h4. Fixed Versions   * >= 8.5.38     * >= 9.0.16"""

summary: "Ability to remove option in user's profile which shares their filters by default with all logged in users."
description: """h3. Summary    Users by default have the option to choose the default *Sharing* setting for when they create new filters and dashboards, which can be either shared with all other users ( *Public* ) or restricted in their User Profile.    Use of this option means that any external clients in the Jira instance can see the names of filters etc which may well be completely irrelevant to them or even business-confidential.    This is because for e.g. any filter shared with 'any logged in user', client users can see the name of the filter. If this name includes e.g. other client names then this is potentially business-damaging.    Due to this, it is important for Jira Admin to have the ability to remove that option from user's profile to avoid any security breach.     """

summary: "Language packs contain duplicate keys and strings"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The language pack contains duplicate keys. They're not in the translation database as duplicates so this indicates some kind of minor issue with the language pack build process.         I've found this issue in 7.13.0 as well as the latest 8.0 20190201000500 release.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Download the distribution and grab the language pack Jar file (e.g., jira-core-language-pack-es_ES-7.13.0.v20181107105324.jar)   # Unzip   # Check the contents of com/atlassian/streams/jira/i18n_es_ES.properties - the file contains many duplicates, as do several other files (though not all strings are duplicates).    h3. Expected Results    File should not contain duplicate keys  h3. Notes    I don't think this is user-impacting though since some customers do modify their langage packs having duplicate keys makes this task harder and somewhat confusing.  h3. Workaround     """

summary: "Servlet response redirect to mailto: URI schema causes NPE"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A servlet in our product Vivid Trace for Jira is used to redirect the browser agent to various URLs. In the case where the browser is to receive a URI using the {{mailto:}} schema, Jira's code at {{RedirectInterceptingResponse.isPathInCurrentContext()}} causes a {{NullPointerException}}. The NPE arises because the code erroneously assumes a non-null value at an intermediate method invocation in both method calling chains, described below.    It would be great if the fix makes it into all future Jira releases. (Kindly see suggested fix below).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Code sample to reproduce the bug:  {code:java}  public class RedirectionServlet extends HttpServlet {        @Override      protected void doGet(              final HttpServletRequest request,                           final HttpServletResponse response      ) throws ServletException, IOException {          response.sendRedirect(""""mailto:blinky-bill@outback.au"""");      }    }  {code}    h3. Expected Results  Browser agent receives the redirect to the mailto: URI    h3. Actual Results  Throws an NPE      h3. Notes  * Bug not present in at least Jira version 6.3.13  * Bug is expressed on at least Jira versions 7.13.1, 8.0.2    h3. Suggested fix  Current Jira code that causes the NPE:      Suggested fix:      h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior, however, a suggested fix is in place."""

description: """h3. Summary    When a user creates an issue and changes the reporter (while on issue create screen) to anyone else other than the logged user, the notification email says the reporter created it.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log as user *A* and click on *""""Create""""* button changing the """"*Reporter*"""" field to anyone else before submitting the issue creation.   # Check the *Reporter* mailbox, the notification says the *Reporter* created the issue and not the user *A*.    h3. summary: "Required fields are not required when editing the issue by inline edit."
description: """h3. Summary  Required fields are not required when editing the issue by inline edit. When creating an issue or editing an issue, if you have required fields, you cannot edit/update fields (not required) without putting information in the required field if it has no information.    Now, if you edit these same field through inline edit, the required fields are not triggered and you're able to edit other fields even having the required fields blank.    h3. Environment  * 7.13.x    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Make a field required in a field configuration scheme for any issue type for an existing project with existing issues  # Now, try to edit that issue by clicking on the edit button. If you try to edit one field that is not required, it won't let you unless you put some information on the newly required field.  # If you try the same edit by inline edit, it doesn't require to fill the required field.     h3.Workaround  - [Disable inline edit for a field|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-disable-inline-edit-for-assignee-field-604537586.html] if you want to prevent that from happening."""

summary: "Kanban Release link should check if the user is capable of releasing issues in the current view"
description: """Right now, the Release link in a Kanban board only checks if the user has project administrator permissions on all the projects in the board, before enabling itself.    However, users should still be able to release issues from the board should they have administrator permissions to _some_ of the projects in the board. Release link will stay greyed out for them right now, even if they are capable of releasing all issues in the current view (e.g. with a quick filter applied).    The suggestion is to implement a permission check for the issues in the view only if the user clicks Release link."""

summary: """Refresh All Application Links"" button in Applications Navigator clears (removes) remote links from UI instead of refreshing them"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    """"Refresh All Application Links"""" button in Applications Navigator clears (removes) remote links from UI instead of refreshing them  h3. Environment    JIRA 7.13, JIRA 8.x with application links to multiple remote applications  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Example scenario:All applications should remain on the page   # Deploy JIRA 7.13 or 8.x   # Deploy Confluence 6.10.1, Bitbucket 5.11, Bamboo 6.7.2 (I believe other versions will do, but I only tried these and customer is using Confluence 6.10.1)   # In JIRA create Application links to the applications   # Navigate to JIRA -> Settings -> Applications -> Application Navigator. Notice that all applications are there on the page   # Hit the 'refresh all application links' button (on the right).    *Expected Results:* All applications should remain on the page   *Actual Results:* Some applications will get removed (in my case only JIRA stayed)  h3. Cause:    List of remote/external applications is stored in the cache which is cleared (not refresh) by the button. After that site make next request: """"/rest/custom-apps/1.0/customapps/list?=<id>"""". This method returns local app links and remote one which is stored in cache. This cache is refreshed every 10 seconds by default – so if you refresh (cmd+R or F5) this site 10 seconds after you clicked refresh button list should be complete. The problem is with UI. UI should indicate that it still wait for cache invalidation and after that reload list of app links. The interval of cache refreshing is 10 seconds by default and can be set by system property “navlink.navigationlinkscache.delay” but remember if you decrease(<10s) value of this delay you can make Jira slower or starve other threads in environment.  h3. Workaround    a) Wait 10 seconds and perform refresh of the whole page (i.e. F5 or cmd+R)    b)* You can specify the system property with “-D<property>=<value>” JVM flag e.g. “-D navlink.navigationlinkscache.delay=10” (there is no whitespaces)    1. Please go to setenv.sh script and add your option to “JVM_SUPPORT_RECOMMENDED_ARGS” [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html]   2. Restart Jira instance    Please be mindful, workaround b can make Jira slower or starve other threads in environment (i.e. depending on the instance usage it may cause performance impact)"""

summary: "Making an workflow inactive discards changes in draft"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When editing an active workflow, a draft will be created with the modifications and only make those effective once the draft is published. However, if you deactivate a workflow (remove it from all schemes), the draft is discarded regardless of having changes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit an active workflow (assigned to at least one workflow scheme;  # Apply any modification to it;  # Go back to the """"Workflows"""" screen and navigate to the scheme/s associated with that workflow, and disassociate it from it/them;  # Notice that the workflow will be moved to the """"Inactive"""" section and its draft, discarded;    h3. Expected Results  The draft should be kept with the applied changes.    h3. Actual Results  Changes are lost upon deactivating the workflow.    h3. Workaround  Make sure changes are published to the workflow prior to making it inactive."""

summary: "Better communication of major bugs"
description: """*Background*  A major bug was discovered when JSD 4.0.1 / Jira 8.0.1 was rolled out. This caused major functionality loss whereby a couple of key features stopped working. In-product notification was pushed out to instances of JSD / Jira that are on the impacted version.    *Feedback*  The in-product message appears on the """"Applications Page"""" of the Jira UI. This page may not be frequently accessed by users and admins and this delays the time in which stakeholders are informed of the problem.    *Suggestion*  The suggestion is to make the notification more visible, perhaps to include in on the top section of login page/dashboard or etc. This would improve visibility and the stakeholder would get the message rather immediately compared to if he/she stumbled upon the message in the """"Applications Page""""."""

description: """Administer Project Permission in Project Management permissions grant many abilities to anyone granted that permission, as explained [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver0712/managing-project-permissions-959313533.html].    It would be much more helpful and useful to have it divided in to subcategories including, but not limited to the following:  (x) Edit project role membership  (x) Create new project components  (x) Create/ manage project versions  (x) Edit project details     I want to assign a few users the ability to create new components (this can still be done by anyone using labels instead of components as well, but it would still be nice to have a project specific component) or to *create and manage versions* in a specific project without giving them the right to change roles or the name of that project.    Thanks for listening :)"""

summary: "Allow Jira to embed images in email messages"
description: """Currently, when an email containing an image is sent, the email message contains a direct link to the image.    This can create problems if:   * the configured mail server uses proxies to cache images, is unable to authorize to Jira, and caches the 403 responses (e.g. when Gmail with the default settings is used as the outgoing mail server)   * the user is not logged in when viewing the email message, getting authorization error when querying for the image   * a mailing client is used that doesn't allow to set up a session inside Jira for authentication purposes    As a solution, Jira could give admins an option to embed the images inside emails.   Such a solution needs to be toggleable, as it adds a potentially expensive step to email creation, e.g. if a user attached multiple big images in one comment."""

Steps to Reproduce  # Install Jira with Japanese language  # Create new project A  # Install [WBS Gantt-Chart for Jira|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211768/wbs-gantt-chart-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview]  # Add value use the WBS Gantt-Chart  # Create backup XML  # Delete all issues, versions and components of project A  # Import XML to same instance    h3. Expected Results  Import project succeed.    h3. Actual Results  Return following error.      h3. Workaround  # Unzip the exported XML    # Confirmed the line in {{entities.xml}} which needs to be fixed    (i) Please replace {{<english-custom-field-name>}} with the one you saw in the UI error  # Check the Japanese translation of the custom field    (i) Please replace {{<english-custom-field-name>}} with the one you saw in the UI error  # Replace row #2 {{name=xxxx}} to result of #3  # Recreate the XML    # Import {{xxx-fixed.zip}} as usual"""

summary: "Jira integration with SIEM"
description: """As a Jira admin, I'd like to be able to integrate Jira with SIEM tool"""

summary: "View Usage for Project Role doesn't analyze projects with users in role"
description: """Existing View Usage feature shows when roles are used for schemes or workflows (except inactive workflows or add-on functions, see JRASERVER-44344), but what about projects that have users in the role, regardless of actual scheme or workflow usage? If a role is going to be removed or renamed, then the teams using it need to be alerted. If a team is using a project role that serves no purpose for their project, we could discover that with this feature and alert them."""

summary: "As an admin I would like an API to update User Directory attributes."
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As an admin I would like an API to manage user directories. For instance to update directory attributes e.g. update ldap.password via script on a schedule based on internal security policies.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide a JIRA API endpoint to update User directory attributes. """
comments: "Hi All,    This request was from my question in GHS-146217, and Taiwo Akindele kindly posted the request here on my behalf. Kindly please consider implementing the feature as it would greatly make our lives as JIRA admins easier. Thank you.    https://getsupport.atlassian.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/20/GHS-146217"

summary: "JIRA Issue Collector Filter/Limit User Email Domain"
description: """It would be great if JIRA issue collector allows only specific list/group of email domain to use issue collector. For example, only user with @mydomain.com able to raise a ticket with issue collector.  """

summary: "Option to delete add-on tables when uninstalling"
description: """Hi guys,    Current behavior when you uninstall an add-on is that the tables will remain there and for big instances this is a nightmare as your database increases significantly due to audit tables, etc. This also has a negative impact on the performance.    My suggestion would be to actually have a tick box that you should check if you want to delete the tables from the database when you uninstall the add-on or leave it unchecked in the situation when you only want to reinstall the add-on.    I hope this will get implemented!"""

summary: "Plugins feature flags are disabled when choosing 'Set it up for me' option in setup wizard"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    During the setup of Jira or while upgrading, if an admin decides to follow the 'Set it up for me' path then after completing it, all plugins' feature flags will be disabled - even the ones that should be turned on by default. This means that any feature provided by a plugin and hidden by such flag will not be available to users. Jira Core's feature flags are not affected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    As this also affects Jira Service Desk, and an example of its feature that would be disabled by this bug is its 'Canned Responses', the steps to reproduce may be following:   # Download JSD installer for 4.0 (Jira 8.0)   # Start JSD locally with H2.   # Once Jira starts - Click on option """"Set it up for me"""".   # Follow the remaining setup instruction.    h3. Expected Results    As 'Canned responses' is a feature meant to be available by default, its feature flag is turned on.  h3. Actual Results    'Canned responses' flag is turned off, the feature is unavailable.  h3. Workaround   # Restarting Jira after completing the setup/upgrade fixes the issue (plugin upgrade tasks are executed and feature flags are set appropriately)   # Follow the 'I'll set it up myself' path in wizard which does not disable plugin feature flags."""

summary: "Ability to bulk update issue type schemes"
description: """h3. *Summary*    Currently, the only way of adding an issue type to an *issue type scheme* demands a manual work, with the need of editing one issue type scheme at a time.    That becomes an even exhaustive task when there is a big number of issue type schemes in the instance.        h3. Solution    Add an option to edit multiple issue type schemes at the same time.    Perhaps, there could be an additional option in the issue types section (e.g. a button called *add to scheme(s)*), where it would simply display a list with all available issue type schemes with a checkbox next to them, so the user would simply need to check each checkbox (issue type scheme) and click on save to apply the associations.    Another alternative, a CSV import with such settings."""

summary: "The version of moment.js used in Jira was vulnerable to a regular expression denial of service"
description: """The version of moment.js used in in Jira before version 7.12.3, from version 7.13.0 before version 7.13.1 and before version 8.0.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service in user's browsers via a regular expression denial of service. For additional details see [https://github.com/moment/moment/issues/2936.|https://github.com/moment/moment/issues/2936]"""
comments: "This is an independent assessment and you should evaluate its applicability to your own IT environment. CVSS v3 score: 4.3 => Medium severity    *Exploitability Metrics*  || Attack Vector | Network |  || Attack Complexity | Low |  || Privileges Required | Low |  || User Interaction | None |    *Scope Metric*  || Scope | Unchanged |    *Impact Metrics*  || Confidentiality | None |  || Integrity | None |  || Availability | Low |    https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L"

summary: "Board Limited Filter "
description: """As a Board Administrator I want to edit the filter query and sharing of the board without being tied to a Saved Filter.    Ideally there would be radio buttons to choose from “Board Limited Filter” or “Saved Filter” and an option to copy a “Saved Filter” to the “Board Limited Filter”. Then if it was a “Board Limited Filter” any of that Board’s Administrators could edit the Filter Query / Sharing Permissions of that board without affecting anything else.    Currently when I add a Board Administrator I also have to remember to give them Edit permission to the Saved Filter. Also I can't stop someone else from using a shared filter for their board or widgets meaning I could change the filter and break their board/widgets purpose. It’s an Administrative / Change Management nightmare.          """

summary: "Text pasted from clipboard on the same line after an @mention is stuck to the mentioned user's display name"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On a line with an @mention and some text, any text pasted from clipboard anywhere after the @mention is stuck to the mentioned user's display name. This only happens in Visual mode.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Copy any text to clipboard using Ctrl+C / CMD+C   # Write a Comment in Visual mode   # On a line, mention a user -> type some text -> let the cursor be at the end of the line   # Paste the text copied to clipboard using Ctrl+V or CMD+V    h3. Expected Results  The text is pasted at the cursor position    h3. Actual Results  The text is stuck to the mentioned user's display name:  !screenshot.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  Undoing the pasting (Ctrl+Z or CMD+Z) and redoing it should work"""

summary: "Escape code on Description field when exporting to CSV"
description: """When opening CSV files exported through the CSV Export of Jira on Excel, if there are written Excel codes on it, they will run automatically.    The suggestion is to provide a setting/configuration that  automatically escape special characters on the export."""
comments: "additional details on the related vulnerability it helps prevent:    [https://www.owasp.org/index.php/CSV_Injection]    it also affects many other tools opening CSVs (google sheets open office and many others)" [SEP] "this is particularly important when the Jira instance is public facing or worse anonymous" [SEP] "Is there is any setting provided for this?" [SEP] "We have a large system and there is potential for this to be exploited within the company as you are more likely to trust macros from an internal source."

summary: "Creating linked issues in a Service Desk project carried over Participants from original issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    From [Linking issues - Atlassian Documentation - Create a new linked issue from an existing issue in a service desk or business project|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver0710/linking-issues-953145879.html#Linkingissues-Createanewlinkedissuefromanexistingissueinaservicedeskorbusinessproject], only Project, Issue Type and Summary information are carried over.  {quote}The newly created linked issue contains the same Project, Issue Type, and Summary information stored in the original issue.  {quote}  However, for Service Desk projects with participants added to it, the newly created linked issues via *More > Create linked issue* carried over the participants from the originating issue.  h3. Environment    (Optional - If Applicable)   * It is NOT reproducible in Jira 7.10.0   * Reproducible in Jira 7.10.1, Jira 7.12.3, Jira 8.0.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new issue in a Jira Service Desk project.   # Add a participant in the issue.   # Create a linked issue via select *More > Create linked issue.*    h3. Expected Results    Participants are not carried over in the new issue.  h3. Actual Results    Participants are carried over in the new issue.  h3. Workaround    Manually linking the newly created issue and the original issue via [*More > Link.*|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver0710/linking-issues-953145879.html#Linkingissues-creatinglinktoanotherissueCreatingalinktoanotherissueonthesameJirasite]"""
comments: "I do have the same Problem with Jira 8.5.4"

summary: "Add the ability to enable failover feature for Incoming Mail in Jira."
description: """h3. Summary    For bigger infrastructures, it is essential for Jira Incoming Mail to have a fail-safe system in case the mail server configured for incoming mail is down, the other mail server can automatically take over and process all incoming mail. We can add an optional field in the incoming mail configuration page to add an additional mail server for fail-safe purpose.    For example, if two microsoft exchange server are both set up in a HA-setup. If one is down the other automatically will take over. """

summary: "Add REST APIs methods to clean old node ids in JIRA Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem    Jira DC administrators experience a problem with tons of records about their cluster nodes on _System Info_ page in the admin panel. DC enterprise customers restart or kill or add new nodes as part of the everyday activities connected with managing their cluster. All those activities lead to the creation of records in the database and on _System Info_ page under _Cluster Nodes_ bookmark. As a result, administrators see not the actual state of their clusters, but all active & nonactive nodes in a long list that is hard for them to understand and brings more confusion than visibility. This problem becomes more significant in the case of AWS deployments.      h3. Suggested solution    Since Jira 8.1.0 we would like to provide the (!) experimental REST API which allows Admins in Jira Data Center to manage the state of the cluster. We would like to provide three methods:   * GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_ - return all nodes in cluster in JSON format.    * PUT  _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/\{nodeId}/offline_ - change the node's state to *OFFLINE* if the node is reporting as *ACTIVE*, but is non _""""alive""""_ (alive = false).   ** This step could have a negative effect on the cluster consistency, so it's under the discretion of Jira admin. Make sure the node *will not* rejoin the cluster, in another case it will have non-consistent index/cache data. The node can be restarted.   ** The node is marked as non """"_alive_"""" after missing the heartbeat timeout (default 5min).   ** Thus the node potentially could be marked as  *OFFLINE* only after heartbeat timeout.   ** (!) Remember this function is not able to stop Jira instance.   * DELETE _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/\{nodeId}_ - delete the node from the cluster and database, if state of node is *OFFLINE*.  ** Safe to execute    Only Jira System Administrators can execute those methods.    h3. Example  Example response of GET method:    Example usage:   # Make a request - GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_   # Jira returns a list of nodes*   # Find """"*ACTIVE*"""" and _""""alive""""=false_ nodes ID in the previous response - e.g. """"node1""""   # Make sure that nodes (e.g. """"node1"""") are not running in your environment and will not back to the cluster in the future.    #* (!) *They should be stoped*   # Make a request - PUT _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/node1/offline_   # Make a request - GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_   # Find out that """"node1"""" now has """"*OFFLINE*"""" state   # Make a request -  DELETE _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/node1_   # Make a request - GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_   # Find out that """"node1"""" was deleted"""

summary: "Manually entering a date with a two-digit year results in a negative epoch time in the database when system date settings are set to 4-digit years"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a date is entered by a date picker is then updated manually in the date field to only include 2-digit year, this date is stored as a negative epoch in the database.   One example of a date is a sprint start date.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to the system advance settings.  # Change *jira.date.picker.java.format* to *d/MMM/yyyy*  # Change *jira.date.picker.javascript.format* to *%e/%b/%Y*  # Change *jira.date.time.picker.java.format* to *dd/MMM/yyyy h:mm a*  # Change *jira.date.time.picker.javascript.format* to *%e/%b/%Y %I:%M %p*  # Start a sprint with a custom duration  # Pick a date  # Update the date filled by the date picked to only include two digits in the year. For instance, from 28/Mar/2019 04:37 PM to 28/Mar/19 04:37 PM.  # Start the sprint     h3. Expected Results  The sprint start date in the DB should be stored as if it was in the year 2019.  h3. Actual Results  The sprint start date in the DB is stored as if the sprint started in the year 0019. Thus, it's a negative epoch.     h3. Notes  * This bug breaks reporting done by add-ons such as eazyBI.   * If possible, Jira should enforce the choice of a 4-digit year in the front end.   h3. Workaround  The data needs to be fixed manually in the database.   """

summary: "When moving an issue, the new project dropdown is incorrectly displayed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When moving an issue, the arrow on the dropdown list for the new project is located at the far right.   h3. Environment  * This issue is only reproducible on Windows in the customer's environment.   * The issue could be reproduced by Atlassian Support.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Move an issue via *More > Move*    h3. Expected Results  The project dropdown should show properly:   !10_project_dropdown.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results  The arrow of the dropdown is located at the far right of the screen:   !10_project_dropdown_broken.png|thumbnail!   h3. Notes  This issue could not be reproduced by Atlassian Support. It's put here to gauge interest from other users running into it.   h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Comment Replies Should Notify the Original Commenter"
description: """As a Development team member I would like to be notified when another JIRA user replies to my comment so that we can appropriately collaborate within JIRA.         Currently the ability for an original commenter to be notified of a Jira ticket reply is limited by the notification scheme. Original commenter should be added to the notification scheme to allow for comment replies to properly notify the users who commented on their reply."""

summary: "Workflow buttons disappear when the detail view of issue search is resized smaller"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If the detail view width can't fit all the workflow buttons, the buttons that exceed the width are not one line lower.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a project and assign a workflow with 10 transitions to it.   # [Advanced workflow configuration > Customize how transitions appear|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/advanced-workflow-configuration-861253591.html#Advancedworkflowconfiguration-customisingtransitionsonviewissue] to make sure all button are visible by default.    # Search for issues within the project you created in the detail view.    # Click on one of the issues in the search results to preview it on the right hand side.    # Resize the issue detail view smaller.     h3. Expected Results  All button should be visible in the issue detail view. They don't have to keep their positions but they should be moved around so they are always visible.   h3. Actual Results  Jira trims the buttons list and only show what it can fit in the view:   !14_workflow_button_small.png|thumbnail!  !14_workflow_button_bigger.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround  Reduce the number of transition buttons that appear on the screen by default: [Advanced workflow configuration > Customize how transitions appear|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/advanced-workflow-configuration-861253591.html#Advancedworkflowconfiguration-customisingtransitionsonviewissue]."""

summary: "workflowSchemeManager.removeSchemesFromProject doesn't clean up previous workflow scheme"
description: """h3. Summary    When using the workflowSchemeManager.removeSchemesFromProject to remove current workflow scheme of a project resulting to default Jira workflow scheme,_ the previous workflow associated with workflow scheme remains *active_* and the _current workflow scheme is *inactive_* (classic Jira workflow scheme)    *Steps to reproduce*  # Create Workflow Alpha  # Create Workflow Scheme Alpha Container  # Associate Alpha with Alpha Container - all issue types  # Create Project Beta  # Associate Workflow Scheme Alpha Container with project Beta.  # Now Workflow Alpha and Workflow Scheme Alpha Container both show as active and Workflow Scheme Alpha Container shows association with project Beta.  # In your plugin, remove workflow scheme of project thru: {{ComponentAccessor().getWorkflowSchemeManager().removeSchemesFromProject(project);}}    Expected result:   # Project Beta is associated with the Default Workflow Scheme.   # Workflow Scheme Alpha Container is not associated to project.  # Workflow Scheme Alpha Container  is inactive(because is not associated with any other project).  # Workflow Alpha is still associated with Workflow Scheme Alpha Container and it is inactive.(because Workflow Alpha is not associated with any other Workflow Scheme).    Actual Result:    # Project Beta is associated with the Default Workflow Scheme.   # Workflow Scheme Alpha Container is not associated to project.  # Workflow Scheme Alpha Container  is inactive(because is not associated with any other project).  # Workflow Alpha is still associated with Workflow Scheme Alpha Container and it is show as acttive. (even if Workflow Alpha is not associated with any other Workflow Scheme).    h3. Notes"""

summary: "Ability to @mention user without 'Browse Users' global permission"
description: """My request is to be able to mention without requiring to assign the """"Browse Users"""" global permission. JRASERVER-7467 asks to restrict the browse users permission to project users, which would resolve our issue, because then we would be able to assign """"Browse Users"""" global permission to all our customers with no information leakage.    However, JRASERVER-7467 has been parked because it is very difficult to solve. It is difficult to decide exactly which users to show and which users to hide. Therefore, I request to track the mention subproblem separately, because it is much easier to solve: show the users which can browse the issue you are mentioning on - just like JIRA already does."""
comments: "Thank you for creating this issue.    We serve multiple partners with our Jira installation and they frequently request to be able to mention other team members. However, we cannot assign ""Browse Users"" global permission because that would allow them to view users belonging to other partners in the user browser - users they should not be able to see.    Perhaps another project level permission could be added: ""Allow Mentioning."" It would allow you to use the mention functionality on issues in that project without having ""Browse Users"" global permission. This would give the mentioning users the ability to see all the users that can browse the issue they are mentioning on, but it is much more limited than browse users global permission, which also allows you to see users completely unrelated to the project you are in.    In case people virtually never want to allow comments but not mentions - we could also tie the ""allow mentioning"" permission to ""allow commenting."" That would be an even simpler solu

summary: "Provide the sprint estimate summary more consistently in the backlog view"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The very helpful sprint estimate summary that is available for planned sprints on the backlog view as ilustrated below, isn't displayed in a few scenarios.   !Selection_544.jpg|thumbnail!   This summary is very useful as it allows you to have an overview of the sprint estimation without any additional clicks. The following are some scenarios where you don't have this summary displayed:  * When the sprint panel is collapsed, this estimate summary disappears.   !Selection_545.jpg|thumbnail!   * The estimate is also not displayed for active sprints, only planned sprints.   !Selection_547.jpg|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  * Keep the estimate summary at the bottom of a collapsed panel, there's still plenty of empty space to display it for the collapsed panel as currently is.   * Display the estimate summary for active sprints as well.    h3. Workaround  There is a button (with 3 dots or ellipsis) underneath the sprint name that can actually be used to preview a more detailed sprint estimate summary as illustrated below. Although it requires an additional click to display, it's a consistent way to always be able to see the sprint estimate summary from backlog view:   !Selection_546.jpg|thumbnail! """

Environment    IE 11  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to JIRA as administrator   # Go to any project's settings   # Click on Permissions (left menu)   # Click on Actions button   # Select Edit permissions   # Try to delete any permission.    h3. summary: "$webResourceManager.requireResource does not work for some plugin keys and results in AUI error"
description: """h3. Summary    Vendor has a JIRA Server plugin that has a web-panel configured at location """"servicedesk.portal.subheader"""" as such:      _/src/main/resources/atlassian-plugin.xml_      This velocity template uses $webResourceManager.requireResource to load """"com.atlassian.auiplugin:dialog2""""    _/src/main/resources/templates/portal.vm_        In *Jira 7.10.2* with *ServiceDesk 3.13.2*    Customer Portal loads properly when plugin key in pom.xml is 'com.view26.jira'    _/pom.xml_      But Customer portal does not load when plugin key is changed to 'com.view26.servicedesk-report.jira' with everything else remaining the same.    _/pom.xml_      The blank screen has a console error - """"Uncaught Error: No aui/dialog2""""    Sample App:  [^jira.zip]      h3. Background and Notes  * I have replicated this issue for their app but with a fresh app with the same plugin keys, velocity template with webResourceManager.requireResource, and appropriate versions, I don't have the issue. Sample app: [^myplugin.zip]   * Vendor needs help finding out the root cause as this is a blocker        """

summary: "Unable to search 'status' column in issue navigator when I have many custom fields include name 'status'"
description: """h4. Issue    * Unable to search 'status' column in issue navigator when I have many custom fields include 'status' in the filed name.   !status.png|thumbnail!     h4. Suggestion    * Users should be able to search the 'status' column successfully even when there are many custom fields contain 'status' in their name.     h4. Workaround    Option I     # Perform an issue query or click """"View all issues and filters"""" to cause a list of issues to be displayed.   # Click on the """"Columns"""" dropdown.   # Scroll to the bottom of the list.   # Right-click on the text """"Continue typing to refine further"""". (You can actually right-click anywhere in the Columns chooser, but this puts you close to the tag you're going to be looking for in step 6.)   # Select """"Inspect"""" (on Chrome). This should open a browser development window to the right where you can inspect and modify the page's HTML.   # In the """"Elements"""" window at the top of the inspection area (on Chrome), scroll down until you see an element titled """"<select id=""""user-column-sparkler""""...>"""".   # Expand """"user-column-sparkler"""".   # Expand """"optgroup label"""" within it.   # Scroll down until you find the node """"<option title=""""Status"""" value=""""status"""">Status</option>"""" (It may be necessary to click """"Show all nodes"""" to see it.)   # Right-click on that node and select """"Add attribute"""". Type: selected=""""selected"""". The node should now read:  """"<option title=""""Status"""" value=""""status"""" selected=""""selected"""">Status</option>""""   # Click """"Done"""" back in the column chooser widget.   # The """"Status"""" column should now appear in your issues list. It should not be necessary to repeat this.    Option II    # Rename the custom field to something else which does not cause this issue in the Column search.  # Add this renamed field as a column to the List View in the Issue Navigator  # Rename the custom field back to what it originally was"""
comments: "Hmm, perhaps a better approach would be if Jira had restricted words not allowed for custom field names. Start by not allowing any custom field with the same name as a system field such as status  "

summary: "Migration fails with ORA-12899 error while migrating to Oracle database using XML export/import"
description: """h3. Summary    Active Objects library, which presents the abtraction for data types and object relational mapping, does not work consistently across supported platforms, hence migration fails.    When migrating from another database (I.e. mySQL) to Oracle database, using an XML export generated from source system, following exception appears in the logs;    This happens with an third-party app that has defined a string column with length 255. In the MySQL database it was possible to store 255 characters, no matter which kind of characters was used. E.g. it is possible to store 255 Chinese characters. In Oracle the column is created as VARCHAR2(255 BYTE). But in order to store 255 special characters, the suitable data type in Oracle would be VARCHAR2(255 CHAR). So the reason for the exception is, that 255 special characters need more than 255 bytes, but the column definition in Oracle is limited to 255 byte.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    A test to recreate the problem is provided at JRASERVER-62833. Quoting;    h3. Expected Results    Migration is successful, and all AO data are transferred to new database successfully.  h3. Actual Results    Migration fails with ORA-12899 error.  h3. Workaround    None"""
comments: "It's not only the migration which fails, but also when running the plugin on Oracle this leads to problems.    In case of the mentioned table (AO_544E33_AUDIT_LOG_ENTRY) the impact is, that the email does not show up in the email history provided by Email This Issue (JETI). In the JETI log I can see the message known from the migration:    But I don't see this as a JETI issue, since they have used the provided API correctly.    Some of our users use JETI to communicate with customers and they rely on the email history to see what the customer had sent. This information is lost in case the summary of an issue has <=255 characters but counts more than 255 bytes, which happens from time to time. summary: "Code on visual mode isn't correctly rendered and indentation isn't rendered showing text incorrectly."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Code on visual mode isn't correctly rendered and indentation isn't rendered showing text incorrectly.    h3. Environment    (!) Jira 7.7.1    h3. When you insert a Code block, if it's populated with // code placeholder, then there's no problem  But if it's not populated with that placeholder, then the problem happens.
