summary: "Missing token when attaching a file after login while accessing a project."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA doesn't correctly create a session token when you access the *{{<Jira URL>/projects/<Project key>/issues}}* page prior to login. If you directly click on the create button and try to attach an attachment a missing token error is thrown. This is rectified by cancelling the issue creation or navigating to the dashboard.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log off from Jira   # Go to <Jira URL>/projects/<Project key>/issues   ** (i) after login automatic redirected to {{<Jira url>/projects/<Project key>/issues/<Issue key>?filter=allopenissues}}   # Click create button   # Try to attach a file    h3. Expected Results    Attachment is attached.  h3. Actual Results    An error is thrown:   JIRA could not attach the file as there was a missing token. Please try attaching the file again.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Note on fix  *xsrf-token-addition* filter was moved before Dispatcher so in case request is flushed, it will have all necessary XSRF headers.     h3. Workaround    Cancel the issue creation and try again."""
comments: "I moved this issue to JIRA Server (including JIRA Core) project as problem is not in Jira Agile" [SEP] "I can confirm, that this is exactly reproducable also on our environment." [SEP] "Reproduced with Jira Core Server 8.1.0" [SEP] "We are using Jira 7.6.3 and we have a slightly different situation.  I am able to follow the Steps to Reproduce above and I can reproduce the error.     However, we are using an Excel macro that creates a Jira Issue and then logs the user into Jira to allow them to add attachments.   The url used is the same as what is mentioned in Step 2 (i.e. <Jira URL>/projects/<Project key>/issues).  At this point some people receive the missing token error when trying to add attachments and others don't.  In my case I don't receive the error with this approach but I do receive it following the outlined steps.       I'm trying to determine the root cause as to why some receive the error and others do not receive it ... I was hoping this bug might be it, but it doesn't appear to be si

summary: "Add functionality to process outlook message attachments into .msg format in regular Jira incoming mail handler"
description: """h4. Issue    * Currently, if you forward a mail as an attachment using Microsoft Outlook (or if you directly attach an email as an attachment), it shows up in a JIRA issue as a file with no extension. Because there is no extension, clicking on the file does not open it in the appropriate way and file is not readable.   * If the same mail attachment was sent to Jira via the mail web browser, the file extension exists in the original email and the .msg file can be viewed correctly.     h4. Cause     * It is because when the mail was sent from outlook, there is no file extension in the original email that was sent to Jira's incoming mail handler.     h4. Suggestion    * Jira should be able to detect the file as mail attachment without file extension so that end user can view the .msg file properly after the new issue is created. """
comments: "Closing this ticket as a duplicate of this other open ticket: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-40477"

The reason is that for Jira Service Desk, it's not always convenient to have batching, since it causes delay in receiving notifications. summary: "DeterminedProjectsInQueryVisitor doesn't work for default Kanban sub-query"
description: """h3. Problem    While converting JQL query to Lucene query Jira tries to guess projects to which the results will belong. This enables Jira to perform some optimisations. For example:   * Optimised security qeury   * version functions are expanded to versions from only related projects.    Unfortunately, those guessed projects are not passed into subqueries.  The problem reveals itself on Kanban board with default Kanban sub-filter:      h3. Test case   * Create 2 Kanban projects   * Create 33k unreleased versions for each project (66k in total)   * Try opening Kanban board    h3. Expected result    Everything works. Lucene query contains 33k versions from current project. (optimization worked and sub-filter receives the project scope)  h3. Actual result    Jira tries to create Lucene query with 66k versions from the whole instance. (Optimization failed)   It also fails due to BooleanQuery limit.  h3. Workaround    Removing '{{OR fixVersion is EMPTY}}' fixes the optimisation."""
comments: "I recommend adding the comma-separated related project keys to the unreleasedVersions function  This has bitten us in the past with 80K versions" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]    We've fixed this issue.  After upgrading to 8.4.0 you won't need to fill those projects manually ;)"

summary: "Ability to remove specific information from history and activity Tab"
description: """There could be times where some sensitive data could be inadvertently added to an issue in a text format, on a comment, description, or other forms (Fields, etc). For security reasons and due to regulations (GDPR and others), this information needs to be removed from the instance. Although the user can remove the text, the information will still be present on the history and activity tab of the issue.     Since the history tab can't be disabled (JRACLOUD-67300), Would be useful, and at some point necessary, that the administrators have the ability to remove data from the history or activity tab in case they need to hide sensitive information from the instance.  This would also remove the information on the activity stream where users can see all the changes.  """
comments: "We've looked into this. I even used ScriptRunner to delete entries from the changeitem database table. Turns out the simplest thing to do is to create a new issue without the sensitive or very large text, then delete the old issue" [SEP] "Unfortunately creating a new issue and deleting the old issue would remove all links that existed to the old issue.     It would appear that Jira is currently not suited for handling issues containing personally identifiable information, while complying with GDPR regulations." [SEP] "Hi all,    you may have a look on this two GDPR-AddOns. With this AddOns you will be able to be *fully compliant* to the GDPR-Policies.   * JIRA: [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1218962 6|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1218962]   * Confluence: [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1219041 3|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1219041]    Best Regards   Domenico" [SEP] "I had a look at the first link ( [https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1218962|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1218962)]

summary: "Actual Time in SLA"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The SLA fields by design would show either time left to breach or time since breached. However, when you hover over them you're able to see actual time elapsed since the SLA started ticking as illustrated in screenshot below:   !Selection_538.jpg|thumbnail!   As a user I would like this important 'actual time' information to be a little more obvious e.g. displayed together with the sla time without having to hover over the field first to see it.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a user I would like this important 'actual time' information to be a little more obvious e.g. displayed together with the sla time without having to hover over the field first to see it.  * Make this improvement both in the SLA section of the Issue View and in the issue navigator column for the SLA fields."""
comments: "Thanks for creating this suggestion.  From my perspective the preferred behaviour is the that the SLA counter counts down (as it does now) showing the remaining time while the clock is running or paused, but once the SLA clock stops it then displays the amount of time that elapsed before the clock stopped.         Hopefully Atlassian can give us a couple of toggle switches to configure the behaviour, including also offering as you suggested the ability to see perhaps both stats on the Issue View screen." [SEP] "We are also in search of such kind of functionality, please consider this suggestion as this would be a great to get more details for any such SLA."

summary: "Update Jira MySQL documentation in regards to installing Jira and Confluence on the same database server"
description: """In our [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL 5.7|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7-966063305.html] documentation, there is a bullet at the top with the following information:  {quote}  If you plan to set up Confluence and Jira on the same MySQL server, read the Confluence MySQL setup guide. Confluence requirements are more strict than Jira's, so you should configure MySQL to suit Confluence. This configuration will work for Jira, too.  {quote}    Since the release of Jira 8.0.0, Jira now supports the use of the {{utf8mb4}} collation with MySQL databases, while Confluence using MySQL still does not. See the following request: [MySQL databases incapable of handling 4byte UTF-8 Characters. Confluence should handle this gracefully|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-18509]    The wording in the documentation should be changed as both databases cannot be setup in the same way with Confluence unable to support the {{utf8mb4}} collation or there should be a disclaimer to advise on using an unsupported collation."""

summary: "Filter plus one comments"
description: """""""+1"""" comments are often annoying and generate extra email and comment """"noise."""" To discourage users from posting +1 comments, I suggest the following:    If a user attempts to add a comment with the text """"+1"""" display the following:   # Their comment in editable form   # A message that   ## indicates that a """"plus one"""" comment was detected   ## describes why this is a bad idea   ## describes how the user can vote for an issue instead   # Buttons with the following actions:   ## Vote for the issue without a comment   ## Vote for the issue and submit the comment   ## Submit comment without voting   ## Cancel         The feature should check if the user has voting rights and adjust what it displays/offers accordingly.    The feature may also provide administrators the ability to customize the message.    The feature may restrict where is searches for the """"+1"""" text and or search for additional text. For example, maybe only """"+1"""" on the first or last line will be flagged. Or, maybe the text """"plus 1"""" and """"plus one"""" is also flagged. Atlassian might be able to grep their own Jira databases to see what the most common patterns are and only search for those to help avoid false positives."""

summary: "Unable to use Manage Add-ons page if the user is using Dutch language"
description: """h3. *Summary*    Unable to use the Manage Add-ons page if the user is using the Dutch language / Nederlands (Nederland) in JIRA user language setting.    !Screenshot 2019-03-07 at 1.52.19 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. *Steps to Reproduce*  # Simply change your user preferred language to Nederlands (Nederland).   # Access the page at *Administration > Add-ons > Manage add-ons*.     h3. *Expected Results*    Expect it to load normally and we can manage the add-ons.     h3. *Actual Results*     !Screenshot 2019-03-07 at 1.52.19 PM.png|thumbnail!      h3. *Notes*    - This is also affecting the JIRA system language but the user language setting will override the system language.   - The problem itself lies in the JIRA Core language pack located at {{[$JIRA_INSTALL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/JIRA/JIRA+Installation+Directory]/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins/jira-core-language-pack-nl_NL-8.0.0.v20190201000500.jar}} where there is a file in the JAR: /com/atlassian/upm/i18n_nl_NL.properties is having a wrong close bracket type at line 541:   {code:none}   upm.plugin.action.license.role.dialog.upgrade=Mogelijk moet je je {0}-licentie upgraden voordat je je {1)-licentie kunt upgraden. summary: "FieldConfigScheme#getAssociatedIssueTypes returns a collection of size 0 even if there are IssueTypes associated to it"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  FieldConfigScheme#getAssociatedIssueTypes returns a collection of size 0 even if there are IssueTypes associated to it    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create project  # Create two IssueTypes  # Create IssueTypeScheme   # Associate IssueTypeScheme with the two IssueType just created.  # In the project setting, associate a project with the Default Issue Type Scheme. Now the created IssueTypeScheme is not associated with any project.  # Call FieldConfigScheme#getAssociatedIssueTypes    h3. Expected Results  The two issue types should be returned i.e. a {{Collection}} containing 2 elements of class IssueType    h3. Actual Results  A {{Collection}} of size 1, containing null. System reports all elements are null when debugging, or the list is of size 0.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Webhook does not send special characters when copying/pasting from Microsoft Tools (i.e Word) when Jira is configured with SQL Server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Environment  Jira and MSSQL Server    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a webhook configuration setting at Jira  # Copy a few entries from a Microsoft Suite Tool such as Word and paste in a text field (e.g Comment) such as the example below:  #* Hey there – this is typing in MS Word 2013.  Let’s see if the ‘apostrophes’ and “quotes” make it to JIRA as weird characters!!!  Here is another “ quote “ just in case grammar is not someone’s forte!    h3. Expected Results  Jira sends the webhook with the same formatting than the source (i.e Microsoft Word)    h3. Actual Results  Jira sends the webhook, but the special characters are translated to hexadecimal or to question marks as the log below that's displayed when the class """"com.atlassian.webhooks"""" is set to """"debug"""":     h3. Notes  * This problem does not occur when inputting special characters to Jira directly.  * This problem does not occur when Jira is associated with different DBMS such as PostgreSQL    h3. Workaround  Besides the notes above, there's no known workaround  """

summary: "deactivate or inactivate a sprint in place"
description: """JIRA would be much more Agile if a sprint could become Not Active, after becoming Active.    Currently, it is impossible in JIRA for the user to change their mind, once a sprint becomes Active.  I have been unable to find a practical reason for this behavior.    JIRA should be consistent between different features in the product, such as Issues and Sprints.  Notice the design and support for moving individual issues between the various states and statuses, such as Start Progress and Stop Progress, and Resolve, Close, and Reopen.  The same concepts should apply to Sprints.    There are arduous workarounds, such as completing, deleting and recreating a Sprint.  Frankly, this is not a viable workaround, it is more of a desparate hack.  Project Managment is hard enough without having rework imposed by tools.  As an example, the rework of completing a Sprint, deleting a Sprint, then rebuilding a brand new Sprint from scratch, exactly like the Sprint which was just deleted, for the sole purpose of having an idential Sprint that is Inactive, due to JIRA lacking an Inactivate button.    This new feature would function exactly like the Start Progress and Stop Progress buttons do for an Issue."""
comments: "It's interesting that our favorite project management tool would have this feature that inserts bottlenecks into our work process...    if one task is stalled the entire team is bottlenecked.     Parallel sprints feature is ok but still not quite right .... and doesn't work in next-gen projects." [SEP] "+1" [SEP] "+1" [SEP] "+1" [SEP] "+ 1"

summary: "Linux installer overrides Jira backup during upgrade"
description: """h3. Summary  Directory backups generated during the upgrade process in Linux / Unix overrides any manually generated backup if they have the same file name    h3. Environment  * Jira installed in Linux / Unix environment  * Upgrade is performed with the installer    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Jira 7.12.1 in Ubuntu using the Linux installer  # Create some sample data  # Manually backup the Jira installation and Jira home directory with the name {{jira-<old-version>-back.zip}}. In this case the file would be jira-7.12.1-back.zip. Note the file size  # Upgrade Jira to 8.0.0 using the installer  # Opt-in for 'Back up JIRA home directory?'  # Notice that both manual backups are overridden by the upgrade process with the same file name    h3. Expected Results  Jira installer detected backup file exist and either:  * Stop the installation and returns an error message indicating backup file already exist  * Backup the directories with a unique file name for example jira-7.12.1-back-1.zip    h3. Actual Results  The manual backup files are overriden     h3. Notes  * This is only observed in Linux / Unix environment   * Windows installer will stop the upgrade and throws an error     h3.Workaround  Avoid using the file name in this format for manual backups - {{jira-<old-version>-back.zip}}"""

summary: "Actions for the sub-tasks should be displayed in the same sequence provided for opsbar-sequence property"

But the issue actions (which can be accessed by typing '.') or the actions for a sub-task show the transitions as ordered by the transition id in the workflow. It does not follow the sequence id given with opsbar-sequence property provided under transition. """

summary: "Sufficient issue field values not supplemented for Inline issue creation when quick filter applied on board that is created from saved filter"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Normally suitable field values are supplemented when an inline issue is created in backlog screen. For example, """"user1"""" is automatically supplemented in an assignee field if a user created an issue from quick filter that has a JQL expression {{assignee=user1}} .   But these field value supplement doesn't work well when quick filter is applied on a backlog of a board created from a saved filter.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create users – """"user1"""" and """"user2""""   # Create scrum project and name it as """"testproj""""   # Create a search filter with following condition and save it with name """"filter A""""   {{project=testproj and assignee in (user1, user2)}}   # Create a board using """"filter A"""" and name it as """"board A""""   # Click """"Configure"""" in the backlog screen on """"board A"""", and create a quick filter with following condition   {{assignee=user1}}   # Inline-create an issue by the link """"Create Issue"""" in backlog screen on """"board A""""   # Create a search filter with following condition and save it with name """"filter B""""   {{project=testproj and assignee in (user1, user2, EMPTY)}}   # Create a board using """"filter B"""" and name it as """"board B""""   # Click """"Configure"""" in the backlog screen on """"board B"""", and create a quick filter with following condition   {{assignee=user1}}   # Inline-create an issue by the link """"Create Issue"""" in backlog screen on """"board A"""" and """"board B""""    h3. Expected Results    Issues created from inline-creation in both board A and B has a preset field value """"user1"""" as an assignee.  h3. Actual Results    """"user1"""" is set in assignee field in board A, but the assignee in board B is left unassigned.  h3. Notes    Above is just one of examples.  h3. Workaround    Manually supplement the values."""

summary: "Regular Jira incoming mail handler should turn on dispatching CommentCreatedEvent in Jira when comment come from mail"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Comments from emails managed by the Jira's regular incoming mail handler are able to change the status of an issue, but doesn't update the SLA due to the regular mail handler doesn't do not perform any actions on WorkflowTransitionUtil class after creating new comments under the ticket    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create Jira regular incoming mail handler and config 'time to resolution' custom filed for Jira service desk.    # Make a comment by email to change the status of an issue in order to update the SLA.  # Issue status is changed as expected    h3. Expected Results    * SLA pause or unpause as expected.     h3. Actual Results    * SLA doesn't pause or unpause as expected.     h3. Workaround    * User JSD mail handler instead of the regular incoming mail handler as JSD's mail handler use CommentCreatedEvent to update the SLA custom fields. """

summary: "Regression: Projects may be reverted to the default issue type scheme during the change due to race condition"
description: """h2. Problem  When performing multiple issue type scheme changes at the same time, it can result in issue type schemes being reverted to use the Default Issue Type Scheme.  * This occurs when making simultaneous changes with multiple projects via from the Project Admin page: Project settings > Issue types > Actions > Use a different scheme    h5. Steps to Reproduce  # Generate 2000 projects.  # Created a new issue type scheme, named 'test issue type scheme'.  # Associated 1854 projects to the newly created issue type scheme 'test issue type scheme'.  # Updated three projects simultaneously, originally associated with the default issue type scheme, and set it to use the new 'test issue type scheme'  ** *Simultaneously* via *Project settings* > *Issue types* > *Actions* > *Use a different scheme*.  ** In this case, I had 3 browser tabs open and quickly triggered the change at once using shortcuts to click """"OK""""  # Review the number of total projects associated with 'test issue type scheme'.    h5. Expected Behaviour  The number of projects associated with 'test issue type scheme' should now be 1857    h5. Actual Behaviour  The number of projects associated with 'test issue type scheme' is 626.  The missing projects were reverted back to use the Default Issue Type Scheme"""
comments: "Hey everyone,    Just a heads up, we'll be closing this current bug report (JRASERVER-68948) out as a duplicate and reopening the original bug report (JRASERVER-66625) for further development and tracking.    The fix implemented in JRASERVER-66625 does reduce the possibility of the race condition occurring, but does not fully address the issue altogether. As such, JRASERVER-66625 should not be considered fully fixed. These details will be clarified in JRASERVER-66625 as well.    Please leave a comment on JRASERVER-66625 if there are any questions!    Cheers,  Kevin Liou  Premier Support Engineer"

Expected Results  The text box is resized within the window boundaries.    h3. Actual Results  It's possible to resize the textbox outside the window boundaries and therefore you cannot resize back.  The *Share* and *Cancel* buttons go with the text box, and depending on how large is the resize it's not possible to see the buttons:  !broken-resize.png|thumbnail!   !broken-resize-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  If you add several recipients into the """"Name Email"""" field the modal also behaves in the same way, pushing the text box and the submit button to an inaccessible area.    h3.Workaround  None."""

summary: "'Issue in progress' gadget should perform search using statusCategory = 'In progress' instead of status = 'In Progress'"

Summary    Since there are a lot of interpretation on what 'In Progress' means in a status(It could be 'Triaging' or 'Investigating'), it is better if the gadget returns result from the statusCategory = 'In Progress' instead of the status = 'In Progress' itself. This will return all the issues with that are in In Progress.     """

summary: "Add performance statistics for FieldIndexer plugin points during issue reindexing"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The per issue indexing time is strongly determined by the time taken by various {{FieldIndexer}} (plugin) modules. As a result, badly behaving plugins can severely limit Jira’s maximum indexing throughput.     h3. Suggested Solution  Add logging and performance statistics for FieldIndexer plugin points.   This will help to identify the top contributors to the indexing performance and how it affects overall indexing time.     h3. Workaround   None"""

summary: "Cloud Migration Document"

Problem Definition    In a cloud migration doc [Migrate from Jira Server to Jira Cloud|https://confluence.atlassian.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=779160826], the following phrase sounds misleading.    bq. *Upgrade Jira Server:* If you aren't already, you'll need to be on version 7.0.0 or higher before migrating. To learn how to upgrade, check out [Upgrading Jira applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/ADMINJIRASERVER/Upgrading+Jira+applications].    Basically, it should be on the latest *Feature release* at the point as described in [Jira Server to Cloud FAQs|https://www.atlassian.com/cloud-migration/jira-faq#faq-817fbf20-7a58-43c7-a96e-63be8e929c91].    bq. If you're on Jira Server version 7.13.1 or earlier, you'll need to upgrade before migrating.    h3. Suggested Solution    Please update [Migrate from Jira Server to Jira Cloud|https://confluence.atlassian.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=779160826] with considering the above information."""

summary: "Attaching files on comments not working properly on Safari 12.0.2"
description: """h3. Summary    Whenever users try to attach files directly on comments while on *Visual* mode, Jira won't load the attachment on the issue.    h3. Environment  * Jira 8.0.1  * Safari 12.0.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access Jira 8.0.1 while using Safari 12.0.2  # Browse any issue  # Click on *Comment* while on Visual Mode, then click on *Attachment > Browse* and choose a file     h3. Expected Results    Jira will add the attachment to the issue successfully    h3. Actual Results    The file is not added to Jira. No errors are thrown in the logs or in the UI    h3. Workaround    The same behavior does not happen when attaching files while on *Text* mode. Also, if the mode is changed from *Text* to *Visual* again, Jira will attach the file normally."""
comments: "This one drives me nuts.  Using text mode is my workaround, but really..." [SEP] "Hi, when will Version 8.1.1 be made available?" [SEP] "Another work around for this is to click and drag the file to attach it.     nonetheless – this not being resolved the first time that it was claimed to be is a fail. Better luck next time?"

summary: "Deleting project completes but the data remains in the database."
description: """h3. Summary    When deleting a project Jira says it was successfully deleted even though it was not. The project remains in the database.   After that Jira start throwing the following error    If you look in the """"_nodeassociation_"""" tables using the following query, you can see that the association was actually deleted.    h3. How to reproduce    Try to delete a project.  h3. Actual Result    Jira says the project is deleted but if you look in the database you can still see the entry for it.  h3. Expected results    JIRA should delete the project.  h3. Workaround    None at the moment.    Note: Jira should not continue the deletion if there are any errors, this would avoid the permission scheme from being disassociated and cause further problems."""

summary: "Updating a project category via the API sends incorrect webhook data"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a project category is updated via the API, the webhook setup for project updates doesn't send the new project category. Instead, it sends the old category.   h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create two project categories.   # Assign a project to one of the categories.  # Go to https://webhook.site/ to get a webhook URL.  # Create a webhook that fires on project updates to the URL you'll get from the website above.   # Make a project category update via a REST call. For instance:   {code:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}  curl -k -X PUT -u username:password https://jira.example.com/rest/api/2/project/SCRTEST -d '{""""categoryId"""": 10100}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -v    h3. Notes  This issue will occur if the project category is changed by a Jira add-on such as [ira Command Line Interface (CLI)|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/6398/jira-command-line-interface-cli] or the [Run CLI Actions in Jira|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210941/run-cli-actions-in-jira]  h3. Workaround  Change the project category from the UI.   """

summary: "Ability to add additional columns when viewing 'Attachments' in list view"
description: """h3. Summary    When viewing 'Attachments' in list view, there are three columns which are 'file name', 'file size' and 'file upload date and time'. We would like for the ability to add different columns such as 'labels' for these attachments to be easily categorized.     """

summary: "Problem to upgrade Script Runner from 5.4.49 to 5.4.49-jira8"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When upgrading to Jira 8.x, it's not possible to upgrade Script Runner to its latest version by clicking on:  * Update  * Manually updating the app (i.e Specifying the URI of the download version or uploading the plugin from your computer)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Ensure you're using Jira 8 with Script Runner 5.4.49 or any other previous version   # Clicking on update through the """"Manage apps"""" page  #* If the instance has no access to the Internet, you can upload the app manually  # Jira confirms that Script Runner upgrade has been done  # Upon refreshing the page, the app was not updated correctly and it's still showing that an upgrade for this particular app is required    h3. Workaround    Uninstall the app, and install it again (this procedure won't erase your Script Runner data)"""

I reckon this is to denote that upgrading to a higher version (e.g. 5.4.49 -> 5.5.0) will be successful whereas upgrading to the same version with a _jira8_ suffix (e.g. 5.4.49 -> 5.4.49-jira8 or 5.5.0 -> 5.5.0-jira8) will not. Do I interpret it right? Or will upgrading to the same version with a _jira8_ suffix work correctly from version 5.5.0 onwards?    Cheers,    Pawel, Jira Server Bugmaster" [SEP] "It depends on if you are on Jira 7 or Jira 8 what version you upgrade to: https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/6820/scriptrunner-for-jira/version-history    All versions including 5.5.0 and above have this fix. " [SEP] "Hi all,    I'm resolving the issue as fixed. Even though upgrade from 5.4.49 to 5.4.49-jira8 is still affected, the problem has been addressed in ScriptRunner 5.5.0 and above.    Cheers,    Pawel Drygas, Jira Server Bugm

summary: "Global Issue Detail View"
description: """Using Jira as a relatively small company makes it very often more complicated to configure a lot of things. It would be a nice feature if this could be applied on a global scale instead of having different settings for each project."""

summary: "Allow search feature to ignore accent"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    We have an issue here with JIRA that prevent us to adopt it fully in our company .    In the French language, we use accents, such as é,à,é,etc.. and we need Confluence to ignore those accents for searchs, like google does.    For instance the search text: """"majore"""", should give us pages with """"majoré"""" or """"majore"""" or """"majorè""""    h3. Suggested Solution     It would be very useful if we could configure the search feature to be able to ignore accents.  """

[SEP] "This not only affects 8.x but also 7.x (maybe all versions up to now)." Can we go ahead and implement this feature ?" [SEP] "Reminder of french accented letters and what letter they should be associated to.  (search should work in both direction. Searching ""même"", ""mémé"" or ""meme"" should give the same results)       |*Accented versions*|*Base letter*|  |À à  â Ä ä|a|  |È è É é Ê ê Ë ë|e|  |Î î Ï ï|i|  |Ô ô|o|  |Ù ù Û û Ü ü|u|  |Ÿ ÿ|y|  |Ç ç|c|  |Æ æ|ae|  |Œ œ|oe|"

summary: "MySQL 5.7 running utf8mb4_bin throws Health Check error"
description: """h3. Summary    With https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-36135 we began officially supporting the MySQL 5.7 with the {{utf8mb4}} collation.    The documentation can be found here: [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL 5.7|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7-966063305.html]    However, using this supported platform in Jira 8 still prompts the following Health Check warning:        h3. Environment  * Jira 8.0   * MySQL 5.7 using {{utf8mb4_bin}} as outlined in [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL 5.7|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7-966063305.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Jira 8  # Setup MySQL 5.7 as outlined in [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL 5.7|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7-966063305.html]    h3. Expected Results  Jira 8 runs using the supported database collation without errors.    h3. Actual Results  The following error is thrown on startup:        Screenshot:     !health check error.png|thumbnail!     h3. Fix  To resolve the issue, you can run {{config.sh/bat}} to update your {{dbconfig.xml}}, or manually change {{database-type}} from {{mysql}} to {{mysql57}} in your {{dbconfig.xml}} file.    This is discussed in [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL 5.7|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-5-7-966063305.html#ConnectingJiraapplicationstoMySQL5.7-Knownissues]    (i) Only works in JIRA 8."""

summary: "Improve Documentation about running Jira Server on Azure/Azure SQL"
description: """Jira Server 7.5 and above are currently supported in the Azure platform as well as the Azure SQL database, but there's no specific documentation about what configuration/template is best.    There's this document: https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/getting-started-with-jira-data-center-on-azure-938846970.html, but it was created towards Data center instances.    The request is to create a document specific to Jira Server instances telling what configuration is best to be used in Azure(e.g. database throughput speed)."""

summary: "Project listing is not alphabetically ordered while sharing filters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The list of projects while sharing filters is ordered (what looks like) as per ASCII values and not alphabetically.  Listing the projects while creating an issue orders the projects alphabetically.    h3. Environment  * 7.6.9  * 7.13.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create projects with names similar to *""""Android"""", """"Blackhole"""", """"absolute"""", """"andromeda""""*   # Try to share a filter to projects and click on the dropdown list      h3. Expected Results  The list of projects should be Alphabetical order:    """"absolute""""  """"Android""""  """"andromeda""""  """"Blackhole""""    h3. Actual Results  The listing will be according to ASCII value of the character strings:  Android  Blackhole  absolute  andromeda    h3. Notes  This could be considered an improvement as well but since the behaviour is not consistent across the product we consider this a bug      h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

description: """h3. Summary  In some rare cases, preview image attachment macro in issue description open link to the wrong image, while renders the correct image.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Not clear, at the time of reporting, JRASERVER-65890 has this problem.   See:    !JRASERVER-68923_image.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The wrong link is displayed   * Based on JRASERVER-65890, it's {{/secure/attachment/341192/341192_jmx_comparing_sample_page_img001.png}}     h3. Notes  html code      h3.Workaround  None"""

summary: "Order of subtasks in an issue should correspond to order of subtasks on the board"
description: """Currently, Jira seems to be using two different sort indexes for subtasks: the order within the parent task (seen in the subtasks list when the parent issue is opened) and the """"Rank"""" on the board.    This is quite confusing and forces us to always reorder issues twice, within the parent issue and on the board.    Therefore, Jira should use the same order of subtasks in both views. If this is difficult due to it being a breaking change, either add a board configuration option to do this or at least add a button to explicitly perform the synchronization within the issue (e.g., """"adjust rank on agile boards according to subtask order"""")."""

summary: "Moving multiple subtasks in a bulk operation should preserve their relative order"
description: """When I move multiple subtasks at once using a bulk operation, they'll be reordered within the destination parent issue. This is problematic because the order is usually relevant, indicating the order in which they should be implemented, and this order is still valid when the subtasks are moved to another issue.    Therefore, the bulk operation should preserve the relative order of the subtasks in the destination issue."""
comments: "The re-shuffling of subtasks really annoys me a lot, and very often. Is there any response to this suggestion?"

summary: "Batched notifications does not support custom events"
description: """Adding a custom event described on [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/adding-a-custom-event-861253643.html] causes batched notifications to ignore event resulting in no e-mail send on transition with configured custom event.    Also these events are not handled right now:    public static final Long ISSUE_WORKSTARTED_ID = 11L;  public static final Long ISSUE_WORKSTOPPED_ID = 12L;    from what I can see they are used in """"Jira default workflow"""" firing events on some transitions         *Workaround:*    Turn off batched email notifications and use old email notifications    [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-email-notifications-938847633.html]     """
comments: "Hi, when will Version 8.1.1 be made available?" [SEP] "|  |I am using Jira Software 8.2.1 but the changes of a ticket are only visible for listeners of a generic event, not for a custom event. Listeners of the custom event recieve an email without changes.     Will this be fixed in further versions so that custom events will be included in batched notifications? |  |"

summary: "Unable to reset OAuth settings for DVCS Accounts"
description: """h3. Summary    Unable to reset OAuth settings for a DVCS account (github or bitbucket) when there are multiple DVCS accounts present  h3. Environment    JIRA 7.x - JIRA 8.0.1 linked to *multiple* github and / or bitbucket accounts  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create at least two OAuth applications in GitHub and / or Bitbucket. Take a note of the Client IDs and Security Keys for the created accounts   # Link JIRA to these applications via JIRA -> DVCS Accounts -> Link Bitbucket Cloud or GitHub account   # On the latest configured account in JIRA -> DVCS Accounts select tripple dots menu to the right of the account and select """"Reset OAuth Settings"""", """"Configure OAuth for account [accountname]"""" should come up - take a note of the current key and authorizing account    h3. Expected Results    JIRA should display the correct """"Current Key"""" value and """"Authorizing account"""" and should allow to reset the account token  h3. Actual Results    JIRA shows """"Current Key"""" and """"Authorizing account"""" value of the very first DVCS account configured which is incorrect (and it actually shows the same key and authorizing account across all other DVCS accounts in JIRA) As a result, unable to reset the OAuth settings as per the ticket summary.   When trying to Edit the settings (i.e. click EDIT on the Configure OAuth for account [accountname]"""" and proceeding to change the Key and Secret, and then hitting """"Regenerate Access Token"""" - JIRA comes up with """"OAuth key must not be blank"""" value even though the values are filled in  h3. Notes    This happens only when multiple DVCS accounts are present in JIRA. When there's only one DVCS account configured, there's no issue  h3. Workaround I    Delete the DVCS account that requires token renewal in JIRA and re-add it    h3. Workaround II    * Identify your organization ID by hitting https://<base-url>/rest/bitbucket/1.0/repositories. You're looking for an organizationId value that matches the organization you want, which you can tell from the repositoryUrl value.  * Using Postman or curl, make a x-www-form-urlencoded POST to https://<base-url>/rest/bitbucket/1.0/org/<org-id>/oauth, passing the parameters  {panel}  organizationId: <org-id>  key: <key>  secret: <secret>  {panel}  * If you want, you can confirm this worked by running SELECT * FROM AO_E8B6CC_ORGANIZATION_MAPPING in your DB and confirming that the values are in place (directly modifying this table is likely another route to solving this problem, but would likely require a restart and the next step to take effect).  * In your browser, hit https://<base-url>/secure/admin/RegenerateGithubEnterpriseOauthToken.jspa?organization=<org-id>, click Retry if prompted, then authorize the app on the GHE side.    """
comments: "This bug just struck me after following the instructions linked in the ""Action required"" message, which links to [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/upgrading-the-jira-dvcs-connector-plugin-967900869.html]:    !image-2019-07-23-14-35-42-130.png!    I followed the workaround but it is still synchronizing after more than half an hour. We have around ~120 repositories, now about a quarter of our repositories are at less than 200 commits synchronized.    _Edit 2019-08-02:_ In total, it took 3.5 hours for 49k Commits." [SEP] "This issue is causing an outage for the DVCS connector for our company currently. Our only option is to delete and remove an account with ~40 repositories, triggering another full sync on some 500k+ commit repos, which causes a ton of pressure on our GHE instance." [SEP] "Figured out a better workaround from this bug by examining what was happening in the handshake via a HAR. All of this has only been tested with Github Enterprise and Jira Data Center 7.13.3, YMMV on other platforms.

summary: "activity stream - relative date filters"
description: """hi,    please enable activity streams to support relative date filters (just like STRM-1804).    Absolute date filters doesn't make any sense.         thanks in advance,    gerald Weber"""

summary: "Bulk edit 'Change Fix Version/s' is not available for issues in different project"
description: """h4. Issue   * Currently, when we bulk edit 'Change Fix Version/s' for different issues in the different project, the 'Change Fix Version/s' shows in the 'Unavailable Actions' at the bottom of the 'Step 3 of 4: Operation Details' page due to 'NOTE: This operation can be performed only on issues from ONE project.'   !fixversions.png|thumbnail!    h4. Feature request   * We understand that fix versions are local to projects, but in some customer's configuration, all different projects have exactly the same fix versions. We would love to be able to bulk edit all the issues in different projects.     h4. Workaround    * Bulk edit issues from one project at a time. """

summary: "When a project changes name/key etc any filters used in that project should be updated as well."
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently if you change a key of a project or name filters will no longer work. Filters should be updated when a key changes or project name.  h3. Suggested Solution  Filters should be stored with the project IDs and their queries should be translated to project keys/names.   Any other solution that would guarantee all filters are accurate after a project key/name change is acceptable.   h3. Workaround  # Identify the filters that need an update before making the project key/name change and store their IDs.   # Once the project key/name change is done, run a SQL to replace the old project key with the new one in all the filters saved from the previous step.   """

summary: "Upgrading Jira Manual documentation had incorrect mysql jdbc driver recommendation"
description: """In the page [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/upgrading-jira-server-manual-938846939.html]     Has the following recommendation:  {quote}  h2. Install the database driver    *If you're using an Oracle or MySQL database*, download a new JDBC driver. For other databases, you can omit this step.   If the driver is up to date, you can also copy it from your previous version.   # Download one of the following drivers:     *    ** *Oracle:* JDBC driver 12.2.0.1.   ** *MySQL:* {color:#de350b}the latest JDBC driver.{color}     # Place it in {{<installation-directory>/lib}}.{quote}   However Jira can't currently use the latest mysql jdbc driver, which is version 8.  Instead Jira needs to be using the latest 5.1 driver as explained in both the supported platforms doc and the Connecting Jira to mysql documentation.       When users follow this guide and install the 8.0 version, Jira can't correctly connect to these database types."""

summary: """Remaining"" should recalculate automatically based on logged work and Original Estimate"
description: """As a user, """"Remaining"""" should recalculate automatically based on logged work and Original Estimate.    Current behaviour, when manually modifying the """"Estimate"""" field, the """"Remaining"""" remains the same.    This can cause some problems, for example, If someone incorrectly inputs the estimated amount of time for an issues and makes a mistake, they have to update the estimated time and then manually recalculate the remaining time."""
comments: "This is assuming the original estimate was 100% correct on the amount of time the task would actually consume.  Remaining time should be it's own value that is based on the remaining effort.  This will result in valid burndown views and more accurate projections of completion dates."

summary: "External System CSV Import report is not accurate"
description: """h3. Summary    Jira importer allows importing issues from different systems. During the import process, the import may fail to create some issues for various reasons such as missing statuses, post-function, etc. After the import is finished, Jira displays post-import detailed logs to show the number of issues that were imported successfully and the number of those failed.    In certain cases, the logs report inaccurate results in the number of issues that have been created and the issues that need to be re-indexed.    For example, the below logs are for an import of two issues, with only *one* issue that was successfully created:    Here is the corresponding screenshot:    !image-2019-02-23-00-29-44-100.png|width=98,height=71!    In the above logs, the report shows that 2 issues were created and no further issues need to be re-indexed. However, only one issue was created successfully as the message in the UI indicates.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a CSV import with a mix of issues that are guaranteed to succeed and some which are guaranteed to fail   # Run the external CSV import wizard   # After the import is complete, click on 'download a detailed log'   # The logs will show that all issues have been created but only the correct ones are indexed    In some other cases, the issues are successfully created but not re-indexed. In this case a manual re-index is required for the issues to become searchable.   h3. Expected Results    Jira will always show the correct results of the issues that were successfully created, imported or the issues that needs to be re-indexed  h3. Actual Results    A discrepancy is shown in the import logs with inaccurate number of the issues that have been created or those that required a re-index.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available. It is important however, to do a manual re-index after doing the import to see which tickets have been successfully imported."""
comments: """It is important however, to do a manual re-index after doing the import to see which tickets have been successfully imported."" I think that all issues that are successfully created are reindexed during the import. No need to reindex that I can think of" [SEP] "Matt Doar - thats not true. I am the reporter, and if you got a post function in create transition, and that post function from whatever reason fail, issues are imported, but not indexed." [SEP] "I thought that if a custom post function failed, then the other post functions still ran, including the one to index the new issue. What kind of post function did you have that was failing?" [SEP] "Nope, once you got post function ending with   ERROR  state, the other post functions are not done, including re-index one.    And, there is no rollback as well.     I founded that behavior with post function included ""self transition if condition is true"" by innovalog jmwe. But obviously it doesn't matter what post function are you using, point is that fail

summary: "Labels and Select List (single choice) values are not rendered correctly in Windows."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding a value to a custom field that uses the type 'Select List (single choice)' and 'Label', the values added are not rendered correctly and is partially cut off in the bottom. For example, when selecting 'yes', it will appear as 'ves'. This issue is also apparent in the 'Labels' field in the View Screen.    This issue is apparent in all Create/Edit/View screen.  h3. Environment   * Tested in all Browsers (Firefox, IE, Chrome)   * Jira 7.13, 8.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field 'Test Option' with the type 'Select List (single choice)'. Add options such as 'yes' and 'no'. Make it appear in all screens.   # Create a custom field 'Test Label'. Make it appear in all screens.   # Click Create button on the top navigation bar.    # Choose the option 'yes' for 'Test Option' and add a label 'NewBug'.   # Observe the fields in View and Edit screen.    h3. Expected Results    The value of the fields will render correctly.  h3. Actual Results    The value of the fields are not rendered correctly.  h3. Workaround    There is currently no workaround."""
comments: "I wonder that wrong and misleading representation of data is priority ""Minor"".    May be Atlassian underestimates the relevance of proper information to the processes of their customers." [SEP] "Temporary workaround: inject the following code via the announcement banner:    {code:html}<style>select.select {padding: 0 5px !important;}</style>{code}"

summary: "NIce To Have | Dashboard View | Card | Add latest action word on card with data time below"
description: """When viewing the Jira Project's Dashboard, it would be nice to see the cards have a notification word on the top right hand side.         See [4 seconds recording|[http://recordit.co/KnQvRmhnvN].]     Same scenarios:   # When a new ticket is *created* and added to the Project's Dashboard, the ticket card displays {color:#00875a}*""""NEW""""*{color} on the top right hand corner of the card with a date time signature below it.   # When a newly created ticket's *STATUS* is *updated*, the ticket card displays the new STATUS ( In Progress, Done, etc., ) in {color:#FF0000}*bold red*{color} for at least 24 hours with a date time signature below it.   # When a newly created ticket's *text* (any section e..g., Summary, Description, Comment) is *updated*, the ticket card displays the new change ( e..g., Summary In Description, etc.,) in {color:#0747a6}*blue bold*{color} bold for at least 24 hours with a data time signature below it.    Thanks Atlassian for reading."""

summary: "Jira 8.0 Oracle 12c driver listed as 2 different versions on Supported Platforms vs. Manual Upgrade"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There are two different driver versions listed in the public documentation for Oracle 12c databases for Jira 8.0.  h3. Environment    Jira 8.0 public documentation    *Supported Platforms* [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html]    *Upgrading Manually* [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/upgrading-jira-server-manual-938846939.html]   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Check the supported Oracle driver on the Supported Platforms page - it lists 12.2.0.1 in the Oracle database section.   # Check the supported Oracle driver on the Upgrading Manually page - it lists 12.1.0.1 in the Installing driver section.    h3. Expected Results    Both documents should list the same driver version.   h3. Actual Results    The documents list different versions.   h3. Workaround    Currently there is conflicting information in the public support pages.  Once the correct driver is established, the public pages should be updated and this ticket will be closed. """

summary: "Allow only certain issue types in Epic link"
description: """Currently, when you create an issue within an Epic, you can choose among all issue types in the project. The only way to limit this is to remove the issue type altogether from the project.    The suggestion is to make it possible for the admin to allow only certain issue types link in the epic."""

summary: "Extended project administration can see add and remove hidden fields on screen configurations"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Extended project admins under the Screen configuration have the ability to See, Add, and Remove hidden fields from a screen, and are unable to configure the field to be visible and are also given no indication that the filed is hidden Due to the [limitations of Extended project admins | https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/managing-project-permissions-938847145.html#Managingprojectpermissions-Extendedprojectpermissions], so once a hidden field is added to a screen it will not show up on that screen, causing counter intuitive behavior      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a hidden field  # log in as a user with Extended project admin  # go to Project Settings >> Screens >> Edit a screen  # select the dropdown to Add a field to the screen    h3. Expected Results  Either the Hidden field is not listed as the Extended Admin cannot configure this field OR flagged as a hidden field to indicate that it will not be visible once added to the screen    h3. Actual Results  the Hidden Field appears as if it were any other field, and can be added or removed from the screen    h3.Note:  This also allows a Extended project administrator to remove Fields from a screen that were configured to not be visible by the global admin and could break intended behavior from add-ons relying on the field being present such as post functions, conditions or validators that rely on that field to either update the value or validate a value is present.    h3. Workaround  The only indication that this field is hidden is that once added to the screen configuration it will not display on the actual screen, you must have a Global Admin log in to either show the field or make changes to the configurations"""

summary: "Specifying a delimiter other than comma in the CSV import Configuration File does not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira allows users to specify the delimiter of the CSV file during the CSV import. However, when using a CSV import configuration file which has a delimiter other than comma ','; Jira fails to recognize it.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a CSV Import Configuration file or amend an existing one to have a delimiter other than comma   # Go to *Administration > System > External System Import* and select CSV   # Select a CSV file and the configuration file above for the import   # Begin the import    h3. Expected Results    Jira will recognize the delimiter specified in the configuration file (i.e. under config.delimiter field in the configuration JSON file)    h3. Actual Results    Jira does not recognize the delimiter from the file. It always gives precedence to the comma ',' delimiter specified by default in the UI.  h3. Workaround    Always specify the delimiter at the UI."""
comments: "I'm not sure why it's useful to use a separator other than a comma in CSV files. Anyone?"

summary: "System backup of Jira Server v8.0.1 is not valid for Jira Cloud's import process"
description: """{panel:title=Bug Fix Policy|titleBGColor=#e0efff}  (i)  At the moment, we can't set an ETA for the fix to be released, since there's a number of factors that determine how our product team prioritizes bugfixes.  You can learn more by reading [Atlassian Server Bug Fixing Policy|https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-server-bug-fix-policy-201294573.html].  {panel}  h3. Summary    System backup exported on v8.0.1 complains an error on Jira Cloud's import process.  h3. Environment   * Jira Server 8.0.1#800009-sha1:f5186e157f3e26c0180673ce49d51cb56d8f7579    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a sample project on Jira Server   # Go to [http://EXAMPLE.COM/secure/admin/XmlBackup!default.jspa|http://example.com/secure/admin/XmlBackup!default.jspa] and export backup   # Go to [https://EXAMPLE.atlassian.net/secure/admin/CloudImport!start.jspa|https://example.atlassian.net/secure/admin/CloudImport!start.jspa] and try import at """"Restore system""""    h3. Expected Results    System backup on Jira Server v8.x can import to Jira Cloud.  h3. Actual Results    The below error is shown in the application UI on Jira Cloud:  {quote}There was a problem configuring the database for import. Please contact Atlassian support. org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: column """"pkey"""" contains null values  {quote}  After the import error, the application shows the error below and all the functionality become unavailable except trying import again:  {quote}Jira is locked for import but no import could be found. This is likely to be a temporary issue and should clear up within one minute. If you continue to see this error, please contact support.  {quote}  !Screenshot 2019-02-21 20.07.53.png|thumbnail! !Screenshot 2019-02-21 20.16.55.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    Reindex and data integrity checker don't solve the problem.  h3. Workaround    None.  Consider adding yourself as a watcher to be kept informed as to the state of the bug moving forward. With that way, if our development team updates the ticket, you'll be notified via email."""

summary: "Calling IndexManager.isIndexConsistent() while a foreground reindex is running may lead to corrupted index"
description: """h3. Summary  Repeated calls to {{IndexManager.isIndexConsistent()}} while a foreground reindex is running may lead to corrupted index.    Note, that in Jira 7.x the API method that was responsible for determination of whether index is available, {{IndexManager.isIndexAvailable()}}, is defective. See JRASERVER-68900.    This problem was reported and reproduced on Jira 7.6, at the same time we couldn't reproduce it on Jira EAP 8. That being said, Jira 8 and corresponding Jira 7.x versions contain the proper fix.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: " Checking IndexManager.isIndexAvailable() returns true while foreground reindex is in progress"
description: """h3. Summary  In Jira API, calling {{IndexManager.isIndexAvailable()}} returns {{true}} while the node runs foreground indexing. The intended function of the API method is as per the documentation:  {quote}Whether this index is available. The index is not available if the index is being rebuilt or recovered. In a clustered environment this reflects only the state on the local node.  {quote}    h3. Expected result    This call returns {{false}} if index is during index recovery or foreground index is being rebuilt on the local node. This call returns {{true}} if index is healthy, or background indexing is underway.  h3. Actual result    This call returns {{false}} if index is during index recovery. This call returns {{true}} if index is healthy, foreground or background is underway.    h3. Note  * Although fix for this problem will be available in future 7.6 and 7.13 versions, vendors building plugins to be compatible across the entire range of 7.x versions are advised not to lean on this method.  * This problem does not affect Jira 8."""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some shortcut keys don't work correctly on editor with *Visual* mode.    h3. Environment    Tested with the following browsers  - Chrome : Version 72.0.3626.81 (Official Build) (64-bit)  - Firefox : 65.0 (64 bit)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Please just pressing the short cut keys listed below in the editor.    h3. Expected Results    The following short cut keys work correctly.    - Command+Shift+S or Ctrl+K : Strikethrough  - Command+K or Ctrl+K : Link  - Command+B or Ctrl+B : Bold   - Command+I or Ctrl+I : Italic  - Command+U or Ctrl+U : Underline  - Command+Shift+B or Ctrl+Shift+B : Bullet list  - Command+Shift+N or Ctrl+Shift+N : Numbered list    h3. Actual Results    Some of the shortcuts are not working correctly with Visual mode.    ||Shortcut||What it does?||Works for Visual mode?||  | Ctrl+Shift+S      command+shift+S  |Strikethrough|(x)|  | Ctrl+K      Command+K  |Link|(x)|  | Ctrl+B      Command+B|Bold|(/)|  | Ctrl+I      Command+I|Italic|(/)|  | Ctrl+U      Command+U  |Underline|(/)|  | Ctrl+Shift+B      Command+Shift+B|Bullet list|(x)|  | Ctrl+Shift+N      Command+Shift+N|Numbered list|(x)|    h3. I'm surprised there's been so little interest in this bug - isn't everyone using bullet points to list scope, requirements, test scenarios, assumptions, etc. in the _Description_ field? summary: "Amazon AWS JIRA Data Center Template Outdated"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  At the moment, there is a difference between the Quickstart template in the Official AWS Template guide and Atlassian's own Bitbucket Repository.    For the Quickstart from the AWS side we can refer to the link below:  - https://aws-quickstart.s3.amazonaws.com/quickstart-atlassian-jira/doc/jira-software-data-center-on-the-aws-cloud.pdf    While for Atlassian:  - https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-jira-data-center-on-aws-938846968.html    Highlighting the part regarding SSL:  - Amazon Template = SSL Certificate Name  - Atlassian = SSL Certificate ARN    Then, using the template from the Official Amazon Template, it would not have the option to set the SSL Certificate ARN. *But*, the option is available when using the template from the Bitbucket Template:  - https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment/src/master/quickstarts/  (i) quickstart-jira-master.template.yaml    h3. Suggested Solution  To have the template updated on the Official Amazon Page or have more information about the two locations of the template itself"""

Environment  JIRA 7.13.1, JIRA 8.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Upload an image attachment to one of the issue ticket  # Include the attachment image in the description  # Try to break the image by deleting it from *JIRA Home Directory\data\attachments*  # Refresh the issue ticket and you should see a broken image as shown below:-      h3. Expected Results  The broken image should show tooltip or a URL to the broken image.   !link.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  JIRA showing only a broken image without tooltip   !JIRA8.0.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Right click on the broken icon/image, open up inspect element and it should show in the source"""

summary: "Alternate JQL Creation for Multiple Words in Quick Search Box"
description: """Currently if you type multiple words into a Quick Search Box, the JQL generated is _text ~ """"foo bar""""._  I would like a mechanism whereby if I type multiple words into a Quick Search Box, the JQL generated is _text ~ """"foo"""" and text ~ """"bar""""._    This would allow the results to find the cases where one of the words is in summary, and the other word is in description or comment.  At the moment, a result is returned if both words are in summary, or both words are in description or both words are in comment.    You can think of this as equivalent to a google search that will return all the text on the individual case page.  At the moment """"foo bar"""" won't find case where foo is in summary and bar is in comment.    Whilst I can type the expanded JQL in Basic Search, that gets painful when you start having 4 or more terms.     For more detail, see this question https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/Multiple-Words-in-Quick-Search-box/qaq-p/997365"""

summary: "Identify the source of a change in an issue's history"
description: """*Background:*    Issue history shows changes that occur, along with the date/time and the user making the change.    At times it can be difficult to determine the exact source of the change (method used to make the change)    For example an issue can be transitioned in many ways:   * Transition button on issue   * Dragging issue on board   * JIRA Service Desk automation causing transition   * Third party plugin performing transition   * Rest API    *Requested Change:*    As an admin I would like to identify the source of a change I see in an issue's history.  * This would be useful when a change occurs and the user associated with that change is unsure of how it was performed (or claims they did not perform the change).    Example of how this could work:   * Ability to enable debug or trace logging for issue history (changeitem)    * Log a stacktrace when issue history is being updated. This stacktrace could be reviewed to determine the source of the change.   ** An improvement would be identify the lines that the admin needs, and only log those lines from the stracktrace   ** Additional improvement ideas:   *** Make this logging show in the UI   *** Capture this level of detail by default (instead of having to enable debug logging)"""

summary: "Remove the stale indexing Job associated with current node on startup"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, when Jira Datacente node is shutdown while running FullReindex task (see related JRASERVER-47045, JRASERVER-66204), there is no way to remove this task from the cluster. All other nodes still think the task is running even the node is down, so this prevents corresponding operations to be done.     h3. Suggested Solution  Add the start-up check which removes the stale indexing job. For a node which was shutdown ungracefully and then started again, it will clean up its own stale ForegroundReindex task.     h3. Implemented Solution  * During the start-up, the node will find its own stale re-index task and remove it.   * Jira is identifying reindex task by the _node-id_ - to make sure it can be cleaned, please keep the same _node-id_.   * If for some reason you cannot start the same node again (e.g. AWS instance got removed), we provide another way to remove the task from a cluster without a need to starting node again, see: JRASERVER-68616    h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "Hi,    how can I enable the ""start-up check""?          "

Click *Update*.   # Head to a project where userA receives notifications for, edit the component field to include 'A&W'.   # Observe the mail notification received by user.    h3. summary: "Separate user defined Tomcat/JVM/GC/Logging configuration from default ones specified in Jira"
description: """h3. Summary    Some of configuration of Jira is stored in Jira installation directory. In order to change Jira's configuration it is required to modify existing configuration files.    Because those files can change with every Jira release, after upgrade admin should reapply every change in those configuration separately. This is complicated process and it's common practice to just replace the new configuration with the one copied from old Jira's installation.    Splitting the user configuration out of existing configuration files would simplify the upgrade experience and make it less possible for Jira to be incorrectly configured after upgrade. """

summary: "User cannot delete an Issue Link he created to an issue from a project he cannot view"
description: """h3. Summary  As per [JRASERVER-61246|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-61246], a user can create a link from an issue of a project he has viewing and linking permissions to another issue in a project he *does not* have viewing and linking permissions. Then, he won't be able to delete that link he created himself because he does not have permission to view the other ticket.    The issue link appear under a *Non-Authorized Links* section, and the issue key is visible but it's title says _You are not authorized to view this issue_. The user cannot delete the link he created himself.    h3. Expected behaviour  As per [JRASERVER-61246|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-61246], the user either should not be able to create the link, or at least be able to delete it after creating it.    h3. Current behaviour  User cannot delete the link himself. It needs someone with permissions on both projects to delete it.    h3. Workaround  The Jira Administrator should have rights to delete the link if needed."""
comments: "+1" [SEP] "This is oddly inconsistent"

summary: "Email notification should be following event order"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JIRA Mail Queue sends notification in batches. When multiple events are created with a small timestamp difference, the notification sent out will be sent out in bulk without following the timestamp, hence making it possible to receive email notifications in a later timestamp before the email notifications in the earlier timestamp.    h3. Suggested Solution  Make JIRA Mail Queue to send out notifications in batches while still following the timestamp sequence accordingly.    h3. Workaround   None"""

summary: "Add GitHub webhooks during autodiscovery"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When Jira is connected to GitHub and """"Automatically sync all repositories"""" option is enabled, Jira create webhooks for existing repositories.    However, when new repositories are added to Github, Jira does not automatically create webhooks for the new repositories.      h3. Suggested Solution    Add option to Jira/GitHub integration to allow (re-)creation of webhooks, including, when new repositories are auto-discovered.      h3. Workaround     Follow workaround in [Slow Appearing Commits from BitBucket or GitHub in JIRA|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/slow-appearing-commits-from-bitbucket-or-github-in-jira-779160823.html] to manually create webhook for the newly added repositories."""

summary: "Not able to edit description when using panel with bgColor, e.g. bgColor=white"
description: """h3. Summary  It's not possible to edit the description field in the issue if the text contains a panel with """"bgColor"""" using the name of the color instead of the hexadecimal representation. This only happens if you edit the issue in the """"Visual"""" mode, on """"Text"""" mode it works fine.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create an issue.  # Add a panel in the description with the bgColor parameter like so:  # Save the description.  # Click on the description to edit again.  # Select visual mode.  # Change the description(without removing the panel or the bgColor).  # Save the edition.    h3. Expected behavior  The description is saved with the changes you've made.    h3. Actual behavior  The description doesn't change.    h3. Workaround  * Change the color name to its [hexadecimal representation|https://www.w3schools.com/colors/colors_picker.asp].  * Edit the comment using the text mode.    h3. Note  If you look in the payload of the """"/secure/AjaxIssueAction.jspa?decorator=none"""" POST call you can see that the description is sent without the changes made."""

summary: "Prevent the usage of shortcuts when executing a filter search"
description: """h3. Description  On instances with a lot of issues that, when searching for a filter, it might take a few seconds to load it all and if the user accidentally hits a shortcut like the letter *a* on his keyboard, which opens the {{Assign Issue to User}} popup, the user might become the assignee of an issue of the previous or current filter being searched by accident.    h3. Suggestion  Prevent shortcuts of being executed when loading filters."""
comments: "I don't think this is a feature request. I think this is a bug. The search option becomes available while the search is still going on. Thus allowing you to start the search. When typing in a search, the shortcut keys should not be active. It is a bug."

summary: "Having pagination option for configuration page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When a customer has a large number items listed in the configuration page the page takes > 60 seconds to load. This impacts configuration page such as:  * Workflow (<baseURL>/secure/admin/workflows/ListWorkflows.jspa)  * Custom Fields (<baseURL>/secure/admin/ViewCustomFields.jspa/)  * etc    For enterprise customer, it's common to have more than 1000 of fields, workflows, screens.      h3. Suggested Solution  If we could add pagination, we could limit the number of items to be displayed in one page so it wouldn't time out and it will load faster. This is addressing all configuration page in JIRA (screen, workflow, fields).    h3. Workaround   There's no workaround at the moment"""

summary: "Automatically append attachment to comment when attaching via screens"

summary: "File viewer plugin used by Jira to preview PDF cannot preview PDF files with certain embedded fonts"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    File viewer plugin used by Jira to preview PDF files cannot preview PDF files with certain embedded fonts. It expects the loca and glyf tables to decode the embedded fonts. At times some of the PDF files created by third party vendors do not provide these tables and expect the PDF reader to be able to decode them. The same file can be previewed successfully by acrobat PDF viewer and firefox or chrome which uses OpenType Sanitizer for decoding fonts.    The PDF which cannot be previewed had MicrosoftYaheiUI as the embedded font with   Truetype (CID) and identity-H encoding. It errors out with       h3. Workaround  Download the file and preview it with acrobat reader or default browser"""

You can also us an absoulute path like this:    The path in this case should use the 8 character form, the one without spaces.     * Once the JRE_HOME Path is set and you are still in the installation directory: *C:\Program Files\Atlassian\JIRA*, run the service command as below:      This should allow the service to be installed."""

summary: "Installing JIRA 8 as a Windows Service defaults -xms to 256MB instead of the recommended 384MB"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When installing JIRA 8 as a Windows Service, the default memory allocation value is -Xms256m and -Xmx2048m. However, in the startup logs, we can see a warning message that indicates the following:  {quote}  The initial memory allocation pool (-Xms) is recommended to be at least 384m.  {quote}    h3. Environment  * Windows    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Download the JIRA 8 installer  # Install JIRA as a Service.  # Start the application and review the startup sequence logs.    h3. Expected Results  # The default memory allocation should be set to -xms 384MB instead of 256MB.  # No warning appear on the logs.    h3. Actual Results  # The default memory allocation is set to -xms 256MB.  # The following warning appears:      h3. Workaround  * Update the memory parameters to have at least 384MB of xms as per [Increasing Jira application memory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/increasing-jira-application-memory-938847654.html]"""

summary: "Keep the 0 unit in decimal place of Numeric field types with whole tens at the end"
description: """h3. Description  The variation between how the numeric value differs the decimal place between a comma and a dot depends on the Language Pack selected at the Default Language configuration at the System Administration page of Jira. However, there is an issue when using number that end with a whole number at the decimal units, Jira eats the 0 (zero).    *Example in English language*  User input: 7.90  After saving: 7.9    User input: 7.92  After saving: 7.92    *Example in Portuguese language*  User input: 104846,40  After saving: 104.846,4    User input: 104846,43  After saving: 104.846,43    As per [this article on Significant Zeroes|https://www.physics.uoguelph.ca/tutorials/sig_fig/SIG_dig.htm]:  {quote}Zeroes placed after other digits but behind a decimal point are significant; 7.90 has three significant digits.{quote}    From my perspective, Jira shouldn't be """"eating"""" the decimal 0 (zero) on whole numbers and that cause issues to customers from other countries that use Languages where it is important to have the whole decimal number displayed.    h3. Workaround  There is no current known workaround to be applied on this situation. If you find one, please leave a comment with the steps to achieve it."""
comments: "Por gentileza, resolvam esse problema na casa decimal, está causando problemas na contabilidade de algumas aplicações desenvolvida no JIRA."

summary: "Some issue menu's sub-items are not accessible and are not readable when using screen readers"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When browsing Jira, not all sub-menus are accessible using keyboard, and thus are not readable by the screen readers.  h3. Environment   * Jira 7.6.11, 7.13.1, or some of the earlier versions   * Any modern browser (tested on Edge, Firefox and Chrome)   * NVDA 2018.4.1 (but will also be reproducible with older versions, as it's an issue with Jira's navigation, not with the screen reader)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Head to the issue.   # Press 'H' until you reach the Summary heading.   # Using the 'K' button on the keyboard, head to the next link until you reach the 'More' button. Enter Focus Mode by pressing NVDA+Space. (NVDA is the insert button by default)   # Press 'Enter', then navigate to the sub-menu using the keyboard.   # You are unable to navigate the submenu properly using just the arrow keys - the furthest you can go is until the """"Attach files"""" item.    h3. Expected Results    All menu items will be accessible and readable by the screen reader.  h3. Actual Results    Not all menu items are accessible using just the arrow keys.  h3. Workaround    One has to jump to the end of the virtual buffer to access the menu items after """"Attach files"""".  Those items will be accessible by keyboard, *but will not be read properly*."""
