summary: "Jira duplicates REST resource root path in WADL "
description: """h3. Summary    The {{application.wadl}} file provided by the Jira REST API duplicates the API path in both the resource base and the resource path, leading to an invalid URL.    This also breaks integration with the [Atlassian REST API Browser|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211542/atlassian-rest-api-browser] plugin making it provide wrong REST resource URLs.  h3. Steps to reproduce   # Log in to a Jira instance running Jira 8.0 or above   # Access <JIRA_URL>/rest/api/2/application.wadl (in case you are checking from the browser and you are seeing JSON instead of XML, right click and select """"View page source"""")    h3. Expected result    {{<ns2:resource>}} elements don't contain """"{{api/2/}}"""" prefix, as it used to be in previous Jira versions (Checked Jira 7.12.0)   e.g.    h3. Actual result    {{<ns2:resource>}} elements contains """"{{api/2/}}"""" prefix   e.g.    h3. Notes    The duplicated """"{{api/2/}}"""" prefix breaks integration with [Atlassian REST API Browser|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211542/atlassian-rest-api-browser] plugin making it provide wrong REST resource URLs.  h3. Workaround    For applications that rely on the WADL and do not function correctly (such as the Atlassian REST API Browser) it might be possible to rewrite the URLs. The following workaround has been tested in Jira 8.2.1 with the most recent version of the Atlassian Rest API Browser:   # Shut down Jira, and make a backup of {{$jira-install/conf/server.xml}}   # Add the following to {{$jira-install/conf/server.xml}}, just before the closing {{</Host>}} tag:       # Create a new file at {{$jira-install/conf/Catalina/localhost/rewrite.config}} with the following contents:    (!) Note: The path to the rewrite config file will depend on your server.xml. The folder path will be created - so you can use whatever folders have been created under {{$jira-install/conf}}.   (!) Note: If you're using a context path, make sure to include that in the {{rewrite.config}} file - both in the rule, and the rewrite section as well, before {{/rest}}     # Restart Jira    While this workaround doesn't affect the incorrect display in the WADL file, it will ensure that any requests to the REST API will be redirected correctly to the same API; and allowing integrations using the WADL to function correctly (such as the REST API browser)."""
comments: "This also seems to affect Confluence Server.     A large number of endpoints show up in the REST API browser that are invalid.  The WADL providing the endpoints is affected by this same issue.      !image-2019-05-09-15-23-21-333.png|width=535,height=522!" [SEP] "Atlassians, please fix this.  The REST API Browser is an _extremely_ valuable tool, being unable to use it _really_ handicaps us severely. " [SEP] "Hi [~juergen.lanner499876916],    Dave from Atlassian Support here! As a part of making sure our bugs have valid workarounds and things on them, I was able to come up with a workaround that would redirect requests to a malformed API to function correctly. You'll find it in the bug report here; but the gist of it is using a Rewrite rule at the Tomcat level to redirect /rest/api/x/api/x to /rest/api/x. I tested this locally, and restored the functionality of the REST API browser using this workaround. It's not ideal, but it should make things a little less painful while waiting for a fix. Let me know ho

The script below might help:  {code:none|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}  #!/bin/bash  USERNAME=""""admin""""  PASSWORD=""""admin""""  BASE_URL=""""http://localhost:8080""""  URL=""""$BASE_URL/rest/api/2/issue/""""  KEY=""""TWO""""  AMOUNT=25  for i in $(seq $AMOUNT)  do    COUNTRY=$(shuf -n1 -e 'Malaysia' 'Indonesia' 'Hong Kong' 'China' 'Thailand')    DATA=""""{ \""""fields\"""": { \""""project\"""": { \""""key\"""": \""""$KEY\"""" }, \""""summary\"""": \""""Task #$i\"""", \""""description\"""": \""""Task #$i \"""", \""""customfield_10007\"""": [{ \""""value\"""":\""""$COUNTRY\"""" }], \""""issuetype\"""": { \""""name\"""": \""""Task\"""" } } }""""    curl -u $USERNAME:$PASSWORD -X POST --data """"$DATA"""" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' $URL    echo  done      h3. Expected Results  All issues are sorted by their value of *Country*, in alphabetical order.    h3. The suggestion is to include a Popup or notification in some way, either after installation of the first time an admin user logs in, where it is hinted that Service Desk/Software can be installed and tested for free without performing another installation, and maybe even adding a link to direct the customer to Versions & Licenses where they can see the """"Try me"""" buttons for the application that is not yet installed.    """

summary: "Jira should provide an API for querying data about its gadgets"
description: """Jira does not provide an API for rendering/querying the state of its internal gadgets. This could be useful for e.g. other products and plugin developers who would like to embed them within their apps. Currently, the only way to obtain info about these gadgets is to use Jira's internal API which hasn't been designed with this use case in mind. This may lead to issues such as https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-41311.       {panel:title=Previous bug description}  h5. Title: The Two Dimensional Filter Statistics Gadget REST api renders links using the request URL, rather than the configured base URL  h5. Steps to Reproduce   # Setup JIRA behind a reverse proxy, with an alternative access port   ** Assume that {{[http://localhost:8080/jira]}} is the unproxied URL   ** Assume that {{[http://localhost/jira]}} is the proxied address   ** The base URL is set to the unproxied URL until after the application link is created   # Setup Confluence   # Create an application link to {{localhost:8080/jira}}   # Change JIRA's base URL to the proxied address   # In the application link configuration, change JIRA's display URL to the proxied address   ** Communication will go from Confluence to JIRA over the unproxied port, but user facing links will use the display URL for hyperlinks.   # Insert a Two Dimensional Macro Chart Macro in Confluence    h5. Expected Results    The links in the chart link to the proxied address  h5. Actual Results    The links in the chart use the application URL instead. If that port is not accessible to users, this may result in links failing, or if the DNS does not resolve.  h5. Cause    This bug is the result of a problem in Jira's gadget REST api ([http://hostname:1990/rest/gadget/1.0/twodimensionalfilterstats/generate?filterId=jql-]), which incorrectly uses the request url as the base for links in the results, rather than Jira's configured baseUrl.    This means that any applicationLink requests made to this REST api would have the results rendered using the applicationLink's baseUrl, rather than the configured displayUrl.    The two dimensional charts macro can't fix this problem. Instead, this bug has to be fixed inside Jira's gadget REST api. Either make the links in the twodimentionalfilterstats rest api a relative link, or use the configured baseUrl as the base for the (absolute) links.  {panel}"""

summary: "upgrade.xml file not updated in newer releases and triggers upgrade on every restart"

Summary  The _upgrade.xml_ file is not being updated and as a result Jira triggers an upgrade ({{c.a.jira.upgrade.UpgradeScheduler}}) on each restart due to build number difference    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Jira 7.3.x   # Restart the instance   # Note the build number in the logs      # Compare this with the last line in {{$Jira_Installation_Directory/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes/upgrade.xml}}    (i) Build number 73011 refers to Jira 7.3.0 as per [Jira Build and Version Numbers Reference|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-build-and-version-numbers-reference-347341143.html]    h3. summary: "As an administrator, I don't want login failure counts to be incremented in an authorization failure scenario"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Presently, Jira doesn't distinguish between authentication and authorization failure in terms of incrementing the login failure count. In the latter case, this could lead to unexpected lockouts of LDAP accounts for users who aren't aware they don't have access to Jira, especially for environments with restrictive lockout policies.     In addition, locking accounts due to authorization failures can lead to administrative overhead in that these accounts will likely need to be manually unlocked.    h3. Suggested Solution  Don't increment the failed login count for authorization failures, only authentication failures.     h3. Notes  This _might_ have security implications, as changing authentication vs authorization behavior may lead bad actors to distinguish valid vs invalid usernames."""

Workaround: use a third party add-on like [Jira Enterprise Mail Handler|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/4832/enterprise-email-handler-for-jira-jemh?hosting=server&tab=overview] or [Email this issue|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/4977/email-this-issue?hosting=server&tab=overview] which seem to have options to deal with these scenarios."""

summary: "Custom Field Value cache can grow indefinitely"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} caches all Custom Field values related to specific Issue from {{customfieldvalue}} table in _request-scope_ cache.    This caching was introduced as a performance optimisation to speed up issue indexing.   Probably it also helps in ViewIssue, issue-nav's table view and sorting by custom field through a database.    However, currently this cache can grow indefinitely and this can cause high memory pressure.    h3. Suggested Solution  Introduce some limits to the cache size, I.e. 1/10/100 issues only    h3. Notes  In the following example, we have cached values for 62k issues. (each having ~50 cf values)  !cf-request-cache.png|width=362,height=302,thumbnail!    h3. Notes on fix  Copy from [focusedCommentId=1972882|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66555?focusedCommentId=1972882&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-1972882]    h4. Background  In Jira many custom field values are read from DB. These values are often read in a loop, one by one. To minimise the number of DB calls, when one custom field value is read, {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} reads and caches all of the values for the given issue. This is a request-scoped cache, so as soon as one Jira page is displayed, it's binned.    h4. Problem  In some cases the request scope lasts for long time. This is the case for example for CSV export, where all issues are exported in one go. The cache keeps on accumulating custom field values for longer than needed - as soon as an issue has been written out, it won't be accessed any more. There's no point in keeping it in memory. Yet, until this fix, we did that.    Another example (which is not really a real web request) is when a batch of issues is indexed. As soon as one issue has been indexed, we don't need to keep it in the memory. For this we had a workaround of cleaning the cache manually, which is redundant now.    h4. Fix summary  *We changed the custom field cache from unbounded to size 10 (LRU)*. In most cases size 1 would be enough, but in some scenarios, e.g. reading a subtask and its parent, a slightly bigger cache will be beneficial.     h4. Fix performance  I tested CSV export of 100K issues, each having 10 custom field values. Before the fix the request cache would have ~1945MB at the end of the export, now it has ~57MB.    Running our [JPT|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/jira-performance-tests] showed no performance regression. Indexing speed wasn't impacted either."""
comments: "This issue was fixed together with https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66555.  Please, have a look at [comment|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66555?focusedCommentId=1972882&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-1972882] for more details regarding the fix."

The options should be removed since it seems a bit redundant to use them or they should work only for the users who actually are reporters or assignees in the issue."""

summary: "Improving documentation about 'Creating Issues and Comments from Email'"
description: """2 improvements to make in this documentation: https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/creating-issues-and-comments-from-email-861253784.html    *Improvement 1*    I would be great if we could improve the definition of the *Default Reporter* setting for the mail handler of type *Create a new issue or add a comment to an existing issue*.    This setting is described as follows, which is correct:  {quote}  Specify the username of a default reporter, which will be used if the email address in the From: field of any received messages does not match the address associated with that of an existing JIRA user  {quote}    However, it might be helpful to add something like this:  {quote}  If the mail handler is able to match an existing JIRA user with the email address of the From: field, and if this user does not have the permission to create issues within the project or does not have application access or is inactive, the issue creation will fail with either error """"Reporter (XXXX) does not have permission to create an issue. Message rejected."""" OR """"Cannot create issue due to invalid license: [Sorry, you can't create any issues right now, as you need to have access to a Jira application to be able to create issues"""".  In this case, the mail handler won't try to create the issue using the default reporter as the reporter to compensate for the error.  {quote}    This information will help our customers understand/tackle the situation described in the bug https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-36497 faster.    *Improvement 2*    If multiple users in JIRA are using the same email address, but some of them are inactive, there is a chance that JIRA will try to match the email address of the From: field when trying to create the issue by email, and will fail with either error """"Reporter (XXXX) does not have permission to create an issue. Message rejected."""" OR """"Cannot create issue due to invalid license: [Sorry, you can't create any issues right now, as you need to have access to a Jira application to be able to create issues"""".    It might be good to mention it somewhere in the documentation, maybe as a warning with a link to this KB article https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-mail-handler-cannot-create-issue-from-some-users-email-827097833.html, so that our customers can tackle this scenario more easily when they are facing the bug https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-33116    """

summary: "Github Importer may Fail with API Rate Limit Exceeded"
description: """h3. Summary  If the account used to authenticate with Github has access to too many repos, JIRA will not be able to finish retrieving all the repos for the import if it hits API Rate Limit Exceeded.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Own a Github account that has access to a huge amount of repos (as in the case of Enterprise customers).  # Use Github Importer from External System Import to import Github issues into JIRA  # Authenticate using the account in #1    h3. Expected behavior  JIRA is able to retrieve the repo list for the import in all cases    h3. Actual behavior  JIRA may fail with this error:      h3. Suggestion  The importer should have an option to specify the repos to import from, rather than have JIRA scan through all the repos    h3. Workaround  Temporarily transfer the ownership of the to-be-imported repos to a different account and use that account for the import"""

summary: "Using filter picker in gadgets logs JS error 'Object doesn't support property or method includes' in IE 11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When clicking one of the existing filter suggested by filter picker on IE 11 a Javascript error is thrown and logged to dev console.  h3. Environment  * IE 11    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to a dashboard   # Add a gadget which incorporates a filter picker (e.g. Two Dimensional Filter Statistics)   # Advance to gadget's configuration   # Start typing an existing filter's name in the filter picker   # Select one of the displayed suggestions    h3. Expected Results    Clicking a suggested filter name sets it as the configured filter.  h3. Actual Results    Clicking a suggested filter name does set is as the configured filter but there's also this exception thrown in console    This is because IE 11 does not support 'includes' method. Luckily, the 'includes' method is used here when attempting to send an analytics event (if the analytics events are enabled on the instance) about using the filter picker. This means that the error does not impact the feature or dashboard in any way but just prevents this particular event (selecting a suggestion in filter picker) from being sent.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Update REST API documentation to differentiate between username vs user_key"
description: """The REST API documentation (e.g. https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.0/#api/2/project/)  uses the terms user_key and username interchangeably. This leads to confusion because both values exist in the database schema and one value over the other fails.      If the endpoints can't be changed to accept username (instead of userkey) then I'd also suggest careful use of the relevant term in each endpoint's description and a description of the difference earlier in the documentation.    An explanatory sentence of this bug (JRASERVER-68189) on the REST documentation would be really useful or a more specific error message when the incorrect value is used. """

summary: "Service Desk Email Channel: show some reaction in case a mail larger than 25MByte is received"
description: """Ladies and Gentlemen,    in [https://confluence.atlassian.com/servicedeskserver/managing-the-email-channel-939926306.html] you describe that mails with attachments larger than 25MBytes will not be pulled.    We have run into the situation and are unhappy with the fact that no proper reaction is set in this case. The mail is left there unprocessed and neither the sender nor the service desk agent is informed that it was not processed.    I'd like to see a mail returned to the sender telling him that his email was rejected due to attachment size > 25MByte. Knowing his the sender can react and reduce the attachments and send it again and can be sure the service desk agent will receive the information.     The other way around the service desk agent could be informed that there was a mail with attachments stripped due to too many data. Maybe together with a way how to retrieve the data via a different channel.    Thanks         Juergen"""

summary: """Original Estimate"" field is divided by 60 twice when using the ""Import from CSV"" tool"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using the """"Import from CSV"""" tool, the *Original Estimate* field as per Atlassian documentation is supposed to informed in seconds, but it is divided by 60 two times if you have the """"default unit"""" of Jira's time tracking configured to *minutes*, resulting in a wrong estimate.    h3. Environment  This was reproduced on the latest version Jira 7.12.1 and 7.13.1.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # When importing an issue from CSV, have the Original Estimate field in seconds, for example, * 28800* seconds.  # Have the """"default unit"""" of your Jira Time Tracking configuration as *minutes*  # Use the *Import from CSV* tool and import the issue.    h3. Expected Results  The estimate of the issue will be 480 minutes, or 8 hours as expected.    h3. Actual Results  The estimate will be 8 minutes, instead of 8 hours as expected.   !image.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Set the """"default units"""" of the Jira Time Tracking to *hours* instead of *minutes*."""

summary: "Project keys with special characters causes 404 errors"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira Allows a project key pattern to be customized, this includes allowing special characters such as _, *,^, etc. However, this may break some functionality and throws a 404 errors  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Jira project key pattern to allow special characters.   # Create a project with a special character such as _ or ^ in the project key   # Create an issue in the project    *Scenario 1*   For a project that has a '_' in the project key:   # Open an issue in a new tab.   # Delete the issue   # This shows a 404 error, despite the issue is successfully deleted    *Scenario 2*   For a project that has a '^' in the project key:   # Open the project in the issue navigator   # Try to switch the filer to anther from the 'Switch Filter"""" menu   # This shows a 404 and the filter does not work.    h3. Expected Results    If a project key pattern is allowed, it should not cause any error.  h3. Actual Results    a 404 error is thrown which may affect some functionality such as the below:    """"The value 'undefined' does not exist for the field 'project'""""    !value_undefined.PNG|width=200,height=150!    A HAR file shows the following POST Request:    h3. Workaround    For Scenario 1, delete the issue from the issue navigator in the list or details view instead of opening it in a new tab.    For Scenario 2, a JQL search may be used."""
comments: "Hi everyone,     In case of *scenario 1* we would like to gather more interest in this issue. As far as there is reasonable workaround we would like to focus on issues with higher severity and user impact.     According to [project key format specification|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/changing-the-project-key-format-938847081.html] using '^' is not supported. Only letters, numbers or the underscore character can be used. If we take into account these circumstances, we shouldn't consider *scenario 2* as a bug. Please make sure your project keys meet the requirements of project key format specification. Please do not hesitate to contact us whether you have any questions.          Mateusz Marzęcki    Jira Server Team     "

summary: "Issue view and activity show inconsistent due dates, leading to user confusion"
description: """h3. Problem    Due dates may differ between the issue view and the activity stream when Jira timezone and user timezones are different.    As an example, if the server is set with a GMT+0 timezone and an issue is set with a due date of 28 February, a user with a GMT-8 timezone will see the due date as 27 February in the general or user-specific activity streams.    Because due date is a *date* field, rather than a *date/time* field, the due date will technically be 00:00:01 am on the 28th, which would be 16:00:01 pm GMT-8 on the 27th, hence the disparity.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Jira with a GMT+0 timezone   # Add a user, set the user's timezone to GMT+0   # Add a second user, set the user's timezone to GMT-8   # Create a project and issue, assign a due date to that issue   # Login and view the issue as the first user, then the second user to see the due date disparity. The second user should see that the due date is the prior day, the first user should see the the due date as the expected day. In both cases the issue view will exhibit the expected due date.    h3. One Suggested Solution    Change due date to be a date/time field and also display the timezone. This will help users to better understand why the dates are different and as a nice bonus allow to set a specific due time.  h3. Workaround    None at this time."""
comments: "I think that Date fields have an implicit time of 00:00, so the start of the day. When the user's timezone of CMT-8 is applied then the date is the previous day. I agree that storing timezones with dates in the database would be the real solution, but that's a large change.  "

summary: "Clarify documentation about ""Any logged in user"" for Browse Project Permissions"
description: """As of Jira 7.13, it is understood that most, if not all functions related to a project are ruled by the Project's Permission scheme. On top of this, it is assumed by most users that Application access is on a security layer on top of the project's Permission scheme and this is generally true but there is an exception:   when a user is involved in a ticket as the reporter, watcher or assignee, and an event is triggered, the Notification scheme checks who should receive the notification for it, and it is required that each user has the _Browse project_ permission.     When talking about the Browse project permission, one of the sections of options is *Application Access* and from that list, we have """"Any logged in user"""". In theory adding this to the Browse Project permission will allow any existent user in the database to see the issues of the project BUT it does not consider if the users have application access and this leads to users without any application access (like Service Desk customers) to technically have browse project permissions even when they cannot log in directly to Jira. This itself is not a problem, as cannot log in to Jira after all, but this in turn enables an scenario with the Jira Notifications, as the Notification scheme does not validate application access either, and when an event is triggered, the validation seems to be like this:  # Event fired (let's say Issue creation)  # Checks the users/groups/roles involved included in the event (Assignee, Reporter, watchers, etc) and validates they have_Browse projects_ permission and sends the notification email to all that do (regardless of application access).    When a user creates and issue and sets another user as the reporter, if that new reporter does not have application access, and the previous scenario is met (Browse project permission including """"Any logged in user"""" and Notification scheme including the reporter for the """"Create  Issue"""" or any other event), the user without application access will receive Jira notifications, showing internal comments and changes that would normally not be visible to customers.    All this make sense in a logical way but since the assumption for """"Any logged in user"""" is that it does not supersedes the application access, the behavior seems counter intuitive.     The suggestion is to add a note in the [Managing project permissions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/managing-project-permissions-938847145.html] OR in the [Configuring email notifications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-email-notifications-938847633.html] documentation pages, indicating that Users without application access will still receive notifications in this scenario."""

To help with prioritisation, they would like SLA fields to show in the card layout view on agile boards.      h3. Workaround     None  """

summary: "Ability to add watchers who primarily didn't have access to browse the issue"
description: """h3. Description  If a customer wants to set up their permission scheme so only Administrators, Reporters and Watchers can Browse Issues on that project, the Watcher can only be added when creating the issue. If the issue is already created, the same user cannot be added as Watcher as he primarily doesn't have access to browse that issue.    h3. Use Case  Customer wants to configure a project permission scheme where only Administrators, Reporters and Watchers can Browse Issues on that project.    At first, he changed the permission scheme like the above. When creating a new issue on that project and adding a Watcher that primarily didn't have access to the issue (because he wasn't an Administrator, nor the Reporter), the issue is created with success and the user is added as a Watcher normally.    But, if the issue is created without him added as a Watcher, and then the Administrator tries to add him, Jira returns with the message that he doesn't have access to view the issue, so it won't add him as Watcher.    h3. Investigation Done  We successfully reproduced such issue internally on the latest Jira version available. We tried to work with Issue Security Schemes, where the standard Permission Scheme would have """"Browse Issues"""" to anyone logged on, but setting a Default Security Scheme where the previous permissions were configured, but the same behaviour was noticed. Even if we had no security scheme on the issue, added the Watcher (as he HAD permission), but tried to edit the issue to set the Issue Security Scheme we had, it didn't work because the Watcher wouldn't have permission with that Security Scheme.    We also tested creating a """"Watcher"""" role, adding users to it and configuring the Issue Security Level to allow the role to browse issue, but the same thing happens.    That being said, we speculate that when the issue is being created and a Watcher is added, either there is no validation on the Watcher field permission to view the issue, or the validation occurs after considering the user a Watcher of that issue. Differently from when an already created issue denies adding the user as he doesn't have access to it.    h3. Solution Request  When you have an already created issue which Issue Security Scheme or Permission Scheme allows the Watcher field OR role to browse the issue in that project, and you try to add someone as a Watcher to that issue, Jira should understand that I, who already have access to the issue, am adding that person as a Watcher so it can view the issue and have access to it, just like it works when creating the issue."""

summary: "relax ejira regexp validation rule for label"
description: """From the JIRA UI, I can easily add labels such as below to a ticket   * flow:foo_123   * script:bar_456.hql    h2. problem    The use case is to assign meaningful labels via ejira. But if I try any of the options in ejira, the labels are not accepted:  {code:java}  jira-label: flow:HDP_ADS_MEMBER_FACET_CHANGES, OnCall, script=shell_gw.sh     jira-label: 'flow:HDP_ADS_MEMBER_FACET_CHANGES',OnCall, """"script:shell_gw.sh"""", [autosys]    jira-label: [flow:HDP_ADS_MEMBER_FACET_CHANGES], script|shell_gw.sh, OnCall  {code}  In the source file LabelParser I see the line    Which explains why [ and ] are not allowed either. There is also a limit of 255 chars on a label    [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Confluence-questions/Can-I-put-spaces-in-Jira-Label-fields/qaq-p/125926]  h2. suggestion    Relax the validation regexp to allow at least the following signs in labels   * colon   * equal   * sharp   * pipe    Thank you!"""

summary: "Set an explicit filter export limit for specific groups"
description: """h3. Problem Definition     Currently, there is built-in limit of 1000 issues that can be exported. However, (per [Exporting a filter result containing more than 1000 issues|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/exporting-a-filter-result-containing-more-than-1000-issues-191500982.html]), you can either,  # Raise the limit for all users  # Set the limit to {{unlimited}} for a specific group.    The gap here is that there is no method of setting an explicit limit for a specific group, other than {{unlimited}}.    h3. Suggested Solution     Add an option to set an explicit limit (for example, 2000) for a specific group while leaving the normal 1000 limit for all other users.    h3. Why this is important     Administrators may not want to take on the potential instability that can occur when a group is given unlimited export abilities. However, they may want to use a sensible limit for a specific group that is higher than 1,000. """

summary: "Align on how Jira handles trailing spaces in username/groups from internal and external directories"
description: """h3. Summary    There have historically been plenty of issues caused by a trailing empty space in usernames/groups. Jira appears to handle this in internal directories in recent versions, but there's a misalignment when it comes to external user management.    Here's a *few* bugs and behaviors that ultimately are traced back to trailing spaces. Here's some examples:    https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-67262 - Updating roles through REST API did not work using username   https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-38290 - Can't add group with trailing spaces to a project role.   https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66599 - LDAP group can't be added during login due to duplicate key row   https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66959 - Trailing non-breaking space in user's USER_KEY causes error when adding user to a project role    [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-login-fails-with-user-exists-but-has-no-unique-key-mapping-373261121.html]    Jira needs to handle these scenarios and be consistent, whether it originates in a local directory or through an external directory.       h3. Expected Results  Jira seamlessly handles trailing spaces in user and groups and does not throw exceptions when performing basic user management operations. Details outlined in the respective bugs above.     h3. Actual Results  There have been plenty of historical bugs caused by Jira's inability to handle trailing space."""

summary: "Implement progress bar for XML backups to prevent proxy timeouts"
description: """h3. Summary    A Proxy Error screen will be displayed when attempting to generate an XML backup while running Jira behind a proxy.  h3. Environment   * Any version of Jira   * Running behind reverse proxy with conservative timeout    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to *Administration > System > Backup System*   # Start a backup of the instance   # Wait a few minutes (until the proxy timeout is reached)    h3. Expected Results    Backup is completed with no error message.  h3. Actual Results    User receives 504 Gateway Timeout error  h3. Notes    This error happens because the backup procedure does not have a progress bar to indicate the state of the operation. When the backup is started, the browser keeps waiting for the backup to complete.    If the instance is fairly large, the backup will take longer than the proxy timeout configured and result in a 504 gateway timeout error. This is because there is no response from the application while the backup is being created.    Notice that the backup operation is not impacted. If you wait for its completion, the backup will be available in the *export* directory of the local home. However, we do not have any indication of the process state besides checking if the backup file stopped growing and is zipped in the directory above.    A possible solution would be having a progress bar to this process, as we have for the XML import procedure.  h3. Workaround    (!) Increasing the proxy timeout is not feasible as the backup can take hours to complete   # The first workaround is to wait for the backup to complete by checking the backup file in the *export* directory. Once the file stops growing and a .zip file is created, the backup should be completed   # The second workaround is to add a non-proxy connector to server.xml, access the site using the connector and execute the backup"""
comments: "Its a user experience defect, which says 504 error, but in background it creates the back up file, Can we get the work around for the issue other than changing the server.xml proxy.."

Steps to Reproduce   # Create a trial account at [http://pivotaltracker.com|http://pivotaltracker.com/]   # Create a project with epics and stories in those epics   # Follow steps from [Importing data from Pivotal Tracker|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-pivotal-tracker-938847553.html] to import data from Pivotal Tracker into Jira    h3. summary: "Trigger Directory Syncs using the REST API"
description: """It is common to have an LDAP directory that does not support incremental syncs and is moderately sized. These directories don't have the option to lower the sync interval to pick up group membership changes for known users, so admins currently have to trigger syncs from the UI if the sync interval is not an acceptable time to wait for the change to reach Jira. If the REST API had a means of triggering a sync remotely, it would give admins an easy (and scriptable) way to pick up changes to a directory.  h4. Workaround  {panel:borderColor=#FFE7E7|bgColor=#FFE7E7}  (i) The Bash script below runs a directory sync of all enabled directories, be sure to update your credentials.   (i) The script and its contents are not supported by Atlassian and are only provided on a best-effort basis. Use at your own risk.   {panel}  {code:bash|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6A8EB3|bgColor=#F8F8F8}  #!/bin/bash    set -x    # we need the base URL of the app (ie: https://jira.domain.com)  BASEURL=$1  [ -z """"$BASEURL"""" ] && echo """"usage: $0 BASEURL"""" && exit 1    # build the URL needed to grab the xtoken  URL=""""$BASEURL/plugins/servlet/embedded-crowd/directories/list""""    # store the cookie as a temp file that is deleted as we finish  COOKIE=/tmp/GetRest.$$  RESP=/tmp/GetToken.$$    # login and save the cookie  curl -k -c $COOKIE -s -X POST \   --data 'os_username=your_admin_username'  --data 'os_password=your_admin_password' --data """"formname=loginform"""" --data """"login='Log In'"""" \   """"$BASEURL/dologin.action"""" -o /dev/null    # get the list of sync opertaions by getting the URL with the cookie  curl -k -b $COOKIE -s -X GET """"$URL"""" > $RESP    # here is an example of the sync operation  # href=""""/plugins/servlet/embedded-crowd/directories/sync?directoryId=10501&atl_token=d5265f974829752eaa45a94011452b1b05f3f730"""">    # look for all of the available sync operations and call each one-by-one  for i in `grep /sync $RESP | grep atl_token= | grep -v """"{id}"""" | sed 's/^.*href=""""//; s/"""".$//'` ; do          curl -k -b $COOKIE -s -X GET """"$BASEURL$i""""  done    # delete the temp files  rm -f $COOKIE $TOKEN  {code}  """
comments: "The grep and sed command simply do not return any value to me at all." summary: "Comments are sorted and displayed incorrectly if they're created within the same second"
description: """h4. Summary    When an issue has enough comments to be collapsed, if there're comments created within the same second, they may be retrieved and displayed incorrectly by Jira.    (i) This bug has a similar concept to JSWSERVER-13996.    h4. Steps to reproduce    So far this is only reproducible by using comment clone functions by 3rd-party add-ons e.g. Clone Plus for Jira or Backbone Issue Sync for Jira:  # Create an issue with 10+ comments  # Use add-on function to clone the comments to another issue  # View the issue where comments are cloned to    (i) Alternatively, manually edit the db to set the comment created values to the same second for all comments.    h4. Expected behavior  Comments are retrieved and displayed correctly as they are in the original issue.    h4. Actual behavior  A random comment may be missing whereas another random comment may be displayed twice.    h4. Cause  This is caused by the way Jira retrieves comments from the database for the Comments tab when there're enough comments to be collapsed. Basically, Jira relies on the *created* column to sort the comments, and it assumes that there's a time gap (1 second+) between any 2 sequential comments. Due to the way comments are cloned using add-on, some comments might be written into the database having exactly the same created values (< 1 second difference), causing the comment sorting mechanism to break.    h4. Notes  # Without collapsing, the problem doesn't appear  #* Collapsing can be disabled by setting _jira.comment.collapsing.minimum.hidden_ to 0 from Advanced Settings  # The problem never appears in All tab (as opposed to Comments tab)    h4. Workarounds  # Vendors to modify add-on code to ensure comments are not created within the same second  # Manually modify the db to set the comment created values to have at least a 1-second gap sequentially  # Disable comment collapsing as per the Notes section"""

summary: "LuceneCorruptionChecker is not processing corrupted indexes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Jira crashes and Lucene indexes become corrupted, LuceneCorruptedChecker is confused about the indexes state and is unable to fix the corruption:        h3. Expected Results    Since LuceneCorruptionChecker is able to correctly detect the corruption, we would expect it to fix the corruption as well, or report something else but """"index is healthy"""" in case it cannot fix the corruption.    h3. Actual Results    LuceneCorruptionChecker is reporting the index as healthy when it is clearly not.    h3. Workaround    Try to re-index manually, if this also does not work, delete the index files manually, those will be repopulated after that due to full reindexing."""

summary: "Update issues asynchronously in the client when they change on the server side"
description: """When viewing an issue, whether in Jira or in the Customer Portal there is the possibility that more than one user will be accessing it, and while the issue is already loaded in the web browser of one user, another user can add a comment or even change the status, and the other users that already have the issue open won't notice this, until they try to perform an action like moving the issue to a different status or refreshing.     Another scenario that may happen is when using automation: if there is an automation to change the status after a comment is made, the user triggering the automation to change the status will not see the change until they refresh, and if they try to transition the issue after making a comment, it will indicate """"You can not make this transition"""" which can be confusing.    The suggested solution is to have issues being updated asynchronously (at least the status) so it would reflect the change immediately."""
comments: "The reason this got recorded as a suggestion is because we've started seeing reports from Service Desk customers who have tried clicking on the ""Resolve this issue"" button and are just getting an error.    It is not intuitive to the customer that they need to refresh the page after leaving a comment, so that the web page is updated with the correct status. This, I feel, is the simplest and most common scenario that needs fixing and should be relatively straightforward since the page code could force a reload of the page after submitting the user's comment to Jira.    Without this getting fixed, customers just end up asking agents to close the ticket which pretty much defeats the whole point of the customer being able to resolve it!     "

summary: "Subscribe Filter To A Large Group Of User Causing JIRA Trigger Subscription Mail To All User In The Group Even If ""email this filter, even if there are no issues found"" Was Unchecked."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Creating a  """"Process Management"""" project type, will create a Project Management Resolve Issue Screen    h3. Environment  JIRA 7.10.2, 7.13    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project called """"JIRA"""" in your JIRA instance  # Create a new filter as below, save it and share it with everyone.    (i) You can choose to use your existing JIRA project if you want, do ensure you update the filter accordingly if you do so.  # Create 10 users and assign with *JIRA Software User* group  # Create 2 issues and assign to user 1  # Create another few issue assign to the user 2  # Subscribe *JIRA Software User* group to the filter we created earlier  # Do ensure you do not tick *""""email this filter, even if there are no issues found""""*  # Trigger the subscription 1-2 times or more  # Access to [*Mail Queue*|https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-mail-queue-151387.html] and you will see JIRA generate subscription mail for all 10 users in *JIRA Software User* group even though 8 of them shouldn't return any results in the filter  (i) These emails were generated in [*Mail Queue*|https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-mail-queue-151387.html], however only those with results sends out to correct recipient (user 1 and user 2)    h3. Expected Results  JIRA should only trigger subscription email to 2 of the recipient in [*Mail Queue*|https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-mail-queue-151387.html].     h3. Actual Results  JIRA trigger subscription email to all recipient in JIRA Software User group. It triggered for all users, but this doesn't send out to the 8 users which listed in the [*Mail Queue*|https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/the-mail-queue-151387.html]. It seems *""""email this filter, even if there are no issues found""""* had filtered out those email to send out.     h3. Workaround  -"""

Workaround  Use the workaround described in the following KB article:  * [Error rendering 'com.atlassian.jira.jira-view-issue-plugin:linkingmodule'|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/error-rendering-com-atlassian-jira-jira-view-issue-plugin-linkingmodule-961804219.html]"""

summary: "Search on partial word (substring) does not work on Create & Edit Issue Screen"
description: """Using the Service Desk Portal, the search on a partial word (substring) works on all fields. The user can select a field and start typing partial words of the preferred value. Jira will displays all the field values which contains the typed characters, independently of the position.    However, when a user is creating or modifying an issue within a Project (Create & Edit Issue Screen), then the search on partial word does NOT work properly. Jira displays only those field values which +begins+ with the partial word that the user has just typed in. This is very inconvinient and generates huge effort to users to find the desired value in a very long list, especially if the user does not know how the value starts but he/she knows partial words.    Jira partial word search needs to be fixed on the Create & Edit Issue Screen."""

summary: "Jira should check disk space availability prior re-indexing"
description: """*Problem.*     While foreground reindexing, disk space usage could surge to double the current size of the index directory. This could lead to indexing process to fail midway when there is insufficient disk space availability leaving the instance with broken index.     From Lucene document  [https://lucene.apache.org/core/5_0_0/core/org/apache/lucene/index/IndexWriter.html#addDocument(java.lang.Iterable])  {quote}Merges temporarily consume space in the directory. The amount of space required is up to 1X the size of all segments being merged, when no readers/searchers are open against the index, *and up to 2X the size of all segments being merged when readers/searchers are open against the index (see forceMerge(int) for details).* The sequence of primitive merge operations performed is governed by the merge policy.{quote}  *Suggestion.*     Jira should check for disk space availability (at least double the current index size) or display warning to user. """

summary: "Per-user option to automatically go to tickets created via lightbox"
description: """Creating an issue through the QuickIssueCreate lightbox causes a pop-up to appear with a link to the newly created ticket. Several of my users have said that this does not make sense for their workflow. They suggest that it would be more natural if they were immediately taken to the issue they just created.    Further, there have been a few cases where issues have had incorrect information entered into them, when the user hasn't realized that they aren't looking at the new issue. Sometimes the user realizes and cancels the edit, but if the current issue and the new issue look similar enough, they may not notice and then you have a lying ticket.    Some other users appreciate the current behaviour, so I suggest a configuration option in the Preferences section of a user's profile. Something like a checkbox that says """"automatically open newly created tickets."""" Or, in keeping with the current screen for user preferences, a drop-down menu that would enable this feature.    I've made my users aware of various workarounds, including right-clicking the 'Create' button to open the link in a new tab, thus avoiding the lightbox. However we all feel that the point of software like Jira is to minimize the potential for human error that impacts the integrity of the information in issues.    Thanks for your consideration,"""

Steps to Reproduce  # Go to a project  # Click on Project settings;  # Click on Screens page  # Click on Actions > Use a different scheme  # Now click on Cancel button    h3. Actual Results  Jira returns the following error:  {{No project could be found with key 'screens'.}}   !screenshot.jpeg|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  The 'Cancel' URL link leads to wrong link:   {{<baseURL>/plugins/servlet/project-config//screens}}  but the original link should be:  {{<baseURL>/plugins/servlet/project-config/<project_key>/screens}}    It is visible that the project key is missing between two slash characters ( // )    h3. Workaround  To get back to the """"Screens"""" page, insert project key between slashes in the {{<baseURL>/plugins/servlet/project-config//screens}} link and resend the request."""

summary: "As an administrator, I want the /auditing/record/ REST endpoint to reflect the current record total when applying a filter"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, if you invoke the {{/auditing/record/}} REST endpoint you'll receive a count of the total number of audit events in the JSON dictionary, e.g.:    This total *does not* change if you append a filter to the REST endpoint, e.g.:   {{JIRA_BASE_URL/rest/api/2/auditing/record?filter=version}}  h3. Suggested Solution    Update the code to provide the total number of returned records, not the overall count of events.  h3. Why this is important    Not having an accurate record total complicates scripting; it's definitely to determine pagination of results without an accurate total.  h3. Workaround     None at this time."""

summary: "Intermittently, an attachment from a different issue opens from the thumbnail of an unrelated issue"

Steps to Reproduce (Example)  *Wrong URLs:*  https://HOST/secure/attachment/2131980/2131980_example1.PNG    Correct URLs:  https://HOST/secure/attachment/2141711/example2.png    The attachment links in the attachment section at the top of the ticket go to the right place, but the thumbnails do not.    h3. Notes  We confirmed that affected users can't view attachments for issues they do not have access to.     h3. summary: "Exporting issues to HTML including the Team portfolio field makes resulting file unopenable"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Exporting issues to HTML including the Team portfolio field makes resulting file unopenable    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # From the Issue navigator, make a search with a large number of results, including the field 'Team' provided by Jira Portfolio (10k+ issues, preferably).   # Export the file to HTML.   # Open the file with your preferred spreadsheet program.    h3. Expected Results  The file is opened    h3. Actual Results  The program starts loading the file and after a while stops responding, staying stuck until forcefully closed.    h3. Notes  * Tested in MS Excel and LibreOffice Calc with the same results.  * The problem seems come from the field 'Team' including an image on each row, which causes a much greater load for the program opening it, even when the file size between the one that includes the Team field is similar to the one that doesn't.    h3. Workarounds  * Avoid including the field 'Team' from Portfolio for exports.  * Export to CSV  ** This could be usable only in certain scenarios as there are fields that are not exported to CSV in the same way they are in HTML. """

summary: "WYSIWYG editor on Jira does not work properly when changing font color"

summary: "Allow non JIRA Admin users to view the whole list of available workflows"

The only thing that the user can do when going to the page *Projects > Workflows* is view and edit the workflows from the workflow scheme that is already associated to the project.    This user can't do the following:  - change the workflow scheme associate to the project  - view the whole list of workflow which already exists in JIRA     !Screen Shot 2019-01-04 at 11.08.22 AM.png|thumbnail!       *Suggestion*    Allow any project admin user which does not have the JIRA admin permission to do the following:  - change the workflow scheme associate to the project  - view the whole list of workflow which already exists in JIRA    """

summary: "Improve Jira DC documentation to explicitly mention Communication ports"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira 7.13.1 and 8.0.0 will have default ports specified for a node to node communications. This work is done in https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66608    We improved the doc previously in [JRASERVER-64976|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-64976] to include the parameter to a specific Object port. It comes to the point that this will now be the default and additional configuration is optional.    h3. Suggested Solution  Update the documentation ([Installing Jira Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/installing-jira-data-center-938846870.html]) to explicitly mention opening two ports between the nodes  * Ehcache port default 40001  * Object port default 40011    This is *best* to be mentioned early in subtopics of the following  * Before you begin > What are the prerequisites?  * 3. Configure your existing Jira instance to work in a cluster    h3. Why this is important  Jira DC requires these ports to be open and reachable between nodes to ensure node-to-node communications.  """

summary: "Jira Windows Service no longer reports in Event Viewer Windows logs start/stop"

The information is logged if there is a service start/stop issue reporting the error however nothing if the start/stop is successful.    This is a problem since there is no information about who stopped/started Jira."""

summary: "Improving Jira Service Desk Organizations "
description: """h1. Issue    Using Organizations is tough because of the following issues.   - Organizations have to be maintained manually in JIRA. We are a very large enterprise with 10k user licenses for JIRA. Creating JIRA Customer Organizations would be a duplication of data (taking into account we already have hundreds of email and security groups set up in Active Directory)     - Automatic sharing of a ticket with an Organization happens only if the user belongs to only one organization.      - Our customers specify the group they want to share with by adding email distribution groups to the CC field of the email they use to submit the case. We need JIRA to recognize the entries in the CC field and look up not just user emails to add Participants, but also User Group emails and add those group emails as participants, and then actually send notifications to those group emails just like it would to a user email.    h1. Suggestion   - Make Service Desk tickets available to multiple users based on a specification of a recognized (by JIRA) User Group     - Make JIRA issue-creation-from-email capable of recognizing Email Distribution lists added to the CC of an incoming email, and make the resultant issue visible to the members of the group (where the email address of the group and/or the group name match to a User Group recognized by JIRA)     - Allow customer to select User Group(s) with whom the ticket should be Shared, through the Service Desk portal     - Allow for multiple User Groups' members to have visibility on an issue in this solution     - Support sending issue-related email to the group members, identically to how emails would be sent to Request Participants"""

summary: "In rare situations custom field delete causes NullPointerException in JDC"
description: """h3. Current Behavior    After deleting a custom field, one or more nodes in JIRA Data Center show NullPointerException when searching for issues or when viewing issues.   * NullPointerException may occur on other pages as well.    Example stacktrace:    h3. Expected Results    Deleting custom field does not cause errors on any node  h3. Cause    The cause is believed to be a race condition where the cache on NPE nodes reference a custom field that no longer exists.  h3. Environment    JIRA Data Center with two or more nodes  h3. Steps to reproduce   # Set breakpoint in DefaultSearchHandlerManager.java#getVisibleClauseHandlers   ** Line 186, pause thread   # Attempt to add issue link - From an issue, add link then add by searching for issues (so that JQL autocomplete will be retrieved)   ** Breakpoint is it.   # On another node, delete custom field   # On paused node, flush caches   # Mute breakpoints, run   ** Stacktrace in logs, unable to use JQL autocomplete    h3. Workaround    Nodes showing NullPointerException will need to be restarted or have caches flushed. NullPointerException should not appear after restart.   * Success has been reported when using ScriptRunner to flush caches   ** /plugins/servlet/scriptrunner/admin/builtin/exec/com.onresolve.scriptrunner.canned.jira.admin.ClearCaches   * NullPointerException that continues after restart indicates a different problem is occurring"""

summary: "AWS template doesn't add the parameters relaxedPathChars and relaxedQueryChars to the connector on port 8443"
description: """h3. Summary  AWS template doesn't add the parameters relaxedPathChars and relaxedQueryChars to the connector on port 8443    h3. Environment  AWS templates available at https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment/src    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy the template https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment/src/master/templates/JiraDataCenter.template.yaml  # Attempt to access a node on port 8443    h3. Expected Results  The parameters {{relaxedPathChars=""""[]|""""}} and {{relaxedQueryChars=""""[]|{}^\`"<>""""}} are added to all connectors.    h3. Actual Results  The parameters {{relaxedPathChars=""""[]|""""}} and {{relaxedQueryChars=""""[]|{}^\`"<>""""}} are only added to the connector on port 8080, while the connector on port 8443 does not have these parameters added. Therefore, Jira is not accessible on port 8443.    h3. Workaround  Manually add the parameters {{relaxedPathChars=""""[]|""""}} and {{relaxedQueryChars=""""[]|{}^\`"<>""""}} to the connector on port 8443.    h3. Notes  Originally reported at https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment/issues/10/jira-templates-missing-required-7123"""
comments: "This issue was fixed and deployed in an AMI update in November: JRASERVER-68116. I suspect that anyone running into this issue is using an AMI that does not contain the fix.    If you are experiencing this issue, please let us know the ID of the AMI you are using, or please compare the atl-functions script to the [current master version|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment/src/master/ami/scripts/init.d/atl-functions] if you are building your own."

summary: "JIRA Importer for Redmine to bypass basic authentication when it get redirected"
description: """h3. Summary    JIRA Importer for Redmine to bypass basic authentication when it gets redirected  h3. Environment   * Redmine that store the attachment in AWS S3.   * JIRA Importer version 2.1.9.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Import a Redmine project that store attachments in AWS S3 storage.   # During the import, the attachments will not be uploaded successfully with an *only the X-Amz-Algorithm query parameter, Signature query string parameter or the Authorization header should be specified* error below:      This is because in to get the attachment in our Importer it used a Basic authorization:  h4. Importer when the attachment is stored locally    (i) Noted that the Authorization used is Basic : i.e username and password    h4. Importer when the attachment is stored in AWS S3 storage    (i) Please note that Redmine Importer in JIRA used the redirection URL from S3 that contain AMZ token in the URL. When passing this through the importer that use Basic authentication, it will cause the error in *Steps 2*.    h3. Expected Results  JIRA will bypass the Basic authentication and used the token provided by AWS URL to transfer attachment over.    h3. Actual Results  JIRA will still use a Basic authentication and will cause the error in *Steps 2*.    h3. Workaround  * Upload the attachment using [Importing data from CSV \- Importing attachments|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-csv-938847533.html#ImportingdatafromCSV-Importingattachments].  * Move the attachments from the AWS to a local server that will allow a Basic authentication.  """

summary: "Deleted issues can be shown in searches in Jira DC"
description: """h3. Summary    When an issue is deleted on one node, it can still appear in searches on some other node. The key and summary of the issue are visible, but after clicking on the issue they change to """"This issue can no longer be opened"""" message.    This happens when an """"UPDATE"""" replication event is placed into the database after a """"DELETE"""" event *and* they are processed in the immediate following batches by the node.   For example, with the following database entries, if one batch is processed at 20:22:48, it will reply the DELETE event, and then the next batch will process the UPDATE event, reviving the deleted issue.   !Screen Shot 2019-01-02 at 10.34.23.png|thumbnail!      In production instances, this usually happens due to some automation performing very quick operations, e.g. changing the issue's description and immediately deleting it.   Sometimes this can lead to the DELETE event being stored in the database before the UPDATE event that was performed earlier.   Even though both of the operations will be added to the database milliseconds apart, with some unlucky timing they can be processed in separate batches, as described above.  h3. Environment    Jira Data Center  h3. Steps to Reproduce    It's very hard to reproduce this in the UI. The easiest way is to create the scenario directly in the database.   1. Create some test issue and grab its id (e.g. by running select on the _jiraissue_ table), here it's 10005   2. Delete the issue from the database:    3. Create a DELETE replication event, like the one below. Note that the node's id (_c44634_) has to belong to a running node, and the operation's id (_11035_) needs to be larger than the last processed replication event from that node.    4. After ~5 seconds, create an UPDATE replication event, with the same constraints as above. Waiting too little can make this event be processed in the same batch as the previous event, or 2 batches later - in both of those scenarios the bug seems to not be reproducible.    h3. Expected Results    The issue does not appear in the search results.   Nice to have: some message is logged in the application's log about the race occurring.  h3. Actual Results    The issue's key and summary are visible in searches. Users are unable to view issue details.   !Screen Shot 2019-01-02 at 10.30.26.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    To fix the index inconsistency, run a project reindex, or a full reindex.    [How to remove from Jira lucene index, issues that have been deleted in the database, but still present in index|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-remove-from-jira-lucene-index-issues-that-have-been-deleted-in-the-database-but-still-present-in-index-998896911.html] offers a workaround that does not require a project or full re-index.     The the occurrences can also be limited by modifying automation scripts to avoid updating issues right before deleting them."""
comments: "We have seen this in Jira Data Center 7.2.10 and now in 7.6.9. We have a script that creates and issue in each of 4 nodes once per minute. The script updates each issue issue, then deletes the issue. When we run a search we see issues created on other nodes that were in fact deleted." [SEP] "We tried making the issue edit then sleeping for 1s before deleting the issue. Didn't seem to change the symptoms" [SEP] "Still present in 7.13.8" [SEP] "This issue is mentioned in https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-remove-from-jira-lucene-index-issues-that-have-been-deleted-in-the-database-but-still-present-in-index-998896911.html but not linked" [SEP] "Looks like it's still happening in 8.5.4" [SEP] " Przemyslaw Czuj  what areas of Jira are changed by the fix for this?" [SEP] "[~mdoar2]    * we have added versioning of all entities - now every change which triggers an issue, comment or worklog reindex bumps this entity version (bumping the version is only triggered by user actions, not full/background re-i

summary: "Allow for backup restore to replace only data, not instance settings"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira doesn't allow to restore data (projects, issues, workflows, etc) without touching the instance settings such as authentication, external directories, etc.    h3. Suggested Solution  There could be an option while restoring an XML backup to only import data without changing the instance settings.    h3. Why this is important  Some companies prefer pre-configuring an environment to confirm settings are working fine and only then import the data.    h3. Workaround  Set up the Jira instance first, then use the *Project import* functionality to bring all needed data."""

summary: "Setting an entity property via the Issue Update REST endpoint does not trigger IssuePropertySetEvent"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While developing a JIRA Server (7.7.1) P2 Plugin, I have discovered that updating an entity property via the [Issue Edit REST endpoint|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.6.6/#api/2/issue-editIssue] results in no IssuePropertySetEvent being published.  h3. Environment   * JIRA Server 7.7.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a P2 Plugin with an EventListener for the IssuePropertySetEvent     # Start up a local Jira with the plugin {code}atlas-debug{code}   # Create an issue, e.g. HSP-1   # Perform a PUT to  {code:java}  http://localhost:2990/jira/rest/api/2/issue/HSP-1{code}  with contents:  {code:java}  {    """"update"""": {      """"summary"""": [        {          """"set"""": """"My new summary""""        }      ]    },    """"properties"""": [      {        """"key"""": """"key1"""",        """"value"""": """"{\""""key\"""":\""""value\""""}""""      }    ]  }  {code}    h3. Expected Results    The EventListener should be triggered with the change to the issue's entity property.  h3. Actual Results    No event is being published and therefore cannot be listened to.  h3. Workaround    Only perform updates to the issue entity properties via the [Set Issue Property REST Endpoint|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.6.6/#api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey\}/properties-setProperty]. Otherwise there is no workaround."""

summary: "Asynchronous cache replication queue - leaking file descriptor when queue file corrupted"
description: """h3. Problem    If a cache replication queue is corrupted when a node is shutting down, then on the next node start Jira will try to open this queue file every time it is required (a cache replication message is being send to another node on this particular channel = file). If the existing file is corrupted it fails with the following error:    This results in:   * cache replication message not being delivered   * leak of file descriptor   ** Jira hits into """"Too many open files"""" error. Reviewing lsof output points to many localq entries.    h3. Desired Jira behaviour    If the file is corrupted backup this file (copy with corrupted_ prefix) and create a new file.    h3. Workaround    Delete the corrupted queue file. Step to identify the corrupted file can be found following [comment-1917799|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-68653?focusedCommentId=1917799&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-1917799]  It should not be necessary to shut down this node. It should recreate this queue file automatically.     """
comments: "7.6.10 is also affected by this issue." [SEP] "@Maciej : We faced the same issue few weeks back on one of our Jira data-center node.     [c.a.j.c.distribution.localq.LocalQCacheManager] Error when creating cache replication queue for node: *<node-name>.* This node will be inconsistent. Error: File is corrupt; length stored in header is 0. java.io.IOException: File is corrupt; length stored in header is 0.    Just wanted to know if we face the same issue again, do we need to remove the *<node-name>* file from jira-home/localq folder ?    We do see lot of files under the *<node-name>* in jira-home/localq folder :    queue_<node-name>_0_558f4e07e022f3d0952240a1abc249c3     queue_<node-name>_1_558f4e07e022f3d0952240a1abc249c3    queue_<node-name>_2_558f4e07e022f3d0952240a1abc249c3   queue_<node-name>_3_558f4e07e022f3d0952240a1abc249c3   queue_<node-name>_4_558f4e07e022f3d0952240a1abc249c3    Do we need to remove all of these ?" [SEP] "Hi [~bvaradha1],    Unfortunately the log message does not tell you which

summary: "Deleting Confluence page/s with lots of issue links degrades JIRA performance"
description: """h3. Summary  It's typical to have a Confluence page with a certain number of child pages, each with a certain number of JIRA issues. When deleting the parent page choosing to delete the child pages altogether, Confluence will send a huge number of requests to JIRA for deleting the issue links. This will cause JIRA to stall for a certain amount of time.    Example:  # Create a page with 50 child pages  # For each child page, mention 20 unique JIRA issues  # Delete the parent page and choose to delete all the child pages  # Confluence will send 50 * 20 = 1000 DELETE requests to JIRA like this:      h3. Suggestions  For Atlassian - come up with a better way to handle this scenario, so that JIRA performance won't be noticeably affected.    For JIRA/Confluence end-users - schedule a maintenance window whenever it comes to deleting a page that involves a huge number of JIRA issues."""

summary: "Deletion of Table doesn't delete the Extra line"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When we add a Table from the drop down option in the visual mode while updating any issue field (be it comments section) , It adds an extra line after the table and and if we delete the table again in the visual mode, the extra line doesn't go away and it gives a drop down option with nothing listed.   h3. Environment    Issue reproducible in 7.6.6 and 7.6.9  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a simple text custom field which has Wiki Renderer.   # Add the custom field to all the screens including edit issue screen.   # Open any issue from a project.    # Click on Edit issue   # You should be able to see the custom field where you can input anything either in Visual mode or in Text mode   # Stay on Visual mode and click on drop down box to create a table   # Save it.    # Now click on the edit issue again and in visual mode, delete the table by selecting delete table from the options that you get when you click inside the table   # You will see that it has not removed the extra space and instead leaved behind a expand or a drop down menu   # This behaviour is not seen when adding and removing table from Text mode.     h3. Expected Results    Expected Results, is that when we remove a table from visual section, it should also remove the extra line that it inserted when adding the table.  h3. Workaround    Two Workarounds available :    1) Remove the table from Visual Section and then remove the extra line from text mode.     2) do a CTL+A (select all) in the visual mode and delete the whole table which also works at the customer side."""

summary: "Notification Scheme condition to allow JIRA to send email notifications to a single email address for a group users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JIRA by default is designed to send out email notifications to user's individual email addresses should they be eligible for the notification events.     It would add to the notification scheme configurability if JIRA is able to send out email notifications to one email address for a group of users rather than sending it out to each of the individual email addresses.    e.g.: If user A, B and C are in a group, any notification event will send email notifications to one email address set for the group.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow groups in notification scheme to receive email notifications from a singular email address.    h3. Workaround   None"""
comments: "Hi @Irfan    Adding to it, the condition should not only applicable to groups but any defined condition which could trigger the notification to any individual mail id."

summary: "Add in documentation Specifying Field Behavior that a field will not be searchable when hidden"
description: """In the documentation [Specifying Field Behavior |https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/specifying-field-behavior-938847255.html], in the section :  {quote}  h3. Hiding or showing a field    If your organization or project has no use for a particular field, you have the option to hide it. Hiding a field will ensure that the the field does not appear on any screens (i.e. issue operation screens, workflow transition screens) where the field configuration applies.    (i) *Please note:*   * Hiding a field in the field configuration is distinct from not adding a field to a screen. Fields hidden through the field configuration will be hidden in _all_ applicable screens, regardless of whether or not they have been added to the screen.   * For fields that have a default value: If the field is hidden in the field configuration, then it will not receive a value when an issue is created, regardless of whether the field is present on the [*Create Issue* screen(s)|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-a-screen-938847288.html]. (The following fields can have a default value: [resolution|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-resolution-field-values-938847105.html], [status|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-status-field-values-938847108.html], [priority|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-priority-field-values-938847101.html], [issue type|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-issue-type-field-values-938847087.html], [security level|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-issue-level-security-938847117.html], and [custom fields|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/adding-a-custom-field-938847222.html].)   * The fields *Summary* and *Issue Type* cannot be hidden and as such there is no *Hide* option available for these fields.   * If you hide the *Fix Version/s* field, the Change Log report will not work.{quote}  Please add that when a field is hidden, it will not be searchable in both Advanced Searching and Basic Searching."""

summary: "Hidden fields should not be searchable when using Advanced Search"
description: """h3. *Summary*    When searching for a hidden field in Advanced Search, the JQL query shows that the hidden field are searchable, however, this should not be the case as hidden fields are not supposed to show. Basic search does not allow hidden fields to be searchable.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a test project 'TEST', assign the issue to an assignee 'testuser'.   # Hide the field assignee.   # Using Advanced Search, search :       # Click on Search.    h3. Expected Results    The Advanced Search should be shown as  !error.PNG|thumbnail!  and the warning message 'hidden field is not searchable' as the field is hidden and there will be no results returned.  h3. Actual Results    The Advanced Search will be shown as correct  !green tick.PNG|thumbnail! and user can proceed to search. This should not be the case as search with hidden field will not return any results.  h3. Note    If value is already added to the field before hiding it, results will be shown when performing an Advanced Search if re-indexing is not performed.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
