This page should be edited to reflect the correct versions listed on the page (6.4 and below are not listed)."""

summary: "Logs flooded with ""Broken pipe"" or other connection errors when using Firefox"
description: """h5. Bug Summary:    Logs flooded with """"Broken pipe"""" errors When using firefox 59.0.2 (64-bit).    Opening a Jira-specific error for proper tracking from the Confluence variant tracked at  CONFSERVER-55279       h5. Steps to replicate   # Start Jira   # Access Jira starting page   # Login to application and view/edit any issues    h5. Actual results    Following errors are present during login and page viewing/editing    Other errors users have reported receiving """"{{An established connection was aborted by the software in your host machine}}"""" and """"{{An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host}}"""" errors.    h3. Note  Please note that this error is not a bug, but rather a generic exception thrown by the application server when a connection is closed unexpectedly (usually on the client side) see JRASERVER-63503 for more details.     h3. Workaround  Use a different browser instead of Firefox. """
comments: "Hi,     I am closing this issue as far as it is a duplicate of JRASERVER-63503.     Cheers,    Mateusz Marzęcki    Jira bugfix team"

summary: "As an JIRA Datacenter Administrator I want to delete reindexing task from offline node"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, when Jira Datacente node is shutdown while running FullReindex task (see related JRASERVER-47045, JRASERVER-66204), there is no way to remove this task from the cluster. All other nodes still think the task is running even the node is down, so this prevents corresponding operations to be done.     h3. Suggested Solution  Allow Jira system admin to delete stale FullReindex task.     h3. Implemented Solution  * (i) Before using this feature, please check if JRASERVER-68885 fits your needs as it's a much simpler and safer option.  * In order to remove stale re-index job from a cluster you can use REST API call (JIRA _Sysadmin_ permission required):  ** Method: DELETE  ** URL: {{<JIRA>/rest/internal/2/reindex/unlock}}  * It will try to find active foreground re-index task and remove it if it belongs to +OFFLINE+ node.  ** (!) Since call removes only re-index task (which is assumed to be stuck), and doesn't kill the re-index thread itself, make sure that an actual node went down before you use this API (to ensure safety, there is also a check in the code).     h3. Workaround   None"""

summary: "Suppress Excessive java.io.IOException: Broken pipe ERROR when serving static files"
description: """Summary   During our research into JRASERVER-63503 we found the majority occurrences of broken pipe log spamming is occurring when serving static files. An example of what this will look like can be found below:    The purpose of this feature request is to track the progress on suppressing these entries entirely from logging."""
comments: "I personally think that the error is OK to display, just catch the exception and provide an error message (and/or make it a warning/info level log event so it can be turned off more easily) rather than displaying the the Java stack trace." [SEP] "Agree with Sten.  This would be fine, if they had a way of disabling it.  Also, it shouldn't be categorized as an ERROR if there is nothing to act on.  WARNING is far more appropriate" [SEP] "Will this be fixed in the 7.6.* release?" [SEP] "This also happen on version 8 :(" [SEP] "Still happening in version 8.1 :(" [SEP] "same here, version 8.1" [SEP] "Hello [~mkornatzki] and [~a.slomic]! Clarifying, the fix applied from this feature request aims to suppress the excessive number of entries for these exceptions, but the error itself can still appear. The idea is to prevent it from flooding the logs. If you are experiencing a behavior where these exceptions are flooding your logs, please raise a support request at https://support.atlassian.com/contact/" [SEP] "Well

summary: "XSS in the labels widget gadget - CVE-2018-20232"
description: """The labels widget gadget in Atlassian Jira before version 7.6.11 and from version 7.7.0 before version 7.13.1 allows remote attackers to inject arbitrary HTML or JavaScript via a cross site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the rendering of retrieved content from a url location that could be manipulated by the up_projectid widget preference setting."""

CVSS v3 score: 5.4 => Medium severity    *Exploitability Metrics*  || Attack Vector | Network |  || Attack Complexity | Low |  || Privileges Required | Low |  || User Interaction | Required |    *Scope Metric*  || Scope | Changed |    *Impact Metrics*  || Confidentiality | Low |  || Integrity | Low |  || Availability | None |    https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"

summary: "Add the full instructions for Migrating Jira to another Server in the latest documentation"
description: """h3. Summary    When migrating Jira to another server, it is required to copy over the attachment folder from old instance to new instance. However, it is not mentioned in the latest documentation [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/migrating-jira-applications-to-another-server-938846962.html] up until the documentation for Jira 7.4. Along with other important details.    Could you please update it accordingly to be a full and complete documentation like the one written for Jira 7.3 [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/migrating-jira-applications-to-another-server-861253107.html?]"""

summary: "Update Jira AWS Quickstart support information regarding RDS. "
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The documentation around support for the AWS Quickstart template is confusing around what is supported.   {quote}Since we wanted to create a quick implementation, the Quick Start doesn't allow the same level of customization as in the case of manual installation. It should be treated as a reference implementation that shows how JIRA Data Center can be used with AWS, and be either used as-is, or serve as an example for creating your own template. {quote}  ??[Getting started with Jira Data Center on AWS|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/getting-started-with-jira-data-center-on-aws-861253115.html]??    h3. Suggested Solution  As this is similar to using the H2 database for Jira server, we should update our documentation to reflect that the template is for evaluation use only and we recommend to change this to a supported database collation for production use.     e.g. {quote}JIRA ships with a built-in database (H2). While this database is suitable for evaluation purposes, we strongly recommend that you configure JIRA to use an external database.{quote}   ??[Supported Platforms|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/supported-platforms-861253018.html]??  """

summary: "Broken link in jira standalone windows installer"
description: """There is a link in the windows installer to """"How to Install JIRA as a Service"""", the link is currently broken."""

summary: "Ability of automating application links creation"
description: """h2. Description  Currently, applinks need to be manually added on an instance whenever a new environment is setup. This causes extra costs to organizations that need to have instances that need programmatic setup.    h2. Suggested Solution  Allow application links to be automatically added through configuration files (such as jira-properties)"""
comments: "By having a configuration file implemented in the application home directory.  Such a solution may also be valuable to other Atlassian server products, such as Bitbucket/Bamboo/Confluence to provide the same feature (rapid re-delivery of servers), and would be quite valuable to have a replicable file in all cases, say an xml with all necessary fields for various server connections.    Aside from server installation, a use-case I can think of would be security related;  If access between Jira/Confluence/Bamboo or Bitbucket requires strict access control (in a 0-trust environment for instance), then this could be achieved through rapid modification to such a file, provided this resource is not cached in RAM, and could be provisioned through an addon, as opposed to the server app itself."

summary: "Webhook for ""Reopen Issue"""
description: """Currently, we have;   * created (_jira:issue_created_)   * updated (_jira:issue_updated_)   * deleted (_jira:issue_deleted_)   * worklog changed (_jira:worklog_updated_)    events in Jira. It will be nice to have Re-open event as well."""

summary: "Provide a way to set the maximum number of results when syncing with OpenLDAP"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:    When customers connect JIRA to OpenLDAP, in some cases users get randomly disabled and re-enabled after a sync from the OpenLDAP.    The problem usually comes from the fact that the customer has more than 500 users in OpenLDAP, and openLDAP has a default search limit set to 500 users.    Although there is a workaround on the OpenLDAP server side for this issue (which is to increase this limit on the OpenLDAP side), it would be great if we could improve the way JIRA syncs with OpenLDAP, so that the default limit does not cause any sync problem.    h3. Suggestion Solution:    Allow the directory configuration in JIRA to set the maximum limit on the JIRA side instead of the OpenLDAP side.    h3. Workarond    Increase the *olcSizeLimit* limit on the OpenLDAP side to make sure that JIRA can sync more than 500 users."""
comments: "The ""Use paged results"" feature in Jira's user directory settings already makes it possible to set maximum size of search result sets per page, for loading larger search result sets efficiently or working around LDAP server limits. There may be a openLDap specific limitation described here https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CWD-5075  "

summary: "Outlook appointment sent from Jira"
description: """We're looking for an option to send out an outlook appointment from Jira to a person which is linked in a custom field, without opening outlook.         Is there any solution for that problem?         kind regards    Patrick"""

summary: "UPM should manage cross-plugin dependencies"
description: """Jira is becoming a more common platform than ever and it is more and more common to see plugins that """"depend on"""" or """"integrate with"""" other plugins. Universal Plugin Manager (UPM) should also consider and manage these dependencies. For example if plugin A is dependent on plugin B:   * While publishing plugin A in the marketplace we should be able to declare compatibility with plugin B. This declaration should also contain version detail (just like we declare compatibility with specific Jira versions).    * When starting Jira, UPM should load plugins in an order that respects the dependency chain.   * While searching for add-ons through admin screens the UPM should show the version of plugin A that is compatible with the installed version of plugin B. (just like it shows the version of A that is compatible with your Jira)   * While uninstalling (or upgrading or disabling) plugin B from a system, it should warn about plugin A being dependent on plugin B."""

summary: "Typo: ""status"" is misspelled in docs"
description: """h2. Bug report    There is a small typo in all the configuring time tracking docs: [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-time-tracking-938847808.html]          {quote}[Time Reports|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1212077/time-reports?hosting=server&tab=overview]: Flexible time tracking and reporting of time spent on projects, epics, or initiatives broken down by days, users, or issue stases  {quote}          at the very bottom of the page, ‘stases’ should be ‘statuses’       h2. Content design notes    Presumably, need to make sure that the typo is fixed in the master spaces so it doesn't keep reoccurring. I fixed it on the published space."""

summary: "Development field causes slower REST search performance"
description: """h3. Problem  REST search endpoint used to be slow. However it got even slower somewhere between 7.6.10 and 7.13.0    Even single calls take ~1700 ms on 7.6.10:   !image-2018-12-07-16-21-46-028.png|thumbnail!   while it's above 2000ms on 7.13:   !image-2018-12-07-16-21-15-722.png|thumbnail!     When doing proper performance test it shows regression is ~25%:   !image-2018-12-07-16-24-15-846.png|thumbnail!     The content of response differs by 100 lines out of 78k lines of response:   !image-2018-12-07-16-20-52-953.png|thumbnail!     h3. Cause  By default, if no fields are specified when searching via REST, all fields are returned.  As of Jira 7.7, the """"Development"""" field was introduced to provide the ability for users to search information related to the Development Panel.  The result is that this field is included whenever REST searches are performed and causing the longer response times.    h3. Workarounds  When performing REST api searches, ensure to specify the fields:  * Request fields without """"Development"""" custom field. Example:      When performing searches via the UI, an admin can remove the """"Development"""" field from the system default:   !Screen Shot 2019-06-06 at 11.37.24.png|thumbnail! """
comments: "Searching issues on 7.13 is slower than 7.6, because of additional ""Development"" custom field delivered by Development Status plugin. It's an additional cost for providing additional data.    *Workaround*  Request fields without ""Development"" custom field. Example:      *Test results*    Text search - get all fields  7.13 is slower, because it returns additional data.   !image-2018-12-21-17-46-59-371.png|thumbnail!       Text search - get all fields but ""Development""  There is no difference between 7.6 and 7.13 if ""Development"" is not amongst the requested fields.   !image-2018-12-21-17-48-08-597.png|thumbnail!       Text search - get selected 10 fields  There is no difference between 7.6 and 7.13 if ""Development"" is not amongst the requested fields.   !image-2018-12-21-17-47-39-967.png|thumbnail!   " [SEP] "Another workaround is:   # unlock the field   # apply a context to the field to a blank decomm'd project   # success    Note: The panel will not display for any project.     A good resolution

summary: "WebHooks for Version Released should contain startDate and releaseDate"
description: """h3. Summary  Using REST API to [get a project version|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.13.0/#api/2/project-getProjectVersions], we get these:    * startDate and releaseDate are in universal format regardless of user language  * userStartDate and userReleaseDate follows the user language    However, when configuring a WebHook to be triggered when a version is released, we only get userStartDate and userReleaseDate in localized format which are trickier to handle:  bq. Some of our system admins are in different countries, so they will have different national language preferences, resulting in different month names.    (i) It seems the localized format varies depending on the profile language of the admin that creates the WebHook.  (flag) This may affect all Version related events (not tested).    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Log in JIRA as an admin that uses Italian language  # Create a WebHook for Version Released  # Release a version to trigger the WebHook    h3. Expected behavior  The JSON body contains startDate, releaseDate, userStartDate, and userReleaseDate similar to the REST API mentioned above.    h3. Actual behavior  The JSON body only contains userStartDate and userReleaseDate with the month in Italian.    h3. Workaround  Configure the *Day/Month/Year Format* from *Look and feel* to use *YYYY-MM-dd*. This is not ideal when:  bq. Also, we want a number of different JIRA systems (including some not owned by us) to send data to the same webhook endpoint. We can't insist on all users across all those systems having the same date/time format and primary language."""

summary: "Prevent using invalid group/decimal separator when entering numeric values"
description: """If you are using a language where comma is the decimal separator and dot is the group (or thousands) separator, and you enter a numeric value (like when work is logged) using the group separator in any part of the number, Jira will accept the number ignoring the group separator. This also applies for the dot where dot is the group separator and the comma is the decimal separator. This behavior can be confusing and cause customers to log time in the incorrect format. See example  !logged work.PNG|thumbnail!    The suggested solution is to prevent the value from being accepted when it contains a group separator, indicating that it is not a valid value. """
comments: "We would definitely be interested in this being implemented, as we have a time tracking system plugin which pulls data directly from Jira - making a mistake by using the wrong decimal is a real nightmare to sort once the wrong numbers have been sent over to our time registration system." [SEP] "Software should not silently alter the input and introduce errors of orders of magnitude. Please implement this input validation."

summary: "Allow configuring decimal separator "
description: """Currently the user language sets the decimal separator. It would be useful to retain the current behavior and on top of it allow:  * Allow administrators to set a default decimal separator for all users.  * Allow each user to set their own decimal separator from their profile, independently from their selected language.    In this way, for example, users would be able to switch to English, and still use comma as a decimal separator.    Additionally, this request is also linked to [JRASERVER-68600 Prevent using group separator when entering numeric values|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-68600].  """
comments: "{color:#172b4d}Software should not silently alter the input and introduce errors of orders of magnitude. Please implement this input validation.{color}" [SEP] "We have Estonian company, but using English in our Jira as a default language, because we have also other international offices. But in our region we are used to use comma as a separator. Now some forget that "","" cannot be used and 7,5h become 75h. It is huge issue for us." [SEP] "From the original Issue, please see https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-30128?focusedCommentId=1022805&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-1022805"

summary: """Not IN"" Operator for Parent Link in JQL"
description: """In JIRA, it's currently possible to use the IN operator for Parent Links in JQL.         Example:    """"Parent Link"""" in ( EPIC-1, EPIC-2)         However, it does not support Not IN operator.         Example:    """"Parent Link"""" NOT in ( EPIC-1, EPIC-2)         The purpose is to have a quick filter enabled on our boards to hide/show relevant epics.          """

summary: "As a Jira Administrator I would like to limit number of files attached to issue"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In some cases due to automation errors, there could be too many files attached to the issues.  This creates rendering problems and also might cause disk space usage problems.    h3. Suggested Solution  * Create limit for number of files allowed to be attached to the issue, eg: 1000  * Allow this limit to be configurable through jira.properties      h3. Workaround   None"""

summary: "Typing after pasting a link in the WYSIWG editor will add extra characters at the link"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When using Jira's WYSIWYG editor and finishing a sentence with an URL followed by a character (e.g. https://www.atlassian.com.), Jira will include the dot at the URL.    h3. Suggested Solution    When pasting links, ensure that Jira won't add any characters at the URL.    h3. Why this is important    It will ensure that our WYSIWYG editor can be used more efficiently    h3. Workaround    Using https://www.atlassian.com/. instead won't cause the same behavior."""

description: """sdadadasdasdasd"""

summary: "Allow for multiple Epic Issue Type"
description: """h3. Problem Definition     Jira only allows for one Epic Issue type.  h3. Suggested Solution    Users would like the ability to duplicate the issue type and rename it.  h3. Why this is important    Like cloning a ticket, it allows us to customize at the project level instead of the global level. For example, changing the icon reflects globally. """

summary: "When the Jira mail handler reads mail if it rejects any mail it should mark them as read so they are not processed again."
description: """When Jira reads the mail Inbox it reads all unread messages. Since many messages get rejected for bulk, auto reply, etc Jira should mark them as read so not to read them again. It would be nice if this could something that the admin could enable/disable as needed.    Maybe even have an option for allow some notification to the administrator on the rejected mail that was marked as read."""

summary: "Thumbnail size in Jira cannot be bigger than 200x200px"
description: """h3. Summary    As per the documentation - [Configuring file attachments|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver0712/configuring-file-attachments-959314260.html], users are allowed to configure the thumbnail size. However, changing the thumbnail width and height to anything bigger than 200x200px does not reflect on the UI. Note that, the thumbnail(s) under {{$JIRA_HOME/data/attachments/<project-key>/10000/<issue-key>/thumbs}} are generated with the correct dimensions.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open {{$JIRA_HOME/jira-config.properties}}   # Edit (or add) the values of the following properties with values greater than 200   {code:none}  jira.thumbnail.maxwidth = 300  jira.thumbnail.maxheight = 300{code}   # Delete all existing thumbnail images within the attachments directory (that is, those containing '_thumb_' in the filename)   # Restart Jira    h3. Expected Results    Thumbnails in Issue are resized accordingly to the new {{maxwidth}} and {{maxheight}} values.  h3. Actual Results    No changes observerd to the thumbnail size in the issues."""
comments: "Affected in Jira version 8.2.4 as well.    Please consider and fix it soon. "

summary: "Jira incorrectly sorts options from Select List custom fields with multiple contexts in Two Dimensional Filter Statistics Gadget"
description: """h3. Summary    All of the Option sorters in Jira ignore the fact that a custom field can have multiple contexts.  Because of that, they sort only using a sequence number, sometimes grouping items that shouldn't be grouped, and sometimes incorrectly removing results.    h3. Environment  * Using Two Dimensional Filter Statistics Gadget  * Select List with Multiple contexts    h3. Steps to reproduce    One of the scenarios in which an invalid Option sorter is used:   # Set up custom field named """"New Select"""", that is a Select List (any type)   ** Create options A, B in this sequence   # Create some issues for some project to assign the value   ** 1 with New select = A   ** 1 with New select = B   # Create Two Dimensional Filter Statistics Gadget and point to a filter containing these issues (e.g. _New select is not empty_)   # Create new context on """"New Select"""" field, name it context """"Second context""""   ** Select issue type = any   ** Select all projects in the project context   ** Create new options B, C, A in this sequence   # Create new issues with different values of New Select (B, C, or A)   # Refresh the gadget    h3. Expected results  All of the values are displayed properly, being grouped by the option's name, even if the options are assigned to a different context    h3. Actual results  The gadget shows weird results, e.g. two rows for the value """"B"""", while there are also issues with values C and A."""
comments: "Jira will now properly group the options with multiple custom field contexts by their names (starting from Jira 7.6.11, 7.13.1, and 8.0.0)."

summary: "I would like a way to search who has (or hasn't) approved a request through JQL"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As an admin, I’d like to see which users have already approved (or conversely those who are yet to approve) an approval step.    Currently when searching through JQL, the approvers can be viewed but there is no way to tell who has approved or not approved the ticket.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide a way to search for this through JQL or a specific report in Service Desk.  """

summary: "Update documentation about Created vs Resolved gadget"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    In Created vs Resolved gadget, when a user clicked on the circle in the graph, it shows the number of issues created/resolved for the particular month (Eg: December). However, this behavior is not documented in [Created vs Resolved Issues Report|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira064/created-vs-resolved-issues-report-720416052.html] documentation.    This behavior needs to be documented because users could think that the circle in the graph indicates that there are issues resolved on the last day of the month.     !Screenshot 2018-12-13 at 9.12.03 PM.png|thumbnail!       h3. Suggested Solution    Add explanation in the [Created vs Resolved Issues Report|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jira064/created-vs-resolved-issues-report-720416052.html] documentation about the function of the circle in the graph."""

summary: "Created and Resolved gadget interval is broken"
description: """h3. Summary    Currently, if a user configures the Created vs Resolved gadget, the gadget will display random dates which will be misleading.    +Steps to reproduce+    # Create a filter which contains resolved and newly created issues.  # Add Created vs Resolved gadget in a dashboard.  # Add the newly created filter to the filter.   3. Set the interval as """"Period"""" as Monthly  4. Set the """"Days Previously"""" as 180 days  5. Save the gadget and we'll see gadget like below:     !Screenshot 2018-12-13 at 9.12.03 PM.png|thumbnail!     The dates shown in the x-axis are random and misleading. For example, the 28 December shown in x-axis indicates that there are issues resolved on 28th December. At the time the bug is created (13 December 2018), no issue was created in this date.      h3. Environment    JIRA 7.0.10, 7.10.0, 7.12.3, 7.13.0      h3. Expected Results    Each interval in the x-axis should be set as the last day of the month. (Eg: 30/06, 31/07,31/08 etc2)      h3. Actual Results    JIRA is showing random dates in the x-axis.      h3. Workaround    None"""
comments: "Bug confirmed in version v7.13.8" [SEP] "Today, we've migrated from 6.4 to 7.13.8 and discovered the problem of dates in X-axis. This is really annoying cause now, the users don't understand why the last date is in may instead of july"

summary: "Attachment link to specific comments"
description: """Hi,  I find it very difficult to link specific attachment to a comment the other user wrote.  We are using the system to report and follow bugs.   In the process, we upload software versions to be tested and they are usually by the same name and uploaded with comment of how to test the version, Or in other cases the tester upload logs with standard name.  currently I have to check the date and time to link a comment to the attachment.  in case were the ticket is very long it is very problematic.  Tnx Roi."""

summary: "Kanban board should bring only statuses from the projects workflows of the issues returned by the filter."
description: """h3. Summary  A simple JQL like """"Issue in (TST-01, TST-02)"""" that filter two specific issues is recognized as too complex by the board and doesn't pick any specific project workflow to it, bringing all statuses from all workflows of the instance already mapped to the In Progress column. On a big instance, it can get pretty bad to drag all the undesired statuses to the unmapped column.    h3. How to reproduce  Have two issues from a single project TEST that has subtasks from the same project as well. Filter the two issues with the filter:        Create a Kanban board with the filter above. It will load all the statuses from the instance and not only the ones from the workflow of the project TEST.    h3. Desired behaviour  For queries like the one above that specify issues from the same project, it should bring only the statuses from the project's workflow instead of all the statuses of the instance.    h3. Workaround  Remove the column with all the undesired statuses so they will move to the Unmapped Statuses column, and then create a new column again and drags only the statuses you want to it    *or*    Adjust the filter to specify the project as well:        *or* or create a Kanban board specifying the board and not an existent filter.    """

summary: "Similar to the JQL filter 'startOfWeek' and 'endOfWeek', Jira should have JQL filter for 'last week','this week' and 'next week'."
description: """h3. Summary  For more advanced searching, we would like Jira to add filters according to 'Weeks' without having to put date ranges. This would be like the filter 'startOfWeek' or 'endOfWeek'."""

summary: "Created vs Resolved Chart to Show the Present Date "
description: """h3. Definition  If you set the *Days Previously* field to a larger date span, the X axis of the chart does not show the present date.  !chart.png|thumbnail!    In the above example, the date shown is only up to October 2018 whereas the present date is 12th December.     h3. Suggestion  Reduce the chart size if the *Days Previously* field has a larger date span to show the date up to the present. """

summary: "When using CSV import to update an issue, issue is moved to a the old project that it was moved from"
description: """h4. Summary    When using CSV import which contains multiple issues to update an issue, issue is moved to a project that it was previously in. For example, issue DEI-1 was moved to USAPI-3. Then, while performing an import, issue is moved back to the project DEI as DEI-3.   h4. Steps to Reproduce.   # Head to *Administration > System > Import & Export > External System Import*.   # Select *CSV* to open the *CSV File import* page.   # On the *CSV File import* page, select your *CSV Source File*.    # Click the *Next* button to proceed to the *Setup project mappings* step of the CSV file import wizard. Map the issue key, comment and summary.    h4. Expected Results    Jira following the issue key will update only the issues specified.  h4. Actual Results    Jira will move issue to the previous project the issue was on and issues will be updated wrongly.    """
comments: "Hi,     Any update for this or when we could expect this to be fixed?   Would this have more priority than low as if this problem happens there is no possibility to do rollback automatically or based on some rule (only option would be restore backup) ?   With this bug is easy to affect many projects in jira by accident.   " [SEP] "[~pdrygas]  Just FYI"

summary: "Missing custom issue type icons after upgrade"
description: """h3. Summary  After upgrading an instance from v7.3.8 to v7.12.3, there are some missing custom issue type icons. The app was disabled-enabled and re-installed but that did not fix the issue.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Imported a backup of production to development v7.3.8  # Upgrade Jira from v7.3.8 to v7.12.3     h3. Expected Results  Icons should be displayed properly    h3. Actual Results  Missing some icons   !image001.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  The following were also tried  - Uploaded new icons file in issue type  - Manually place file (for which we are getting file not found exception) under ../JIRA_HOME../Application Data../avatars     Uploading icons again fixed _most_ of the screens for our issue types, but still create issue popup couldn't show icons. Other than that, we also re-indexed JIRA but it didn't fix this issue.    Note that other than that after the upgrade, many other icons like project default icon, priority etc. were also missing."""

summary: "JIRA support for Postgres 11"
description: """PostgreSQL 11 has [been released|https://www.postgresql.org/about/news/1894/], but is not a [Supported platform|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html].    Requesting PostgreSQL 11 support."""
comments: "Postgres 10 suggestion here: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-65910" [SEP] "Debian 10 Buster is out with PostgresSQL 11.4. Must have feature in my opinion. Honestly I do not understand why it is not supported already - Everyone on Debian whould now move to 11.4:)" [SEP] "End-of-life of official Jira bundled Postgresql Version 9.4 is only nearly half a year away and the release of Postgresql 11 already nearly one years ago. For enterprises upgrade planning already takes month, so what should be do when Atlassian does not release supporting version in time? At least the better DR concepts of Postgresql 10 and 11 would be needed by as aside from the Performance enhancement." [SEP] "(!) Debian stable features Postgresql 11 and Postgresql 9.6 is already deprecated. Please officially support v11 so i can use the current distribution. It's not like debian uses ble

summary: "Change project selection method of ""Choose applicable context"" in custom fields"
description: """When configuring a custom field and choosing projects for a context, users need to press Ctrl to add new projects other than the existing ones.  The risk is where a user clicks on a single project without pressing the Ctrl key, if he clicks Modify, then the context will be applied to this project only and the previous projects/settings will be lost.    This method is very risky when having hundreds of projects as there is no way to revert this action.         An improvement would be to have 2 windows; one to add and one to remove projects, similarly to the way members are added removed from Jira groups. This will eliminate the risk.     """

summary: "The validation error message overview in Issue Screen feel duplicate to the message already exist under the issue field"
description: """h3. *Problem Definition*    The customer complained about the overview on top (the error message box) and the error message under each required field feel like a duplicate for them.     h3. *Suggested Solution*    Add an option for users to turn off the validation error box.     h3. *Why this is important*     The validation error message is a duplicate of the error message already shown under the fields that didn't passed the validation, so they prefer to have a minimalist design with lesser information displayed.     h3. *Workaround*    As a workaround, I wrote a style script that can be used in the *Administration > System > User Interface > Announcement banner*:   {code:html}   <style>   .aui-message.aui-message-error, form.aui div.error {        display: none;   }   </style>   {code}     h3. *Note*     * This was tested in JIRA 7.7.0 but it should be the same for other versions as well.   * No functionalities is lost as well. """

summary: "The validation error message in Issue Screen shown the custom field ID despite the custom field name has already been shown"
description: """h3. *Summary*    The validation error message in Issue Screen shown the custom field ID despite the custom field name has already been shown.     h3. *Steps to Reproduce*  # Set up a new project for each (Business, Software, and Service Desk) project type.   # Add a new custom field of any type. (I used *User Picker (single user)* here)  # Configure some fields (both system and custom field) to be required in these projects.   # There are two scenarios here: One with the required fields added (affected) and one without.   # This only affecting the full-page Issue Screen.     h3. *Expected Results*    Expecting the validation message is shown with only its custom field name just like its counterpart *+without+* the required custom field in the Issue Screen.   || Description||Business||Software||Service Desk||  |Without required custom field in the Issue Screen \\ (The required custom field is not added to the screen yet)| *+Page+* \\  !Business-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Page+* \\ !Software-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Page+* \\ !ServiceDesk-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! |     h3. *Actual Results*    || Description||Business||Software||Service Desk||  |With required custom field in the Issue Screen| *+Page+* \\ !Business-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Page+* \\ !Software-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Page+* \\ !ServiceDesk-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! |      h3. *Notes*    * This bug is affecting only the full-page Issue Screen as the popup with the required fields added doesn't show the validation error message box. This is being addressed in another feature request ticket ([JRASERVER-68573|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-68573]) to standardize the validation error message box.   || Description||Business||Software||Service Desk||  |With required custom field in the Issue Screen| *+Popup+* \\ !Business-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! | *+Popup+* \\ !Software-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! | *+Popup+* \\ !ServiceDesk-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! |   * This was tested in JIRA 7.7.0 but it should be the same for other versions as well.  * No functionalities is lost as well.    h3. *Workaround*     None.     """

summary: "The validation error message is not standardised across the Issue Screen components for the full-page and popup"
description: """h3. *Problem Definition*    The validation error message is not standardized across the Issue Screen components for the full-page and popup.   * You can see that popup is not showing the message while the full-page does.   * The system field is omitted in the popup as well when the required field is in the Screen.     || Description||Business||Software||Service Desk||  |With required custom field in the Issue Screen| *+Popup+* \\ !Business-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! \\ *+Page+* \\ !Business-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Popup+* \\ !Software-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! \\ *+Page+* \\ !Software-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Popup+* \\ !ServiceDesk-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! \\ *+Page+* \\ !ServiceDesk-With-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! |   |Without required custom field in the Issue Screen \\ (The required custom field is not added to the screen yet)| *+Popup+* \\ !Business-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! \\ *+Page+* \\  !Business-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Popup+* \\ !Software-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! \\  *+Page+* \\ !Software-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! | *+Popup+* \\ !ServiceDesk-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Popup.png|thumbnail! \\ *+Page+* \\ !ServiceDesk-Without-Customfield-In-Screen-Page.png|thumbnail! |     h3. *Suggested Solution*    Standardize all the scenarios above to have the validation error message displayed.     h3. *Why this is important*     Customers find it confusing with the different message shown.     h3. *Workaround*    None.     h3. *Note*     * This was tested in JIRA 7.7.0 but it should be the same for other versions as well.   * No functionalities is lost as well. """

summary: "The Activity Streams feed from SDK doesn't allow to restrict by project"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Activity Streams feed from SDK has a project filter as recorded in  https://developer.atlassian.com/server/framework/atlassian-sdk/consuming-an-activity-streams-feed/ This currently doesn't work.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   (i) A quick way to test this is from the browser itself  # Make sure you have two projects with recent activities   # Make sure you are logged into the Jira instance  # Update the URL as mentioned below:      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Update REST API documentation for ""Update project"""
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, the information on [Update project REST API|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.0/?_ga=2.39423075.1967832540.1544403528-1198903000.1539825650#api/2/project-updateProject] is misleading. The Example provided includes *projectTypeKey* which indicates that this can be updated using this REST API call. However, that is incorrect and we will need to use [Update project type REST API|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.0/?_ga=2.39423075.1967832540.1544403528-1198903000.1539825650#api/2/project-updateProjectType] to update project type.    h3. Suggested Solution  Update the description of [Update project REST API|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.0/?_ga=2.39423075.1967832540.1544403528-1198903000.1539825650#api/2/project-updateProject] to indicate that if we want to update the project type, we will need to use [Update project type REST API|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.0/?_ga=2.39423075.1967832540.1544403528-1198903000.1539825650#api/2/project-updateProjectType]. """

description: """h4. Summary  Method {{com.atlassian.jira.sharing.DefaultShareManager#isSharedWith}} performs call _sharedEntity.getOwner()_ two times.    h4. Suggest fix  Reuse the result    h4. Note  * Method profiling    * Note:  ** isSharedWith(DefaultShareManager.java:64) -> getOwner  ** isSharedWith(DefaultShareManager.java:65) -> getOwner   """

summary: "Unknown RPC service: update_security_token"
description: """h3. Summary    During updating security tokens on dashboard there is an error """"Unknown RPC service: update_security_token"""" which prevents from updating tokens. Moreover in case when first gadget on dashboard is not placed inside iframe the update phase isn't invoked at all.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Prepare a dashboard with plenty gadgets (some with auto-refresh)   # Make sure first gadget is not embedded inside an iframe    # Check console.log(s) - messages that update token is no needed because there are no gadgets on dashboard should appear.    # Move your gadgets, make sure that iframe gadget is on first slot   # Watch your console, errors about update_security_token should appear in a while (you can invoke the refresh function faster using ___st_refresh_ parameter eg. ?__st_refresh=4000)    h3. Expected Results   * Jira should perform token updates for iframe gadgets in all cases - regardless of order   * Jira should should successfully update security tokens for all gadgets inside iframe     h3. Actual Results   * Jira doesn't perform token updates in case when first gadget is outside an iframe   * Jira throws an exception during token update when there is a mix of outside/inside iframe gadgets without auto-refresh.    h3. Notes    Issue is already fixed and will be shipped with Jira 8.0.   h3. Workaround    There is not known workaround """

summary: "Jira user home page is sometimes not changed when selecting it on the user's profile"
description: """h3. Summary  Jira home page is sometimes not set when navigating to *Profile Icon > My Jira Home*.    h3. Environment  * Jira 7.13.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Click on the *Profile Icon*, at the top right of Jira's UI  # Select one of the options from the section *My Jira Home*.    h3. Expected Results  * User's home page is correctly set every time.    h3. Actual Results  * Sometimes the home page is not set at all. When this happens, it's possible to assess the following:  ** No network requests are made by the browser.  ** There are no messages logged in the browser's console.  ** Jira access logs do not show any requests to *{{/secure/UpdateMyJiraHome.jspa}}*.    h3.Workaround  Click once more on the desired option, as this often will result in the browser making the needed request to *{{/secure/UpdateMyJiraHome.jspa}}*."""

summary: "Copying of SearchRequest performs user search"
description: """h4. Summary  Method {{com.atlassian.jira.issue.search.SearchRequest}} CopyConstuctor:     _SearchRequest(com.atlassian.jira.issue.search.SearchRequest)_ performs user search while copying details from initial _SearchRequest_    h4. Suggest fix  Copy the user and avoid searching for a user.     h4. Note  This _CopyConstuctor_ can be triggered by {{getSearchRequestById}} method.   """
comments: "from recent performance data 2018-12-14    h4. getOwner stats  Method: com.atlassian.jira.issue.search.SearchRequest.getOwner  Sample count: 14 (10.94%)  Global frame frequency: 93 (72.66%)    JRASERVER-68564 and JRASERVER-68567 fixes should remove 3 out of 4 {{getOwner}} calls during execution of {{getSearchRequestWithoutSanitization}} (see JSWSERVER-16130).    h5. FlameGraph   [^flame_boardloading_2018-12-14.html] " [SEP] "Huh. It's not obvious to me why DefaultSearchRequestManager.getSearchRequestById() copies a search at all.  Perhaps the docs need a note about that" [SEP] "Hey [~mdoar2],    I understand this could be confusing, {{getSearchRequest}} returns a copy of the object from cache since it could be modified later.  Regarding the docs for {{getSearchRequestById()}}, I don't believe it's the case, this is an internal implementation of the API and covered by 2 layers of abstraction :)     Hope this helps.   Cheers "

summary: "Problem with copying text from PDF preview"
description: """h3. Summary    When user copied few paragraphs from a previewed PDF file and paste it in a text editor, the pasted text does not retain the formatting from the PDF file.    +Steps to reproduce+    # Login to JIRA version 7.6.X, 7.11.X & 7.12.X, JIRA 7.13  # Attach a pdf document  # Click the document to preview the PDF file from JIRA  # Try copying the paragraph or few lines from the preview and try to paste it to a text editor.     !Testing Screenshot.png|thumbnail!       h3. Environment    JIRA 7.6, JIRA 7.11, JIRA 7.12, 7.13      h3. Expected Results    When pasting the copied content everything is copied fine with line breaks.      h3. Actual Results    When pasting the content the line break is ignored.      h3. Workaround    Download the PDF file, open it from a different PDF reader, copy the text and paste the text to a text editor."""

Run the following SQL query in the JIRA database:        If there are any entries there -- please take a note of the entries IDs and proceed to step 2    2) Delete the offending entries from the jiraaction table:        where issue ID1, Issue ID2 - are the entry IDs determined in step 1 above"""

summary: "Wrong month abbreviations used for Deutsch language on different pages"
description: """h3. Summary    On different pages there are different month abbreviations used in date displaying and date pickers.  h3. Environment    Jira Core 7.13.0, Jira Core 8.0.0-m0021  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start Jira Core 7.13.0 with: {{mvn amps:run-standalone -Dproduct=jira -Dproduct.version=7.13.0}}   # Setup Jira with defaults and create sample project and issue   # Got to user preferences and switch language to: Deutsch (Deutschland)   # Navigate to sample issue detailed view (e.g. [http://localhost:2990/jira/browse/DEMO-1)]   # Click Edit and set due date to 10.03.2019. After selecting in date picker the input value is *10/Mrz/19* (screenshot1)   # Save Issue. On issue view you can see due date as *10/Mär/19* (screenshot2)   # Go to project versions administration view (e.g. [http://localhost:2990/jira/plugins/servlet/project-config/DEMO/administer-versions?status=unreleased)]   # Add new version with start date equal;l to 10.03.2019. After selecting in date picker the input value is *10/mär/19*. (screenshpot3)   # Save version. On version list you can see start date as *10/Mär/19* (screenshot4)    h3. Expected Results    Different pages and components use different formats: *10/Mrz/19,*  *10/Mär/19,* ***10/mär/19*  h3. Actual Results    All dates in Jira system for logged-in user should use the same format, language and month names/abbreviations. E.g. Jira should use everywhere on pages and in components: *10/Mär/19*  h3. Workaround    None  h3. Quick investigation    *On Jira issue view:*   {{console.log(Calendar._SMN) > [""""Jan"""", """"Feb"""", """"Mrz"""", """"Apr"""", """"Mai"""", """"Jun"""", """"Jul"""", """"Aug"""", """"Sep"""", """"Okt"""", """"Nov"""", """"Dez""""]}}    *On project versions administration view:*   {{console.log(Calendar._SMN) > [""""Jan"""", """"Feb"""", """"Mär"""", """"Apr"""", """"Mai"""", """"Jun"""", """"Jul"""", """"Aug"""", """"Sep"""", """"Okt"""", """"Nov"""", """"Dez""""]}}"""

summary: "Controll access to Rest API"
description: """h3. Task  I want to control which third party applications can access my Jira Server, both by white- or blacklisting. Thus only applications that are deemed trustworthy can access my Jira Server.      h3. How it could work  * An app must be signed by a valid vendor key and a valid key specific to this application to be able to access the Rest API.   * Jira admins can black or white list apps or a vendor (covering all apps of this vendor, existing and future).  * Access to Rest API is allowed only on basis of the black or white listings; or to all and everybody, if both are empty.    This would definitly be an infrastructure project, but it will appeal to corporations or organisations where more control over their system than usually is needed.    (!) This would be something worth considering across the whole product stack of Atlassian."""

summary: "Cluster cache replication can cause high CPU across all nodes in the cluster and require a restart"
description: """This issue is similar to JRASERVER-63137 in symptom (but likely a slightly different cause).    We have 3 nodes in a Jira cluster. We saw CPU increase and plateau on one node, and then each other node increased as well:   !Screen Shot 2018-12-05 at 10.38.34 AM.png|thumbnail!     Thread dumps show lots of RMI TCP Connection threads 'stuck waiting for response from external systems' (see attached thread dumps, run them through fastthread.io).    This appears to indicate an issue with the cache replication. There were no user reports of incorrect cached data however.    There _were_ reports of timeouts and slowness processing requests, which would be expected with CPU this high. Without performing a restart when we had, this issue would have caused a full system outage.    h3. Workaround  Perform a rolling restart of the cluster (bring each node down and back up before bringing the next node down). CPU will drop back to normal.  """
comments: "[~dunterwurzacher]  Hey Denise,     Thanks for reporting the problem and attaching the threads dump with CPU data.   I've reviewed the files and looks like Jira has Code cache problems with JIT in JVM    h5.  10.104.240.18  results from 10.104.240.18_thread_dumps_20181205_162647.tar   * time-frame:    * CPU/JVM      * So as you can see *C2 CompilerThread* are very CPU active all the time, which matches symptoms you mentioned.     h5. 10.104.241.117  results from 10.104.241.117_thread_dumps_20181205_162639.tar  * time-frame:    * CPU/JVM      snippet of *http-nio-8080-exec-73* thread:       * Looks like there is different situation here, active threads:  ** automation-rule-executor  ** http-nio-8080-exec-73 - findRelatedUsersToMention  ** SdOffThreadEventJobRunner - see JSDSERVER-5736     h5. 10.104.241.240  data from 10.104.241.240_thread_dumps_20181205_162646.tar  * time-frame:    * CPU/JVM      Problem is the same as at 10.104.240.18, *C2 CompilerThread* are very CPU active all the time, which matches symp

summary: "Data Center - Reindex snapshot file fails to create if greater than 8GB"
description: """h3. Summary  Data Center Full re-index fails to create the snapshot file if the output is greater than 8GB. Following the exception. The issue is a limitation of [Class TarArchiveOutputStream|https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-compress/javadocs/api-1.10/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/tar/TarArchiveOutputStream.html] from the default library used by Jira.      h3. Workaround  # Shut down your Jira applications;  # Open the jira-config.properties file (located at the root of your Jira application home directory) in a text editor;  ** (!) This file may not exist if you are using a new installation or an upgraded installation where your previous JIRA application versions had never been customized. If this file does not exist, create it using a text editor;  # Edit the jira.index.snapshot.copy.archiver property in all nodes as follow (you can add it, if it's not there);    # Save your modifications to the jira-config.properties file.  # Restart your JIRA applications.  """
comments: "So our 11GB snapshots mean that we won't be able to use the new Snappy archiver in 7.13? That's a shame"

summary: "Updating issue type via Rest API ignores differences in issue type workflows leading to inconsistent state."
description: """When updating the issue type via Rest API, the differences between workflows is ignored.    Given 2 issue types with the following workflow statuses:   * Bug (workflow: open -> closed)   * Improvement (workflow: feedback -> resolve)    When updating the issue type from Bug to Improvement via the Web UI, it is not possible to update the issue type directly if the target issue type workflow does not have a matching status. You have to go through the """"Move Issue"""" flow and select a new valid status in the workflow that applies to the target issue type.    However, the same does not apply for the Rest API, where you can update the issue type directly (without having to go through the corresponding """"move issue"""" API)    Example: a HTTP POST request to [/rest/api/2/issue/\{issueKey}|https://cd.bb.atl-test.space/jira/testing/rest/api/2/issue/17621] with the following body:    *Expected result:*   * Operation fails with an error    *Actual result:*   * Operation succeeds with 204. Checking the issue through the Web UI shows the new issue type with the old workflow status (which is invalid in the new workflow)."""

summary: "Allow customized colors to be set for Reporting and Gadgets"

When options in Look and Feel are modified it has no impact on the colors used inside of the reports or gadgets. Users would like to customize their colors.  h3. Suggested Solution    Provide options via Look and Feel menu for users to change the colors used inside reports and gadgets.  h3. Why this is important    Customizing colors is important because it allows for admins to use colors best suited for their users. For example, certain reports such as Created versus Resolved are hard to see for colorblind users. Be able to customize colors would allow for the affected user to fully enjoy Jira reporting. """

summary: "FogBugz import not working with attachments"
description: """h3. Summary  In April 2018, Fogbugz started storing new attachments into Amazon S3 and migrated existing ones into S3 as well:  https://support.fogbugz.com/hc/en-us/articles/360011263014-New-Features-2018-04-23. Jira fails to import migrated attachments (older than April 2018) but can import newer attachments, added after April 2018.    It is possible to download the Fogbugz attachment using Fogbugz API.    h3. Environment  Replication requires Fogbugz instance with cases having attachments older than April 2018    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Try to import issues into JIRA using the FogBugz importer provided under JIRA's external system import.    h3. Expected Results  Issues import successfully with their attachments    h3. Actual Results  The import throws errors about certain attachments illustrated below       h3. Workaround  Download attachments separately using FogBugz APIs and then import respective JIRA issues using the CSV importer or using JIRA's REST API.  *Relevant KBs*  [Bulk import attachments to JIRA using REST API|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/bulk-import-attachments-to-jira-issues-via-rest-api-729750741.html]  [Importing data from CSV|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/importing-data-from-csv-938847533.html#ImportingdatafromCSV-howRunningtheCSVfileimportwizard]  [Export FogBugz cases to CSV|https://support.fogbugz.com/hc/en-us/articles/360011243974-Export-to-CSV]  [FogBugz Knowledgebase|https://help.manuscript.com/fogbugz]"""
comments: "FogBugz On Demand have the concept of events which maps to comments in Jira. Both FogBugz events and Jira comments can have attachments. When importing from FogBugz, attachments for events gets added to the Jira issue instead of matching comment. There is no link or mention of the attachment in the imported comment, so its hard to make sense of issues with many attachments and comments. Please, when importing attachment, attach them to matching comment instead of to the issue." [SEP] "We are also waiting on this fix. Our FogBugz case database, including the attachments, contains critical information for our project history. Without being able to import our attachments from FogBugz, Jira is useless to us." [SEP] "We appreciate that you acknowledged that the issue is a bug in Jira Import.    On December 12, 2018, the symptom severity for the issue is set to ""Severity 3 - Minor"". We would argue that it should be ""Severity 1 - Critical"" instead, in fact FogBugz On Demand import for Jira is not usable with t

summary: "Issue collector form is broken "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When embedding JIRA issue collector code into an html or Javascript the issue collector *Provide Feedback* button is not displayed.   The form can't be loaded.    h3. Environment  * JIRA 7.12.3     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install JIRA 7.12.3  # Create a poject  # From the project setting configure an issue collector, copy the code to embed the issue collector form into a test html.  # Follow the JIRA html example and embed the code  # Load the html page    h3. Expected Results  Issue collector *Provide Feedback* button appears and when clicked the form is loaded and we can submit an issue.    h3. Actual Results  The page is broken and both Firefox and Chrome will block the page loading from completion with a console error similar to below:     !Screenshot_20181203_144234.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes  * Most modern browsers will do strict checks on Mime Types and there is no way to stop them from doing that due to security reasons.   * in JIRA 7.12.3, The call to the REST Endpoint */rest/collectors/1.0/configuration/trigger/d60737ee* uses the wrong *Content-Type* header, the expected type is:    While the code sends a wrong content type:     Which causes the browser Mime type check to fail.  * Both JIRA 7.6.10 and 7.13.0 doesn't show this problem.   !Screenshot_20181203_143202.png|thumbnail!  !Screenshot_20181203_151551.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Upgrade to JIRA 7.13.0, the issue is not observed there."""

summary: "Create a field type Component Picker for custom fields"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, a ticket can only have the project components values in the Component/s system field.   In some scenarios, users need to use component data more than once in a ticket. For example: the component where a bug is visible and the component that triggered the bug.   h3. Suggested Solution  Allow admins to create custom field with *Component Picker* type so they can use the project component data outside the Component/s system field.   h3. Workaround  If on *Jira Server*, the [Component Picker add-on|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1217020/component-picker?hosting=server&tab=overview] can be used. """

summary: "AD password expiration and Remember my login"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, if JIRA is configured with AD which has the password policy where users password are expired after a certain time and the user selected """"Remember my Login"""" option in login screen; and the password expires, the user will not be able to login till they clear the browser cache.    This is mainly because the browser needs the existing cookies to be cleared and cookie with an updated credential is created in order for the user to login.      h3. Suggested Solution    Allow JIRA to invalidate the cookie for JIRA if it detects that the authentication failure. This way, the user will be able to relogin to JIRA by inserting their latest password.      h3. Workaround     # JIRA Administrator can clear the token for all the users monthly from """"<JIRA's URL>/secure/admin/user/AllUsersRememberMeCookies!default.jspa"""". This will require the users to update their credential at least once per month.  # Rather than using the """"Remember my login"""" option, users can use browse's password manager."""

summary: "Make Jira DC replication event retry mechanism more resilient"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As a workaround for JRASERVER-64922, a new mechanism has been added to Jira, that retries the failed index operation events.    The current solution works in-memory (is not persistent), not attempting the retries if the node is restarted.     h3. Suggested Solution  A potential improvement would be to store data about the operations to retry in the database.    This would not only allow for a more resilient solution but also to potentially browse the historical data about the failed index replication requests across the DC cluster."""
comments: "[~drauf]  Thanks a lot for creating a new feature request!     you mentioned:  bq. As a workaround for JRASERVER-64922, a new mechanism has been added to Jira, that retries the failed index operation events.  Do we have a ticket (documentation) covering this new retry mechanism?  Or do you suggest that as part of JRASERVER-64922 that retry will be added?" [SEP] "[~ayakovlev@atlassian.com] we are making final touches to the https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-64922, and the fix introduces the retry mechanism described briefly here.    When it gets merged (should be today/tomorrow), I'll post a comment under the 64922 ticket, describing the solution in detail and the additional log messages added. :)"

summary: "The VerifyPopServerConnection resource was vulnerable to SSRF - CVE-2018-13404"
description: """The VerifyPopServerConnection resource in Atlassian Jira before version 7.6.10, from version 7.7.0 before version 7.7.5, from version 7.8.0 before version 7.8.5, from version 7.9.0 before version 7.9.3, from version 7.10.0 before version 7.10.3, from version 7.11.0 before version 7.11.3, from version 7.12.0 before version 7.12.3, and from version 7.13.0 before version 7.13.1 allows remote attackers who have administrator rights to determine the existence of internal hosts & open ports and in some cases obtain service information from internal network resources via a Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability."""

summary: "Make editing issues better by minimizing overwrites"
description: """A common problem is have is overwriting each others issues.  2 of us are creating stories and we don't know when the other is currently the story.  My partner will be in the story, I come in and make a change, then she saves her changes and wipes mine out. (We work in a Kanban setting).    Jira would be greatly improved if it had a marker to tell users when another user had the story open in edit mode.  Here are my suggestions.      If an issue is open in edit mode   # in the issue listing page (Kanban board), add """"In use"""" in red text in the lower right corner above the issue number.   # in the actual issue page, display in red text on the top of the page """"[user name] is currently editing this story.  You risk overwriting your associate's changes, or your associate may overwrite your changes.  """

summary: "Various issues editing links in visual editor"
description: """I'm seeing several issues when editing links in the Visual editor.    On a Mac using Safari 12.0.1 or Chrome 70.0.3538.110, running Mac OS 10.14.1., accessing Jira Server 7.12.3.   # Copy a URL like [https://www.google.com|https://www.google.com/] to the clipboard from any text editor.   # Go to a Jira issue and add a comment, using the Visual editor.   # Type some text, select it, and type command-K. *Nothing happens.*   # Instead, select External Link from the Link menu in the toolbar. The selected text becomes a wiki-style link like {{[test|http://example.com]}}, *which seems wrong since we're in a visual editor*.   # Select the text after the | character, """"{{http://example.com}}"""", and type command-V to paste; this should replace the example URL with the one we copied.   # Instead of changing the text after the | character to the pasted text, it changes {{http://example.com}} to a blue underlined link to [https://www.google.com,|https://www.google.com%2C/]. So the resulting text looks like {{[test|[http://example.com|http://example.com/]]}}. *You get a visual-style link within a wiki-style link.*    Or there's this:   # Go to a Jira issue and add a comment, using the Visual editor.   # Type a URL followed by a space. It becomes a blue underlined link.   # Backspace over the end of the URL and type some text to change it. For example, change [https://www.google.com|https://www.google.com/] to [https://www.google.biz.|https://www.google.biz./]   # Type a space and continue typing. *Everything you type becomes part of the URL*, even after the space. So it ends up looking like: [https://www.google.biz etc etc|https://www.google.com/]   # In addition, *the actual destination of the link is unchanged*; it still goes to [https://www.google.com.|https://www.google.com./]   # And *you can't edit the destination* either. Selecting External Link from the toolbar just wraps another wiki-style link around the original one."""
comments: "I tried submitting this as an issue, rather than a suggestion, but it said ""User [my user] doesn't have the ""Assign Issues"" permission""."

summary: "When you remove a user from a group added to the Dashboard Editors, that user will not be able to edit their own Dashboard"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you remove a user from a group added to the dashboard Editors, that user will not be able to edit their own dashboard directly from the Dashboard.     On Jira 8.5.0-8.5.4, this problem is also experienced if shared to """"Anyone on the web"""", but _Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web_ is turned off. This makes it harder for users to remove this permission from their own dashboards.     h3. Environment   * Jira Software 7.12 introduced the ability to share edit rights for filters and dashboards    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create and Save a Dashboard   # Add a Crowd Group that you are a member of as a Editor of the Dashboard   # Verify you can edit the Dashboard   # Remove your account from the Crowd Group   # You can no longer share or edit the Dashboard directly from the Dashboard despite being the owner.    h3. Expected Results   * Users should always be able to edit a dashboard they own.    h3. Actual Results   * The user can no longer share or edit the Dashboard directly from the Dashboard despite being the owner.    h3. Workaround   # Navigate to Dashboards > Manage Dashboards > My   # Click the gear icon next to your Dashboard   # Add your account as an Editor     * Alternatively, you can add yourself back to the group you were removed from. Users may move between roles in a company but still need to edit the Dashboards they own."""

summary: "Add timezone support to JQL dates."
description: """We should look at adding timezone support to dates. This would allow the users to create dates that were consistent no matter where they are run. The main change will be to JQLDateSupport.ACCEPTED_FORMATS and the tests.    For example: 2008/09/12 GMT.Add time    Here is my scenario. We have Shift 1 in India and Shift 2 in USA. I write a query to find all tickets that are still open to let Shift 2 take over. When a Shift 1 person gives that query to a Shift 2 person it is 12.5 hours into the past, and is useless.    But if I could include a timezone, or at least specify to use server time zone that would fix it.    Copy of JRASERVER-59615    Here is my example query:    project = """"Security Incidents"""" AND status was in (""""Create"""", Detect, """"In Progress"""",Analysis) during (""""2018/11/27 18:30"""", """"2018/11/28 02:30"""") AND """"Assignment Group"""" in (Tier-1, Tier-2) AND status was not in (Closed,Validated) DURING (""""2018/11/27 18:30"""", """"2018/11/28 02:30"""")"""
comments: "I agree this would be very helpful to query issues when you have teams spread across multiple time zones!" [SEP] "I agree too. Please add this ad we have multiple users in different timezones and JQLs return different results when using time-based queries."

summary: "Disable -overstrike markup-"
description: """Jira supports -dashes around text- for overstriking in rich text fields and comments. Finnish text tends to trigger this interpretation unintentionally, because the orthography occasionally requires a dash at the start or end of a word. The overstriking can be removed by adding a backslash in front of each dash, or by replacing each dash with &#45;. However, we don't really use overstriking by purpose, so it would be easier for users if we could disable overstriking altogether in the Jira Server instance, or have the rich text editor add those backslashes itself.    *Example:* Jira Server -koulutus on tiistaina. JavaScript- ja TypeScript-kurssit pidetään seuraavalla viikolla.    *Renders as:* Jira Server -koulutus on tiistaina. JavaScript- ja TypeScript-kurssit pidetään seuraavalla viikolla.    *Translation:* The Jira Server training will be on Tuesday. The JavaScript and TypeScript courses will be held next week.  """

summary: "Users would like for Jira to export columns in search based on what is last on screen"
description: """Users would like for Jira to export columns from search based on columns seen on the screen   # open a saved search with multiple custom fields (columns)   # edit the query   # go to Export > Excel (Current Fields)    Expected result: Export contains the same columns as is seen on the screen    Actual result: Export contains only the fields in personal column order (if you have set this) or the system default    Business case: User would like for Jira to inspect the columns on screen to include in the export so that it is no longer necessary to save the edited filter to a new name. This causes the users to 1) have the extra step of naming and saving the filter, and 2) removing the temporary filter in order to clean-up saved searches     """

summary: "Edit Issue in Microsoft Edge with the Assignee field hidden from Edit Screen will clear the Assignee if it is the current user"
description: """h3. *Summary*    Edit Issue in Microsoft Edge with the Assignee field hidden from Screen will clear the Assignee if it is the current user.  h3. *Environment*   * Microsoft Edge    h3. *Steps to Reproduce*   # Use Microsoft Edge to access JIRA.   # Open a new project and create a new issue.   # Assign the ticket to yourself which is also the current user.   # Edit the ticket and hide the Assignee field (which is current set to the logged in user) simply by untick it from the *_Configure Field > Custom_*.   # Save it and you will notice that the Assignee is clear.    h3. *Expected Results*    Just update the ticket values from the Edit Screen and nothing else.  h3. *Actual Results*    The assignee of the ticket is clear.       h3. *Notes*    This is critical as in this customer case, the ticket involved payment to other parties. Un-assigning the ticket will cause it to go back to their queue of payment processing.  h3. *Workaround*    No workaround other than using a different browser is known at the moment."""
comments: "In addition to the above scenario it also happens when the edit screen has more than one tab and the Assignee field is not present on the first tab. This is impacting lot of our customers.   "

summary: "DC Disaster recovery ignores index snapshots when Snappy or tarball archive is used"
description: """h3. Problem    During disaster recovery, the pattern used for identifying index snapshot files matches only *.zip files. This pattern should include .tar and .tar.sz. Snappy and tarball archive was introduced in Jira 7.12 and users can choose the type of archiving used for creating snapshots. Currently, Snappy is the default option. This will produce snapshots that cannot be used by the disaster recovery mechanism. Since disaster recovery is a feature of Jira DC, this is a regression.  h3. Expected behaviour    Disaster recovery mechanism should be able to use any index snapshot created by Jira itself.  h3. Workaround    In a case where the only available snapshot is a tarball or Snappy archive, unarchiving and compressing it to a zip archive will work. However, if the snapshot size is large, which is expected in Jira DC, this method most likely wouldn't be able to satisfy the high availability requirements.  h3. Notes    See  {{com.atlassian.jira.index.ha.DisasterRecoveryLauncher#restoreIndex}} and {{com.atlassian.jira.index.ha.IndexUtils#INDEX_SNAPSHOT_PATTERN}} """

summary: "DC index copy does not clean up old files when Snappy archiver is used"
description: """h3. Problem   {{DefaultIndexCopyService}} doesn't clean old index snapshots in {{caches/}} under Jira shared home director when Snappy archiver is used. It only works with zip snapshots.  Snappy archiver is used by default for Jira 7.12.0 and newer.    h3. Expected behaviour  {{DefaultIndexCopyService}} should keep at most 3 index snapshots in {{caches/}} under Jira shared home directory.     h3. Workaround 1  Disable snappy archiver by setting      h3. Workaround 2  Manually inspect and remove old {{IndexSnapshot_*}} files from {{caches/}} directory from time to time.     h3. Note  See {{IndexUtils#INDEX_SNAPSHOT_EXT}}."""
comments: "Hooray!   It is good way to remove our cronjob scripts :)    Cheers,  Gonchik Tsymzhitov"

summary: "Update Jira upgrade notes"
description: """*[Jira Software 7.12.x upgrade notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-7-12-x-upgrade-notes-955173978.html]* mentioned:  {quote}  h2. Upgrading from earlier versions?    ...   * *Earlier than 7.0*    Consult the [Migration hub|https://confluence.atlassian.com/migration/jira-7]. The Jira 7.0 release introduced significant changes. You must first upgrade to Jira 7.0 before upgrading to later versions.{quote}  *[Skipping major versions when upgrading Jira applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver071/skipping-major-versions-when-upgrading-jira-applications-802592265.html]* mentioned:  {quote}Jira Software specific notes   If you plan to upgrade from Jira and Jira Agile less than 6.4.x and 6.3.5 respectively, you must upgrade to Jira Software 7.0 before upgrading to Jira Software 7.1.  {quote}  However, it would be great to *instruct users to specifically upgrade to Jira 7.0.11* (the last release of 7.0) to avoid users running into [known issues within the release versions of 7.0.x.|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-7-0-x-release-notes-776997721.html]    Other alternative would be to include *small note about the known issues* so users would just skip the affected version releases.    For example, a user in [GHS-135977|https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/GHS-135977], was in the process of upgrading to Jira 7.0.0 (as the intermediate version), and he ran into the bug JRASERVER-47267 which affected Oracle users and users coming from Jira 6.4.8 or later."""

summary: "Add an option to choose the display of avatars to be round or square"
description: """h3. *Problem Definition*    The JIRA is recently updated to use the round avatar but there are customers that prefer the square avatar.     h3. *Suggested Solution*    Add an option for the customer to choose round or square according to their preference. It will be even better if this can be done on user-basis instead of the entire instance.     h3. *Why this is important*     Some avatar or logo are designed in a square so the rounded avatar caused it to not being displayed fully.     h3. *Workaround*    As a workaround, you can add the following to your Announcement Banner at *Administration > System > Announcement banner (under User Interface)*.   {code:html}  <style>  .aui-avatar-inner {      display: flex;      justify-content: center;      align-content: center;      border-radius: 100%;      overflow: visible;  }  </style>  {code} """
comments: "You can add this in addition to round the edes:  " [SEP] "+"

summary: "Improve style of h1 heading inside of Activity Stream"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When text fields which use Wiki style renderer are updated, the activities are shown in Activity Stream.    If """"h1"""" heading is used in the update, it is rendered differently in Activity Stream compared with the one in the issue page. The heading is alined in the center.     !image-2018-11-28-10-41-32-465.png|thumbnail!     When we check it on the developer console, the style seems to be specified as follows.    {code:css}  .gadget h1 {      text-align: center;  }  {code}    This behavior can be seen regardless of the dashboard layout.  Also, the other headings like """"h2"""", """"h3"""" are working as expected.    h3. Suggested Solution    It would be nicer if """"h1"""" heading is rendered as the other ones are."""

summary: "Provide granular permissions for managing releases and versions"
description: """Currently Jira requires that a user have project administration permissions in order to create and manage versions and releases.    This is not particularly practical for software dev shops that have release managers. We certainly don't want to have to allow our release manager access to all of the administrative functionality that project administration access allows, but there's currently no way to allow someone in that role to manage versions and releases without providing that access."""
comments: "This is essential since we need to keep tight permissions to configure permissions/roles due to audit and compliance, we want to grant projects as self service as possible." [SEP] "Is this not a duplication of [12891|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-12891]? If so, please vote on that item."

description: """When a customer writes a long comment I will copy that text into a new comment and then surround my comment in brackets to denote my response.    For example: ***    { !https://jira.cargill.com/images/icons/emoticons/check.png|width=16,height=16!  done}    *    When I am writing the comment the (/) icon appears correctly, but when I save the comment it shows:    {{* {(/) done}* }}    where the checkmark icon does not render as an icon image and stays as text.    When I edit the comment and a space before the text that should render as an icon and then save the comment again then the text renders as the appropriate icon.         I expected to not need to separate the text that should render as an icon by spaces.         _I tried to add as a bug, but it gave me an error that said that I do not have assign privileges._"""
