summary: "As an admin, I would like Jira to handle large log rotation more gracefully"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When logging is enabled at a high level, for example, all com.atlassian packages.    Log4j cannot handle log rotation and the atlassian-jira.log grows extremely quickly.    h3. Suggested Solution  - Add a warning or prevent users from adding logging at this level.   - Use a different approach for logging.    h3. Workaround   - Add more resources to support the rotation   - Change the log4j settings to rotate at a larger amount of data (larger than 20MB) or   - Change the debug logging level for the package"""

summary: "GC params limitation on Windows"
description: """Java 9 added a common logging system for JVM components. Unified JVM logging system has one drawback which I found during Java 11 migration. We cannot define an absolute path in Windows e.g.    because    is not compatible with logging system syntax.    More information about syntax:   * [https://dzone.com/articles/disruptive-changes-to-gc-logging-in-java-9]   * [https://grokonez.com/java/java-9/java-9-unified-jvm-logging]   * [https://blog.gceasy.io/2017/10/17/43-gc-logging-flags-removed-in-java-9/]     *Workaround*: Customers can use relative paths.    I am glad I could help.    Best regards,    [~mmasiorski@atlassian.com]         CC: [~lwlodarczyk]"""

summary: "SQL for checking usages of version in custom fields is slow even if no version picker custom fields exist"
description: """h4. Summary    When deleting a version, Jira checks if the version is used in custom fields.  This is done in methods _versionService.getCustomFieldIssuesCount_ and _versionService.getCustomFieldsUsing_.    The check is very slow on big instances, even if the instance contains no custom fields that are version pickers.  h4. Expected results    If instance contains no custom version pickers, the queries should execute almost immediately.  h4. Notes    For a bigger context, see https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-67198   """

summary: "Jira - Clarify documentation on temporary directories <JIRA_HOME_DIR>/tmp and <JIRA_INSTALL_DIR>/temp"
description: """There are two temporary directories that Jira uses;   * <JIRA_HOME_DIR>/tmp   * <JIRA_INSTALL_DIR>/temp    Documentation, [Important directories and files|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/important-directories-and-files-861253886.html], states for <JIRA_HOME_DIR>/tmp that  {quote}*tmp*    Any temporary content created for various runtime functions such as exporting, importing, file upload and indexing is stored under this directory.    You can remove files from this directory while JIRA is running, but we recommend that you shut down JIRA first before altering the contents of this directory.  {quote}  However, this definition seems to belong to <JIRA_INSTALL_DIR>/temp instead of <JIRA_HOME_DIR>/tmp, as java.io.tempdir environment variable is set to <JIRA_INSTALL_DIR>/temp on a default installation.  h3. Current outcome    As described above  h3. Expected outcome    <JIRA_INSTALL_DIR>/temp is added to the documentation with the correct definition"""

However, during JIRA upgrade, all the bundled language packs will be restored automatically and the JIRA Administrator will have to remove the unused language package manually again.    Some JIRA Administrators would like feature with the upgrade where JIRA will only install language pack currently available in <installed-plugins>.      h3. Suggested Solution    Add a feature in JIRA where JIRA will be checking the bundled language pack available in <installed-plugins> folder and only install the language stored in the <installed-plugins> during each upgrade.      h3. Workaround     Manually remove the unused bundled language packs from <installed-plugins> folder """

summary: "Look and Feel changes do not affect Quick search"
description: """h3. Issue summary  Changing the color configuration in the Look and Feel functionality does not affect the Quick search styling.    h3. Steps to reproduce   # Go to Administration > System > Look and Feel   # Modify the color scheme for _Menu Item Highlight Background Color_ or any other relevant configuration.   # Refresh the page and go to the system dashboard.    h3. Expected results  The selected color should change the link colors everywhere in JIRA.    h3. Actual results  The link colors do not affect the items in the Quick search menu.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!  !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Apply this on the announcement banner, replacing the colors/color codes in it to be what you require :    * Full credit to [~it1344275202] for this workaround."""

summary: "JQL proximity searches are behaving differently on cloud and server"
description: """text searching seems to have some differences between server and cloud    specifically this proximity search     text ~ """"\""""like a\""""~0""""    on the server returns an exact match where 'like' and 'a' are adjacent    on the cloud this returns items with text between the words.    Is this a known issue.    Raised in question    [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Software-questions/How-do-you-search-for-an-exact-phrase-in-JIRA/qaq-p/944817#M36814]    is this a known feature or bug?    To recreate run the above JQL search on words known to be adjacent and separate in different issues using server and cloud instances"""
comments: "bump" [SEP] "How are you still gathering feedback on this? Google-like searching for an exact string surrounded by quotes has been standard for two decades and It Just Works in github issues." [SEP] "We have the same exact issue here. We are on cloud. How can it be so difficult to match a string exactly?" [SEP] "Trying to use validators based on whether the subject already exists. This fails when another issue _contains_ the subject.         Example: subject is ""Nicks Car Wash"" exists, so subject ""Car Wash"" does not validate because the second one exists (""~"") in the first. I need to validate based on an exact match (""="") OR equality (""=="")" [SEP] "Any news with this?" [SEP] "I really want to be able to search for ""test_create_feature_stream"" without getting the results of test create feature stream. I want just things that mention the method name.     " [SEP] "bumping this... this is frustrating. I guessing this has something to do with word stemming. It would be nice to be able to turn that o

summary: "As a Jira Administrator  I would like to create Lucene index snapshot manually at local Jira home. "
description: """h4. Problem  Currently, you can create Jira Lucene Index snapshot only by scheduling it.  In some cases, you may want to create snapshot ad-hoc so later it can be reused (and recovered at another node).     h4. Suggested option  Create option to create a snapshot ad-hoc in local Jira home. """
comments: "I would remove the  ""at local Jira home"" - the snapshot should land in the shared home so it can be accessed manually or automatically from other nodes; " [SEP] "[~mswinarski]  got it  What will be the option for Jira standalone? Will it fall back to at local Jira home?"

summary: "JQL query doesn't return matching issues if fixVersion field is a numeric string (123) instead of traditional version float/double value (1.2.3)"
description: """h3. Summary    JQL query doesn't return matching issues if fixVersion field is a numeric string (123) instead of traditional version float/double value (1.2.3). Only queries with exact matching {{fixVersion = 123}} returns expected result(s)    In the background:   * If a float/double value is searched for - looks like Lucene takes it as the *field value* (vname column)   * If an integer/string is searched for - it seems Lucene considers it the *field string* (id column)    h3. Environment   * Tested on 7.6.9    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a version which is an numeric string like viz., '100' and *not* 1.0.0   # Assign the version as the fixVersion on an issue   # JQL search with {{project = XYZ and fixVersion was 100}}     _the above would *not* show any result_     # JQL search with {{project = XYZ and fixVersion = 100}}     _the above will show result_    h3. Expected Results    Since it's valid to enter any varchar value in Version (""""vname"""" column), the expected behaviour would be to get results against {{project = XYZ and fixVersion was 100}}       h3. Actual Results    Here's my projectversion table:       * JQL {{project = uyfkhgf AND fixVersion was 10}} doesn't return matching issue(s)     * JQL {{project = uyfkhgf AND fixVersion was 10000}} shows matching issue(s)     * JQL {{project = uyfkhgf AND fixVersion = 10}} shows matching issue(s)    h3. Workaround   * Use the field string (id) value in your JQL query   * For older Jira version (6.4.13) where we could enter double-quotes (fixVersion was """"10"""") in the JQL query) it would work"""

summary: "Update JIRA AWS quick start quide to point to Bitbucket repo"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The https://aws.amazon.com/quickstart/architecture/jira document (referred to in our official docs e.g. https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/getting-started-with-jira-data-center-on-aws-938846966.html) points to a template maintained in https://github.com/aws-quickstart/quickstart-atlassian-jira which hasn't been updated in a while. This repo points users to raise feedback in a different repo on Bitbucket https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment which appears to be more up to date.    h3. Suggested Solution  Update our official JIRA Data Center getting started documentation to point to newer templates in the Bitbucket Repo instead.    h3. Why this is important  * The AWS template in the Bitbucket repo template is actually more up to date with more customization options and features than the old template e.g it provides more choices of EC2 instance types. It is also more consistent with the quickstart deployment for other Atlassian products  * The old repo is no longer being maintained so pointing to it in our documentation does create some confusion to users."""

summary: "Missing 'browse attachment' icon in Create Issue screen when default language is not English"
description: """h3. Summary  The browse attachment icon is missing in the Create Issue screen. This only occurs when Jira's default language is not English.    h3. Environment  Jira configured to use any other language but English, as default language    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to Jira Administration > System > General Configuration  # Edit the 'Default language' from English to some other language on the list  # Trigger the Create Issue screen and observe the Description field    h3. Expected Results  The 'browse attachment' icon appears next to the 'link' icon in the Description field  !browse-attachment-1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  The 'browse attachment' icon is missing in the Description field   !browse-attachment-0.png|thumbnail!    h3.Workaround  Use the 'browse' feature in the Attachment field"""

summary: "As a Jira Admin I want to configure a tolerate threshold for amount of errors during Jira reindexing"
description: """h4. Problem  Jira has full reindex process. If any error happends during this time, error is accumulated and showed at the end, eg:      In some cases, reindexing process for 3m+ issues might take 20+h and at the same time, number of errors could be quite small (~10) so it's preferable to have non-consistent index then to loose all that accumulated work.   It could also mitigate problems like JRASERVER-69826     h4. Suggestion  * Allow Jira Admin to decide tolerated level of error during reindexing    Extra  * Create a tool which will allow to identify missing issues and fix the discrepancy. """

summary: "Improve logging for Jira Lucene reindexing"
description: """h4. Problem  Jira has full reindex process. If any error happends during this time, error is accumulated and showed at the end, eg:      The problem is that the error just says: _Indexing completed with 2 errors_ without providing more details, so it doesn't help with troubleshooting and it's quite hard to find the cause.     Actual problem could be many hours (logs files) before, eg:        h4. Suggestion  Improve logging and accumulate actual error. """

summary: "Information about on which node the Background Reindexing is triggered."
description: """h3. Problem Definition.  If we run an Indexing especially Background Reindex, while it will be executed in one node, the information will be shown in the other nodes as well. In case that there is a problem with the Re-indexing and it is stuck, there is no way we can know which node is doing the Indexing from the UI. Then, if canceling is not doing anything as well, the only way to stop it is to stop the node but since there is no information on which node that is doing it, we won't know which node that needs to be stopped. While we can check this in the logs or database, it is not an easy task to do.    h3. Suggested Solution.  To have the Reindexing page to show which node is doing the background reindexing to help the Admin and Support on the investigation in case there is an issue with the Reindexing process.    h3. Notes  To get the information from the database, we can use the query below with a caveat:    (!) The message will only appear if the Reindexing is already finished    (!) There is a cleaner job for this table hence if the row is old enough, it will be removed  """

summary: "As an admin, I would like the Web Hook URL to also append variable for Transition Name"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently it is not possible to append the transition name into the wehbook URL.     h3. Suggested Solution    Add transition name as a valid variable substitution in the webhook URL"""

summary: "Allow Administrator to limit Component field for single selection instead of multiple selection"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, the default Component field allows users to insert multiple values. However, some administrators would like to allow users to only insert a single value at a time.      h3. Suggested Solution    Allow JIRA Administrator to set the Component field as either single selection field or multi selection field.      h3. Workaround     Users can install the JIRA Misc Workflow Extensions and add an additional validator """"Field Has Single Value"""" in the transition to check only a single selection is made for the component.    (i) This will require installing a third party add-on."""

summary: "Jira deletes AD user record from the cwd_user table when user is removed from Jira Access group if there is an internal account with the same username in the database"
description: """h3. Summary    If we have an user in both the internal and LDAP directory and remove him from the LDAP synchronization, Jira deletes the LDAP row from the cwd_user table.  If we have an user only in the LDAP directory and remove him from the LDAP synchronization, Jira will only disable the user instead of deleting from cwd_user.    h3. How to reproduce  Let's say we used to have only an Internal Directory, and decided to migrate them to an LDAP directory (AD). After the migration we'll have in the database two accounts for the same username, one from internal and other for the AD accounts.  When the AD accounts are expired or we have to remove the user access from Jira, the Jira Access group is removed from his account.    *Expected*: Jira turn the user inactive in the database but keep his records.  *What happens*: Jira deletes the LDAP row from the cwd_user table    This causes any user that is expired or removed from the group, when restoring his access to Jira, Jira creates a new accounts instead of just reactivating the previous one, losing all it's group memberships and other information.      h3. Workaround  Only workaround we can apply is to manually remove the unused internal users from the cwd_user table.  The below query should do the trick, but review it to match your case and IDs may vary:    """
comments: "Hi all,    I increased the Symptom Severity to major because of the potential of user data loss. At the same time, this seems like a rather rarely occurring scenario hence setting the Occurrence Factor to 10%.    Cheers,  Pawel, Jira Developer"

summary: "As an administrator, I'd like to see the IP addresses for my nodes stored in the clusternode table"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Presently no IP information for nodes are stored in the *clusternode* table.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add a new column to the table to store each node's IP address.    h3. Why this is important  In environments (such as AWS) where nodes may be added/removed with a degree of regularity, it would be very helpful to store IP address information for nodes. This can helpful to query for scripts that are targeted for specific nodes.  """

summary: "Workflow scheme actions leave left-over rows in 'clusterlockstatus' table."
description: """*Summary*   When deleting something in a workflow scheme on Jira Data Center, the entry in the 'clusterlockstatus' table remains left behind. The 'locked_by_node' for that row will be empty, but they pile up in large numbers.    *Environment*   Jira Data Center 7.6.1/7.8.4 with two nodes   Postgres 9.3   h2. *Steps to Reproduce*    +*Scenario A, delete a workflow scheme*+   1. Delete a workflow scheme.   2. Once deleted, the 'clusterlockstatus' table will have a new entry like the example below (trailing number is corresponding to 'id' in 'workflowscheme' table.)    3. Repeat step 1 and another entry will be there (and so on).    +*Scenario B, changing the associated issue types*+   Edit a workflow scheme and add or remove an issue type.    Adding issue type to workflow scheme, 2 new rows added.    Removing issue type from workflow scheme, 3 new rows added.    (trailing number corresponding to 'id' in 'workflowschemeentity')         *Expected Results*   Once the operation is done, the entry in the 'clusterlockstatus' should be removed.     *Actual Results*   'lock_name' entries like """"com.atlassian.jira.workflow.DefaultWorkflowSchemeManager$WorkflowAction.DELETE_ENTITY_10105"""" pile up in the 'clusterlockstatus' table.    One customer had over 8000 of them.    *Note*  Caused by JRASERVER-69114    *Workaround*    If necessary, delete the unnecessary 'lock_name' entries manually."""
comments: """ If necessary, delete the unnecessary 'lock_name' entries manually."" - so when might this be necessary? What is the impact, if any, on Jira? We have thousands of such rows" [SEP] "Hey [~mdoar2]   bq. We have thousands of such rows  This makes it necessary :) There is no exact threshold here.     bq. What is the impact, if any, on Jira?  There is no direct impact, it just pollutes the table. The indirect impact will be larger table size and eventually slower search in {{clusterlockstatus}}.    Hope it helps. "

summary: "Custom Field Optimizer shows blank screen (Jira Data Center) with Oracle database"
description: """h3.Summary  Users on Data Center trying to open the CustomFieldOptimizer after the first scan will get a blank page and will not be able to make use of this feature.    h3. Symptoms    The 'Custom Field Optimizer' is running into the following NullPointerException:      This is affecting JIRA application running with Oracle database.    h3. Cause:  When we run the optimizer scan, """"AO_0AC321_RECOMMENDATION_AO"""" database table is populated. If the PROJECT_IDS is empty, NULL is inserted instead of en empty string -- for Oracle database.    package com.atlassian.jira.optimizer.ao  where class resides -  {code:title=RecommendationsDao.class}  public static List<Long> parceProjectIdString(String projectIdString) {          return """""""".equals(projectIdString) ? Collections.emptyList() : (List)Stream.of(projectIdString.split("""","""")).map(Long::parseLong).collect(Collectors.toList());      }  {code}    h3. Reproduce  # Create Oracle database for Jira  # Connect Jira DC to database  # Create Project (empty or sample)  # Create custom field with global context  # Navigate to *Jira administration* -> *System* -> *Custom Field Optimizer*  # Do initial scan  # Come back to 'Custom Field Optimizer' page    h3. Expected Results  User should see recommendations    h3. Actual Results  Blank screen for this feature    h3. Workaround   No workaround for Oracle database users, as """"AO_0AC321_RECOMMENDATION_AO"""" is always re-populated on scan, so removing the NULL records won't work."""

summary: "Quickstart AWS template for Jira does not set up SSL properly if an SSL certificate is specified during setup"
description: """h3. Summary  Quickstart AWS template for Jira does not set up SSL properly if an SSL certificate is specified during setup.    h3. Environment  * AWS    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy a Jira AWS Quickstart template specifying an SSL certificate    h3. Expected Results  * The load balancer includes an SSL listener.  * {{JIRA_INSTALL/conf/server.xml}} includes the appropriate settings in the connector, like in the example below:    * Base URL is correctly set to https://<DOMAINNAME>.    h3. Actual Results  * Load balancer does not include an SSL listener.  * The connector in {{JIRA_INSTALL/conf/server.xml}} has incorrect parameters such as {{scheme}} and {{proxyPort}}:    * Base URL is set to http://<DOMAINNAME>.    h3.Workaround  * Manually add an SSL listener to the load balancer.  * Change the connector settings in {{JIRA_INSTALL/server.xml}} in all nodes to correctly set the {{proxyPort}}."""

summary: "Cannot create issues with ""Assignable User"" restricted by custom group field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Cannot create issue after populating assignable user field and modifying the assignee field on create issue screen     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a custom field as Group Picker type (Single type/Multi group)    !CustomField.png|thumbnail!   # Associate the custom field with the issue screen   # Choose the newly created custom field as the value for Granted to for Assignable User for Issue permission for a project    !IssuePermission.png|thumbnail!   # Create an issue and populate the Custom field and modify the Assignee field from Automatic to the desirable user (In this example , My group picker is the custom field whose value has been populated as jira-software-users and Assignee has been modified to local admin  # Issue does not get created and error pops up """"User 'localadmin' cannot be assigned issues.""""   !CreateIssue screen.png|thumbnail!   # Once the Assignee field has been set back to Automatic/Unassigned the issue gets created.  # Post issue creation, the Assignee field can be modified to desired user    h3. Expected Results  Assignee field should get populated with desired user on create issue screen and issue should be allowed to get created     h3. Actual Results  Issue does not get created and error is being thrown """"""""assignee: User 'XXXX' cannot be assigned issues.""""    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "This is a large issue for our client.    I thought about using another field for the input, adding a custom workflow validator for group membership, and then postfunction to copy that to assignee. Unfortunately, due to JRASERVER-64719 we cannot do that either. We need CSV import to work, and right now it does not really work with validators.    As it is, there does not seem to be a good way to enforce this kind of constraint." [SEP] "Same functionality would be beneficial when *editing issue*: to change group of assignable users and assignee in the same edit screen."

summary: "Unable to mark a priority as 'default' under Default Priority Scheme"
description: """h3. Summary  Before Jira 7.6, it *was possible* to mark a priority as Default so that when users create new issues, Jira will pick that Default priority.  After 7.6 and the introduction of Priority Schemes, it is not possible to mark a priority as 'default' under Default Priority Scheme.    Next to that, when upgrading from pre 7.6 release to post 7.6 release, if there was a default priority set, then it is lost now.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set up a Jira instance on 7.5  # Set a particular priority as default  # Upgrade to Jira 7.7  # Notice that the default priority is now lost. So when users create an issue, Jira might start picking up a different default priority and there is no way to change this.     h3. Expected Results  Jira should continue to use the old default priority and even provide means to change it.    h3. Actual Results  The default priority may have changed and there is no way to change it.     """
comments: "Is there a way to work around this by updating relevant database records? The default value reference must be stored somewhere."

description: """h3. Summary    {{AG.getAbsoluteURL()}} method in atlassian-gadgets-publisher-plugin returns URL with duplicate context paths in some specific circumstance.    h3. Environment    * atlassian-gadgets-publisher-plugin-4.2.31.jar  * Base URL is set as the following format  *- http(s)://*xxx*.yyy.zzz/*xxx*  *- http(s)://*aaa*.bbb.ccc.ddd/*aaa*    h3. Steps to Reproduce    Please copy the following minimum code and paste it to your browser's developer console.        h3. Expected Results    The test code should return """"https://xxx.yyy.zzz/xxx"""".    h3. Actual Results    The test code returned """"https://xxx.yyy.zzz/xxx/xxx"""".     h3. Notes    This problem occurred because of the behavior of {{endsWith()}} method.  It seems to try to match the context path from *the beginning* of the base URL.    """

summary: "When creating an issue, Configure Fields list will have missing fields when there are too many custom fields"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Jira, users have the option to configure which custom fields they'd like to show on screen while creating an issue via a Quick Create (clicking the *Create* button while viewing an existing issue, for example).    However, it appears that options will completely fail to show or render when there are too many custom fields configured; the actual threshold is still uncertain at this time.    h3. Environment  Jira 7.6.7    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a large number of custom fields in the instance (for this test, 30 custom fields were sufficient to reproduce the issue)  # Configure a screen to show these custom fields. Consider separating them between different tabs to exaggerate the issue.  # Set this screen as the *Create Issue* screen for a project and issue type  # Attempt to create an issue    h3. Expected Results    When attempting to select custom fields using the *Configure Fields* drop down list, all custom fields and tabs will show.    h3. Actual Results    Some tabs and fields will be missing completely. Some tabs and fields will be present, but there is no visible scroll bar to scroll down, and so a trackpad/mousewheel must be used.    Screen Configuration showing 5 different tabs:   !Screen Configuration.png|thumbnail!     *Tab4* and it's fields are completely missing:   !Configure Fields.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Attempting to resize the zoom on the browser may allow some fields to come in and out of visibility, but this does not seem to affect the fields and tabs which were outright not rendered."""
comments: "Hi, When can we expect an update on this issue?"

summary: "User Permission KB Could vs Server inconsistency"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In the KB [How to restrict project access to different isolated user groups | https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-restrict-project-access-to-different-isolated-user-groups-290750862.html]  Is listed in the [Jira Server KB Space | https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-knowledge-base-781397172.html] and Notes:      However the KB does included Steps for both Jira Server and Jira Cloud, BUT the current Steps listed for Jira Cloud are not up to date with the current Cloud ADG3 Layout, as an EXE one section reads:  {quote}  granting the groups access to your instance at (Image of the Admin Settings Cog wheel that no longer exists in cloud) > User management > Application access and clicking the View configuration button for JIRA Cloud users.  {quote}    But the Actual Path for Jira Cloud currently is:  {quote}  (Image Icon for the new AppSwitcher the 9 square boxes in the lower left) >> Site Administration >> SITE SETTINGS >> Product Access  {quote}    h3. Suggested Solution  The Cloud Specific Steps should be removed from the Server KB and secondary KB for Cloud Specific configuration Added to the [Jira Cloud KB space | https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-knowledge-base-781397172.html], then add links between the two platform specific articles for easy transition between the Articles and better visibility.     h3. Workaround  Currently the Article Does Indicate the correct steps for permission changes and the actions to take for a Cloud Instance but the navigation paths to the menus are incorrect use """"G+G"""" or """"."""" to open up the search navigation to locate the desired menu location to make the noted change"""

summary: "Jira Version Control in Configurations"
description: """I tried to submit a support request with my question, but I am only allowed to submit suggestions.  I'm hoping this finds its way into the right hands...    I am trying to determine what Atlassian's solution is to Version Control in Jira Configurations.  For example: if a change is made to a workflow configuration, how would you version control this change?  We are looking to apply version control to several places within our Jira Administration settings, but I am not seeing the best practice way in doing so.  """

summary: "Documentation request for Event mappings"
description: """In regards to Events in Jira with notifications, it's not always known what specific fields map to which specific notification events. For example, the Resolution field maps to the *Issue Resolved* event but sometimes, you see custom fields updated and it's not clear what kind of events/ fields map to one another. It would be useful to have some specific developer documentation in regards to what fields map to which events as this can be helpful from an event creation perspective."""

summary: "Please allow special character creation (e.g. ⚠️) also in summary line / cards"
description: """In description field special characters can be created esily and effectively by combination of ASCII characters.    A very useful special character (""""emoticon"""") is the yellow warning triangle, which can be created, inserting parenthesis+!+paranthesis, like (!)    This sign could be effectively used to mark tasks within a """"Task Board"""" as """"critical"""" or """"escalated"""" if it could also be created within the subject line of a ticket.    Unfortunately this shortcut only creates a (!) if used within the """"Description"""" field (see number 1 in added screenshots), but does not create a warning tiangle if used within the """"Summary"""" field (see number 2 in added screenshots).    Consequently you cannot tag that way a task within a card (e.g. on a Jira task board), as it only would show up like number 3 in added screenshots.    We believe that Atlassian could increase the value of Jira very effectively and yet with low effort if creation of a yeellow warning triangle would also be enebled within Subject line !    +Suggested Change Request+:   * *Enable creation of yellow warning triangle also within Subject line of Jira ticket !*         Remark:  JOPSCLOUD-128 seems to request a similar (the same ?) change, which would add significant benefit to Jira while only requiring almost negligable effort by Atlassian."""
comments: "Hi [~gerhard.landauf], thanks for reaching out. This JOPSCLOUD project is for the Jira Ops Cloud product offering specifically. Your request seems to be about Jira in general and based on the screenshots it seems that you're using Jira Server. I'll move this to the right project for the Jira Server team to address.    The linked issue is a distinct problem specific for the Jira Ops Severity field, so I'll remove that link." [SEP] "[~gerhard.landauf] - here's a couple of quick suggestions:   * [You can use flagging|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/flagging-an-issue-938845533.html] on Jira Software boards (version 7 and later, I think) to indicate an issue that is blocked or requires additional attention. The semantics are up to your team.   * You can use Unicode Emoji in Jira summary fields, as long as your Jira Server instance has the encoding settings set correctly. [Warning sign|https://emojipedia.org/warning-sign/] is an emoji you can use: ⚠️    Hope this helps!" [SEP] "Thanks @Matt

description: """Currently, in the Bulk Operation feature, there is already an option for moving the issues over to an existing or new project.  However, it would be very convenient and time saver to also have a bulk copy option.     +*Use case*+  For our software, we have a large collection of dev and user requirements that always gets used each time we start a project, in addition to having to create new ones for the same project.  These requirements are detailed and have screenshots.  As a result, we created a """"template"""" as a master project that houses all the re-usable requirements.  Since we can only bulk move the issues, we have to clone each issue individually from the master project template and then move them over to the new project. You can imagine how much time is wasted by having to do this each time we start a new project.  Having the option to bulk copy and then move at the same time so that we can keep the master project """"template"""" in tact would be awesome!    Thank you!    Ruddiah Cinéus  Project Coordinator  Infoware  50 Richmond Street East  Toronto ON     !image-2018-11-19-09-57-59-137.png!"""

summary: "PostgreSQL 9.3 Deprecation in the Supported Platform page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition.  From the link below, we could see that Postgres 9.3 is stated with the icon (!) that means """"Deprecated - support for this platform will end in an upcoming release.""""  - https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html  (i) The documentation content might change following JIRA Releases as the link is for the latest available JIRA Server.    Where if we would like to get more information by going through the available [link|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/end-of-support-announcements-938846831.html], it will mention that:  bq. Jira 7.12 will be the version of Jira to officially deprecate PostgreSQL 9.3.  In which, it seems that JIRA 7.12 is the last to support PostgreSQL 9.3 while JIRA 8 is the start where it is no longer supported.    h3. Suggested Solution.  Change the wording here as it could confuse the reader whether is it still supported on the version or not. For example:  - In the supported platform page """"Deprecated - it is still supported on this version but will end in an upcoming release.""""  - Jira 7.12 will be the version of Jira to officially deprecate PostgreSQL 9.3 in which the last JIRA Release to support it.    Or any wording that clearly stated whether it is supported or no on the version."""
comments: "Hey, thanks for raising this suggestion.    I’ve changed the description of “Deprecated” to:    *Deprecated* - you can use Jira X.Y with this platform, but we're planning to end support in an upcoming release.    In this specific case (PostreSQL 9.3), it was an advance notice, and the official version to deprecate it was not Jira 7.12, but Jira 7.13 - we just wanted to let users know early. I’ve decided to leave the deprecation icon, so that’s still the case, but changed wording in End of support announcements to make it clear that 7.x will continue supporting this database."

summary: "Issue history displays user key instead of username for reporter or assignee changes"
description: """{panel}  When viewing a Jira issue, the History tab records changes in assignee/reporter using *app_user.user_key*. This is the only place in the UI where this identifier, normally used only internally in Jira, is used. This should be *app_user.lower_user_name* to maintain consistency with other UI elements. Raising as a bug as this has GDPR implications; even after anonymizing usernames personal information could be leaked to the UI as *user_key* is an immutable value.  h4. Steps to Replicate   # Create a user (e.g. {{jsmith}}).   # Change its username (e.g. to {{john.smith}})   # Go to any issue and select {{john.smith}} as either the assignee or the reporter.   # Click on the History tab.   # The change will display the username {{jsmith}} instead of {{john.smith}}:   !userkey.png!    *Expected Result*   Since the user key is only used internally by the application, we should not expose this information to end-users. The current username should be displayed instead ({{john.smith}}).    Additional information about the user rename feature:   [https://developer.atlassian.com/display/JIRADEV/Renamable+Users+in+JIRA+6.0]"""
comments: "Hi ,    Can we have  workaround for same?    We are facing same issue for Jira 7.13.0 version.    Regards,    Priyanka Ghongade" [SEP] "Please fix this as soon as possible or provide a safe way to change the wrong username." [SEP] "How end-user can differentiate between two people with the same name & surname now? How admin can use audit log, if they see those anonymized keys? Yes, in audit log user_key seems to be used too.   Please fix this!" [SEP] "We have this bug in enterprise version 8.5. Is there some plans/roadmaps. Please share."

summary: "Jira does not reindex issues after a link is added to them"
description: """*Problem Experienced*    When a link is added _from_ the issue (e.g. by selecting More->Link on the issue view), both the database and Lucene index are updated.    When a link is added _to_ the issue (e.g. by mentioning it on a Confluence page or creating a reciprocal link from another issue), the database entry gets updated, but the Lucene index does not.    *Expected Behavior*    The Lucene index should get updated in both scenarios.    *Notes*    As far as I can tell, this is not a problem from the end-user perspective (they are still able to see proper content in the """"Issue Links"""" panel, and there is a proper entry in the """"History"""" tab).    The issue, however, makes index consistency analyzer tools unreliable, because they contain many entries with different """"lastUpdated"""" times in the index and in the database, making them hard to use due to a lot of noise."""
comments: "The issue is a duplicate of JRASERVER-65564"

summary: "Improve FieldCache memory utilization for Jira instances with large Lucene"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Lucene uses {{org.apache.lucene.search.FieldCache}} to improve sort for text fields.    Lucene loads corresponding structures from disk into memory and stores in {{FieldCache}}.    That means the bigger the Lucene Fields (including Jira CustomFields) size the more memory will be required for them in JVM heap    * For large Jira instances (4+ mln issues) {{FieldCache}} might use up to 9GB+, which adds extra pressure for the JVM    h3. Suggested Solution  Optimize memory usage of the {{FieldCache}}.  According to Lucene docs, those structures are gone in latest versions, so upgrade of Lucene 7+ should fix the problem.   bq. Jira 8.0 no longer uses field caches to store lucene index parts in Java heap. They migrated this functionality to sth called Doc Values which does exactly the same but does not consume JVM heap space - it's all stored on disk and mmaped into process virtual memory.    h3. Note  Related bog   https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/where-is-fieldcache-in-jira-8-0/28042     h3. Workaround   Since memory usage  is proportional to the size of the Lucene Fields, there are a couple of workarounds here, in the order of preference:  # *Disable Lucene search index for unused CustomFields*  ** You can use the queries provided in [this KB|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/managing-custom-fields-in-jira-effectively-945523781.html] to identify the unused custom fields.   ** In order to disable the Lucene search index for these fields, you can 'Edit' the custom filed and change the 'Search Template' to 'None', the Lucene search index for this custom field will be disabled.  # *Reduce scope for CustomFields (limit for specific projects)*  ** You can find the custom fields with global contexts and then change them to specific projects by following the steps mentioned in [this KB|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver0713/custom-fields-with-global-contexts-964983618.html]. Try applying the changes in batches and during off-peak hours, since when one of your users opens an issue that uses a certain custom field, the cache will need to be recreated.  """
comments: "Support and support issues"

summary: "The way the Permission ""Application access - Any user logged in"" works with mentions is misleading"
description: """When a project has the project permission """"Browse Projects"""" with """"Any logged user"""" in it, this actually causes any user registered in the application to appear (not users that are able to log in) and this in turn could cause users with no application access (and therefore not being able to log in) to receive mails from mentions, that include information about issues that they cannot actually see.     This behavior is confusing, and the proposed solution is to rename the """"Any user logged in"""" application access category to just """"Any user""""."""

summary: "Perform an integrity check when creating an index backup"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Lucene index backups are created without an integrity check. This can lead to scenarios where the backup is corrupt or otherwise unusable in a scenario where the backup is needed.  h3. Suggestion    Perform an integrity check when creating an index backup  h3. Workaround    None"""
comments: "What part of the standard Integrity Check are you hoping will help index integrity?" [SEP] "Just that it has a valid index based on the results of /rest/api/2/index/summary    Index should be readable and count of issues in index is close to the number of issues in the database.    We've had several instances (in non-prod, thankfully) where a node spun up and before it got an index from another node it tried to execute a scheduled index snapshot creation, which resulted in a blank index snapshot as the node had no issues in it's cache." [SEP] "That's the index REST api, not the integrity checker. Yes, that is something that's worth checking"

summary: "Add sub-columns option to Jira Server Board"
description: """It is very common in true Kanban use to utilize """"Done"""" sub-columns under various activity columns.  The Jira Software does not allow that.    It should not be that hard to allow creation and usage of a """"Done"""" sub-column, for example, under """"In Progress"""".  Currently, this requires adding a brand new column, so if there are 3 columns between """"To Do"""" and the final """"Done"""", each of those columns requires a duplicate """"Done"""" columns, so we go from 5 to 8 columns.    A sub-column would simply divide the total column width.  Yes, it would of necessity shrink the cards, but info displayed on the cards could be adjusted accordingly.    It appears there was a decision made at some point to not do this, but I've never seen it explained or defended.    Please consider this.  Also, we are using Jira Server, and have no current plans to go to Jira Cloud.               """

Expected Result:  A message where adding an attachment is not possible will appear and the image won't be attached.    h3. Actual Result:  A message did appear and the image still rendered. Proceeding on creating the ticket will have the image broken."""

Step 2: Edit the Description, it will look like this in Text Mode   !scr2.png|thumbnail!     Step 3: Switch to Visual Mode, highlight 'content 1' and apply Heading 1   !scr3.png|thumbnail!     Step 4: By switching back to Text Mode, the anchors are gone (even those that are not edited yet)   !scr4.png|thumbnail! summary: "Login Gadget customizations for messages does not reflect until browser cache is flushed"
description: """h3. Summary    The administrator wants to customize login gadget at Dashboard.jspa (i.e. change the error message when an authentication failure occurs). This is done as usual by adding the new string to the suitable key at language jar file as below. However, the change is not visible to end-users until they flush browser caches. This is a problem for larger deployments with thousands of users, where you can't all users to flush their caches.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # install a new Jira Server   # add the following line as the last line to   *{{com/atlassian/jira/web/action/JiraWebActionSupport_en_US.properties}}* file at *{{WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins/jira-languages-7.6.7-en_US.jar}}*       # restart the server   # try to login with a wrong password    h3. Expected Results    """"This is the new customized message"""" shall be displayed as the error message  h3. Actual Results    Old message is displayed, instead of """"This is the new customized message"""". This happens until you flush the cache, and then the new message is displayed.  h3. Workaround    Changing widget color scheme causes the browser to fetch new resources without flushing the cache   # go to *Administration* > *System* > *User Interface *> *Look and feel*   # scroll down *Colors* > *Gadget Colors*. Click on the line containing *Color1*   # change the color, and click *Update*   # logout, and test to log in with a wrong password to see the customized error message    note: reverting back to old color causes also the message to revert back to the old value"""

Actual Results  The error message reports wrong number as 154/80 is not comprehensible :(    h3. Workaround  Jira administrators should focus on the  _Database Monitoring_ page within Jira Administration console as that should be more accurate than the logs."""
comments: "Affects also 7.11.2" [SEP] "Affect also 7.13.1    After pool usage touch the maximum there are tons of logging similar to     also with limited connection pool usage  !image-2019-02-22-15-12-30-018.png!" [SEP] "Affect also Jira v7.11.1 several times a day  " [SEP] "I see about 250 instances of the message in 3 months across our 4 node cluster, so several per day.  Please would someone confirm that these are false positive WARN messages" [SEP] "Here are some details for those who had not found it yet: https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/monitoring-database-connection-usage-938847726.html" [SEP] "Hello [~mdoar2],    This is a bug that just affects logging and not performance, so it does cause false positives to be printed in the logs. Notes  Both issues will show in the activity stream if they're both reported by the user who's refreshing their profile.     h3.Workaround  Update the issue creation time of one of the issues in the DB to be at a different second. """

summary: "Jira Data Center local indexes get inconsistent over time"
description: """h3. Summary  Difference in number of issues in index and the database increase over time, until a *lock & re-index* operation is done to get all indexes in sync again.    h3. Environment  Jira Data Center    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Still unknown    h3. Expected Results  Difference between number of issues in the database and in the nodes local indexes is eventually zero.    h3. Actual Results  Difference in the number of issues in the database and in the nodes local indexes start to drift after a *lock & re-index* operation, and indexes will not catch up as expected.    The charts below were generated using the results of the REST API endpoint {{/rest/api/2/index/summary}}. Note how the delta between issues in the database and issues in the local index starts to drift. Indexes will remain inconsistent in relation to the database until a *lock & re-index* operation is performed.   !image.png|thumbnail!     h3. Note on fix  * The following contributors to this issue that we've identified:  ** JRASERVER-64922   ** JRASERVER-65564  ***  have been fixed and are awaiting release (fix versions: 7.6.11, 7.13.1, 8.0.0).  ** JRASERVER-68642 - pending  * As of 2019-01-02, we were unable to identify any other contributors to this issue.    h3.Workaround  There's no workaround to prevent the issue from happening. It's necessary to perform a *lock & re-index* operation to make the indexes consistent again."""
comments: "We've identified [JRASERVER-65564|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-65564] as one of the contributors to the index inconsistency problem." [SEP] "Two contributors to this issue that we've identified, JRASERVER-64922 and JRASERVER-65564, have been fixed and are awaiting release (fix versions: 7.6.11, 7.13.1, 8.0.0).    At this time, we were unable to identify any other contributors to this issue." [SEP] "Another contributor, [JRASERVER-68642|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-68642], has been identified."

summary: "Jira doesn't warn user about expired license for other installed application during the upgrade procedure"
description: """*Scenario 1:*  Let's say that user extends Jira Service Desk license and decides to upgrade Jira Service Desk to newer version. During upgrade of Jira applications (e.g. Service Desk), Jira Core also gets upgraded.  * If this instance includes Jira Software as well, and if this application uses expired license, Jira won't warn the user that Jira Software won't be able to run due to expried license  * After the Service Desk (+Core) upgrade has been finished, when Jira Software get upgraded, user cannot activate Jira Software and gets """"License Too Old For Build: We found a problem with your JIRA license"""" error    We suggest implementing mechanism that would clearly mention to user that specific license wouldn't be usable with the upgraded application (and therefore, the application won't be usable as well).    *Scenario 2:*  This can actually lead to JIRA database and folders being upgraded regardless that the license actually is not eligible for the upgrade, which will result in a broken JIRA installation. I.e. JIRA is upgraded, but, the upgraded JIRA won't be able to start due to expired license and then reverting both the database and JIRA folders back to the pre-upgrade state will be required.   For this scenario, it would be great if a license check is done during the JIRA startup, and if the license is invalid for the upgrade, JIRA shouldn't proceed with the upgrade advising the customer accordingly (License is invalid for the upgrade, please roll back to the previous version or something like that)"""
comments: "The documentation does mention making sure that your license(s) are up to date before upgrading. But having Jira stop earlier rather failing the upgrade would be helpful"

summary: "Pasting formatted text into Visual Description removes formatting"

OR    Use the Text editor to paste formatted text will also semi-respect and maintain the format."""

summary: "Deleted issue key may be reassigned during a CSV import"
description: """h3. Summary  When accidentally importing a CSV into the wrong project – and in a specific set of circumstances – a deleted issue key can be reused. This is an edge-case bug requiring a very specific set of circumstances to trigger, but raising since it's not expected for a previously-deleted key to be reused like this.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Create two test projects (Project A, Project B) in Jira (I used Basic Software)  * Create five test issues in Project A  * Create five test issues in Project B  * In Project B, set no default issue type and move *Epic* to the first position on the list  * In Project B, delete one of the five test issues  * Export Current Fields CSV from Project A, edit and leave only the issue key and summary columns  * Add a new column - """"Relates to""""  * For each issue in the Issue Key field (e.g., PA-1) add the corresponding issue from Project B to the """"Relates To"""" column (e.g., PB-1). See attached example.  * Import the CSV file into Project B (this is incorrect procedure, but is needed to expose the bug). Import the """"Relates To"""" column as *Link """"Relates""""*  * If reproduction was a success, you should note the recreation in Project B of the previously-deleted issue key, now as an Epic issue type      h3. Expected Results  Issue created during import should have used the next key in sequence.    h3. Actual Results  Previously-deleted issue key is reused."""
comments: "Gnarly Steps to Reproduce"

summary: "Pie Chart Gadget Colors Inconsistent"
description: """The colors of the Pie Chart gadget are inconsistent in terms of matching color to a specific category of a """"Statistic Type."""" For example, using """"Status"""" as the """"Statistic Type"""" can result in different statuses being mapped to different colors across multiple pie charts. It doesn't make much sense to upper management to display a pie chart for one project that shows the percentage of """"In Progress"""" issues in red for one project then to have another pie chart show """"In Progress"""" issues in blue for another project. This can quickly get very confusing when updating upper management on project statuses. Furthermore, the color scheme does not make sense at all for the issue statuses. Associating the color green with """"To Do"""" and red with """"Done"""" makes zero sense to most people. It should be the other way around. Please fix this as it makes it very difficult to use Jira as a Program Management tool when its charts can cause miscommunication to upper management this easily. My suggested fix would be to let the user pick which color is assigned to each category of the """"Statistic Type"""" via a drop down on the Pie Chart's legend.      """

Lot of products have such functionality.         this was created 3 years ago but was closed and never fixed see JRASERVER-59010 and JRASERVER-59005    Note, this issue doesn't get addressed in JRASERVER-1369 as [~jwong@atlassian.com] suggested """

summary: "Issue is inaccessible after project move and security level change"
description: """*Steps to reproduce*:    1) Create two projects A and B with following Security Levels.    Project A Security Levels:   A1 (default)   A2   A3    Project B Security Levels:   B1 (default)   B2    B3    2) Create an issue """"A-1"""" in project A and set the value of """"Security Level"""" field to A1.   3) Open issue A-1 in two separate browsers (e.g. Chrome and Firefox).   4) In chrome, move issue A-1 from project A to project B using """"Move"""" operation and select B1 value for """"Security level"""" field during the move.   5) In Firefox, try updating issue A-1 using inline edit and change the value of """"Security Level"""" field from A1 to A3. Jira will show message """"_Saving of A-1 failed!_"""" (Expected behaviour so far)    6) Try to open issue A-1    *Observed behaviour:*   Error message:    _This issue can't be viewed_   _The issue you're trying to view can't be displayed._   _It may have been deleted or you don't have permission to view it right now._     *Expected behaviour:*   Issue A-1 is accessible    *Investigation:*   Regardless of the error message displayed in step 5, the {{changeitem}} table is updated with a new entry: {{OLDSTRING """"B1""""}} and {{NEWSTRING """"A3""""}}   This results in the issue to be inconsistent and not accessible in neither of the projects."""

summary: "Remove BIG_PIPE_KILLSWITCH feature flag "
description: """h3. Problem  A follow-up to JRASERVER-64095,  bq.  However as this technology is used outside of server Jira too it contains timeout feature. Timeout is calculated from the beginning of the request, so when the whole request is too long, the pipe will give up on rendering. *Fix removes this timeout for Jira.*    h3. Suggested option   Remove the dark feature {{com.atlassian.jira.config.BIG_PIPE_KILLSWITCH}} so BigPipe will be enabled for sidebar always. """

summary: "Can't edit username case through the UI"
description: """*Summary*    When editing a user's username and only the _case_ is changed, the user's row in {{cwd_user}} is _updated_ but the change is silently not performed.    *Steps to Reproduce*  # Go to the *User management* page (/secure/admin/user/UserBrowser.jspa)  # Click *Edit*. The *Edit user profile* pop-up appears.   # Change the case of the *Username* (i.e. change *""""peter""""* to *""""PeTeR""""*) and click *Update*.  # This shoots off an HTTP POST to {{/secure/admin/user/EditUser.jsp}} which contains the updated username _with_ the changed case.    # *cwd_user.user_name* remains unchanged. No error in the log.    *Expected Results*  *cwd_user.user_name* should be changed to reflect the change in case (i.e. changing *""""peter""""* to *""""PeTeR""""*)    *Actual Results*  *cwd_user.udpated_date* changes, but *cwd_user.username* does not change (i.e. changing *""""peter""""* to *""""PeTeR""""* in the UI results in *cwd_user.user_name='peter'* still).    *Workaround*  No known workaround"""

summary: "Certain REST API endpoints require UserKey instead of UserName"
description: """h3. Summary    In Jira's [REST API|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/latest/], there are certain endpoints which accept and require a user's *UserKey* (represented in the database as app_user.user_key) as an input parameter or argument.    However, the documentation suggests that the parameter should be the user's *UserName*, which is not necessarily the same value as the user's *UserKey*. In general, a user's *UserKey* is the first username that they were ever created with; if in the future, the user were to have their username changed for any reason, their *UserKey* will still be the original, first value.    This can confuse Admins and Users who try to use these REST API endpoints by providing a *UserName* (likely the value which they use to log in with, for example), and find that the endpoints do not function correctly.    Furthermore, the *UserKey* is not visible in Jira's UI. Admins can only find the *UserKey* by:  * Querying against the database against the *app_user* database table  * Using the *Get User* REST API endpoint, which does accept *UserName*, and returns the *UserKey* as a value (key)  ** $JIRA_URL/rest/api/2/user?username=<User_Name>    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new Jira user (i.e. UserName = test)  # Immediately change their username (i.e. UserName = newtest)  # Add them to a Project Role in any project  # Attempt to use the *+Delete Actor+* method for the api/2/project/{projectIdOrKey}/role endpoint to remove them from the Project Role    h3. Expected Results    An HTTP 204 is returned per the REST API documentation, and the user is no longer in the Project Role for the project in question.    h3. Actual Results  An HTTP 204 is returned, but the user is still there in the Project Role for the project in question. No information is provided to the admin clearly demonstrating what has gone wrong.     h3. Workaround  The behavior of the REST API endpoints which demonstrate this behavior cannot be changed. Instead, Admins and Users must first identify the *UserKey* through either of the methods described above before using these endpoints.  * Querying against the database against the *app_user* database table  * Using the *Get User* REST API endpoint, which does accept *UserName*, and returns the *User Key* as a value  ** $JIRA_URL/rest/api/2/user?username=<User_Name>"""
comments: "If the user is not found by key (e.g. because a username was provided) the return should be a 404 with a message indicating ""maybe you supplied a username when you should have provided a user_key - <link to documentation of the difference>""    Also (for anyone finding this while wondering why their REST calls don't work), note that the user_key value is always lower case, but the username value can be mixed case." [SEP] "After reviewing the REST APIs, we see two affected endpoints:    We are going to make them accept usernames, to be consistent with the rest of the APIs.  We will also update the documentation accordingly, as per JRASERVER-67263." [SEP] "FYI: There are 2 more endpoints that should have been included in this fix but weren't. They are really impacting us for new user accounts, since [JIRA started using random user keys for GDPR|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/gdpr-changes-in-jira-975041009.html#GDPRchangesinJira-userkeys].  * *Add project role actors to role*         If the username

summary: "As an administrator, I'd like to restrict the ability to copy attachments when cloning issues"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Presently, it is up to the user to decide if attachments should be copied when cloning an issue. In secure environments, administrators should have the ability to restrict the ability to copy attachments outside of the original issue.    h3. Suggested Solution  Either implement a flag to disable the copying of attachments globally, or implement it as a per-project feature.    h3. Why this is important  Cloning of attachments may lead to the leak of sensitive data (depending on who the clone is shared with).  Additionally, when using an add-on such as [Smart Attachments|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1212507/smart-attachments?hosting=server&tab=overview] to categorize attachments (and place restrictions on them), such data is lost during a clone operation.    h3. Workaround  * Use Javascript in the announcement banner to hide the *Clone attachments* checkbox in the Clone issue dialog."""

summary: "Include joda 2.10.5 in Jira"
description: """h3. Summary    Currently, Jira includes joda 2.8.x, which does not include the updates for daylight saving time on Brazil (starting Nov 4 this year).    This causes Jira to display date fields incorrectly, since the daylight saving rule is outdated.    h3. Environment  * Any Jira environment using the America/São Paulo timezone    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start Jira in any version  # Make sure to use the America/São Paulo timezone  # Create an issue    h3. Expected Results    Jira will display the created date correctly, following the rules for the America/São Paulo timezone.    h3. Actual Results      Jira will display the date with 1 hour difference, since it is applying the daylight saving incorrectly    h3.Workaround      # Replacing the [joda jar file|https://search.maven.org/remotecontent?filepath=joda-time/joda-time/2.10.1/joda-time-2.10.1.jar] at *$JIRA_INSTALL/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/lib*, which could have some problems in other places as we haven't tested it thoroughly  # Replacing the tz files as suggested in https://www.joda.org/joda-time/tz_update.html    It's also necessary to update Java's tz with tzupdater since Jira uses both Joda and Java's times:    * https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/downloads/tzupdater-download-513681.html  """
comments: "What are the affected Products? Jira, Confluence.. others?" [SEP] "Joda-Time library has been bumped in Jira Core and Jira Software to {{2.10.5}}. This version contains version {{2019c}} of timezone data. Please note that, as stated in the description, as Jira uses both Joda- and JRE-provided timezone data, one still needs to update JRE to fix the underlying problem."

summary: "Update MySQL configuration documentation"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currrently, in [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-938846854.html] documentation, we provide the MySQL permission configuration for """"For MySQL 5.5, MySQL 5.6, and MySQL 5.7.0 to MySQL 5.7.5"""" first before """"For MySQL 5.7.6 and above"""":    {quote}  For MySQL 5.5, MySQL 5.6, and MySQL 5.7.0 to MySQL 5.7.5:     GRANT SELECT,INSERT,UPDATE,DELETE,CREATE,DROP,ALTER,INDEX on <JIRADB>.* TO '<USERNAME>'@'<JIRA_SERVER_HOSTNAME>' IDENTIFIED BY '<PASSWORD>';  flush privileges;  For MySQL 5.7.6 and above, you must also include the REFERENCES permission:    GRANT SELECT,INSERT,UPDATE,DELETE,CREATE,DROP,REFERENCES,ALTER,INDEX on <JIRADB>.* TO '<USERNAME>'@'<JIRA_SERVER_HOSTNAME>' IDENTIFIED BY '<PASSWORD>';  flush privileges;  {quote}    If a user configured the permission for 5.7.5 in MySQL 5.7.6, certain feature such as project creation will silently fail.       h3. Suggested Solution    Update the [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-938846854.html] documentation so the permission configuration for """"MySQL 5.7.6 and above"""" is listed above """"MySQL 5.5, MySQL 5.6, and MySQL 5.7.0 to MySQL 5.7.5"""" permission configuration.    This way, if a customer configured the MySQL permission meant for 5.7.6 in MySQL 5.7.5 and lower, he/she will see an error from the UI and the user can apply the correct permission.      h3. Workaround     Apply the correct permission based on the version of MySQL provided in [Connecting Jira applications to MySQL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/connecting-jira-applications-to-mysql-938846854.html]"""

summary: "Improve Issue Create/Update action when WebHooks are configured"
description: """h3. Summary  Create Issue takes longer when there are configured WebHooks with Create Issue event.    The WebHooks are fired asynchronously, but the _logic that evaluates whether a WebHook should be called is *synchronous* and executes one JQL query per each configured WebHook_.    On instances that have problems with slow JQLs or with overly complicated WebHook filters configured, this can increase the time it takes to perform actions in Jira.  h3. Environment    An environment where there are WebHooks with Create Issue event and with complex JQL queries.  h3. Expected Results    When a customer creates an issue, they should swiftly see it's created on the UI. Slow JQLs configured for the WebHooks shouldn't impact the issue creation time.  h3. Actual Results    The customer clicks on Create Issue, Issue is created and the logic determining which WebHooks to call is executed. After all JQL filters are executed, user sees the new issue in the UI and the actual WebHooks are called in separate background threads.    Jira log:    h3. Workaround    No workaround if Webhooks will be used. But if the aim is to increase the performance of creating issue, then Webhooks should be disabled or JQLs should be tuned."""
comments: "Hey,    I agree with [~mwarkocz] it could be just an improvement request, unless the delay causing issue create dialog to fail.         [~ayanar], can you please elaborate more in time units on how long the action processing can be delayed because of the webhook? At which threshold we can start observing the dialog is failing? Thanks for the clarification.    Cheers,  Ignat Alexeyenko  Jira Bugmaster." [SEP] "@here,    We were affected by this issue and it tremendously affecting our delivery.    Could you please increase this priority to high and expecting some workaround to retain the application performance immediately.    Thanks,    Suresh Kumar G    Pearson" [SEP] "I thought webhooks were ran in the background--but if it's holding up the UI experience, that's not good. I imagine this extends beyond just Create and includes Edit issue actions as well...basically anything the Webhook will compare against." [SEP] "Hi, team!     We have affected also.   Our affected versions are 7.6.3, 7.6.10/     Thanks  I

summary: "Silent failure when editing an issue collector if non-anonymous custom fields have been set to required"
description: """h3. Summary  Editing an existing Issue Collector will silently fail when the selected issue type's field configuration has had a non-anonymous custom field (such as Due Date) set to required. The page will act as though the configuration is saved upon clicking the *Update* button, but if you click back into the collector you'll note that your changes were not saved.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In a project, configure a new issue collector. Specifics don't matter.  # Configure a new Field Configuration Scheme for the project, for one of the issue types set a [non-anonymous custom field type|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver076/using-the-issue-collector-945111295.html#Usingtheissuecollector-Addinganissuecollector] (such as Due Date) to be required.  # Attempt to edit the issue collector (e.g., change the reporter)    h3. Expected Results  Jira should throw an error indicating that the issue collector cannot be modified due to required fields that can't be displayed to anonymous users, similar to the behavior reported in [JRASERVER-67094|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-67094].    h3. Actual Results  Silent Failure in the UI, nor are there any errors thrown to standard logging.     h3.Workaround  Temporarily convert required fields to Optional prior to making your edits."""

summary: "Make FieldLayoutCache population more efficient in Jira"
description: """h3. Summary  JIRA inefficiently populates _fieldLayoutCache_ due to slow loading and large size of FieldLayoutItems. JIRA loads elements into _fieldLayoutCache_ by FIELDLAYOUT and at the same time load all related  rows from {{FieldLayoutItem}} table.   See related ticket for context JRASERVER-66013.     Since _fieldLayoutCache_ is used in many places, slow population will cause performance problems, see for example: JRASERVER-29310 and JRASERVER-61166.     h3. Environment  * Large number of rows in {{fieldlayoutitem}} table - 200k+:   {code:SQL|borderStyle=solid}   select count(1) from fieldlayoutitem;   1355666  {code}    h3. Expected Results  Population of _fieldLayoutCache_ is fast    h3. Actual Results  Population of _fieldLayoutCache_ is slow.     h3. Note  * SQL logs will have the following:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  SELECT ID, FIELDLAYOUT, FIELDIDENTIFIER, DESCRIPTION, VERTICALPOSITION, ISHIDDEN, ISREQUIRED, RENDERERTYPE FROM public.fieldlayoutitem WHERE FIELDLAYOUT='10100'""""  {code}  * Stack-trace of the long running thread will be similar to this:     * Other action might also flush _fieldLayoutCache_, some examples (not limited to that):  ** Add issue type - /secure/admin/AddIssueType.jspa   ** Remove issue type - /secure/admin/DeleteIssueType.jspa  ** Add issue type scheme -  /secure/admin/ConfigureOptionSchemes.jspa  ** Remove issue type scheme -  /secure/admin/DeleteOptionScheme.jspa """

In the text mode:    !Screen Shot 2018-10-25 at 2.48.22 PM.png|thumbnail!      h3. *Notes*  * This could be caused by the new line inserted causing the renderer to think that these are new rows.   * The new line in Wiki Markup renderer is indicated as follows with a blank space ("""" """") right after the two back-slash (""""\"""").     * This could be happening to all fields that have been using the Wiki Markup as well.     h3. *Workaround*  There is a workaround where you can edit the table in Text mode to insert the new line as follows:    !Screen Shot 2018-10-25 at 2.49.00 PM.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2018-10-25 at 2.49.07 PM.png|thumbnail! """
comments: "This is an unfortunate show-stopper as the table would have helped a great deal in encouraging users to provide sculpted details to assist in investigation and prompt resolution of issues. A set of our request types need to be put on hold since this is broken. Hope the issue will be resolved soonest - thanks in advance!" [SEP] "Interestingly this has impacts on the add-on side. For example, a popular Xray add-on (from Xpand) using the table for copying its test steps in order to create a defect for the failed test cases. Very annoying (sometimes impossible) to fix all cells manually in the text mode. Please fix this. "

summary: "Change Wiki Markup backslash escape behaviour"
description: """As per ticket JRASERVER-9258, Jira is not able to escape backslashes without using workarounds.    We suggest implementing functionality (changing how escaping works), so that backslash could be escaped without using mentioned workarounds, that is, by adding additional backslash."""

summary: "As an admin I would like to be able to track JIRA webhooks delivery success or failure from the UI"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JIRA webhooks currently have no way of guaranteeing delivery, thus, integrity between changes made in JIRA and updates to a remote system are very hard to reconcile in the case of momentary failure or outage at the remote endpoint. See https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-29856    h3. Suggested Solution  * Provide a convenient log in JIRA webhook administration that records basic information about webhook POSTs and their status (failure/success), not necessarily detailed contents. So that admins can filter out failed messages easily.   * Ensure there is a job that automatically cleans or trims this record - say every 30 days or so to avoid blowing up the database from it.  * It may also be a neat feature to allow this webhook logs to be queried by an API, so that users are able to automate fetching of missed issue updates for changes whose webhooks POSTs had failed to deliver, after the remote system has recovered"""
comments: "Some more context from our customer, with some additional desirable requirements:  {quote}  a UI may help the administration to verify integration errors and I agree with You that it make sense to clean its content "" every 30 days or so"". However I think that a notification mechanism is required to activate as soon as possibile this investigation activity of the administrator. For example could a daily notification (e.g by mail) that there are some integration failures be one viable solution? An alternative may be a custom application notification mechanism that we have to implement using those api that you have proposed (those api to automate fetching of missed webhook posts).    This previous paragraph is about identifying and investigating integration error but now I see another element: the resend activity. There are two types of errors: messages not delivered for server temporarily not available / overloaded or messages not delivered due to an ""application"" error in message delivery (e.g unrecognize

summary: "As a user who is assigned an editor role for a filter, I'd like more contextual error messages when I can't save edits to the filter"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In Jira 7.12 new functionality was added to assign filter viewers and editors. For an editor to be able to save changes to a filter, however, they *must* be a member of all groups that the filter is shared with. If this condition isn't met, when attempting to save the filter the user will be presented with the following error:   !filter_error.png|thumbnail!     The error isn't very helpful and does not provide the user with an understanding of why they're unable to save their filter changes.    h3. Suggested Solution  Capture this scenario and provide a more contextual error message describing why the filter can't be saved.    h3. Why this is important  The current error is very ambiguous and is therefore a call-driver to support. Providing a more contextual error will allow users and their administrators to understand the failure case."""
