summary: "Background reindex can be triggered with broken Jira index"
description: """h3. Summary    Background reindex is not supposed to fix the broken index. It shouldn't be possible to trigger it when the index is broken.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Run Jira 7.12.3 with existing issues  # Stop Jira  # remove index files    # Start Jira  # Go to """"Jira indexing"""" administration page ({{<Jira_URL>/secure/admin/jira/IndexAdmin.jspa}})    h4. Expected result  Jira won't allow you to perform background reindex  In Jira 7.6.9 the screen looked like this:   !indexing-jira-7.6.9.png|thumbnail!     h4. Actual result  Jira will allow you to perform background reindex   !indexing-jira-7.12.3.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  No workaround known  """
comments: "There should be some kind of message to explain what has happened    ""Index files are corrupt or missing""    I suppose some admins of small instances might want to be able to run a background index?" [SEP] "Is this bug affects all Jira 7.12.x versions?" [SEP] "is this happens in 7.12.1??" [SEP] "[~heshanm], [~ashish.kanaparthi], yes, all 7.12.x versions are affected." [SEP] "@[~pczuj] is there any workaround for this one. We are planning for the upgrade." [SEP] "[~ashish.kanaparthi] I'm not aware of any workaround to not allow the background reindex to be triggered in case of broken index.     Fix for this bug should be available in upcomming 7.13.0 release. The date of this release is not yet decided, but it should be matter of upcomming weeks."

summary: "Loading of batch.js and batch.css is slow making page load time longer in JIRA"
description: """h3. Summary    Jira page-load-times are taking more time than usual.  Specifically, the files batch.js and batch.css take much longer time to load, sometimes in the range of 10s of seconds.  The issue is reproducible on all browsers.  h3. Environment   * Jira 7.10.x, 7.11.x, or 7.12.x   * Jira runs under load, many concurrent users 100+ concurrent users.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a loaded Jira instance, try to load any page, eg: Issue navigator.    h3. Expected Results,    The page should load in a reasonable time.   * _Issue navigator on jira.atlassian.com load in around 3 seconds._   * Resources are cached at the Jira side and not recompiled    h3. Actual Results   * The page takes much longer time to load.   !Screenshot_20181005_153609.png|thumbnail!   * Some resources are *not* cached at the Jira side and recompiled on every page view   * Taking thread dumps we see multiple user threads running the below Mozilla Rhino compiler code for Javascript and CSS resources:      No errors are noticed in the logs.  h3. Notes   * In Jira version 7.10.0, a piece of logic was introduced to a file that lists resources to import on some pages, e.g. on the backlog view.   * Due to the added logic, the file became dynamic, and thus Jira is unable to cache this file or any of the imported resources.   * As a result, Jira needs to recompile some LESS files on every page view. This operation is CPU intensive and can create a performance bottleneck.   * The piece of logic was added in Jira 7.10.0 and was removed in Jira 7.13.0.    h3. Workaround    Please upgrade Jira to version 7.13.0 or newer to receive the fix. Unfortunately, there are no known workarounds for the problem."""
comments: "We are actively working on this issue. -We made changes in Jira 7.13.0 that we thought would alleviate this issue, but it turns out the problem is still present.-         After an extensive investigation, we are confident to say that Jira 7.13.0 does, in fact, alleviate the problem. For more details, please look at the comment below." [SEP] "We fixed the performance problem in Jira 7.13.0.  Please upgrade to this version to receive the fix. Unfortunately, there are no known workarounds for the problem.  h3. Root cause analysis of the problem    To provide a better development experience, Jira product and plugin developers can use an intermediate language (LESS) for creating the style resources.  That intermediate language needs to be compiled to a language understandable by the browsers.    Normally, a resource that was compiled one time would get cached in Jira/Tomcat. This allows Jira to serve that already compiled resource on subsequent page views, to different users, without the need to compile it aga

summary: "Search via REST API might fail due to ClassCastException"
description: """h3. Problem experienced    Search requests via API (_/rest/api/2/search_) can consistently fail on a DC node with a ClassCastException, under a set of very particular circumstances:   - Jira DC,   - full cache flush,   - some unidentified (but very rare) condition that makes a node load wrong Spring library via OSGI.    The error stops some caches from working and will result in an intensified number of database queries executed, possibly making the performance of an entire DC cluster _a lot_ worse, and even making it unresponsive.    Sample stack trace of the related error:    h3. """"Workaround""""    Restarting the problematic note should resolve the issue. In one known case, the problem fixed on its own after a few hours.  h3. Notes    The full cache flush is not done by the Jira itself, but by plugins"""
comments: "I set the severity as Major according to our [Bug Fixing Policy|https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-bug-fixing-policy-201294573.html], as this is a critical issue where there is a workaround." [SEP] "The root cause seems to be related to JRASERVER-62252, so I'm adding a link between the issues." [SEP] "To prevent this situation from happening in the future, we've removed the following dynamic import from the jira-rest-plugin:    We've replaced the AOP dependency with mandatory OSGi dependencies:    The remaining Spring Framework dependencies are loaded as provided Maven dependencies from Jira Core."

summary: "Custom field search field refresh rate prevents Chinese character input while using IME "
description: """h3. Summary    Custom field search field refresh rate prevents Chinese character input while using IME   h3. Environment   * Using any Input Method Editor (IME) to input Chinese, Japanese character.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a new custom field with Chinese character.   !Custom fields - Jira 7.12.1 - Google Chrome 2018-10-23 17.43.46.png|thumbnail!   # Search the custom field using custom field search field.    h3. Expected Results  JIRA able to search for the custom field and IME did not get cut.    h3. Actual Results  IME get cut off halfway before it can convert the character to Chinese.   !CustomField.gif!   [^Custom Field.mp4]    h3. Workaround  Convert the Chinese character in a notepad and paste the converted Chinese custom field to the search field."""

summary: "Description of the status 'To Do' will not appear when personal language is changed"
description: """h3. Summary    When you add a description to the status 'To Do' to the default language (US - English), it will appear in the Status page. However, when you change your personal language to another language, the description will not appear. This is only observed with the status 'To Do' which is bundled with Jira.   h3. Steps to replicate   # Go to *Issues* > *Status*   # Head to the status *To Do* and click on *Edit*. Add a description to the Status, then click *Update*.   # Click on *Add Status* and add a Status with the name, *Investigating* and description, *Investigating this issue*.   # Head to your profile and change the language to French.   # Go to *Status* and observe that the description of *To Do* does not appear. However, the description of *Investigating* appears.    h3. Expected Results    The status *To Do* will appear with the default description edited when it is not translated in another language.  h3. Actual Results    The status *To Do* description will not appear.  h3. Workaround    Add a translation for *To Do* in every language.  h3. Note    The description will appear when you are in the *Status Translations* page."""

summary: "JIRA Anonymous User Able To Search Creator Name In JQL Search When Key In Full User Name Even When Browse User Permission Doesn't Allow Anyone"
description: """h3. *Summary*  JIRA Anonymous User Is Able To Search For Creator Name Via [*JQL Search Screen*|http://localhost:8080/issues/?jql=] By Insert Full User Name Even When *Browse User* Global Permission Doesn't Allow """"*Anyone*"""". This is definitely not an expected behavior if """"Browse User"""" wasn't set to anyone.    Although it will only show when full name entered matched, but this is still considered as a security issue. An attacker can use this bug to identify which user ID available in JIRA.    h3. *Environment*  JIRA Version: 7.12.3    h3. *Steps to Reproduce*  # Open up *JQL Search URL* http://localhost:8080/issues/?jql= (This link can access directly even without login.  # Select *More* > *Creator* > Key in a username in full for example *admin.jira*  # It will show the user when username matched JIRA available user as below:-   !image-2018-10-23-16-17-55-782.png|thumbnail!     h3. *Expected Results*  # It shouldn't show any username.  # Besides, we shouldn't even able to access the JQL page if anonymous user access is not allowed.    h3. *Actual Results*  # JQL Page Opened and we are able to key in full username, JIRA will show it out when it is available in JIRA user directory.    h3. *Workaround*  Installing this plugin will force the user to login before they able to access to JQL Search page  https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1213129/prevent-anonymous-access?hosting=server&tab=support"""
comments: "*{color:#FF0000}Major security issue in Jira Service Desk.{color}*      *This allows Service Desk customers to search for other customers names and groups.*  This should not be possible for Anonymous users as it can reveal information about other customers.  Creator search function searches for users and groups.  Bug is present on v7.13.1 also."

summary: "Basic search Development field is not working when install Jira with Japanese Default language"
description: """h3. Summary    When install Jira with Japanese Default language, Basic search development field is not working. It will display error """"フィールド 'development' が存在しないか、そのフィールドを閲覧する権限を持っていません。(Field 'development' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it.)"""" .    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira with Japanese Default language   # Connect with Bitbucket Server or Bitbucket Cloud or Github or Bamboo   # Search issue by Basic and select """"Development(開発)"""" field   # Error message """"フィールド 'development' が存在しないか、そのフィールドを閲覧する権限を持っていません。(Field 'development' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it.)"""" will be displayed and it doesn't return any issue    h3. Expected Results  - When user search issue with """"Development(開発)"""" field by Basic search, return correct result without error message  - JQL should be """"{{開発[commits].all > 0}}"""" not """"{{development[commits].all > 0}}""""    h3. Actual Results  - When user search issue with """"Development(開発)"""" field by Basic, JQL will be """"{{development[commits].all > 0}}""""  - Error message """"フィールド 'development' が存在しないか、そのフィールドを閲覧する権限を持っていません。(Field 'development' does not exist or you do not have permission to view it.)"""" will be displayed    h3. Workaround  - Change search type Basic to Advance  - Change JQL """"{{development[commits].all > 0}}"""" to """"{{開発[commits].all > 0}}"""""""

summary: "Custom field of type Date Picker store year as 0018 instead of 2018 in the database"
description: """Custom field of type Date Picker store year as 0018 instead of 2018 in the database     *Environment:*   - Jira 7.6.7   - PostgreSQL 9.3    *Steps to reproduce:*   - Create a custom field of type """"Date Picker"""" and add to screens   - Set the {{jira.date.picker.java.format}} format to: {{MM/d/yyyy}}   - Create a new issue. Manually type the date in the date picker field as: 11/30/18    Run the following query in the Jira database, where 'Date Picker Field' is the field created in step 1    *Observed Behaviour*   datevalue in the database is stored as """"{color:#FF0000}0018{color}-11-30 21:00:00""""    *Workaround 1:*   Change {{jira.date.picker.java.format}} format to {{MM/d/yy}}    *Workaround 2:*   When creating the issue, use the date picker instead of manually typing the date"""
comments: "I also noticed this issue.    And it´s not only the wrong year (0018 instead of 2018), the date also moved 3 days back in my case.    For example the user types 01.11.18 the date is saved as: 29.10.0018    If the user types 01.11.2018 everything is fine."

summary: "Reduce Create Issue Request Size"
description: """As Jira instances grow in size and complexity, there are increasing numbers of projects, issue types, schemes, etc.    See:  !quickcreate-size.png|thumbnail!     This request is also performed every single time an user clicks on the """"Create"""" button.  If there are no changes to the list of parameters, browser should not need to request the entire list again.    h5. Workaround  A workaround is to ensure that gzip compression is enabled either in Jira or on the network layer, but *not both*.  Compression in multiple layers will result in content parsing problems."""

summary: "Provide Connector for Eclipse"
description: """h2. Description  Customer has a requirement to integrate JIRA with their IDE tools (Eclipse, Visual Studio), where their developers will edit and update the JIRa issue inside IDE tools without jumping between environments.    h2. Workaround  N/A    There used to be a connector developed by Atlassian Labs, but it has been de-commissioned. More info here: https://developer.atlassian.com/blog/2015/06/discontinuing-ide-connectors-support/  """
comments: "A small example of why this would be useful:    I want to ensure that stories in Jira have a data link to the Bitbucket pull request that implemented the story.  This is done when the developer puts the entire issue id into the commit comment. In our system, that is within square brackets at the start of the commit comment. There is presently no automation that ensures that the developer puts in that string, and there is no automation to ensure that they spelled the id correctly.    If developers were able to create mylyn tasks associated with the Jira issue, we should be able to define a commit template that has the task id within square brackets at the start of the commit comment.  We can't do this without a Jira connector.    In general, it gives us more options for automating parts of the process and avoiding manual error-prone steps."

summary: "Internal directory user's password needs to match the one in Crowd if SSO is enabled"
description: """h3. Summary    Internal directory user's password needs to match the one in Crowd in the following conditions.    * SSO is enabled  * Jira Internal Directory is in a higher position than Crowd Directory in [User Directories|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-user-directories-938847049.html] setting    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # [Integrating Crowd with Atlassian Jira|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/integrating-crowd-with-atlassian-jira-192625.html]  # Create a user who has same username in in each directory as follows  #* Jira Internal Directory:  #** username: testuser  #** password: *password1*  #* Crowd Directory:  #** username: testuser  #** password: *password2*  # Move Jira Internal Directory in a higher position than Crowd Directory in _Administration > User management > User Directories_  # Enable SSO with following instruction in the above page  # Try to login Jira with the latter one (in Crowd Directory) credential  #* Please login Jira from Jira's login page and do not login from Crowd or the other SSO enabled applications    h3. Expected Results    As [the document|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/integrating-crowd-with-atlassian-jira-192625.html] said,    bq. It is possible to define multiple user directories in Jira. However, if you enable SSO integration, you will only be able to authenticate as users from the Crowd server defined in the crowd.properties file.    the credential in Crowd Directory should be used for the authentication and the login should succeed.    h3. Actual Results    The login failed. Even if we try to login with the credential in Jira Internal Directory, it failed as well. It means both of the passwords need to match each other.     h3. Notes    The user seemed to be authenticated twice by both Jira Internal Directory and Crowd Directory in this situation.    There was also [a suggestion|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-28994] which asked for disabling other directories than Crowd if SSO was enabled but it was already closed as """"Won't Fix"""".    h3.Workaround    We can still login Jira from other SSO enabled applications' login page."""

summary: "Tab focus on issue search from projects is invisible."
description: """h3. Summary    Tab focus in Jira 7.10 and 7.11 when searching for issues in a project is invisible.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a few issues in a project and search for the issues under the *Issues* tab.  # Tab through the different buttons on the screen until you get to the issues in the sidebar to tab through.      h3. Expected Results    You can visually see the issues you are tabbing through in the issue panel.      h3. Actual Results    The tabbing goes out of focus and is invisible and the focus remains on the issue at the top of the filter although it's clear you are tabbing through issues. On the bottom of the screen on Chrome, you can clearly see that you have tabbed to a different issue when hovering over and the focus on that issue will come back when you click Enter.      h3. Notes    I could not reproduce this on Jira 7.6.8 or 7.7.2. This can be easily reproduced on JAC and versions starting at 7.10.      h3.Workaround    Click on the issues directly without tabbing through them so focus remains on the issue or use the J and K hotkeys to tab through the issue."""

summary: "Duplicated groups in AD are removed from JIRA after synchronization"
description: """JIRA synchronization with the AD will remove any occurrence of duplicated groups in the AD tree with the following example message, even if the group is on different locations of the AD tree.    This message occurs for each duplicated group found and the application will remove all occurrences of the group during the sync.    If you are using the """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" for """"every time the user login in"""", the application will add the group into JIRA groups during the user login action, removing the group again on the subsequent synchronization causing the strange symptom of losing and gaining permissions regularly.    h4. Expected results    JIRA should merge all occurrences of duplicated groups, aggregating each group members into a singular group on JIRA UI.    h4. Workaround    The only known workaround is to write up a [group filter|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-write-ldap-search-filters-792496933.html] and filter the duplicated groups out of the sync. Since the groups have the same name, you must filter using an attribute that differs them to take effect."""

summary: "Update on ""Increasing JIRA application"" documentation"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:  At the moment, the documentation of [""""Increasing JIRA application memory""""|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/increasing-jira-application-memory-938847654.html] it mentions that:  {quote}  h4. Guidance    As a rule of thumb, if you have fewer than 5000 issues, Jira applications should run well with the default 768 MB. Granting Jira applications too much memory can impact performance negatively, so it is best to start with 768 MB, and make modest increases as necessary. As another data point, 40,000 works well with 768 MB to 1 GB.  {quote}  This seems to be a really rare case where the Default Memory could cater to 5000 issues including 1GB for 40000 issues. Except that the instance does not have any plugin installed and the concurrent users are low.    This section seems to not update since JIRA 4 and even in JIRA Sizing Guide below for JIRA 6.4, for low scale instance, the recommended Memory for JIRA is more than 1GB.  - [JIRA Sizing Guide|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/jira-sizing-guide-461504623.html]    h3. Suggested Solution:  To remove the section completely or replace it with the current pages that we have such as:  - [JIRA Sizing Guide|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/jira-sizing-guide-461504623.html]  - [JIRA Data Center size profiles|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/jira-data-center-size-profiles-955171062.html]"""

summary: "Improve Kotlin interoparability"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When using Kotlin to create a Jira plugin,  one runs into this problem very quickly:   [https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-19515]    The problem is, that Kotlin inheritance respects generics. There are many classes in the Jira source code and/or it's libraries that do not use generics and instead use the raw type.    For example:   * com.atlassian.jira.util.ErrorColleciton - uses Generics   * webwork.action.ActionSupport - does not use Generics   * This leads to a clash of signatures in com.atlassian.jira.web.action.JiraWebActionSupport:   ** setErrorMessages(Collection)   ** setErrorMessages(Collection<String>)   * This is fine for the Java compiler. However, the Kotlin compiler is more strict here.   * Thus, in Kotlin, just subclassing JiraWebActionSupport will lead to a compile error    h3. Suggested Solution   * The problem cannot be fixed at client/call side (the plugin).   * Adaption of the Jira source code and it's dependencies is necessary.   * Specifically, scan all Java files and add missing generics.   * Specifically, webwork-1.4-atlassian-30.jar should have generics added (or rather, it should be replaced with a 1.5 version of the library), as this contains the often needed Action base classes.   * There should not be anything blocking adding generics going forward, as Jira already requires Java 1.8 as stated on """"Supported platforms"""" page    h3. Why this is important    Kotlin is picking up momentum and it can be expected that more and more plugins will be created with Kotlin    Kotlin was on the """"Adopt"""" stage of the Atlassian Tech Radar this year. So if we shall adopt, let's make it as smooth as possible  h3. Workaround    Whenever you need to extend a Jira base class that will lead to such a compile error in Kotlin, create the subclass as a Java class instead."""
comments: "+1" [SEP] "Our company with 7 years of history in writing in Java has completely switched to Kotlin recently. We rewrote our whole product in Kotlin so our next release will be in Kotlin. See how much I used Kotlin? It is definitely needed."

summary: "Jira should remove the unused custom field values from the database after a context change "
description: """h3. Problem Definition  After a custom field context change from global to per project, the custom field will disappear from screens in projects where it is not applicable.   However, The values of the custom field on issues from projects where it's not applicable are *not* removed from the database table *customfieldvalue*.    h3. Suggested Solution  After a custom field context change to only apply to certain projects, all values of the custom field in issues that belong to the excluded projects should be deleted.     h3. Workaround  * While a *SQL DELETE* statement is feasible, this option is to be avoided.   * A re-index will remove issues from searches in projects where it's not applicable.   * The UI will not display any values for this custom field in the excluded projects.     """

summary: "As an Admin, I'd like for webhook events to more consistently provide variables in the URL so I can ensure my webhooks work consistently"
description: """h3.Summary  Not all webhook events provide the same URL variables. This can lead to scenarios where a single webhook has multiple events - but the webhook will not always execute consistently, because some variables will not be present.    Currently, the list of variables is:     * *board* related webhooks: *board.id*   * *sprint* related webhooks: *sprint.id*   * *attachment* related webhooks: *attachment**.id*   * *comment* related webhooks: *comment.id*   * *issue* related webhooks: *issue.id, issue.key, project.id, project.key*   * *project* related webhooks: *project.id, project.key*   * *version* related webhooks: *project.id, project.key, version.id*   * *version merge* webhook: *mergedVersion.id*   * *worklog* related webhooks: *worklog.id*    The following event types *do not provide any URL variables*:  * Issue Link related webhooks:  * Features status change related webhooks (voting, watching, etc)    (Please note that this list may change in the future)    h3.Suggested Solution  All webhook events should provide a bare minimum of variables, where it makes sense to do so.    h3. Why this is important  This is important so webhooks operate consistently when multiple events have been selected."""
comments: "Issue Links not passing variables cost me like an hour trying to figure out why my service wasn't receiving anything. I set it to post to https://my.internal.service/api/jira/$\{issue.key\} and my handlers wouldn't match, since the issue key was not being populated at all.  I can work around this by grabbing the issue key from the JSON body, but even then the {{issuelink_created}} event does not include an issue key (only an issue id) which makes handling of those slightly more involved than it probably should be."

summary: "As an Admin, the webhooks UI does not clearly show which variables I can use within a URL"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When using the webhooks UI to create or edit webhooks, some variables will be allowed to be used in the URL.    However, not all event types provide the same URL variables; and it's not entirely clear which event types provide certain URL variables.  h3. Suggested Solution    Provide a clearer way in the UI for an administrator to see which variables are provided by which event type.  h3. Why this is important    Administrators may create webhooks with multiple events to make management easier - but if an event fires (that does not provide the variable) then the webhook will not behave as expected.  h3. Workaround    You can insert the following CSS into Jira's Announcement Banner - it will make the """"All variables are available"""" and """"Some variables are available"""" states clearer so Administrators can at least see the difference.    Please ensure you test this change in a test environment first, before applying to production. If you see [unusual behaviour in pages after applying this fix|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-pages-go-hidden-or-blank-after-adding-an-announcement-banner-396298891.html], you may need to remove the adjustments - we also have steps to [remove the announcement banner through the database|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-remove-jira-announcement-banner-through-the-database-429916703.html].    *Without the workaround*   No workaround, all variables available:  !no-workaround-all-available.png|thumbnail!    No workaround, some variables available:  !no-workaround-some-available.png|thumbnail!    *With the workaround*:   Workaround, all variables available:  !workaround-all-available.png|thumbnail!    Workaround, some variables available:  !workaround-some-available.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Outgoing email to group should be sent to the associated distribution list not to all the individual users"
description: """In a JIRA setup where a User Directory is configured to sync with a corporate LDAP or Active Directory database, distribution lists are pulled into JIRA as groups, and the members of the group are the users associated with the emails in the DL. However, when a notification is sent to one of those groups, it turns into *many* emails to all the individual members of the group instead of one email to the DL associated with the group. This is very undesirable.    It is problematic in situations where the mail servers are configured with a limit on the number of emails that can be generated from a particular IP address. In our case, our servers are set to only allow 200 emails/minute and we are now hitting this limit frequently. Our mail admins will not increase this limit, saying:  {quote}This limit helps prevent internal applications from inadvertently creating an internal DoS attack on our messaging servers. So the raising of the submission rate is not an option as this will affect corporate wide email services.    The way JIRA handles emails is not correct. They should send to the distribution list’s SMTP address so exchange can expand the list and send the messages as needed. This also bypasses all our security controls that are in place to prevent users from sending to DLs with a high number of members that are restricted.  {quote}  We would very much like to see this behavior changed so that notifications to a group (we have several instances where this happens) send only one email to the distribution list associated with the group instead of many emails to the individual members of the group."""
comments: "Define an internal Jira user with the group address. Costs you one Jira user license.    But be aware that this will break any Jira security around the issues. Jira checks whether each member of a group is permitted to see the issue before sending email to that person" [SEP] "Defining an internal Jira user with the group address is not an option.  That would require manual maintenance of thousands of distribution lists that are already being maintained in Active Directory and automatically imported to Jira."

summary: "Make JIRA mentions compatible with Office365/Outlook's @Mention feature"
description: """As of 2016, Microsoft Office365/Outlook supports a @Mention feature, which highlights/filters emails which explicitly call out the recipient. This feature is similar in purpose and intent to JIRA's Mention feature (available since 2013). Thus, the features are equivalent, however, the syntax of Mentions in JIRA email notifications is different and incompatible: * JIRA uses {{\[~username]}} syntax in text entry, and {{\@username}} in the user interface * Office365/Outlook expects e.g. {{\@lastname}} (plain text) and {{lastname}} (HTML) This email combination is likely to be common among enterprise customers, so interoperability should be valuable. I am aware of other requests for improving JIRA Mentions, such as JRASERVER-37777, JRASERVER-41364, but this specific request should integrate better and have more immediate benefits to a large user base. Since non-Office365/Outlook email won't necessarily honor the same @Mention syntax, this proposal should be a configurable option."""

summary: "Update Tomcat to 8.5.34 to avoid CVE-2018-11784"
description: """h4. Open redirect in default servlet [CVE-2018-11784|https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/cve-2018-11784]    When the default servlet in Apache Tomcat versions 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.11, 8.5.0 to 8.5.33 and 7.0.23 to 7.0.90 returned a redirect to a directory (e.g. redirecting to '/foo/' when the user requested '/foo') a specially crafted URL could be used to cause the redirect to be generated to any URI of the attackers choice.    Fixed versions:   * >=9.0.12     * >=8.5.34     * >=7.0.91    Workaround:  {quote}Use mapperDirectoryRedirectEnabled=""""true"""" and mapperContextRootRedirectEnabled=""""true"""" on the Context to ensure that redirects are issued by the Mapper rather than the default Servlet. See the Context configuration documentation for further important details.  {quote}"""
comments: "Hi Team,    There are security vulnerabilities in Apache 8.5.34 as well.    [https://www.tenable.com/plugins/nessus/133845]"

summary: "Jira pages containing Agile reports do not load in Confluence's HTML macro"
description: """h3. Summary    Jira pages that contain Agile reports (Velocity charts, Burn-down chart, ...) and are loaded via a iFrame in the Confluence's HTML macro do not load.  h3. Environment   * Confluence Server 6.6.8   * Jira Server 7.6.8    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # [Disable clickjacking in Jira|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/security-headers-in-jira-939919914.html] via:       # In a Confluence page, put an [iFrame in a HTML macro|https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/how-to-put-an-iframe-into-confluence-598837531.html] that points to a Jira Agile chart URL (Velocity charts, Burn-down chart, ...)    h3. Expected Results    The page loads in the iFrame and show the report/graph.  h3. Actual Results    The page does not load, instead it show the error:  {quote}Exception: Uncaught SecurityError: Blocked a frame with origin """"<Jira base URL>"""" from accessing a cross-origin frame.  {quote}  h3. Notes   * The issue does not occur when loading a dashboard or an issue page.   * This is a Jira problem since a part of the page does load. The error in the UI is show by Jira and not Confluence.   * Adding the JVM arg {{-Dcom.atlassian.jira.clickjacking.protection.disabled=true}} should allow all pages to load in an external iFrame.    h3. Workaround    For the Agile Velocity chart, use the [Agile Velocity Chart Gadget|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1215768/agile-velocity-chart-gadget?hosting=server&tab=overview] add-on."""

description: """If I create a ticket by hand, I am the """"created by"""" and """"reported by"""" user.    When an issue is created by processing an email that was sent to Jira where I'm the default assignee, I become the """"created by"""" and """"reported by"""" user no matter who sent the original request in.    My email rules and workflow cannot differentiate between these events since """"created by"""" and """"reported by"""" are both me (let's ignore the """"Assignee"""" because sometimes I *do* manually create tickets assigned to myself - in that instance, I'm all three: created, reported, and assignee).    Basically, if the ticket is created via email, either make the """"Created by"""" value be the """"from address"""" of the email sender OR set it to a dummy Jira service account so I can tell which ones were really created by me OR created via email."""

summary: "Frontend freaks out after enabling/disabling RTE with no temporary access to administrative functions "
description: """h3. Summary    When losing """"temporary access to administrative functions"""" in Jira administrator panel you still can be viewing already loaded configuration page of RTE (Rich Text Editor).    Clicking RTE switch in {{<Jira_URL>/secure/admin/ConfigureRTE!default.jspa}} results in frequently repeating error displayed as flag (right side message). The switch will rapidly switch between enabled and disabled.    h3. Steps to reproduce    # Go to {{<Jira_URL>/secure/admin/ConfigureRTE!default.jspa}}  # Open the same page in 2nd browser window/tab  # In 1st tab click """"Drop access"""" link button on top of the page.  # In 2nd tab click on RTE switch """"Enable rich text editor for users""""    h4. Expected result    Single error is displayed informing about lost access to administrative functions.  RTE switch is moved back to previous position.    h4. Actual result    Frequently repeating error message.  RTE switch rapidly switching between enabled and disabled.  """

summary: "Improve developer docs covering ComponentAccessor"
description: """Currently, there is not enough documentation from Atlassian covering usage of _ComponentAccessor_ API for accessing Jira components.   JavaDoc: [ComponentAccessor.html|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/7.1.1/com/atlassian/jira/component/ComponentAccessor.html]  * Instead of using _ComponentManager_, plugins should actually be using _ComponentAccessor_ from the jira-api module instead.    Example of docs from Adaptavist:   [UpgradingToJira7.html|https://scriptrunner.adaptavist.com/latest/jira/releases/UpgradingToJira7.html]  bq. now you should use:  def commentManager = ComponentAccessor.commentManager"""

summary: "Broken XML causes workflow admin page to crash"
description: """h3. Summary    When navigating to Administartion >> Issues >> Workflow, an error (The JIRA server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation) is displayed. This is due to a special character injected on the Workflow XML that made the XML of that Workflow broken. When Jira tries to parse that, it fails resulting in the error previously mentioned.  h3. Environment   * Jira 7.8.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Inject special character on Workflow XML file (this could be accidental or malformed). This will cause the Workflow XML to be broken on the database.   # When trying to access to the Workflow administration page, this will result into an error (The JIRA server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation).    h3. Expected Results    Jira will mention that the Workflow XML is broken due to special characters, or even if one XML is affected, this won't affect to the whole experience and page will still be displayed.  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian_jira.log file:    Note: The error does not mention the id of the workflow that is broken. Logging the id would also be helpful in determining the broken workflow.    And this error will be displayed on the UI:    !JIRAError.JPG|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  Manually check the workflow xml for all workflows in the database:      Looking up the Workflows on the database, it will show that the XML is malformed. Here's a comparison with a different and well formed Workflow XML displayed in DBVisualizer:    !Screen Shot 2018-10-04 at 15.50.14.png|thumbnail! !Screen Shot 2018-10-04 at 15.50.05.png|thumbnail!    Currently, the XML can be either analysed and fix by finding the special character, or to just delete the workflow after identifying which Workflow XML has been broken.    This way, the broken XML won't be there anymore and Jira will only see well formed XML and will be able to display the Workflow Administration page."""

summary: "JIRA displays NullPointerException consumer when trying to get user OAuth tokens"
description: """h3. Summary  When trying to extract user OAuth token via REST API, they get a NullPointerException (see stack in attachment).     h3. Environment   * Customer has upgraded Jira from 7.2.x to 7.6.7.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Fire up Jira and Confluence instances  * Link them  * Create page in Confluence and put some Jira Macros  * Open page and approve access to Jira so it will create Auth tokens  * Have auth tokens like in attachment  * Make a DB backup of Jira  * Spin up another Jira instance  * Restore database  * Check same user auth tokens    h3. Expected Results  User auth token is retrieved    h3. Actual Results  The NPE exception is thrown as seen in attachment file.     h3. Notes  The workaround from https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-displays-nullpointerexception-consumer-when-deleting-application-link-776801530.html was not sufficient.    h3.Workaround  Problem has been worked around by cleaning out the existing entries from 'oauthsptoken' table and re-creating them. """

summary: "Give users an authentication challenge that allows them to save their pending changes immediately after successfully establishing a new session."
description: """h3. Description    When users go to save updates or a new filing, they lose all their edits if the session has expired. We'd prefer not to set Tomcat session inactive timers to zero and eliminate timeouts completely, as we really don't want infinite sessions and all the risk that entails, what we'd prefer is that users are given an authentication challenge that allows them to save their pending changes immediately after successfully establishing a new session.    This happens to developers on a daily basis, the recurring loss of work is a productivity issue    h3. Workaround    If users are using Chrome,  it could be installed the Lazarus Chrome extension, or Typio Form Recovery. These extensions saves anything you type in forms even if the page errors out."""

summary: "Kanban - would like to have issues in columns based on where I put them"
description: """I would like to be able to put an issue into whatever Kanban column I want regardless of status.  Think of it as similar to putting issues on a scrum board into a sprint.    The reason is that my team works on projects from many different teams and not all of them have the same status.  since a status can only belong in one column this makes it impossible for me to get some of the tasks into the priority work column.  This would be a great feature for me."""

description: """h3. Workaround    Edit the Workflow associated with the project and in the Statuses that you want to hide the *Assign* option, click on _View Properties_ and then add the following :     You will still see the assignee field when you are viewing the issue in that status."""

summary: "Possibility to just highlight stories in the backlog through a predefined filter"
description: """Hi everyone,    it would be great to have a possibility to highlight stories in the backlog, which have a label or a specific attribute.    *Current situation:*    Currently, I just have the quickfilters to search directly in the backlog. When I filter the tickets, I just see e.g. the tickets labeled with """"Design needed"""". Because I won't see all the other tickets, I can't rank them in the backlog in a proper way.    *New feature:*    With the possibility to just highlight stories in the backlog through a predefined filter (e.g. labels=design needed), I have a proper possibility to find the stories and I can directly rank them in the backlog."""

summary: "Allow some flexibility to the jira.issue.editable flag"
description: """Currently, configuring the {{jira.issue.editable}} flag on the property of a Workflow status makes all fields but the comment one on that issue as read only. It will still allow new comments to be added, but not editable or deletable.    There should be a way to work around this flag in order to allow for specific field behaviours like, for example, edit and delete of comments, while still making the rest of the issue read-only.    """
comments: "Agreed - it seems inconsistent that users can add new comments to closed issues, but not edit or delete them. " [SEP] "Also agreed. A comment can currently be added to a published Co-ordination memo but no warning is given that it will be permanent and not editable."

summary: "Shift + Tab does not work when creating a ticket"
description: """h3. Summary  When creating an issue in Jira and using only keyboard keys, Shift + Tab gets stuck on the Description field     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue  # Tab through each field   # Shift + Tab to go back and return to top of ticket    h3. Expected Results  You are able to get back to the top of the ticket    h3. Actual Results  You get stuck at the Description field    h3. Workaround  Change the Description render in Administration > Issues > Field Configuration to the *Default Text Renderer* instead of the *Wiki Style Render*."""
comments: "Admin access is needed for the workaround.  Users without admin access will not find the workaround effective; only a workaround for some users." [SEP] "Visual editor uses tab and shift+tab for operations.  For example tab increases bullet level and shift+tab decreases it.    Please set default editor to text one as in workaround if you indeed need tab functionality."

summary: "Application links and Jira Administrators"
description: """According to [Managing global permissions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/managing-global-permissions-861253290.html]:    {quote}  People who have the JIRA System Administrators permission can perform all of the administration functions in JIRA, while people who have only the JIRA Administrators permission cannot perform functions which could affect the application environment or network.  {quote}    I feel like this should also apply to the creation and removal of Application links in the sense that it should be restricted to Jira System Administrators. For example, app links can make use of smart commits which can potentially affect many customers, like the restricted functions of System Admins could."""
